Rio rolled her eyes. “Again, no. I bought this for me, no matter what you say. You look amazing, that orange was a good choice.”

Alice wore an orange bikini, black board shorts, and a blue tank top. Her black and red hair was pulled back in a ponytail and flip-flops adorned her feet. “Let me just grab my bag, and I’ll be ready to go. Raven’s been fed.”

Ten minutes later, they were on their way to the beach, bags safely in the back, and the lyrics of ‘Feminomenon’ filling the car. Rio wasn’t entirely sure why she was so nervous. It was just a massive party at Agatha Harkness and Wanda Maximoff’s beach house.

“They do this every year?” Rio asked, turning to Alice.

“Yes, like Wanda said, they do all the things if they can. They are the super moms. Drew loves to do things at the elementary and middle school levels, and Tony Stark loves a good party. It just depends on the occasion. SHIELD does quite a few of events, but independent activities like this? Wagatha is usually behind it if their schedule allows.” Alice answered.

Rio shook her head in disbelief, “I think that’s amazing, but wow. That’s a lot.”

“They love it. Especially Wanda. Agatha just goes along with it.” Alice added.

“I would not do those things for you.” Rio laughed.

“Oh, you would absolutely throw random parties for me, just not school parties.” Alice agreed.

Rio looked out the windshield as they headed towards Malibu. “Why, though? They’re these huge megastars. Yes, back-to-school parties happen, but why would they do something like this, especially just for the teachers and other parents and then go over the top for other things?” She looked at Alice who was focused on the road.

Alice thought about it for a moment. “You know the basic Agatha Harkness back story.” She stated.

Rio nodded. “Of course.”

“Okay, recap it.”

Rio looked at Alice out of the side of her eyes but did as requested. “Agatha’s mom put her in a beauty pageant when Agatha was a baby in October 1973 at six months old, and she instantly went into commercials and then was cast in a TV show at the age of one. Boom, instant child star.” Rio started to recap. “She was cast in movies at five, and her super crazy religious mother tried to control her, no freedoms, private tutors, etc., despite the fact that she was the one who had introduced her child to Hollywood.

“Her mom kept her as sheltered as she could, and Agatha rebelled and was friends with all the other child stars of the time.

“Her mom, Evanora, was very abusive and caught Agatha making out with another star of the time, and outed her, thinking it would ruin Agatha, and it didn’t.

“The crazy bitch then tried to shoot her in public in the eighties and missed. Agatha filed to be emancipated from her mother at sixteen and won, becoming America’s lesbian sweetheart in 1989. She went on to get her high school degree that year and graduated from college, but never had an actual school, childhood, or parental experience.

“Then, Wanda was dumped in Hollywood in 1994 after her family was killed in the war in Sokovia, and Agatha took her under her wing. Something about Wanda’s aunt was a producer on Agatha's new show, and they cast Wanda as the younger sister.”

“So far, yes. It was Sisters, by the way. I love that show.” Alice chimed in.

“We can quote that show,” Rio replied. They still watched it, even if it was nearly 30 years old. Rio and Alice watched it as a comfort show, even though it had ended in 2001.

“So Agatha took Wanda under her wing, and in 1995, things with the aunt went south, and Agatha basically took her in like a sister, and we see where that ended up,” Rio concluded.

Alice nodded her head. Those were all the basics Rio needed to know for now. Alice knew a few additional details more, but Agatha always seemed to provide more details when Alice thought she had known all there was to know. And Lilia mainly told Alice things about Alice’s mother.

Agatha could fill in any more nitty-gritty details from there when she was ready. That could be part of their bonding and eventual love story. Alice knew Rio had her own nitty-gritty back story to share with Agatha.

“Right. They met Nat in 1997, that’s not my story to share, though. They, like us, are a found family with many traditions. We’re all phoenixes. Jen and Lilia even fit into the puzzle along the way, and so did I. We were all covenless witches at one point, and Agatha refers to the group as the coven, we all do.

“Agatha and Wanda wanted to make sure their kids had great memories and wanted to give back to the places they found homes in.”

Rio took in her friend's words and looked at her for a long moment. “You really have kept so much from me,” she stated, pieces of a puzzle falling into place. Alice shrugged “I signed several NDAs, and they were covered under that umbrella, Rio. My hands were tied.”

Rio looked down at her lap. This was Alice, the only family she had left. She couldn’t be mad at her. Not really. She understood, it just hurt. She had told Alice everything about her friends in San Francisco and Rio had also signed an NDA.

She pushed that all aside, determined not to ruin the night.

“Tell me about Jen,” Rio changed the subject with a knowing smirk.

Alice’s cheeks pinkened as she seemed to focus even more on the road, if it was even possible. “What do you mean? She’s a friend.”

“I saw how you looked at her yesterday.”

Alice shook her head. “It’s nothing, we’re just friends. We both moved out here about the same time, and I wouldn’t want to lose that friendship.”

Rio grinned. “Oh good, because she’s really cute and I was thinking I would —”

“I didn’t say you could make a move.”

“That’s what I thought.” Rio picked a new song to play, and they drove the last few minutes in a comfortable silence.

⭐⭐⭐

Everything was perfectly set up: the food, the bar, and the music. Agatha was wearing a simple black high-waisted two-piece with a white open-front cover-up monogrammed with a purple monogrammed A.H.. Wanda stood beside her in a black and white one-piece and a matching cover-up with a scarlet monogrammed W.M.

They were never beating those couple of allegations.

“Relax, Alice texted me. They’re on their way.” Wanda put a comforting hand on Agatha’s arm, noticing how tense her best friend was.

“I don’t know what you mean,” Agatha replied, her shoulders automatically relaxing.

“Sure you don’t.” Wanda teased.

Natasha stepped up next to them wearing a black tank top and shorts with a red one-piece underneath. She was on duty, as evidenced by her earpiece, but the swimsuit made sense. “Making a move tonight, Aggie?” She said with a slight smirk.

“You’re all terrible friends,” Agatha pouted as Jen joined the group in her pink swim shorts and halter top.

“This event is too public for you to do anything anyway.” Jen nodded in agreement.

Wanda’s shoulders sagged. “Must you take the fun out of everything, Jen?”

⭐⭐⭐

Rio’s eyes widened as they drove through the gate and up to the Malibu beach house. Alice ignored the man who directed her where to park and chose a place of her own.

“Just leave your bag here and bring your phone for now,” Alice instructed with a smile as she climbed down from her Jeep.

Rio looked up at the massive beach house that was more like a beach mansion.

She followed Alice up the few steps to the double doors, and they walked straight into the open-concept house, seeing straight to the back of the house, which was entirely glass.

The back doors were open, leading out to where the pool was, and beyond that, a path led to the beach. Rio’s eyes landed automatically on Agatha, who was talking to Coach Rogers. She was headed towards her when someone grabbed her arm and spun her around. Rio found herself face to face with her boss, Lilia Calderu, wearing a classy one-piece with a wrap-around skirt.

“Rio, I’m glad you’re here. This is the perfect time to get to know the parents, especially some of the very well-known, award-winning ones. Have a lovely time.” Lilia smiled at her warmly and slipped back into the growing crowd. Rio stared at the spot where she had been with a baffled look on her face as Alice took her arm.

“Remember, the woman knows more than we do and speaks in riddles.” Alice handed her a drink. “Here, now go talk.” Rio nodded and turned to where Agatha had been prior. She wasn’t sure why that was the first person she wanted to talk to, Rio just felt drawn to the older woman. Instead, she found herself in conversation with Pepper Potts-Stark and Drew Barrymore.

“I’m so glad you came, I was telling Agatha about you when we were filming the other day,” Drew had a hand on Rio’s arm, drawing circles on it with her thumb.

Pepper had an eyebrow raised and a smirk on her face. As a waiter walked by, she took Rio’s empty drink from her and replaced it with a full one.

Rio looked at the older blonde woman thankfully. She had a feeling that she and Pepper would be good friends.

“Oh? About what exactly?” Rio managed to get out. Why was Drew Barrymore having a conversation about her?

Drew smiled widely. “I had read about you in the welcome back email and told our dear Aggie that she needed to brush up on her science is all,” she said with a wink.

Rio was going to melt. Drew Barrymore was trying to set her up with Agatha Harkness. LA truly was lesbian heaven.

Pepper put a hand on Drew’s arm. “Drew, you’re going to cause her to short-circuit.” Pepper laughed and changed the subject. “Alice has told us great things about you. I’m sure Morgan will love your class.”

They chatted a little longer before Rio stepped away, their numbers added to her phone. She didn’t need Drew’s number necessarily, however, Drew insisted.

Rio smiled and blew air out of her nose with a small smile upon seeing Agatha’s number with a purple heart next to it. Devils, she thought.

The inside of the house was becoming rather crowded, and the sun was beginning to set outside. Rio took that as a sign and excused herself from the group of English teachers she was speaking to. Rio found English teachers to be the most dramatic group on any campus, and this group was already living up to that reputation. Rio slipped out the back door, picked up her third drink of the night, walked past the pool, and down the stairs and the short path to the private beach, surprised that it was empty.

Rio sat in one of the beach chairs in the sand and took in the relative quiet, the muted sounds of the party behind her and the waves in front of her.

Rio removed her sandals, set them to the side, and let her toes dig into the sand. She leaned back and took in the pink and orange hues of the sunset, the light breeze slipped over her skin.

It was a beautiful view and something she hoped she would get to do again. Maybe next year, she thought.

There had been another beach house once, but it hadn’t been nearly as peaceful. This was peaceful, and comforting, a place that spoke of family and memories.

She was lost in her thoughts and the scenery when she heard the unmistakable and familiar voice of Agatha Harkness behind her.

“I’ve always said the sunset is the universe’s way of celebrating lesbianism,” Agatha said as she sat down in the chair next to Rio, drink in hand. “Is it okay if I join you? I can leave if you want to continue enjoying the peace and Mother Nature’s lesbianism. I know the party gets pretty crowded and overwhelming.” She added.

Rio laughed. “It’s your house, pretty sure you’re allowed to sit where you want. Thank you for asking and not assuming though. Very polite of you.”

“I have been told I’m rather polite for Hollywood elite.” She retorted.

“Don’t get ahead of yourself there, Harkness. Don’t want to inflate your ego too much.” Rio was shocked at herself. Apparently, all it took was three incredibly strong drinks and she could banter back and forth with Agatha Fucking Harkness. Wonder if I can drink at work, she considered.

“I think the awards have already done that job.” She laughed and snorted, causing her to turn bright red.

Rio smiled reflexively upon hearing Agatha’s snort. It was something that she had always found charming about the actress in interviews and her acting. It was something that seemed genuine. “I love your laugh. That snort is adorable.” Fuck, Vidal, she thought as a blush spread across her cheeks.

The two women sat there in embarrassed silence without looking at each other.

Rio was the first to break the silence between them. “Do you spend time here often? The sunset is beautiful here. Everything is really.”

Agatha took a drink and nodded. “It depends on our schedules and life. We begin and end our summer here and try to spend time here during the summer. Girls’ weekends when we can, it’s a nice little getaway. We also have Wanda’s cabin in Lake Tahoe, and our apartment in New York. The New York apartment doubles for work, of course.”

“Wow, that’s taking U-Hauling to a new level.” Rio immediately slapped a hand over her mouth. “I’m going to go drown myself in the ocean.”

Agatha laughed and snorted again, before groaning. “I’ll join you.”

“You’re not completely wrong, though. It does look like that. Wanda is my family. When we found each other, it became an older-younger sibling situation. It made sense to buy property together and share it that way. We didn’t both need our own. Our houses are even on a compound of sorts. I’m sure Alice told you.”

Rio looked at Agatha, mouth slightly agape. Agatha and Wanda lived on the same piece of land, too? “She most certainly did not! I just found out she knew you both this week.”

Agatha nodded. “Right, NDAs.” Agatha ran her hand through her elbow-length hair. “It’s public knowledge that we’re neighbors. What’s not as commonly known is that it’s actually a very large enclosed piece of land. Her house is on one side, mine is on the other, and the original house is in the middle. The boys now hang out in that middle house, we call it the Clubhouse. We have driveways that connect them. There are what goes for woods in LA, towards the back, and space to build more houses if we wanted to. I bought the land when I turned eighteen, back when there was still a lot of land on what was the outskirts of LA at the time. Now there’s a housing development across the street.” She laughed before continuing, “Nat lives next to us, even though she could live on the property if she weren’t so hardheaded.

“With us being so close, Wanda and I are somewhat codependent, and we realize that. It’s not in a toxic way, more in the way that we’ve experienced trauma, and we’re each other’s security blankets. Obviously, we can go places without each other. We’re not that bad. But knowing that your sister is right there unless she’s off filming somewhere? It’s priceless. I imagine it’s the same for you and Alice from what she has said?” Agatha was looking directly at Rio, waiting for her response.

Rio nodded. She had never expected to have such an easy conversation with this woman. “Yes. She’s all I have, family-wise. We’ve lived together before, and we used to be extremely close. Being in different cities for so long was excruciating, and with the confidentiality, we didn’t know everything about each other anymore. So being neighbors and working together again has already been amazing.” Rio enjoyed talking to Agatha. It was as if this weren’t their third actual conversation, ever.

Agatha’s smile softened. “I know keeping things from those you love can be extremely difficult. We share so much, especially since we are very much one big family. We share the private jets. We got to a point where we couldn’t fly commercially a long time ago. The security team, styling team, domestic teams, and a few vehicles- that’s all shared. Except favorite cars, those are personally owned, Vision is absolutely hers, my flings are mine, for a few hours at least, and the boys, well, we all have a hand in raising them.

“Maybe one day we’ll split things, maybe one day we’ll split the compound, maybe we won’t. If people want to think we’re a couple or think we have a polyamorous thing going on, it’s fine. This family works for us, and I love it.”

Rio exhaled in wonder. “That all sounds amazing. Raising your kids together, and being right there when you need each other?”

“It is pretty great.” Agatha agreed.

The Agatha image that the media portrayed was not the side of Agatha that Rio was seeing now. Yes, she could be flirty like that public persona, Rio had seen that firsthand. However, she was attentive and caring. The media made it seem as though she were all sarcasm, wit, and sexuality unless it came to parenting or her activism. However, Agatha was very focused on her inner circle, which included more than just Nicky. She had built a chosen family, was kind, offered women water when they were choking, and enjoyed watching sunsets. Agatha Harkness even had awkward moments and slipped up. Rio was looking forward to getting to know more about this woman.

As they talked after the sun went down and the moon had come up, Wanda and Alice looked on from the balcony of the beach house.

“Friends to idiots to lovers?” Alice asked, watching as Rio laughed at something that Agatha said and put a hand on Agatha’s bare arm.

“Yep,” Wanda said, popping the p.

Jen stepped up next to Alice, careful not to brush up against her, but giving her a warm smile.

Nat sidled up next to Wanda, quickly taking her hand and squeezing it, before letting it go as if nothing had happened.

Jen and Nat’s eyes automatically caught what the other two had been looking at.

Jen sighed. Apparently, she was now an accomplice to this. “Slow burn?” She added.

“Unfortunately.” Alice and Wanda said in unison.

“Angst and drama because they’re afraid of their feelings and being hurt in the process,” Lilia added, stepping forth from the shadows. The four younger women just nodded. Completely unsurprised by her appearance.

“...and we will be here to push all the way,” Nat added, looking at Wanda expectantly.

Wanda nodded and turned to Jen, ready to begin her plan. “At ten, you and Alice will leave in Alice’s Jeep, which is why I made you ride with us. I don’t care where you go.” In her head, she was screaming ‘one of your places, idiots’, but she didn’t voice it. If she could successfully matchmake two couples, she’d be thrilled. “You’ll leave Rio’s bag here. I’ll text Agatha saying you both left. Knowing Agatha, she’ll offer to either take Rio home or back to the compound to continue their conversation. Since Nat came in a different vehicle tonight, I’ll have Nat take me home.” Wanda grinned. She loved when her plans came together.

Jen huffed a laugh and looked at Wanda fondly. “Got it, boss.”

Jen and Alice went back to the party, and Nat returned to do her job, fingertips lightly brushing Wanda’s arm as she departed.

Wanda’s eyes trailed after the other redhead, Lilia carefully watching both of them.

The older woman turned toward Wanda. “Sometimes we do what we think is right, even though it is not what we want. You might be hurting someone by doing the ‘right’ thing. Times have changed.” She left before Wanda could respond. Wanda shook her head. She’s right and you know it, Maximoff.

⭐⭐⭐

The stars twinkled in the sky and the sounds of the party filtered out of the house behind them. Agatha found it odd that no one else had wandered down to the beach and she had a feeling that was the doing of one Natasha Romanov and would have to thank her for it later. She enjoyed having this time to talk, uninterrupted, with Rio. The younger woman made Agatha really laugh, something few were able to do, and was easy to talk to.

The two women had abandoned their chairs and were sitting on a beach blanket, sides pressed together so they could hear each other over the sounds of the ocean and the party.

There was a natural lull in the conversation, and Agatha’s eyes had locked on Rio’s, when they dropped to Rio’s plump pink lips.

The tip of Agatha’s tongue swept over her own lips.

Agatha wasn’t sure if it was the alcohol, the ocean, the moon, or that shooting star, but she was extremely tempted to lean forward and find out if Rio’s lips were as soft as they looked.

Rio cleared her throat, and Agatha’s eyes darted back up to meet hers.

“Agatha?” Rio said, voice wavering.

Rio looked into Agatha’s sapphire blue eyes that reflected the stars. Agatha had been staring at her, and Rio had thought about kissing her.

No, that was not something Rio could do. Agatha was a parent, and that was a boundary Rio had drawn. It was a mixture of the alcohol, the setting, and her silly childhood crush.

“I can be your friend.” She said. Idiot. You’re an idiot.

Agatha’s smile reached her eyes. “I’d like that.”

Something in Rio was relieved. She was worried she would cause offense or that Agatha would laugh and tell her that she was only here to add Rio to her CVS receipt length list of women.

Rio’s smile was bright. She did her best to mimic a teenage girl, “OMG! Wait until I tell my friends that I’m friends with Agatha Harkness!”

Agatha smacked her on the arm, mood ruined. “You’re a child.”

Rio nodded enthusiastically. “You were almost an EGOT winner when I was born.”

Agatha rolled her eyes. Picking up her phone as it vibrated, notifying her of a text. “Oh wow, we’ve been down here awhile.”

Rio grinned. “I’m amazing company.”

Agatha pretended to ignore her as she suppressed a smile and read the text from Wanda.

SuperStar Wanda:

Alice and Jen left together!! In Alice’s car.

Here’s hoping they finally get their act together.

She left Rio’s bag though. MAKE A MOVE.

Rio’s phone went off next, to a text from Alice.

Sister Alice

Taking Jen home…not sure if to mine or hers,

Probably a movie night!

Left your bag with Wanda.

Ask Agatha to take you home. You know, to

her house.

Rio and Agatha looked back up at each other. Rio spoke first. “Did you know that Jen and Alice were into each other?” She asked.

Agatha just looked at her. “You didn’t?”

Rio sighed. “I didn’t even know Jen existed until this week, NDAs, remember?”

Agatha put a comforting hand on Rio’s knee, their sides still touching. They had decided to be friends, hadn’t kissed, yet hadn’t parted either.

This was not lost on Agatha.

Agatha flashed an Oscar-winning smile. “Would you like to go back to my place? As friends, of course.”

Chapter End Notes

It burns but it burns pretty.

Little Does She Know Her Whole World’s About To Change

Chapter Summary

Agatha takes Rio back to her house.
As friends of course.

Chapter Notes

Trigger: implied child abuse
Also:
Sorry if I got anyone's hopes up.

No, I'm not. I warned you all, it's a slow burn.
This story unfolded quickly, and I finally came back and started editing! All thoughts and writing are mine (with inserts/suggestions from DaretoDream82).

UPDATED - 5/6/2025

Also, Agatha's full name is now Agatha Fucking Harkness, and she's basically the Idina Menzel of this universe.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

🎶

Agatha met a boy with eyes like a man

Turns out her heart fits right in the palm of Rio’s hand

Now he'll be Agatha’s shelter when it rains

Little does Rio know, her whole world's about to change

When Emma Falls in Love - Taylor Swift

🎶

“Angel, did you change the lyrics and gender-bend them?” Agatha raised an eyebrow at Wanda.

Wanda shrugged. “Maybe?”

Early August

Agatha stood up from the sand in one fluid motion, without using her hands, in only a way Agatha Fucking Harkness could, and held out a hand to help Rio, which she gladly took.

“You don’t mind leaving your party early?” Rio asked, noting that it was far from dying down.

“Oh, gods no. I usually leave before midnight anyway. It’s not about me and I prefer smaller affairs anyway, I’m getting too old for these big to-dos. I only stay out late for the awards after parties because it’s almost required.” Agatha tugged Rio to her feet and picked up their empty glasses. Agatha led them up the beach to the side of the house where a car was waiting, handed the glasses to one of the security guys, and nodded. “Pizza and movies?” Agatha asked, gesturing for Rio to get into the large black SUV first.

Rio laughed. “I said let’s be friends and you’re asking me to Netflix and chill? Isn’t that a little outdated Harkness?” She asked with a bemused smile. Noting that her bag was in the car, surely Wanda’s doing.

Agatha rolled her eyes as the door closed behind them. “Trust me, if I was you up for nefarious reasons, you would know. I’m serious. And, as you don’t sleep with parents, I don’t sleep with my son’s teachers,” she thought for a moment. “anymore.”

Rio laughed. “Ah, one of Hollywood’s bad boys cleaning up her act? For moi?” She replied, batting her eyelashes.

“Sure, that’s why.” In reality, no one else interested her, but she wasn’t telling Rio that. “What kind of pizza do you want? I’m ordering now so it should be there when we arrive.” She asked, changing the subject. If they were doing this whole friend thing, she needed to shelve this line of talk.

“Veggie please, if that’s okay. Is Nicky home?” Rio asked. She hadn’t seen him much and wondered if he was doing something with his de facto cousins. She hadn’t even met Vision yet. She didn’t think she’d meet him now, but was starting to wonder if he existed.

“Veggie it is, and all three boys are with Vision having a horror movie marathon at the Clubhouse I was telling you about. The boys could stay by themselves, but they’re getting to the age where we wouldn’t put it past them to throw their own party, and we’re not quite ready for that. So, adult supervision it is. Billy wouldn’t, but the other two absolutely would.” She smiled.

Rio looked out the window, watching the ocean pass them as they headed back towards the city. “What’s it like? Knowing everyone knows so much about you?” She asked without thinking, automatically realizing how invasive the question was. “Oh no! Don’t answer that.”

Agatha regarded her for a second, thinking about her response.

“No, it’s okay, no one outside of an interview has ever asked me that before, and interviewers never want the real answer, a completely honest answer. It seems like the entire world knows everything about me, but it’s only what I want them to know.

“When my mother outed me in 1989, stripping my life bare for all to see and in turn exposing herself for who she really was, I was taught to curate a certain image.

“Yes, the public sees the dedicated mom, activist, trailblazer, ‘bad boy’ persona—because that’s what I want them to see. Just like I only show the parts of my personal life that I want to show.” Agatha chose her words carefully. She felt like she could tell Rio a lot, but she didn’t want to give just anything away. Agatha had layers and she never just stripped herself raw. She had done that once before and refused to do it again.

“Information leaks, it always does. That’s why I have Jen and Nat. It’s why I send Nicky to a school with ironclad NDAs run by a woman I have known forever and trust. As a testament to how well it all works, Alice is a close friend of mine and has been a decade, and you had no idea.” Agatha watched Rio process and mull over everything she had just said before plowing on.

“If you know what you’re doing, know the right people, and play your cards right, then Hollywood can be both very public and extremely private at the same time. We have learned to control the narrative, and while our lives are on display, it’s the show we want people to see, and we take comfort in that.

“People will believe what they want to believe, but we can steer them in the direction of what we want them to believe. Few people know what really goes on behind these walls and that’s why I bought this land and enclosed it. So my family, chosen and otherwise, had an escape. A place we could be our authentic selves.

“You’ll start to learn that there are things about me you don’t know and you can decide for yourself if that’s a good or a bad thing.” Agatha kept her eyes locked on Rio. This was not a speech she often gave to people, it was not something she really ever told anyone. The last time she had broken down how curated her life was, was to Alice. That had been out of necessity, and she felt like this was too.

Agatha knew that Rio was important to Alice, and Alice was important to Agatha, whether Alice believed it or not. Alice was Lorna’s daughter, and Agatha had made a promise to Lorna.



Rio took everything in and processed it. She wasn’t completely surprised that there was a hidden side to Agatha and that she carefully revealed what people knew about her. Rio had read about what had happened to her when she was outed and could see why Agatha had been advised to live the way she did.

Rio remembered Alice’s mother, Lorna, being an open book to the public, but keeping Alice hidden from view and out of the public eye. Rio knew double lives were possible, though Lorna didn’t really have a double life in as much as she had a mother who was willing to raise the daughter she rarely saw.

Rio realized that the more she learned about Agatha Harkness, the more of a mystery she became.



The large vehicle drove through the automatic gate and to the left, snaking around a driveway and coming to a stop in front of a large house that Rio assumed belonged to Agatha.

Agatha stepped out first, offering her hand to Rio, who stepped out, slung her bag over her shoulder, and stopped abruptly when she saw the house before her.

For the second time that night, Rio was amazed by the beauty and architecture of a house.

The house was composed of glass and stone. A mix of modern and a hidden witch’s cottage. Rio chuckled. “Never beating those witch allegations Harkness.”

They walked through the covered porch and in the double doors, the smell of pizza in the air.

Agatha shrugged. “Maybe I don’t want to. I like my vampire glass witch cottage thank you,” Agatha glanced over her shoulder to see if Rio caught the reference.

The younger woman laughed. “Surrounded by the trees, it does look like that. When did you have it built?” Rio followed Agatha through the foyer and into the living room. Three plush black couches surrounded a large dark wooden coffee table. A large TV was mounted on a wall surrounded by built-in shelves with pictures, books, and various decor items. The back wall was completely glass with a door that led out to a pool, which was lit and had various lights hanging in the patio area. The kitchen was open concept like the beach house, and was Rio’s dream kitchen. There was a bar directly behind the center couch. Someone could cook, fetch something, whatever was needed, and still be a part of whatever was happening in the main room. There were stairs back in the foyer, and she thought they had passed a bathroom. She was just realizing she was still in her bikini and shorts.

Agatha had gone to the kitchen and was getting something out of the fridge. Also still in her bikini and monogrammed cover-up. “Let me think, I bought the land in ‘91, and Wanda and I built the clubhouse in ‘97. She built her house in 2005 when she and Vision got married, and I started building mine around the time I decided to have Nicky and it was completed in ‘08.” She walked into the living room carrying the pizza box, a bottle of wine, and two glasses.

“Impressive. That’s quite a history lesson. Before we eat, where’s the bathroom? I should change out of this swimsuit.” She asked.

Agatha looked down at herself and laughed.

“I completely forgot. When you’re so used to wearing whatever people tell you to, you just go with it.” In reality, Agatha had been admiring Rio’s current attire. Friends could appreciate the scenery. “It’s back down the hall, on the left. I should probably change too.” Agatha set everything down and followed Rio, before disappearing up the stairs.

It wasn’t long before they were seated on the couch, Rio in forest green shorts and a Stanford Basketball tank top, Agatha in black yoga pants, and a purple SHIELD football mom shirt.

Agatha had turned the music on for background noise, a nineties playlist Wanda had made. Agatha knew it had to be safe if Wanda had made it.

“Aren’t you cute in your mom shirt?” Rio smirked, finishing her second slice of pizza in about as many minutes.

“Has anyone told you that you eat like a teenage boy?” Agatha responded, still eating her first slice.

“Only everyone I’ve ever met.” Rio sipped her wine. “It’s a habit left over from how I grew up.”

Agatha raised an eyebrow. “Sounds like there’s a story there. How did you meet Alice? You know a lot about my background, what’s the Rio Vidal story?” Agatha asked, taking another slice of pizza. Agatha knew some of Rio’s story, but wanted to hear it from the source.

They sat on the center couch, the same way she and Wanda often did. Her tucked in one corner, Rio tucked in the other, facing each other. It felt natural, even if they were new friends.



“Well,” Rio started, biting her lip, thinking about how much she wanted to divulge, and then sighing. Might as well go all in, no telling what Alice had already shared. “My parents moved to San Francisco when I was 12. We moved around a lot and didn’t live in the best places. They weren’t the best people. It was my biological mom, she was great until my first stepdad left when I was about five. That’s when she started dating the wrong people. I never knew my dad. Anyway, she and my then stepdad, number six I think?” She scoffed. “I started at a new middle school, an extremely awkward age to start over with no friends and very little anything. I met Alice on the first day I was there. She was standing off to the side judging people like you do at that age, and I awkwardly stood next to her and made a comment about the group she was watching. Our preteen surliness was a match made in melodramatic heaven.

“We had several advanced classes together and we would work together on our projects. After a few weeks, I missed the bus home one afternoon. When I told her the neighborhood I lived in, she insisted that she walk me home. When we reached the door, we heard gunshots in my apartment. She dragged me back to her Gran’s house and refused to let me go back to my apartment. She and Gran insisted I stay the night, especially since my mom didn’t pick up the phone when I called.

“When I finally got through to my mom, they were both fine. They had made someone upset and what we had heard were warning shots.” Rio smiled slightly at the memory and looked up at Agatha. “You were friends with Lorna, right? So you knew her mom, Norma?”

Agatha nodded and Rio continued, “Well, imagine how pissed Norma Wu was when she found out about my home life. From then on, I spent most of my time there, and by the time I was fourteen, I had my own room and lived there ninety-five percent of the time. It got to where she was the one school called, she was my emergency contact. Gran finished raising me. I was shocked when I saw a picture one day and realized that Lorna Wu was Alice’s mom.

“Anytime I did go home, it wasn’t a good time. My parents basically forgot I existed unless they needed my help with something illegal, and as much as Gran tried to fight them, they had legal rights. When I went back to Gran’s, it was with marks, bruises, burns, and so much trauma.

“They really didn’t care where I was, as long as I was there whenever they needed me for whatever illegal activity or scheme required a pretty young girl. They finally died at the beginning of my senior year of high school. I had recently returned from a short stint with them, things had gone terribly south, there was a lot of yelling when I was finally able to sneak out and run back home.

“I wasn’t there long when the police showed up and told me they had died. I stayed with Gran and Alice, and as terrible as it sounds, a weight was lifted. Gran seemed a lot less worried after that too.

“We both went to Stanford, something we worked so hard for. While teaching is not the most glorious thing we could do with our lives, it was something that allowed us to give back. Alice wanted to do something, not simple, but normal. Civilian, as she called it, and I think Lorna put it into her head when she was young. I’ve just always loved learning and sharing knowledge. So that’s what we did. We also wanted to make Gran proud of us, and we did.” Rio looked back at Agatha, whose eyes were red and watery. “Agatha, please don’t cry, we all have our past.”



Agatha knew how much the past affected Alice,  and how much Rio meant to her. Agatha also knew details that Rio would never be privy to.

She wanted to wrap her arms around Rio and hold her, to try and take the pain away. She wanted to cradle this woman’s head to her chest and hold her there.

Instead, she put a hand on Rio’s shoulder. “I am so sorry you had such a rough start and had to go through all that.” Agatha’s eyes took in the lines and light wrinkles in Rio’s face. Memorizing every detail. “I can’t believe parents would do things like that to their children. I mean, my mother did, but she was a very special case. I am so glad Norma was there for you.”

Rio’s breath caught at the way Agatha’s eyes were taking her in. “Agatha, it’s fine, I’ve done a lot of therapy since then.” Rio looked away and tucked her hair behind her ear. “Thank you though. Norma Wu was an amazing woman and I loved her like she was my Mom.”

Agatha dropped her hand from Rio’s shoulder, letting her fingertips trail down Rio’s arm, and moved back slightly. “That makes sense as to why you were there those times I came to see Lorna, and at the funeral.” Agatha also remembered seeing an ‘R’ on the door of the room next to Alice’s door with the ‘A’ on it.

Rio nearly choked, something Agatha was becoming good at making her do. “You remember that.”

“You looked like you were going to kill me. Yes, I remember. If anyone asks, I do not remember, as I very pointedly told everyone I did not, did you ever tell Alice what happened?” Agatha asked.

“Which time?”

“Either one.”

Rio laughed. “That I caught you sneaking out of her mother’s room? No. I did tell her I thought the two of you were a thing and she assured me you weren’t. I believe her now.”

Agatha nodded. “We very much were not. I was just there to get her to come back and finish shooting, then I got her into rehab.” It was the lie that Agatha always told.

“She never did outrun her demons. Alice doesn’t blame you though, and neither do I.” Rio added.

Agatha’s heart clenched. Rio still didn’t know, and it wasn’t Agatha’s place to tell her.

“Oh, I know,” Agatha assured her. “Come with me, I want to show you something.” She said, standing and picking up the now-empty pizza box and the empty bottle of wine.

Rio rose up to follow her as Agatha led through the kitchen first, disposing of the empty containers, and then back to the front of the house into what Rio assumed was an office.

“This is my Harkness brag room,” Agatha turned on the light.

“How humble of you,” Rio smirked, noting that she was wrong about the office. Her breath caught as she took in the cases full of different awards, the memorabilia from different shows and movies Agatha had been in, pictures of her with other celebrities, trophies, and medals Nicky had won, various pictures of him, her, Wanda, and Nat. There were so many memories captured around the giant room.

It wasn’t about bragging per se, it was about preserving precious memories, like a living time capsule. Rio walked around various set pieces, even costumes encased in glass. “This is amazing. I can’t believe they let you keep some of this stuff.” She came to one piece in particular and squealed. “THE COAT! From Witches Road! I thought it was destroyed!”

Agatha laughed.

“No, that was a backup. This one is the original, with all of the details and everything. I even have Lorna’s jacket from the movie.” She nodded to another case.

“You two were close.” The younger woman stated, looking at a picture from that night at The Oscars.



“She was one of my mentors.” Agatha lowered her voice, measuring her words carefully. “Of course, we were. I was in the ambulance that night, I held her hand as she…I promised I would watch over Alice.” She looked away as a tear fell.

This time, it was Rio’s turn to be the comforter.

Rio wrapped her arms around Agatha.

“No, you didn’t. Lorna knew what she was doing. That movie consumed her, it happens to some of the best actors and you did everything you could.” Rio stepped back as Agatha gave her a watery smile.

“Why are you so easy to bare my soul too?” Agatha asked, wiping a tear away with her thumb.

“Because I’m secretly a soul-stealing witch,” Rio replied with a tooth-baring smile.

Agatha huffed a laugh and pointed out a few more set pieces and several pictures.

Rio took in the many pictures from various decades. She noted that Lorna was in many of them, and of course, Agatha and Wanda were constantly paired together. The room was a large, living, breathing time capsule, and Rio was fascinated.

Pictures from the opening nights of Rent and Wicked, the premier of Frozen and Frozen II, Agatha having played Elsa.

“Can you tell me anything about Frozen III?” Rio quirked a brow.

Agatha ran tongue along the inside of her bottom lip. “I cannot.”

Rio huffed. “Fine, want to play a game to Frozen?”

The morning sun was warm on Rio’s face as she rubbed her eyes. She blinked multiple times, trying to get her bearings. Rio had suggested a drinking game to Frozen, as you do at thirty-five and fifty-one.

Rio had learned that Agatha Harkness did not back down from a challenge.

The game was simple. Drink every time there was queer subtext.

They had to switch to water rather quickly and then continued talking through Frozen II.

Watching Agatha Harkness movies with Agatha Harkness was enlightening, especially when Wanda was in them. Wanda played Anna in the movie, given their connection, and Agatha told multiple stories about the recording and artistry.

Rio looked around her as she gathered her thoughts. She was lying on the couch opposite the glass wall. Agatha was still asleep on the center couch, face buried in her arms. Rio grabbed her phone, noticing that it was seven and past her cat’s scheduled breakfast time. Rio was many things and a crazy cat lady was one of them. Not wanting to wake Agatha and having received zero texts from Alice, she thought about who else she could text for a ride home. Rio didn’t know many people here yet. She had however gotten the impression that she was being welcomed into this group of women, and of that group, Nat was the most likely to be awake. Considering meddling had happened to get Agatha’s number into her phone, she probably had everyone’s number. Rio opened a new message and typed in a few letters when the contact appeared. Thank you, meddling bitches.

Me:

Hey, it’s Rio, are you awake?

Natasha R.:

I just got back from a run. Need a

ride home from Agatha’s?

Me:

Yes. Wait, how do you know?

Natasha R.:

It’s my job. Be right there.

Don’t worry about waking

Aggie, she sleeps like the dead.

Give me 10.

Me:

Right. Thanks

Rio let out a sigh and found her bag. She went to the bathroom, to freshen up and slip her shoes on. Nat had disarmed the alarm remotely and Rio stepped out of the house right as the petite bodyguard pulled up in a red SUV. The younger woman put her bag in the backseat and climbed in the front. Nat handed her Tylenol and pointed to the coffee.

“Thought you would need these.” She smiled.

“Thank you,” Rio grumbled as she took the Tylenol and chased it with the coffee. “Any word from Alice and Jen?”

Nat shook her head. “No, we’ve been waiting for them to pull that trigger for years now. At least they actually stayed the night though.”

Rio just chuckled. “Creeper.”

“It is my job to know where everyone is,” she replied.

“Which is why I didn’t bother giving you my address.” She looked over at the redhead.

“You live in the same building as Alice, so I don’t need it anyway, but you’re right. I assume you talked, drank, and that’s it?” Nat asked, keeping her eyes on the road, although there was no traffic to speak of early on a Sunday morning.

“Exactly. You can report that back to the Meddling Bitches group chat.” Rio took another sip of her coffee.

“That’s a good name for it, actually. You know Agatha will report everything to Wanda anyway.”.

“Oh, I know she will.” Rio had gathered that Agatha told Wanda everything. “How did you join up with Wanda and Agatha? I know the ‘met at a club in New York’ story, but I have a feeling there’s more to it. I know you’ve been with them almost as long as they’ve known each other.”

Nat raised an eyebrow. “Fast learner. Well, you know I’m from Russia, that’s not hard to gather, the Romanov is a dead giveaway, and I still have the slightest accent on certain words. Would you believe me if I told you I was a very young assassin?” Nat looked at Rio from the corner of her eye to gauge Rio’s response.

The response was laughter as Rio looked at her. “Oh please, I mean, I had heard the rumors, but…” She noticed that Nat was not laughing. “Wait, it’s true? You actually were sent by Agatha’s mom to kill her before she died in prison?”

“Sure was.”

“But how?” She was flabbergasted at this point. What the hell kind of people had she become friends with?

“Turns out Agatha Harkness and Wanda Maximoff are not the type of people you want to meet in a dark alley. Assassin or not. It’s a longer story, but basically, Agatha punched me in the face, Wanda stepped on my throat, which was surprisingly pleasant...but anyway, Agatha made me an offer to be on her security team, some legal stuff happened, and the rest is history.” She shrugged as they pulled up to Rio’s building.

Rio sat back, “So Agatha just collects people like Infinity Stones?” She asked.

The other woman nodded. “Women that need a family, yes.” Don’t forget to text Agatha so she doesn’t wake up and freak out that you’re gone.”

Rio walked into her apartment to one very angry cat.

“Okay, okay, I’m sorry. Let me put my bag down.” It took her longer than usual to feed Raven due to the self-appointed queen getting under her feet, jumping on the counter, and yelling at her in her way. “You better hope I never start dating anyone and sleep over at their house you freeloader.” She said as she put her bowl down. She pulled out her phone and texted Agatha.

Me

Sorry for leaving without a word

this morning. Had to get home to

feed the cat. Nat gave me a ride.

She was hired to kill you?

Rio assumed she’d hear from her later and knew Alice would just come over. She had a lot to process but needed to shower first.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha had woken up, read Rio’s text, and taken a shower. It was too early to formulate words. What ungodly hour had that woman gotten up at? The time stamp said eight, who did those things on a weekend? In Agatha’s mind, that constituted as a red flag.

Agatha leaned against the counter, coffee mug raised to her lips, as she ran through what to text back.

“Sorry for crying on you? No. Desperate.” Agatha sipped on her coffee but didn’t put it down as she stared at the phone, thumb hovering over the letters of the touchscreen.

“Glad we got drunk together? Yeah, absolutely not, Harkness, this is not the early aughts.” She took a long drink of her coffee and sat the mug on the counter, holding the phone with two hands now.

Agatha was in her fifties, she knew how to communicate with women.

It was literally part of what gave her her reputation.

Me:

I find friends in odd situations.

What kind’ve cat? Definitely important

to get home and feed her. Glad

we got to hang out!

Okay, that wasn’t too bad, maybe a little overly enthusiastic. Agatha found that she wasn’t completely in control of herself around Rio. A fluke, really.

Rio 💚

She’s a menace is what she is.

She’s a tortoiseshell, quite possibly

immortal, definitely a familiar. You’ll

Have to meet her.

Rio 💚

Thanks, I enjoyed it. Can’t wait to do it again.

Agatha had noticed the green heart those wenches she called friends had put next to Rio's name and decided to leave it. At least she hadn’t scared Rio off, yet. So far, the friends thing was going well and she knew it was just a matter of time before Wanda ran in to demand to hear everything unless she had already talked to Natasha. Wanda would still want to hear everything directly from Agatha.

As if summoned by her thoughts, Wanda ran through the door.

Agatha hadn’t bothered resetting the alarm, which would have been useless, as Wanda could deactivate it from her phone.

“Get in here and tell me everything,” Wanda demanded, launching herself on the couch as though she weren’t forty-five, and assuming her normal position.

Agatha groaned and moved to join her.

“...and that’s all. Nothing happened. Friendly touches, tears, pizza, a drinking game, and we passed out.” She summarized after droning on for the past hour.

“That’s it? You acted like you were twenty-two, were moderately drunk, and you didn’t even make out? You make out with everyone when you’re drunk, we’ve done it a time or two.” Wanda admonished.

“Not everyone, only women, and we haven’t done that in a while, I remember being threatened. I also don’t drink to get drunk and have things under control.” She pointed out. “Besides, Rio and I are friends. ONLY FRIENDS.” Agatha gave Wanda a pointed look.

“Whatever.” Wanda pouted. “I happen to think with the similar backgrounds, similar interests, etc., it would go well.”

Agatha shook her head, holding her hands out in front of her. “Whatever you say, but it’s not happening.”

⭐⭐⭐

After leaving Jen’s house, Alice went straight to Rio’s apartment instead of her own. Alice walked in, using her key, and handed Rio a container of vegetable fried rice. “It tastes almost like Gran’s.” She sat the rest of the containers on the coffee table and sat on the couch.

“The fried rice I make tastes just like Gran’s, because she taught me how to make it.” Rio looked at Alice suspiciously. Chinese take-out meant one thing.

“We don’t have time. I’m panicking in a lesbian way.” Alice whined.

“There it is, water? Soda? Tea?” Rio listed off beverages and headed toward the kitchen.

“Water, I need to hydrate.”

“I bet you do!” Rio chuckled.

Alice pouted, “Unfortunately, not like that.”

Rio came back and handed Alice her water before settling into the couch with her fried rice, Alice already cradling wonton soup close to her chest. “We went back to Jen’s place and had a movie night.”

“What movies?”

“Bound and I Can’t Think Straight. Movies we have both seen. Multiple times. With each other.” Alice lamented.

“Shit, Alice, what happened?”

“Straight up gay panic of the middle age variety. We cuddled, Ri. We fell asleep like that, and then I snuck out and didn’t even say goodbye. We have been decently close friends since I moved out here. Ten years, Rio. Ten.” Alice took a bite of a dumpling, shaking her head at her own idiocy.

“So let me get this sapphic, the two of you have been circling each other for years now, dropping hints like carabiners at Lilith Fair, and still nothing?” Rio asked, disbelief written all over her face.

“Our inaction is physically painful at this point,” Alice winced.

“What the hell are you waiting for? Her to invite you to a Melissa Etheridge concert?” Rio had finished eating and was gesturing with hands.

“Muna or Xana might be more her speed, but no. It’s just, that she’s so serious and aggressive with everything and I’m way more laid back. Plus she’s always busy.” Alice explained.

Rio sighed heavily. “You’re a teacher, there are months where no one even sees us. Her clients are your family, and she spends time with them too, right? You would balance each other out well.”

“If this were a book or a movie, I would yell at the characters right now.” Alice groaned. “Enough about me. You and Agatha, stepping into friendship, that’s awesome.” She gave her a double thumbs up and a sarcastic wink.

“Oh go to hell, Wu,” Rio replied. Rio had filled Alice in on everything that had happened the previous night and Rio was very glad she had made the decision to join SHIELD and that Alice could talk to her more freely now. There was so much Rio hadn’t known about Alice the past ten years.

“It’s the last week before school starts. Open House and Meet-the-Teacher is on Tuesday, and we have planning and all of the things that come with it for the rest of the week. Anything next weekend?” Rio asked.

Alice shook her head. “Nothing that I can think of. Sports haven’t started yet, and I can’t think of any parties or anything.”

“Great.” Rio wanted time to prepare to be in this new world of celebrity parents, celebrity children, dealing with public relations reps, possible paparazzi, and the other things that might pop up. It was going to be a wild ride and she needed to be prepared.



Tuesday arrived and Rio was dressed in the required jeans and SHIELD t-shirt for the night. The faculty were lined up on stage behind Lilia Calderu as she addressed the parents, guardians, and adults responsible for the various students of SHIELD before setting everyone free for the event. Calderu would dismiss the teachers to their classrooms first. She would then address the families separately before allowing them to roam the campus grounds.

“As a reminder, everyone has signed a set of iron-clad NDAs that covers everything that could possibly come up. These NDAs apply for students, all reps, agents, etc. We take privacy very seriously here at SHIELD and it’s one of the reasons we are the elite educational institution for the best and brightest in Hollywood and the upper class. While our teachers get to their classrooms…” That was their cue and the faculty turned and exited the stage.

Rio had spotted Agatha and Wanda as they entered earlier, and a few other now familiar faces. She waved to Alice as they split to go to their separate rooms. Rio left her door open, double checked that the slideshow was scrolling automatically and that the handouts were neatly organized. She took a deep breath and took her position at the front of the room to meet parents and students, just like she had every year at her previous school. With six different periods, she would be meeting a lot of students and parents over the next hour and a half.

The secretary’s voice came over the loudspeaker to announce that parents and students were being released to make their way around their respective buildings.

“Here we go” Rio whispered to herself as the first few parents started to filter in.

She shook hands, handed out information, made small talk, told students what they would need, and smiled.

Her smile widened when Wanda, Agatha, boys, and a man she assumed was Vision walked in.

“Rio Vidal, this is my husband, Dr. Vision Maximoff. He’s a professor at UCLA.” Wanda smiled brightly, though it did not reach her eyes.

“It’s so nice to meet you Dr. Maximoff, I’ve heard a lot about you.” Rio lied. Wanda’s smile melted into one of sincere appreciation that Vision didn’t see.

“Please, call me Vision. I’ve heard a lot about you from all the ladies in my life, and have a feeling you’ll soon be a fixture at the compound,” Vision replied with a grin.

The boys just groaned and Rio gave a polite chuckle. It seemed as though Wanda made sure to keep the little coven around at all times.

“Nicky and Tommy, it’s nice to see you both again. You must be Billy.” Rio shook each of their hands.

“Yes, hi Ms. Vidal.” He replied shyly. He was the kid who took after Vision. They spoke for a second before Vision and the boys moved to the next class. Wanda put her arm on Rio’s bicep and spoke softly.

“Hey, we’re meeting up with Jen, Nat, and Alice after this for a late dinner, you’re coming.”

It wasn’t a request.

Rio glanced at Agatha who was talking to Pepper Potts-Stark, who had a hand on Agatha’s arm. What was that story? Rio wondered.

“I rode with Alice and it sounds like I don’t have a choice.” She blew air out of her nose in amusement.

Wanda’s smile widened. “Glad we’re on the same page, see you later!” She turned and grabbed Agatha’s arm as she said goodbye to Pepper, and they moved to the next classroom.

Rio looked at the clock, noticing there was still another half hour to go.

⭐⭐⭐

The rest of the night was uneventful as Rio collected her belongings and walked out the door, hearing it click shut behind her as Alice walked toward her from down the hall.

“Hey, Wanda told me about dinner,” Rio said as her friend came up beside her.

‘Oh, yeah, it’s tradition. I was going to tell you when we were done.” Alice chewed nervously on her pinky nail. “We don’t have to go if you don’t want to though. It’s late and we have work. Plus Raven needs to be fed,” Alice blurted out quickly.

Rio turned her head and looked at Alice suspiciously as they walked out of the building.

“You let me take your car before anyone arrived so I could go feed her highness early, and it’s only 8:00. We’ll get home later than normal, but we have a late report time in the morning. Since when do you not…” Rio stopped as realization hit.

“Ohhhh! You haven’t talked to one Ms. Kale since you ghosted her Sunday morning! How many texts has she sent you? Calls? Voicemails? No. She’s not that type.”

Alice was at the car, glaring back at Rio.

Rio jogged the rest of the way and got in the vehicle. “Oh, Wanda is going to make the two of you sit next to each other!” She cackled as they got in the car.

“Well, we know where you’re sitting!” Alice shot back.

“Oh, I would sit in Agatha’s lap if it meant having a front-row seat to whatever this is about to be.” She replied, laughing harder.

When they arrived, just as expected, the only two seats that were left were one next to Jen and one next to Agatha, with Wanda directing each of them as to where to sit. It was an old-style diner, a burgers, and milkshakes type of place. Everyone placed their orders before delving into the conversation.

It was a round table so that everyone could see and speak to each other. Rio and Alice sat next to each other and could have switched places, but Wanda’s look said that they would not be doing that.

Wanda sat on the other side of Agatha, with Nat next to her.

Alice immediately dug her nails into Rio’s thigh and turned to talk to her.

Jen ignored Alice and spoke with Nat.

“Alice,” Rio whispered. “She is not happy with the way you left things. Talk to her, now.”

Jen looked across the table at Agatha, “I know you have a lot of press to run, a few projects in post, but I have a few — “

Agatha put up a hand to stop her. “No shop talk right now, and I’m with Rio.” She pointed at Alice. “Talk.”

Alice had turned to Nat and Wanda who had done the same thing and told her to talk to Jen.

The two women turned to each other with four sets of eyes on them. When Jen turned to look at everyone else, the other four started talking to each other. Jen turned back to Alice.

Alice took a deep breath, she had no idea what she was going to do or say. “I’m really sorry for how I left Sunday morning. I don’t know what got into me.”

I’m tired of running and I wanted to kiss you because I want more and I’m tired of pretending I’m okay with just being friends. She left that unspoken, not for here.

Jen almost always knew what she wanted out of her life. It’s not that she didn’t know what she wanted, it’s that she knew Alice was one of her closest friends. She cherished the times they had together. She was already worried she would lose Alice now that Rio was in town, she couldn’t risk dating her and them breaking up.

The fact remained that Jen wanted nothing more than to actually be with Alice.

She took a sip of her drink, her throat suddenly dry.

“I understand. And, about that, it was nice, but you know I’m not looking for anything serious right now.” Jen’s heart ached as she spoke the words, her eyes searching Alice’s to see how she took it. Maybe, just maybe, Alice would read Jen’s mind. You, I want you.

Alice looked down, not wanting Jen to see into her shattered soul. “Oh, right, I know, and you know I’m not either. How often have we had those talks with each other, right?” Alice tried to give a small laugh, but it fell flat. This bitch. “Just friends and occasional movie cuddle buddies?”

Something had flashed across Alice’s face, but it happened so quickly that Jennifer couldn’t quite tell what it was as she was clouded by her own hurt. “Of course, always.” She smiled and turned back to the group. “Yes, can we help you bunch of Nosey Nellie’s?” Jen asked.

Rio put a comforting hand on Alice’s leg.

“Oh, just a natural lull in conversation is all,” Agatha mused.

“For the duration of ours?” Jen asked dryly.

“Coincidence.” Agatha looked across at Nat and asked a random question, getting everyone to start talking again.



Wanda wanted to scream. Why would these idiots not let her push them together? Jen and Alice were the perfect balance for each other, and they may not see it, but Wanda knew there was love there.

Agatha and Rio were basically soulmates.

Idiots.

Wanda Maximoff was surrounded by relationship-impaired idiots, why wouldn’t they listen to her when she obviously had all the answers?



Nat bit back a laugh. She could tell Wanda was about to implode and it was adorable. She shook her head slightly, trying to shake the thought out of her head. Nat knew Wanda wanted everyone else to be happy in love, to live their happy ending, when Wanda herself was very much not.

Nat quickly squeezed Wanda’s thigh and watched the other redhead visibly relax.

Rio looked at Alice and Jen. Maybe they should focus on themselves instead of meddling with others. She glanced at Agatha. She couldn’t believe she was friends with this group of women, let alone Agatha.



Agatha looked at each woman one by one. Maybe if they would stop meddling and focus on themselves, they would figure out what they wanted.

Their food came and they chatted over current events and other celebrities while they ate. She and Rio had a private conversation about a television series that only they watched, agreeing that the background sapphic romance needed to take more screen time. Season three was set to premiere the following spring and they made plans to watch it together.

Agatha was planning to watch a show with someone months in advance. Like a friend . She told herself.

Wanda kept looking at her throughout their meal, the two stars had a way of speaking by just looking at each other.

Wanda darted her eyes at Rio. Take the leap, ask her out.

Agatha rolled her eyes. Take your own advice.

Wanda glared. It’s complicated and you know that.

Agatha sighed. We’re a mess, angel.

Wanda shrugged and nodded. At least we’ll always have each other Aggie.

Agatha nodded and winked. I love you.

Wanda smiled and looked down. I love you too.

Once in the car, Rio smacked Alice’s arm.

“You’re an idiot! Gran would smack you so hard your ancestors would have felt it!” Rio exclaimed.

“Ow! Good thing I hadn’t started driving yet!” Alice exclaimed as she started to drive.

“You like that beautiful woman and you two decided to just be friends. What is this? High school!? If it doesn’t work out, then it doesn’t work out and you act like adults. When we get home, you will be calling her and asking her out on a date. Do I need to be there to witness it? Because I will. Ten years, you useless lesbians.” She sighed and sunk back into her seat after her rant.

Alice stared out of the windshield, a sly smile on her face.

Alice was overjoyed to have her sister back in her life.

Jen was grateful to be in her car.  She made a quick escape, waving goodbye to her friends with a tight-lipped smile and practically running to the car before Wanda could get on to her.

Once in the car and on the road, she turned on the music and did something that Jennifer Kale did not do.

She cried.

⭐⭐⭐

Rio showered, and settled in bed with her bedtime playlist on. Raven curled up next to her when her phone dinged.

Agatha 💜

Well, at least Wanda is currently

focused on Alice and Jen. She’s

so mad.

Me

I noticed. I’m upset with Alice though.

She truly likes Jen. Maybe more?

She’s just worried they’ll lose the

friendship. I understand, but they’re

grown women. They’ll figure it

out. They’d be good for each

other.

Agatha 💜

What makes you so sure?

Me:

Because Alice Wu-(Gulliver)

doesn’t run when shit gets hard.

She also is as hard-headed

as Kale is. If anyone can handle

that bitch, it’s Alice.

Agatha 💜

Haha. You’re absolutely right.

Me:

Alice is going to ask her out.

Agatha 💜

You’ll be Wanda’s favorite.

Me:

Speaking of Wanda. I am not

an idiot. What’s up with her?

Me:

I mean, she’s so desperate for

everyone else to be happy.

She spends way more time

with you all, and especially Nat

than her husband…

Agatha 💜

That is her story to tell.

Not mine.

But you can’t just ask her either.

She has to tell you in her time.

You have to be patient.

Nat may tell you some of it first.

Don’t hold your breath though.

Me:

So there is something there.

Knew it. Everyone is queer

in this town, I swear.

Agatha 💜

I didn’t say that. However,

the queer part, not everyone, but

most.

Me:

I knew I had died and gone

to lesbian heaven.

Agatha 💜

Or hell, depending on who you

find.

Agatha 💜

Anyway, on Labor Day weekend, we usually

hang out at the beach house. I’m assuming

Alice hasn’t told you yet. It’s the group, the

boys, and Vision. Though the menfolk

generally go fishing during the day. Want

to join us?

Me:

I mean, Alice and I are as

attached as you and Wanda

are to each other, so yes.

Agatha 💜

You know, not offended by that at

all.

Me:

Good, you aren’t supposed to be.

Agatha 💜

It’s getting late and you have work

Tomorrow. Go to sleep. Goodnight, Rio.

Me:

Goodnight, Agatha.

Rio put her phone aside and smiled. She would tell Alice all about it in the morning. A weekend at Agatha’s beach house sounded like a lot of fun, even with three students. With this new group of friends, she had a feeling everything was going to be a lot different from now on.



Chapter End Notes

Thank you for the comments and the kudos! You can find me on tumblr at deathbylesbianwitches, or twitter and bluesky at agathalovesrio.

Sometimes I forget what handles I use where. It's cool. This story is currently in the forefront of my mind and I'm trying to get it out as fast as it happens in my brain. AuDHD and all.

I Could Make The Bad Boys Good For The Weekend

Chapter Summary

Alice calls Jen, school begins, and everyone heads to the beach house for Labor Day weekend.
Lilia provides more cryptic messages and a bit of counsel, and we see a little more meddling.

Chapter Notes

Agatha and Jen are back to bickering, and Alice and Jen decide to pick up the pace.

I have no idea how Hollywood works, so...go with me there. I know how teaching and schools work. Granted this is HIGHLY fictionalized. HIGHLY.

Y'all, this is when Dare2Dream83 called me out and now we're besties.
Everyone tell her thank you for cracking open my Neurospicy brain...

Updated 5/14/25

See the end of the chapter for more notes

🎶

Ain't it funny? Rumors fly

And I know you heard about me

So hey, let's be friends

I'm dying to see how this one ends

Blank Space - Taylor Swift

🎶

August - September

Jennifer Kale had cried for exactly two minutes. It had quite possibly been the most she had cried in ten years over something personal. She walked into her townhouse, locked her door, and began to go through her nighttime routine. She had a meticulous skincare regimen she followed. Alice constantly told her that she didn’t need it and that Jen’s skin was naturally perfect, but she wasn’t taking any chances.

Alice.

Jen huffed and stamped her foot in the empty bedroom, now wearing silk pajamas.

How dare Alice sneak out of here like a coward Sunday morning and then tell her they should just be friends? They had cuddled on that very couch multiple times and fallen asleep. Didn’t ALL sapphics with crushes on each other do that? Wasn’t that some unwritten law somewhere?

Didn’t Alice know she was supposed to stop Jen and say, “No. We will not be doing the just friends thing. We should be together.”

Jen should have known that Alice wasn’t going to do that. Alice was a protector, she defended. She didn’t charge in blindly.

Neither of them did. It’s why they had been in this predicament forever.

Jen had hoped that Rio's moving here would spur Alice to make a move. Alice talked about Rio constantly, about how Rio always gave her the courage to do things.

So much for that plan.

Jen rinsed and washed her face before rubbing in moisturizer when her phone rang. It was the distinct ringtone she had given to Alice.

She patted her face dry while reaching for the device.

A video call.

“Fine, Alice, I’ll play.”

She answered and immediately smiled upon seeing Alice wearing Jen’s University of Oregon shirt.

“Go on a date with me.” Alice blurted.

Jen couldn’t hide her shock as her mouth hung open. “W-what did you just say to me?”

“I like you, Jen, and I have for years. I know we don’t want to risk our friendship, but I can’t pretend anymore, okay? Go out with me. Saturday. There’s —”

“Yes. Saturday.” Jen cut her off with a wide smile, her eyes sparkling. She didn’t care where they went. She just knew it was about time.

⭐⭐⭐

The rest of the week flew by as Rio and Alice prepared for the first day of school on the upcoming Monday. Alice had asked Jen out, and they were scheduled to go out the upcoming Saturday. On a real date. Their friends were possibly more excited than they were. The Friday before, Agatha and Wanda met with Jen about offers.

Jen walked into Agatha’s house, where they often met. Her office was more for her to have phone calls and handle meetings with heads of studios and the like. When she dealt with her ‘talent’, which was only Agatha and Wanda, she did so at their houses, usually Agatha’s, and killed two birds with one stone.

She turned on her laptop and laid her folders down.“First, I would like to thank you both for recently making the PR side of my job easy. Agatha, you’ve been lying relatively low. A certain brunette keeping you from hoeing about?” She jabbed, “Wanda, you always do great.” She smiled at both of them, sincerely at Wanda, who smiled back from her normal spot as Agatha flipped her off from hers.

Jennifer rolled her eyes. “Real mature, Harkness. Anyway, you both have the press for your upcoming releases, various things in post, multiple commercial campaigns running, and awards season coming up. Also, Wanda, you, and Nat are leaving this afternoon for some things in New York this weekend. And... I have offers for you both.

Marvel is offering you roles, but as you know, Marvel comes with massive secrecy. You won’t even know what those roles are until after you talk with them and accept them. Disney, knowing how well you work together, also has opposite roles for both of you. Then there’s a TV show, Agatha, a comedy, something you’d be great at. Wanda, a movie for Netflix, definitely would be an award-winner. What do you think?”

They looked at each other and back at Jen.

“Yes.” They said in unison.

“Neither of you even knows the location, dates, anything.” She responded.

Agatha looked at her. “You didn’t provide any of that information, meaning you don’t have it.”

Jen sighed. “Caught me. We know Marvel doesn’t have it yet. Disney will work around Marvel, and we have to know those two to know about the third roles for each of you. We’re looking at the next few years being taken up by Marvel and Disney, with some smaller projects in between. I will get back to everyone, and we’ll start the processes.” She shot off the emails she had already drafted, put everything back in her bag, and looked at them. “Now, on to more important things. I have a date with Alice tomorrow, and I don’t know what to wear, and, oddly,  I’m nervous.”

Agatha was idly scrolling through her phone. “Just go back to her place, strip, and get it all out of both of your systems. You’ve been circling each other like horny lesbian sharks for years.” She responded.

Wanda’s jaw dropped. “AGATHA!” She gasped. “These are our friends, and we want them to be happy!”

Agatha looked up. “Getting laid makes me happy.”

Wanda smacked her on the leg and looked at Jen, who had an exasperated look on her face. “Ignoring this one, wear something comfortable. Where are you going?” She asked.

“Thank you, Wanda,” Jen said, shooting Agatha an exasperated look. “And thank you for absolutely nothing, Agatha.” Agatha shrugged. She was not the person to go to for dating advice. Sex advice, absolutely. Dating, absolutely not. Jen continued, “Lunch and a multi-cultural festival I wanted to go to that I mentioned a month or so back.” She responded with a slight smile.

Wanda squealed, “She listened and remembered! Okay, so comfortable shoes for walking, obviously, and then something comfortable for being outside in the heat. A sundress! Go with a sundress. Pink is your color, do that!” She was giddy and in her element. As much as she didn’t want her sons to grow up, she couldn’t wait to help them out with dates either.

Agatha groaned. “Don’t forget a hat so you don’t blind her.”

“Did you even brush your hair today? The iconic hair that you’re supposed to take meticulous care of?” Jen responded.

“Did you polish your head?” Agatha shot back. “And I do take care of my hair when necessary, thank you.”

“Will you two stop it? Agatha, you're just jealous because there are so many rules and stipulations with your hair and how iconic it is. The times you have been able to do anything with it, even that was meticulously curated and written into contracts and insurance policies.” Wanda turned to Jen, “She was in her late twenties and had to shave it for a role when she found out how terrible she looked bald. It took months just to greenlight the haircut and the movie. They almost had to get a body double just because it was taking so long.”

“I am not jealous,” Agatha mumbled. Much like other celebrities had iconic features, hers had been her hair since she was a kid, to the point where, like Taylor Swift’s legs, Mariah Carey’s vocal cords, and America Ferrera’s smile were insured, so had been her hair. It hadn’t been her idea, and it still wasn’t her favorite thing in the world. However, it made sense business-wise.

“You’re also jealous because I have a date, and you don’t,” Jen added.

“Oh, please. I could have any date I wanted.” She huffed, hating that they were teaming up against her.

“Stop it. Both of you.” Wanda warned, using her mom voice and glare. The other two women muttered as they changed the topic.

Agatha was slightly annoyed that Alice and Jennifer had figured it out right after deciding to remain friends. What was worse was that she knew Rio had been the one to make the final push. She had reminded herself that she and Rio had just met. Agatha Harkness possessed many things. However, patience was not one of them. She didn’t want to screw things up, and Rio was extremely young. Agatha was not entirely sure where this was coming from, and knew that remaining friends was the best course of action. Clearly, she was letting Wanda and her other friends get to her, and she needed to focus on her career. She needed to devote her time to preparing for her commitments and promoting her upcoming projects.

Agatha knew she was bored and that was all.

“...football starts up in September, so we have that, and then the premiere for Death’s Beloved in October.” Wanda looked over at Agatha.

Jen perked up. “This one has been promoted for months. There’s already award talk, as you both know. The theme song has been everywhere already.”

Wanda turned to Agatha with a wide grin, and Agatha could already see the wheels turning in her head.

“You should take Rio,” Wanda said excitedly.

Agatha shook her head. “No, I know this is not one for Nicky to attend, but I thought we’d go together since we played opposite each other.”

Jen held up a hand, eyes lighting up. “No, Wanda’s on to something. Wanda, people have been wondering about you and Vision.” She gave her a pointed look as Wanda looked away. “So you should probably walk the carpet with your husband. Unless you planned on walking it with someone else, that’s not Agatha?” Wanda shook her head.

“Right. So, go with your husband, and Agatha, take Rio. We’re not doing fake dating or any publicity stunts like that, so no one goes down that road, but it will be nice for people to see that you have other friends. I’ve been trying to get Alice to walk the carpet for years as the daughter of Lorna Wu, but she won’t touch it.”

Agatha glared at both of them. “I’ll think about it, and we’ll circle back. How’s that?”

Wanda nodded. “I’ll take it. Now, Jen, about your date tomorrow.” She wanted to change the subject as quickly as possible before this could turn back to her at all, just glad Nat wasn’t here.

⭐⭐⭐

Alice was nervous. It was Saturday, and she was finally going on a date with Jen. The events from the previous Tuesday continued to play in Alice’s head. She and Jen agreeing to be just friends, and Rio pushing Alice to call Jen an hour later.

Alice had been so afraid Jen would say no, but instead, she said yes before Alice even finished speaking. Apparently, Jen’s “I’m not looking for anything serious” was more of a defense mechanism than anything. So here Alice was, in her closet, with Rio sitting on her bed, telling her what to wear.

“Seriously, Alice, she’s going to think you’re beautiful no matter what you wear. She’s absolutely into you.” She said, popping a chip into her mouth.

“You didn’t even know we liked each other,” Alice replied, holding a black shirt up to herself before making a face and replacing it.

“Okay, true, but I just met everyone, and anytime I see the two of you together, Agatha is there. You try paying attention to anyone else when Agatha Harkness is standing RIGHT THERE.” She gestured with her hand to make the point.

Alice pulled on a pair of black shorts, moving so that Rio could see how they looked. “I do it all the time. Almost a decade, Ri.”

Rio nodded her approval of the shorts as the other woman disappeared back into the closet. “I applaud your strength. Yes, the orange halter. It honestly is your color. She was also apparently very close with your mother, found that out last weekend, and I’m new to the friend group. The Converse, the plain black ones. I’m surprised you didn’t see her more when you were younger. ” She added. The more Rio was learning, the more hurt she was becoming. How much longer could she tell herself that Alice was just doing what was best? “Wear your hair down, it’s so pretty like that. I’m so glad you didn’t do it all red.

Alice had never made dating a major priority in her life, and Rio wanted the best for her. The more Alice shared about Jen, the more it seemed like Jen would be a good fit for her. It helped that they had known each other for so long, and they already knew each other’s quirks and habits. Alice had been cautious of LA, anything to do with Hollywood, dating, and even drinking for a very long time. Rio had been the bad influence of the two. Anything that could take her down the path of Lorna Wu, she kept at arm’s length. Alice knew she had the same addictive personality as her mother did. She had to take things slowly and in moderation, integrating into the world of Hollywood, and wine nights, she was still careful with any type of medication, and with that, came dating.

“I’m happy for you, Alice. It’s going to go great. If you need any help with dating advice, well, there’s maybe Wanda?
