She seems to have good luck in that department, just not in the decision-making aspect of it. If you want to find yourself in a horrible situation though, I’m your girl.” Rio gave her the thumbs up as Alice came out and closed the door, showing off the completed look.

“That was not your fault. You had no idea ANY of that was going to happen. Now, wish me luck, and text Agatha to see what she’s doing tonight.” Alice winked.

Rio laughed and hugged her tightly.

“It’s going to work out. She’s already wrapped around you like that finger trap that got stuck on your finger when we were fifteen and drunk.” Rio whispered before she let Alice go and left to go back to her own apartment.

“It was Lunar New Year, we had too much sake, and it was made for toddlers!” Alice said after her.

“Then drink some sake!”



Rio stared at her phone as she thought about texting Agatha. “Friends hang out with each other. If Alice wasn’t going out with Jen, we would do something tonight.” She said, looking over at the cat, who had jumped up on the couch with her.

Me:

Hey, what are you doing this evening?

She hesitated before sending it and looked over at Raven. “Alice won’t come back and want to go over everything, right? Surely they’ll hang out all night, right?” Raven gave a soft meow before climbing into her lap. “Right.” She pressed send and automatically saw that Agatha was typing a response.

Agatha 💜

Nothing, actually. I don’t expect Alice and Jen’s

date to end, nor do you, I assume. 2/3 boys have

a football weekend retreat thing. Vision and Billy

went somewhere on some nerd thing with Vision’s

best friend, Herb. Wanda and Nat are at some press

thing this weekend for something Wanda did.

I’m solo.

Rio looked down at the phone. She hadn’t changed Agatha’s contact from what Alice and crew had entered, so the purple heart had stayed. She wondered what they had put in Agatha’s phone. She thought for a moment and looked down at Raven. “You might meet a new friend today, old woman. Hopefully, you like her.”

Me:

Sounds boring, but same. Want

to come over and be bored together?

I’ll even make dinner.

Agatha 💜

I’d love to. 5 good?

Me:

Yes. Are you allergic to cats?

Agatha 💜

I was going to make a joke,

but I know you mean the

actual animal. No, I’m not.

Rio laughed, disturbing Raven and causing her to jump up and run off before sending her a picture of the cat in question.

Me:

This is Raven. She may be older

than God herself.

Agatha 💜

I will see you both later.

Rio placed her phone on the coffee table and stood up to make sure her apartment was tidied up. Rio kept it clean but didn’t want any dishes in the sink or accidental underwear on the floor. She scooped out the cat box, took out the trash, double-checked that the bathroom was clean, and all the usual ‘someone other than my sister is coming over’ chores were done. She looked in the fridge to see what she had on hand and decided on a chicken and bacon pasta from scratch. It was simple but with a homemade sauce, so somewhat impressive. She was wearing normal lazy Saturday clothes: black sweats and a green tank, with hair clipped up, and barefoot. She wasn’t going to bother changing as she saw no need. It wasn’t like she was trying to impress Agatha. She wasn’t. She fed Raven in hopes that she’d leave them alone while they ate, knowing full well that she wouldn’t. She already had music playing as she couldn’t stand silence and always had something playing. Currently, it was some random Spotify mix. She looked at her phone as it dinged, seeing a text from Alice.

Sister Alice

The date’s going well. We’ll probably

grab dinner and then I think we’re going

to see a movie and maybe back to my place.

Don’t come over. I will check in later. Love you!

Rio grinned, she was genuinely happy for her friend and so glad she pushed her to do this. Meddling was good sometimes only when she did it, though.

Me:

YAY! I won’t bother you. Tell Jen

I said hi. Agatha is coming over.

Dinner and movies or whatever.

Love you too.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha had packed a bag, not because she thought anything would happen, but because she wanted to be comfortable at Rio’s house. She was used to having to look a certain way when she was out during the day, which was why she wore jeans and a simple black top. Nice enough to be photographed if she were seen between the vehicle and Rio’s building. She had packed sweats and a UCLA shirt to change into later. She was glad Rio had texted her, although honestly, Agatha had been about ten minutes away from texting her new friend herself. It was the weekend before school started, and she wanted to spend time together before she was buried in work this week.

Rio had sent her the address, though she already knew where she lived, having been to Alice’s plenty of times over the years. Her thoughts wandered to her friends in New York, wanting to check on them, but knowing it was best to leave them alone when they were on their own. Agatha sighed at the thought of Wanda and that whole…thing. She knew Wanda would always do what Wanda felt was best for the boys, and that she and Vision loved each other in their way. It was a messy situation, and she needed Wanda to hurry the hell up and fill Rio in so she had someone to talk to about it.

Agatha shook the thoughts away as the elevator doors opened on Rio’s floor and walked to the door. She had been let into the building easily. The doormen were all the same and knew her, even if they hadn’t, no one told Agatha Harkness no. She was one of the most recognizable people in the world. She knocked and wasn’t surprised when the door opened almost immediately.

“Hey,” Rio said, standing aside to let her in. “Make yourself at home, put your bag wherever. What do you want to drink?”

“Hi,” She replied, not setting her bag down yet. Eyes landing on what looked like something fruity. “Whatever you’re drinking is fine, and where’s your bathroom? I want to change. I hate having to look put together all the time.” She grimaced.

Rio laughed. “I have a rum and pineapple, and there’s one down the hall on the right. I completely get it. I’m not ready to wear teacher clothes every day. I’ll grab our food. We’re even eating at the table. Like adults!” She responded.

“Oh goody!” She said sarcastically as she headed down the hall.

When she returned a few minutes later, she set her bag behind the couch and joined Rio at the table. “You cook?”

“Alice and I both do. Gran made sure of it. I had taught myself growing up anyway, but Gran honed my skill.” She answered as they both started eating.

Agatha hummed in satisfaction, “You did a great job. I do okay, but this is amazing. If that whole teaching thing doesn’t pan out, you’ve got a backup skill to fall back on.” Agatha downplayed her own skills. She had been taught to cook by two women who believed that she should know how to cook for herself. One of those people had learned from the same person Rio had.

Rio’s cheeks tinged a light pink. “You’re not the first one to say that.” Her eyes darted to Agatha’s feet. “I apologize if Raven tries to steal your food.”

Agatha raised an eyebrow, “The cat? I haven’t even…” She was cut off by a feline intruder jumping into her lap. “...and you must be Raven. Sorry, but I am not sharing with you.” She said as she put her fork down and removed the cat from her lap, only for Raven to jump into the other empty lap and be dismissed for a second time.

Rio shook her head. “I promise I feed her, do not listen to her lies.”

“I’ve never seen a cat beg for human food before,” Agatha remarked, watching as the cat sat between the two chairs, green eyes watching them both.

“Oh, that’s not a cat, as I said before, that is an immortal familiar.” Rio finished her food, putting her fork down, watching Agatha.

Agatha looked at her and down at her plate. “Do you even taste your food? We’ve been sitting for five minutes.”

“That was me tasting my food and eating slowly.” She replied.

Agatha shook her head and gestured ahead with her fork. “Well, do continue about Raven.”

“Alice and I adopted her when we got our first place in college. Alice is more of a dog person, but she humored me. We were at the shelter, about to adopt another cat, when a kitten was meowing incessantly. Suddenly, this mostly black paw shot out of the adjacent kennel, attempting to smack the kitten to get it to stop, and all we saw were these shiny green eyes. I swear to this day that cat was not there. The shelter didn’t even have the paperwork for her and made up some random name, and I’m pretty sure falsified information when we decided that we obviously had to have the magical mystery cat. That was nearly sixteen years ago. They guessed she was around two, though not a single vet has been able to age her properly. On paper, she’s eighteen, but in reality? No idea. In my heart? At least a thousand.” Rio smiled affectionately at the aged feline, now more brown and orange than black as she once had been. She turned her attention back to Agatha, passion in her voice as she continued speaking about her cat.

“Shortly after Norma died, when Alice moved out here and I moved to a different place of my own for the first time, Raven disappeared for six weeks. I thought she was gone, that I had lost her, too.” Rio’s smile faltered, something dark sliding over her eyes before it left just as quickly as it had appeared. “Thankfully, she returned without a scratch, no parasites, nothing. A little thin and in need of a bath, which she hated. The vet was mystified. I’ve also seen her chase off predators, hunt things much bigger than her, all kinds of unusual things. She’s basically a friend-shaped Eldritch creature.” Raven now perched in Rio’s lap, eyeing Agatha, as Rio absentmindedly scratched her head.

Agatha looked at the animal, having finished her dinner, and now leaned forward with her chin propped on her fist. “You are one interesting cat.” She noted, as the cat in question judged her. She turned back to Rio. “Why do I feel like she’s staring into my soul?”

“Because she is. Seconds?” Rio stood and took their plates.

“No, thank you. That was great. Let me help.” Agatha stood, collected their empty glasses, and followed Rio into the kitchen. As she helped clean up, she noticed how intently Raven watched her. Agatha had never felt judged by a cat before.

“I feel like she’s sizing me up.” Agatha finally said as they finished cleaning up, putting leftovers away, and refreshing their drinks.

“She does that.” Was all Rio said as she walked back to the living room, and they both sat on the couch. “Have you told anyone that I’m going to the beach house yet?”

“No, have you?” Agatha turned toward Rio.

“No. No particular reason. I just haven’t shared it with Alice yet. She’s been so excited about this date, and I keep forgetting.”

“Oh, Jen has been, too, and Wanda, of course.” Agatha shook her head.

“Movie?” Rio asked, changing the subject before asking questions she knew Agatha would not give her the answers to. She tried to think of a movie that wasn’t an Agatha or Wanda film, she was in the mood to watch.

“Yes. Want to watch one we’ve probably both seen a million times and make commentary?” Agatha asked with a sly grin. “It’s one of my favorite things to do.”

“If we can find one, I’m not sure I’ve seen that many black and white movies.” Rio joked.

Agatha hit her with a pillow, smirking at the ‘Live Laugh Lesbian’ saying. “We can watch another Disney or PG one, child.”

Rio looked to the ceiling and poked her tongue into the side of her cheek. “Okay, point taken. What about the lesbian classic D.E.B.S?”

“The extremely cheesy spy thriller with a killer 80s soundtrack?” Agatha asked with a knowing smirk.

“The one and only,” Rio answered, already searching through her various subscription services for the movie.

“Oh, absolutely, but only if we turn it into a sing-along.”

They shared sarcastic comments and sang along with exaggerated movements, noting the various tropes of the movie. Agatha pointed out different people she knew, including those she knew.

“How do you even keep track, or remember?” Rio shook her head, laughing.

“A gentle sapphic never forgets, lest she repeats.”

Rio scoffed. “How many little black books do you have?”

“A small library full,” Agatha winked at Rio scandalously.

Rio rolled her eyes and shook her head again, saying nothing.

Agatha’s face softened as the credits began to roll. “Does it bother you?” Her voice was softer now, quieter. Agatha was not ashamed of her decades of conquests and dalliances. It was part of who she was, and it was widely known. Yet she found herself somewhat self-conscious of it for the first time since she was thirteen.

Rio tilted her head to the side and thought about it. This was Agatha Harkness. Quite possibly the most respectful ‘playboy’ to roam the Earth. She knew women who had encountered her. You couldn’t live in the state of California and not know at least one. Agatha was legendary in terms of respect, boundaries, and aftercare. She wasn’t going to call you back, and lightning never struck twice, but for someone with her reputation, it was a respectful one.

“No. It would if you were arrogant in the way men are, or if there were rumors of you treating women horribly, but no. Besides, everyone has hobbies.” Rio gestured around her apartment at her plants.

For some reason, Rio Vidal’s approval was the validation that Agatha needed at that moment. Not that it mattered, it’s not like they would ever be doing anything. Rio was a friend, not food.

Agatha had only really sought the validation of five people in her life, not counting the public as a whole. Two of those people were dead, one was no longer in her life, one was a middle-aged redhead, and the other was a teenage boy.

None of them were Rio Vidal, yet here she was, overcome with warmth from knowing that the beautiful, friend , woman next to her, did not see her as less than, or a horrible person for her choice of extracurricular activities.

The next movie started automatically while they talked and laughed late into the night. Rio looked over at Agatha when the older woman’s voice cut off mid-sentence to see that she had fallen asleep. Rio looked at her for a moment, taking in Agatha’s sleeping form, now in sweats and an oversized UCLA t-shirt. It was a deep-set couch, one that Rio had spent many nights on when her bed felt too big and empty. She glanced toward her room and back to Agatha.

Rio had a decision to make. She groaned as she stood and stretched. She glanced down at Agatha and again in the direction of her room as she tidied the living room and turned off any remaining lights.

Rio went to her bathroom and brushed her teeth, trying not to think about Agatha sound asleep on her couch. The couch that could, in theory, safely fit both of them. Innocently, as friends. She walked back through her room and picked up a blanket to drape over her sleeping friend as she cast a cynical look at her bed. “Oh, what, like you have any room to judge. You’re not comforting.” She scoffed at the offending piece of furniture as she went back to the couch, having decided on her fate.

Rio curled up in a ball opposite Agatha and draped the blanket over them. They weren’t cuddling, they weren’t even touching—just two friends who had fallen asleep on the same piece of furniture together. Rio had a feeling this was a normal occurrence in this co-dependent coven. She settled in, whispered goodnight, and went to sleep.

Raven hopped up on the couch and weighed her options before climbing on top of Agatha and falling asleep.

For the second Sunday morning in a row, the early morning sunlight filtered in and gently cast its light across Rio and Agatha, this time asleep on the same couch, legs intertwined.

Alice burst through the door barefoot, hair askew,  wearing nothing but an orange sports bra and Garfield boxers. She pounced on Rio and planted a giant kiss on her cheek.

“I love you and am forever indebted to you. Good morning, Aggie.” Alice grinned, quickly disappearing. “Enjoy your morning!” She called as the door shut behind her.

Agatha was still half asleep, but mumbled. “Was that her thanking you because they fucked?”

Rio sleepily shook her head. “No. Knowing my sister, they decided to date, finally kissed, Jen spent the night, and they woke up holding each other. Lesbians. It was her thank you for that.”

“Ah,” Agatha clicked her tongue, eyes still closed. “She’s going to text their group chat about this, isn’t she?”

“Yep,” Rio popped the p, shifting under the covers. “Wanda will be insufferable this week.”

Agatha sighed. “I’m going back to sleep.”

It wasn’t until after Agatha had left and Alice had come over to tell Rio about her date that Rio finally let herself process last night’s sleeping situation.

“Our legs were all wrapped together, Alice!” Rio exclaimed, making some complicated motions using both arms.

“Okay, we’ve slept like that, no big deal,” Alice shrugged as she put the final touches on a seating chart.

“Yes, but you’re my sister, and while gorgeous, you’re not Mother Harkness,” Rio referred to Agatha by her Instagram handle and what she was commonly referred to by the lesbian community.

Alice didn’t take offense as she knew exactly what Rio meant. “Knew you still had that crush, Ms. ‘We’re just friends’. Besides, you’re the one who made the very conscious decision to sleep on the couch.”

“We are just friends, which might complicate things. I knew I shouldn’t have told you I chose to sleep on the couch.” She grimaced. “No, she’s the parent of a student.”

“So, you’re both tactile and you like to cuddle. It’s cute, even if you don’t end up together. You have another platonic cuddle buddy.” Alice tried to spin it in a positive light.

Rio just sighed. “Meanwhile, you’ve found yourself a whole-ass wife,” she grinned.

“No one said anything about any of that.” Alice glared back. “We are taking things slow, still painfully so. We are officially dating though, and it’s about fucking time.” Their whole relationship had been based on caution, why not remain at that speed? Alice sighed. At this rate, Agatha and Rio would be grandparents, and Wanda would figure out her life by the time she and Jen even moved in together.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha had been waiting for Wanda to return from her trip. Agatha had told Wanda that she was to come to Agatha’s immediately.

Agatha had just finished her fourth set of Pilates when the non-lethal redhead finally came in the door.

“I had Nat take my stuff to the house. What’s wrong? You wouldn’t tell me, is everything okay?” Wanda asked, eyes searching the older woman’s face.

“We fell asleep on the couch, our legs were all,” she gestured with her arms to mimic their legs, “when we woke up, and it just felt natural. I don’t cuddle with just anyone.” Agatha plopped on the couch, running out of energy, as the words flowed forth.

Wanda tried to control her excitement and not overreact. Agatha was right. She didn’t spend the night with people. She was tactile with very few, the core group being it, and even then, mainly Wanda. “How did Rio react?” She asked cautiously.

Agatha shrugged. “We both acted like nothing happened.”

Wanda mimicked Agatha’s plop and looked over at her. “A bunch of useless lesbians, I swear.” She sighed.

“The worst.” Agatha agreed. “She’s fun, attractive, smart, I can tell her anything.” She shook her head. “But, no. I will not be dating my son’s teachers...anymore.”

“What a great time to up your standards,” Wanda remarked. “You didn’t date them in the first place, nor do you date. You won’t even mention how you’re feeling about the intimacy of it all.” She studied Agatha’s face for any signs of how she felt, and while she saw a few twitches, Agatha’s multitudes of awards weren’t for nothing.

Where Wanda had to put forth the effort to keep her emotions from being written all over her face, Agatha had been born with a resting bitch face.

“I will not be doing that at this moment. We are friends and nothing more.” Agatha replied.

Wanda shook her head. After nearly three decades with Agatha, this didn’t surprise her. “Do you need a shovel to bury those feelings?” She asked.

Agatha scoffed. “Can I borrow yours?” She asked.

“I hate you.” Wanda smiled.

“You love me,” Agatha responded.

⭐⭐⭐

The first day of school started with a school-wide meeting before students arrived. Lilia looked at the faculty and staff and reminded everyone, once again, of the NDAs that had been signed, their unusual student body and parents, the school’s mission, and so forth.

Rio was beginning to detest the term.

“In conclusion,” Lilia had said, “we are not just working with parents, but nannies, managers, agents, PR, etc. You will develop all types of relationships. We are all adults here, and we sign NDAs for a reason. I have eyes and ears everywhere. I know what goes on. Act like adults and keep things out of the public eye, or at least let the reps handle it how they need to. Think logically and do not involve students in their parents’ affairs. While a few relationships have worked out successfully in the past, many have not. Be mindful of that.” She dismissed them all to get ready for the students to show up. It was the same speech every year, though it hit harder this year for a large population of the faculty.

Lilia pulled Alice aside with a knowing smile. “Remember what I’ve told you, while Jen’s not a parent, it could still affect things. I’m happy for you, but you are both hard-headed and stubborn.” She squeezed Alice’s arm and walked toward where Rio spoke with another science teacher.

Lilia pulled Rio aside, a hand on her shoulder, “May and December are not that much different,” then the older woman walked away without another word.

“What the fuck does that mean?” Rio asked as Alice came up beside her.

“I don’t understand half of what that woman says, and I’ve been here for ten years,” Alice replied. “Oh well, off to start the day.”

“Oh! Alice, I forgot to tell you. I’m going with you to the beach weekend! Happy first day of school!”

⭐⭐⭐

Wanda was easily the most excited about Rio joining them for the upcoming holiday weekend. However, she was also perplexed as she sat in her living room the last week of August with Jen and Nat. Alice and Rio were teaching, and Agatha was at a press interview.

“Six bedrooms at the beach house. The boys will have the bunkroom, which isn’t a bunkroom anymore, but they’ll want to all stay together. Vision and I.” Her eyes darted down as she said it, hands fidgeting.

Jen had been looking at her phone, but Nat caught her look and lightly brushed her fingertips down her arm in comfort, eliciting a tense smile from Wanda before she continued her bedroom planning.

“Nat, you have your room. Jen, do you and Alice want to share a room?” She asked.

Jen’s eyes shot up. “Umm, we’re not..but yes.”

Wanda’s smile widened slightly, happy someone had figured it out at least. “Finally. That leaves Agatha in her room and…damn, that’s just enough rooms with no way to force her and Rio to share a room.” Wanda had been hoping to force Rio and Agatha to room together, but unfortunately, she and Agatha had bought a large house all those years ago.

Jen shrugged. “They’ve had four movie nights, we’ve been present at two of them, and they usually end up falling asleep in the same room or on each other anyway. They’re comfortable together and have barely known each other for a month.” She reminded her.

“That’s like a year in lesbian time!” Wanda whined.

Nat fully put her hand on Wanda’s arm, looking at her softly. That look conveyed more than Nat’s words did. “I know you want Aggie to find someone, but give it a bit more time, okay?”

Wanda nodded. Jen looked from Nat to Wanda and back. “If you try to force Agatha too much, she’ll rebel,” Jen added.

“I agree with Jen,” Nat stared at Wanda. “You know she’s right.”

Wanda nodded. “Fine, but that doesn’t mean I’ll stop nudging.” She chewed on her lip and looked at Nat. “I want her to have…to be…” She whispered.

Jen watched the interaction between Wanda and Nat carefully. Wanda was quickly spiraling, and Jen knew it was only a matter of time. She jotted down a few notes and sighed. “I’m going to head to the office. I have a few contract negotiations to handle. Have a good afternoon.” She waved and left.

Upon hearing the door click behind Jen, Nat pulled Wanda into her, an arm around her waist. Wanda laid her head on Nat’s shoulder, eyes darting to the clock to gauge how much time they had. Nat pressed her lips to Wanda’s temple.

“Talk to me dragostea mea, my love,” Nat whispered.

Wanda relaxed into Nat, careful to watch the time. Always aware of the time. “I don’t know how much longer I can do this, Natasha. Something has to change, and I still don’t know what to do.” Her fingers drew circles on Nat’s arm as she tried to keep her breathing steady. This was not a new conversation for them, and it was becoming more frequent.



Nat caressed Wanda’s cheek, her brows furrowed. Nat would always respect Wanda’s decisions, even if they did rip at her heart. “Have you talked to Agatha? Told her where you’re at?”

Wanda shook her head. “You know what she would say, and my therapist isn’t helpful, and no, I haven’t talked to anyone else. Everyone’s too close to us, Nat. They will all say the same thing.”

Nat knew they would. She was selfishly counting on it.

Nat curled her index finger under Wanda’s chin and tilted it up, their eyes meeting. “Sunshine, I…” She was cut off when they heard the front door open, and they flew apart.

“Afternoon, Darling!” Vision called, ruining the moment.

⭐⭐⭐

The first few weeks of school had gone well. Rio was getting to know her students well and building relationships with them and the other parents. So far, no shenanigans had been started by Nicky or Tommy, and Billy was a great kid. Labor Day weekend had finally arrived, and she was excited to go on this little trip with her friends, something she hadn’t had with any of her friends in San Francisco. She enjoyed their weekend movie nights, no matter who joined, dinners with Alice during the week, and Jennifer, who was starting to dine with them too. Rio knew that this weekend at the Wagatha beach house was going to be both memorable and fun. They were all headed up to Malibu that evening and would return Monday afternoon.

As the final bell rang, Rio grabbed her things and met Alice in the hall as they walked towards her car, as it had been Rio’s week to drive.

“Are you excited to stay in the massive beach house all weekend?” Alice asked, knocking her shoulder into Rio’s.

“That’s not what I’m excited about. I’m excited to spend time with this forged family you’ve roped me into.” Rio responded with a grin. It was true. That’s what this felt like.

She and Alice had always been family, but she was happy it had expanded.

Alice smiled back. “It’s always a fun weekend. You’re going to love it.”

They stopped by the apartment to change and load the car. Rio set everything out for Raven, who was being taken care of by Morgan Stark, Pepper, and Tony’s daughter. Though she didn’t know Morgan all that well yet, Agatha had said that she was very trustworthy, even if Tony was her father. There was apparently history there.

Rio trusted Agatha.

“Raven, be good for Morgan, okay?” Raven yawned in return.

As they drove toward Malibu, Rio scrolled through socials and noticed several posts on Agatha’s subreddit mentioning how no one had seen her out. She closed out of Reddit and opened her music app.

“Is it weird that Agatha hasn’t been out and about? She hasn’t gone out partying, hasn’t been seen making poor decisions, none of the normal Agatha Harkness behavior.” Rio asked casually as she scrolled through the ‘Beach Weekend’ playlist she had made.

Alice shrugged. “She goes through periods, especially as Nicky gets older. Maybe she’s deciding to tone it down, finally. Or she’s just gone through all of Hollywood and doesn’t want to corrupt all of the newer talent.”

“I don’t know. I know it hasn’t been that long, but Reddit is noticing, and it won’t be long before the rest of social media and the media in general start picking up on it soon.” Rio mused. No, it wasn’t odd for people to change, but Agatha had been the same for decades.

However, the more Rio learned about Agatha over the past few weeks, the more she saw her for who she was. This would be a major public persona change, and people would notice.

While it didn’t bother Rio, she noticed the change.

Alice was quick to change the subject. “Jen and I are sharing a room.”

“Of course you are. I’d be more surprised if you weren’t.” Rio responded with a smile. “Just, don’t be too loud.”

“Please. We are not there yet.” Not that she didn’t want to be, but they weren’t rushing anything, especially not at beach weekend. It was like having sex while on vacation with your parents.

“Whatever you say. You’re already going painfully slow for lesbians.” Rio reminded her.

“And you’re not? At this point, Jen and I should have kids, you and Agatha should have U-Hauled, and Nat and Wanda...well. That’s…” She trailed off.

“Am I the ONLY PERSON that doesn’t know that story?” She asked, frustrated. “I mean, obviously, something is going on. I am a lesbian with eyes.”



“Nat, Wanda, and Agatha are the only ones who know everything,” Alice looked over at Rio for a moment as she continued down the highway. “The boys have no idea, I don’t think. I’m not sure how much Vision really knows. If it’s just that his wife isn’t completely honest with him, or if there’s an unspoken agreement. Jen and I don’t know everything, just what we’ve seen. There’s something there, though. I think the whole Wagatha thing is to keep the public from figuring out what’s truly going on.” Alice shook her head, a slightly sad expression on her face. “They’ll share when they’re ready if they ever are. For now, Nat protects both of them, and they all have a dynamic that works,” she finished.

Rio pouted a bit before letting out a resigned sigh. “It’s starting to make sense why Wanda is so invested in wanting everyone else around her to be happy when she clearly isn’t.”

⭐⭐⭐

Dinner had been eaten, rooms sorted, and luggage settled. They were on the beach around a bonfire. Tommy and Nicky were throwing a football around; Vision, Billy, Nat, and Wanda were pointing out stars and constellations to each other; Alice and Jen were catching each other up on the week, and Rio was taking it all in. Agatha came up next to her and handed her a bottle of some mixed alcohol, and sat next to her. Agatha wore shorts over a swimsuit with her hair down. Rio, dressed similarly, nodded her thanks.

Without thinking, Rio automatically remarked. “I’ve noticed your weekends haven’t exactly been your usual style recently.” She flinched as she said it.

Agatha just gave a wry smile. “Even Hollywood bad boys are allowed to take a break. Besides, Jen needed a break. She was about to have a stroke spinning everything and cleaning up my messes.”

Rio nodded. “I can only imagine. The woman could be a fixer for the mafia at this point.”

“I’m not that bad,” Agatha responded with a huff.

“Depending on which website you read.” Rio smiled.

“Which ones are you reading?” Agatha asked, meeting Rio’s eyes.

Brown eyes met blue, really seeing Agatha. “I don’t believe everything I read.” She replied softly.

“Oh? And what do you believe?” Agatha asked, the noise around her fading. All she was focused on was Rio’s answer. Sometime in the last month, this woman’s opinion had become almost as important to her as Wanda’s. It was something she couldn’t quite put into words yet, but the gravity of it was not lost on her. She had stopped truly caring about what the public thought of her personal life, relationship-wise, years ago. Yet here she was staring into the eyes of this younger woman who had captivated her since the moment they’d been re-introduced, hanging on her every word, finding that the next few minutes were either going to destroy her or give her a hope that she hadn’t had in decades.

“I see you, Agatha,” Rio responded and was about to continue when a shout interrupted the moment as a redhead crashed into them, catching a rogue football.

Agatha swore. She could murder her son.

“Sorry, Mom! Sorry, Rio!” Nicky shouted. They had all agreed that the boys could call Rio by her first name when they weren’t at school, just like they did with Alice.

Nat rolled off both of them. “Sorry. I didn’t think either of you wanted a black eye.”

“There are worse ways to go than death by redhead, Natasha. It’s fine.” Rio replied, standing up, her drink spilled all over her, and the moment was lost. “Thank you for the save.”

Agatha had fixed the expression on her face and mumbled her thanks as well.

“I think I’m going to go shower and head to bed,” Agatha said, standing and heading off.

Several others also took that moment to excuse themselves and call it a night.

Rio wasn’t exactly sure what she had been about to say, but she had done a lot of thinking about this developing friendship and Agatha herself in the past few weeks, telling herself that Agatha’s behavioral changes had absolutely nothing to do with her.

Returning to her assigned guest room, Rio readied herself for bed. She showered and lay down in the luxuriously comfortable bed, alone.  It shouldn’t have bothered her, but she knew what the odd energetic feeling in her chest was. It’s just a crush, she told herself. Nothing to get that worked up over, and nothing to be done about.

I don’t date parents, her thoughts continued as she let sleep overcome her, even if they’re her…

Chapter End Notes

Thank you for the kudos and comments! They are really helping a lot!

This thing is evolving, a lot, thanks brain! While Agatha and Rio remain the main relationship, the Wandanat of it all is going to be explored more as well. Since Agatha was very much "I'm here and queer" from a young age, Wanda is a pathological people pleaser. We'll delve more into that in later chapters.

Jen and Alice said "fuck it, we're doing this."

I'll Be Yours For The Weekend

Chapter Summary

The Labor Day Weekend trip and more insight into Nat and Wanda's relationship.
Agatha and Rio grow closer, but still not.

Chapter Notes

Thank you so much to my lovely Beta! She's been helping me flesh things out and reminding me that people cannot read my mind.

Anyway, we're approaching fall in fic-time where we will see more of the celebrity side of things.

Updated 5/21/25!

See the end of the chapter for more notes

🎶

And the heart I know I'm breakin' is my own

To leave the warmest bed I've ever known

We could call it even

Even though I'm leavin'

And I'll be yours for the weekend

Tis The Damn Season - Taylor Swift

🎶

Labor Day Weekend

It was early. The sun had yet to rise, and the two redheads were only up because they had gone for a run on the beach, their daily runs something they had established when they were eighteen . They sat on one of the back decks looking out over the ocean, drinking coffee and enjoying the cool morning breeze. Wanda enjoyed these mornings with Natasha when they could be alone. Their chairs were pushed together, and they knew no one else would be up for at least another hour. They still wouldn’t risk it and would part ways to shower shortly after finishing their coffee, but they enjoyed the fleeting moment.

Wanda held Nat’s hand and ran her thumb over her knuckles. She was careful, knowing that her husband and sons were in the house behind them. She wasn’t worried so much about Vision. It was the boys. She sighed as she dropped the other woman’s hand and wrapped it around her coffee mug. “Jen and Alice have gotten their shit together, it seems.” Wanda let her native accent slip through.

Nat nodded. “Thanks to Rio for giving the final push. That only took ten years.” She set her empty mug down and placed her hand on Wanda’s thigh.

Wanda nodded, “Now for the other two. What else can we do? How can we get them into a one-bed situation? Stranded? Forced proximity? Jen already nixed fake dating.” She rattled off.

“Why don’t we just give it time, sunshine? They’re just getting to know each other and are already draw n to each other. It’s going to work out.” Nat replied. She felt bad for breaking up their moment last night, and at least wanted to back off for the weekend.

“If I can’t be completely happy, everyone else is going to be, fuck.” Wanda bit back, bitterly, fire in her eyes. The more time passed, the worse things got for her situation, it seemed. No amount of stardom, therapy, or money could fix that. She had traded her soul and happiness for this life.

“Hey,” Nat knew Wanda was unraveling, and until Wanda made a decision, it was better to validate and change the subject. “You fixed the football schedule to where they’re working the concession stand together for a quarter of each home game, Jen will make it so Rio goes to the premiere as her date, we won’t always be at movie nights , you’re going to plant it in Agatha’s head to take Rio to SNL when she hosts, AND the Halloween party. That’s a lot of forced proximity.” She pointed out, her own accent rather prominent. Dropping their American accents around each other was something they had been doing since they were teenagers , another layer of intimacy between them.

Their accents would slip through occasionally with the others, but not like this.

Wanda nodded. “I know. I’m just worried that we’re going to be traveling a lot soon, and they won’t be together as much.”

A grin formed on the former assassin's lips as she turned to look at Wanda. “Then you either get Agatha to send her flowers, or you do it. Or plants, we know Rio has a green thumb. Talk to Alice. You also make sure she’s calling, texting, whatever, daily. Since Rio is here now, that means we have Alice and Rio join us for the holidays, and if, for some idiot reason, they STILL aren’t together by winter, we send them up to the cabin together, and all make excuses as to why the rest of us can’t go. Manifest a blizzard. Oh! Rio loves music! Get Agatha to take her to some concerts, pull some strings. You’re crafty, Wanda. We’ll figure it out, together, like we always do. I’m going to shower before everyone wakes up.” Nat stood and kissed Wanda on the crown of her head, deliberately ignoring the cameras on the perimeter of the house, before walking inside.

Wanda closed her eyes and sighed as she went to take their cups inside and get ready for the day herself, focusing on the plan she had previously come up with.

As everyone else woke up for the day, they gathered in the kitchen, various adults making breakfast. Vision and the boys would be taking the boat out to go fishing, Wanda decided that she, Nat, Jen, and Alice would go into town, and she suggested that Rio and Agatha remain at the house.

“Why, exactly, should we stay at the house?” Agatha asked, eyebrows high on her forehead.

“Someone needs to stay here in case something happens to us on the boat. Someone always stays back.” Vision piped up as he loaded a cooler. He knew his wife well and knew when she was meddling.

Agatha glanced over at him. “That’s why you have someone going with you, we have a guy at the house, and what the actual Coast Guard is for.”

“I would prefer to stay at the house, but you don’t have to stay with me. I have been told that I’m an adult.” Rio said, poking her tongue in her cheek absentmindedly as she leaned against the kitchen island, drinking her coffee.

Agatha almost immediately changed her mind, but she didn’t want to give Wanda the satisfaction. “I guess I can stay, but only because I don’t want to be on the pier on the last weekend of summer,” Agatha huffed and rolled her eyes, acting annoyed.

“It’s settled then!” Wanda clapped her hands together. “Everyone disperse!”

Vision and the boys left shortly after, and the four women headed into Malibu. Rio and Agatha changed into swimwear. Then, Rio grabbed her beach bag, waving at Agatha as she went down to the private beach.

Agatha had a decision to make. Stay poolside or go down to the beach with Rio, and one sounded much more inviting than the other, which is how she found herself moments later sitting under the large beach umbrella where they had sat that first night at the party roughly a month ago.

Rio smiled as she turned a page of the book she was reading. “Careful, Agatha, Wanda might think her meddling is working.”

“Wanda needs to worry about her own shit. She won’t, but she needs to,” Agatha said as she situated herself and looked over at her companion for the day. They both had on sunglasses. Probably for the best. She smirked to herself. “What’re you reading?” Agatha asked.

“Lesbian smut,” Rio responded, face deadpan, her eyes cutting to the side to see Agatha’s reaction.

Agatha sucked in a breath. “Good smut, I hope.” She replied, side-eyeing her friend as well. Fuck Harkness. FRIENDS. Stop trying to get in her bikini bottoms, s he thought to herself.



“I know what I’ll be doing later.” Rio teased. Fuck, Vidal. You’re just FRIENDS. Stop flirting. You know where this leads. Her inner alarm went off.



Thankfully, Agatha barely caught herself before making any slightly embarrassing sounds and quickly changed the subject. “SO, the first football game is coming up…” She said, face red.

Rio took the cue as they went back to safer territory. “Yes, we’re working concessions together on Friday. Wanda’s doing, I’m sure. We’re working all the home games together.” She knew that was all Wanda and Lilia.

Agatha knew it, too.

“She means well,” Agatha replied. “She wants to see me, and everyone around her, happy. She throws herself into her work, her kids, her friends, and the school. Anything and everything she can.”

“Everyone except herself? She does realize that you, her best friend and sister, are gay and living your best life, right?”

“Am I?” Agatha asked. “She’s still worried about how it would affect everything she’s built. Once I hit 16, I built my career on being America’s Lesbian Sweetheart. She was America’s Imported Straight Sweetheart. The girl-next-door, ” Agatha sighed . “Nope. Not my story to tell, even though I was there . Wanda wants the best for me, and by extension, you.”

Rio smiled. “Well, so far, I have gained what feels like an entire family, and I don’t feel weird and starstruck around the two of you anymore. It does suck that Nat’s off-limits though. She’s hot.” She shot Agatha a devilish grin as Agatha rolled her eyes.

“She’s not off limits, but she refuses to date. Wanda would make sure we never found your body, though. ”

Rio nodded solemnly, “I believe it. ”

Agatha laughed. “That woman is feisty, and we have had a lot of training over the years.” She looked out at the ocean and back at Rio. “Going back to concession duty for football games. How do you feel about having to serve hot dogs?” She asked, already plotting something.

Rio wrinkled her nose. “Eww. I’d rather not.”

Agatha jumped to her feet and shed her shorts and sunglasses. “The last one waist-deep in the ocean, is on hot dog duty.” As she darted off.

“NOT FAIR HARKNESS!” Rio shouted as she scrambled to her feet and chased after her, taking her shorts and glasses off as she ran.

Agatha hit the water first and turned around to watch Rio, feeling the refreshingly cold water hit her skin as she slowly walked backward to let the younger woman catch up a bit. Agatha could admit she had played dirty, but that’s how she liked it. “Come on, kid! You can do better than that!” She shouted over the waves, turning back around. She was only knee-deep, and Rio was catching up. The water was up to her thighs when Rio jumped on her back, and they both went down.

Agatha yelped as she hit her knees and got a mouthful of saltwater. Rio was still wrapped around her.

“I AM VICTORIOUS!” Rio cried as Agatha spat out the ocean water.

Agatha leaned back laughing, dunking Rio in the ocean. Rio tightened her grip around her neck and took Agatha down with her as they both went under the water and rolled over. Rio finally unwrapped herself from Agatha, and they came back up, sputtering and laughing.

“Technically, I hit the water first. I win,” Agatha said, continuing to cough up water and laugh.

“Only because you cheated, bitch.” Rio responded, splashing water up at the other.

“Oh, is that how we’re playing this now?” Agatha teased as she splashed back.

The two women splashed and chased each other, reliving a childhood neither really had. They were closer to the shore when Agatha pounced on Rio. This time, it was Rio who yelped in surprise and went down. They rolled in the water for the second time, coming to a stop with Rio on top of Agatha. Rio was straddling her hips, hands on either side of her head.

“Truce?” Rio asked, keeping her pinned down, grinning madly.

“Truce!” Agatha laughed. The water was shallow enough that it wasn’t going over her face, but her hair floated a bit around her. “We'll make whoever is working with us do hot dogs.”

“Perfect,” Rio replied softly. It was then that Rio realized their positioning but didn’t immediately move. She studied Agatha’s face, and her gaze fell to her lips. This would be the perfect moment. Nope. Parent. Danger Will Robinson. Abort. Remove self. Rise. Move. She didn’t.

Agatha looked up, searching Rio’s eyes and seeing the conflict happening. She knew she should make some snide remark and push Rio off her. Splash her. Ruin the moment. SOMETHING. This was dangerous. But instead, “You’re perfect,” escaped in a whisper.

It was a long moment before Rio’s body finally responded to her brain. She stood up, shattering the moment. “I’m hungry. Are you hungry? We should eat something.” The whisper still echoing in her ears.

Agatha closed her mouth, still ajar in shock at her own declaration, and sat up, shaking her head to clear it. “Yes, good idea. I also need to wash the sand and ocean out of my hair. Have to take care of it or Jen will kill me. ”

Rio held out her hand to help her up. “So it’s true then, and not just made up? Your hair really is insured?”

“Absolutely.”

“What’s the real amount? I’ve seen everything from one million to one billion,” Rio inquired out of curiosity as Agatha took her hand and stood, the two immediately putting a few feet in between themselves.

“Twenty million with various stipulations for maintenance, coloring, that kind of thing. I cut it on a whim without permission when I was sixteen, the smallest piece. Thankfully, it was 1989, and not everything was so monitored. I was due for maintenance anyway,” Agatha shrugged.

“And the locket?” Rio asked. “I heard that was insured too, which makes sense, but wasn’t it just a random thrift store find?” Rio had read about it in a few interviews. Agatha always said it was something she had found while out with friends, and it was one of the bigger mysteries surrounding her persona. They walked back to their spot under the umbrella.

“Yes, but I’ve grown attached. It now has a GPS chip on it, so I never lose it. It’s truly irreplaceable and priceless. Yes, it’s insured, though I don’t tell anyone how much.” Agatha didn’t currently have it on, it was in a safe within a safe, inside a wall safe in the house.

“Understandable.” Rio nodded. She could tell from the tone of Agatha’s voice not to ask any more questions about it. She had seen it on Agatha on multiple occasions, and it was even more beautiful and detailed up close. Rio did not ask her what was in it, and no one knew.

The early afternoon sun shone brightly as they gathered their things and headed back up to the house. Wanda had texted that the Malibu group wouldn’t be getting back until dinner time, and they knew the fishing group wouldn’t get home until later.

“What’s for lunch, chef?” Agatha asked as they walked into the kitchen, setting her things on the breakfast table. They would shower after eating, and frolicking in the ocean had left her hungry in more ways than one, but Agatha could only do so much.

Rio followed suit and looked in the fridge. “Spaghetti leftovers from last night, Your Highness.” She replied, taking out containers. “Grab plates, will you?”

Agatha feigned shock, placing her open hand in the middle of her chest. “Par-don?” She emphasized the word. “Moi? What if I break a nail?” She asked, dramatically.

Rio laughed and rolled her eyes. “Oh, please, you keep your nails short for other activities, and you just spent the morning playing in the Pacific.”

Agatha smiled a genuine smile that reached her eyes. The smile that Rio had been seeing a lot lately, whenever they bantered like this. Agatha turned and got the plates.

Agatha couldn’t remember the last time something had come this easily to her, this back and forth, this closeness. Not since Wanda, and that had been more of a trauma bond, and immediately developed into a protective feeling. Even with the other women she was close to, it had taken her longer to develop this close of a relationship. There was her original coven, but that didn’t exist anymore.

The first night she and Rio had really talked, she had wanted to bare her soul to her. A little over a month, and they had already developed an easy back and forth. Yes, there was some flirting, but they would eventually tamp that down. Agatha was just naturally flirty, after all. It was harmless.

They ate, cleaned up, and went to shower separately. When Agatha came back down, she wore an oversized purple Malibu t-shirt and black lounge shorts. Long, dark brown, damp hair pulled back in a ponytail. Rio was already on the couch with her book. Her hair was pulled up, and her dark green tank top showed that her skin had darkened a bit in the sun. She wore black bike shorts that had some type of leaf pattern on them.

“How’s your smut?” Agatha asked as she sat down on the other end of the couch with her Kindle.

“Hot. I miss sex.” Rio responded automatically. Drown yourself in the ocean, Vidal. She groaned inwardly.

Agatha chuckled. “Same.” Play in traffic, Harkness, she thought. “I can leave you alone to read if you prefer.” She added, face red.

Rio shook her head. “No, I prefer your company." Do they make something for verbal diarrhea? “It’s funny, as a teenager, growing up with Lorna Wu's daughter, I would have killed to be close friends with huge megastars. Now that I am, I forget you guys are even famous. You’re just normal people,” She looked up at Agatha, adding, “With more money than God.”

Agatha couldn’t help but give a small laugh, “It’s a downtime for us. There are times when there are paparazzi and cameras everywhere, names splashed all over the media, and then there are the quiet times like right now. I’m sure pictures will show up tomorrow of Wanda around Malibu, but nothing huge. Just ‘Wanda Maximoff enjoying a weekend with friends’, hopefully. A few of the lesbian Twitter accounts will probably say something about Nat being there and not Vision. Jen will have it scrubbed. Then, a few of the accounts that are obsessed with Jen will say something about Jen and Alice, but that’s it. Someone will mention my absence, and we’ll continue like normal. We both try to act as down-to-earth as possible because we are. We still have our diva moments and definitely did when we were younger, but we have an example to set.” She explained.

Rio took it all in. She chewed on her lip for a minute and took a deep breath before finally asking the question that had been on her mind for, years, now that she thought about it. “Agatha, why, exactly, don’t you date seriously?”

“Pass,” Agatha replied.

“Oh, come on. We’ve shared a lot of things.” Rio huffed, trying to see past the neutral facade that Agatha was now wearing.

“Why won’t you date parents?” Agatha countered.

“Point taken.” Rio was about to ask Agatha about the faint scars she had noticed on her leg and back earlier when the front door opened and their friends walked in. Rio hadn’t realized that it was already evening, meaning the fishing group would be back soon as well.

The Malibu group had brought pizza, and the fishing group returned with nothing except sunburns and fish tales. They were around the fire again, talking and roasting marshmallows, when Wanda decided it was time to interfere.

“As we all know, our movie premieres mid-October,” Wanda said, silencing everyone, garnering a few looks. “Agatha, you should bring Rio.” She added, before taking a drink of whatever concoction she had.

All eyes turned to the two women who had previously been discussing musical interests. Rio choked on her water. Maybe she needed to just stop drinking anything around this group when they were speaking. Agatha glared at her best friend. If looks could kill, Wanda would have burst into flames.

“It’s still two months…” Agatha started.

“Six weeks,” Nat clarified.

“Isn’t that a school night?” Rio asked.

“We’re on fall break then,” Alice informed her.

“How convenient,” Agatha and Rio muttered simultaneously.

“The twins and I can’t go see the movie. Our moms get naked,” Nicky supplied, matter-of-factly, as he popped a marshmallow in his mouth before either Rio or Agatha said anything.

“Mom is taking Dad,” the twins added as they each set a marshmallow on fire.

“I prefer to stay out of the spotlight, but occasionally I am forced into it.” Vision’s expression was dour, a rarity for him. “I’m an introvert at heart,” he added quickly, his normal, easy smile returning.

“We need to go ahead and set it now to coordinate what you’re wearing,” Jen finished.

Wanda watched Agatha and Rio carefully. Wanda knew what every look, glance, twitch, and eye sparkle from Agatha meant. They could quite literally have entire conversations without saying a word.



She watched Agatha look at Rio, the question in her eyes. Do you want to come with me?

That was an easy one, surely Rio could catch that one, thought Wanda. She looked towards Rio, watching the young teacher tilt her head as if responding to Agatha's unspoken question. Both broke out in a subtle smile.



Wanda was delighted to see this silent exchange. It had taken Agatha and Wanda years to develop their connection. It had taken Agatha and Rio a month.

Soulmates!

She had called it. She couldn’t wait to send a text later..

“I’d love to go. I think it will be fun to tag along as your plus one.” Rio finally said aloud.

“Great!” Said Wanda, ignoring the added comment. She turned to look at the boys, “It’s getting late. Go shower and head off to bed. We’re just going to talk shop for a bit.”

They groaned but knew she meant to just go inside and play video games or something. “Yes, ma’am.” They replied. Vision took that as his cue as well. “I will make sure they don’t burn the house down, Darling. He kissed Wanda’s forehead and stood. The boys hugged their moms and aunts, said goodnight to the others, and went inside.

“Okay, schedules for the next few months.” Wanda started.

Rio and Agatha exchanged looks before Rio turned to Wanda. “This doesn’t involve Alice or me…”

Wanda cut her off, “Yes, it does, stay.”

Rio remained where she sat and pulled out her phone, opening the Calendar app. She had a feeling it was about to fill up.

“In addition to school happenings, what else do we have, Jen?” Wanda started.

Jennifer cleared her throat. They had planned all of this out on their trip. Go over their schedules for the next few months and push Agatha into inviting Rio at least to the premiere, New York for SNL, and the Halloween party. That would at least get them to November. Maybe by then, they’d know each other well enough to at least kiss.

“You two have talk shows around LA, and a few NY appearances, especially the closer we get to October. Wanda, your SNL episode is at the end of September, and Nat will go with you. Agatha, yours is the weekend after the premiere.”

Rio looked at Agatha. “Who’s your musical guest?” Rio asked quickly.

Agatha smiled, mischief in her eyes. She already knew the reaction she was about to get. “Chap…” She didn’t get to finish before Rio squealed. “Want to come?” She asked when Rio stopped. She shrieked a yes.

Jen nodded and put it down. Wanda smirked, she knew what that response was going to be.

“Then there’s the annual Halloween party. Wanda, costumes?” Jen asked.

“I think Billy is going with his ‘friend’ Eddie Wilson, Sam Wilson’s son . Tommy and Nicky will probably go as zombies, right, Aggie?” Wanda asked, as Agatha nodded. That was their current thing.

“Vision and I are doing Glinda and Fiyero from Wicked.” Wanda finished.

Agatha snorted, laughing as Wanda gave her a sharp look. Agatha mumbled something about Rio saying something funny, and Rio nodded conspiratorially.

“Alice and I are going as Xena and Gabby,” Jen said.

“Don’t you need…” Agatha started before a graham cracker connected with the side of her head. She turned to see Wanda looking around innocently. Instead, she muttered something about them making such a gay choice and picking graham cracker out of her hair.

Jennifer smirked and looked at Nat. “What about you? Are you and Agatha doing something? Agatha letting you borrow the Elphaba costume, perhaps? ”

Nat shook her head. “No. I decided to be on duty that night and not dress up.”

Agatha automatically threw her hands up. “She volunteered, I swear.”

Nat nodded. “I want to have eyes on all the camera screens in The Tower make sure all your drunk asses are safe,” she said with a smile that didn’t reach her eyes.

Jen nodded with a glance at Wanda, who was suddenly very interested in picking at her shorts. She looked back at Agatha. “Alright, Agatha?”

Agatha looked up and then over at Rio. “Want to go to the Halloween party and wear some ridiculous coordinated costume with me?” She asked, keeping her face neutral.

Rio nodded, stilling her face as well. “Yes, want to do Wheel of Time?” It was a series they both loved that only they watched.

“Of course,” Agatha replied. She thought about it for a second. “I’ll be Moirane…”

Rio finished quickly, not wanting to be cast as the love interest, “I’ll be Lanfear.”

Agatha nodded, somewhat disappointed that Rio would go as her nemesis. For the best, though, she’ll be hit on all night. She thought, but what did it matter to her?

Jen just nodded, inputting it all into her phone.

“I shared the calendar with everyone. Just to make life easier. In reality, Jen knew it would put everyone’s mind at peace if they knew where everyone was. The collective amount of trauma, trust, and abandonment issues in this group was staggering.

⭐⭐⭐

That night, Rio lay in the guest bed, unable to sleep. It was extremely comfortable, and her sleep playlist was on, but she just couldn’t get her mind to turn off. The conversation around the fire replayed in her mind. Everything had happened so fast. She knew she couldn’t talk to Alice about it right now, which also ruled out Jen. Wanda was stuck in her own personal ‘Good Luck, Babe’, and had orchestrated everything, most likely to distract herself . Nat was probably conversing with the security that Rio knew were crawling around the property, plus she would do whatever Wanda wanted. She sighed. The person Rio needed to talk to was the one she wanted to talk to the most.  She just had to trust herself and not throw herself at Agatha. There was just something about Agatha Harkness that she could get lost in.

Rio looked at her phone, one in the morning. “Worth a shot,” she breathed.

Me:

Hey, are you awake?

She waited to see if the three dots showed up. A few minutes went by, and nothing. Rio sighed, resigning herself to a sleepless night when her door opened and closed.

“Yes. I am.” A familiar voice said as the bed shifted.

“Very presumptuous of you to just crawl into bed with me,” Rio said with a nervous smile.

“Please, we snuggle on the couch most movie nights,” Agatha responded, making herself comfortable.

She was right, and Rio didn’t mind. She felt like they had known each other for longer than they had, and Rio had grown accustomed to falling asleep near Agatha every Saturday night, which was four, but who was counting? “I can’t sleep.” She whined.

Agatha rolled her eyes, knowing Rio couldn’t see it in the dark. “It’s all the smut you’ve been reading.”

“It is not,” Rio sighed, “I keep thinking about what happened earlier and how you were kind of forced to invite me to everything. You don’t have to, you know.” Rio offered Agatha an out.

“What if I want to? What if I don’t want to take someone else to a premiere? Or to New York? As a friend, of course. No one else will do Wheel of Time with me for Halloween, and you will have women all over you in whatever Lanfear costume we come up with.” Agatha replied sincerely. She could handle whatever happened at the Halloween party just fine. You’re lying to yourself. “I’ll be your wingwoman,” She added.

Rio smiled, her nerves dying down, knowing that Agatha hadn’t felt obligated. “Okay, as long as you didn’t feel forced.” She didn’t comment on the wing-woman remark. While Rio knew that was the better option, she also already knew it wasn’t what she really wanted.

“Please, kid, no one forces me to do anything.” Agatha scoffed. She was thankful for the darkness of the room, as she knew her eyes had drifted to Rio’s mouth. Just friends who had spent the past month of Saturday nights together. You have shared a bed with Wanda a million times. You aren’t attracted to Wanda, but it’s fine. You have slept on the same couch with this woman multiple times. You’ve platonically cuddled. A bed is no different, Harkness. Get it together. She rolled over and created a bit more space between them.



Rio watched as Agatha rolled over, and she copied the movement, so that their backs were towards each other, with several feet in between them. This is a bad idea. This has to be a bad idea. She thought. The problem was that while she was hyperaware of the other woman in the bed with her, she already felt her eyelids getting heavier. Yes, the temptation was there, but so was the comfort and the ease of their relationship. She could easily fall asleep with Agatha Harkness every night. Preferably in more of a relationship context. Nicky graduates in two years. She sighed and froze when she realized how loud it had been.

“Everything okay?” Agatha asked sleepily. She had still been battling with herself, but she was an actress for a reason.

How the fuck was she almost asleep? She thought. Though, her body was nodding off. “Just thinking.” Rio yawned, surprising herself.

“I can go…” Agatha started.

“Please don’t,” Rio said immediately. What the fuck? The woman is pure temptation. “I really am used to falling asleep near you on Saturdays. It’s what we’ve done for a few weeks now.” She added.

Agatha nodded, “So am I. It’s just a different environment. Who are you hoping to see at the movie premiere?” They were still turned away from each other, but naming off celebrities became like counting sheep, as they drifted off to sleep.

⭐⭐⭐

Sunday morning was a repeat of Saturday as Wanda and Nat sat at the table again after their run, drinking coffee close together with careful touches and native accents.

“Agatha spent the night in Rio’s room.” Nat shared. She had been checking security footage, and a hallway camera had caught Agatha entering Rio’s room. There weren’t any cameras in private spaces, but with as many parties as they held here, they had been installed in all public spaces.

Wanda thought about it momentarily before responding, “Nothing happened. If they had even kissed, she would have already told me.” She still smiled. Doesn’t date teachers, indeed. We’ll see about that.

Nat knew Wanda was right. The two found sisters could practically read each other’s minds. It was why Agatha was the only other person who knew everything about Nat and Wanda, other than because she had been there right from the start. “Things are moving in the right direction for them if they’ll let it continue.”

Wanda nodded as she blew out a breath, stood, and walked back into the house.

The Russian knew that there was no use in going after her, that Wanda was lost in her thoughts and needed a moment. Nat gathered both of their mugs and took them to the kitchen. The boys were staying at the house today, and she knew that while Wanda loved her family, she wasn’t happy about it.

Breakfast was the same as the day before. Everyone gathered in the kitchen, doing different things. It had been decided that everyone was spending the day at the house, out by the pool. Everyone changed into pool wear after eating and reconvened.

The boys goofed off and near heart attacks were had as Tommy and Nicky did flips into the pool, nearly hitting their heads multiple times.

Rio turned to Agatha, who had taken a chair next to her, “How many ER trips have those two had?” She asked.

“Surprisingly less than either of us thought they would. People know who we are regardless, however, when people know your security team and the nannies, you know it’s an issue.” Agatha recalled.

Rio blew air out of her nose. ”That was still less than you thought?”

“Billy only had to go once or twice because he did something they told him to do,” Wanda said, as she pulled a chair up by their loungers. “The other two, though. Stitches, broken bones, and a few false alarms. They’ve calmed down a lot, but still, they think they’re invincible.”

“Which is why I have to do PR clean up for them too,” Jen added in, as she and Alice joined the group, Nat, behind them.

“Which is also why Vision is teaching them all to drive, he’s the safest and most patient of everyone,” Nat said as she sat in a chair beside Wanda.

As it was, Vision was having a discussion with Billy while yelling something about angles and rotations at Tommy and Nicky.

Agatha laughed, “He tries, but those two are not applying physics to their recklessness right now.” Wanda nodded as she looked affectionately at Vision, and then over at Nat, who was in deep conversation with Alice and Jen.

Rio stood and sat on the edge of the pool with her legs in the water . Billy had gone off to take turns jumping in with the other boys. Vision stood next to Rio and started a conversation, and she took the opportunity to get to know more about him. He was an astrophysicist at UCLA and had met Wanda on a set when he was consulting. They had fallen in love and gotten married, according to him. Rio did see the love there and didn’t doubt that at all, though she still wondered about the full story.

Rio shared about her love of plants and all things science, loudly mentioning the upcoming quiz on Tuesday.

“I’m giving a quiz on Tuesday, too.” Vision laughed. “Evil teachers unite.”

“Here, here!” Alice said from where she sat, overhearing the conversation.

“You’re all so mean!” Wanda shouted.

“I’ve been studying for Alice and Rio’s classes while we’ve been here,” Billy said, before jumping in the pool.

“I’ll probably fail,” Tommy said, shrugging.

“I’m just going to wing it, but I paid attention,” Nicky said before he pushed Tommy in.

Jen had snuck up behind Nicky, “You’re such a brat.” She said before pushing him in.

“Hey! That’s my son!” Agatha said, having snuck up behind Jen and pushing her in.

Rio couldn’t remember the last time she felt like this. Happy, surrounded by people she cared about. She stood up to get a drink when Agatha tried to push her in, too, but she grabbed on to her and pulled her in.

“Didn’t you learn your lesson yesterday, Harkness? If I’m going down, so are you.” She laughed.

Wanda almost gave herself whiplash. “What happened yesterday?”

Rio just grinned. “Ocean shenanigans.”

Jen sighed loudly, “Keep it out of the media.”

Nicky groaned. “Mom.”

Agatha shook her head. “Nothing happened, and you all need to get your minds out of the gutter .”

Nat bit her tongue, so tempted to add that someone slept in someone else’s bed last night.

The boys went in after lunch to play video games, and Vision joined them. He had been around this group to know they enjoyed their time together, and he did not enjoy the girl talk. He also had a book to read, essays to grade, football to watch, and his own friend to talk to while he did all of that.

April and Jen went upstairs, muttering about a nap while the other four women remained poolside.

Nat finally let her secret out . “So, sleeping together now?”

“Fucking cameras.” Agatha scowled.

“What?” Rio looked shocked.

Wanda suppressed a laugh.

Agatha turned her head to look at Rio. “There are cameras in the public areas of the house since we have so many events here,” she explained. “Ms. Head of Security, here, must have been checking them.” She added, eyes darting over at Nat.

“Nothing happened. I couldn’t sleep because I felt like SOMEONE,” Rio cut her eyes at Wanda, “had pressured Agatha into inviting me to things, so I texted to see if she was awake. She came to my room instead of texting, we talked and fell asleep. That’s it.” she explained.

“You do not have to explain anything to these two busybodies. We are grown adults and friends. Close friends and nothing more.” Agatha glared at Wanda.

Wanda put her hands up in defense . “I didn’t say a single thing. It was the other redheaded demon this time.”

Nat grinned wildly . “Hey, I’m allowed to create trouble now and then. We’ll see how long the two of you remain friends. I’m going to check cameras and do my job.” She said as she got up, eyes lingering on Wanda before she left.

Rio scoffed. “You two aren’t very subtle.”

“Neither are the two of you.” She shot back.

Rio bit her tongue before she said anything else. She didn’t have anything against Wanda and didn’t want to hurt her. She could tell that whatever the situation was, it was painful and tearing her apart. Instead, she reached over and put a hand on her shoulder.

“If you ever need to talk to someone other than Agatha, or if Agatha isn’t available, I’m here. I know a few things about extremely difficult situationships.” Rio’s voice was soft, and she gave her a meaningful look when she saw the tears Wanda was trying to hold back.

Wanda nodded. “Thanks,” Wanda whispered, standing to leave . As she left, she gave Agatha a pointed look, bent over, and whispered, “I give you both until the new year.”

Agatha shook her head, earning a sigh from Wanda as her sister muttered something in Romanian and went inside.

Agatha turned to Rio. “Want to play a game?”

Rio raised an eyebrow. “What kind of game?”

“Get your ass in the pool and find out.” She replied as she got in herself.

Curious, Rio followed her. “Okay, what’s this game?” She asked as they were both treading water in the deep end.

“ A little game of ‘ask me anything’. We’re going to race to the opposite side and back. Whoever wins gets to ask the other a question, and they have to answer it. The questions we refused to answer yesterday are off limits, and we can’t ask about…”

Rio cut her off, knowing what she was about to say, “Other people’s stuff. I wouldn’t. Not our stories to tell.”

Agatha nodded. “Right. Up for it?”

Rio nodded. “ This is a trap, but prepare to bare your soul, Harkness!” She exclaimed as she set up to push herself off the wall.

“Whatever, kid. Ready, set, go.”

They pushed off, both gliding underwater most of the way to the other side, they each got a few strokes in before flipping and pushing off the other side. Rio was only an inch taller than the older woman, so speed mattered. She touched the starting side right before Agatha did.

“Ha! I win!”

“Barely!” Agatha shouted, but fine.

“I’ll start easy, favorite color?” Rio already knew it, but she didn’t want to start with soul-baring questions.

“Oh, come on. Purple, yours is green.”

Rio smiled. “Okay, then you can skip that one.”

Rio barely won the next round. “Favorite food?”

Agatha made an annoyed face. “Rio. We each know that about each other, too. Yours is cereal, and mine is Italian, and anything you make.”

Rio smirked. She was just trying to annoy Agatha at the moment.

Agatha sighed, continuing, “We know all the basics about each other. Our favorite movie? The Witches Road . Which you don’t think is conceited on my part, because it’s a fucking good movie. Our favorite TV show? Sisters . Yours to watch, mine for memories and milestones in my career. We both prefer the mountains and almost always have some type of background noise. My first pet was a rabbit named Señor Scratchy. Raven is yours. We’ll be here forever if I start on music and books, so I think we can skip the easy questions and go a bit deeper.”

Agatha’s face was soft during her monologue, neither angry nor upset. She wanted, needed , Rio to understand that she had heard her and appreciated Rio, for Rio-as is.

“Wow, and we just met.” Rio joked, trying to lighten the moment, though the look in her eyes matched the seriousness in Agatha’s. “Fine. What are those scars on your back and your leg? They’re very faint, so I’m sure you have them edited out of pictures and covered with makeup, especially the one on your leg.”

Agatha nodded, “Good job, detective. They’re souvenirs of when Nat tried to take me out that one time. Knife down the back, bullet grazed my leg. It was all very dramatic.” She would not be telling Rio about the other scar from that night.

Agatha reached forward and used her fingertip to gently close Rio’s mouth, which had come open from shock. “I’ve lived an interesting life. Let’s go again, Vidal.”

This time, Agatha won. “Alright, let’s see. Most embarrassing high school moment.”

“Oh, that’s brutal and not fair because you didn’t go to high school!”

“I also don’t have to answer.” She laughed.

Rio thought about it. “I had a crush on a girl and tried to let her cheat on my test in science class. Not only did I get caught, but our tests were different, so she would’ve failed anyway.” She responded.

“Smooth.” Agatha laughed.

“I was absolutely not.”

Rio won the next round.

“Worst person to work with?”

“Tony Stark. Completely full of himself. Pepper is great, love her. Tony is just all ego, even if he is a great dad and a decent husband. Don’t ever tell him I said those things .” Their rivalry was well known, though as an award-winning director, they did work together frequently.

Rio nodded. “I get that vibe. Morgan is a sweetheart of a kid, though. I took your recommendation and she’s cat-sitting for me this weekend. ”

Agatha smiled fondly at the mention of Morgan, “She and Nicky have known each other since birth, really, but have been friends since pre-k. Again, great kid.”

Rio won again. “If you hadn’t been forced into acting, what would you have wanted to do?”

Agatha bit her lip, deep in thought, and mulled over the question for a moment, putting one hand on her hip and lightly gripping the lip of the pool with the other, while treading water. She finally looked back at Rio, an eyebrow raised . “You know, I have never thought about it. Hollywood has been my entire life. Even if I were to retire, I’m set and could just do charity work, some business, whatever I wanted. I never had that childhood ‘when I grow up’ dream.”

Agatha looked somewhat lost, and Rio felt for her. Neither of them had a traditional childhood.

“I never had unrealistic career goals.” Rio’s fingertips barely gripped the edge of the pool. “ I never dreamed of being a rocket scientist or an astronaut. It was always a teacher, something that was practical and doable. Something that even someone from the neighborhoods I lived in could get a scholarship and do. I was good at science, and while there were other things I could do with that, teaching was the most achievable. Even though I grew up an unofficial Wu, it still made the most sense. Again, it's not glorious, absolutely not the best or easiest career choice, but not the worst. Solid. Achievable. Practical. ” Rio explained.

It was then that Rio realized that despite the age gap and the different worlds they were from, they were extremely similar, and it was why they got along so well and why they felt so seen by each other.

“Tell me you have trauma without telling me you have trauma.” Agatha smiled sarcastically. “One more?”

Rio nodded, sensing some trick hidden under Agatha’s bikini strap, and she was right. As Rio closed in on her win, Agatha shoved her shoulder, pushed her back, and touched the wall before Rio could.

“I win!” Agatha said, grinning.

“You’re awful,” Rio said, splashing Agatha in the face.

“You love it.” She replied.

I could. She thought. “What’s the question?” She asked instead.

“I haven’t thought that far ahead.” Agatha thought for a moment. “Do you, or did you, ever want kids?”

“Why, Agatha Harkness, are you asking me to be your baby daddy?” she asked before breaking out into giggles. Rio Vidal did not giggle.

It was the sweetest sound Agatha had ever heard, and she vowed to make her do it again as much as she could. She couldn’t help but laugh, which caused her to snort. “No!” She said, “It was an honest question, you brat!”

“Well,” She said after regaining some semblance of composure, “If I were to be with or marry someone with a child or children, then yes. It was always a hard “no” to that question growing up, due to my fears of falling in my mother’s footsteps . However, it depends on who I’m with and if that happens sooner rather than later. I’m not getting any younger. I’m not opposed. ” There was a time in the not-so-distant past when she thought she would become a stepmother to three blonde teenagers of whom she had been very fond, and possibly have a child. It was not something she wanted to dwell on right now as she pushed the old ache away.

Agatha wasn't entirely sure why that answer was so important to her, but she had found herself invested in Rio’s response . She had Nicky, and anyone close to her had to understand that he was number one in her life, even if he was older. She certainly wasn't having any more children, though the thought of being with a younger woman, or adopting, hadn’t completely been ruled out for her . Agatha saw a flash of something across Rio’s face with her answer, but didn’t want to pry. Something to unpack during a sleepless night, she thought.

They climbed out of the pool, went inside to shower, and changed to reconvene with the whole house.

⭐⭐⭐

The four women had been watching from an overlooking balcony. Wanda couldn’t have done much better if she had been in their minds herself. She knew Agatha and knew she wasn’t going to move faster than Rio was comfortable with.

“Why won’t they just kiss already!” Jen said, exasperated.

“Do you know how long we said that about the two of you?” Nat asked.

“Touché,” Alice said, raising her wine glass.

Wanda pinched the bridge of her nose. “This is painful.” She sighed.

Nat looked over at her, knowing it wasn’t just the scene below them that she was referring to.

Jen leaned forward. “Between the football games, the entire month of October, and whatever we need to do after that, they should figure it out.”

Alice shrugged. “I don’t know. Rio has a damn good reason as to why she doesn’t date parents.” As three sets of eyes looked at her, she shook her head, “Oh no, not my story to tell. That’s all her.”

Wanda just nodded. “Agatha is many things, but if she really likes Rio, and I can guarantee she does, then she’s not going to do anything to go against that.”

They spent their last night around the poolside fire pit. Rio and Alice kept throwing out random questions for the boys to help them study. Tommy was trying, desperately.

“Rio, can you give me extra points because you’re into my aunt?” He teased.

Rio’s eyes went wide, and her face was one of shock, strictly from the audacity.
