The looks on every face varied from shock to trying not to laugh. Agatha was biting back a laugh.

Nicky was not amused. “Dude! That’s my mom!” He turned to Rio. “That is my mom, though, so do I just get an automatic A, or…”

“Nicholas Scratch Harkness!” Agatha yelled, trying so hard not to laugh.

“I am not into Agatha!” Rio cried, face bright red.

“The blushing says otherwise,” Alice goaded.

“Aunt Aggie has the hots for you, too,” Billy said, grinning.

“You are no longer my favorite!” Agatha shouted, her own face turning red.

Wanda owed the boys for this one. She had a shit-eating grin on her face and pointed at Agatha, who rolled her eyes at her.

Vision was laughing at the boys. “Now, boys, stop teasing. They’re just friends.”

“That’s what Alice and Jen said for years,” Tommy replied.

Alice blushed. As Jen looked at Tommy. “Isn’t it past your bedtime, rugrat?”

He stuck his tongue out at her. Wanda just nodded.

“Actually, boys, it is time for you three to head upstairs. Go.” She directed.

They nodded, gave their goodnights, and left, teasing as they went. Vision also made his departure, once again kissing Wanda on the forehead as he left.

Wanda looked pointedly at Rio,

“What? Your son needs to study. He’s not getting extra points!” Rio replied.

“It was worth a shot.” Wanda laughed.

The women discussed upcoming football games and gossiped about which parents would be sneaking alcohol into the game, and what kids would be sneaking underneath the bleachers.

The night grew late, and everyone departed to their rooms, the beach weekend coming to a close.

Rio had changed into her pajamas, having showered after swimming earlier, when she heard her door open and close softly. She let out a breath she didn’t know she was holding as she turned around. She knew it was Agatha and found that she had hoped she would come.

Rio nodded as Agatha looked at her with the question in her eyes before settling into what was now her side of the bed.

“Not a Saturday. So, when we’re in the same place overnight, we share a bed?” Rio asked.

“Like friends,” Agatha stated, nodding.

“Absolutely,” Rio replied.

Even in their sleep, the two gravitated towards one another. The large California king mattress was no match for their attraction. ONLY FRIENDS, Rio reiterated to herself. Disentangling herself from Agatha's warm arms, which had wrapped around her in deep sleep. Still, it felt like the most normal thing in the world.

When they headed back the next day, she knew she had grown closer to everyone over the weekend, especially Agatha. She had to pull Jen and Alice apart, reminding them that they would see each other in less than twenty-four hours, and reminded the boys about their quizzes the next day.

If her friendship with Agatha was this strong after a month, where would it be by the time they went to New York?

Chapter End Notes

When will one of these women cave? WHEN?!

Soon, I hope.

The Best People In Life Are Free

Chapter Summary

Lilia gives some advice, High School football starts, and Rio and Nicky bond a bit.

Wanda finds herself at a major crossroads and turns to Rio.

Alexa - Play "Good Luck, Babe"

Chapter Notes

Grab some tissues kids and buckle up. I'm not sorry.
Here is the Wanda backstory you've all been waiting for.
Your regularly scheduled Agatha/Rio slow burn returns next chapter.

Updated 5/21/25

See the end of the chapter for more notes

🎶

The rumors are terrible and cruel

But honey, most of them are true

New Romantics - Taylor Swift

🎶

Early September

Rio never expected anything from her friends except for the ease of being around them. She never expected to be friends with superstars or get invited to movie premieres, Saturday Night Live, parties, or any of the things she was suddenly preparing to attend next month. It was overwhelming, but she felt like she was just doing things with her friends. Some of those things just happened to be red carpet and star-studded events, no big deal.

Before any of that, there was her actual job and the events and responsibilities that came with it. She had administered the first quiz of the semester the week after Labor Day and their amazing beach weekend. While most of her students had hit it out of the park , a select few had not, kicking off after-school tutoring and make-up testing . Rio’s Thursday tutoring session had just wrapped up, and she was about to leave herself when Lilia Calderu, principal of SHIELD, walked into the large science classroom and closed the door behind her.

“Vidal, have a seat, I need to speak with you for a moment,” the esteemed principal directed as she perched on top of a desk like it was the most natural thing in the world for her. “It won’t take long,” she assured, noting the young teacher’s hesitation.

Rio nodded. “Yes, ma’am. Let me text Alice and tell her it will be a minute.”

“Of course.” The older woman waited , looking around at the way Rio had set up the classroom, while Rio sent off the text and looked back up, meeting her boss’s eyes.

“I know that you have joined a certain inner circle, and I must remind you that it is imperative to stick to NDAs and make certain you guard any secrets that may be shared with you by certain parents ,” Lilia said seriously.

Rio nodded. “Of course.”

Lilia regarded her for a moment. Lilia read people and she did it well. She wouldn’t be the woman she was or in the positions she was in if she didn’t. She had trusted Alice last spring and could tell that Rio was someone who was honest and genuine. Lilia Calderu also had her sources.

Lilia had a sworn duty to protect the people around her, and that included the secrets that those people held.

Lilia’s face softened, having found that Rio was worthy. “You can trust Agatha, you know. It was I that made her bring Nicky here, connected the two of them to this place. Made sure she had the lawyer she had as a child. You do not know any of this, by the way,” She paused to make sure the teacher understood what she was conveying. Seeing Rio nod, she continued ,  “When you run a specific institution, you know a lot of things about a lot of people. You would do well to remember that. ” Lilia stood and looked back at a large planter in the corner of the room. “Pilea Peperomiodes, not an easy plant to grow inside Ms. Vidal. I’ve only known one other person to successfully do so, and yours is quite possibly healthier than hers. That’s quite the green thumb.” She turned back to her. “Oh! And, parents like you so far, keep it up! Looking forward to the game tomorrow! Go Superstars!” She finished and left before Rio could reply, leaving the door open as Alice walked in.

“You ready to go?” Alice asked. “I knew Lilia wouldn’t stay very long.”

“I never know what the fuck Lilia means. It takes me a good two business weeks to figure it out.” Rio continued to stare out the door.

Alice nodded. “It’s been a decade and I’m still learning.”

Rio tilted her head as she slung her backpack over her shoulder, “You’ve been here for a while and know everyone well, was Lilia the reason Agatha brought Nicky here?” Rio asked as she turned off the lights and they walked out of the door.

Alice made sure she chose her words carefully. She wasn’t prepared to share everything with Rio yet. It wasn’t lying, really. She was just telling her half-truths. “Lilia was one of Agatha’s tutors when she was young, and so when Nicky was old enough to go to school, she found out Lilia was the principal, and she trusted Lilia, so yeah.”

Okay, that’s close to the truth. Alice thought.

As they drove home, Rio couldn’t help but think about how normal everything felt so far. Football games, her best friend dating someone that seemed perfect for her , concerned parents, parents, and teachers still volunteering to help out at the concession stand. So there were bodyguards and heightened security everywhere, state-of-the-art facilities, a sniper’s nest at a high school football game…it wasn’t completely normal, but it was done in a way that it was very concealed.

“Other than the football game, what are your plans for the weekend?” Rio asked.



“Jen and I are going out Saturday night,” they were actually having dinner with Lilia, but Rio didn’t need to know that. “ So we’ll miss movie night, nothing Sunday, you?” Alice looked over at her.

“Movie night Saturday, and it looks like everyone except you and I are headed out Sunday for promo events in New York for a few days, then they’ll be back here.”

Alice shrugged. “Get used to a lot of texting.”

“I don’t know what you mean. Just a normal amount of texting a friend of mine is all,” Rio shrugged back, slightly mockingly.

Alice rolled her eyes. “We’ve taken bets on how long it takes. Do you want in?”

“Sure, put me down for fuck you guys.” Rio groaned.

“It’s only a matter of time. You’ve already shared a bed.” Alice grinned.

“I was spilling my very complicated feelings to you and you’re using that against me right now! I am hurt!” Rio joked, knowing that Alice didn’t mean any true harm.

“I am your sister from an unknown mister, and it is my duty.”

After her usual bedtime routine, switching her playlist, and getting comfortable with Raven purring next to her, Rio grabbed her phone to text Agatha, as had also become routine.

Me:

The meddling bitches have

started a betting pool for

when we get together.

Agatha 💜

I know. I’ve seen it. Everyone

is in on this damn thing too.

The boys, Vision, security, my

personal trainer, Lilia, Stark, Rogers,

fucking Danvers…

Me:

What the fuck?

Me:

Agatha…

Me:

Did you place a bet?

Agatha 💜

Absolutely not.

Agatha was holding on to the fact that she didn’t date Nicky’s teachers, or anyone, for that matter. She had her heart utterly annihilated once and would not go down that road again. She also didn’t want to lose Rio’s friendship if things went poorly. Lines were being blurred, but they could handle that like the grown adults they were.



Agatha 💜

Did you?

Me:

Apparently ‘fuck you guys’ wasn’t

an option.

If the last time Rio had dated a parent had ended terribly, this was bound to end even worse, and that was definitely not an option. The only way this could end worse , she thought to herself, is if you actually died this time. Okay, so not worse, but losing Agatha’s friendship would break her in a much different way.



Agatha 💜

I’m sure Wanda was disappointed.

Me:

She was, according to Alice. I’m

going to sleep. See you tomorrow!

Goodnight Agatha.

Agatha 💜

Goodnight Rio.

Agatha found herself almost adding kissing emojis like a teenage girl. She groaned. This was not normal for her. Agatha Harkness did not pine. She did not yearn. Women came to her. Women threw themselves at her. What was happening? She was fifty-one years old, damnit, not fifteen. She screamed into her pillow before rolling over and trying to go to sleep, only to dream of Rio.

⭐⭐⭐

Of course, a school for the children of Hollywood stars would have ‘superstars’ as its mascot. Rio had rolled her eyes when she had first heard it and still thought it was too on the nose even now. She thought cougars would have been appropriate and funny, but she hadn’t been in charge of choosing the mascot.

Rio looked around the gym as students gathered for the pep rally. As she mentally went over the students’ names, she was reminded that it wasn’t just the Hollywood elite that sent their children to SHIELD, it was athletes, CEOs, billionaires, tech bros, politicians, and people up in that range.

She stood with the other teachers and cheered as the football players came out, cheering for Nicky. She waved to Billy in the stands and cheered for other students. Rio had always enjoyed supporting students for all activities, not just sports. Football days were also fun days for most or make-up days for students to bring up grades. She enjoyed it, and the chance to get to know her students better.

By the time the last bell rang, she was anxious for the football game. She didn’t care if they won or not. She was just ready to see a certain megastar. She told herself it was because they had gotten so close, and she would be excited to see Alice and the others as well. She was lying to herself, but it made her feel more like an adult and less like one of her students with a fleeting crush of the week.

Even if there was nothing fleeting about this.

By the time she got to the concession stand to report for her shift, she was vibrating with excitement. She knew they were also working with several other parents, including Carol Danvers. She and her wife, Maria, had a daughter Nicky’s age who was in Rio’s advanced class. Carol was another award-winning actress, and Maria was a literal rocket scientist.

Rio walked into the building to see Agatha setting up with other parents and teachers. Agatha smiled when she saw her, excusing herself and making her way to Rio.

“Hey! You’ve got popcorn, I’ve got candy.” She said as she hugged Rio in greeting , a hug that was over too quickly for Rio’s liking.

Rio was slightly surprised by the friendly greeting in such a public place, cut considering people were taking bets on them getting together, and they had shared a bed, she’d take it.

“They just happen to be right next to each other,” Danvers said as she walked over to the hot dogs with a smirk .

“You’re on hot dog duty?” Rio asked innocently.

“Yes, which works for me.” Danvers shrugged with a wink.

Agatha leaned over and whispered, “Today’s was Danvers’ pick in the betting pool, and thought I’d like to be next to you.”

Rio stifled a laugh, remembering their ocean battle from last weekend and the bet they had made.

“Nicky going to lead us to state this year, Agatha?” Another parent asked, interrupting their conspiring.

Rio watched as Agatha turned into Agatha Harkness, football mom, superstar, and America’s Sweetheart. “Hopefully! As long as your son can protect him long enough.” There was laughter before someone opened the windows to the stand, and their work began.

Shifts were only a quarter, and thankfully, the time went by quickly. Working in such a confined space, Rio and Agatha had bumped into each other constantly, mostly by accident, but neither woman minded. When their time was up, the next shift took over, and as they left the building, Agatha grabbed Rio by the arm and led her away before she could get caught up in any conversations with parents who wanted to know how their student was doing in her class.

“Hey! Mrs. Chavez needs to speak with me .” Rio laughed as Agatha pulled her away .

Agatha shook her head. “ I can assure you that she is not interested in the science you teach, and we have a game to catch and a quarterback to cheer for. ”

“ Oh, right! Nicky. I haven’t seen him play yet. ”

They reached their group in the stands. Tommy was on the junior varsity team and wasn’t playing as their game had been the previous night. He was still paying very close attention to the plays and all the action on the field below.

“ How’s he doing ?” Agatha asked as they scooted into the bleachers .

“One passing touchdown so far, and twenty yards from second. Tied game. Defenses are doing well and stopping everything on both sides .” Tommy rattled.

Agatha looked out at the field where Nicky was lining up. They were at the 20-yard line a pass he should easily be able to convert for a touchdown . He saw Agatha right before the snap, and that was the good luck he needed. Nicky couldn’t find an open receiver, scrambled, and ran it in for a touchdown . The stands erupted.

His ability to think quickly on his feet was what had secured him the starting quarterback position the previous year.

The game had been close , and the Superstars barely won 28-27. Nicky, a perfectionist like his mother, knew he needed to improve and discussed strategies with Vision and Tommy after the game.

“I know there are a lot of great high schools around here, Nicky, but have you thought of watching tapes from schools in other great football states? To get plays and tips from those?” Rio asked as they all walked to the parking lot.

He shook his head, droplets of water from his quick shower flying off. “No. I hadn’t thought about that.”

Rio smiled at him. “I’ll text Rogers and give him some names of schools. I have a friend who coached basketball, and she did that, helped the team a lot.”

“Thanks Rio! So you like football?”

Rio and Nicky continued their conversation back to the vehicles and at their after-game victory dinner. A tradition Agatha had started with Nicky.

Wanda leaned over and whispered into Agatha’s ear. “They’re already bonding.”

Agatha nodded. “Yes, like you do with your friend’s kids.”

“That’s a little more…”

“Shut up and eat your fries before I steal them.”

⭐⭐⭐

September continued, filled with football games for two out of the three boys, and Billy started rehearsals for the fall musical. The coven continued with their movie nights, and everyone had work, whether it was celebrity or teaching-related.

Rio and Agatha continued to build a closer rapport, Alice and Jen were disgustingly adorable, and something was happening in the Maximoff household. Wanda was growing more and more restless, and it was affecting Nat.

As the month crawled along Agatha started table reads for her new TV show, as well as costume fittings, and promotional events were picking up for both stars. It was a Thursday evening later in the month as Wanda sat in the living room helping Billy run lines for his role in the fall musical. They were doing Beetlejuice, and he was the lead. Vision was on the floor at the coffee table, helping Tommy with his math. Tommy was failing and hadn’t been able to play in the junior varsity game that night. Coach Rogers wouldn’t even let him sit on the bench until he passed, and he was surly about it, but trying his hardest to truly understand the material. Nicky was listening to music on the opposite couch, typing a history essay for Alice’s class. He was staying over while Agatha was in New York for a few days. Wanda smiled as she surveyed the scene of happiness before her, then looked over at Nat, the beautiful redhead she had… until reality hit her, as it always did. Nat’s face quickly dissipated into Vision’s, her husband. The man that she had married when she was twenty-six and had been married to for almost twenty years, and would be with for so much longer…

“Mom? Mom?” Billy’s voice cut through her thoughts.

She startled and looked at him, “Oh, sweetie, I’m sorry. I just thought of something. Give me a quick minute.” She got up, grabbed her phone, and tried to walk as normally as possible to her bedroom.

“Okay, Wanda, hold it together.” She whispered to herself, voice shaking. She did her breathing exercises to calm herself down so she could get through her mom and wife duties for another hour. Think. Agatha and Nat were in New York, so that was out. She couldn’t talk to Jen, who would just go into PR mode instead of friend mode, which also ruled out Alice since they were attached at the hip. Wanda opened her texts without thinking. If Agatha trusted Rio, she could too, remembering Rio’s offer from Labor Day weekend.

Me:

I know it’s a school night, but it’s an

emergency. Can I come over in about

an hour and a half?

She sent the text and then realized how the ‘emergency’ part might be taken.

Me:

It’s not about Agatha.

Rio:

Of course.

Are you okay?

Me:

I’ll bring the wine.

DO NOT TELL AGATHA.

Rio:

I have the emotional support cat.

Shit.



Wanda smiled before blowing out a breath. She just needed to get through the evening, and then she’d drive over to Rio’s and just let everything out. She knew she could trust Ri,o and she had a feeling that the younger woman, like so many of the women that surrounded her, was wise beyond her years.

She returned to the living room, resuming her position next to Billy.

Vision looked up at her with a questioning look. “Is everything okay, darling?”

“Yes, though, is it okay if I head to Rio’s in a bit? She’s having a bit of an emergency.” She replied in her best concerned friend voice.

Billy raised an eyebrow as Tommy smirked. Nicky, thankfully, couldn’t hear due to his headphones.

Vision ignored his sons, seeing through his wife’s white lie. “Of course, dear. I can take over running lines with Billy if you want to leave now,” he offered. Vision had noticed changes in Wanda’s behavior over the last few months. It put him on edge. He didn’t know what could be brewing next.

Billy’s head popped up as he gave his mom a pleading look. His dad was not good at acting.

Wanda just smiled. “No, it can wait. Boys first.”

This was one of the reasons she had fallen in love with Vision, and she really had loved him. She still did, but it wasn’t a romantic love anymore if it ever had been in the first place . He was sweet and selfless, always putting his family first. He hadn’t had to think before offering to take over for her.

Forty-five minutes later, homework was finished. Wanda gave Vision a quick peck on the cheek goodbye, said goodnight to the boys, grabbed two bottles of wine, and headed to Rio’s. She was going to drive herself, but Vision had insisted on having someone drive her since she had planned on drinking. Not her having a caring and thoughtful husband while she weighed her options of leaving him.



Rio had been on edge since she got the text from Wanda. What could possibly be going on that she was coming to her for? She could only think of one topic that Wanda would come to her for right now, and not Jen or Alice, and that was a certain Russian. Agatha’s ‘wait for her to come to you’ when she had asked had apparently been more prophetic than anything.

Knowing they’d be up late, she was already in PJs and mostly ready for bed, her work bag ready for tomorrow and next to the door. She had chilled wine glasses and random snacks out, an appropriate playlist on, and a fresh box of tissues ready to go. She was ready to be a supportive friend.

Wanda took a deep breath as she stood in front of Rio’s door. She had been here before, normally one door down at Alice’s, and now about to share parts of the last twenty-seven years of her life, of which only a few other people knew every part. She clutched the tote bag that held the wine bottles and knocked, knowing that if Agatha was going to eventually be with Rio, then Wanda had to tell her at some point. Might as well be now.

Rio opened the door and let Wanda in. She closed the door and had barely turned around when Wanda broke into a sob and melted against her. Rio suspected it was bad if that’s all it took. She wrapped her arms around Wanda, and they stood there for a moment.

“Hey, it’s okay. We’re going to talk through it. You don’t have to keep it in, you can talk to me, okay?” She soothed. She knew how it felt to have your best friend sister in another city and no one to talk to. It’s how she had felt for the past few years.

As Wanda’s initial sobs died away, she stepped back, wiping her eyes. “I’m sorry, I’ve just been holding that back for longer than I thought,” she said with a watery laugh.

Rio’s eyes were soft as she gave a small smile. “It’s fine, I’ve got Kleenex and everything ready to go, come on. Tell me everything.” She gestured to the living room.

They settled in on the couch, turning to face each other. Rio opened the wine and poured them both a full glass. She felt they were going to go through both bottles rather quickly. Raven had already found a home on Wanda’s lap, for which Wanda looked grateful. She needed to do something with both hands. Petting a cat with one hand and holding a wine glass in the other worked.

Wanda took a sip of her wine and looked at Rio, trying to decipher how to start the conversation, and she wasn’t sure how. She looked at Rio with big green eyes, “I don't know where to start…”

Rio looked back at Wanda, raising an eyebrow. “Wherever you feel comfortable. Agatha hasn’t told me much of anything, really. She would never betray your trust, so everything I’m about to say is what I have picked up over the past nearly two months.” Rio took a sip of her wine as Wanda nodded.

“What I know is that you and Nat met when you were both 18. She was hired to kill Agatha, and from what I saw when Nat told me that story, she was not planning on you being there, or just you in general. Agatha struck a deal with her, and she joined the merry band of misfits. You married Vision, I assume, because you decided it was easier to fit into the mold created for you , and now you’re realizing that it wasn’t necessarily a mistake, but it’s not the life you wanted

“You and Nat are obviously in love, and you both have been doing a terrible job at hiding it around us, or at all, lately. I don’t know how deep it goes, I don’t know the whole backstory, but that’s what I’ve gathered.” Rio finished her summary, watching for Wanda’s reaction.

Wanda sighed heavily, finishing her wine and pouring another. “You aren’t wrong. That’s an oversimplification, but you aren’t wrong.”

Rio watched Wanda pour her second glass and realized just how much of a toll this had taken. Yes, Wanda drank, they all did, but usually not this quickly. “You can share as much or as little as you want. There is no judgment here, Wanda. I am here for support, and insight if you want it, friendship, and the occasional snide remark.” She said sincerely. These women meant so much to Rio, and she truly wanted to be someone they could come to about anything and everything. She also wanted every single one of them to be happy and live their best life.

Wanda was quiet for a moment, still trying to decide where to begin, before taking a deep breath and letting it out. “Nat once told me that she saw me and knew she couldn’t kill someone that immediately took her breath away, and that’s the only reason we got the upper hand.” She laughed. “How’s that for a line?”

“Man, oldest line in the assassin handbook, I’m sure,” Rio interjected, refilling her now-empty glass. She silently reminded herself that she had to work tomorrow and needed to slow down. She did not have to keep up with the Eastern European who could probably drink two bottles of wine and act out one of her earlier movies from memory.



Wanda laughed, “I was eighteen and Agatha was...twenty-four? When Nat tried to murder her that night. Good thing I looked cute. Agatha wanted to keep it out of the media, so she pulled some legal strings and found out who had sent Nat.” Wanda stopped, waiting for Rio to put a few pieces together.

Rio turned her head slightly. “What year was that?”

“Ninety-Seven.”

Rio remembered dates and years, especially major headlines, even if she had only been eight that year. Her eyes widened. “Isn’t that the year Agatha’s mom died in prison?” She asked cautiously.

Wanda shrugged. “The Russians really hate it when you promise them something you can’t give them, and they lose one of their best assassins. They also love legal favors.”

Rio knocked back her wine and poured them a third glass, emptying the bottle. “Fuck, that’s information I didn’t need . I’m going to pretend I didn’t hear that.”

“That’s what I do, with a lot of things .” Wanda continued. “Anyway, we were our little trio, Agatha sleeping with every woman with a pulse. I was, somewhat interested in guys, but not very. I had my relationships, all PR. I was not out, and I didn’t even know I was gay. I was an absolute mess for two years, though. I had very conflicting feelings for Nat. She was my best friend, but more than that, and Nat? She dated other women during those two years.

“ One night in 1999, I found myself asking Agatha Harkness, this lesbian icon and bad boy, how to know if I was into women and, subsequently, if they were into me. That lovable bitch straight-up laughed at me. I was twenty, still so innocent, and extremely vulnerable, and the bitch laughed at me.” Wanda laughed at the memory.

Rio could imagine a young Agatha, fully knowing what baby gay Wanda was asking, laughing in her face. “Sounds like Agatha. Did she tell you to ‘Just go kiss her, Maximoff’.”

“She didn’t say kiss, but yes!” Wanda exclaimed brightly, her native accent was starting to bleed through because of the alcohol. “You know her so well. Anyway, so I did. I went directly to her room. We were all living together in the original house up on the hill. Her bedroom was right next to mine, and much like you and Agatha, we had already been lying to ourselves.” Wanda gave Rio a knowing look while Rio downed her wine, throwing caution to the wind, and uncorked the second bottle.

Rio raised an eyebrow in question to Wanda, and she nodded, a silent agreement to ditch the glasses. Tomorrow was Friday, she could teach with a hangover if she had to.

Wanda continued with the story. “I crawled into bed with her, something I had done hundreds of times before. It wasn’t unusual for us to cuddle, finding comfort in each other. She gave Rio another pointed look, causing Rio to roll her eyes, huff, and take a drink from the bottle . “And I cupped her face and kissed her.” Wanda finished her glass and set it down.

“What happened next?” Rio breathed, handing Wanda the bottle.

Wanda took a drink before responding. “She kissed me back, and then I cried. I was so afraid to come out. I wasn’t as established as Agatha, I was already seen as this wholesome girl-next-door, date-the-boy type of star. I didn’t know what to do. We agreed to just keep it a secret and see what happened.”

“You were already in love,” Rio stated, trying to remain serious, though her words were beginning to slur. How strong was this wine?

Wanda nodded as a tear fell down her cheek. She wiped it away and continued. “We kept it hidden for years, Agatha kept telling me there was room in the world for more than one lesbian superstar, but I wouldn’t listen. I wanted kids, I wanted to live an open life, and so did Natasha. Anytime I brought up coming out, our PR shut it down. That was way before Jen. There were so many arguments and fights. We were madly in love, but I couldn’t hurt my career. It was all I had , or so I thought . I kept having these bearded and PR relationships, though I didn’t need them. They really were just to keep the rumors at bay. They were all with famous men who had gay rumors. After the last one, I had decided I was done, I couldn’t do it anymore.” She took the bottle back from Rio, accent coming out even more . “We might need more alcohol. Shit’s about to get fucking shittastic.”

“On it. Eat a snack.” Rio gestured towards the table in front of them as she gathered the empty bottles and glasses, left the half bottle, and went to the kitchen. “How old were you at this point?” Rio called, words slurring, as she looked in the refrigerator, chose two ciders, and fixed two large tumblers of ice water with electrolyte packets .

“Twenty-free, I mean, three.” She called back, giggling at her misspeak, opened a bag of popcorn, and grabbed a handful.

Rio laughed while she opened the wine and came back to the couch, resuming her position. She had done the math and looked at Wanda, understanding why she had been sent to get more alcohol even though they had half a bottle left .

“You met Vision and things got complicated.”

Wanda nodded as she took another drink. “I met Vision. He was consulting on a movie, and for the first time, I actually felt something deep for a man, or so I thought . Color me startled.”

“What color is startled?” Rio took the bottle back and a handful of popcorn herself. She was completely captured by Wanda’s story and was understanding everyone even more with every word.

“Scarlet, I think.” Wanda resituated her legs before continuing. “Nat and I had kept things so under wraps, Vis had no idea I liked women. He was so sweet and caring. Leave it to me to fall for the two most amazing, understanding, selfless people in the world at the same damn time.”

“What did you tell Aga…Nat?” Rio’s sapphics were running together with her increased blood alcohol content.

“I didn’t have to. She came to me. She wasn’t mad, she wasn’t jealous. She just took me in her arms and asked me what I wanted. I was falling in love with someone else, again, or so I thought at the time, and the woman I also loved, who was madly in love with me, asked me what I wanted. She asked me what would make me happy. I was twenty-four, I didn’t know, and I told her that.

“I told her that I was in love with both of them, but that wasn’t fair to either of them. That beautiful woman looked at me and said that she would always be with me, even if it wasn’t the way that she wanted to be. That she would always be mine, even if I wasn’t completely hers. My heart shattered that night.” Wanda grabbed another tissue, a pile of them already in her lap. Raven had jumped down when she had shifted positions earlier.

“What did Vision say?” Rio asked, a tissue in her hand as well. The second bottle was now gone, and they had moved on to their ciders, an unspoken agreement that they were done after that. They were slurring words, and Rio was proud she could understand Wanda through her now heavy accent, and tears were flowing from both.

“That he understood. Can you believe that asshole ? So I went to Agatha. She knew everything, of course , she had been the support for Nat and me both, but never caught in the middle. She, of course, had her own brand of dry humor, and we listened to a lot of Melissa Etheridge and Indigo Girls. She told me that I had to do what I felt was right for me and what I could live with. I had to decide if I could live with potentially losing my fans. She had built her career on being gay. I hadn’t. However, fuck the people I lost because my happiness was worth everything.” Wanda’s eyes were watery.

Rio knew what happened next. She remembered the grainy pictures. She and Alice had been teenagers and were shocked when they saw the headlines in their favorite tabloids of the time.

“Then the pictures broke,” Rio said softly.

“I was twenty-four? Twenty-five? Vis and I were officially dating. Nat remained on security, of course, when that grainy picture of Nat and I making out at a concert was splashed everywhere.” Wanda sighed.

“Your engagement was announced soon after,” Rio whispered, remembering.

Wanda nodded. “PR’s idea. Vis said we could call the whole idea off. He wasn’t even mad. Nat and I were still dating, we just kept it a secret like we had been. It was a moment of weakness, we were at The Killers concert and got caught up in the moment. Nat and I always do at concerts, music is important to us. However, studios had already started calling with questions, worried about their bottom line and how this would affect it and current projects. It was a nightmare. I had to make a statement that it wasn’t a kiss, it was an entire thing. Anyway, we were married in early 2005, shortly after I turned twenty-six .” Wanda blew her nose and placed the empty popcorn bag on the table.

“How did Nat take it?” Rio couldn’t imagine watching the person she was in love with marry someone else.

“Hard. She understood, but she hated it. She stayed by my side though, always mine, never hers.” Wanda swallowed a sob. “So we went through the first few years. I really thought I was in love with Vision then. My star rose, I won awards, and the public forgot about my ‘lesbian scare’. Agatha had Nicky, and Vis could tell I was less than happy. So we started trying for a baby and had the twins. I remember wishing that they had been Nat’s , though she had been there for everything . She would have been such a great mother.” Wanda knew that Nat couldn’t have children, not something she would be telling Rio. The closest Nat would come to having kids was the boys, but Wanda so badly wished at that moment that it had been different .

“As it was, Vis knew the role Nat played . He always knew, it’s like they had some unspoken agreement. I had three people doting on me while I was pregnant. As I’m sure you can guess, Agatha was the third .” Wanda smiled at the memories, a small mountain of crumbled tissues in her lap.

“Natasha Romanov is a great father, I mean mother,” Rio told her. “Those boys have three moms and a dad, and they, and all adults, know it. She would move planets for those boys, you, and even for Vision if she thought it would make you happy.”

Wanda gave a watery smile. “You’re right, and she has. She also refuses to date anyone else , no matter how much I tell her to.”

“You would hate it if she did.” They were well past drinking now, trying to sober up a little, still slurring words here and there.

Wanda nodded. “It would kill me. Just as I know it kills her to see me live the life we dreamed of, but with someone else .” She took another breath. “Around the time the boys turned one, and I’d been married for six years, I started to get restless .” Wanda saw the look on Rio’s face. “I know, it’s been thirteen years since then, yes. Vis knows how I feel , but he’s never said anything. He gives me my girl time and my Nat time . He’s an amazing husband and father.

“ We have an agreement. Kisses, chaste touches. I think Vis would be okay if Nat and I were fully together, but that’s not fair to him, and I can’t be dishonest. I can’t break our vows. They mean something to me. I also can’t risk that leaking. I’m walking a tight line as it is.

“He deserves to be happy. I’m just so afraid of hurting the boys, and at this point, I’ve sacrificed so much for this life, for my career. I hurt two people that I love, albeit in different ways, and I don’t want to do it again.

“I don’t want to hurt him, and at the same time, I’m not in love with him anymore, if I ever really was . I am still madly in love with her, just as much as I was when we were 20. I’ve been in love with her for 25 years, Rio, and all I have to show for that is a few years where we were truly together, and decades of stolen moments. I want her. I want her to be the one doing homework time with me, her to be the one helping me in the kitchen, walking red carpets, and hugging me when I win awards. I want to stand up at that podium holding an Oscar and say ‘thank you to my wife for always believing in me’, not just knowing I mean her when I mention my friends and family.” Wanda’s tears were flowing freely again, as were Rio’s.

“I also feel like I stole twenty years of their lives, Rio. That’s twenty years neither of them can get back.”

Rio leaned forward and hugged her, Wanda hugging her back, burying her face into Rio’s shoulder. They stayed that way for a few minutes before Rio drew back.

Wanda wiped her face and looked at Rio through bloodshot eyes.

“What if it’s too late?”

Rio looked at her with disbelief. “Have you seen the way she looks at you? Nat is as madly in love with you as you are with her. The decision you have to make is, are you willing to do this? I mean, really do this. Live your truth? There’s no keeping this secret. Not with two teenage boys and as famous as you are, social media, everything. You obviously have to talk with your husband, but Wanda. You’ve been living your own personal ‘Good Luck, Babe’ your entire adult life. You deserve good things, and you deserve happiness. How do you expect to set that example for your boys if you don’t do it for yourself? Billy is gay if you haven’t noticed. How do you think he’d react if you kept this hidden for another 30 years? A good half of the public already thinks you're fucking Agatha, there’s a subsection that already ships you and Nat. All of THAT aside, you have to live for YOU, Wanda. We can all see that you’re driving yourself insane. You’ve been spiraling the entire time I’ve known you, and you two aren’t subtle.

“You have not stolen time from either of them. Vision will always support you, and Natasha? Wanda, honey, the moment you are able to, we aren’t going to hear from either of you for at least a week. She only has eyes for you. Talk to your husband, get your woman. As you said, she will always be yours, go be hers.” Rio finished and handed Wanda a new Kleenex.

Wanda looked at the pile of tissues in her lap. “You’re right, you know.”

“I usually am.”

“When will you follow your own advice and go after your happiness?” Wanda asked, not completely innocently.

“We’re not here about me right now. I have my own tragic story, but tonight isn’t that night.” Rio said, putting a hand on Wanda’s knee and squeezing. Wanda returned a slight nod .

“I have some things to do as it is. Like, get home and talk to my husband for starters .” Wanda exhaled a shaky breath and texted her driver.

⭐⭐⭐

Rio gave Wanda a long hug goodbye and wished her luck, telling her to update her as soon as she had one. Wanda promised she would and thanked her before walking to the car, only stumbling twice.

Rio cleaned up, turning off lights throughout the apartment as she stumbled to her room herself. Once she had finished the bedtime routine and gotten situated, making sure to take Tylenol to head off any ill effects in the morning, she realized it was 2 am in New York, and she hadn’t texted Agatha.

Tonight with Wanda had been an eye-opener. She knew that something was going on between the two redheads, but a 25-year-long affair of sorts? That she hadn’t expected. She thought about her feelings towards Agatha. Was she living her truth? Was she being honest with herself? No. You want to kiss that woman. You have dreamt of that woman. You have had impure thoughts of that woman. You want to throw that stupid rule out the window and…

Her phone dinged, interrupting her thoughts.

Agatha💜

Hey! I’m so sorry I didn’t text.

Nat and I went out and drinking

occurred. Never challenge Nat to

anything.

Me:

It’s okay! I was up late grading.

I’ll remember that.

That was believable. She had promised Wanda she wouldn’t tell Agatha about tonight until Wanda was ready for her to know. Rio and Wanda were even closer now, and considering how much Wanda meant to Agatha, Rio would keep her promises. This family Rio had been welcomed into meant everything to her.

Besides, she was pretty buzzed hammered herself, which was dangerous, and she needed to say goodnight. She was trying to keep herself friend-zoned.

Agatha💜

That sounds extremely boring.

We’re flying back tomorrow.

I’ll see you at the game.

Me:

I’m glad you’ll be back in time!

I’m sure Nicky’s missed you.

What she wanted to say was “I’ve missed you.” She didn’t need to go there, though. She kept thinking about Wanda and Nat. Not yet.

Agatha💜

He’s a teenage boy. He doesn’t

care. I’ve missed you though. I’ll see you

tomorrow. Nat said I’m not allowed to

text you when I’ve been drinking.

Goodnight Ri.

Me:

I’ve missed you, too

Goodnight, Aggie.

If Agatha said it first, then she could say it back, and they were absolutely close enough that it was time to use her nickname. This was a start.

Agatha ran into Nat’s room at the Manhattan apartment that she and Wanda owned, jumping on her bed as though the numbers in her age had magically flipped around . Agatha shoved her phone into Nat’s face as if the cute girl had just checked yes on the ‘Do you like me?’ note.

After all, she had, hadn’t she?

“She said she missed me too and called me Aggie!” Agatha said uncharacteristically. She was well past buzzed.

“You don’t date teachers, or at all ,” Nat mocked.

“No, I’m just excited to be missed,” Agatha stated, regaining her composure.



Nat grinned as her phone dinged, and she picked it up to check what was most likely her nightly text from Wanda. She covered her mouth with her hand, and the tears formed automatically.

Natasha Romanov did not create tears.

“What happened? What’s wrong?” Agatha asked automatically, worried someone had died.

Nat turned the phone towards her, her hand shaking.

❤️ Wanda ❤️

I know you’ve always

been mine. Can I be yours

now?

Agatha’s eyes went wide. “WHAT?!” She shouted.

Chapter End Notes

I hope that I did that justice.

I'm going to be spacing updates out a few days. I go back to work on Monday, so I'm trying to write ahead and NOT just post everything.

Thank you for all your comments and kudos!

I'd Be Smart to Walk Away

Chapter Summary

Wanda makes her decision.
It's Homecoming Weekend for SHIELD.
These women.

Chapter Notes

Thank you to the ever-amazing DaretoDream82 for being my sounding board and for reminding me that y'all can't read my mind.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

When Wanda returned home that night, Vision had been waiting for her in their bedroom, reading a book, knowing what was coming. He had anticipated this moment for the past twenty years of their lives and had noticed Wanda’s increasing distance, her frequent absences if the boys weren’t around, and the increased girl time. He gave her the space and time she needed. He would always love her, but he also knew what this had been and he had been grateful for it, but if she hadn’t come forward soon, he was going to. He deserved true happiness as much as she did.

There had been tears and conversation, but absolutely zero hard feelings on his side. They agreed to wait until after the holidays to tell the boys, and Wanda needed to talk to Jen about how they would approach the media. They would work out co-parenting and divorce details later. Neither planned on being nasty about it and they had been more like roommates than anything for a long time. Vision had joked about giving Nat the same shovel talk she had given him once, except she actually could make him disappear without a trace. Wanda had just smiled. She had sent that text to Nat before talking to Vision, knowing Nat wouldn’t respond. Nat would want to have that conversation in person. She also knew what Agatha would do.

Aggie

We will be home around 8 am Cali

time.

Me:

I thought you weren’t coming in

until early afternoon?

Aggie

Well when you basically tell the

love of your life that you’re leaving

your husband for her, your sister

makes phone calls to ready the

jet and deliver her back to you.

Wanda laughed and rolled her eyes. Of course, they both read in between the lines.

Aggie

Did you doubt me?

Me:

I knew I could trust you.

I love you.

Thank you.

Aggie

Be at my house so boys

don’t see. I’m sure they

know. I love you too.

Of course.



Nat had barely made it into Agatha’s house before Wanda had jumped in her arms, legs around her waist. She hugged her before kissing her deeply, sliding her hand to cup Nat’s jaw. Nat lowered Wanda slowly to her feet, careful not to break the kiss, running her tongue across her bottom lip.

Agatha cleared her throat, causing the two women to break their kiss.

Nat looked into Wanda’s eyes. “Yes, you can be mine.” She answered.

Wanda just nodded.

Agatha clapped her hands together, walking past them. “Great, now that that’s cleared up. Jen will be here in an hour, football game this evening, and kid?” She turned to Wanda, “I am so proud of you.” She winked before ascending the stairs.



“WHAT?!” Jen exclaimed out of excitement, surprise, and confusion. There were a lot of emotions, and she said as much. “Wanda, I am so glad you are finally clearing up your decades-long situationship, I am. But right now? Right before awards season? You just signed with Marvel!” Jen wasn’t sure how she was going to spin this, package it, anything. Wanda and Vision had been one of Hollywood’s very few long-lasting couples.

“Jen. Look, Wanda coming out of the closet will not surprise many. Marvel is going the way of queer anyway, so is Disney. We spin it positively. I’m gay and not at all monogamous, and they signed me. Everything will be fine. Our new movie? Wanda and I literally play lovers and have very convincing sex in it. Calm your tits Kale, or let Alice do it for you.” She smirked, eliciting a blush and an eye-roll. “Besides, America’s Stepford Couple will not announce it to the kids, let alone the rest of the world, until after the holidays. Nat and Wanda will just be more open and cuddly around us, but still play it cool everywhere else. Don’t expect them to go gaying it up everywhere else yet, okay? That’s my job.” Agatha explained.

Jen looked a lot calmer. “Okay, that makes me feel…oddly better.”

The couple in question was sitting together looking like two teenagers who had just told their parents that the condom had failed.

Jen looked at them both. “I am very happy for both of you. Truly.”

Wanda beamed. “Me too.” She turned to Nat and kissed her cheek. “Are you okay with this?”

Nat turned and looked at her. “I finally get you, Wanda -body, soul, home, and publically? Yes. I’m okay with it.” She gave her a chaste kiss as Agatha groaned again, smiling this time.

“Now there’s two lovesick couples? Gross.”

Jen waved her hands out in front of her. “No one said anything about love. Alice and I are taking it slow. It’s a very strong like.” She denied.

“Debatable,” Wanda teased. “To be fair, I’ve loved Nat for 25 years.” Nat just nodded.

“Yes, but now you guys will be all open about it.” Agatha grimaced.

“Right, and you and Rio don’t look at each other like middle schoolers discovering their first crush,” Nat said.

Agatha was proud of Wanda for finally coming to this decision. She knew it was one Wanda would make eventually, but was surprised Wanda hadn’t talked to her about it first. She had been just as shocked as Nat was when she received that text last night. She would either ask Wanda about it or Wanda would come to her, however, she suspected Wanda had talked to a certain brunette, and that made her heart soar that her sister was confiding in her new…close friend. Friend.

“We need to make sure that your girlfriends are on the same page,” Wanda said, seeing if Agatha caught what she said.

“I’ll text Alice now,” Jen said, side-eyeing Agatha, waiting for the response.

“I’ll text Ri,” Agatha said automatically. Her eyes suddenly went wide as it hit her. “SHE IS NOT MY GIRLFRIEND!” She yelled.

Nat fell into Wanda’s lap from laughing so hard.

“Only ALICE calls her RI!” Wanda shouted back.

Jen texted every word to Alice.

Across town, Alice broke out into laughter while half her class did makeup work, and the other half debated the believability of various movies their parents starred in.



⭐⭐⭐

September ended and bled into October, Homecoming Week at SHIELD. For some reason, the powers that be, one Coach Rogers, thought that it would be a good idea to play their rivals for the big game. Bayside High. The schools had exchanged pranks and taunts for decades, and this year was no different. SHIELD may have been a K-12 school with a unique student body, but it still had a high school with teenagers with a penchant for bad decision-making. Nicky Harkness and Tommy Maximoff had surprisingly stayed out of trouble so far this year, surprising everyone. They had also met two girls from Bayside and asked them to the Homecoming dance that upcoming weekend after finding out that the guys they usually hung out with were pretty douchey.

That was how Nicky and Tommy ended up on A.C. Slater and Zack Morris’s radar. It didn’t help that Slater was also Bayside’s star quarterback and Morris was their star running back.

Tensions that week had been running high. Teachers were on alert, security teams were on alert, and even parents were on alert. Dressup days were decades-themed, and there were different events held every day. While Rio always enjoyed themed weeks and homecoming in general, she was not looking forward to quarterly exams the following week. However, it was a necessary evil to get to the fall break, which kicked off her series of events with Agatha. The premiere, NYC weekend with SNL, and then Halloween.

She had been looking forward to the second half of October since it had been decided over Labor Day weekend, and she found herself daydreaming about it. She was also anxious about spending a weekend, just the two of them, in New York. They would be staying at Agatha’s place, and while she could stay in the guest room, she knew where she would actually sleep. Her feelings for Agatha kept growing and becoming harder to ignore. Every touch, every lingering glance, every time she was so close to Agatha that she could hear her breathing and smell the scent of her skin. She needed to at least share with Agatha why she had her rule in place. She just worried that once that was all out in the open, Agatha would see her differently. That scared her almost as much as losing her did.

Thankfully, other than some trash-talking online and at the game, everything had gone smoothly between the two schools. While there had been several almost fights among players, students, and parents, nothing had happened at the game. The players were at a celebratory after-game party, and the six women were at Agatha’s. Agatha was still on high alert, however, just waiting for the other shoe to drop.

“I just feel like something’s going to happen,” Agatha said for the fourth or fifth time since they had been at her house. Each pair of women had a designated couch now. Agatha and Rio had the middle couch, Nat and Wanda to their left, and Alice and Jen on their right. They had gotten pizza, and their various drinks were scattered around.

“Aggie,” Nat started “There’s security with the boys, and they’re fine.” She reassured her.

“So they’re going to a high school dance with girls from a rival school who are technically dating football players from the other team. What’s the worst that could happen?” Jen teased. “Did they learn that from you, Harkness?” She added.

“Mother, son, and nephew bonding?” Rio asked before looking over at Wanda. “You need better role models for your sons, Maximoff.”

“Et, tu, Brute?” Agatha looked at Rio, feigning shock and hurt.

Alice laughed. “At least they know how to fight.”

“Real reassuring everyone,” Agatha said.

“Usually, I’m the one that worries, and I’m not worried,” Wanda said nonchalantly, snuggling up against Nat with a yawn.

“As long as I can focus on positive PR, I’m good,” Jen added, her arm around Alice, smiling at her.

“You’re all disgusting,” Agatha murmured. Rio was pressed against her side, and if anyone who didn’t know their status saw them, they would assume they too, were dating. The painful truth tore at Agatha. She was getting close to wanting to ditch her rule of not dating parents. There weren’t many rules Agatha was afraid of breaking or bending. She had done a lot of things to survive and get where she was today. This, however, was more for her son. The no relationships period rule was for her. She had had one relationship in her life, and it had gone poorly. No one knew about it, and only Wanda knew snippets. Hell, Wanda didn’t even know who the woman was. It would always be Agatha’s secret.

She had to get to a point where she could share that with Rio, and she didn’t know if she could.

⭐⭐⭐

The boys and their dates were taking pictures around the compound and looked adorable. Tommy, Nicky, Billy, and Billy’s ‘friend’, Eddie, were wearing matching suits. Tommy’s date, a girl named Kelly from Bayside, was wearing a silver spaghetti strap dress with a sweetheart neckline and a flared skirt that came to her knees. She had matching silver heels, and her hair was pulled up. Tommy had a matching silver vest and bowtie with his suit to match. Jessie’s dress was a strapless purple number that managed to be fitted but appropriate for a sixteen-year-old. She had black heels, and her hair reminded Agatha of her own, long, wild curls with a mind of their own. Nicky’s vest and bowtie were purple to match Jessie’s dress, and Agatha teared up at how he looked simultaneously grown up and like he was still her little boy. Then there was Billy and Eddie. Billy had a blue vest and red bow tie, and Eddie with a blue bow tie and red vest. The six of them all looked amazing. The three women who had known Nicky and the twins since birth had tears in their eyes. Even Rio was tearing up.

Rio took pictures and sent them to Alice, even though she and Jen were out on their own date.

After the pictures, the teenagers left for dinner and the dance. Everything had gone fine the night before after the game, and Agatha was still oddly anxious waiting for something bad to happen.

The last pictures they had taken were taken on a balcony at Agatha’s house, and they had all come back through the house.

“I’m getting a drink,” Agatha announced.

“We are going to Nat’s place for date night since we can do that,” Wanda stated, grabbing Nat’s hand and leading her towards the door. “Love you both, have fun, confess your feelings, bye!” She said, closing the door behind them.

“You’re a bitch, and there’s nothing to confess!” Agatha yelled before the door closed.

Rio was leaning against the bar with her face buried in her hands, ears pink. “Why do they keep doing this shit?” She asked.

“At this point, it’s just poking the bear,” Agatha said, pouring whiskey into her coke, handing one to Rio. “Cheers to shitty friends that mean well.”

She lifted her head, taking the glass with one hand and putting the other on the cool marble of the bar. “Cheers,” Rio said, lifting her glass as she clinked it against Agatha’s.

They turned and assumed their usual positions on the couch, their sides pressed against each other.

Rio poked her tongue in her cheek, trying to decide if she wanted to stay in safe waters or go deeper. She had so many things she wanted to ask, but she wasn’t sure if she was ready to cross into those territories quite yet.

Agatha decided for both of them.

“What are you wearing to the premiere?” Agatha asked, trying to sound innocent.

Rio looked at her, pretend shock on her face. “You know good and well that I have been sworn to secrecy, and no one is allowed to say anything!”

Jen had given them all very specific instructions not to tell each other what they were wearing. They were all getting ready together, and they would see then, but for some reason, it was very hush-hush until then. Agatha didn’t understand it either, and she had been doing this her whole life.

“We are riding together. We are getting ready together with the same make-up and hair team, at my house. I do not understand why we can’t tell each other. Yet, just to prepare you, it’s this way for every red-carpet event. Whether it’s a premiere or an awards show.” Agatha looked over at her to gauge her reaction.

Rio met her gaze, trying to read the look.

“Why would you need to prepare me?” Rio asked, searching her eyes. She needed Agatha to confirm it,

“Well,” Agatha was trying to put it in just the right words. “For any future awards shows or premieres, I was hoping you would go with me. As my new standing red carpet buddy. Wanda will probably start showing off her not exactly new girlfriend, and Nicky is getting too cool to go with his mom…” She trailed off, she hadn’t broken eye contact with Rio, and while she was trying to play it off, her eyes were soft and drew her in.

Rio knew this was Agatha’s way of asking her to stay in her life, a commitment of sorts. Rio nodded, her own eyes were a reflection of Agatha’s. “Yes.” She whispered. She didn’t know when they had leaned into one another or how they had, once again, gotten this dangerously close.

Agatha could feel Rio’s breath on her face and her body was buzzing everywhere it connected with Rio’s. She so badly wanted to cross that line, but if they were going to do that, some things needed to be said first, and that wasn’t happening tonight. She quickly turned and stood.

“I need another drink? Do you?” She asked.

Rio nodded, clearing her head. “Just water.” Water was much safer.

⭐⭐⭐

They had fallen asleep watching The Craft, Rio tucked against Agatha, her arms around her, when Agatha’s phone rang. She jolted awake and looked at the time. It was only 10:30 and she hadn’t heard Nicky come in, she grabbed the phone and saw Lilia Calderu’s name on the screen, which shocked her into alertness.

“Lilia? What’s wrong?”

Rio had woken up when Agatha’s phone went off. When she heard the panic in her voice and the name, she grabbed her shoes and phone.

“We’ll be right there.” Agatha hung up and went to call Wanda, only to see that Rio was hanging up her phone.

“Wanda and Nat are on the way here, and we’ll go to the school together. Wanda’s calling Vis.” She said, grabbing their purses.

“How?” Agatha asked as they headed to the door.

“Your voice, the look on your face, and I could hear everything Lilia said through the phone.”

“So you know the Bayside football players showed up, and there was a fight?” Agatha relayed.

“Yes. She didn’t say how serious it was. Shouldn’t be too bad, though.”

When the four women reached the school, Jen was already there in full PR mode, as were a few other PR reps for some other families. As it turned out, it was worse than they thought.

Nicky, Tommy, and a few other football players from both the varsity and junior varsity teams had decided to stop by Bayside on the way to the dance. The victory the night before had been solid, 28 -7, and they had learned about some rather degrading treatment of the girls on campus by boys across the school in general. The SHIELD team took it upon themselves to cross out ‘Tigers’ on the field, replaced it with ‘pussycats’, and stole the head of their mascot’s costume, flying it up the flagpole on the SHIELD campus. They had used paint that would wash off the field easily and planned to return the head on Monday. No major damage and it was all pretty harmless. The problem came when the Bayside football team crashed the dance as students were departing, and a fight broke out in the parking lot.

Agatha was more worried about legal ramifications and the physical well-being of the kids than anything else, which was what Wanda and most parents were also worried about.

“Nicholas, are you okay?” She asked, rushing to where he was sitting in the clinic.

“I’m fine, Mom. Slater won’t be able to open his left eye for a while though,” he said with a small smile and winced when she hugged him.

“Nicky? What’s wrong?” She asked, alarm.

“He didn’t block correctly and took a blow to the ribcage,” Nat said, storming in. “I checked the cameras, and have already had a word with security.” Her accent was thick as she joined Wanda and Vision with the twins.

Agatha shook her head. “Will you and Tommy please not start shit?” She asked, checking him over for any other damages.

Nicky just scoffed as he looked down. Rio had sidled up next to Agatha and had a comforting hand on her lower back, helping to ease her anxiety. It had worked, as Agatha wasn’t shaking as she had been before.

Rio put her hand on Nicky’s shoulder and squeezed. “I’m just glad you and your cousins didn’t get seriously hurt.” She smiled at him, and he returned it.

Agatha knew how they looked right now, and she didn’t care. Let people talk. Jen could spin it to be like another Wagatha situation. Why that stung her, she wasn’t sure. You know why. Her inner voice said. She ignored it as she put her arm around Rio’s waist and gave her a quick side hug.

No one was pressing charges, though there had been a heated debate over who had been in the wrong and what the appropriate consequences were. Security footage proved that Bayside had attacked first and SHIELD students had defended. Tommy had a few scrapes and bruises but was fine. Billy had stayed out of it. A few other students were a little scuffed up. Students were picked up, and the excitement died down. Agatha had left the clinic and joined Lilia in dealing with the few police officers who had shown up. Wanda and Vision gathered the twins to go home, letting Rio know, while Nat figured out exactly why security had been nowhere to be found during the scuffle. Jen and a few other reps were scrubbing pictures off of social media, Alice was talking with the teachers who had chaperoned the event, and Rio was sitting with Nicky.

“Your mother was worried.” She said, “What actually happened?”

“What do you mean?”

“You know what I mean.”

“Those guys are real jerks. It’s funny how people think we’re these elitist snobs, or completely out of touch, but we’re not. They’re the ones who don’t know how to treat people.” He said, looking at the ground. They were sitting in the office lobby now, an ice pack on his rib cage. “The girls over there aren’t treated nicely by the guys and they say mean things to the LGBTQ+ kids…it’s like a stereotypical high school you see on TV, Rio.” He looked like a lost puppy, hurt to discover the world's injustices.

“I know, kid. I’ll make sure to tell Lilia, and maybe she can talk to the principal. What could you do about it? How could you spread awareness about how not to be a teenage douchecanoe? You’re the child of one of the most famous people in the world. You have a platform.” She knew he wanted to make a difference and knew that he could if he wanted to.

“Do you honestly believe that? That I could inspire others?” He asked, looking hopeful.

“Your mom is Agatha Fucking Harkness, kid. Which makes you Nicholas Fucking Harkness. You are unstoppable.” She grinned and ruffled his hair. “You can do anything.”

Agatha had watched the exchange, and while she wasn’t sure what was said, she could see it had been a heartfelt conversation that ended in grins and her son being comforted by the woman she was falling for.

“She is just as scared, hurt, and cautious as you are. If you would just open up and take a chance, you might find love again.” Lilia said.

Agatha jumped. She had no idea when the woman had drawn up beside her, but here she was in all of her wisdom. Agatha looked around, noticing that everyone had left or was in the process of doing so.

“What are the consequences?” Completely ignoring what the principal had said.

“For the boys? They’ll clean up Bayside’s field tomorrow and do whatever exercises Rogers has for them. However, I believe Nicky has something to tell me, and an investigation into student conduct will be open at Bayside. He’s a good kid with a good heart. Like his mother, despite her rough exterior,” she looked over at Agatha. “For you? Well, if you don’t finally open up, then you lose that amazing woman in there, and you go back to your old ways, and you’re as miserable as you ever were. If you do open up and get rid of that stupid rule? Well, hopefully, she follows suit and everything works out.” Lilia looked back at Rio, who was looking at things on her phone with Nicky and pointing at something on the screen.

“You’re not talking in riddles right now. Why?” She looked over questioningly. Lilia was rarely this candid.

“You know why. Now take those two and go home, Agatha.” With that, Lilia turned and left.

Agatha just shook her head and walked through the doors into the office. “C’mon, you two. Let’s go.” She smiled and led the way out.

As they rode back to the compound, Agatha thought about the night's events. Rio had stayed. She had called Wanda automatically without hesitating, hadn’t once complained, had swooped in, comforted Nicky, sat with him, and waited with him. Even now, Nicky had fallen asleep leaning on Rio. This woman, who had only been in their lives for two months, had stepped up, been a calming presence, and shown up for both of them without being asked. Like a partner would.

It was two a.m. by the time they got home. Nicky hugged Rio and thanked her, before hugging Agatha and whispering ‘I like her’ in his mom’s ear. He waved and went upstairs to bed.

Rio yawned. “I’m going to head out myself.” She grabbed her bag and turned before Agatha caught her arm.

“It’s been a long night and a rough one at that. Will you…” Agatha hated being vulnerable, and she had never actually asked Rio to stay before. It just had worked out that way. Rio turned, looking at Agatha.

Rio knew that they were in deep at this point. Very deep. To the point that they would soon need oxygen masks. The normally collected woman before her was anything but. While the night's events had turned out to be a minor innocent, Rio had seen the panic and fear on Agatha’s face and heard it in her voice. She could see the adrenaline wearing off and knew that she was still in need of comfort. The great Agatha Harkness, as rough around the edges as she could be, just needed someone to hold her and tell her everything was going to be okay. At that moment, Rio desperately wanted to be that person for her. She would take away all of her pain, trauma, and fear if she could. Forever and Always. She just nodded before responding. “Okay, Agatha.”

Rio knew that it would have been so easy to close the gap between them right now. To cup Agatha’s face before sliding her hands into her hair as she kissed her, and damn. She wanted to. She wanted to feel her lips against her, wanted to explore her mouth with her tongue, bite her lip, swallow her moans... No. You can’t risk it.

Agatha thought for a moment and knew she had to think quickly. She was vulnerable, what Lilia had said was still fresh in her mind, and yet, she didn’t want to fuck anything up. If she and Rio went upstairs, she wouldn’t be able to trust herself not to do something stupid. The couch was the safer option of the two. Letting Rio go home had been the better option, but she needed her. Honestly, she and Rio needed to be on different continents to be safest, yet here they were.

She led Rio to the couch and they positioned themselves in a way that Agatha had her back leaning against Rio’s chest, Rio’s legs on either side of Agatha, Rio’s back against the arm of the couch. Rio played with Agatha’s hair as they turned on a random TV show.

“What were you and Nicky talking about?” Agatha asked, remembering how intently they had been speaking.

“Oh! Something is happening at Bayside. We need to talk to Calderu. Nicky said that it’s a very typical high school like you would see on TV; bullying, homophobia, misogyny. He wants to do something about it, and we talked about how he can use his proximity to fame to rally other teens to use their collective voices and be a force for change. He’s such a great kid. You should be so proud of yourself, Aggie,” Rio’s voice was full of admiration and awe.

Agatha’s heart swelled, not only because of Nicky but because of how Rio had talked about him. “Thank you for being there for him. That meant a lot to me. Just the way that you jumped in and took control of everything.” She was thankful that Rio couldn't see her face as she bit back tears. “Not many people would have done that.”

“I care about you both, you’re…important to me, Agatha. With that comes Nicky. I’m fond of the kid, whether I’m his teacher or not. He’s an extension of you. I’m going to treat him like he’s mine.” She smoothed the ends of Agatha’s hair. You might as well tattoo ‘Just Friends’ on your foreheads at this point to remind yourselves.’ It was one of the many reasons this couldn’t go any further. She couldn’t hurt Nicky like that.

The words ‘like he’s mine’ stayed with Agatha and landed, like so many other things with Rio, in her heart. It’s what she had wanted, wasn’t it? Someone who truly cared about them both? Someone who wasn’t there to try and be a second parent necessarily but wasn’t afraid to love and guide him either?

Maybe they could just wait until Nicky graduated Agatha thought. Jen and Alice had waited years to finally get together, and Nat waited twenty years to fully get Wanda back. It was less than two years, and as smart as Nicky was, he could probably graduate earlier. Then she would just need to find a way to open up about why she had shied away from relationships. No, they wouldn’t make it that long, either. They were doing a bad job at making the two-month mark. Useless lesbians.

Despite her inner turmoil, the adrenaline had worn off and Agatha couldn’t resist sleep anymore, not while Rio absentmindedly played with her hair, the smell of Rio’s eucalyptus shampoo filling her nostrils.

Rio felt Agatha’s body become heavier against hers, knowing she had succumbed to sleep. She knew Agatha needed it and was glad she had finally let herself find it. Meanwhile, Rio was mid-crisis. She was attempting to suppress feelings and recreate boundaries, but those lines were all but erased at this point. She managed to sleep for a few hours, but only due to her personal Agatha-shaped weighted blanket.

She remembered they had shifted in their sleep and was not entirely surprised to wake up sandwiched between the back of the couch and Agatha. Agatha’s back was flush against Rio’s front, Rio’s arm over her middle, Agatha holding her hand. She breathed in the familiar lavender scent that she now associated with the woman in her arms and let herself to just be. It was moments like this when Rio didn't have to be scared of Agatha, the possibility of them together, and all the ways it could go horribly wrong. Rio relaxed, imagining all the good without the risk of the bad.

Before she fell asleep again, Rio wiggled away, grateful that Agatha was such a heavy sleeper. Rio quietly gathered her things and left. She needed to prepare for the final week before break and had things to think about.

As she glanced back at Agatha, sleeping peacefully, she tried to crystalize the scene and how happy she felt right now in her heart and mind. I can't lose her , she thought. No matter what I am feeling, I have to turn it off. It can't turn out that way again .







Chapter End Notes

WHEN?! C'mon you two!

So far, I am a chapter ahead of myself and I hope to post every few days. The next chapter should be Thursday!

You Came Along and Changed Everything

Chapter Summary

Feelings are starting to be confronted, and a premiere is attended.

Chapter Notes

This chapter was going to include a few other events, but it would have been 15k or more words. So, I decided to split it. I hope to post Saturday or Sunday. Winter break ended this week and those darn students really cut into writing time. However, snow days today and tomorrow hopefully will provide the time to write...except we're moving this weekend. So...we'll see what happens!

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Rio found herself storming into Alice’s apartment Sunday evening after a day of preparing for mid-terms and quarterly finals. She had also been reflecting on her feelings all day. She was happy being Agatha’s friend, she really was. There were also feelings there, and she needed to get them out and talk to the one person she could always count on to help her keep a level head. The one person who knew everything. She sent Alice a quick text to let her know she was coming over in case Jen had been there. Rio was agitated and had to get out her frustrations. It was impossible to turn these feelings off.

Jen was sitting cross-legged in an armchair, typing statements from last night's teenage shenanigans. Alice was on the couch and offered Rio a cider, which she declined.

“No, I’ve had enough alcohol the past two months. Maybe I need to stop drinking. Maybe I need to keep a clear head.” She said, agitated, as she gestured wildly while pacing around the living room. She had sat much of the day while preparing for the week and now Rio needed to move.

Jen looked at Alice and raised an eyebrow. Alice shook her head once. She knew Rio and knew she needed to let everything out.

“I don’t date parents. I don’t. We both know there’s a fucking good reason for that. That two-year affair in San Francisco nearly KILLED me, Alice, you saw me. You came to the hospital and saw me.” She realized that Jen was present and quickly turned to look at her. “Say a damn word of any of this to anyone, and I’ll make a phone call to Nat. She owes me a favor,” She said, pointing a finger in Jen’s direction.

Jen looked at Rio with confusion for a moment. “Why would Nat…” the pieces clicked for her. “OH! You’re the one, got it,” Jen nodded, waving her hand for her to continue.

“Nothing leaves this apartment,” Rio added, watching as the other two women nodded their agreement. She continued her movements around the room. “Yes, I know this is different, but I almost lost my career. I don’t want another relationship to almost cost me everything again. I don’t want a breakup to cause me to lose a friend, which is the most important part of this dilemma. She means so much to me. I can tell her almost anything. I can’t lose that! I can’t lose this coven of lovable bitches either.” Her gestures were wild, and she roamed every space in the main area of the apartment. This was not a new experience for Alice as her eyes tracked Rio around the room. Jen, however, was amused.

Do you know what she asked me last night? She asked me to be her consistent date for red carpet events. Agatha. Agatha Fucking Harkness, who always takes Wanda ‘In Love With the Deadliest Woman Alive’ Maximoff or her own son, wants me to be her forever red-carpet buddy. You know, as FRIENDS. I can count on one hand the number of times she has taken someone that wasn’t one of those two since I’ve been ALIVE.”

“Wait,” Jen interjected, shocked. “I have been with Agatha for ten years, and she has NEVER wanted anyone other than Wanda or Nicky on a red carpet more than once or twice. It’s because of all the pictures that are taken. She doesn’t want to look back and be filled with sadness repeatedly because of what she’s lost. She knows Wanda and Nicky are constants, and she’ll always be filled with happiness and joy when she sees those pictures, no matter what.” Jen looked at her. “Rio, that’s huge.”

Rio leaned against the wall, feeling as if all the air had been sucked from her lungs. Agatha knew that she would always associate good things with Rio. AFTER TWO MONTHS? What kind of witchcraft was happening? Agatha wanted her to be a constant in her life, and Rio so badly wanted that, too, which was what had brought forth this crisis.

“Hang on, then why were you all so insistent on FORCING her to take me to the premiere? Knowing it was such a big deal to her?”

Jen waved her hand. “Agatha isn’t forced to do anything. If she didn’t want to take you, she wouldn’t, and again, more than once or twice. One premiere, okay. Wanting you to be her constant? Rio. She’s planning on you being beside her, in some capacity.” Jen internally screamed. Everyone had gotten on to her and Alice for taking so damn long to get it together, Wanda and Nat took a quarter of a fucking century, and now Rio and Agatha were alternating between going too fast and slamming on the brakes. Maybe she should have set up a fake dating ploy. Those situations seemed to move more quickly.

Rio nodded slowly. “So she wants me in her life forever but isn’t sure about the capacity. Cool. Cool, cool, cool, cool, cool.”

“You don’t date parents.” Alice reminded.

“EXACTLY! And why don’t I date parents? Because of the bitch back in San Francisco! I fell in love once, ONCE! Everything was going great, but then I found out she was NOT separated from her husband. That she was not going to get a divorce, and I broke it off, because OBVIOUSLY. She didn’t get the hint. Suddenly, she’s EVERYWHERE. Do you know how odd it is for a teacher to have to get a restraining order against a parent of a student?” She was power-walking all over Alice’s apartment at this point.

Jen looked at Alice. “Is she just monologuing?” She asked.

Alice looked back, “She does this. I just go with it.”

Rio kept going like the Energizer bunny, not even hearing them, “Long story very short, Jen, I survived but ended up in the hospital with sustained injuries from the fucking husband! Swore off parents after that. Calderu knows, yet even SHE seems to want this. I also don’t trust that bitch not to show back up solely because she’s the jealous type, and I have to keep everyone safe.
