And then, THEN, there’s the NICKY of it all. I fucking love that kid! I’ve known that damn kid for TWO MINUTES, and I would do anything for him. He’s so much like her, the mischievous little shit. If anyone had said it was his fault last night, I would’ve caused some damage of my own. Do you know why he and Tommy wanted to take those girls out? Because the Bayside dickheads are misogynistic assholes, and our boys wanted to learn more to know what THEY could do to raise awareness about douchey behavior in high schools. Who does that sound like? His activist mother. That’s who. He is an amazing kid and I would be so proud to be a part of his life.

I shared what he told me with Agatha and I’m sure she let Lilia know since Lilia listens to her for some unknown reason. The kid is 16 and has more empathy in his pinky than some grown-ass adults because the people that raised him, I know it wasn’t ALL Agatha, are just that fucking good. Another reason I can’t risk any of this is because this family that I have been accepted into is so amazing. Alice, it’s always just been you and me, and now, I have this whole family, and I can’t lose that.

Then, we have an ENTIRE WEEKEND in New York, BY OURSELVES. I’m not canceling whatsoever. Of course, we’ll be sharing a fucking bed because we can’t spend the night under the same roof and sleep separately. We’re so fucking gay for each other, but I can’t lose this, and I can’t lose her, and I can’t risk anything, but sleeping next to her is the most natural thing in the world. I didn’t even feel that way with the stalker chick. Sharing a bed with you, Alice is like sleeping next to my sister because you are. This? This is something entirely different, and I can’t put it into words yet. Anytime we spend the night in the same house, I want us to wake up in each other’s arms because I feel like that’s where we belong, and it scares the hell out of me.

Yet I can’t get over my OWN issues long enough to even tell her what MY fucking bullshit is, and I don’t want to hurt either of them. So here I am ranting to you two lovesick idiots, don’t deny it, while she probably is just enjoying her wine night with those other lesbians finally living their truth.” Her eyes were wild, cheeks flushed from the constant movement. She gulped down her water while she tried to slow her breathing.

Alice looked at her calmly. “Rio. As you said, it’s been two months. The San Francisco thing was bad, I know. I saw you in the hospital. I almost killed that bitch myself. It won’t happen again, and you need to tell Agatha everything. You both need to lay all your cards on the table and decide, like the adults you both claim you are, if it’s worth it. However, it has only been two months.” She knew Rio was worried, and her feelings were valid, but Rio was in deep and needed to talk to Agatha sooner rather than later. Alice wanted to see her truly happy.

Jen smiled at Rio, “I can tell you that you can tell Agatha anything, and it won’t change how she sees you.”

Rio huffed. “Right. Two months,” She chewed on the inside of her cheek as she thought about everything.

“Don’t give yourself an aneurysm,” Jen remarked, seeing the gears turning in Rio’s mind.

“You have time to work everything out, Ri. Neither of you is going anywhere. Agatha is beginning to film, which means less time together and not as much texting. That’s a good thing. Keep it friendly in New York, have fun at the Halloween party, and then we’ll make it through the holidays. After the first of the year, she’ll be on location for filming, and the distance will be good for both of you.” Alice offered. “Just keep building on this.”

Rio nodded. “Okay. Yes. Less time together could be good,” She had started moving again, refilling her water.  “I need to suppress these feelings, ignore them, and they will eventually go away. Maybe I’ll go on a few dates, maybe I’ll read more, actually tend to my plants more, and pick up some other hobbies. YES. Will I ever lay my soul bare for her? Maybe. Right now, I’m going to go home to my cat.” With that, she walked out of the door. Alice buried her head in her hands as Jen looked at her.

“Why is she an idiot?” Jen asked.

Alice looked up at her. “I blame her parents. My mother had an influence too. Gran tried so hard with both of us. She really did.”

Jen shook her head, “She’s not going to suppress a damn thing, is she?”

Alice stood and stretched, “She’ll try, I give it until Halloween. I don’t see her going on a date.”

Jen smiled. “Imagine. If she even mentioned it, they would mysteriously cancel. Wanda would make sure of it.”

As Rio tried to settle herself for the night, she kept thinking about the monologue and conversation at Alice’s. If I’m ready to tell her about why I have my rule, then am I ready to hear why she has hers, and be rejected?

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha had woken up alone, a blanket tucked around her, and a text from Rio explaining she had to get home and would talk to her later. It was probably for the best since Nicky was home. He didn’t need to see them spooning on the couch.

She was in the kitchen fixing her coffee and making pancakes when her son walked in, rubbing the sleep out of his eyes.

“Where’s Rio?” He asked.

Agatha was thankful for all the years of acting experience as she kept her face blank and put pancakes on a plate for him. “She went home last night.” She handed him his plate and turned back to the stove to finish breakfast.

Nicky was not stupid. “Mom. She spends the night every Saturday when you guys have movie night. You cuddle. The twins and I know everything. We’re even in the betting pool.” He said with a smirk. He was her son. Agatha didn’t know why she underestimated him.

She flipped the pancakes and turned to look at him, shock on her face. “Excuse me, young man?” His admission had taken her by surprise.

“I’m your son and, therefore, the second smartest person in the room, Mom. Anyone with eyes can see you two are attracted to each other. Like I said last night. I like her.” He carefully watched her reaction to make sure he hadn’t overstepped.

She finished the pancakes, plated them, and turned off the stove. “You have a field to clean up today,” She said, taking her own breakfast to the table as she thought about what he had said. “Have I told you what a great kid you are?”

“I had some pretty decent role models. By that, I mean Uncle Vision and Aunt Nat,” He grinned as she playfully smacked his arm.

“Smartass.” She laughed.

“Learned from the best.” He smiled at her as he took a seat at the table. “I love you, Mom,” he gave her an honest look before nearly inhaling his breakfast.

“I love you too, kid,” She smiled back before laughing. “You eat like Rio does.”

He gave her a mischievous look before putting down his fork. “See, already picking up habits from my future stepmother.” He bolted upstairs to get ready before she could process what he said.

Her jaw dropped before she just groaned. “Assholes. I’m surrounded by assholes.”

By Sunday evening, while Rio was going on her diatribe, Agatha was going on one of her own. She was lesbian spreading on her couch while Wanda and Nat were being disgustingly cute on what was now their couch.

“You two are disgusting,” Agatha said, rolling her eyes, drinking water of all things. She needed to be sober. She had told them about the conversation with Nicky this morning and Wanda had excitedly told her that had meant she could drop her rule. Agatha, however, was too hard-headed for that.

“Please, we’ve always been like this when it’s just the three of us,” Wanda responded. “You’re just sensitive.”

“I am not,” Agatha argued. “Anyway, back to me. Even if I did drop the no-teacher rule, I still don’t have relationships and can’t risk losing Rio. That’s what the whole thing boils down to. I have to tell her why I refuse to have a relationship, but what if it’s something she can’t handle? What if it changes the way she looks at me? I fell in love once, and it went badly. I refuse even to tell the two of you everything.” Agatha shook her head as if to shake the memories away. “I know I was much younger, and so much has changed since then. I know what it’s like to be the younger person in a relationship, and it’s hard. I think she could handle it a lot better than I did though.” She smiled sadly as she continued. “I’m not sure how she would handle the fame and pressure, though. It’s a lot, and it’s a big conversation to be had if we even got to that point. As for the teacher thing, as long as Nicky understood that this did not change anything and that he would not get any special treatment, then that could be changed. That’s a newer rule, anyway. It’s the no relationship rule that I’m more inclined to stick with.”

Wanda stared at her. “For the smartest person I know, you’re an idiot. Nothing you say is going to change the way she feels about you. She also has Vision and Nat for support on dating a megastar. I know you still outshine me. You always will. Don’t look at me like that. In my eyes, you will always be THE Agatha Harkness, and I will ALWAYS be in your shadow. ALWAYS. Your last relationship was decades ago. I still don’t even know who it was, and only Nat and I even know you had a relationship. Rio will want to be public with you. You just have to talk to her.”

Nat nodded, placing a kiss on Wanda’s head. “Red’s right. We tease, and we push, but Agatha, in the 27 years I’ve known you, not once have I seen you look at someone the way I’ve seen you look at Rio Vidal. It’s been a little over two months, and if I didn’t know the circumstances, I would think you two had been together for years.”

Wanda turned and kissed Nat on the cheek before turning back to Agatha. “We’re not going to stop meddling either because Rio looks at you like you’re the only person in the room. Yes, it’s only been two months, yet you’ve even said yourself that it feels incredibly natural and like you’ve known each other for years. So why does actual time matter? I don’t think the age gap even matters to you anymore, even if it scared you at first and now I know why. This is Hollywood! Age gaps are everywhere! Sixteen years is NOTHING here. Sarah is a dear friend of yours, and Holland is thirty-four years older than she is. That’s almost as long as Rio has been alive, and that’s not even the largest. Get over yourself, Harkness.” Wanda loved Agatha and wanted what was best for her. If she needed to be both the voice of reason and the person pushing her off the cliff, she would be.

Agatha was processing everything. It was a lot to take in. “Filming is about to pick up a lot, and I’ll be in New York for SNL rehearsals, then back here for the premiere, then back in New York for our weekend together where I am set on nothing happening.” She ignored the clearly disappointed looks on her friends' faces. “Back here for more filming, and then Halloween.” She looked down at her lap, knowing she needed to tell Wanda what she had asked Rio. “I asked Rio to be my companion for premieres and award shows…” She barely got it out before Wanda sat up and squealed.

“Why didn’t you lead with that?” Wanda asked with excitement in her eyes.

“I didn’t want you to get too excited. I know you’ll want to start going with Nat, and Nicky is getting too old to join Mom.” She waved it off.

“Oh, whatever. Justify it all you want but Agatha Harkness does not ask someone to be her recurring date for major events if she doesn’t think they’ll be a permanent fixture in her life. You want to slow down, yet you’re speeding up, too. You’re an idiot.” Wanda shook her head as she reassumed her position against Nat.

“Look who’s talking.” Agatha shot back.

“Go to hell,” Wanda replied.

“I think she’s already in one of her own making,” Nat said knowingly.

Agatha just grimaced. If anyone knew how she was feeling, it was those two. She couldn’t help but think to herself that maybe she was ready for something more with Rio. You absolutely can’t stand to be away from her, and when you are with her you can’t help but touch her. You imagine kissing her or other things. Now, how could she get her to open up about Rio’s no-parents’ rule?

⭐⭐⭐

Midterm week went by both slowly and too quickly. Rio was looking forward to the next week’s events, however, she had a lot to wrap up and a very short amount of time. She somehow managed to complete everything by the final bell on Friday, and thankfully, there were no football games for the next two weeks. Her exhaustion was apparent as she just dropped her bag by the entryway as she walked into her apartment. The first nine weeks had been completed, and she was thankful for the week off. She barely got to the couch before collapsing, kicking her shoes off, when her phone dinged.

Agatha 💜

Hey, filming for the day will run

later than I thought. Want to

grab a late dinner?

Rio read the message and thought about it. They hadn’t seen each other all week, and she missed Agatha. She was also tired and knew if Agatha came over, she would fall asleep on her, Agatha would stay, and they would end up spending most of the weekend together. The following weekend would be New York weekend, and Rio was supposed to be backing off.

Me:

I am exhausted.

Probably will turn in

early. Sorry!

She refused to focus on the pain in her chest when she hit send. Of course, she wanted to grab a late dinner. She wanted to order something, have Agatha come over, and then go to bed together. Instead, she ignored Agatha’s reply, put her phone down, and let the exhaustion overtake her.

Agatha responded with a quick “okay.” She didn’t expect a reply. Agatha wanted this after all, she had wanted to back off, right? This would be fine. They would have movie night this weekend, and everything would be back to normal. The premiere was Wednesday, and New York was next weekend. Everything would be fine.

Agatha could have murdered their friends. All six of them were gathered for movie night, and Wanda had shirts made for the four meddling bitches. They all said Team Get Together All Ready, causing Rio and Agatha to blush. Wanda thought it was hilarious and Rio had rolled her eyes while blushing. While Wanda had said she would continue meddling, Agatha preferred when it was more subtle. She had already made a concerted effort to sit in a way where Rio would not cuddle up next to her, and it felt like Wanda was rubbing salt in the wound. It felt wrong not to have Rio right next to her. This was going to be painful.

Jen had taken that opportunity to address the upcoming premiere on Wednesday. Agatha would spend much of the week in New York, and Jen wanted to take advantage of everyone in one place.

“Okay, we know the media will jump on why you didn’t go with each other since you're co-starring, and historically, that’s what you’ve done before anytime you’ve done projects together or been nominated together. So what do you want your statements to be?”

Wanda was still taking Vision, as nothing on that front would change until after the holidays. “While Agatha and I are co-stars, Vision has been another constant support in my life, and with the importance of this movie, it felt right for him to be here.” She stated, staring at Nat the entire time. Nat would be there in the wings like she always was. Wanda was hopeful that Nat would be her date at the Golden Globes. She would let Jen handle the PR shit show.

Jen wrote it down, knowing it would be picked apart in a few months. She looked at the two women, desperately trying to distance themselves from each other. “Agatha?”

Agatha thought about it, knowing no matter what she said, it would be spun however the media or fans wanted to spin it. No comment would almost be worse. “Rio is a close friend of mine, and her support means a lot to me.” She said as she made eye contact with Rio. It was a simple statement that held a lot of weight to it. Rio nodded her agreement.

Jen looked around before landing on Alice. “Someone is going to see you and know who you are.”

Alice shrugged. “Fuck ‘em. I’ll stay as hidden as I can and handle it if I need to.” Alice generally didn’t attend anything but wanted to start supporting her friends, especially with Rio entering this world now.

Jen looked back at the two movie stars. “You know there’s going to be a lot of questions, and Nat will step in if she needs to. I know you can handle it, but this is very out of character for you both.”

“It will be fine,” Agatha reassured her before looking over at Rio, who was looking at Agatha intently.

“I’ll have you next to me. I can handle anything,” Rio said, forgetting the four other women in the room.

“JUST KISS ALREADY!” Jen cried, breaking the tension, as Agatha glared at her and Rio stared at the floor uncomfortably.

Jen regained her composure, “I think we’re good,” she said, normally, putting away her notebook and nodding at Alice to start the move, trying to suppress her laugh.

Agatha turned back to Rio, speaking quietly. “What did that mean?” She asked.

Rio just shrugged, trying to play it off. “You’re Agatha Fucking Harkness. Who is going to come after me when you’re by my side..”

“True….” Agatha gave her a small smile.

Rio turned her attention to the movie. Here she was trying to distance herself, and yet she was running straight towards Agatha again.

As the movie went on, every shared whisper and comment brought the two women closer together, inch by inch. Soon, Agatha found that they were just about in their familiar cuddling position. Well, that lasted. She thought but sighed, relieved.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha had flown back from New York Wednesday morning. Now, here she was with her and Wanda’s team prepping them and Rio, for the night.

“Why am I nervous? We’ve done this most of our lives.” Agatha asked Wanda as she checked her final look in the full-length mirror in her bathroom. She wore a fitted black suit with a lace bra underneath instead of a shirt. Her hair was parted to the side in loose waves, signature locket on a chain around her neck.

“Because there’s so much pressure on this movie to be amazing, and the woman you refuse to date is getting ready across the hall,” Wanda replied. Her auburn hair was done in an updo to show off ruby drop earrings. Her black sequin halter dress had a deep v-neck just below her sternum and came to her knee before opening in a split and showing off her red-bottom black heels. If anyone asked her though, her favorite part was that it had pockets.

Agatha just shook her head. “I am not refusing to date her. It hasn’t even come up. We’ve agreed just to be friends.”

Wanda clicked her tongue. “How long are you going to lie to yourself?” She asked sincerely.

Agatha didn’t answer, she looked herself over one more time in the mirror before walking out.

Rio was waiting downstairs with Vision and the other three women. Vision appeared every inch the doting, supportive husband in a dapper black suit. He was in conversation with Nat, who looked every part the bodyguard in her suit. Rio admired them both for how much they had done for Wanda and how they had stood by her all this time. Jen wore a pink and black ombre suit and Alice had chosen a simple black dress, hoping to blend into the background. Rio however, couldn’t blend in if she tried. Jen, Agatha’s stylist, with help from Wanda, Rio presumed, had worked together to choose her outfit. It was a strapless cream dress that came to her mid-thigh with a black lace floor-length overlay. Her hair was half up, half down, and for some reason, she was eager to see Agatha’s reaction to the look. Rio also couldn’t wait to see Agatha’s, for that matter.

“There are the stars of the evening.” Vision announced as Wanda and Agatha entered the room.

Rio turned around and forgot how to breathe. Agatha a natural beauty, looked even more stunning than usual. As she approached Rio, Rio regained her ability to function like a normal human.  Agatha smiled, tucking her hair behind her ear.

“You look amazing.” They said simultaneously and laughed nervously, quietly routinely admiring each other’s outfits.

“They’re like teenagers on prom night,” Wanda said to the others with a smirk.

Agatha pretended not to hear her, nor did she really care. Before her, Rio was gorgeous, and for once, she, Agatha Fucking Harkness, was nearly speechless. “You, just, wow.” Agatha breathed, blushing.

Rio had also turned red, “You too.” She replied, before adding. “As a friend, of course.”

“Of course,” Agatha said, feeling like she had just taken a punch to the gut.

“Liars,” Wanda said to them as she walked past, leading everyone to the two SUV’s that were waiting for them outside.

“Will one of you keep her in line?” Agatha asked, speaking to Nat and Vision.

Vision laughed. “Neither of us is capable of that, and you know it.”

As they arrived at the theatre where the premiere was, Rio was nervous. She knew there would be questions, cameras, and lights. It was all overwhelming. She felt Agatha’s hand on her thigh, and she stopped shaking her leg. She hadn’t even noticed she was doing it until she felt Agatha’s hand, and the accompanying jolt of electricity from the touch.

“Hey,” Agatha said quietly, getting her attention. “If you get nervous, just look at me, okay?” She gave Rio that genuine smile that was reserved for just a few people, as her blue eyes looked deeply into Rio’s. “Everything will be fine. It’s a few minutes outside, and there are a few interviews that I’ll handle. Then we go inside and watch the movie. Afterward, more pictures, a few more interviews, and back to my house so you can go home. Sound like a plan?” Agatha’s voice was calming, her eyes were soft, and in that moment, Rio would have followed her anywhere with the way she was speaking.

"Yeah, I can do this. Don't walk too far off and abandon me to the wolves, Harkness."

"Never," Agatha replied.

Jen cleared her throat. “I hate to break up whatever that moment is, but it’s time to exit the vehicle.”

Agatha broke the eye contact as she scooted to the door. “Right, let’s go.”

Other movie stars were already there, posing for pictures and speaking with the media. Wanda and Agatha, as the two movie leads, were at the very end of the line. Wanda and Vision went first, though the four posed together, and Agatha and Wanda took several pictures with castmates. Nat and three other bodyguards walked with them, followed by Jen. Alice had gone to blend with the crowd.

Cameras were flashing, and questions were being asked from all directions.

“Why didn’t you come as each other's date like you have in the past?”

“Agatha, who’s your date?”

“What’s your name?”

“Vision, what do you think about UCLA’s current odds of making a bowl game?”

“Agatha, is this your girlfriend or just your current girl ?”

“Agatha, why haven’t you been seen out lately?”

“Wanda, why haven’t we seen you and Vision together lately?”

While they had been making the press circuit, neither star had been seen in public recently, other than school functions, and rumors had been flying. They gave their rehearsed answers and statements and spoke about the movie, before moving into the theatre. As they sat, Rio’s eyes went wide as she turned to Agatha. “Is it always like that?” She asked.

Agatha laughed. “Not always, but I had someone new with me. So, new questions. Now, watch the movie and enjoy seeing me naked.” She winked as Rio blushed. She completely forgot about those scenes. Yes, she had seen Agatha in sex scenes before, but this was different. Completely different.

Wanda leaned over to Rio. “Don’t kill me. While we do kiss, and I am touching her, I’m not actually having sex with your woman.” She whispered conspiratoriously and flashed Rio a shit-eating grin before sitting back in her seat.

Rio just groaned. This was going to be a long movie.

Chapter End Notes

Look, these characters are telling the story and I'm just writing it. It's such a slow burn and I'm with Jen. JUST KISS ALREADY. DO SOMETHING.

YET HERE WE ARE.

Oh, Nicky. Wanda needs to get him a shirt too.

You Kiss Me In A Way That's Gonna Screw Me Up Forever

Chapter Summary

The premiere, a weekend in New York, and a Halloween party. Our favorite woman have so many opportunities to talk and get closer.

Chapter Notes

Thank you so much to the ever-so-amazing DareToDream82! She has been indispensable in this! She has absolutely helped me sort out my Carolina Reaper level of neurospicy and put it in order.

I apologize for the wait! Have 13.5k words. I refused to split this chapter and I hope what I (we, really) have brought you makes up for it!

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Rio was captivated by what was unfolding on the screen before her. There was such amazing chemistry between Agatha and Wanda, even she believed they were passionately in love. The beautiful love scene at the end of the movie was the perfect end to this dramatic psychological thriller. Agatha played an FBI agent, married to Wanda, when they began tracking a major serial killer that consumed Agatha’s character. Wanda’s character struggled with nearly losing her wife so many times and trying to be supportive. The emotional roller coaster ending in this physical and naked scene was almost too much for Rio.

Rio had seen Agatha naked in movies plenty of times. However, that was before they were close friends or sitting right next to her. She certainly had never felt awkward during any of Agatha’s intimate scenes, but now? She knew it was fake, yet here she was, not just feeling a little awkward, but bubbling jealousy over Wanda? It was Wanda Maximoff of all people. Wanda, who was madly in love with Nat. Oh yes, calm and collected, Nat. Nat who had years of experience watching these scenes, of watching Wanda film these scenes over and over, and never being able to react publically. Rio couldn’t imagine. She would have incurred assault charges at this point. Where were these thoughts and feelings coming from? She had zero claim to Agatha and no right to be jealous. Agatha could do whatever she wanted with whoever she wanted, couldn’t she? You want her to be yours .

Instead, she just watched the scene before her. Rio’s mind began to wander as she pictured herself with Agatha instead of Wanda. It wasn’t the first time since they became friends that she fantasized about Agatha, putting her various toys to good use. But, this was different. She hadn’t had the imagery and audio to go with it. As the movie played, so did the one in her imagination, which was much better, if you asked her.

Rio pressed their mouths together in a needy kiss. Her wife had almost died, and she needed to show her just how much she loved and needed her. Maybe Agatha would stop this futile pursuit before it actually did kill her. She backed her into the wall, deepening the kiss as she slid her tongue across Agatha’s bottom lip before biting it, eliciting a gasp. Rio had one arm around Agatha’s waist and the other hand twisted in her hair. Agatha held Rio securely against her, hands fisted in the back of Rio’s sweater.

Rio slid her tongue against Agatha’s, exploring her mouth, as she spun her and moved her to the bed, clothes shedding as they went.

She lowered Agatha to the bed. “I can’t lose you,” Rio whispered, echoing the line from the actresses and breaking the kiss.

Rio leaned in and kissed Agatha again before trailing kisses down her neck and to her chest, taking Agatha’s right breast in her hand and running her thumb over her hardened nipple. Rio’s imagination now diverging from the movie and in control of the fantasy.

She wrapped her lips around the other nipple and sucked, mimicking the rolling and pulling that she was doing with her fingers. She was rewarded with several gasps and moans. Agatha grabbed her chin and yanked, pulling her up to kiss her again and take control.

Agatha rolled over on top of her, their bodies pressed together everywhere. Rio tried not to shiver visibly, as her body manifested the fantasy. Agatha still had a hold on her jaw and gripped it as she kissed her like she needed all of the oxygen from Rio’s lungs to survive.

God, why was this also making her cry? Agatha Harkness was on a giant screen, naked, she was fantasizing about her, and she was still captivated by her acting. The build-up was fantastic.

Agatha kissed her again, slower, more deliberately this time, like she was trying to map the layout of her mouth, and Rio responded the same way, tracing Agatha’s tongue, the shape of her lips, the roof of her mouth, everything. Her hands explored Agatha’s back, arms, neck, fingers tracing every muscle.

Her fingers twitched, bringing her back to the moment. She itched to reach out to the woman next to her. Keep it in your head, Vidal.

Agatha’s hands were on the move, she was straddling Rio, one hand next to Rio’s head for support and the other running down the length of her body, sending sparks of electricity everywhere she touched her skin. She finally reached where Rio wanted her most and shifted to spread Rio’s legs further apart.

The camera was more focused on faces and glimpses of skin, it was all very tasteful, but Rio could take it from here.

Agatha dragged her nails up Rio’s thigh as she gasped from the sensation, gripping Agatha’s shoulders and kissing her harder. Agatha smiled into the kiss before lightly ghosting her fingers over Rio’s core and feeling how wet she was. Rio was filled with heat and want. She needed Agatha to touch her, to make her come. Agatha lightly brushed over Rio’s clit, causing her to whimper. Agatha kissed down Rio’s jaw and down her neck, before licking her pulse point and focusing there. She pressed down on Rio’s hardened clit before making circles around it, causing her hips to buck. As Rio got closer, Agatha stopped, only to push two fingers into Rio’s entrance as she bit down on her neck. Rio cried out as she dug her nails into Agatha’s back and her hips came up to meet Agatha’s thrusts. Agatha pumped in and out, Rio was so close. She slung a leg around Agatha’s waist to allow her to hit a different angle. Agatha pressed her thumb back on Rio’s clit at the same time as she curled her fingers forward and she came hard with a scream.

Rio broke from her thoughts when she heard Agatha’s moans in real life. Rio had gotten so caught up in her thoughts that she had missed Agatha’s climax scene.

She squeezed her eyes shut, breathing deeply, and trying to clear her mind and make the heat between her legs go away when Agatha put her hand on hers, leaning over.

“I’ll take you to a G-rated movie next time,” She whispered playfully.

If you only knew, she thought, as the touch from Agatha sent sparks all over her body and made the flame in her core grow brighter. Great .

Rio focused back in on the movie, the sex scene finally over. Agatha’s character came face to face with Agatha’s wife revealed as the killer. The scene had brought Rio to tears. The heartfelt words, the admissions, Agatha knowing it was Wanda but not wanting to admit it to herself. The FBI closed in as Agatha had to decide between letting Wanda’s character go or capturing her as they stood on a pier. Gunshots rang. Wanda had shot Agatha, and Agatha had shot her back. Agatha woke up in the hospital to be told that Wanda’s body hadn’t been found, but by the amount of blood, she had to be dead.

Rio realized she was holding Agatha’s hand. Agatha had never moved it from earlier, and now their fingers were intertwined, still in Rio’s lap, dangerously close to where she needed it after the earlier fantasy. She let it be for the last few minutes of the movie. No sense in moving it now.

In the final scene, Agatha returns to the place where their characters first met, only to find Wanda there. They kiss. The title song, sung by Agatha, plays, and the credits roll.

A psychological thriller about Agatha hunting Wanda, her fucking serial killer wife, had Rio both in tears and ready to throw caution to the wind.

Rio let go of Agatha’s hand abruptly and immediately missed the connection. She stood and hugged her as the theater erupted in applause. As she stepped back, she looked at Agatha in awe.

“You are amazing,” Rio said, regarding her in all of her award-winning megastar glory.

Agatha had received the highest praise from some of Hollywood’s most legendary icons. Yet it all paled in comparison to the look in Rio’s eyes right now. The sheer appreciation and admiration she saw. All she could do was smile back, anything else, and she might cry, or kiss her. Agatha had no idea why three simple words, a hug, and a look from one woman meant everything to her, but they did. If she never received another award, another positive word from the media or a movie critic, it wouldn’t matter. As long as Rio thought she was amazing, that’s all she needed.

“You two will absolutely get nominated!” Alice’s voice broke through the moment as she scooted down the row they were in to hug Agatha and Wanda.

Wanda laughed. “For best kiss at the MTV Movie Awards, at least.”

“Wouldn’t be the first time.” Agatha agreed.

After more interviews and mingling, they finally climbed back into their vehicles to return to the compound.

“That was fun and exhausting at the same time.” Rio sighed, leaning against Agatha. She was tired and thrilled she had nowhere to be the next day.

“It usually is,” Agatha agreed as she instinctively put her arm around Rio.

Alice looked over at Jen, raising an eyebrow and cutting her eyes to the two women. Jen silently took a picture and sent it to Wanda in the other car.

Wanda checked her texts and almost screamed, solely from how Agatha looked at Rio. Nat just shook her head. “These lesbians are going to be the death of you.”

Vision smiled. “Funny, I’ve said that for twenty years.”

Nat and Wanda nearly choked.

Rio had changed out of her dress and ridden back home with Alice, thankful for the decision to ride together. She was exhausted and knew she wouldn’t have been able to drive herself. She kept thinking about her feelings as she watched the movie. If she were to ever actually date Agatha, would she be able to get over the nudity in movies? Agatha kissing other people?

“Alice, am I a jealous person?” She asked honestly.

The laughter from Alice lasted a bit too long.

“Why?” Alice calmed the laughter. “Feeling jealous?”

Rio blushed in stubborn silence.

“Are you JEALOUS that other people see Agatha naked? Or are you jealous of Wanda?”

“A bit of both?” Rio spilled her thoughts, which had bothered her for hours.  “In the last relationship I was in, she was a model, and I wasn’t jealous. I wasn’t jealous when I found out she was still married, either. I was never jealous of people who had better lives or were famous or prettier. Yet, other people looking at Agatha? I know that’s not the first time she’s had a nude or sexual scene, and it’s not the last…And Wanda kissing and TOUCHING Agatha? I have zero claim to her, so why did I feel like this?” She asked Alice earnestly.

Alice pulled into her parking spot and turned off the car before regarding Rio. “You know the answer just as well as I do, but you continue to lie to yourself. I know you’re afraid, and rightly so. That bitch back in San Francisco screwed you up. But, If you ever do decide to date Agatha though, you’re going to have to deal with these feelings and more movies and scenes like what you witnessed tonight. You have to ask yourself if you can handle it, and if it’s worth it.”

With that, Alice got out of the vehicle, and Rio followed. They went up to their apartments quietly, Rio lost in her thoughts. Could she handle more of this? She honestly wasn’t sure. She had been so caught up in her rule, that she hadn’t thought of what dating THE Agatha Fucking Harkness would entail.

She hugged Alice before entering her apartment and being greeted by one pissed-off Raven.

“Right, I was gone all day, and for some reason, you require food,” She said as she walked to the kitchen. “Don’t you know how to do this yourself by now?” She looked at Raven, who waited rather impatiently. “You know, you’re lucky. You have me and whoever I bring home. You don’t have to worry about the complexity of relationships or work. You don’t have to worry about getting jealous about your close friend, who you might be extremely attracted to, having fake sex with your other close friend, who also happens to be gay, but not gay for your crush.” She set Raven’s food down and cleaned up a few things around the kitchen, watering plants before bed. “You also don’t have to worry about what would happen if you ever DID date her. How would you handle dating said friend?” She finished her chores, turned off lights, and took care of a few things as she walked towards her room. Raven trailed behind her. “Do I want people to know who I am? Just being on Agatha’s arm tonight was anxiety-inducing. I’m afraid to look at my phone right now. Who knows what pictures have been posted and what’s been said?” She changed into her pajamas as Raven curled up on her side of the bed. “Not to mention, what would you do? You would lose your side of the bed.” Raven looked at Rio and meowed at that. Raven did not share well and whenever someone was in bed with Rio, Raven took personal offense.

Rio just sighed. “No. I’m getting ahead of myself. It will never happen, only in my dreams and fantasies.” She climbed into bed, finally looking at her phone, which was full of notifications. “See, Raven. I told you, might as well take a look.”

She tapped on some of the stories to see the pictures and headlines.

“Death’s Beloved Bound to be A Box Office Hit”

“Harkness and Maximoff Deliver Oscar-Winning Performances in New Film”

The first several headlines were movie-focused and she was relieved. Then she saw the tweets, skeets, and pictures that focused on the two leading ladies themselves.

“Breaking tradition, the two leading ladies co-starred but brought separate dates instead of going together.”

“It’s a wild Vision sighting! Wonder if it’s PR? So many pictures with her ‘bodyguard’ lately…”

“Who is the mystery brunette on Agatha Harkness’s arm?”

“Did Agatha bring one of her throwaways to a premiere? That’s a first.”

“Both Wanda and Vision look like they’re out with their best friend, not love..”

“Wait, did Wagatha break up? Is this the end?”

“Where’s the bodyguard? Wanda getting with her is on my 2025 Bingo Card.”

“HOLY SHIT! IS THAT LORNA WU’S DAUGHTER BACK THERE?”

The comments continued, and some of them were just downright mean, especially about Rio being there with Agatha. Her reading was interrupted.

Agatha 💜

Thank you again for coming

with me tonight. I had a lot

of fun. Don’t read

anything about the event online.

Me:

And again, thank YOU

for giving in to our friends

bullying. I had a lot of fun.

Me:

How did you know?

Too late. I already did.

Agatha 💜

Because I know you.

You’re curious. You also

want to know what people

are saying.

Me:

Got me. What about you?

Do you read them?

Agatha 💜

No. I stopped caring a long

time ago. People will say

what they want. Fuck ‘em.

Only a few people’s opinions

matter to me. Those people

are in my life.

It was a lie, but she wouldn’t tell Rio that she monitored what was said, mainly to have certain things scrubbed.

Me:

Agatha Fucking Harkness. The wisest

woman to be found at 1 am.

Agatha 💜

Don’t you forget it. Now, go

to sleep kid. You need to pack

tomorrow. We leave very early

Friday.

Me:

Fine. Yes, Mother.

Good Night, Agatha.

Agatha 💜

Good Night, Rio.

Rio just grinned at the Mother comment. She knew it had multiple meanings, and she meant it in a few ways. She sighed as she set her music up, saved some of the pictures she had seen posted, and settled in. Some of the comments stuck with her though.

“That woman is definitely not with Agatha. Agatha is out of her league.”

That was true. Rio wasn’t even in the same stratosphere as Agatha. As it was, she had zero idea what to pack this weekend. She had never considered what to wear around Agatha in the real world. Rio was always herself, but now she was very aware that she would be in public around her. Very public.

“Agatha doesn’t do relationships. Whoever she is, she isn’t permanent.” Another comment, another trueism. Agatha didn’t do permanent. How long would Rio be around if they did date? It was better to remain friends.

Yet, there was another post that really stuck with her. It was a picture of them looking at each other. Rio remembered the moment: Agatha had answered something, and their eyes caught one another’s, and they both just softened. Rio had saved the picture because of the way they were looking at each other, and someone else had noticed.

“I’ve been a fan of Agatha Harkness since she was a kid,” the post said “and I’ve never seen her look at anyone like that.”

It actually scared Rio a bit, but she saved it too. She couldn’t quite put her finger on it, but something was there. They looked at each other as if no one else in the world mattered. It was as if, at that moment, time had stopped, and a declaration was being made that had knocked the wind from both of them. Rio had to think. What question had Agatha been asked? Something about the theme, maybe? The meaning? What her character was thinking? Then Rio remembered.

“Agatha, your character is faced with this decision. What was going through her, and your, head to really show the love she had for her wife?”

Agatha smiled. “Without giving too much away, these two have this history and are just so good for each other.” Agatha’s eyes met Rio’s, as if speaking directly to her.  So, no matter what happens or what deep dark secrets they harbor, my character, Ann, knows that her wife will always be the person she wants. That there is nothing that will stand in the way of that.”

Rio let out a breath she hadn’t known she was holding. Okay, so that was certainly a declaration. Was Agatha actually speaking to her, though? They hadn’t known each other when she had filmed that scene, so surely it was just a coincidence. Rio needed to go to sleep. She was reading too far into things. She was not Megastar girlfriend material. She was best friend material.



Rio woke up to Wanda Maximoff throwing clothes on her bed. “It’s past noon, and we have to get you packed. Wake up, sleepyhead.” She said as she found Rio’s half-packed suitcase.

Rio rolled over, pulling the blankets over her head. She was having extremely pleasant dreams about a certain blue-eyed actress. “How the hell did you get in here?” she grumbled.

“Sorry, Ri. I let her in.” Alice apologized from the other room. “I also fed Raven. She was starving.” She added.

“Don’t believe her lies. She woke me up at five and made me feed her.” Rio got out of bed, made it, and got dressed in her bathroom. “What is all this anyway?” She called as she pulled her shirt over her head.

“You leave for New York tomorrow, well, tonight really, and Jen sent me to make sure you are dressed appropriately,” Wanda called back as she rifled through Rio’s suitcase. Alice just watched with amusement.

“I know how to pack for colder weather, Wanda,” Rio emerged from the bathroom in yoga pants and a t-shirt.

“No, I mean that,” She waved her hand at Rio’s current outfit. “The two of you will be alone, but in public a lot. Security will be around but at a distance. Wear something else when you’re around her. Plus, you’ll be going out Friday night, so bring something other than jeans and a T-shirt, Rio! You are gorgeous! Show it off! You can wear a coat when you’re outside.” Wanda was smiling, wickedly.

“That’s what the clothes are for? To make sure I’m wearing coven-approved clothing that puts a spin on things?” Rio asked skeptically.

Wanda nodded after removing most things from Rio’s suitcase. At least her lacy, coordinating underwear had made the cut. Rio had that going for her. “Why couldn’t we just go shopping?” Rio looked at the large stack of clothing and several shopping bags.

Wanda shook her head, “No time, I sent Alice and Jen to do it.”

“It’s a weekend, Wanda, not a month. This is entirely too much,” Rio was getting overwhelmed.

“I agree,” Wanda had started holding clothes up to Rio, Alice trying hard not to laugh from her spot on the bed. “If I had my way, you’d spend the majority of the weekend wearing mostly nothing, but both of you are idiots and won’t make a move.” She sighed.

“Thank you for your concern about my love and sex life,” Rio grabbed a short red dress from Wanda, if you could call it that, and went to try it on.

“We just want the best for both of you,” Alice called out behind her.

Wanda nodded as they waited for Rio to come out. “You treat her like a regular person, and that is so important. You went from starstruck to ‘this is just Agatha’ in record time. You could handle the fame. You meet her at her most sarcastic. Alice and I are so protective of the two of you, and we agree that we would allow this.”

“I still get starstruck,” Rio replied.  Last night was the first time I truly saw her in her element. Thanks for the blessing to date your daughter there Mr. Harkness.” She smirked as she came out of the bathroom. Wanda and Alice just stared.

Rio could feel herself blushing. “What? Does it look bad?”

The dress was bold. It was short. It was revealing. And Rio was proud of herself for getting into it without help. It was classic apple red, one strap holding it up on her right shoulder that went diagonally across her chest. While the dress completely covered her left side, on the right side, her breast was covered, but there was a cutout that went up to the bottom of her sternum, showing off most of her ribcage. Much of her back was exposed, except where the fabric from the right went around as if it was a strapless bra and connected to the left, where the shoulder strap also came down, diagonally, and connected. It was mid-thigh length, hugged every curve just right, and showed off every muscle. Rio liked to stay in shape, and in this dress, it showed.

Wanda shook her head vigorously.

“Holy shit. You’re fucking hot, Rio. Wear that Friday night. You might have a hard time dancing, nope, that’s perfect for dancing.”

Alice nodded. “Agatha will love it.”

Rio hadn’t even really looked at herself. “You’re both exaggerating,” She went back to the bathroom to look at herself. “Fuck. I’d fuck me in a heartbeat.”

Wanda threw herself back on the bed. “That’s the point!!”



Rio was suddenly more excited than she had been before.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha had finished her packing, knowing they would be seen around the city. They had spent most of their friendship in private places or school functions, and now they would be in public spaces. Pictures were bound to be taken, there would be speculation, and Jen would be working her ass off from LA. Jen was the Tree to her Taylor, and Agatha would forever be in her debt.

Her suitcase was ready by the door since they were leaving the house around one a.m. and would meet the private jet on the tarmac. It was roughly a six-and-a-half-hour flight to New York and a three-hour time difference. They would get there by early afternoon, she would run through a few things at the studio, and they would go to dinner and then out. Wanda had gone through Agatha’s suitcase and approved what she had packed, as well as added a few things. She replaced some old pajamas with much shorter ones, though. Agatha allowed it. She had older ones in the Manhattan apartment. She’d wear them and send Wanda a picture.

Agatha had seen the comments and pictures posted online, and Jen had already posted statements and scrubbed a few unsavory things for both of the starlets. Things that speculated about Wanda and Vision, information that had leaked about Rio, the standard fare. She would never trade this life, but there were parts of it that she could do without. For one, the hateful articles about the movie were a part she hated. She loved some of the comments from her fans, though.

“It is 2024, another year of our Lord and Savior Agatha Harkness, and there is STILL homophobia? HOW?!”

“The entire country rallied around this woman in 1989. She propelled the entire community, yet there are still pockets of these people? It’s been 35 years!”

“This just in: the movie is amazing, and no one cares about your hate.”

“The true tragedy is how she’s clearly in love with that woman. Is Agatha about to be off the market for good? I mean, My chances weren’t AMAZING, but now they’re zero.”

Agatha did a double take. She was absolutely NOT in love. At all. Strong like, maybe. Definitely lust. So much lust. She loved Rio like a friend, but she was not in love with Rio.

She clicked on her hashtag to read more comments, just for fun.

“That poor woman doesn’t know what’s coming.”

“IS WAGATHA OVER? I AM A CHILD OF DIVORCE!”

“HOW DO YOU GET AN INVITE FROM AGATHA?!”

“Could you imagine having to handle all of the fame that goes with just DATING Agatha?”

“RIP WAGATHA!”

“So, Mother broke up with Wanda and went and found a child to date?”

“Imagine going to a movie premiere with Agatha and then watching her have sex with Wanda Maximoff.”

“I know her, she’s a teacher!”

“Not Agatha being hot for teacher.”

“LORNA WU’S DAUGHTER IS THERE!”

Agatha would have to make sure Jen kept an eye out for information about Rio leaking. She would also need to talk to Rio and ask her what she wanted Agatha to post on her official social media pages.

She looked up from her phone as Wanda walked in. They had apps on their phones to disarm the alarm systems, as well as keys, to each other’s houses. Jen and Alice also had that power, and recently, she’d given Rio that access. She trusted her. It’s what friends did.

“Is Rio coming here tonight, or are you picking her up?” Wanda asked as she went straight to the kitchen to refill her water cup.

“Picking her up.”

“Change it. Have her come here. It’ll be easier.” Wanda walked into the living room and flopped down next to Wanda, traces of cat hair on her.

Agatha scoffed, noticing the feline evidence, “You just packed for her and want to make sure she doesn’t unpack everything,” she stated with a knowing look.

“Damn that amazing cat and her hair.” Wanda looked down at herself, knowing she’d been caught red-handed because of Raven.

“I would rather have her over anyway,” Agatha muttered, seeing Wanda’s sly grin. “Whatever, where’s your wife?” Agatha continued.

Agatha shot Rio a text asking her if she wanted to come over, eat dinner, and leave from there instead. Rio replied instantly with yes and that she’d be there at seven. It was currently five-thirty.

“We are not married. We’re not even technically dating,” Wanda rolled her eyes. “The boys wanted to go to some college sports thing this afternoon with Vision, and she went with them as extra security. Did you not know? Your OWN SON went.”

“I knew he was going. I just didn’t know Nat was going with them instead of spending the time with you. She usually spends all possible moments with you. Trouble in paradise?” Agatha asked, looking at Wanda seriously.

Wanda just smiled. “No. Truthfully? I asked her to go. After seeing some…comments, online today, she needed to be seen protecting someone that wasn’t me.”

Agatha gave her a warm look. “Oh honey, I’m sorry you saw those. I’m trying to get all the nastiness taken down,” Wanda nodded as tears started to form. Agatha held her arms open, and Wanda buried them into her like she had done hundreds of times over the years. Agatha soothed her, smoothing her hair down, ever the protective big sister. “Hey, it’s just speculation right now. Almost all of the comments were positive. We still have so much time to prepare for everything, okay? We’re able to get ahead, and we’re going to tackle it all head-on. All of us, okay? This family that you and I built, starting with you seducing Nat out of killing us, we’re going to do everything we can, and we’ll protect each other.” She kissed Wanda on top of her head in comfort.

Wanda shifted so her head was leaning on Agatha’s shoulder, legs over her lap, arms wrapped around her waist. Agatha had her feet propped on the table in front of them, and both arms around Wanda’s shoulders.

“I know we will. I’m just worried about the boys and what people will say at school, or what if Nat decides she doesn’t want this?” Wanda’s lip quivered as she worried out loud.

If not for the seriousness of Wanda’s tone, Agatha would have laughed. “Natasha Romanov has been in love with you for 25 years. She has handled every single thing that has come your way with poise, grace, and, at times, violence if needed. You two found ways to be together in your own way, and now you truly get to be together again. I have never seen a love as pure as the one between you. That woman would marry you the second the divorce was finalized, and Vision would hand you the rings. You think he didn’t know? He absolutely did. As for the boys? No one will say a thing, and if they did? Between Lilia, Rio, Alice, and Nicky? They’ll be fine. Don’t let other people get into your head and try to ruin your happiness, angel. I will always defend you, and you know it. I will burn my whole life down to protect our family, and don’t you forget it.” Agatha squeezed Wanda’s shoulders as a few tears of her own fell. She had always been protective of Wanda and tried to shield her from all negativity, speculation, and anything she could. Wanda had had enough trauma in her life before she had even come to America, she didn’t need more of it, and Agatha had spent nearly 30 years trying to keep it that way.

Wanda nodded and pulled away so she could look at Agatha. “I went to Rio that night. The night you and Nat were in New York? You trust her so much, so I knew I could too. We, Vision and I, were helping the boys with homework, and for a moment, I, I don’t know, hallucinated?. That Vision was actually Nat. I nearly lost it. So I texted Rio, and when homework was over, I went to her place. We got drunk, and I told her everything. She was the one that gave me the final push I needed. I told her not to tell you, to let me be the one to tell you that I went to her instead of you.” Wanda confessed, chewing on her lip.

Agatha smiled softly. “I knew. She didn’t tell me. I just knew. I also noticed the two of you have been closer since then, and it means a lot to me. I’m glad it was her, and I’m just happy you finally did it. Now, stop worrying. Nat will be home soon, and the two of you can go spend the night together. Forget the speculation from a tiny, tiny portion of fans. The majority still think Vision just doesn’t like the spotlight or that you and I are dating. Though now a lot think we might have broken up.” Agatha looked up as she heard the door open.

Rio walked in and saw tears drying. “What happened?” She asked, concern in her voice.

“Oh, nothing, sweetie. Wanda just had a moment, everything got sentimental, and emotions happened.” Agatha answered, not noticing the nickname she had used.

Wanda’s eyebrows shot up as she looked between the two of them. Rio also raised an eyebrow.

“Well, okay, darling . Glad that happened before we left. I put my suitcase next to yours.”

She sat down next to Agatha as Wanda moved over. “I hate the people of the internet, by the way.” She was completely ignoring the ‘sweetie’ of it all.

The other women just nodded. Wanda grinned broadly, while Agatha ignored the ‘darling’ of it all.

⭐⭐⭐

Rio had flown before, but never on a private jet. Let alone a movie star’s private jet. Alice was taking care of Raven for the weekend, Rio had everything prepared to go back to school for the next week, and she had made sure that the only thing she had to focus on this weekend was New York and Agatha. She still wasn’t exactly sure how she had gotten here, but she was here. Agatha was now one of her closest friends, even if they were attracted to each other. They were clinging to stupid rules for reasons that held merit, apparently. Despite that, all the lines were blurred, and all the streams were crossed.

She was both confused and thinking clearly.

When she got to Agatha’s, the plan had been to get a few hours of sleep. Instead, the three of them talked until Nat got back, and Wanda attacked Nat with kisses. Absolutely planned by Wanda, Rio was sure of it. Once red-headed demons one and two had left, Rio and Agatha continued talking about New York until they headed to the airport, which was an experience just to be driven up to the plane.

Now, Rio sat in what felt more like an actual recliner on the plane that turned into more of a bed. Thankfully, this one was a double seat, and the arm came up.

“Can I sit next to you?” Agatha asked once they were in the air.

“Of course, I was slightly offended when you didn’t.”

Agatha got up and moved over, sitting next to Rio, not bothering to separate them with the armrest. “Don’t laugh,” She paused, waiting for Rio’s response.

“I won’t.”

“After all these years of flying all over the world, I still get airsick. I get seasick, too. I’m surprised I don’t get carsick. So I sit by myself on take-off and landing just in case. Usually, it’s just the feeling, nothing else. I just don’t trust it.” She searched Rio’s face for any judgment, laughter, any negativity at all. She knew it was slightly ridiculous as she had been flying almost her entire life.

Rio just looked back with empathy. “In middle school, I got sick on the bus on a field trip. It was awful. I absolutely get it.”

“THAT wasn’t your most embarrassing moment?”

“You asked about high school, not middle school. Once in college, I got so drunk at a party and threw up all over this girl I was dating. She completely freaked out and broke up with me, said she couldn’t handle anyone that vomited,” Rio laughed at the memory. “Turns out I had been sick. She didn’t care. Puking was a red flag, apparently.”

Agatha shook her head as she laughed. “If someone I cared about got sick, no matter what the cause, I would just take care of them because that’s what you do when you care about someone.”

“Agatha Harkness, award-winning megastar, one of the most famous women in the world, the best mother ever, Hollywood’s bad-boy and womanizer, which I have found not to be true, activist, looks great in everything, the best friend anyone could have, protector of all, also has a heart of gold and cleans up puke. Who would have thought?” She was looking at Agatha with awe by the time she was done with her list. For years, she had idolized and fantasized about this woman, and now, Agatha was one of her best friends. She was Nicky’s teacher, and she so badly wished Nicky could transfer to another teacher, but no one else taught her subjects.

Agatha knew she needed to confide in Rio. She still didn’t know when the perfect time would be. Here she was, staring into those beautiful brown eyes that she could get lost in, breathing in the eucalyptus scent, wanting so badly to close the distance between them. However, she knew they had to lay everything out before that happened. Coward. Instead, she just sighed. “We should probably try and get some sleep. Want to sleep with me on an airplane?” She gave Rio a comically exaggerated wink.

Rio giggled in the way only Agatha could make her do, which elicited a snort of laughter from Agatha. She was exhausted, and they had another four hours to go. They both needed some semblance of sleep as she yawned after her giggle fit and nodded. She reclined her seat as Agatha did the same while handing her a blanket. Rio wasn’t ready to tell Agatha everything, but she was done pretending like she didn’t want to melt into her when they slept, and the crew and security aboard the plane had all signed ironclad NDAs and most likely been threatened by Nat. She lay on her side, curled up against Agatha, head on her shoulder, arm over her stomach, leg over hers. Rio positioned herself before she could overthink it.

Agatha stiffened at first when Rio had assumed her position and then relaxed once she settled. No wonder their friends were teasing them so much lately. She wrapped her arms around Rio and scooted a bit closer. Agatha knew that in their sleep they tended to end up intertwined, but deliberately falling asleep like this? She could get used to it. She wanted to get used to it.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha’s alarm went off a few hours later. They were roughly an hour away from landing. It was about eight-thirty California time but eleven-thirty New York time. She knew Rio’s body was going to be thrown off. Agatha was used to this.

Rio raised her head, “Are we there yet?” She looked around, blinking.

“Not yet, kid. We have about an hour,” She raised their seats. “Are you hungry?”

Rio yawned and stretched as she was forced into a seated position. “Maybe a granola bar? Something light. I’m going to change first, though,” She got up and grabbed the backpack she had brought on board, her suitcase in the cargo area, and walked to the bathroom.

Agatha watched her go. The longer they were friends, the more she noticed, and the harder it was to hide her feelings. “You’re an actress, Harkness, fake it,” she muttered to herself.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha loved New York, and she was excited to have Rio by her side. She had to run by the studio first, though it wouldn’t take long, and Rio had insisted on going with her. Rio felt weird going to Agatha’s apartment without her and didn’t mind spending time around actors she admired.

They walked onto the set, and Agatha hugged some of her friends and people they knew, introducing Rio to everyone. Rio smiled as several people gave Agatha knowing smiles as she had talked about Rio just a bit earlier in the week. A few former castmates were guest-starring this week as well, Kate McKinnon being one of Rio’s favorites.

Rio sat in a room backstage, scrolling on her phone as Agatha quickly ran through a few skits. Rehearsal would last most of the day tomorrow, but some things had changed, and they wanted to go over some things a few extra times today. Rio heard footsteps and looked up to see two of the cast members, Heidi and Chloe, coming towards her. Riosmiled before greeting them. “I hate to sound like a total fangirl, but Heidi, I love you in What We Do in the Shadows, and your annoying coworker Crystal’s character is spot on. Chloe, your impressions are fantastic, and the skit with Wanda a few weeks ago where the two of you made fun of the whole Wagatha thing was hilarious. I can’t believe you didn’t break.” She said, her eyes going between both of them.

They both smiled and thanked her.

“And you must be Rio. Agatha talks a lot about you,” Chloe looked at her, “are you two dating?”

Rio shook her head and did her best to laugh. “No, we’re close friends. We’re not dating.”

Heidi nodded and glanced at Chloe knowingly. “Agatha mentioned she was taking you out tonight, and she always goes to the same place, so maybe we’ll see you. Bye!” She waved as they left. Rio raised an eyebrow as she watched them leave. Agatha came up beside her not a minute later.

“Are you ready to go?” Agatha asked. She had seen and heard the entire exchange.

Rio nodded as Agatha led the way. Still slightly confused. Where did Agatha always go?

The Manhattan apartment was huge. Wagatha had converted the entire top floor of the building into one large residence.

“This is my room,” Agatha said, walking into one of the largest master suites Rio had ever seen. There was a black four-post California king bed with purple bedding and more pillows than she could count. All of the furniture was solid black wood, and dark hardwood floors continued through the entire home. Rio noticed a private balcony through sliding glass doors and a huge en-suite. Double doors led to a closet that she was sure was the size of her bedroom at home.

“You can share with me or take the room across the hall.” She added softly.

Rio thought about it. They had to keep at least a dotted line of a boundary, though. “I’ll take the room across the hall, but I’m still sharing a bed with you, Harkness. You know, tradition at this point.”

“Right,” Agatha said with a smirk, “Tradition.”

They had both taken to their separate bedrooms to shower and get dressed for the evening. Rio was double-checking everything. She had chosen black high-heeled ankle boots and a black clutch to go with the red dress. Her hair was down in loose waves, with a smokey eye and a dark red lipstick. Small, simple silver hoops completed the look. She took a deep breath and looked at herself in the mirror. “Alright, Rio Fucking Vidal. Go for it.”

She walked into the main room of the home, her heels clacking on the hardwood, and for the second time that week, was blown away by Agatha Harkness. Agatha was wearing a black and white corset top that buttoned in the front with black tapered pants that were perfectly tailored to her, and black heels. Her hair was somehow tamed and pulled back into a low ponytail. Rio wanted to undo each button personally. Her make-up was light, and she just oozed Agatha Fucking Harkness, Queen of Fucking Everything.

Agatha heard Rio walk in as she texted Nicky to check on him. She raised her eyes and almost dropped her phone. She put it away as she gathered her composure and her ability to function for the second time that week. Rio was gorgeous, but in this outfit, her natural beauty and sexiness were brought out and on display. She exuded confidence, and Agatha wanted to push her up against the wall and test out how kiss-proof the lipstick was. She would have to remember to thank Wanda. She had a feeling this dress was her doing.

“I don’t know if I’ve told you this recently, but you are gorgeous,” Agatha said softly, eyes locked on Rio’s. Part of her really wanted to say ‘That dress looks amazing, it would look even better on the floor of my bedroom’, but she was trying not to be that woman anymore.

Rio blushed but didn’t break the eye contact. “You have said something to that effect, but I like hearing it. Especially from you.” She broke the contact long enough to look her up and down, making it obvious she was checking her out. “You are beautiful yourself, Harkness,” She got right next to Agatha and said softly in her ear, “Sexiest woman alive.” Before gently brushing past her and grabbing her black peacoat. “Ready to go, m’lady?” Rio had a plan. They would go out, come back, and as they lay in bed, Rio would tell her everything. Hopefully, Agatha would share, and they would make their decision. She was tired of going back and forth, tired of walking around it. It was time to lay everything out on the table and decide if they could do this.

Agatha’s breath had hitched in her throat when Rio called her the sexiest woman alive. It was a title she had received before. Plenty of women had called her sexy. However, hearing it from Rio was different. It held more meaning. She turned and grabbed the jacket for her outfit and her clutch, following Rio. Agatha was hoping that tonight would be the night that they talked, laid it all out, and realized they could do this. She wanted Rio more than she had wanted anyone or anything else in her life. At the premiere on Wednesday, Agatha watched that scene imagining that Wanda was Rio. It had been quite the fantasy, but she wanted the real thing. Not this weekend, necessarily, but soon. She just so desperately wanted to finally kiss Rio and find out if it was all fireworks, stars, kittens, and rainbows like she thought it would be.

Agatha took Rio to one of her favorite places for dinner, ensuring they had a private area. Jen had previously set it up for them, though it was known she was in town since she was hosting SNL this weekend, so paparazzi pictures had been taken. Agatha had taken a moment in between courses to ask Rio what she wanted her to post on the official socials. “Since you went to a premiere with me and you’re being seen with me here, there’s going to be speculation. I like to address things on my socials so I can control the narrative. What would you like me to say?” She asked, trying to pry into Rio’s thoughts.

Rio knew what Agatha was doing, and she refused to show her hand. “We can wait a few days to see if it settles down first? After that, maybe post that we’re just close friends?”

“Perfect.” Or we can talk this weekend, and I can tell them we’re dating.

They ate while Agatha told Rio about New York and different things about SNL, making Rio laugh with different stories. When they finished, she propped her chin on her hand and looked over at Rio, “We have two options. We can go back home, or we go to an invite-only lesbian club that’s owned by a celebrity friend of mine. Your choice,”

Rio thought about it for a moment. She was so nervous about talking, and while they had each had a martini with dinner, it wouldn’t hurt to go dancing and drinking to help loosen up their nerves.

“Take me dancing!” She said with a grin.

Agatha was a tad disappointed but also excited to take her out. They had all night and tomorrow night, plus the flight back. The night was still young and showing her off would be fun.

The club had a strict ‘no phone, no pictures’ rule, which had probably saved Agatha a lot of bad publicity over the years. The music was loud, and there were many high-profile women. Rio took it all in while she could before Agatha was swarmed.

Agatha had forgotten exactly why she was so popular here.

Because up until Agatha met Rio, Agatha fucked. A lot. Shit.

Rio just watched as women were all over Agatha. Famous women, high profile women, random women.

“We told you she comes to the same place.” A familiar voice said in Rio’s ear as another woman handed Rio a shot. She looked over to see Chloe and Heidi. Rio nodded. “I didn’t know what you meant at the time. Cheers,” she said, raising the shot and then downing it.

“We need more of these,” Chloe said as she disappeared.

Agatha was trying to escape the attention while being polite. Three shots later and a drink in hand, she got to Rio. “I am so sorry!” She said joining Rio and her two companions.

Rio masked her jealousy well, smiling at Agatha.  “No problem! I’ve been drinking a bit,” She gestured to a table holding a few shot glasses, drink in hand. “Looks like people missed you. Who’s the drink from?” She asked, feigning innocence.

Agatha shrugged. “A singer.”

“That’s nice.”

They were interrupted by someone asking Agatha to dance. Some actress from a TV show that Rio didn’t really watch. Agatha turned towards Rio to say something.

Rio couldn’t believe that Agatha was going to ask her for permission to dance with someone else when Rio was so close to finally confessing her feelings to Agatha. She just nodded and waved her hand.

Agatha was shocked Rio was telling her to go off with someone else! She was about to tell this woman that she couldn’t dance with her, and Rio was drinking and acting like it was no big deal. Two could play this game. She pointed to a VIP booth to the side to let Rio know she’d meet her there, downed her drink and set it on the table, grabbed the other actress's hand, and stormed off.

Rio had other plans. She told Heidi to get more shots and showed her where to take them, took Chloe by the wrist, and led her out to where they were close enough to Agatha and whoever the fuck, to clearly be seen by her. Chloe laughed and leaned close to Rio’s ear so she could be heard, “Are we playing the ‘make my just-a-friend’ jealous game?” she asked. Rio made a guilty face, and Chloe nodded with a grin. “Permission to play along?” Rio nodded. Maybe it was the alcohol, but she automatically trusted her and knew that if anyone could make this look semi-believable, it was Chloe.

Chloe leaned in close to her ear. “That dress looks amazing on you and Agatha is extremely lucky.”

Rio blushed and lightly smacked her arm, “Chloe! Stop! You’re making me blush!” She laughed, Chloe was quickly becoming a friend, and she recognized the flirting was deliberate and innocent.

Chloe quickly glanced at Agatha, who was shooting daggers at her before dancing even closer to Rio and leaning in again, “We have her attention. Try to keep eye contact with her, glare, give her fuck-me eyes, whatever. Just make sure she knows that you wanted me to be here later so she doesn’t murder me, okay?” Chloe instructed before she spun Rio around by the hips, pressed their bodies together, and guided their dancing. Rio followed and let her lead. She found Agatha, who was running her hands through her hair and glaring at Rio as she was doing some type of hip thrust against a supermodel half her age. Rio was young, but not that young. Agatha looked directly at Rio, fire in her eyes, and bit her lip. Rio saw that Agatha was also here to play, and Rio was finding that a jealous Agatha was doing things to her.

Agatha did not get jealous. She had everything she wanted in life. Until now. Now, there was one untouchable person because neither of them could use their words like adults. Instead, here they were, grinding on other women like teenagers, giving each other bedroom eyes across a crowded dance floor to highly suggestive music. She hoped Chloe was acting right now because her hands on Rio’s bare skin went past friendly.

Rio ran her tongue over her bottom lip as she put her hand on top of Chloe’s and guided it down her thigh, eyes screaming, ‘this should be you.’

Agatha could feel the heat low in her abdomen, but she could also feel the tightening in her chest. She had no right to Rio. They weren’t dating. They hadn’t even confessed feelings for each other. Yet at this moment, Agatha wanted to part the sea of bodies, rip her out of Chloe’s arms, pull Rio against her, and kiss her in a way that made it very clear to everyone that Rio was off limits. Agatha was not territorial or possessive, but Rio just brought out something primal in her. Agatha was also too competitive for this.  She was going to double down. She could own Rio from afar. It was then that Andie, her friend and club owner, walked by. She caught her arm and slid behind her. She leaned in and spoke in her ear.

“Making someone jealous, play along.”

Andie understood the assignment as she turned around and pressed herself against Agatha. What Rio couldn’t see was that Andie was laughing the entire time.

Who is that, now? Rio thought. Agatha hadn’t had to do much but whisper in someone’s ear, and now another gorgeous celebrity was already rubbing herself against Agatha. Rio gave up, she couldn’t outact an Oscar winner or apparently, out seduce her. Plus,  the longer she saw Agatha grind on that blonde in a way that was borderline sex, she wanted to scream. Rio tapped Chloe’s wrist as she turned around. “Drinks,” she demanded as she led her towards the table. Heidi raised an eyebrow, seeing them return.

“Trying to make Agatha jealous?” She asked, drinking water.

Rio took two shots. “How did you know?”

Heidi looked at Chloe and then back to Rio. “The way she’s talked about you this week, and the way you two look at each other. You like her and she likes you. I’m surprised she brought you here. What a bad idea for someone she genuinely likes. Maybe this is her trying?”

Chloe nodded. “We don’t know her super well, and the woman has a reputation. We see her here a lot.” She added that last bit in a whisper.

Rio was already well into drunken territory, and her common sense failed her as she took two more shots. This wasn’t what she had wanted from the night, and she doubted her ability to handle any of this. Rio was racing towards blackout drunk when Agatha joined them.

“Having fun?” Agatha asked as Rio stumbled into her. “How much have you had to drink?” She looked at her, startled concern etched into her face.

“Not sure,” Rio replied, straightening up. Everything she had eaten at dinner was alcohol-soaked, and she felt like the excess was going straight to her head.

Agatha wasn’t sober, far from it, but she was nowhere near where Rio now. Agatha had been drinking since she was a child and could drink just about anyone under the table. She took one of the remaining shots from the table before turning to Rio. “Are you too drunk to dance?”

Rio’s heart raced and she went pale as she shook her head so hard she almost fell over. Agatha put her hand on Rio’s waist to steady her. This was what Rio had been waiting for, and she was drunk. Good job. She downed another drink, for courage, and let Agatha lead her back to the floor just as a remix of “Red Wine Supernova” came on. Rio smiled and looked down, her face already flushed from the alcohol, still gained an added blush.

“What?” Agatha asked in her ear as they moved to the music.

“Just, this fucking song.”

“Fitting?” Agatha asked with her signature smirk. Fuck being subtle. It’s not like they were good at it anymore and the alcohol was not helping.

“Perfectly,” Rio replied with a smirk of her own as she tried to give Agatha a smoldering look, but realized she was extremely drunk as she rolled her hips into Agatha’s at the “want me to fuck you?” line.

Agatha’s eyes went dark with need. They needed to have that talk, and now. Not in some club, not in the car with people listening, but at home. She gripped Rio’s wrist and pulled her towards the door. She caught Heidi’s gaze and waved. Agatha knew her tab would be automatically closed, and she’d shoot a text to Andie later.

Rio let Agatha lead her out of the club. Hell, she’d let Agatha lead her into the woods and drain her of her life force. She didn’t care. Rio was drunk and she needed Agatha in more ways than one. They needed to get back to the apartment and talk. Rio couldn’t remember the words which she had practiced in her head so many times,  but knew it would come to her. As the car door shut behind them, Agatha handed Rio a bottle of water. “I need you to dilute some of that alcohol. I’m drunk, but you’re blitzed.”

Rio downed the water, knowing whatever it was they were about to do required more than half of a functioning brain cell. She stared at Agatha. She was so pretty, and famous, and perfect. How had Rio gotten to this point in her life? She was about to do something, she wasn’t entirely sure what, with Agatha Harkness.

Agatha drank her water slowly. She was careful not to move Rio too roughly or push her too hard. She was worried about her. How many drinks did Rio have when I wasn’t looking?  Her answer was apparent as Rio looked a little green around the edges. Agatha needed her to hold out till they could at least talk. Maybe just finally kiss? She wanted so much more, but  Agatha never took advantage of those situations. They made it home and up to the apartment, Rio leaning heavily against Agatha as they hung up their coats, tossing aside Rio’s heels that had come off in the car. Agatha sighed in relief. She hated wearing heels but liked the extra height. She turned Rio toward her, hands lightly on her waist. Rio brought her hands up and placed them on Agatha’s shoulders, looking at her, Rio’s brown eyes were bloodshot but full of emotions that Agatha couldn’t quite place.

Agatha brought her hand up, tucking Rio’s hair behind her ear as Rio leaned into the touch, sighing as she stepped closer. Agatha leaned their foreheads together, it was intimate and they were so close, not yet, she told herself. “We need to talk,” she whispered, her lips almost touching Rio’s.

“Agatha, I…” Rio started. Suddenly, she pushed Agatha back, eyes full of alarm as she ran towards the nearest bathroom. Agatha sighed heavily as she followed her, hearing the undeniable sounds of too much partying coming back up.
