Agatha, true to what she had said no less than twenty-four hours earlier, pulled Rio’s hair back in a loose ponytail and placed a cool washcloth on her neck as she rubbed her back. Rio had definitely tried to hang with the professionals.

While Rio brushed her teeth in Agatha’s bathroom and cleaned up a bit, Agatha pulled out pajamas for her. A green tank top and short set with lace down the sides that she knew was Wanda’s doing. Rio came out of the bathroom with wobbly legs and looked at Agatha with big brown eyes. “Will you help me?” She asked in the most vulnerable tone Agatha had ever heard come from Rio’s lips. The dress was complicated and she couldn’t work it in her state.

Agatha nodded as she found the zipper on the side with the most fabric. She carefully unzipped it, still facing Rio. She was trying her best not to touch Rio’s skin. Rio wasn’t wearing a bra, and Agatha was painfully aware of that fact as she quickly turned around and faced away from Rio.

“Agatha,” Rio’s voice trembled as Agatha heard the sound of fabric hitting the floor.

“Yes?” Agatha tried to keep her own voice steady and normal, though it came out a bit breathy.

“You can look. We’re grown adults,” Rio said, placing a hand on her shoulder, partly to stabilize herself.

Agatha was still facing the other direction. “Rio, you’re drunk. I’m not going to take advantage of you. Contrary to popular belief, I am not that kind of girl.”

Rio removed her hand and sat on the bed so she wouldn’t fall over while she put her clothes on. Agatha was right. Rio was drunk, and she had lost her chance. “And they say chivalry is dead.” Rio joked, trying to mask her disappointment. Her head started to swim again. She really needed to stop drinking around Agatha.

“It’s just the gays keeping it alive. I’m going to change. Get in bed,” she ordered as she grabbed a pile of clothes and walked to the bathroom.

“Ooh. Yes, ma’am,” Rio tried to say seductively, but she was slurring her words again.

Agatha just sighed. “So you don’t hurt yourself,” she added.

Agatha was thankful for the pajamas she had in her dresser. The ones in her suitcase were the same as Rio’s, except purple. She had rolled her eyes and smiled at Wanda’s cheekiness when she pulled them out. Tonight was not the night for those, though. She knew the lace would show off sideboob, and drunk McGee in there didn’t need that temptation. Instead, it was plaid flannel pajama pants and an old promo shirt from The Witches’ Road. She had also decided a bra was necessary.

“No temptations.” She told herself in the mirror, for me or her.

When she joined Rio in bed, her very inebriated companion was half asleep. Agatha lay on her side facing her, “How do you feel?” She asked.

“The room is spinning, and I think I might fall off the planet,” Rio said sleepily, eyes half open.

Agatha knew now was not the time for sharing their deep, dark secrets. She didn’t blame Rio for what transpired tonight. It wasn’t her fault. They had engaged in a power struggle and completely lost the plot. Agatha knew she was afraid of opening up to Rio. She wanted to, but what if Rio wasn’t willing to accept the part of Agatha that didn’t know how to be loved and accepted for who she was? She had been rejected for being famous and kept a secret. So what if that was decades ago, even before she had Nicky. It had still turned her off from the whole serious relationship thing.

Rio was struggling to stay awake. She wanted to tell Agatha about San Francisco, about what’s her name, but she couldn’t form words. It probably wouldn’t have made sense anyway. She felt awful for drinking so much, but had she really been ready to open up? To be rejected? Would Agatha date her, knowing she potentially still had a crazed stalker out there somewhere? It was too much. Her eyelids were heavy, her brain hurt, and she needed to sleep. She slung her arm over Agatha’s middle and clumsily scooted closer to her.

“Night Aggie. I’m sorry I fucked up,” she mumbled. Her eyes had closed, and her words were barely audible.

“You have nothing to apologize for. Good night, Ri,” Agatha didn’t always call her Ri, only sparingly since it felt so intimate. They were going on three months of friendship, and she realized they were more than friends at this point. Rio began to snore a few minutes later as only the drunkest do, and Agatha chuckled. She twisted a little to look at Rio’s face. She kissed her on the forehead and whispered, “you might be worth the risk.” She went to sleep as she thought about all of the pros and cons.

⭐⭐⭐

Things were strangely awkward on Saturday. Neither woman acknowledged anything from the night before. Rio was clearly hungover and felt awful about how things had unfolded, choosing silence over apologies. Agatha was acting normal for the most part but acting like they hadn’t shared any intimate moments. The women had gotten dressed and had a quiet breakfast, making small talk about the day ahead of them before going to the SNL studio. There, Agatha rehearsed and got ready while Rio hung out with Chappell Roan’s girlfriend, of all people. It was quite the experience, and they got along swimmingly, helping the day fly by without forcing Rio to think about last night or confront it with Agatha.

It was roughly half an hour until show time, and Agatha hadn’t spoken to Rio most of the day. The show was about to begin, and she had no idea where Rio even was. Things had been awkward all day, and she hated it. Rio had barely said anything to her since breakfast. They had been so close last night, but she wasn’t entirely sure what had happened. Wanda had checked in earlier, and Agatha just told her they drank too much. She’d fill her in later.

Rio was avoiding Agatha. That hadn’t been her intention when she woke up that morning, but it was now her tool of choice. She knew she’d have to face her after the show, but that was future Rio’s problem. Rio did want to wish Agatha luck though, and that was her current conflict. The good friend in her won, as she walked up to Agatha, who wore a flattering white suit for her monologue.

“Hey, break a leg tonight. You’re going to be amazing. You always are.” She said, walking up behind her.

Agatha turned around and smiled. NOW Rio was talking to her, and she didn’t sound mad or upset. A wave of relief washed over Agatha.  No matter what happened or was going on in that head of hers, Rio was still there to support her. Agatha hadn’t realized that it would mean so much to her.

“Thank you,” she responded.  What’d you do today? I didn’t see you around much.”

Rio noticed the sadness in Agatha’s eyes as she said it, and she felt the pain in her own chest, knowing she had caused it.

“I hung out with Chappell Roan and her girlfriend, talked to Chloe and Heidi when they weren’t busy, met Kate McKinnon and fan girl’d there, talked to Keenan, oh, and met Colin and Michael.”

“I’m glad you had a good day. The show’s about to start. See you in a bit.”

Agatha rushed off with a sweet smile on her face. Now that she had confirmation Rio was done avoiding her, she wanted to get back in the right headspace to host a live show.

Agatha’s face told Rio everything she needed. Not only had Rio ruined last night, but she hurt the one woman she truly cared about today. I need to fix it when we get back to Agatha’s later, she thought. For now, she took her reserved spot in the audience and got ready to watch the great Agatha Harkness dazzle everyone. Rio had watched Agatha countless times on screens, but seeing her live was an altogether different experience. It almost canceled out her awkward and negative feelings from the night before. Before she knew it, the show was over and Rio was determined to make up for last night.

The show ended, and Agatha found Rio quickly. She needed to know why she had been avoiding her all day, but not here. But, just in case, Agatha was going to give her an out: “Some people are going to go…”

Rio cut her off. “No. We’re going to your place.”

Agatha nodded, relieved that Rio had chosen to return with her instead.

They had changed into pajamas and were sitting in the bed facing each other. Agatha had opened her mouth to start talking when Rio held her hand up.

“Wait. I need to say something first. I am so sorry about last night and today. I spent today avoiding you because of everything that happened last night and how awkward I made things. I came here with a plan, but it didn’t go the way I thought it would. I think part of my drinking was needing courage, but I know that if I needed to drink to have the courage to talk, then Agatha, I’m not ready.” The look on her face was earnest, and there was regret and pain in her eyes. She hated herself for not being ready.

Agatha nodded. If she were being honest with herself, she wasn’t quite there either. She nodded, her own blue eyes meeting Rio’s, filled with the same pain and sadness. “You’re right, and we can’t force it. No matter how tempting it may be.,” Agatha said softly.

Rio sighed heavily. “I doubt either of us will go to sleep quickly.”

“No,” Agatha laid on her back and put her arm out. “Come here.”

Rio assumed her position against Agatha, head on her shoulder, arm and leg over her. “Why is this so natural?” Rio asked.

“I’m a very comforting presence.” Agatha smiled.

Rio laughed. “You must be. Confession. Ever since the Ballad of the Witches’ Road came out, it’s been on my sleep playlist. All iterations of it. Several of your songs have been from over the years. Your voice has always calmed me and helped me go to sleep. When we met that day at SHIELD? I even fell asleep to The Ballad that night,” Rio knew her face was bright pink from her confession, but she didn’t care. It was important to share something personal, even if minor compared to the confession still locked up within.

Agatha looked down at Rio, the small divulgence rolling around in her mind. She had comforted this woman for years and hadn’t even known it. Sure, she heard things like this all the time, but coming from Rio was, as always, different and felt special. “You can tell me that, but…”

“It’s different, Harkness.”

“Thank you for sharing that with me. It makes me feel very special, and now I know how to get you to sleep.”

“You are special, and having Agatha Harkness sing me a lullaby? Dream come true.”

“Well, then prepare yourself,” She smiled into Rio’s hair as she began to sing, “Down, down, down the road…”

If you had told 22-year-old Rio that Agatha Fucking Harkness would one day be one of her best friends and hold Rio in her arms while singing The Ballad to help her get to sleep, Rio would ask you to share whatever drugs you were on. Yet here she was, living that exact scenario, with zero drugs involved.

Agatha felt Rio succumb to sleep as she sang and softly ran her fingers through Rio’s hair. Agatha thought it was one of the sweetest and most intimate things she had ever experienced. She didn’t understand how she could have these extremely intimate moments with Rio, yet to profess that which rattled about her core and begged for release scared the hell out of her. Because you’re afraid she’ll reject you, and you can’t handle losing her.

⭐⭐⭐

The return flight was rather uneventful, and they both knew they wouldn’t be able to see each other much the next few weeks. Agatha shared everything with Wanda Sunday evening. Wanda seriously considered slightly maiming her.

“I told you that taking her to ‘The Hex’ was a bad idea!” Wanda practically shouted.

Nat was at football practice with Nicky. The last game of the season was Friday, and they were playing Bayside again before playoffs began. SHIELD was undefeated and had already secured their spot. Bayside had to win on Friday to earn theirs.

“I thought it would be fun! And it was fun but also a bad idea. It turned out, though, that neither of us was ready to have that discussion. And we’ve agreed to hold off that conversation until we are both ready. Not to force or rush it.” Agatha looked at her, knowing she would understand.

Wanda deflated. “I know, I just was hoping for a summer wedding,” she teased.

Agatha froze. She was trying to get the courage to date someone, which was already a leap from her fuck and forget days. She was NOT in love and nowhere near marriage. And here was Wanda talking about weddings.

“I’m sorry, what? Absolutely not. That is, just. No. Your wedding, maybe.” Agatha was short-circuiting.

Wanda laughed. “WHOA! Okay, I struck a nerve there. Also, no, we haven’t talked about it either, though I do want to marry Nat. I’d marry her yesterday if I could.”

“I know you would,” Agatha said, putting a hand on Wanda’s. “Back to me, though. What do I do now?”

“You have an extremely busy schedule leading up to Halloween. You focus on that. Go cheer Nicky at the football game, and go nerd out together at the party. You’re there together anyway.”

Agatha nodded. “I’m surprised. That was a very mature answer.”

“I didn’t choose any days in the betting pool for the next two weeks.”

“I hate you.”

“You fucking love me.”

⭐⭐⭐

Alice and Rio's conversations were mirror images of the ones that Wanda and Agatha had, as if in parallel universes. Ultimately, Alice conceded that she was proud of Rio for not forcing the issue no matter how much she had hoped otherwise.

The next two weeks had gone by quickly. Rio fell back into the school routine, and that Friday, Bayside beat SHIELD. Both teams would be in the playoffs the day after Halloween. Agatha was engrossed in filming, and while it was in LA, it often ran late. They texted, but many nights, it was just to say good night. They watched Halloween movies on their Saturday night, and they ended up falling asleep halfway through Hocus Pocus.

Finally, Halloween arrived, and with it the annual party. They were at some venue owned by Tony Stark. He held the party for the older kids and adults, around 10, and it transitioned to an adults-only event. Stark could be a pretentious asshole, but he liked parties and tried to do right by his daughter.

Agatha had on THE Moiraine traveling costume, a blue shirt and pants, boots, and a long blue coat with leather on the shoulders. Her long brown hair loose about her shoulders.  She loved the way she could use the coat as a cape and be dramatic with it. Rio was dressed as Moiraine’s nemesis, Lanfear. She had forgone the headpiece, but the black leather dress looked amazing on her. The right sleeve was long, and the shoulder was puffy. There were straps around the neck, and criss-cross straps on the left shoulder, otherwise that side was sleeveless. The neckline went straight across her chest with a studded belt right below her breasts. There was a piece in the middle of the dress that came down to the top of her knee that was separate from the sides of her dress, that split on both sides at the top of her thighs, giving a cape-like effect that went to her ankles. She had on wedge platform boots that laced up to mid-thigh and three black leather cuffs on her left wrist. She looked every bit the part of the evil lover of the devil she was portraying.

Alice and Jen were dressed in the classic Xena and Gabrielle costumes from the 90’s TV show, and Wanda still did the Fiyero and Glinda bit with Vision. It was even funnier knowing that Wanda was in love with Nat, dressed in a green and black suit, giving subtle Elphaba vibes. They were all team Gelphie, themselves. Tommy and Nicky were running around as zombies, and Billy and Eddie were Wiccan and Hulkling from The Young Avengers comics.

“Have they confirmed they’re dating yet?” Rio asked Wanda, nodding at the boys.

“No, but look at them. I wish he would just tell me.” She gestured at the two teenagers leaning into each other and acting like they were the only two people who existed.

“They’re adorable.”

“They’re more put together than you and Agatha,” Wanda looked at her knowingly.

“Oh, so you heard about New York?”

“Yes, I did. And, close only counts in horseshoes and hand grenades.”

“Thank you for comparing my dating life to yard games and weapons.” Rio scoffed.

“Why are we talking about violence?” Agatha asked, walking up to them.

“The Xena and Gabby costumes.” Rio lied before Wanda could say anything.

Agatha nodded.

Wanda rolled her eyes. “I’m going to go find my husband,” she looked at the ceiling before whispering, “or my girlfriend,” and giggled. She had been drinking, and while she was keeping everything quiet until after the holidays, she was getting very impatient.

Agatha shook her head. “She’s going to get too bold, and someone’s going to find out.”

“Probably,” Rio nodded. “After 25 years of hiding, can you blame her?”

Agatha shook her head, “Not at all. I’m so proud of her.” They were in a quiet area upstairs. “She told me that you were the one she talked to who gave her the push.” Rio and Agatha were facing each other, bodies only a few feet apart.

“Alice and Jen, Wanda and Nat.” Agatha smiled. They had both decided not to drink and were completely sober.

“I’m just your regular lesbian matchmaker. Maybe I can find someone for Vision after the divorce.” They both laughed.

“Honestly, you came into our group like a force of nature. It felt like you had always been one of us and truly belonged with us. With me. I can’t imagine our lives, my life, without you in it.”

Eyes locked, they had moved closer together, separated by only inches.

“I don’t want a life without you in it, Agatha.” Rio’s eyes dropped to Agatha’s mouth.

Caught up in the moment, Agatha closed the space between them and wrapped her arms around Rio’s waist, pulling her flush against her, and pressed their lips together. Rio’s arms immediately went up and around Agatha’s shoulders as she pressed herself against Agatha, parting her lips to meet Agatha’s movements.

Agatha raised her hand to cup Rio’s jaw as she slid her tongue against hers, deepening their kiss. Finally, Agatha thought. It was everything she had hoped and dreamed for months. They could go back to her place and talk. She was prepared. She was just so happy to finally kiss this amazing, beautiful woman.

Rio’s hand had slid into Agatha’s hair as she used her tongue to explore Agatha’s mouth. She withdrew momentarily to bite Agatha’s bottom lip and then swallowed the whimper that followed. She scratched lightly at Agatha’s scalp as they battled each other for control of the kiss. Rio wondered if they could go back to Agatha’s since they didn’t have school tomorrow. Then it hit her. This was Agatha Harkness. Mother of one of her students. They still hadn’t talked, and that needed to happen before this could continue. Fuck.

Rio froze, eyes flying open as she released her grip on Agatha and stepped back and out of her embrace.

Agatha’s eyes opened at the sudden loss. “Rio?” She saw the panic in Rio’s eyes and realized what had happened. “Hey, it’s okay, let’s talk.”

Rio’s eyes were wide as she shook her head. “Agatha, I…I can’t.”

She turned and bolted.

“You’re an idiot, Harkness,” Agatha pinched her nose before turning on her heel and going after her. She reached the top of the stairs before she felt a hand on her arm. She turned her head to see Lilia.

“Sometimes, you have to give people processing time before they’re ready to talk to you.”

Rio had run. They had known they needed to figure things out before jumping in, and yet, they hadn’t. Everything could be ruined, and Rio’s emotions and thoughts were too unstable to figure it out here. She had pulled her phone out of a hidden pocket and texted Alice to meet her in the front and that they were leaving. Adding SOS next to it. She was crying as she, Jen, and Alice left.

Agatha found Wanda and Nat staring in confusion as they watched Alice’s ride leave with the three women inside. Agatha silently cried as she watched the car disappear.

Worst Halloween ever.






Chapter End Notes

These two, I swear.

Please don't hate me, we're on a roll now.

The Spell is Cast, How Long it Last

Chapter Summary

The aftermath.
The talk.
The wooing.
The Raven.

Chapter Notes

I hope the longer chapters make up for the longer breaks between updates! Adulting really impedes hobbies.
Thank you so much for the kudos, shares, and comments! They mean so much! I am loving this journey so much!

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Agatha Fucking Harkness had kissed her like a soldier returning from war, and now Rio was curled up on her couch, head in Alice’s lap, hugging Raven, sobbing like she’d been dumped on prom night.

Nothing made sense anymore.

“Rio. It’s okay. Why can’t you just talk to Agatha about it now?” Alice asked, rubbing her back.

“Because I’m a coward, and what if she can’t accept it? What if it’s too much? We can’t go back. She’s one of my best friends, and now I know what it’s like to kiss her, and I will always want that,” She explained as the tears continued to fall.

Alice acted hurt, trying to lighten the mood, “Wait a minute! We’ve kissed!”

Jen gasped from her spot in the armchair, “Wait, what?”

Rio gave a small smile as Alice laughed. “We practiced kissing each other when we were in middle school,” Rio supplied.

Jen looked relieved, “I don’t know what I was expecting, but that makes a lot of sense,” She regarded Rio for a moment. “Agatha won’t push you away, you know. She has an entire security team, everything will work out.” She paused momentarily, “Hell, Nat would probably go after the ex personally, and no one gets past Nat.”

Alice nodded. “Rio, Agatha likes you, not just as a best friend, either.”

Rio shrugged. “I just, I don’t know. There are a lot of other things too. The whole, she’s Agatha Fucking Harkness thing, for one.”

Jen just groaned and looked at Alice. Alice looked at her de facto sister. “If she wasn’t one of the most famous people in the world and a parent of one of your students, and you just met, what would you do?”

“I assume in this hypothetical, all the feelings are still there? I would ask her out,” Rio replied.

“Then respond to one of the ungodly number of texts she’s sent you in the past two hours since we’ve left!” Jen nearly yelled, her tone bordering on frustration. It was only a matter of time before grainy photos leaked, and she still had a job to do.

Rio bit her lip. “I can’t, okay! I can’t bring potential harm to their doorstep.” Fresh tears streaming down her face.

Alice squeezed her shoulder. “You’re safe here, and that bitch isn’t going to do anything.”

Rio rolled onto her back, looking up at Alice, “I just don’t trust it, and pictures are all over the internet now from the premiere and a few from New York. You know that demon has seen them, and she knows, and she is definitely the jealous type.” Rio knew for a fact that she had seen them, but she wasn’t telling anyone that.

Jen moved over to the couch, putting Rio’s legs over hers, “You two have never once told me this bitch’s name. Why?”

“It’s like summoning Beetlejuice,” Alice scoffed.

“She’s not wrong. Also, when I say she’s a model, I mean somewhat well-known, and her husband IS well-known,” Rio added.

“Yet, you’re worried about Agatha’s fame?” Jen shook her head.

“I knew San Francisco was never going to make me public. Ever,” Rio shrugged.

Jen was filled with sadness for her friend. “That wasn’t a red flag for you?”

Alice looked down at Rio. “This one was collecting red flags like they were Pokemon. It’s why all of Agatha’s green ones scare her.”

“Agatha has her red flags, but they’re more from trauma,” Rio said, ignoring Alice’s very correct assessment.

“They’re more yellow, maybe orange,” Jen suggested. “I wouldn’t call them red.”

Rio sat up, causing Raven to finally jump down. Rio crossed her legs and leaned back against the couch. “Agatha being more than willing to break the law, and breaking the law, might be a red flag…”

“Eh, depends on the reason,” Alice said, waving Rio off. “Anyway, back to the matter at hand. What are you going to do? You can’t just ignore her. It’s currently,” She checked her phone, “two in the morning, and there’s a football playoff game at six-ish tonight. You know you have to be there.”

Rio groaned. “You’re right, I can’t skip it. I have to support Nicky, and the rest of the team. With injuries, Tommy is playing in it. I’ll figure it out.”

“It will just be awkward.” Jen mused.

Rio nodded. “I just don’t know what to do. We need to talk, but I don’t know how to start that conversation now. ‘Thanks for kissing me in a way that might screw me up forever if it never happens again, but let me tell you about the time I was almost murdered and how I have a crazy ex-girlfriend who is also a stalker. Oh, and there’s a restraining order. A TEMPORARY restraining order because the judge is a fan of hers and her husband.’ That will go over like a pregnant pole-vaulter.”

Jen sighed. “I’ll be ready for her to call the lawyers, call in favors, pull strings, the things Agatha does.” She put a comforting hand on Rio’s shoulder. “Everything will be okay.” Rio nodded. “If you guys think so. It’s just, my ex is a sociopath.”

Alice put an arm across Rio’s shoulders. “I know she is honey, but we have a…former…assassin, two extremely powerful and connected actresses, a fixer, me - your knight of wands, and you, our mad scientist. It’s going to be fine. Right now, we need to focus on how you’re going to handle this.”

Rio sniffled. “Like an adult. Hug my cat, ignore the pretty girl, and drink.”

“No,” Jen and Alice said simultaneously.

“You don’t have to respond to her tonight or even talk to her in the next day or so, okay? You can take a break. However, you need to decide what you’re going to do. Tell her everything or decide you aren’t ready and take a few steps back?” Alice laid it out plainly.

Rio knew she wanted to tell Agatha everything. She also knew that it wasn’t just dating parents, dating scared her as a whole. She had dated the crazy bitch for two years and been in love with her, and it had ended badly. She wanted Agatha, yes, but they were going to have to take this slowly, and she wasn’t sure if Agatha would be okay with that.

There was also what she wasn’t telling Alice and Jen, and that was that crazy pants had absolutely seen the pictures and had sent Rio a message from a blocked number. Rio had ignored it, hoping there would be zero follow-up, but it still existed.

Blocked

Stay away from Agatha.

I love you, and you are still

mine. I will always love you.

Always find you.

Surely, Rio didn’t need to worry about that yet.

⭐⭐⭐

Wanda filled Vision in on what had happened, and he took over teenager duty while she and Nat took Agatha back to the compound. The boys were going to a co-ed sleepover, and Vision had volunteered to be one of the parent chaperones. No one had trusted a bunch of hormone-filled teenagers to be left to their own devices. They may be rich and famous, but they weren’t stupid.

Back at Agatha’s, free of makeup and in comfortable clothing, Agatha was staring at her phone as if it would do tricks.

“Honey, I don’t think she’s going to respond,” Wanda looked at her like she was delivering horrible news.

Agatha chewed on her thumb. “I fucked up.”

“You did,” Nat agreed. “But in your defense, something had to give, and no one was talking.”

Wanda cut her eyes to Nat and back to Agatha. “Nat is…partially right. You two have to talk, and soon, now. You have now pulled back the curtain and know what’s there.”

“Fireworks,” Agatha interrupted.

“We’ll talk about all that in a moment,” Wanda continued, “Now you two know that for anything to continue, you have to talk. It’s time. You can’t go back. You can’t just push that aside.”

“I know, it’s just, what if she doesn’t like what she sees. We can’t go back, and I can’t take back that kiss. What do I do then? Say, sorry, I shoved my tongue down your throat? That was the best kiss of my life, and we KNOW what that means, but let’s just go back to being friends?” Agatha threw both hands in the air, her fingers spread out towards the ceiling, a questioning look on her face.

“Now we’re going back in circles and beating a dead horse. AGATHA! You won’t know until you talk to her!” Wanda grabbed her by her shoulders and shook her a little while Nat huffed a laugh.

Agatha dropped her arms. “I’m so glad you two are taking this seriously.”

Nat looked at her, “Aggie, we are. Trust me. You have been there for us for the past 25 years as we’ve navigated our own mess, and we will be here for you.”

“How will I talk to Rio if she won’t respond?”

“The football game tomorrow!” Wanda suddenly remembered.

“Yes, let me have a heart-to-heart with my son’s TEACHER at the high school football game,” Agatha said sarcastically, “I am sure she will be totally comfortable baring her soul in front of the whole school. Afterward, we could all go get ice cream.”

“I’d go get ice cream.” Nat mocked.

“Agatha, just, please, don’t fuck it up again. I know you hate talking about your feelings and opening up, but you have to tell her everything. Not the vague story like you told us, everything. You don’t have to tell her who it was, just lay your heart bare and let her heal it.” Wanda looked at her pleadingly.

Agatha was picking at loose threads on her yoga pants and just nodded.

“I really thought I’d be mad at her for running, but I’m not. I can’t be. I respect her too much, and, I just, if we end up not working out and remain friends forever, that’s fine.” A tear rolled down Agatha’s cheek.

Nat just shook her head. “Spoken like a true overdramatic lesbian.”

Wanda rolled her eyes, “I know that you’re worried about losing the friendship aspect of all this, but Agatha, that woman is as down bad for you as you are for her. Neither of you will run.”

Agatha looked at them both as she wiped the tears from her eyes. “I’m going to tell her why I don’t date teachers, and why I don’t do relationships…she doesn’t need to know I was ever in love. Only three people know about that. The two of you, vaguely, and the woman I loved. She and I can discuss that part later.” She spoke calmly, mind made up. The truth about her tragic love life was quite awful, and she didn’t think Rio was ready for that.

Wanda put her hand over her eyes and groaned. “That’s going to blow up in your face later, dumbass.”

Nat put a comforting hand on her unofficial girlfriend’s back, “It will, but we’ll be here to help them both because we are gluttons for punishment.”

Agatha smiled, “Thank you both for your service. In the meantime, why won’t she answer?” She pouted, still staring at her phone.

“Because she’s scared, if it was the best kiss of YOUR life, you whore,” Wanda said with love, “then it probably was for her too. Was it really fireworks?” She perked up, wanting details.

Agatha rolled her eyes at the name-calling, she had zero shame about her past, “God, yes. Do you know how terrifying that is?”

“Yes,” Nat and Wanda said simultaneously.

“Of course you two do, but you ended up together,” Agatha pointed out.

“Yes, but I didn’t always know we would,” Wanda pointed out.

“What if we don’t?” Agatha frowned.

Nat looked at her seriously, “How do you know if you don’t try?”

⭐⭐⭐

SHIELD hadn’t reached the playoffs in years, and Lilia Calderu was extremely proud of her school full of nepo babies and kids born with silver spoons in their mouths. Her kids worked hard, practiced hard, played hard, and were dedicated.  They deserved this just as much as any other school in the Trinity League. Of course, with the NDAs in place and the secrecy that shrouded the school she ran, the media was extremely unhappy as they were only being allowed to cover the very basics of the game. If SHIELD did make the finals, there would be negotiating with lawyers.

The coven sat in the bleachers as they always did, cheering on Nicky, and now Tommy and Eddie who had both been moved to the varsity team to cover injuries. Billy still wouldn’t admit that he and Eddie were dating and just turned red when anyone insinuated as much. While Rio usually sat next to Agatha, she was not sure what to do or say about the kiss the night before. Knowing that a high school football game was not the place for it, Rio did her best to distance herself from Agatha.

Rio hadn’t felt this awkward at a football game since high school when she had been caught making out with Sabrina Spellman, the quarterback’s girlfriend, underneath the bleachers. Maybe she needed to swear off alcohol AND women.

Alice leaned into her friend, “Maybe you should talk to her after the game?”

Rio shook her head. “I’m good, thanks.”

Agatha was in agony. She was trying to watch her son as he was completing his passes, making touchdowns, running in a touchdown, and generally getting the job done. However, she kept looking at the raven-haired woman a row below her who had constantly been on her mind since they had met at the end of July. Why was this so hard? She turned to Wanda. “We’re going out tonight.” She said decisively.

“That’s a terrible idea,” Wanda shook her head. Nothing good could come from this.

“Well, I’m going, with or without you,” Agatha tapped Jen’s shoulder in the row below her. “Hey, after the game, we’re going out. Let Alice and Rio know.” She straightened back up as the other team scored.

SHIELD won, advancing to the next round that would take place the following week. The boys were going to a celebratory party, and Vision had volunteered to be one of the parent chaperones. Rio had decided to skip going out with the coven, though everyone else had decided to go, even if just to keep an eye on Agatha.

It was another exclusive club. Agatha didn’t like to risk paparazzi and bad publicity, for which Jen was eternally grateful. Wanda was concerned. Agatha normally didn’t allow herself to get too drunk in public, yet here she was, ten drinks in, no end in sight, and her four babysitters kept losing track of her.

“Do NOT let her make out with or sneak off with anyone!” Wanda instructed. Nat was on security detail, and she couldn’t even keep track of Agatha. Alice was looking around the crowded club, eyes wide.

“Where did she go?” She asked Wanda, eyes wide, with panic in her voice.

“She’s being stupid and self-destructive,” Wanda replied knowingly. “If you see a crowd forming, check it. She’ll most likely be in the middle, controlling all of the women around her.”

Jen was worried for other reasons, “If anyone pulls out a phone that they didn’t check in, take it. Not everyone follows the rules,” She instructed, knowing good and well that Agatha was one who did not follow the rules.

They had split up to track down the drunken disaster. Agatha, however, was in the middle of the dancefloor having a great time. She was dancing with two women she knew, both actresses' though their faces were blurring together. She had changed before coming out, wearing some black, sleeveless, faux leather number. She was numb from alcohol, having lost count of how much she had had to drink, a feat for her, really, and was ready to revert to her old ways. Agatha had her choice of women and was surveying her choices when a woman she didn’t know cut in and started to take Agatha’s attention and took her from everyone else. The woman was gorgeous, with long wavy blonde hair, blue-green eyes, mid-40s if Agatha had to guess. She was dancing suggestively against Agatha, and as far gone as Agatha was, she had lost all control and inhibition. She vaguely remembered being pulled to a dark corner, a mouth pressed to hers, a flash, and something slipped in and out of her pocket, when a familiar redhead pulled them apart “I am so sorry honey, but she is spoken for,” Wanda told the other woman as she pried Agatha away, “you may have better luck with someone else.”

Agatha turned towards Wanda, “What’re you doing?” She slurred.

“Keeping you from making a huge mistake. C’mon, my toasted friend. We’re getting you out of here,” Wanda put her arm around Agatha’s waist as Nat joined them. “Let’s gather Alice and Jen.”

“I’m not done,” Agatha argued as she stumbled into Wanda.

“Yes, you are. This will just make things worse, especially if you do anything else.” Wanda and Nat led Agatha to the car, who, surprisingly, didn’t put up a fight. Wanda looked at Jen and Alice as they approached.

“The two of you are going to Jen’s place tonight,” she instructed. “Rio isn’t going to have an out. Yes, that’s where this one is going,” Agatha was already in the back of the SUV.

“That’s a good idea,” Jen agreed. “Take the highly vulnerable drunk to Rio’s. Let her sober up there. One of them will confess something.”

They went their separate ways as Wanda and Nat headed towards Rio’s, with Agatha moaning about how much she had messed up. She hadn’t realized where they were going until they stood at Rio’s door. Nat had Agatha’s go bag on one shoulder, and they were both holding her up. Wanda knocked as a courtesy, though she had the spare key Alice had given her just in case.

Rio was not expecting anyone. It was almost midnight, and she and Raven had just finished a bowl of ice cream while they watched Agatha Harkness movies so Rio could torture herself. She was wearing old threadbare pajamas and almost shut the door when she saw a drunk Agatha being supported by the two redheads.

“This belongs to you,” Wanda said, pushing their way in before Rio could slam the door.

“She does not,” Rio replied, much quieter than she meant. Those were not the words she thought Wanda would say, and she wanted them to be true.

“I don’t belong to anybody,” Agatha proclaimed, stumbling onto the couch, “Well, except the Mouse,” she added, giggling after remembering the Marvel and Disney contracts she had recently signed.

Nat patted her on the shoulder. “Of course, honey. You’re going to stay with Rio. Nicky is with Vision, okay?”

Agatha nodded as Wanda handed her water.

Wanda turned back to Rio. “Look, she doesn’t get this drunk, especially in public. Even if you have to wait until morning, just talk, please,” Wanda hugged her, kissed Agatha on the head, grabbed Nat’s hand, and left, giving them a meaningful look before closing the door behind them.

Rio turned back to Agatha, who was eyeing her from the couch.

“Hey, hot stuff, did I leave the oven on, or is it just you?” Agatha said playfully.

Rio rolled her eyes as she grabbed Agatha’s bag and found pajamas, “Can you change yourself?”

“I’d rather you strip me down. This dress would look great on your bedroom floor.” She replied, trying her best to be seductive.

Rio had to bite back a laugh. “Just like you, Harkness, I would not take advantage of you. I am a lady.”

Agatha tried to be serious, “No one has ever accused me of being a lady, and you would not be taking advantage, trust me.”

Rio blushed, “While I am flattered by the drunken admission, you can’t consent when you’re drunk. I won’t accept it. Back to the original question, are you stable enough to change yourself?”

Even in her hazy state, Agatha knew Rio was right. “Can you unzip me first?”

Rio nodded and unzipped the dress, admiring Agatha’s toned back as she stumbled off towards the bathroom. Rio had to admit. The dress would have looked great on her floor, flung over a chair, the couch…wherever it had landed. She did follow Agatha at a distance, just in case. Agatha stumbled out of the bathroom a few minutes later. Rio was impressed that her pajamas were on correctly and that she hadn’t fallen over.

“Alright Blitzen, let’s get you to the bed where you’re safe.” Rio guided her.

Rio had already decided that Agatha was taking the bed and she would take the couch. As much as it pained Rio to sleep in the same place and not sleep next to Agatha, she didn’t trust either of them. As it was, she wanted to feel Agatha’s lips on hers again, but this was not the time.

“Ooh, you are taking me to bed!” Agatha grinned, as Rio tucked her in. “Wait, are you not sleeping with me?” Agatha looked at her, confused and hurt.

Rio gave her a small smile and sat on the bed facing Agatha,, before caressing her cheek with the back of her hand. “No, I’m going to take the couch. I don’t think this is the best time to sleep next to you. Based on our kiss last night, there’s something here, and we need to talk before anything else happens.”

Agatha had wrapped a hand around Rio’s wrist, holding her hand in place as she leaned into the touch. “Let’s talk now.” Her eyelids were heavy, and she looked up at Rio through her eyelashes.

Rio looked at her softly, “Would you remember in the morning? Or stay awake for the entire discussion?”

Agatha yawned. “Fuck.”

“Exactly.”

“Will you stay until I fall asleep?” Agatha asked. She had let go of Rio’s wrist and removed Rio’s hand from her cheek, opting to hold it, intertwining their fingers instead.

Rio nodded. Agatha was vulnerable when she was drunk. It was much different than what she was used to. Rio moved to sit next to Agatha, back against the headboard and played with her hair in the way she knew Agatha liked. Agatha sighed with contentment as the alcohol overtook her body.

“Agatha, why did you decide to go out and drink tonight?” Rio asked, chewing on her lip. If she was going to get an honest answer, it was now.

“Mad at myself. Thought I’d lost you,” Agatha replied, voice laced with sleep.

Rio knew the answer to her next question but needed it confirmed. She knew she was technically taking advantage of Agatha, but also knew that this was the master of deflection, and this was the best time to get around that. “Aggie, when was the last time you slept with someone?”

Agatha nuzzled against Rio’s thigh. “Two days before I met you.”

“Why?”

“You’re the only one I’ve wanted since the day I met you at SHIELD.” Her voice had started to trail off as she quickly lost her fight to stay awake.

Rio sat there, running her fingers through Agatha’s hair as she slept. She knew it was time to tell her everything in the morning. If Agatha rejected her because of her past and the danger it posed, then, she rejected her. But she had to come clean finally. Rio leaned over and kissed Agatha on the forehead, second-guessing her decision to sleep on the couch before she reminded herself it was the right thing to do. She grabbed a pillow and a blanket from the closet before deciding to sleep on a makeshift bed on the floor instead, to at least be close to Agatha. Raven, the traitor, slept with Agatha.

Agatha woke up in the morning a bit hazy but fine. She rarely got hangovers, a superpower from her years of heavy drinking. She heard purring in her ear and turned to see Raven curled up next to her head. “You are not the woman of the house I wanted to see first thing this morning,” She said as she scratched the feline on the head. She looked around to see her bag on top of the dresser and smiled at how thoughtful Rio had been. She followed her normal morning routine, put on sweats and a SHIELD football shirt that had been in her bag, and left her hair down. She wouldn’t make this morning awkward and barely remembered what she had said to Rio last night. She was determined to have that overdue conversation this morning. She could smell coffee and food and walked towards the kitchen to see Rio putting bacon and eggs on a second plate.

Agatha leaned against the door frame and smiled as she imagined cooking together in her kitchen at the compound. “You took care of my drunk ass, and now you’re making me breakfast?” She had one eyebrow raised and her arms crossed over her chest.

Rio laughed as she turned around, handing her a plate. “I’ve gotta make sure you're sober before we talk. We’re eating in the living room. Coffee is already there.”

They both sat on the couch, facing each other. Raven jumped up between them as Rio looked at her, “Bacon is in your bowl, Your Majesty,” Raven meowed her thanks and hopped back down.

Agatha laughed, “I still think it’s funny that she eats the way she does.”

Rio shrugged, “You’ve tried telling her no before. You know how insistent she is.”

Agatha had indeed learned how insistent the immortal cat was. She would use her paw and snatch what she wanted. When you were as old as Raven was, you got to do what you wanted.

Rio looked at Agatha. “Before I share anything with you, you have to know that if anything happens between us, Raven comes first. She is my second longest relationship, after Alice,” she said seriously.

Agatha nodded. “Got it. I feel Raven could murder me in my sleep, so that’s fine.”

Rio had already finished her breakfast and set her plate down, taking a deep breath. “Remember how I told Wanda I moved out here for a fresh start and to be near Alice or whatever bullshit?”

Agatha hadn’t forgotten anything from that day. “Of course.”

“Well, it was partially true. I mainly left San Francisco because of a woman. My main rule for not dating parents is because I did date a parent, and it ended badly.”

“How badly?”

“I ended up in the hospital and almost died,” Rio waited for Agatha’s reaction.

Agatha had to make herself breathe normally. She wanted to know who the bitch was and make sure they could never hurt Rio again. “Rio, what happened?”

Rio took a deep breath, “Roughly three years ago, I had a very sweet student that I noticed right off the bat needed to be in advanced classes. I met her mom at an open house a few weeks after school started. The mom and I hit it off automatically and became friends. We’re just going to call her SF for San Francisco. She’s kind of famous. She was having problems in her marriage and opened up to me about it. Hubby is very famous. I’m not saying what he does. I did sign an NDA. SF had everyone around her sign NDAs, as she’s very paranoid. Anyway, I was under the impression that hubby was out of the picture and they were getting a divorce, or else we never would have dated, and we dated for twoish years, off and on. I knew that she would never be public with me., She would always be closeted, for her career, and I was okay with that,” Rio paused and looked at Agatha.

Agatha took Rio’s hand. “I would never want to hide you, Rio. I can’t imagine how you felt when Wan..”

“No,” Rio interrupted, “That was not the same. I honestly thought the husband was completely out of the picture. Wanda has always been completely honest with Nat. Wanda Maximoff had zero nefarious reasons for what she did. SF is a sociopath. Natasha Romanov is one of the most patient and amazing humans I know who never gave up on the woman she loves. I was an idiot that ignored a lot of red flags.”

Agatha shook her head, “I was going to say, ‘When Wanda had a similar story that turned out to have a happy ending.’”

Rio just blushed. “Sorry, I jumped to conclusions. Anyway, after two years of on-again, off-again, and hubby coming in and out of the picture, I ended things for good. She started to stalk me, and I had to get a restraining order on a parent. A famous one at that, which was hard to do, and the school tried to fire me. The union had to get involved. It was extremely messy. The order is only temporary because the judge is a fan of theirs. That was last March. Well, when the restraining order was filed and served, the husband found out. I was out for a run one weekend, and I was tackled out of nowhere,” Rio stopped and looked away. She had started breathing rapidly, the anxiety filling her with a sense of dread. She wasn’t sure she could handle this. But, she was almost to the finish line, but she wasn’t sure if she could tell Agatha the rest.

Agatha saw that Rio was starting to panic. She took both of Rio’s hands and pulled her closer to her. “Rio,” she whispered, “You’re not going to scare me away. You don’t have to finish the rest if you aren’t ready, okay, sweetie?”

She moved her hands to cradle Rio’s face, looking into her eyes with concern, care, and something else Rio couldn't quite place.

“I understand why dating parents, or anyone, would scare you, but I want you, all of you. I will face anything with you, no matter what it is.”

Rio wanted to melt into Agatha right then. Agatha Fucking Harkness wanted her: Rio Vidal, ordinary human, teacher, sixteen years Agatha’s junior, crazy cat lady, as damaged as she was. This amazing, beautiful goddess of a person that she had somehow automatically befriended three months ago was here, on her couch, willing to take on her darkest secrets because she wanted Rio. Rio searched Agatha’s eyes, looking for any hint of doubt, anything that meant she might not want to do this, and found none.

“Okay.” Rio nodded, tears falling down her cheeks. “It’s easier to just show you, though.” Rio moved back, and Agatha’s hands fell from Rio’s face. Rio picked up her phone, opened up her photos, and scrolled back to the spring, before finding the pictures of her in the hospital. She sighed as she handed the device to Agatha, “I wish I could say it looks worse than it was, but…” She trailed off as Agatha raised the phone and scrolled through the pictures. Rio watched her reactions closely as Agatha gasped at what she saw. Rio’s black eye and swollen jaw, throat red from where he had attempted to strangle her. The black, blue, and purple bruises that had blossomed over her left side from shoulder to hip. The stitches over her ribcage from the stab wounds there. Agatha looked up. “I’ve seen you in a bikini. Why didn’t I see the scars?”

Rio shrugged, “You have to look closely. They blend in. They’ve also faded over the months.”

Agatha looked back, scrapes and cuts on her right side from where she had skidded on the pavement. “Any internal injuries?” She asked quietly, tears falling from her own eyes.

“A few, nothing major. Lost my appendix somehow. Still not sure how that correlated.” She scoffed.

“That’s why Alice left so abruptly. She said there was an emergency and wouldn’t let any of us come. Nat asked if it was her type of emergency, but she wouldn’t say anything.”

Rio nodded. “A day later,  Lilia showed up at Alice’s request. It’s when I got the offer to come here.”

“Of course, Alice did, to make sure you’re safe because you’re important to Alice.”

Agatha gave her a soft smile as she put down the phone. She reached up and lightly ran her fingertips over Rio’s face where the bruises had been as if trying to heal the memories themselves. Agatha trailed her fingers down Rio’s neck and then lightly down the sides of her body over her clothes, careful not to put too much pressure, as if the bruises and cuts were still there. Agatha never broke eye contact as she took Rio in, trying her best to soothe the memories of what had happened all those months ago. She reached Rio’s knees and ran her hands back up to Rio’s shoulders slowly. Rio’s eyes fluttered closed as she gasped lightly from the contact. Agatha ran her hands down Rio’s side one more time before she rested them on Rio’s knees. She didn’t need to explain anything. Rio knew Agatha was trying to replace and erase the memory, as well as make sure she was really here, “I wish I could say I can’t believe he got away with it, but, well…” she trailed off.

Rio just looked at her breathlessly. Agatha had just barely touched her, and she felt as if her soul had left her body. Agatha had tried to replace her memories in a way only Agatha Harkness could, tears slowly rolling down both of their cheeks as Rio continued their conversation. “Yes, well. He did. I finished the school year and moved out here right before school started. I had to wait until I was well enough to move. I had a few cracked ribs that took a little while to heal and a concussion from where my head hit the pavement. They basically bribed me to stay quiet, on top of the NDA,”

She was picking at her sweats. Agatha wiped Rio’s tears away and turned her face to look at her.

“Rio, I understand, and I will never intentionally hurt you. We can go as slowly as you want, but none of that was your fault.”

“Agatha, there’s something else.” Rio worried at her lip. Afraid that this would be the thing that drove Agatha away.

“Yes?”

“She, SF, has a history of stalking. She’s insane. Our photos have been everywhere. She knows…” Rio rambled, not able to actually tell Agatha that SF had contacted her.

Agatha nodded. “Honey. I’ve been stalked before. It comes with the territory. I have a security team. I have a Natasha. We will all be okay. WE are safe. YOU are safe. NICKY is safe. I will make sure of it. We all will.”

She leaned forward and pressed their foreheads together, reveling in the touch and closeness. They were so close to actually starting something, and Agatha could feel it in her bones.

“You’re sure you’re okay with all this?” Rio asked. “Crazy stalker ex and… me? Rio felt like she was naked before Agatha, all the parts of her exposed and visible. She needed to be sure that Agatha truly understood and was clear that she accepted all of Rio and everything that came with her.

“Yes, Rio.”

“Good.” This time, it was Rio who kissed Agatha. In it, she conveyed a simple message, “I’m yours.” It was lighter and shorter than either of them would have liked, but it was Rio’s way of ending her side of this conversation. As she pulled away from Agatha, she brushed a strand of Agatha’s hair behind her ear. “I love your hair and could play with it for hours,” she mused.

Agatha smiled. “I’ve noticed.” Her eyes looked behind Rio to the green eyes that were glaring at her. “Um, how well does Raven share? I feel like she’s plotting my demise.”

Rio grinned. “She does not, and she probably is.”

“Then, before we continue this conversation, it’s Saturday. Want to go ahead and move this to my house?” She kept her eyes on the cat that now looked at her like she was telepathically giving her the rules for dating Rio, and they all ended with death.

Rio just laughed, “I can’t believe you’re afraid of Raven, but yes, let me grab a bag and text Alice to feed her before she comes to the compound.” She said as she went to her room to grab a few things.

“That is not your average housecat, and why don’t you just leave food down for her?” Agatha called towards the bedroom.

“How dare you insult her like that! Have you not noticed she eats a very delicate mix of wet and dry food? How dare you not know what my cat eats!” Rio called back, trying her hardest to sound offended.

Agatha made eye contact with Raven and whispered, “If I buy you a fancy wet food auto feeder, will you like me and put in a good word to your human for me?” Raven meowed and stretched before pawing at Agatha’s nose. “I’ll take that as a strong maybe.”

⭐⭐⭐

Rio drove them to the compound, holding Agatha’s hand the entire time. They talked about the next football game and what movies they thought Wanda and Nat would choose to watch that night. Rio felt so much better having her stalker past off her chest, even if she hadn’t shared the current situation. Agatha was right. SF couldn’t do anything, not with the whole security team around.

As they got to the house, Agatha was starting to get nervous. How much did she want to tell Rio? She knew she would eventually share everything, but she should probably do like she told Nat and Wanda and only share part of it. She took both of their duffel bags upstairs and tossed them in her room before coming downstairs. In the span of five minutes, all three boys had appeared and were talking to Rio.

“Hey Mom! Rio reminded us we have a test on Monday. Please tell her that we should be exempt because we won a playoff game,” Nicky teased.

“For that, she should give you a test and a pop quiz,” Agatha shot back as she walked to the kitchen to grab something for lunch.

“Aunt Aggie, I heard a rumor…” Tommy started.

“Finish that sentence, and I’ll cut out your tongue,” Agatha said from the kitchen as she put leftover vegetable lo mein Rio had made the other night in the microwave.

“Such violence,” Rio giggled. “Billy, how’s Eddie?”

Billy blushed, “Fine,” he mumbled.

As Tommy and Nicky continued to go back and forth with Agatha, Rio put her hand on Billy’s arm and spoke quietly. “Hey, if you ever want to talk to me, you can, okay? You’re surrounded by lesbians. You don’t have to hide anything.”

Billy shrugged. “Don’t I though? Mom and Aunt Nat have hidden for how long now? I know they think we don’t know, but we do. We’ve known forever. Mom and Dad have pretended to be this perfect couple our whole lives like we don’t have eyes.”

Rio was trying her best to remain neutral. “How do you feel about that?”

He sighed, “Tommy and I want all of them to be happy. We wouldn’t be upset if they divorced. Dad deserves to find someone, and Mom and Aunt Nat deserve to be together. We found some really old pictures of them together. Printed ones from the late 1900’s!”

Rio stifled a laugh. “Billy, I think you and Tommy need to talk with your parents.”

He nodded. “Three out of the four. Aunt Aggie can sit this one out. Thank you, Rio. I hope you and Aunt Aggie work out.” He hugged her.

“Hey, Billy, are you done talking to my future stepmom? We’re going to the clubhouse. Oh, Rio, should I call you bonus mom, or are you more of a stepdad?”

“NICHOLAS!” Agatha yelled.

“BYE!” All three boys shouted as they ran out of the house.

Rio had collapsed onto the couch laughing.

Agatha brought their lunch and water and set it down, looking at the younger woman. “I apologize for that child of mine.”

Rio shook her head. “I don’t know, ‘Stepdaddy Rio’ has a nice ring to it,” She teased.

On the inside, Agatha was panicking but appeared calm on the exterior as she smiled and kissed Rio on the cheek, close to the corner of her mouth. The mention of anything related to marriage scared her.

Rio ate quickly before filling Agatha in on what Billy said. Agatha nodded. “We’ll let the redheaded wonders know, and they can have that discussion with the twins. This means Nicky knows too, and we’ll talk to Nicky about us once we decide what we are.” Agatha said, locking eyes with Rio. Rio nodded, taking their empty dishes to the kitchen and loading them in the dishwasher before returning. She sat, turned towards Agatha, and took her hand.

“Talk to me, Aggie. I showed you mine. Show me yours.” She kissed the back of Agatha’s hand as she gazed into the blue eyes that had begun to feel like home, propped her elbow on the back of the couch, and placed her cheek on her fist.

It was Agatha’s turn to take a deep breath.

“Okay, first, my no-teacher rule, which is newer. So, it’s no secret that Lilia lets me do whatever I want. I’m a big donor to the school, and my son is the star quarterback. Well, with my previous reputation being what it was, I had my fun with every somewhat single sapphic teacher, and some not so single or sapphic for at least a few hours, teachers. It caused a lot of drama.”

“I’m not surprised,” Rio said dryly.

“No one was. Since I was the one that brought Alice out here, and I was a good friend of her mom’s, that never happened. Just so you know,” Agatha reassured her.

“I didn’t think it had,” Rio had never even questioned it once.

“So, I swore off any more people at SHIELD. Lilia was very grateful for that.”

Rio sat up. “Lilia is somewhat encouraging of this, though,” She gestured between them.

Agatha smiled, “Oh, she’ll encourage something if SHE thinks it’s right. That’s Lilia Calderu for you.”

Rio just shook her head. That sounded like the Lilia she had gotten to know. “Okay, and the Nicky of that equation?”

“As long as he doesn’t try to get special treatment, it’s fine,” Agatha assured.

“Then, the big question, Agatha Harkness, is why don’t you do relationships?” Rio looked at her softly, awaiting the answer.

“Okay, but remember, you asked,” Agatha’s mind raced as she considered exactly how much she would share. Rio had shared so much, but Agatha didn’t want to put too much on her. “This life is a lot, and it’s a lot to ask of anyone to take on. Filming schedules, my traveling. You’ve only gotten a small taste of it. Not to mention constant speculation, harassment online, just everything that goes along with this much fame. It’s a lot of pressure. It’s a lot to ask of someone else to endure with me. You’re used to NDAs at this point, and not being able to share everything, so that’s helpful. There will be times when we don’t see each other for months at a time, and every time I am pictured with another woman, rumors will fly.” She was drawing from experience, from things that had happened the last time she was with someone. The constant arguing, the accusations.

Rio took Agatha’s hand in hers. “Remember what I told you when we were preparing for the premiere? I can handle anything with you next to me. I meant that, Agatha. We have circled this thing between us for months now, and I know we’re afraid something will happen and affect our friendship, but I think we need to try.”

Rio wanted this, she wasn’t worried about distance or time spent apart, and she could ignore speculation and the media, couldn’t she? After all, sometime in the last three months, Agatha had become one of the closest people in her life. The last person she wanted to hear from before she went to sleep at night. The person she looked forward to seeing every weekend, the arms she woke up in most Sunday mornings. This felt right, and she needed to see where it would lead.

Agatha smiled. She knew she should tell Rio the rest, but Rio hadn’t pushed, so she wasn’t going to say anything either. It’s not like they were in love, and if those feelings came up, then she would potentially cross that bridge. Agatha wasn’t even sure if she was capable of that anymore. She knew she cared for Rio in a way that scared her, but she wasn’t in love with her, right?

“I guess now we decide where we want to go from here. I don’t expect you to jump into anything, and I want to move at a pace you’re comfortable with.” Agatha said looking into Rio’s eyes, and saw the slightest hint of fear and hesitation. She knew Rio was still  nervous.

“I trust you, Agatha, I do, it’s just…”

“You don’t have the best track record.”

“That’s putting it mildly.”Rio grimaced. SF wasn’t the only person she had dated, however, none of her relationships had ended particularly well. “I want you, Agatha, not like a friend either. You’re right that I don’t want to jump into anything.” She had looked away and was rubbing her chest trying to calm herself down.

Agatha used two fingers to tip Rio’s chin and turn it towards her. “Can I take you on an actual date? Next Saturday?” Agatha asked softly.
Electricity was shooting through Rio, and part of her was ready to say fuck it and beg Agatha to take her to bed, but Rio knew she wasn’t ready for that, no matter how much she wanted to or had fantasized about it.

“Have you ever been on a date?” Rio asked as she raised an eyebrow.

“I might be a bit rusty, but I think I can manage.”

“We’ll miss movie night…” Rio stalled with a slight smile.

“Will you please answer me and put me out of my misery?” Agatha slid her hand around to rest behind Rio’s neck.

“Yes,” Rio brought her hands up and ran them through Agatha’s hair as Agatha moaned lightly at the touch. “If you don’t kiss me right now, I might implode.”

Agatha used her grip on the back of Rio’s neck to pull her towards her as Agatha surged forward to meet Rio’s lips with her own. Rio sighed into Agatha’s mouth as their lips met. The kiss was slow, passionate, and exploratory. Hands in hair and light caresses, tongues intertwining.

Wanda was relieved to see Rio’s black Grand Cherokee in front of Agatha’s house. “Oh good, she didn’t kick Agatha out. She brought her home.” She said to Nat, as they walked towards the front door, holding hands.

“Hopefully, they finally talked and are getting somewhere,” Nat said as they walked in the door and to the living room where they froze as Agatha and Rio kissed on the couch before them. Wanda’s hands flew up to her face as she squealed. “They got somewhere all right,” Nat smirked.

Rio turned bright red as she broke their kiss, looking down. “Umm, hi,” She said breathlessly.

Agatha shook her hair back and turned towards the very unwelcome intruders. “Are we going to have to start putting a sock on the door?” She asked with a sarcastic smile.

“I recall there’s an entire scrunchie system,” Nat offered.

“What’s this about scrunchies?” Jen asked as she and Alice came in.

“Thinking about ways to do your hair when it finally grows back,” Agatha grinned.

Alice looked at how Rio and Agatha were still sitting facing each other, Rio with an arm on Agatha’s shoulder, playing with her hair. Agatha’s hand on Rio’s thigh, knees pressed together. Wanda and Nat were facing the couch, and Wanda’s pupils were dilated like she’d seen a ghost… or her sister making out with one of her best friends.

“Agatha and Rio were making out!” Alice suddenly exclaimed.

Rio groaned, “A little louder, Alice. I don’t think they heard you in Mexico.”

Wanda finally regained her ability to function, “So, is this happening?? Did you talk? Tell us everything!”

Everyone took their normal positions and focused on Agatha and Rio. Rio was bright red and nudged Agatha to adjust how they were sitting to face their friends more comfortably. Agatha smiled and got the hint. She knew Rio wanted to lean on Agatha right now, not wanting to be the main focus. She turned, facing forward, propping her legs up on the coffee table. She put her arm around Rio as Rio laid her head on Agatha’s shoulder.

“We are going on a date Saturday if you must know. And yes, we’ve talked. You’re all nosey.” Agatha recapped.

Wanda narrowed her eyes. “That’s all we’re getting, Harkness?”

“For now, yes, because you and Nat are needed at your house. The twins need to talk with the two of you and your husband,” Agatha looked at both of them softly.

The color drained from both redhead’s faces, as Jen’s eyes went wide as she looked at Agatha.

“Do I need to prepare a statement now?” Jen looked between Agatha and Wanda.

Wanda just looked at Agatha, waiting for her to continue, but it was Rio that spoke.

“I asked Billy why he wouldn’t tell us about him and Eddie. I told him he didn’t have to hide. He said that you, Wanda, and Nat have hidden his whole life, so why shouldn’t he? He and Tommy know. They have known, for as long as either of them can remember, they’ve known. They aren’t mad at any of you. They want the three of you to be happy, but they want to sit down and talk to ‘three of their four parents’.”

It was Rio’s turn to look softly at Wanda and Nat, and both women were trying to hold back tears.

“Damn my sons for being empaths,” Wanda whispered as Nat squeezed her hand.

Agatha looked at Jen, “As far as damage control goes, I say we still wait to say anything publicly until after the holidays, calm before the storm and all. Let us all adjust before everyone is calling for interviews and statements. Our family will not be dealing with that over the holidays,” She stated with finality. Agatha was the matriarch of this coven and found family, and they were thankful for it. She looked over at Wanda and Nat. “Now, go get your asses handed to you by two of our boys.”

As they left, Jen turned towards her other potential PR nightmare, “Any statements about the two of you?”

Agatha looked at Rio, “It’s up to you, as fast or slow as you want.” She said as she squeezed Rio’s hand.

Rio smiled at her before turning to Jen. “Nothing yet. We’re going on a first date next weekend, and if people take pictures and speculate, let them. We’re not together, officially, so no need to make any statements.” She felt wrong saying it, but she also knew it was the right approach.

Alice and Jen both caught the flash of hurt across Agatha’s face, but didn’t acknowledge it.

It was then that Agatha Fucking Harkness vowed to woo over Rio Fucking Vidal if it was the last thing she did.

⭐⭐⭐

Tommy and Billy Maximoff had made their parents, and Aunt Nat sit on the couch as they sat on the one to the side. They looked like two fathers who were very disappointed in their three teenage children. Billy, being the more well-spoken of the two, started.

“I’m sure Aunt Aggie and Rio filled you in a bit,” he started. The three adults nodded as he continued. “We found some very old photos a year or so ago that only confirmed what we already knew, and have known, for as long as we remember.” He handed the pictures over to his Mom.

Wanda took the pictures, and the other two adults leaned over to look. Wanda held a picture of her and Nat. Nat was standing behind a smiling Wanda, arms wrapped around Wanda’s waist, kissing her on the cheek, “We were maybe 20 here. This is before I met your father. Your aunt and I had just started dating, and we were at a party that Aunt Aggie was throwing for some show or movie.” She flipped to the next picture, all three women, Nat and Wanda were looking at each other like they were the only two people in the world who existed, and Agatha was making a gagging motion.

“Again, before I met your father, a house party at another actor’s house,” Another picture, this one of Wanda and Nat kissing. Both women turned bright red, “Self-explanatory.” The next picture was of her and Vision, when they had announced their engagement. They were both smiling, but hers didn’t quite meet her eyes and she looked like she would cry. She looked at her husband, “I’m so sorry…”

Vision shook his head, “I knew what I was signing up for, darling,” He said as he squeezed her knee. If a more understanding and patient man existed in the world, Wanda wasn’t sure how.

There were two pictures left. One was the leaked picture of her and Nat kissing at The Killers concert. The other was one she remembered well. Agatha had taken it. They had lived in the clubhouse at the time, and she and Nat were sitting on the couch, facing each other, fingers locked together, when their eyes met and Nat had told Wanda that she loved her for the first time. A tear ran down Wanda’s cheek as she looked at the boys, “This is the first time your Aunt Nat told me she loved me.” Nat wiped the tear from Wanda’s cheek as Vision sniffed.

Billy and Tommy nodded. “Why? Why the secrecy and all this?” Billy asked. “We looked up some things online. There are a lot of theories, but we think we deserve to know the truth.”

Wanda nodded. “You do.”

Vision looked at the two women next to him and then back to the boys. “While this is your mother’s story to tell, I want you both to know that if anyone is the victim here, it’s your mother and your aunt. The industry was very cruel and hypocritical. It celebrated Agatha because she came out at a younger age and it added to her persona. I knew all along, and your mother made a very difficult decision. We love each other very much, just in a different way, and everything we’ve done has been for the two of you.”

The boys both nodded. Wanda squeezed Vision’s hand. He really was a great husband and father. She looked at the boys and took a deep breath. “I fell in love with Nat years before I met your father…” She told them a child-friendly version of the story and brought them up to speed, including how they had decided to divorce and were preparing to tell them after the holidays.

“So, you and Aunt Nat have been having an affair for 20 years?” Tommy asked.

“While you’re still 14 and it’s not your business, no, we have not. Vows have not been broken, well, if kissing isn’t breaking vows,” Wanda answered.

“I told your mother she could,” Vision answered.

“I have always respected your parents' marriage,” Nat added.

Billy grimaced, but he did think it was sweet to a degree. “So you were planning a divorce but were waiting to tell us?”

“We wanted to wait until after the holidays. We weren’t sure how you boys would take it,” Wanda looked at them both with motherly concern.

“Dad, you’re okay with all of this?” Tommy looked at Vision.

“When you truly love someone, you realize that you want them to be happy. Plus, your mother and I have been best friends for much longer than we’ve been anything else.”

“So, is everyone okay with this? No hard feelings, no custody battles, and we’re still going to be a cohesive family. Dad might live somewhere else, and Mom will eventually marry Aunt Nat?” Tommy looked at all three adults.

“Yes,” All three answered.

Both twins grinned.

“In that case, Eddie and I are dating. Moms, dad, I’m gay.” Billy announced.

“No shit, son.” Nat laughed.

⭐⭐⭐

The week had gone by quickly, and Agatha would be at Rio’s door in a few minutes to pick her up. Rio was nervous, though she wasn’t completely sure as to why. It was Agatha, her friend, her…potential girlfriend? Okay, that was a tad scary, and they would probably be all over Page Six by morning, Twitter, BlueSky, Tumblr…she wasn’t used to that either. This was LA, celebrities were everywhere, but Agatha Harkness was never seen out on an actual date, and Rio hadn’t gone out in public with the ex. They knew that even if they didn’t make a verbal or published statement, they would be making a visual one. It was dinner, and then Agatha had some surprise for her. She wasn’t sure what they would be doing on the second Saturday night of November, but she was up for anything as long as she was doing it with Agatha.

She touched up her dark red lipstick and walked through the apartment into the living room. She wore a long-sleeve green v-cut blouse and black pencil skirt with knee-high black boots, with her hair in large curls around her shoulders, “Alright Raven, here goes everything,” She said to the sleeping cat on the couch, who barely stirred as there was a knock on the door.

As often as Rio had seen Agatha in a suit, she still never tired of it. The woman looked stunning in them, and she knew it. This particular suit was black and fitted with a purple vest, no shirt, and purple stiletto heels. Agatha Fucking Harkness, indeed. Rio ran her eyes up and down Agatha twice before she even realized she had one foot propped on a box.

“What is that?” Rio asked, “And what’s with the stance there, Captain Morgan?”

“This,” Agatha picked up the box and walked dramatically past Rio. “Is for the woman of the house. M’Lady,” she presented the box to Raven, who woke up and sniffed at it curiously.

Rio walked over to look at the box herself and laughed. “Did you buy her a fancy automatic feeder for wet food?”

“Yes, I did. I’m tired of you running off at the unholy hour of seven in the morning on Sundays, and Alice can’t always pop over here. Besides, she and Jen will eventually move in together,” Agatha walked off towards the kitchen to figure out the gadget, Raven on her heels.

“It took them nearly a decade to start dating. I don’t think they’ll U-Haul it anytime soon. And mighty presumptuous of you to think I’ll still stay the night on Saturdays.” Rio followed them into the kitchen, ready to watch Agatha set up Raven’s new contraption.

“We start dating and you stop spending the night? Isn’t that backwards?” She smirked as she opened and immediately discarded the directions on the counter.

Rio rolled her eyes, “Why does it not surprise me that you don’t read directions?”

“I didn’t make it this far following the rules, sweetcheeks. Hand me your phone.” Agatha held her hand out for Rio’s phone as she plugged in the feeder.

“I’m not unlocking it for you.” Rio raised an eyebrow.

“Is this a test?” Agatha didn’t take her eyes off Rio as she unlocked Rio’s phone and downloaded an app, “You can control this thing from your phone. Will you hand me Her Highness’s food, please? I know you already have it portioned and ready to go for the morning.” She looked down at Raven, who was pawing at Agatha’s hand as she petted her, “I also know you’ve been fed for the night already, but nice try.”

Rio was impressed as she handed Raven’s breakfast to Agatha. The older woman put the food in the tray, closed the lid, and programmed the time in Rio’s phone before handing it back to her. “You can put up to six meals in it. It keeps everything cold and fresh, the app controls everything, or you can program it on here.”

Rio was impressed. No one had ever used Raven to their advantage before, and Agatha hadn’t even read the instructions.

“You watched a video, didn’t you, Harkness?”

“I will never reveal my secrets.” She stood up in one fluid motion and took Rio’s hand, kissing its back. “Now that I’ve won Raven over, are you ready to go?”

“You mean you aren’t just trying to woo the cat?” Rio looked at her playfully, “Yes, let me grab my bag, and we’ll go.”

The restaurant had been one of LA’s celebrity hotspots, though Rio really didn’t mind. She liked the atmosphere, the food was great, and she was there with Agatha. That was not the important part of this date, nor was it what she had been looking forward to. It was what came afterward, the surprise that Agatha had been hinting at all week. They were back at the car, and Rio was surprised to see that Agatha’s driver was not there.

“Wait, Agatha, are YOU driving? I don’t think I’ve ever actually seen you drive.” Rio said with a mixture of shock and hesitation.

“Rio. I am 51 years old. I drive. Get in the car.” She smiled as she got into the driver’s seat.

“I know, I’m just not used to it. Why are you driving?” She asked, eyebrow raised with suspicion. “Are we doing something illegal?” She buckled her seatbelt, still confused.

“Maybe I’d like us to be completely alone? Maybe it’s a secret. Maybe we’re going on a bank heist.” Agatha threw out.

They were driving up the Pacific Coastal Highway, away from the city, and Rio could see more of the sky and the stars than usual. She reached over and ran her fingers through Agatha’s hair.

“I am trying to focus on driving,” Agatha said as she reached over with her right hand and placed it on Rio’s thigh.

“Both hands on the wheel, Harkness,” Rio instructed, trying not to smile.

Agatha ignored her, smirking instead.

Rio rolled her eyes, “Where are you taking me?” They had exited the highway and were on a dirt road.

“Secret lair, very secluded. I plan on seducing and torturing you, might murder you, might not. We’ll see what happens,” Agatha kept a straight face in the way only she could.

“This is why you have so many awards. If I didn’t know better, I would have believed you. Also, I might be game.” Rio put her hand on Agatha’s.

“Who said I was acting? We will, however, revisit some of what you’re game for in the future,” Agatha cleared her throat, as it was suddenly dry, “Oh look, we’re here.” Agatha had turned into some type of parking lot.

“Where are we?” Rio asked, looking around, confused.

“You tell me, mad scientist.” Agatha was practically bouncing from excitement.

Rio looked around and then looked up at where they could see the sky, free from light pollution before looking back at Agatha. “Aggie, what’s in the back of the car?”

“I thought you’d never ask,” Agatha dramatically flounced to the back of the SUV, opening the back as she went, pulling out a telescope with a shit-eating grin on her face. “I heard you like stars and sciencey shit.”

“You brought me to a national park, away from everything, with a giant ass telescope, by ourselves, on the night of a meteor shower, for our first date?”

“I did.”

“And you haven’t been on a proper date in years?” At this point, Rio didn’t understand why Agatha was single.

“I have not.”

Rio could see the delight in Agatha’s eyes at her amazement and surprise. She had seen everything Agatha had ever been in and couldn’t recall a scene like this so she knew that either one of their friends had tipped her off or she really was just this thoughtful. If she wasn’t already under Agatha’s spell, she was now.

Agatha handed Rio the telescope “I believe this is your department,” before pulling out a blanket and a tote bag. “Follow me.” She strutted across the uneven ground, still in stilettos in only the way Agatha could.
