Rio’s eyes dropped to appreciate the view of Agatha in front of her, “Gladly.”

Agatha led them to a small clearing not far from where they had parked, laying out the blanket, and pulling out two thermos’ of cocoa as Rio set up the telescope and dialed it in.

“Did you bring hot chocolate, you Girl Scout?” Rio laughed.

“I played a Girl Scout as a kid, and yes I did. It’s chilly and I’m driving, celebrities and DUI’s don’t mix.” Agatha handed her a thermos as she joined her.

“Agatha Harkness, an adorable romantic, who knew?” Rio noted.

“I’m full of surprises.”

“So am I,” She peered through the lenses before standing up and motioning for Agatha to look. “See those two stars by the moon?” Agatha nodded, “That’s Jupiter and Mars. Now if you look over here,” Rio shifted the telescope slightly, hand on the small of Agatha’s back, guiding her, “You can see the constellation Phoenix. It has the bright star at the bottom, makes the diamond shape to the north and west, and the triangle to the east.” Rio explained.

Agatha nodded. She knew what a phoenix was, but she could tell that Rio was passionate about the sciences and wanted to listen to her for hours. “Tell me about it?” She stood and looked at her, placing a hand on Rio’s hip and guiding her to lay down on the plush blanket with her.

Before Agatha could lead them down, however, Rio hooked a thumb through one of the belt loops of Agatha’s pants and pulled Agatha against her, looping her other arm around Agatha’s shoulders and slowly slid their lips together, sighing into their kiss.

Agatha parted her lips for Rio immediately, flexing her fingers into her hip, and ran her other thumb along Rio’s jaw. Agatha needed to feel as much skin as she could, as if she had to ensure Rio was real. She whimpered when Rio broke the kiss to guide them to lay down, Agatha’s original intention.

“What’s wrong there, slugger?” Rio had a smug look in her eye as she pulled Agatha down and pushed her on her back.

“You know, funny thing. I was kissing this beautiful woman, and suddenly I wasn’t, which is a rare occurrence for me. However, this particular woman has a pattern and it’s maddening.” Agatha was smiling, clearly joking, as she stared into Rio’s eyes and ran one hand up her back, the other twisting in Rio’s hair.

“You mean there’s at least one woman on this planet who manages to have some self-control around the great Agatha Harkness?” Rio teased as she lay halfway on top of Agatha. One arm propped her up as she snaked the other hand inside Agatha’s jacket and under her vest to rest on the bare skin of her side. When Agatha raised one eyebrow at the touch, Rio had just shrugged, saying her hand was cold.

“Tell me about the constellations.” Agatha reminded her as she raised her head and kissed Rio again.

“I know you know about phoenixes,” she rolled her eyes, as Agatha had been compared to the mythological bird many times, “but, the myth is that when the bird dies, it rises from the ashes and begins anew. An oversimplification, which you know.”

Agatha hummed her admittance. “You’re a phoenix yourself, kid. Giving this,” she gestured between them, “ a shot after what happened. Thank you, for trusting me.” She traced Rio’s jawline, meeting her for another kiss, this one longer, deeper, more meaningful. Agatha held Rio by the nape of her neck and waist. As Rio’s hand on Agatha’s skin tightened its grip, her other hand instinctively found its way to Agatha’s hair. Agatha’s tongue flicked across Rio’s lip as she nipped it with her teeth before she ran her tongue along Rio’s own, the roof of her mouth, the back of her teeth, anywhere she could reach. The only time she even slightly broke from Rio was so they could breathe.

Agatha rolled so that she was on top of Rio, their bodies pressed together, deepening the kiss. She knew they were the only ones in the park, a perk of being Agatha Fucking Harkness. Rio’s hands had moved inside Agatha’s jacket, exploring her back and grateful Agatha had opted against a shirt under the vest, as Agatha moved from Rio’s mouth, kissing down her jawline and drew her earlobe into her mouth. Rio moaned as Agatha dragged her nails across her collarbone.

“Agatha.” Rio breathed. She was flooded with heat and wanted this to continue, but knew it couldn’t, especially not outside, on the ground, not with Agatha like this.

“Mmm?” Agatha was kissing down her neck like she was on a mission, and she was.

“We have to stop.”

Rio had withdrawn her hands and put them both on Agatha’s chest, itching to put them lower, even though that was a bad idea. She looked down at Agatha as the other brunette put an end to her mission and came back up to give Rio a chaste kiss on the lips.

Agatha nodded and rolled onto her back, pulling Rio back to her side as a star shot across the sky. Yes, Rio was right. They hadn’t rushed anything else, so why rush this? Agatha could be patient and Rio was worth it, she was worth everything, and she would prove to Rio that no matter what Rio believed, she had been put in a terrible situation by a psychopathic bitch, but that didn’t mean anything was wrong with Rio, far from it. She had lived a life where things had happened TO her, not BECAUSE of her.

They lay there as the stars fell around them, Rio educating Agatha about the names of meteor showers, this particular one being the Orionids, their causes, and pointing out more constellations as they kissed during silent pauses, played with each other’s hair and held hands. Agatha had never had this with anyone and drunk it all in. She felt peaceful, removed from watchful eyes, the weight of fame, knowing that the woman beside her saw her for who she was. Agatha the person, not Agatha the star. She watched Rio talk as she came to a stop and turned towards Agatha.

“What is it? You’re looking at me all weird, do I have a big bug on my face?” She asked, not even moving to swat at the imaginary insect.

Agatha shook her head. “I just appreciate you,” She looked up at the sky, “What’s your ‘wish upon a star’ there, princess?” Agatha said, deflecting to her sarcastic humor.

That you would finish revealing yourself to me. That I would let myself love you. That you would fall in love with me. Rio couldn’t bring herself to say any of that, instead, she went with something safe, “That you’d finish out your triple EGOT.”

Agatha breathed a laugh, “Please. Your falling rock wish is not for me.”

It is, but not to win an award. “It is!” She argued as Agatha gave her a look. “Okay, fine, for Raven to live forever.” That was not a lie.

“That I actually do believe,” Agatha affirmed. “Mine is not to lose the people close to me.” You, specifically. She revealed. “It’s getting colder. Are you ready to go back?”

Rio nodded.

“Stay the night with me?” Agatha asked in a whisper.

“It’s Saturday, of course,” Rio whispered back as she kissed Agatha again. She had waited to kiss Agatha for months, and now she got to do it freely. Somewhere, past Rio was deceased.

As Agatha drove them back to the compound, she considered their sleeping arrangements. They had shared a bed, but never her actual bed at her main residence, not even the previous weekend when they had kissed for the first time, Rio still opting for the couch. Come to think of it, she had never shared that bed with anyone that she didn’t consider family. It was intimate and a big deal, but she was letting Rio drive this at her speed.

“Where exactly are we sleeping tonight darling?” Agatha asked, trying not to sound shady.

“Your house.” Rio gave nothing in her answer.

“Where in my house?” Agatha prodded.

Rio knew how to push Agatha’s buttons and she had fun doing it. “A room, preferably on a horizontal surface.” She grinned as she placed her hand on Agatha’s upper thigh.

“Smartass,” Agatha mumbled.

Rio turned slightly serious, “If it’s okay with you, sweetheart, your bed, in your room, in your arms,” Rio gave Agatha another bright smile, “I’m wearing clothes though. If you’re good, we can continue that, ehm, conversation we started under the stars.”

Agatha loved Rio’s smile, returning one of her own as she slid her hand up Rio’s thigh, slightly under the hem of her skirt, “I can be good.”

Rio laughed as her phone dinged.

Blocked

Stars are pretty tonight, aren’t they?

Chapter End Notes

What happens next?

As always, thank you so much to my amazing beta and editor!

I Swear There is Someone, Somewhere, Watching Me

Chapter Summary

There's some drama at school and Rio gets flowers!
A decision is made!

Chapter Notes

"Your last name is even a vegetable, Kale. The worst one too, it's like swallowing a doily."

So yes, in Agatha's phone, Doily is Jen.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Agatha lay in bed staring in amazement at Rio in her arms, breathing peacefully. Normally, Rio would have been gone by now, having gone home to feed the main gal in her life. Agatha needed to find the inventors of that darn auto feeder and hug them. Rio already had a camera that she used to check in on the senior citizen periodically, or else that would have been the next purchase. Agatha had always been tactile with her friends but never with romantic or sexual partners. Her ex hadn’t liked Agatha being touchy, so she hadn’t been. Agatha had been kept secret, and so they’d been secretive. Not Rio. No, they’d always touched, and now that they were in solid ‘more than friends’ territory, they were rarely apart.

Agatha considered getting up and making breakfast, but she didn’t want to move Rio, nor did she want to break the contact. Instead, she kissed her forehead, her cheek, her nose, and any place she could with her limited range of motion. She needed to confess everything to Rio, the complete story, before falling even further for her. She pushed that aside for now. She could control that and just would shut that part of her off.

Rio stirred, opening her eyes halfway. “Mornin’,” She said with a yawn before she nuzzled into Agatha’s shoulder.

“Good Morning.” Agatha kissed Rio on the top of the head, bringing her closer. “Sleep okay?”

Rio nodded. “I’m sleeping here more from now on. Your bed is more comfortable than mine.”

“The bed, or me?”

“Both.” Rio rolled away and stretched. “Seriously though, a girl could get used to this, Agatha, better be careful. She winked as she got out of the bed. “Now, come make breakfast with me.”

While Rio’s admission scared Agatha, she also found herself wanting Rio to wake up beside her every morning.

⭐⭐⭐

Rio was ignoring the sociopathic elephant on her phone. It was two texts. Nothing was going to happen, so SF knew they had gone stargazing Saturday night. Big deal. She had done this before, pinging her phone for her location in some third-party app, even once having a private inspector follow her. If anything else like that happened, she would wipe her phone and just watch herself better. She had a few more months until the restraining order expired, it would be fine. What Rio was more concerned about was the fact that the cat was out of the bag. Photos from dinner were all over the internet. That, coupled with the premiere and pictures from New York, people were doing the math. Her first name had even been leaked, and they had a hashtag.

“Agatha Harkness seen out on the town with new friend, ‘Rio’”

“Third Rio sighting! Is it serious, or are they just friends?”

“Just gals being pals!”

“With ‘My Beloved’ nominated for the Grammy for Movie Theme Song, do you think Agatha will take her?”

“She won’t last until February.”

“#Wagatha or #Agathario?”

“We have to find out her last name, surely #Agathario is not the best we can do!”

“This from the people that gave us #WandaNat for Wanda and her bodyguard.

“Because their last names literally form THEIR LAST NAMES.

“I ship them now.”

“It’s temporary.”

“I heard Agatha was making out with some woman at a club last week.”

“Vision, surrounded by lesbians, including his own wife.”

“#WandaVision forever!”

“She’s not even that pretty.”

Rio groaned as she put her phone down, straightened, and got the papers together that she needed for her first class of the day. It was Monday, and there were still two weeks until Thanksgiving break. She didn’t have Nicky until her fifth period but had Tommy first and Billy third. The bell rang as students filed in, strangely quiet for her class. “Guys?” She looked at them all. “What’s going on?”

Her room of 15 freshman biology students looked at each other sheepishly before her eyes landed on Tommy.

“Tommy, tell me.” She instructed as he turned bright red. The team had won on Friday night and would be advancing to the Division Championship game on Friday, so she wasn’t sure why students were quiet.

“I told everyone you and Aunt Aggie were seeing each other, but not together…” He started.

She nodded. The collective wanted the gossip.

“A teacher’s private life is not your business.” She smirked as they groaned.

“Ms. Vidal, our parents are famous. We’re going to hear about it anyway.” Monica, Carol Danvers’s daughter, said.

“That’s true…” Rio said as she thought it over.

“We all know the drama from last year.” Another student added.

“Some of us live on the compound,” Tommy mentioned to laughter from his classmates.

Rio grinned. “Might as well come from the primary source, then. Tommy is right. Ms. Harkness and I went on a date. We are not together as reported. We have been friends all year, happy?”

“Ms. Jones is going to be upset,” America Chavez shook her head. Rio had been handing out study materials and stopped, waiting for America to continue, “Surely you know about everything from last year, Ms. Vidal? A lot of the teachers were…involved, with Agatha. Ms. Jones was absolutely in love with her.” America finished.

Tommy rolled his eyes. “Ms. Jones was just another in a long line of hookups, everyone was. Ri…Ms. Vidal is the real deal.” He defended Rio, and she smiled.

“Okay, well, let’s look at your study guide for your upcoming unit test, please.” She redirected, knowing that this set would spread the word.

Teaching at SHIELD was not for the faint of heart, with students who were on top of all the celebrity gossip running around. That was also how she found out that Carol Danvers had won ‘first kiss on Halloween’, Coach Rogers had ‘Vidal freaks out’, Lilia for their date last Saturday, but not actually dating. Wanda felt like she had an unfair advantage because she knew Rio’s past. Rio and Nat had been filled in on the previous Stalker situation.

Rio ran into Jessica Jones, a middle school math teacher, in the teacher’s lounge.

“You won’t last, you know.” Jessica spat, seething. Rio just shrugged, wanting to avoid the confrontation, and went to Alice’s classroom instead.

“I did not expect drama,” Rio said, sitting on top of a table and eating her lunch.

Alice looked at her. “Agatha has slept with her very unfair share of women, Ri. What did you expect?”

Rio shrugged, “I don’t know, but it wasn’t students prying, other teachers being jealous, the men high-fiving me…Rogers fucking HUGGED me.”

Alice started laughing. “Yeah, because Peggy has a thing for Agatha, and he’s glad she’s off the market.”

Rio looked skeptical, “Peggy is pregnant.”

“If you were pregnant, married to a man, and Agatha Fucking Harkness hit on you, would you turn her down, even if you were, to quote the late, great Lorna Wu, tragically straight?” Alice gestured to the sky as she mentioned her mother.

Rio grinned. “Oh, absolutely not.”

“Where is your girlfriend this week?”

“She’s not my girlfriend. Agatha is filming this week and next/shut down for Thanksgiving, two more weeks in the US. Shut down for Christmas and the New Year, and then Canada in January.” Rio checked the clock to ensure she had a few minutes before class.

“You still knew who I was talking about, though.” Alice pointed out.

“Whatever. Speaking of, any updates on your front?”

Alice looked down, chewing on the inside of her cheek. Rio’s eyes widened. “Shit, I was teasing. Has something changed?”

“Umm, Jen asked me to move in with her.”

“WHAT? It took you two a fucking decade to make a move, and now you’re going all in?” Rio was trying very hard not to shout. “I’m assuming her house?”

Alice shook her head. “We’re thinking of buying a house or even building.”

Rio brought her voice down a notch, “Holy shit, Alice. That’s a lot. Are you sure.”

“It’s been ten years. We haven’t been together until recently, but my feelings were there, and I feel like there’s lost time. I accidentally blurted out that I loved her last week.”

Rio couldn’t put her feelings for Agatha into words, and Alice and Jen had gone into hyperdrive. “What did she say?”

“She loves me too,” Alice smiled.

“Lesbians,” Rio smiled and shook her head.

“Yeah, so, catch the fuck up,” Alice said as the bell rang.

On Wednesday, Rio received flowers at school that Lilia personally brought to Rio’s classroom at the end of her fifth period, AP Chemistry, which one Nicky Harkness was in. Twelve sets of eyes were on her as she took the beautiful and tasteful bouquet of blue, pink, and purple hydrangeas, blue and pink azaleas, purple violets, and lavender. Her cheeks were tinged with pink as the teenagers’ “oohs” and “awws” filled the room, and a few of them ribbed Nicky about how his mom had game. The bell rang, and the students left the room as Lilia stood there, eyebrows raised at Lilia. With a wink at Rio, Nicky had grabbed a beaker, filled it with water, stole three pink hydrangeas, and jogged out of the room, calling after Morgan Stark. Rio couldn’t help but smile after the boy she loved like her own.

Lilia walked over and shut the door before turning back to Rio. “I know I encouraged this Rio, and I do. I expect the drama to be kept to a minimum. That said, I expect this to end much better than your last relationship. As long as you’re completely honest and you confront any issues head-on. Have a good week, Ms. Vidal. It’s almost Thanksgiving!” She left, and Rio just stood there before reading the card.

“I know the next few weeks are busy, but I wanted to let you know I miss you.”

Rio took a picture of her flowers and sent it to the coven before texting Agatha.

Me:

Some famous movie star sent

me these.

Agatha 💜

That bitch. I’ll fight her.

Me:

Please don’t. I think I

like her.

Agatha 💜

You do, do you?

What’s her name?

I’ll put in a good word.

Agatha was in between takes as she sat in a chair with her name on the back. She was filming for her new TV show. She knew the question was silly, but she wanted the confirmation.

Me:

Wanda Maximoff. Do

you know her?

Rio cackled into her empty classroom for a moment, imagining Agatha’s eye roll.

Agatha 💜

Heard she’s taken. Some

Russian. Extremely dangerous.

I wouldn’t mess with her.

Agatha deserved that one, as she smiled and rolled her eyes.

Me:

Damn. Well, I lied

anyway. Her name is

Agatha Fucking Harkness.

She’s amazing, and I like her.

A lot.

Agatha 💜

I heard she likes you too.

Agatha didn’t have the traditional school experience growing up, but she imagined this was what passing the ‘do you like me’ notes was like. She was blushing and knew she looked like she had a school-girl crush right now. She didn’t care. She took a screenshot of the conversation and sent it to Wanda, who told her she was a child.

Rio was smiling at the confirmation that Agatha Harkness liked her as if it hadn’t been blatantly obvious.

Me:

Teenage-style flirting aside.

Nicky likes Morgan Stark.

Agatha groaned. She hoped Nicky got over that real quick. She adored Pepper, but Tony was not who she wanted to deal with when it came to her son’s dating life.

Agatha 💜

How do you know?

Me:

Lilia brought me my flowers

at the end of 5th.

Nicky took some of the

hydrangeas. The ones that

mean romance put them in a

beaker and chased after

her.

Me:

It was so cute. I hope it

works out. She’s a good kid.

Agatha 💜

But why TONY’S kid?

Me:

If anything happens, I’ll

handle Tony. I have to get

ready for my 7th period.

Agatha put her phone down. She couldn’t remember a time in her life when she smiled this much. Rio was talking about handling Nicky’s dating life together, and her heart had soared. She didn’t want to unpack that right now. At the same time, she felt guilty for not coming clean.

Nat was helping Wanda run lines when the picture of the flowers showed up in the group chat. Wanda and Nat looked at each other and smiled.. Jen sighed and jotted down notes about future statements while browsing real estate listings. Alice had already known, as some of her sixth-period students had been in Rio’s fifth period.

⭐⭐⭐

It was another Friday night, and another football game at SHIELD. Lilia ‘Just Keep the Secrets and Keep the Kids Alive’ Calderu was trying her best to keep the media at bay, the lesbian drama to a minimum, the elementary school holiday pageants were coming up, and the secondary school musical was right after Thanksgiving. Then she had parents gambling on one of her teacher's love lives with Agatha Harkness and the usual teenage hormones to deal with. She deserved a vacation, and if it wasn’t for her own affinity to once sleep with parents, and now grandparents, of students, she would consider a no fraternizing policy.

Rio and Agatha were holding hands in public at a high school football game. Elementary parents who didn’t have high school students involved in the game ran the concessions, so she and the coven were free to watch the whole time. It was half-time, and SHIELD was losing to Bayside of all teams. Rio was walking back from the restroom when Jessica Jones confronted her for the second time that week.

“You know you’re just temporary, Vidal.” She said, looking her up and down.

“Jessica, I have no issue with you. I’m sorry things didn’t work out between the two of you.” Rio was trying to be cordial. She didn’t like confrontation or drama.

Agatha came up beside Rio, wrapping a protective arm around her waist. She was wearing a gold and black SHIELD beanie and a letterman jacket with Harkness on the back and looked every bit the proud mom she was.

“Hi, babe.” She said to Rio, kissing her on the cheek. She was making a very bold statement, and she knew it. “Hi Jessica, nice to see you supporting the team.”

Jessica turned bright red, “Hello, Agatha. Nicky’s been great this year.” Jessica was somewhere between embarrassed and shocked.

Agatha regarded her, eyes narrow, face somewhat severe. Rio loved it. She knew Agatha was sending a clear, nonverbal message, and it sent a chill down her spine. The woman that famously didn’t date and that left women longing in her wake, was letting someone know that she had chosen Rio and to alert the masses. It was like some type of lesbian standoff, and Jessica was the first to break.

“I’m happy for both of you,” Jessica said finally before she nodded at Agatha and stalked off.

Rio turned and laughed. “How do you do that? Just mind control women?”

“Again, not giving away my secret,” She kissed Rio quickly on the mouth, seeing her surprise. “Is it okay that I kiss you at school? I should’ve asked that first.”

Rio nodded, “Everyone else kisses there…whatever.” Not sure what to call Agatha.

“Got it.” She led Rio back to where they were sitting.

Wanda had witnessed everything as she Venmo’d Pepper for ‘staking claims at the football game.’

Lilia had also witnessed everything and just hoped that was the end of the teacher drama.

SHIELD lost the game, and their playoff run was over. Bayside rubbed it in a bit too much. Nat wanted to punch the principal in the face and rip his toupee off, ever the protective mom she was. While there were tears, the students were also excited that they had made it as far as they had in sports. In a flurry of emotion, Billy had gone up to Eddie and kissed him full on the mouth in front of everyone. Wanda, through tears at seeing the boys lose, took a picture and sent it to Eddie’s parents. Billy was proud of his boyfriend as he had caught one of Nicky’s passes for a touchdown. He had also fumbled a ball but wasn’t focused on that part. Morgan Stark had told Nicky he did a good job and asked if he was busy the next night and if he wanted to come over and watch a movie. Pepper promised Agatha that SHE would be the one to supervise and that she would send Tony over to Coach Rogers's house. Vision gave Tommy a pep talk about how they couldn’t win them all.

It wasn’t until they got home that the adults realized how crushed their souls were. Tommy was mad and blaming the refs, as Vision waxed poetic about defeat, and the redheads had to tell him this wasn’t the best time for philosophy. Meanwhile, Agatha reassured Nicky that he had done the absolute best he could and that it really was not his fault.

“If I had just called better plays, Mom!” He was full of regret and blamed himself.

“Oh honey, even the greatest players don’t win every game. You are sixteen years old, and you did a great job. I am so proud of you,” They were sitting on the couch and she ran a hand through his hair. He was six foot tall now to her five foot five. They had to sit so she could reach the top of his head. “I love you so much, Nicholas,” she wrapped her arms around his shoulders. He would always be her little boy, no matter how old he was.

“Mom?” He sniffled.

“Yes, baby?”

“Can I ask you something, and you be completely honest with me?” He knew his mother and knew she spoke in half-truths.

“That’s a scary question, but yes. I promise to be completely honest.”

“Do you love Rio?” He sounded like a little boy, and she knew why he was asking. He needed to know if Rio was permanent. His mother had never dated anyone during his life, and this was new to him. He had to know if he could be vulnerable with Rio. If he could let her in and get close to her.

“Honest answer, kid? I don’t know.” She had feelings for Rio, she knew that much, but she honestly didn’t know if she was capable of loving someone again. She couldn’t even bring herself to tell Rio about that part of her life, she was still so damaged, and it had ended eighteen years ago.

“I think it’s possible? I don’t know. I’m new to all this. I don’t want that to change or influence how you treat or act around her, though. Do not keep her at arm's length because I don’t know how to handle big feelings, okay?” Nicky was already forming a close bond with Rio, and she didn’t want to ruin that.

Nicky looked at his mom, the emotional idiot that she was. “You look at her the way Aunt Wanda looks at Aunt Nat, you know,”

She scoffed, “I absolutely do not.” she lied.

He gave her a small smile, “Mom, to borrow your favorite f-word, please don’t fuck this up.”

She couldn’t help but smile at her son, “I’m not even mad that you swore.”

⭐⭐⭐

The next night was movie night, and Agatha and Rio were on their own having a date night at Rio’s. They were making comments on some cheesy lesbian Christmas movie as Rio lay with her head in Agatha’s lap when Agatha’s phone dinged with a text from Jen.

Doily

It’s all set. VIP tent.

December 8th, Vancouver.

Golden Globes Nominations the

next day. She can’t wait to see you

and meet Rio. She expects the whole

coven.

Me:

Thank you. Tell the others.

I’ll tell Rio. I’ll also take care

of Calderu and get Alice

and Rio out of work.

Doily

Did you just thank me?

Me:

Don’t push it, Kale.

Agatha smiled at Rio, who looked at her with a raised eyebrow. “Everything okay, Agatha?”

“Perfect. Just taking care of something real quick,” She widened her smile. She was bursting at the seams with the surprise but had to text Lilia first.

“It’s date night,” Rio whined playfully.

“Famous people stuff,” She partially lied. This wouldn’t be possible if she didn’t have the connections she had.

Me:

Rio and Alice will be

taking off Dec 9th and 10th.

Calderu

I don’t recall working

for you, Harkness. I make those calls.

Me:

They refuse to ever call in.

They’ll be in Canada.

Calderu:

You can’t always get what you

want Agatha.

Me:

Can’t I?

Me:

Lilia.

It’s Taylor Swift.

Calderu:

You’re lucky I know who

that is. Fine.

Me:

You’re a doll.

Calderu:

I’m sure.

Agatha looked down at Rio, grinning from ear to ear, tugging her hair to get her attention.

Rio looked up at her with a questioning look. “You look like you’re about to tell me we’re all mad here.”

“We are, but no. What are you doing December 8th?” Agatha asked her, knowing the answer.

“Watching a grainy livestream of the final Taylor Swift show.”

“How would you like to be there in a VIP tent, with me and the rest of the coven?” Agatha tried to remain composed, but she could not.

Rio shrieked as she sat up. “WHAT?! Really!? YES!” She threw her arms around Agatha and kissed her, pulling back as reality hit, “Wait, that’s a school night.”

“You and Alice will have subs. Just took care of it.”

Rio shrieked again, kissing Agatha. “I promise I like you for you and not just the perks.” She said after pausing for a breath.

Agatha laughed, leaning into Rio, “I didn’t doubt that for a second.” Agatha knew Rio never expected anything. She just liked making Rio smile, no matter what it took.

The moment was broken when there was a knock on the door. Agatha raised an eyebrow, “Expecting someone?”

“Oh, you know, just one of my many other megastar suitors,” Rio joked, opening the door to another bouquet sitting in the otherwise empty hallway. She picked them up and turned around.

“Babe, more flowers? I’m going to run out of room.” She huffed a laugh as she closed the door and turned to kiss Agatha, who had come up behind her.

Agatha was not smiling. “Rio, those aren’t from me.”

Rio stilled as Agatha guided her back to the couch. Rio looked at the flowers, brain quickly working.

“All flowers have multiple meanings, positive and negative,” She mumbled aloud.

Agatha nodded. She had looked up every single flower she had sent Rio, since Alice warned her that Rio would dissect it like this.

Rio went on, “Calla lilies, my least favorite, rebirth and union. Marigolds can symbolize jealousy, as can yellow roses, which can also symbolize possession. Red roses stand for love, of course, but these…” Rio looked at their rich color. “These mean obsession. The dark orchids for mystery and possession, and white lilies for obsession and mourning.” Her voice was devoid of all emotion. She hadn’t even read the card yet they both knew what this meant. Both women just stared at the offending flowers as if they would come alive at any moment and swallow them both whole.

Rio’s eyes landed on the card as she reached a shaky hand towards it. Agatha took her hand and met her eyes. “Together?” Agatha asked. Rio nodded as they took the card. Rio let out the breath she was holding as they opened it. She froze as she took in the words in the all too familiar handwriting.

“I have movie night every Saturday too, watching the movies we made.”

The blood in Rio’s veins turned to ice and she forgot how to breathe. Agatha threw the card down as if it had burst into flames.

“I’m gathering everyone. NOW.” Agatha said, venom in her voice.

Rio shook her head. “No, this is my problem. No one else has to get involved.”

“Oh yes, the hell we can.” Agatha was on the move. “This fucking bitch. Who the fuck does she think she is?” She stopped when she saw the fear in Rio’s eyes. Agatha dropped down to her knees in front of Rio. Agatha put one hand on Rio’s knee and the other on her cheek. “Trust me?” She asked, eyes pleading. Agatha couldn’t confront her feelings, nor could she confess everything, but she could do this.

Rio nodded, tears filling her eyes. Out of all the people she knew, Agatha was the third person in her life who had seen her cry this much. “I do,” Rio replied.

Agatha pulled out her phone and sent a text to the group chat. All it said was ‘my house, 911.’ She pulled Rio into a gentle kiss, wiping away her tears. “Pack yours and Raven’s shit. You both are staying at my house for a few days.”

Agatha and Rio were the last to arrive at the compound, and tensions were high as the other women met them in the driveway. Rio parked so they could help unload.

“How much does this cat need?” Nat asked, grabbing the autofeeder.

“That’s our niece you’re talking about.” Wanda tried to lighten the mood. She knew it had to be serious if Agatha had made both of them come.

Ten minutes later, once everyone was settled and Raven was roaming the house, all eyes were on Rio and Agatha as they sat in the living room as if it was any other Saturday night. Agatha was in protective mode with both arms around the younger woman. “Floor’s all yours. Don’t worry, I’m with you.”

Rio exhaled deeply as she looked at Nat and Wanda. “Did Agatha tell you about the stalker situation?”

Wanda nodded. “Briefly, yes.”

“Well, she reached out today.” As she pointed at the flowers they had brought with them.

Nat automatically checked them for tracking devices or bugs, finding none.

“There was this too.” She flinched as she tossed the card on the table for the other four women to read.

Agatha leaned in to whisper in Rio’s ear, “Jen knew before I did?”

“I was ranting to Alice, and it came up, and she was there, so yes.” Rio looked at Agatha apologetically.

Agatha wasn’t completely sure how to feel about Jen knowing about SF first, but it made sense. They turned back towards the others.

“So can we assume that these are not family-friendly movies?” Wanda asked.

Rio sighed. She had many regrets, and those videos were among her biggest ones. “She hated that we couldn’t be public and that was one of her ways to make it up to me,” Rio explained, voice small.

“It was a manipulation and future blackmail technique,” Jen gulped straight vodka. She sympathized with her friend, she did, but she also saw the potential scandal.

Agatha gave her a scathing look.

“If anything happens, we’ll deal with it. This isn’t the time to worry about reputations, Jennifer.” Agatha said with a biting tone. Jen jumped a little at the use of her given name.

“Damn, using your full name,” Alice remarked as she rubbed her girlfriend's thigh with one hand and put her other hand over Jen’s mouth, looking around the room. “What we need to do right now is worry about how to head this off. I know this bitch. This is a thinly veiled threat.”

Nat nodded. “I’m having my tech girl look into the cameras at Rio and Alice’s place. Those flowers were hand-delivered.”

Rio shook her head. “No, SF is good. She is too smart to come herself. She probably paid some rando outside to bring it. No, you wouldn’t have caught that either. She likely knows where all the cameras are. She may or may not have a past.”

Agatha huffed a laugh, “So, what you’re saying is, you have a type?”

A small smile tugged at Rio’s lips before disappearing when Nat confirmed what Rio had said about disabling cameras. “Darcy just texted. Even worse, all the cameras in your building went down for a few minutes this evening.”

Nat looked up at Rio, “You have to tell us who she is.”

Alice looked at Rio as Rio shook her head. “No, some of you have too much to risk. Only the bare minimum people need to know.”

Nat nodded and motioned towards Agatha’s brag room, “Just me then, come on.”

Agatha kissed Rio’s cheek. “Go,” she lightly nudged her off the couch as Rio rose and followed Nat.

Three sets of eyes landed on Alice as she shook her head, “I’m not telling any of you anything.”

Nat directed Rio into the room and closed the door behind them, looking expectantly towards Rio.

Rio wrapped her arms around herself. “What do you know about models?” She asked.

“They’re hot and vapid?” Nat responded, trying to make Rio laugh, which worked.

“Okay, yes. What do you know about Lou Falco?” Rio asked, looking down at her foot as she flexed her toes nervously.

Nat’s eyes went wide. “Lou Falco, former Victoria’s Secret model? Married to former football player turned broadcaster/actor Shane Falco? Lesbian rumors for years, Lou Falco? OH GOD, YOU WERE THE RUMORED SECRET GIRLFRIEND?!” The pieces were falling into place for Nat as her eyes grew wider.

Rio clapped a hand over Nat’s mouth. “Quiet down, Sherlock. Yes, I was. But, now, there’s a restraining order.”

Nat licked her palm and grinned when Rio grimaced and wiped it on her jeans. “Gross.”

“She’s going to have high-powered lawyers, but not as high-powered as Wagatha’s. When does the order expire?”

“December 31st, 11:59 P.M.” Rio recited.

“This is the first time she’s done anything?” Nat asked, looking at Rio intently.

Rio gave a quick nod, not giving anything away.

“I’m going to dust the vase for prints, but she’s brilliant, went to an Ivy League, and never got caught for her small run of burglaries. I don’t expect to find even an eyelash.” Nat thought out loud.

“How do you know about any of that?” Rio asked.

Nat smirked, “Have you met me?”

“Right. Spy.”

“Rio, I’ll take care of it. You’re safe, okay?” Nat knew Rio was worried, and this was Nat’s job. “You’re staying here for a few days, and I’ll let you know when everything’s okay.”

Rio threw her arms around the smaller woman, “Thank you so much, Natasha.”

Nat hugged her back and smiled. She would always protect these women. It was who she was.

They returned to the main room. Rio returned to Agatha’s side as Nat grabbed the tainted flowers and the card and walked out, Wanda on her heels.

Jen looked at Agatha, eyebrows raised, “So, what’s the battle plan in case this goes terribly?”

Agatha turned her eyes to Raven as the cat hopped on her lap. “We go with plan R,” She was only half joking.

⭐⭐⭐

Nat left for a few days, and everyone was on edge, especially Wanda. This is what Natasha did. She took care of the darker parts of fame, but that didn’t make things easier, especially now that they were on the precipice of finally beginning a proper life together. Vision came gliding through the room and stopped when he saw Wanda, leaning against the kitchen island, staring wistfully at her phone. The boys were at school, and he was on his way out the door to teach a class.

“Nat will be perfectly fine,” he had been briefed on the situation and was trying to be every bit the emotional support human that he was.

“I just worry. She’s not twenty-five anymore,” Wanda knew Nat was still in amazing shape, and her lack of height was useful for sneaking around, but without knowing what she was planning, or who the other woman even was, her anxiety was on high alert.

Vision put a comforting hand on her shoulder.

“Natasha Romanov can run circles around men half her age. When she’s eighty, she will still be able to take down CIA agents at the top of their game. She will always come back to you, darling. I have no doubt.” He kissed the top of her head as he grabbed his bag for school. Wanda sighed as she looked after him.

“Vis?” She said tentatively.

“Yes, dear?” he half turned.

“I really am…” She trailed off as he smiled and put up a hand.

“I’m not. Nothing has changed between us when you think about it.” He waved as he walked out of the door.

Nat 🖤

On my way home to

you. I love you.

Me:

Thank gods.

I love you too.

I missed you.

Hopefully, this meant everything was handled, and they could all move on with their lives.

Nat burned her disguise underneath an unmarked bridge. She still got a thrill when she got to do the super shady things. They were so rare these days. She had sent quite the message to Lou, all verbal, and had made sure she wasn’t tracked, no physical evidence. She made sure that Lou knew to back off and leave Rio and Agatha alone, or she’d be leaking divorce documents, custody orders, things that could ruin her career and reputation. She’d also visit the ex-husband. She would never see her children again.

Nat had saved the ‘I will make you disappear, and they will never find the body’ speech. She didn’t think she needed it.

⭐⭐⭐

Lou looked at the window the pretty brute had slipped out of. It hadn’t been the first time someone had tried to intimidate her.

This one made a simple but important miscalculation. It was silly to think Lou cared about her kids more than Rio. Rio was the most important thing in her life.

⭐⭐⭐

Rio had enjoyed staying with Agatha for the few days it took Nat to take care of the Lou situation. When Nicky had come back to the house from Morgan Stark’s, he had taken one look at Raven and asked where the cat had come from. Those two were fast friends, and when Rio and Raven returned to Rio’s apartment Wednesday evening, Rio wasn’t sure who of the four of them was more upset about it.

Nicky had Raven in his arms, and the big blue eyes he inherited from his mother were focused on Rio. “Does she have to go?” He pleaded.

“Sorry, kid.” Something Rio was quickly picking up from Agatha, “We’re a package deal. My familiar comes with me.” Raven meowed in agreement.

Nicky looked between the two human women, trying to choose the next words carefully, but decided to just go for it, “You’re lesbians. Shouldn’t you be moving in by now? Planning a wedding? Working on giving me a sibling?” He was prepared to run just in case one of them threw hands.

Agatha chose sarcasm. “There’s not a single fast-moving woman in this group, son.”

“Biology doesn’t work that way, but I can try,” Rio offered, making eyes at Agatha.

“Science, and gross.” Nicky made a gagging sound. He had been prepared for physical swinging, not the verbal kind.

Rio rolled her eyes. “Like I didn’t catch you with your tongue down Morgan’s throat this morning.”

Agatha’s eyebrows almost flew off her head. “Wear a condom. We are not sharing a grandchild with Tony before the two of you graduate.”

Rio turned to Agatha, “We?”

“You know what I mean.” She mumbled, blushing.

“Are you blushing?” Rio rarely saw Agatha blush or get flustered.

The teenager took that as his cue, “I’m going to put things in Rio’s car.” He set Raven down and went to load the car.

“We’re sharing a hypothetical grandchild now?” Rio asked.

“No, because he’s going to be smart.” Agatha regained her composure. “Want to spend Thanksgiving with us?” She changed the topic.

Rio nodded as Agatha kissed her, taking her mind off Nicky’s earlier questions.

⭐⭐⭐

It was the week of Thanksgiving and things had been relatively quiet. No texts or deliveries from SF, the public speculation had died down for the moment, and they were on break from school. Alice and Jen had put an offer in on a house they had fallen in love with immediately. The grass was green, the sky was blue, and everyone was gay.

Nicky was officially dating Morgan Stark, which Pepper Potts-Stark and Rio were excited about, and Tony Stark and Agatha were not. Billy and Eddie were going strong, and Tommy was more excited about running track next semester.

Rio and Alice had always spent the holidays together in San Francisco and were excited to spend them with their chosen family this year. Rio was making pies in her kitchen while Alice ‘helped’ by watching and not touching anything.

“Have the two of you decided what you are yet?” Alice asked as she drank tea.

“No. We’re still just dating, but I think I might be ready to take the next step.” Rio slid a pie in the oven.

“Actually fuck Agatha Fucking Harkness?” Alice raised her eyebrows twice suggestively.

“Well, yes, but no. I’m thinking more like making it official and declare we’re dating,” Rio said as she started on a chocolate pie.

“It’s about damn time. Four months, you’ve spent the last one playing tonsil hockey, and she’s taking you to Taylor Swift.”

“She’s taking all of us,” Rio interrupted.

“Whatever, it’s you that counts. She also had Nat get rid of Lou.”

“Nat threatened Lou. Bitch is still alive,” Rio corrected.

“Unfortunately. She moved you into her house for a few days to keep you safe. Which you LOVED,” Alice reminded her.

“Shut up,” Rio blushed. The truth was, she had loved it. Four nights falling asleep in Agatha’s arms, four mornings waking up in them. When Nicky joked about moving in, she found herself fantasizing about it. It was too soon for that, but she wanted it one day. She wanted the Agatha of it all. She cooked for them each night and helped Nicky with his homework. They had played house for a few days, and she loved it. She shook the memories from her head.

“Whether I loved it or not is beside the point. Maybe one day it will happen. When are you asking Jen to marry you?” She flipped the conversation. At least someone was moving their relationship forward. Wanda had to wait until her divorce, since papers were drawn up but not filed yet, and Rio had just decided to make things official with Agatha.

Alice choked on her drink. “Excuse you?”

“What? You’re buying a house. Are you not going to get married? I’ve already appointed myself as Maid of Honor. I’m trying to decide if I’ll wear a suit or a dress. I assume it won’t be a giant affair. Neither of you is that type,” Rio grinned, putting the next pie in the refrigerator.

Alice shook her head, “You’ve spent too much time with Wanda.”

When Alice had gone home and the pies were ready to go to Agatha’s, Rio settled in for the night.

Agatha 💜

I’m looking forward to tomorrow.

Me:

So am I.

I’m ready to create new

memories with you.

Agatha 💜

I know it’s usually just

you and Alice.

Me:

About that. Thanksgiving the

past two years, we went

to SF’s. She had a lot

of her friends over.

Plus her kids.

Agatha read the text multiple times. Rio hadn’t mentioned that before. Why hadn’t she told her that? Agatha had to remind herself that there were still things she was hiding as well.

Agatha 💜

Now I really want to

create new holiday memories

with you.

Rio smiled at that. It was a mix of sincerity and wanting to take away the painful memories, the protective side of Agatha, and a tinge of jealousy.

Me:

I will be over bright and early

tomorrow to help

with the cooking.

Goodnight Agatha.

Agatha 💜

Goodnight Rio.

Rio sighed in contentment. She would have a stress-free day, cook with her friends, and at the end of the day, she would tell Agatha Fucking Harkness that she was ready to be her girlfriend. She giggled to herself at the absurdity of it, like she was about to ask someone out for the first time.

Blocked

Are you coming to Thanksgiving

tomorrow? You’re my family

and I miss you.

Rio groaned and rolled over. Surely this bitch was kidding. Rio decided that if she received any texts on Thanksgiving, she’d let Nat know.

Thanksgiving was perfect. Rio had arrived at Agatha’s at seven that morning, let herself in after she disarmed the alarm, and placed the pies on the counter. She went upstairs to Agatha’s bedroom and found Agatha sound asleep in just panties and an oversized T-shirt. Her hair had returned to its factory settings, and messy curls were fanning out around her. She was curled on her side, both arms under her pillow. Rio toed off her shoes and crawled into the bed next to her, knowing that she could have run and jumped on the bed at full speed, and Agatha wouldn’t have budged. Rio positioned herself under the covers and brushed strands of hair away from the actress's face, scooting closer to her before lightly kissing her forehead, cheek, nose, and then lips.

Agatha smiled as she slowly opened her eyes, “Good morning, beautiful. Have I ever told you how much I prefer waking up like this?”

“Only every time it happens.” Rio kissed her fully, morning breath and all. She knew it wasn’t long before the house was full of people, but she had changed her mind about when she wanted to talk to Agatha. Agatha’s lips parted as Rio moaned and slid her tongue alongside Agatha’s slowly. Rio trailed her hand from Agatha’s cheek down her shoulder to her breast, lightly running a thumb over her hardened nipple through the shirt as Rio rolled them so that she was on top, straddling Agatha.

Agatha whimpered into Rio’s mouth, this woman was going to be the death of her. Agatha ran her hands up Rio’s back, under her shirt, before scratching down her back, eliciting a gasp from Rio. Rio discontinued her ministrations, breaking the kiss and sitting up, still straddling Agatha.

Agatha groaned. “You’re a brat, you know that?” Agatha reached up, lately tracing Rio’s collarbone.

Rio gave her a toothy smile and nodded. “I have a question.”

“You can have whatever you want if we could just go back to what we were doing,” Agatha would find a way to single-handedly combat climate change if it meant she could continue kissing Rio.

Rio’s face went serious as she put her hand on Agatha’s cheek and scooted back, lightly tugging Agatha so that she was sitting up, eyes locked on each other’s.

“You,” Rio breathed. “Agatha, I want you, officially. As juvenile as it sounds, be my girlfriend? You can even post a statement, right now,” She was looking straight into Agatha’s soul.

On the inside, Agatha was jumping for joy like a child on Christmas morning. On the outside, she was grinning at her girlfriend. “It’s not juvenile, and yes I will be. I want you as well.” She kissed Rio in a way that said Always. She leaned back. “Can I post a picture of us later too?” She updated her socials fairly regularly and wanted to share her happiness with the world. She had promised not to keep Rio a secret, and she meant it. Agatha wrapped her arms around Rio’s waist and closed the space between their bodies.

“Now, I believe you started something,” she fisted a hand in Rio’s hair and captured her mouth in a needy kiss.

Rio cupped one of Agatha’s lace-clad buttcheeks in one hand and put the other hand on her shoulder. They were fighting for control of the kiss when they heard the door close downstairs and Wanda yell.

“Now is when you two decide to do this? Get down here. We have a meal to cook!”

They both groaned as they broke the kiss, breathing heavily.

“I’m buying a pack of scrunchies,” Agatha said.

Chapter End Notes

Lou is the Lou from Ocean's 8. So Cate Blanchett.
I would gladly let Cate Blanchett stalk me, but this is crazy Cate Blanchett.
I'd still consider it...

For the next chapter, we are taking scrunchie donations. We will need them.

As ALWAYS - thank you so much to my universe-granted designated neurotypical beta/editor DaretoDream82 who is indispensable in translating my AuDHD thoughts.

I Like Your T Swift T-Shirt On The Ground

Chapter Summary

Thanksgiving Day is rather eventful and Rio makes a confession Agatha isn't quite expecting.
Vancouver lifts everyone's spirits and unspoken promises are made.

Chapter Notes

Alexa, play "Body is Bible" by Fletcher.

Warning: Spice.

Also, yes, I made some changes to real-life events, you know we wanted her to wear those outfits, and I needed her to sing that specific line!

Thank you so much for all the comments, kudos, telling your friends, interaction on my socials, etc. It means so much to me, and my amazing beta/sounding board. I try to respond to every comment! The dopamine/serotonin boost is so welcome! Thank you for reading/liking/loving my drabbles!

See the end of the chapter for more notes

It was Thanksgiving morning, after all, and everyone was waiting downstairs. Once Agatha was presentable and everyone had busied themselves with meal preparations, Agatha took a picture of her and Rio and made a Jen-approved post on her socials.

“Happy Thanksgiving from us to you. Thankful for my girlfriend, son, and amazing family and friends!”

She threw in a few hashtags and let it go. Rio made a mental note to check the comments later when her phone dinged.

Blocked

WHAT?!

GIRLFRIEND?!

YOU’RE SPENDING TODAY

WITH HER?!

There was also a picture attached, except it wasn’t the one Agatha had just posted. It was worse. It was of Agatha and Rio in Agatha’s bed, with Rio straddling Agatha and kissing her. It looked like the picture had been taken through the window. Rio was shaking. The phone dinged again. Another picture. This one of her staring at the phone. She looked up, trying to figure out the angle as she stormed out the door.

Agatha noticed when Rio received the text and alerted Nat. When Rio went outside, everyone followed.

Rio’s eyes were wide with panic and fear. She was rubbing the left side of her chest as she felt the familiar symptoms of a panic attack seep in. She whipped her head around to search for Lou, looking into the trees, bushes, everywhere. Then, she heard whirring of some kind and saw a flash of red hair leap into the air and take down a low-flying drone, landing in some superhero pose.

They heard a woman’s laugh, the slam of a car door, and a car speeding off. Nat looked at the drone.

“There must have been two. This one is a red herring, but I’ll take it to Darcy,” She was pissed, “I’m taking another trip.”

Wanda shook her head, “You’re not leaving until tomorrow. Please,” she pled. “We’re getting to be ourselves this year.”

Agatha was ready to commit murder herself as she wrapped her arms around a frozen and sobbing Rio. “It’s okay, we’ll increase security, it’s okay.” She soothed.

Agatha turned towards the group. “Everyone back inside, blinds and shades closed. We’re not letting her ruin this, okay?” She corralled everyone inside as Nat made calls and increased security around the compound.

All eyes turned to Rio’s phone when it dinged again.

Blocked

Baby, I love you.

Come back to me.

“She’s a psychopath,” Jen exclaimed after she read the text.

Agatha looked at the girlfriend whose hand she still held, “Was that the laugh we heard?”

Rio shook her head, “No. Someone else, a P.I. or someone she hired, most likely. I don’t trust it, though.

“Mom, what happened?” Nicky asked from the table. The three boys had picked up that something was happening but didn’t know what exactly, as they had been instructed to stay inside.

Rio buried her face in her hands as she turned into Agatha and sobbed. Her world had crashed in as she realized that the boys were potentially in danger now.

Agatha held Rio tightly as if she was the only thing holding her together. The four parents exchanged looks, trying to decide how best to handle the conversation.

It was Alice who spoke instead.

“I know you boys have some experience with this from your mom’s fame, but Rio has a stalker. A relentless one. It’s one of the reasons she moved here. We call her SF for San Francisco. If you say her name three times, she’ll appear like Beetlejuice.” Billy smiled at that one. He was playing Beetlejuice in the school play in just a few days.

“Was she hurt?” Tommy asked. He vaguely remembered someone sending his mom random notes a few years ago, but nothing came from it. He had heard horror stories at school, though.

Rio was still seeking solace in Agatha and thankful that Tommy had gathered that now was not the time to ask Rio any direct questions. These boys were wise beyond their years.

Alice nodded. “She was. It’s why I brought her here. I knew she would be safe in our little coven.”

Nicky thought everything over carefully. He knew Alice hadn’t done it intentionally. She hadn’t known his mom would be taken by Rio, but he would have to thank her one day. Things were a little scary right now, but Rio had been good for every single person on the compound, especially his mom. Agatha had never been unhappy, necessarily, but he had never seen her this happy either. He and the twins had also deduced that Rio had been the one to make the final push for Aunt Wanda to make her decision as well, AND Jen and Alice were together. There was something about Rio that was magic. He also knew his mom and Mr. Stark weren’t on the best terms, so if Rio could smooth things over in that department, this could work for him, too.

He came out of his thoughts as Billy shook him, “Hey, space cadet. We’re going to help Jen monitor the internet for anything shady being posted.”

Nicky nodded. “Sounds like a plan.”

The excitement of the morning had died down, and cooking had resumed. There was still tension, but Nat had worked quickly to call in a few favors and increased security, pulled camera footage, and even called Darcy to see if she could trace the number that had texted Rio that morning. Unfortunately, SF was good and it led to a useless burner phone. That’s when Nat decided to create a clone of Rio’s phone on her computer and reverse-stalk Lou. The phone Lou was using was off, so she couldn’t see her current location. However, a few older texts came up. The texts from this morning were not the first Rio had received. That girl had been hiding this. Nat swore under her breath. Agatha had found someone who also dealt in half-truths. Nat walked into the kitchen, where the other women were cleaning up. “Rio, can I borrow you for a minute? Nothing major.” She knew Rio was on high alert and didn’t want to cause her anxiety to go any higher.

“Sure,” Rio put a plate in the dishwasher, kissed Agatha on the cheek, and followed Nat. Wanda and Agatha exchanged looks as the other two women walked out.

Once they were out of earshot, Nat turned and held up Rio’s phone.

“I cloned your phone so I could keep track of SF,” Nat said quietly as she handed it back to the wide-eyed woman. Rio hadn’t even known Nat took it in the first place. “Before you even say anything, why didn’t you say anything earlier, before the flowers?” Nat seemed more concerned than mad.

“I thought I could handle it, or that she would just give up,” Rio said meekly, "I know she’s not the most stable, but I didn’t think she would fly off the handle like this.” Rio’s voice went even quieter, and Natasha had to lean in so she could hear her. “I still don’t think she’d actually hurt me in any way. It was Shane who did that, hurt me, not her. Never Lou.” When she finished speaking, her voice was barely audible, and a fresh tear slid down her cheek.

“Fuck,” Natasha stood. “You still love her.” It was a statement, not a question.

Rio shrugged. “I really don’t know. I am still processing it. It was a large part of my life that just fell apart so quickly.”

Natasha nodded. She knew a thing or two about things happening quickly.

“Talk to Agatha. If you don’t tell her, I will. I’m not going to watch her get hurt…” Nat had to stop herself from adding ‘again’. She knew Agatha hadn’t told Rio about that, but she was ready to threaten Agatha, too. “...and this would crush her if you don’t tell her now. Anyone in the state of California with eyes can see you two…care…about each other. Communicate. Please.”

Rio nodded, gathering her composure. “I will. Tonight.”

Everyone had left, and Rio thought about what Nat had said. She needed to come clean, and she knew that she couldn’t completely be with Agatha in any physical way until she told her about her emotional conflicts. Rio wordlessly took Agatha’s hand and led her to the couch, guiding her to sit so they could face each other.

Agatha had noticed that Rio had been rather quiet since speaking with Nat in the hallway an hour ago, and Agatha had been worried. She put down her wine, taking Rio’s hands in hers.

“What is it, Rio? What’s wrong?”

Rio let out a shaky breath. “I’ve been lying to you, and I need to confess a few things.” The words tumbled out of her mouth, and they were not the ones she had rehearsed in her brain for the past forty-five minutes.

Agatha started replaying every conversation since they first met. “Okay…”

Rio looked down at the couch. “Today was not the first time SF texted me. It was actually a few weeks ago…” She told her about the warning to stay away from Agatha, the text about the stars, and the one from the night prior. “Nat knows now and can now see everything that comes into my phone.” She could tell that Agatha was considering Rio’s words. She wasn’t mad but hurt.

“Why didn’t you tell me?” Agatha ached at this, but at the same time, wasn’t she still harboring a giant secret? One that ate at her more and more by the day?  Although,  this could have caused harm to all of them, and still could. She pushed her hurt aside, knowing it was a small penance for keeping her secrets tight to her chest.

Rio curled into herself, wrapping her arms around her knees, trying to make herself as small as she felt. She hated having to admit these feelings to Agatha. She didn’t want to have them for anyone but Agatha. “I didn’t think she would do anything, Agatha. L…SF had never hurt me, physically. Had never hurt anyone I was close to. I didn’t think she had completely lost it, didn’t want to believe it. It was her ex-husband that hurt me. And then, Alice was with me, there was the order, no contact….”

Another tear escaped the war behind her eyes as her voice died out. She couldn’t bring herself to look at Agatha.

Agatha felt like a fist had closed around her heart. She knew what Rio was saying without her actually saying it, and Agatha was trying her best not to crumble. “Rio,” Agatha’s voice shook, “how do you feel about her?” Agatha had to know if Rio was still in love with her. She had just finally gotten Rio, that fucking morning. Now, here they were with Rio about to tell her she may still be in love with another woman.

Rio winced. Agatha’s voice was too shaky, yet too strong at the same time. “You have to understand everything that happened,” Rio started, “I found out on Christmas Day that things were absolutely not over between her and the hubby, and they most likely never would be. I stormed out of the house and out of her life. We had been talking about our future that morning. A secret marriage, leaving the country, her retiring, very Evelyn Hugo, when she got a phone call from him, and you would think I hadn’t even existed. Like we hadn’t spent the entire morning making love and talking about our plans, so I made up my mind and left. Alice came to town that day and found me sobbing.”

Agatha realized that their pasts weren’t that dissimilar, this would be the perfect time to divulge her own past, but this moment wasn’t about her. She waited patiently until Rio inhaled deeply, ready to go on.

“When she realized I was serious, the texts, gifts, pictures, the whole stalking thing started. When she broke into my house in February, I had to get a temporary order, and you know the rest. I never got closure, Agatha. We were never able to discuss everything. One minute, we were talking marriage and…well, it doesn’t matter, but then we were over. She was the first woman I thought I could spend the rest of my life with, and then it was over.” Rio had stopped crying, and she found that she was angry more than anything.

Rio hadn’t answered her question, and Agatha still needed the answer. She untangled Rio’s hands from her knees, tipped her chin, and locked eyes. She had to be able to see Rio’s eyes when she finally got her answer. “Rio, now that I understand everything. How do you feel?” She asked again.

Rio shrugged.

“I’m not in love with her. I don’t want to be with her. I meant it when I said I want to be with you, Agatha. I care about you deeply. I choose you, Agatha.”

That was the truth. Did part of her love Lou? Yes, but it was more of a mourning of a sudden loss than anything. She was Agatha’s, and all the crazy in the world wasn’t going to change that. She grabbed Agatha by the collar of her shirt and brought her in for a searing kiss, a proclamation, and a promise, the taste of salt from tears mixed in with hot breath, moans, and teeth. Agatha shifted and kissed away Rio’s tears before leaning back slightly. “Rio?”

“Hmm?” She answered, her lips swollen, trying to chase Agatha’s mouth to continue what they were doing.

“No, Rio, be serious.” She said in a slightly more commanding tone.

Rio leaned back and tried to focus.

“Please stop keeping things from me,” The hypocrisy was not lost on Agatha, but this wasn’t about her. “You can tell me anything. If we’re going to do this, we have to be honest and communicate with each other,” she realized she wasn’t applying this same logic to herself. However, she hadn’t survived this long in the industry by being honest.

Rio nodded, “Yes. Thank you for being patient with me. I know this is a lot.”

Agatha kissed her again, “I will always choose you, too.”

Rio sat back, wiping at her eyes. “One minute! If you’re going to do this, so am I.” She pulled up her socials, as some people had already found her throughout the day since she had ‘liked’ Agatha’s posts. “Come here,” she turned so she was in front of Agatha and took a selfie of them, Rio’s back pressed against the front of Agatha, Rio kissing Agatha’s cheek, Agatha looking down at her and smiling.

“Perfect!” She posted it with the caption. ‘So thankful for the woman I call my girlfriend! Happy Thanksgiving!’

Agatha grinned. “I really like the sound of that, by the way,” as she kissed Rio again, slowly.

It was then that Natasha barrelled through the door. “Alice’s, NOW.”

⭐⭐⭐

Lou sat in Alice Wu-Gulliver’s darkened apartment. The restraining order only covered Rio’s physical location and apartment, not Alice’s. Surely, Lou could speak to Alice and tell her how she felt about Rio. If anyone could talk sense into her beautiful Rio, it was Alice. Lou couldn’t believe the pictures the investigator had sent her that morning, and she hadn’t been able to get to LA quickly enough. Agatha Harkness was obviously bad for Rio. She was a known fuck boi, Lou knew that personally. So what if Agatha was slowing down in her old age? She was using Rio. Probably for better grades for her son or something. Rio was better and out of Agatha’s league. Rio belonged to Lou and only Lou. She had thought about cat-napping that incessant beast of Rio’s, but that was the one thing she would never forgive. Crow? Finch? Whatever bird the damn thing was named after meant the world to Rio. Lou hated cats, and the foul thing hated Lou.

Maybe, if this didn’t work out, she could make it look like Agatha poisoned the damned thing. Rio would surely come back to Lou in that scenario. That was a thought. Rio would never love Agatha, then.

Lou snapped out of her thoughts when she heard a key turn. It was about damn time. Why did everyone spend so much time at that damn compound anyway?

“I just don’t see why you can’t tell me who the psychotic bitch is, honey,” Jen said to Alice as they walked in the door.

“Because it’s,” she flipped on the lights, and her eyes automatically landed on the blonde model sitting on her couch. “Lou,” she finished.

“See,” Jen laughed, “That wasn’t so hard,” it was then that Jen’s eyes also landed on the woman, her mouth forming an ‘o’. “Lou Falco? Oh shit. Rio does have a type.”

“It’s about time, Alice,” Lou said, not bothering to get up. She turned her head to Jen, “You must be the infamous Jennifer Kale, charmed, I’m sure,” she looked back to Alice. “I’m here about Rio.”

“You heartless bitch. Leave her alone. She was healing. She was finally moving on from you.” Alice was furious as she stalked towards where Lou was on the couch.

Lou sighed, “Oh Alice, don’t you see I’m here because I love her? I need you to see that so you can tell her so and talk sense into her. Agatha doesn’t love her. Not like I do. Agatha will just fuck and forget her like she does every other woman.”

“You hid her, Lou. Lied to her. Your husband almost killed her. If he had still been in his prime, he would have. You USED her, Lou. Not Agatha.” Alice may have been smaller, but she was ready to kill to protect one of her coven.

Jen had quietly taken a picture and sent it to Nat, and was now discreetly recording the entire thing. She made sure her phone was on silent so if Nat texted back, Lou wouldn’t be alerted. Jen wouldn’t be surprised if this lunatic were armed. She stepped forward and put an arm around Alice’s waist.

“I know who you are, and I can destroy your entire career.” Jen spat.

Lou rolled her eyes. “Please, I’d give it all up for Rio.”

Alice cackled, “Right. If you were willing to do that, you’d have done it already. You’ll do what you always do: promise her the world, make her come back, and then crush her heart. You do it every fucking time. If you did love her, you’d let her go. You’d let her be happy. AGATHA makes her happy. She was NEVER that happy with you. She’s always smiling now, Lou. She doesn’t call me in tears anymore. She doesn’t call me because she saw Agatha on someone else’s arm. Do you know why? Because she’s the one on Agatha’s arm. Agatha Fucking Harkness, who infamously NEVER takes anyone to anything, has and will take Rio to EVERYTHING, Lou. You were afraid to be seen in public with her. Even as a fucking friend unless it was as a group. Even that only happened a handful of times in TWO YEARS. They’ve been friends for FOUR months.” Alice was breathing like she’d been in a boxing match.

Lou’s phone went off and when she checked it, her face went pale. Though Rio had blocked her on all of her socials, Lou followed her anyway on burner accounts. The notification was a post from Rio. A declaration and commitment to Agatha. After the lengths Lou had gone through for her today, Rio was making the relationship public now and even posted a picture.

Alice raised an eyebrow and checked her phone since she received a similar notification. She smiled and handed her phone to Jen.

“Looks like she chose Agatha over you,” Jen smirked, “Do you get it now? She doesn’t love you, Falco,” Jen was choosing her words carefully, the phone still inconspicuously filming.

“She’s clearly been brainwashed,” she scoffed but turned eager once more. “This isn’t over. I was hoping you could help me, Alice. You were always the one to help my sweet Rio make decisions when she was confused about her emotions.” Lou stood and started towards the door, pulling a hood over her face.

“Yes, I usually told her to leave you.” Alice moved out of the way before Lou could even brush against her.

“I assume Agatha has at least one goon on the way.” She eyed Jen. “I’ll be taking my leave.”

Jen knew Lou’s reputation and made sure to have a grip on her phone as Lou made to leave when Alice had an idea. It was a bad one, but it was an idea.

“Rio’s coming with them.”

Lou stopped, “She is?”

Jen nodded in agreement. “Yes, now’s your chance.” Natasha had texted back that all four of them were on the way.

Lou looked around. She was cornered here, it wasn’t a good idea. She could make her escape if she met them outside though. She kept walking and easily slipped by both women and out the door.

Jen looked at Alice, “Do we go after her?” She asked her girlfriend. Alice just nodded. She already had her hand on the doorknob.

Alice saw the door to the stairs close and nodded to the elevator. “She’s going to meet them outside. She’s done this before. Let Nat know.”

The four women had just exited the vehicle in the parking garage as Nat read the text from Jen.

“She’s on her way out here,” She turned to Agatha and Rio. “Aggie, you should get her out of here.”

Rio shook her head. “It’s fine,” She gripped Agatha’s hand.

Wanda was ready to tear this SF bitch limb from limb herself. She knew the threat to her family and her happiness this woman posed and she was ready to do what she needed to do.

“You are to stay back,” Nat said, putting both hands on Wanda’s shoulders. Wanda opened her mouth to protest as Nat kissed her, just in case something went wrong.

“Nu. Te iubesc,” Nat added in Romani, Wanda’s first language. No, I love you.

“YA tozhe tebya lyublyu,” Wanda said in Russian. I love you too.

They turned their heads as they heard Jen and Alice’s footsteps hurry towards them.

“She’s not here yet?” Alice looked around.

Rio shook her head.

“I recorded everything,” Jen told Nat as she turned to Rio. “You need to hear it.”

Rio nodded. Everyone stilled when they heard running. Jen turned her phone on to record as Nat motioned for Wanda, Alice, and Jen to get back in between the vehicles.

Nat, Agatha, and Rio stepped out.

Lou came into view, slowing her steps, taking in the three women. Agatha and the pretty goon weren’t her concern. It was her beautiful Rio that she had her eyes on. She knew she only had a few minutes. She had told her own personal bodyguard to drive through here like a bat out of hell in five minutes, shooting if need be. Agatha, that cad, had her arms around Rio. Didn’t that movie star whore know that Rio belonged to Lou?

“Rio, love, I came for you.” Lou smiled as she approached.

Agatha tightened her grip on Rio as she got a look at the woman coming close to her. She knew it was Lou Falco, but she looked more familiar than that as if she had seen her recently. Maybe in an ad or a commercial or something? “That’s close enough Lou.” She said firmly.

“This isn’t your business, Harkness.” Lou barked. “This is between my fiancée and I.”

Agatha’s eyebrows flew up as she loosened her arms and looked at Rio. Nat didn’t flinch. She knew this was a tactic when she heard Alice hiss at Wanda and Jen that Lou was lying.

“You never proposed. You just made promises you never planned on keeping,” Rio said, voice steady. She laced Agatha’s fingers through her own. “Stop pursuing me, Lou.”

Lou grinned threateningly, “But I love you, and you’re mine.”

“She’s her own person!” Agatha shouted. She hated when people thought they owned others. Yes, you could ‘belong’ to someone if you chose to do so, but you were still your own person first.

Lou was closing in fast and reached towards Rio, who stepped into Agatha at the same time Agatha pulled Rio into her and in between their SUV and another car, knowing Nat was going to take over.

Takeover Nat did. She slammed Lou to the ground with the full force of her body as she slammed a fist into her left cheek. She was just getting started when she heard tires screeching. Nat dove away from Lou and heard the slam of car doors and tires screech away. She waited a moment before jumping up and running to Wanda first, helping her up as she ran her hands over her and covered her in kisses. “Are you okay?” Nat asked as she checked for even the smallest scratch while Wanda laughed.

“Baby, all I did was watch.”

“I don’t care, I was worried.”

“We’re fine too, by the way,” Jen said, pulling Alice up and waving her phone slightly. “Everything was recorded.”

Agatha had gently helped Rio to her feet, lightly caressing her face, arms, and shoulders. If she could have caressed her heart and mind, she would have. “I don’t think it’s over,” Rio whispered as she hugged Agatha.

“I know, sweetheart, I know.” Agatha held her while looking at the other women. She was ready for the fight of her life.

⭐⭐⭐

Someone had poked the bear inside of Agatha, and she was ready to maim.

Nat had left the next morning and gone to Connecticut, straight to Shane Falco, Lou’s ex-husband. He walked into his office at ESPN to find Nat with an envelope and an offer. He could choose to have full custody of his children and file charges against his wife for child endangerment, forcing a psychological evaluation and hospitalization, or Nat could expose the bribery from Falco over what had happened last spring with Rio. She filled him in on the last twenty-four hours and the actual story of his ex-wife’s actions. Nat was not one to use someone’s mental health against them, but this was a different story.

The way Lou spun the ‘situation’, as Shane called it, with Rio was much different than what had really happened. Lou had lied about the entire relationship to Shane. He had finally divorced Lou after seeing holes in her story. He feared for their three kids and was generally concerned about Rio’s safety, as well as apologetic about what he had done.

He wasn’t a bad guy, Nat thought, but another victim of Lou’s manipulation. By Monday, he had the kids, and Lou was admitted against her will. That was taken care of, at least temporarily. Rio hadn’t wanted her name involved or any connection to Agatha, so it was only a matter of time before Lou’s return.

Agatha still had increased security, warned the school, and had bodyguards assigned to everyone. She didn’t care where Lou was. If she wasn’t six feet in the ground or scattered across the earth, she didn’t trust it.

⭐⭐⭐

The comments about Agatha and Rio being an official couple had been mostly positive. Jen had given an official statement to a few of her magazines of choice over the next few days about how Agatha and Rio were just taking it slowly and seeing where things led. With Rio posting from her personal socials, her last name had leaked, and they now had two hashtags, and the internet had gone wild.

“#ViDarkness is much more creative than #Agathario.”

“Have y’all seen Rio’s cat? She’s so freaking cute!”

“Raven needs her own account.”

“Agatha Harkness and girlfriend Rio Vidal make things official after being seen together since October.”

“New photos leak!”

“Agatha and Rio taking things slowly, says a source close to the couple.”

“Everytime ‘People’ says a ‘source’ you know it’s Jennifer Kale”

“Jennifer Kale has such perfect skin.”

“I heard a rumor that Jennifer Kale is dating Lorna Wu’s daughter, Alice.”

“Can you imagine Thanksgiving at Agatha’s?”

“I wonder what Nicky calls Rio?”

“Agatha and Rio could cure my mommy issues AND my teacher issues.”

Agatha was glad the attention was off of Wanda, for now, but didn’t like the spotlight and pressure being on Rio. She didn’t mind sharing the spotlight. She just didn’t want Rio to have any added stress. Rio just shrugged it off. As long as she didn’t have a crazy stalker, she was fine. Agatha knew they would receive a lot of attention in Vancouver, being in the VIP tent. She would have to talk to Jen about media training for Rio.

First up, however, was Billy’s musical at SHIELD. He had been preparing for it all semester, and theater was understandably a big deal at SHIELD. Productions were always a major event and close to Broadway caliber. Billy looked great in his Beetlejuice costume, and the other two boys had quick cameos as dead football players. The adults took their seats, and while Rio knew that Lou couldn’t hurt her at the moment, she didn’t completely trust it and kept glancing around. The middle and high schoolers were amazing, and Billy had performed his heart out, proving that he was very much a Maximoff.

It was finally Saturday morning and time for Vancouver and the Taylor Swift concert on Sunday. Rio was filled with excitement as she and Agatha got to their hotel suite.

“You’re sure you’re okay with your son having a room with two other teenage boys?” Rio raised an eyebrow as she looked at her girlfriend. “There is a spare room in here he could have.”

“No one is sharing a room with us.” Agatha wrapped her arms around Rio as she brought her in for a kiss. “I plan to do a lot of this.”

Rio nodded. “I approve of this plan.”

Agatha’s eyes flew open as she remembered something. She walked briskly to her suitcase and fished out a new pack of scrunchies. “Look! No one will bother us!”

Rio let out a cackle. “Brilliant. We can put them on the doorknob just so everyone leaves us alone,” Rio teased.

“That would allow us to sleep in,” Agatha wrapped her arms back around Rio’s waist.

“Watch the movies we want to watch.” Rio wrapped her arms around Agatha’s shoulders.

“Get dressed in peace.” Agatha kissed Rio gently.

There was a knock on the door, followed by Nicky’s voice, “Mom! Dad! We’re hungry!”

Agatha dropped her head.

“He has his own fucking credit card. I’m going to murder him.”

She stomped to the door like a petulant teen.

⭐⭐⭐

They had all gathered in the main room of Agatha and Rio’s suite for movie night to watch the Christmas classic, Die Hard. The boys and Vision sprawled on the floor, Nat and Wanda on one end of the couch, Jen and Alice on a loveseat, and Agatha and Rio on the other end of the couch. Agatha and Rio were the last two awake as Hans Gruber fell to his death.

“This and The Muppet’s Christmas Carol are the best Christmas movies,” Agatha stated.
