“Bad Mom’s Christmas. I love you in that movie,” Rio added.

“You would,” Agatha smirked.

“What? Like you said, I have a type.”

“Yeah, famous older women with a penchant for breaking the law,” Agatha said as she kissed her cheek.

“At least I’m consistent!” Rio got up and headed to their bedroom. ‘Their bedroom’. Even if it was just for two nights at a hotel, she could get used to that.

Agatha followed her. “I’m consistent! Attractive, breathing, with a pulse. That’s consistent.” She teased.

Rio settled in the middle of the bed, “You sure do know how to make a gal feel special.”

Agatha settled beside Rio, tipping her face up so their eyes met.

“Rio, did I ever date before?”

Rio shook her head in reply.

“YOU are my type. Intelligent, sassy, kind, caring, beautiful, honest…” Agatha punctuated each adjective with a kiss as Rio smiled. Agatha was being honest, even if she was still holding back.

Rio tightened her hold on Agatha as she kissed her back, hands tangled in Agatha’s hair. Rio knew she had fallen for Agatha but refused to admit it. She was Agatha’s, and if everyone hadn’t been asleep one room over like a sleepover, she would show her as much. Instead, she sighed frustratedly as she assumed her now familiar sleep position.

“We have got to set firmer boundaries in this family,” Rio murmured mostly to herself.

“Agreed,” Agatha whispered into her hair as she trailed her fingers up and down Rio’s back before they fell asleep.

It was Taylor Swift Day, and Rio was floating. She had seen the Eras tour in 2023, but this was different. This was the VERY LAST SHOW and she was with AGATHA FUCKING HARKNESS. Her friends, too, but mainly Agatha. Her girlfriend. Rio still pinched herself on occasion to make sure this was her reality. They were all covered in glitter and dressed in various costumes and outfits. Nicky was dressed as a lime green dog, Tommy as a black one, and Billy wore a shirt that said ‘Wide-Eyed Gays’.

There were several VIP tents, and they exchanged friendship bracelets with different people and sang along with Gracie Abrams, waiting for Taylor to come on, and screaming when she did, in all of her tangerine body suit glory.

“I need those boots!” Agatha yelled into Rio’s ear.

“Any and every one of those bodysuits would look amazing on you,” Rio yelled back.

They all sang, yelled, screamed, and danced along for three and a half hours. There were tears and sobbing to Taylor’s speeches as well. They had all screamed the line ‘shade never made anybody less gay’ during “You Need To Calm Down” and Wanda had taken a lot of pictures of everyone. Agatha had told Rio that Wanda and Nat tended to get carried away at concerts and to cover them if it looked like they might. She felt justified when they watched Nat look deeply into Wanda’s eyes singing the line ‘marry me Juliet’, but grateful that Vision and the boys formed a wall around them. Rio and Agatha both sang “We Are Never Getting Back Together” at the top of their lungs. When Rio asked Agatha why she was feeling the song so hard, she said she just liked it a lot, with a shrug. Agatha knew it was because that one relationship she had been in had been on and off again for so long, though Agatha wasn’t going to tell Rio here. It was also why ‘All Too Well’ hit so hard, especially the age gap it referenced. Agatha had been the younger woman and it had hurt a lot, being in such different places in their lives.

It was during Enchanted, though when Agatha wrapped her arms around Rio, and they sang to each other without even realizing it. Agatha noticed how true the lyrics rang for her. She remembered when she asked Lilia if Rio was single immediately the day they met at the fieldhouse. And how she hadn’t wanted the night to end the first time Rio had come back to her house.

Wanda took more pictures with Nat’s arm around her.

Rio sang “Look What You Made Me Do” with a vengeance and then every Folkmore song with her whole chest. Agatha loved how passionate Rio was about music, and how much she got into it. She could tell how elated Rio was as she leaned in to steal a kiss during “Betty”.

When “Illicit Affairs” and “My Tears Ricochet” came on, Agatha sang with emotion from deep within. The line “Don’t call me kid, don’t call me baby” reminded Agatha of how those terms would ring in her ear with a mocking tone, hurting her more and more. Now, every time Rio called Agatha ‘baby,’ it erased a memory of that, mending the scars with love and care. She was carried on those emotional waves through “My Tears Ricochet”, each lyric feeling like she was tossing another shovel full of dirt on the past and screaming for freedom at the sky.  At a couple of points, she caught Rio glancing at her with curiosity. She hoped Rio wouldn’t ask her about them later.

The mood shifted when “Marjorie” started. Agatha knew that Rio and Alice were thinking about Gran, as she saw them sing and sob together. Then, “Willow” brought out the witch in all of them, and they danced through the 1989 era like they were trying to cast a spell.

The music kept them bouncing and singing. At one point, Rio turned and gestured to Agatha as she sang, ‘I can make the bad boys good for the weekend,’ and Agatha laughed with pure happiness. Lastly, they all screamed along to Bad Blood, remembering the events of Thanksgiving and how Lou nearly ruined their day.

Rio squealed with delight at the appearance of the The Tortured Poet’s Department set. The last Eras Tour show Rio had attended didn’t have it.  Despite being sweaty and exhausted, the euphoria and adrenaline fueled her partying. Billy was also having the time of his life. When his current favorite ‘But Daddy I Love Him’ started, he FaceTimed Eddie so they could sing together. The young love made Agatha smile.

‘Who’s Afraid of Little Old Me?’ was another group favorite. Agatha felt as if she had grown up in an asylum, it had definitely felt like a circus. She still had a bite left in her bark, though.

It amused Agatha when she and Rio both changed the pronouns of “The Smallest (wo)Man Who Ever Lived”.. Agatha felt her eyebrows rise when Rio screamed it. She must have been channeling all of her feelings about a certain blonde and it was healing for her. If she had been honest with herself, Agatha would have admitted it was cathartic for her as well. The song drew on memories of both her mother and her ex. Her mother, who had literally sent someone to kill her. And her ex, who had destroyed her and truly ruined a few sparkling summers. Despite the memories and suppressed heartbreak that tried to bubble up, Agatha made sure her facial expressions didn’t give anything away. She didn’t think Rio had noticed anything.

But then, Taylor started “I Can Do It With A Broken Heart.” This song reverberated in the depths of her soul, reflecting the most intimate and painful moments from her youth. Not that Rio knew any of that story.  The story of when someone had told Agatha they would love her for their whole life, and then they very much did not. She wondered if Rio related to the song as much as she did. Or Wanda, for that matter, who returned to her sight at that moment. Wanda had taken Nat’s hand as she and Agatha both yelled, ‘TRY TO COME FOR MY JOB’ at the same time, causing them to giggle afterward.

When it was time for the acoustic songs, Taylor had changed into the pink and orange dress they were all hoping she would wear as she spoke about it being the last show before playing A Place In This World mashed with New Romantics. They all cried at the Long Live/New Year’s Day/The Manuscript mashup, especially when she added the ‘end of an era’ lyric. ‘I had the time of my life fighting dragons with you’ had a special meaning to all of them, and Agatha leaned into Rio and whispered, ‘I want your midnights’ in her ear. When Rio looked into Agatha’s eyes and said, “Never become a stranger whose laugh I could recognize anywhere,” Agatha felt the weight of the implied permanence. Permanence she could handle. It was the depth of feeling she didn’t want to admit.

Still, Agatha shook her head and replied, “Never,” before kissing Rio.

Nicky made a gagging sound behind them.

Taylor dove into the stage and swam off, going into ‘Lavender Haze’. During ‘Vigilante Shit’ the coven exchanged looks and tried not to laugh. ‘Mastermind’ was an Agatha Harkness anthem, if there ever was one, “if you fail to plan, you plan to fail”. Agatha had a carefully constructed plan for nearly everything. She had a Jen, a Natasha, and teams of people. It was something she had learned long ago. She noticed that Rio was analyzing her as they sang. During the final song, ‘Karma’, the boys, Vision, Nat, and Rio, all yelled, ‘girl on the screen coming home to me’. Vision laughed as he and the boys covered Wanda and Nat so they could kiss without being photographed like they were all those years ago. Jen and Alice just enjoyed the fact that they could do what they wanted, and no one paid attention to them.

After the show, Agatha led them all backstage, where they met Taylor, who hugged them all! Rio’s brain buzzed as she looked at the energetic musician, it was completely unfair that someone the same age as her had just done all of that and still looked unphased.

“You must be Rio! It’s great to meet you!”

Taylor Swift knew who she was! If she didn’t have plans for the rest of the night, Rio could die happy.

“That’s me. Great to meet you as well. You were amazing,” She was used to big stars by now, but to her, this was different.

They chatted for a moment and Rio learned that Agatha and Wanda were friends with Taylor. Alice had been holding out on that information, but that explained the fantastic tickets they had last year and for the Reputation tour when she thought about it. They took pictures on all of their phones and Taylor made Agatha and Wanda both promise to post them on their socials and she would post on hers as well for her wrap-up. Taylor had even exchanged numbers with Rio, asked the boys about school and dating, and was thrilled to hear about the updates with Jen and Alice, as well as Wanda and Nat. Of course, Taylor Fucking Swift knew what was going on with Wanda and Nat.

Definitely one of the best days of her life, she thought.

⭐⭐⭐

They had all dozed on their way back to the hotel and departed to their separate rooms upon arrival. Rio was wide awake when they got to the suite, entering after Agatha and closing the door behind her. She had worn high-waisted jeans, a sheer long-sleeved white shirt with a black bra underneath, and now had a Taylor Swift T-shirt on over that. Agatha was wearing a black strapless jumpsuit that Rio had wanted to unzip all night.

Their shoes and bags hit the floor as they walked through the suite and into the bedroom. Rio shut that door behind them as well, placing a scrunchie on both handles, unbeknownst to Agatha. She took Agatha’s hand and pulled Agatha into her. “Thank you for tonight.” she breathed, sliding both hands around her waist and resting them on Agatha’s firm butt.

Agatha put a hand in Rio’s hair, gripping it lightly, fingernails barely scratching Rio’s scalp. She placed the other hand on Rio’s hip, nails slightly pressing through the fabric.

“Thank YOU for going with me, my beautiful girlfriend.” She pulled Rio toward her, sliding their lips together as Rio’s instantly parted for Agatha. “Agatha,”

“Are you sure?”

Rio nodded. “Yes, Agatha.”

Agatha’s eyes were soft, full of warmth and care, something else that Rio was too afraid to name.

“Gladly.”

She went to do the same with the sheer shirt when Rio stopped Agatha’s hands and shook her head with a sly smile. Rio kissed Agatha again before spinning her around, sweeping Agatha’s long hair over her shoulder, unzipping the jumpsuit, following the zipper with kisses while she went, making quick work of the purple strapless bra, flinging it off to the side.

“I thought I was taking YOU to bed,” Agatha said softly.

“Agatha, you’re beautiful.”

She caressed Agatha’s cheek before kissing her again, softly, longingly, conveying things she couldn’t convey in words. A kiss that said I see you.

The words died on her lips as she saw the same want and need mirrored in Agatha’s eyes that Rio felt in her soul. Rio cried out at the sudden sensation and dug nails into Agatha’s back as her muscles clenched around Agatha’s fingers. Agatha thrust hard and fast, curling her fingers forward each time to hit Rio’s front wall. Rio clung to Agatha as she screamed out Agatha’s name and came around her hand. Agatha worked Rio through her climax before withdrawing, kissing Rio slowly.

Rio took Agatha’s hand by the wrist, breaking from Agatha’s kiss. She stared into Agatha's eyes as she licked up her palm, tasting the evidence of herself, before taking Agatha’s fingers between her lips and licking them clean, a smile on Agatha’s lips. As she finished her task, Agatha smirked, “Good girl.” She said as she kissed Rio, fresh evidence of where Rio had licked Agatha’s fingers still on Rio’s tongue, and Agatha moaned at the taste.

Rio took the backs of Agatha’s thighs in her hands and began tugging her upwards. Rio loved the sensation of Agatha on top of her and now she needed her to sit someplace else entirely. Agatha looked at her again, an eyebrow raised in question. Rio darted her tongue across her bottom lip as she nodded. “Agatha, I want to taste you.” it was almost a whine.

“But are you sure you want me to…” Agatha was cut off when Rio crashed their lips together before biting Agatha’s bottom lip just enough that she winced with pleasure. Rio tugged at the back of  Agatha’s thighs again.

“Up here now, Agatha,” she half commanded, half whined.

Agatha crawled up the bed before she carefully settled a knee on either side of Rio’s head, slowly lowered herself onto Rio, and gripped the headboard.

Rio reached for Agatha’s waist and pulled her down so that mouth met labia, and Agatha sighed in satisfied relief as the tip of Rio’s tongue drew lines up and down through her slit. Agatha moaned as she matched Rio’s motions and met the pace, taking control. Rio had always considered herself to be dominant but for Agatha? She was putty in her hands, and she liked it. Rio flattened her tongue, increasing the pressure as Agatha increased her pace. She moved a hand to cup Agatha’s ass, digging her nails in, leaving half-moons in her pale skin. The other hand spread the lips at Agatha’s entrance, allowing her to swirl her tongue, teasing around the outside, before dipping it in as far as she could.

Agatha gasped at the shallow sensation, bucking against Rio’s nose.

Rio knew what Agatha wanted as she moved her tongue back and licked Agatha’s clit in one smooth, long, slow motion as Agatha moaned loudly.

“Oh, Rio, fuck,” she let out as Rio began to alternate between circling the bundle of nerves and licking it.

“As you wish.” She breathed against Agatha’s cunt, causing Agatha to grip the headboard even harder.

Rio knew Agatha was dripping, the evidence on Rio’s chin. The older woman above her was more than ready when Rio pushed three fingers into her.

“RIO, GOD!” She yelled, as she kept one hand on the headboard and gripped the back of Rio’s head with the other, fucking Rio’s face and fingers as hard as she could, moans and curses tumbling from her lips. Rio matched her pace, thrusting hard as she wrapped her lips around Agatha’s clit and sucked. Agatha, came undone around her, her moans and screams quieting as Rio worked her through her climax before carefully removing her fingers and turning her face to kiss Agatha’s thigh, biting it and sucking a bruise there.

Agatha looked down at her, pupils blown, “What are you doing?”

Rio grinned, looking up at her. “Signing my work.”

Agatha smiled and shook her head as she moved to lay beside Rio, kissing her and tasting herself on Rio’s lips. “You think you did that good of a job, huh?”

Rio smirked, “You were pretty loud, so yes.”

“Guilty,” Agatha ran her hand up Rio’s left side before propping herself up and feeling along her ribcage, finding the slightly puckered skin. She stretched across Rio’s body and kissed the scars there.

Rio’s heart was in her throat, and it took her a moment to clear it and find her voice. She had just given herself over to Agatha, mind, body, and soul, and now Agatha was halfway on top of her, kissing the stab wounds from the attack.

“Aggie, what are you doing?” She whispered, trying to hold herself together.

“Making a promise never to hurt you,” she said as she rose and looked Rio in the eyes, before softly kissing her. Rio held her as she kissed her back. This was unlike anything she had ever had before, and she knew it. She rolled them over, Agatha allowing her this time, as Rio propped herself up on her side and ran a hand to Agatha’s right hip, where she had noticed a tattoo on the front of her hipbone earlier. She traced her fingers over the heart with the three swords through it.

“The three of swords. Grief, heartbreak, sorrow,” Rio said, quoting The Witches’ Road. “I didn’t know you had this, and I’ve uh, seen a lot of things where you’re naked,” She blushed, looking at Agatha. They had just had sex, and she was blushing.

Agatha smiled sweetly at her, she was being vulnerable with Rio.

“I’ve always had it covered with makeup or edited out,” she explained. “It’s been for me and me only, really. A reminder of what I’ve been through in life.”

Rio traced it with her fingers, “Grief and sorrow I get. Heartbreak?” She raised her eyebrow at Agatha.

Rio asking her about heartbreak would have been the perfect time to tell her about that time of her life. Right after having sex, however, was not.

“All of those experiences being the heartbreak. My mother dying the way she did, watching Wanda go through what she has, knowing there was nothing I could do for Lorna, my childhood, everything. There’s a lot of heartbreak in this career.” It was another half-truth she could live with.

“I never want to cause you any of those things, just be there for you if you have to face them,” Rio said seriously before kissing her.

As tired as they were, they didn’t get much sleep that night.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha hadn’t been asleep for very long when her alarm went off at five that Monday morning following the concert. She and Rio had slept for maybe an hour. Their naked bodies pressed together. Agatha was wrong about wanting to wake up with Rio in her arms every morning. She wanted to wake up naked with Rio every morning.

“Why is your alarm set?” Rio mumbled, mostly asleep.

“Golden Globe nominations are being announced. Movies up first.”

Agatha rolled back to throw something on and brush her teeth before going to the main area of their suite.

Rio followed her and kissed her with fresh peppermint breath.

Agatha opened the door and entered the main room. Surprised no one was there, she turned back to Rio when she noticed the scrunchie on the handle.

“Rio, did you put one of those on the other handle too?” Agatha asked, going back into the bedroom to retrieve their phones.

“Yes,” she yawned, going ahead and turning on the TV and finding the broadcast.

As expected, Agatha had several texts with a picture of the door, asking if it was safe to come in so they could all watch the announcement broadcast together, an annual tradition. She texted the group chat, telling them it was safe to enter.

“It’s about fucking time!” Wanda breezed in immediately after receiving the text, carrying several coffees in hand.

“Pun intended,” Nat added smirking, carrying more coffee.

“Can we just ignore what happened in here last night?” Nicky asked as he carried in donuts he and the other two boys had gone and retrieved.

“Prepare yourselves to post on socials,” Jen looked at Agatha and Rio, “Shower and look less…like that before posting any pictures, though.”

The announcements started, and they ate donuts and drank their coffee as announcements were made. Death’s Beloved was nominated for Best Motion Picture - Drama, Agatha was nominated for Best Lead Actress in a Drama, and was pissed as she turned towards Wanda, “You deserve to be nominated too. We are BOTH the lead in the movie.”

Wanda shook her head, “I have a better chance in the supporting actress category where I’m not up against you. I’ve never won against you Agatha,” she looked at her best friend in admiration, not jealousy.

Agatha crossed her arms. “That WILL change one day, superstar. You’re so much better than me.” Agatha gave her an adoring look. It wasn’t a competition, but if you asked Agatha, Wanda Maximoff was a thousand times the actress and woman Agatha was.

As predicted, Wanda was nominated for supporting actress. Their director, screenplay, and score were all nominated. The song “My Beloved” was nominated, “If you win this, Aunt Aggie, you should win the Grammy,” Billy said excitedly.

The film was also nominated for cinematic and box office achievement for the records they had broken, especially for being such a hit as a sapphic movie.

Neither of them had TV shows they would be up for that year, so they turned the TV off, not interested in sitting through the rest of the list. Jen would bring them up to speed later.

Jen looked around at everyone. “January 5th for the Globes, so everyone begin working on what you’re wearing and who you’re walking the red carpet with.”

⭐⭐⭐

Once on the plane and headed back to LA, Agatha and Wanda made their celebratory posts about their nominations. Wanda took another group selfie of everyone and posted it along with:

“My family, related and chosen, are so grateful that all of you support me in what I do! Death’s Beloved has been one of the most amazing films I’ve ever gotten to work on, and doing it with Agatha was so special! This is such an honor!”

She had also posted several pictures from the weekend in general. Very cute, very wholesome, very Wanda Maximoff.

Agatha posted pictures from the concert, her and Rio with Taylor, the group with Taylor, just her and Rio, the group on the plane, and her and Rio on the plane.

“This has been such a great weekend! Taylor, you were amazing, as always! We love you, and thank you so much for having us and the fam! Thank you to everyone out there for supporting Death’s Beloved the way you have! We led in nominations, and I am so humbled that I get to do the things I do! I love you all!”

The internet had already been abuzz since last night it seemed, and Jen had already fired up her laptop, fingers flying. The boys were digging through everything they could find, and Nat was making sure there was nothing they didn’t want out there.

“Agatha and Rio Get Cozy at Last Eras Stop”

“Wagatha and Friends Are Swifties!”

“Wagatha’s Boys Are All Grown Up!”

The story headlines themselves were fine. There were plenty of pictures of them all, especially shots looking into the tent, though it didn’t seem any pictures had been taken of Nat and Wanda. For that, they were thankful.

“Rio’s living a damn fairytale, and her Princess Charming is Agatha Harkness.”

“Look at them singing to each other!”

“Agatha is in love with that woman.”

“Yeah, and she’s in love right back.”

“THEY’RE IN LESBIANS!”

“CHAT, my cousin was there, really close to the VIP tent. She said that Wanda and Nat kept being surrounded by Vision and the boys during ‘romantic’ times, and it looked like they were kissing.”

“Who’s Nat?”

“The main bodyguard!! She’s the hot redhead always following them!”

“Oh please, Wanda Maximoff is the straightest woman in the world.”

Agatha cackled from where she had been reading over Rio’s shoulder.

“I think they both should have been nominated for lead! Homophobes.”

“If we complain enough, we could still influence The Oscars.”

The comments and articles went on as Rio put her phone down, leaning into Agatha. They would land soon, and she was exhausted.

Jen had been on the phone and turned to Alice crestfallen.  She removed the device from her ear and whispered in Alice’s ear.

When Alice’s face fell, too, Nat spoke up.   “What happened?” She asked her two covenmates.

“The deal on the house fell through,” Jen said as she took Alice’s hand. “We’ve been putting deals on houses, but we keep being outbid at ridiculous prices.”

The whole mood of the plane had been brought down. Disappointment battled with previous euphoria. Agatha looked at Wanda, shared concern in their eyes. Wanda’s expression suddenly shifted, and Agatha knew immediately where her best friend’s mind had gone. Agatha nodded.

“Hey,” Wanda began. “we have an idea.”

Chapter End Notes

Agatha, girl, I need you to confess.

PLEASE let me know what you think!

Too Soon to Tell You I Love You

Chapter Summary

It's December! A holiday dance at SHIELD, Christmas...

New Year's.

Then there's the SF of it all.

Chapter Notes

This is a very long chapter! I hope the week-long wait is okay when it's a 15k-word chapter. Broken up for easier reading! It's another emotional roller coaster!

Please enjoy!

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Jen had been looking at houses for the past year, knowing she wanted a bigger place. Something had told her she would eventually need the added space, and finally, dating Alice solidified it. Asking Alice to move in with her felt natural, but she didn’t think finding something in their flexible price range would be hard. They were being grossly outbid and refused to pay the outrageously inflated prices. Jen was becoming suspicious and didn’t want to play this game. Getting another phone call that their offer had fallen through didn’t exactly surprise her, and she was ready to make a voodoo doll of a very specific blonde model.

She had always suspected that Wagatha was damn near telepathic, but watching their silent exchanges was still slightly unnerving. Wanda turned to Jen and Alice.

“We have an idea.” She had said, contemplating.

Jen threw up an eyebrow in question, though she had an idea she already knew.

“Build a house on the compound. There’s plenty of room.” Wanda finished.

“Who the fuck are you, and what have you done with Agatha?” Jen turned to Agatha, eyebrow still raised. She loved Agatha, sure, but they tolerated each other the way you tolerated your older sister, whom you cleaned up after.

Agatha smirked. “We wouldn’t live right next door. You could even live further to the back of the property. We’re already clearing land for another house anyway. Either a guest house or if Vision wants to stay within the castle walls.”

Vision’s head snapped up from where he had been helping Tommy with schoolwork, “You’re building a just-in-case house?” That was a complete surprise to him.

“Yes.” Agatha, Wanda, and Nat all replied at the same time.

“Darling, there’s a house near the compound I may be interested in, and it’s close enough for me. I am not a lesbian and do not feel the need to still live within 300 yards of my ex-wife.” Vision turned to look at Wanda.

“We’ll discuss it later?”

Vision nodded as the twins looked at each other. Their parents, biological or otherwise, never argued in front of them.

Alice looked at everyone, “So, one big happy, mostly queer, family compound?” She wasn’t going to add the almost , though she had thought it.

The boys had it covered, though, “Just missing two people and a cat.” Nicky mused with a sly grin.

Rio’s eyes went wide. “Uh, that is…not, we’re not even…” she stammered. These were inside thoughts for her.

Agatha did her best to keep a blank face, looking at Rio next to her and then back to everyone else.

“We’re not there yet,” she stated simply.

Wanda scoffed before adding, “Once the divorce is finalized,” she looked over at Vision. “Sorry, dear.”

Vision just shrugged and waved it off. “No need to apologize, Darling.”

“Then, Nat will move in.” Wanda looked over at Nat, smiling brightly. She had waited to live with her again for twenty years.

As the plane began to descend in LA, the women discussed contractors while the boys continued to check the home network and various devices, employing the background bots and light hacking skills Nat had taught them how to use to monitor for any possible leaks and to delete what they could.

⭐⭐⭐

Nat looked into the very suspicious bidding wars once they had returned home. She wasn’t sure how news was leaking to SF, but it was, and she didn’t like it. Nat didn’t like making people completely disappear, but she was beginning to think this was a safety issue, so she made a note to keep track of who actually bought the properties and who, if anyone, moved in. Nat was also closely monitoring the woman, who hadn’t been released yet, though she knew the model’s lawyers were working on it, and it was just a matter of time. The head of Wagatha, and coven, security had to make a decision, and quickly as Lou was slowly going after more than just Rio. Nat knew what this woman was doing, and it was psychological warfare.

Two could play that game, Nat thought, as she dialed a number to an old acquaintance. “Ya sobirayu eto odolzhenye.” She spoke quickly in Russian. I’m collecting on that favor.

⭐⭐⭐

The fact that Lilia had let Alice and Rio off for two days that week had shocked Rio. How was it that all it took was a mention of Agatha, and the permission was granted? It was finals week, and the winter formal was on Saturday. There had been a few mutterings about favoritism, but they died quickly after Lilia popped into those specific classrooms. What this meant for the teachers of the coven was that they had to hit the ground running Wednesday morning and administer exams Wednesday and Thursday.

This was why, after having spent Monday and Tuesday with Agatha, Rio needed to go home. It was four in the afternoon, and she had to prepare for the next day. She had been home to check on Raven, but she knew her cat and knew she needed actually to be home.

They were lying in Agatha’s bed, facing each other. Rio was twirling strands of Agatha’s hair around her fingers, and Agatha lightly traced her fingers over Rio’s scars.

“Aggie, I really need to head home.”

“I remember you saying that two hours ago,” Agatha smirked.

“I did, and then a very beautiful cougar of a woman pushed me against a wall, kissed me, and took me to her bed.”

Agatha clicked her tongue. “You’ve got to watch out for those cougars. I hear they’re bad in these Hollywood hills,” Agatha moved forward and kissed Rio. “Stay the night again?” She asked as she moved back.

Rio gave a small smile. “No, I don’t have everything for tomorrow, and I need to be home for Raven occasionally. Come over tonight? Nicky could stay with Wanda?” Rio countered.

Agatha knew that Nicky could stay by himself or with Wanda, but it was a school night, he had finals tomorrow, and routine was important. If only she could admit her feelings or be an adult about literally any of this. However, she could not. Instead, she found herself giving Rio a sad smile as she saw the disappointment in Rio’s eyes. “I’m sorry, babe, it’s a school night, and I have to keep up the routine while I can. I film the next three days, and then we will film in Canada, starting a few days after the Globes and going for three weeks.”

Rio had completely forgotten about Canada. “Will you be in Canada the entire time?” Rio whispered, feeling preemptively sad. She knew she was being ridiculous.

“I’ll be home Friday afternoon and leave out on Sunday evening. It’s just for three weeks. Disney reads start in May. Marvel things start shortly after that, and then they’ll revolve around each other. For the TV show, we’ll see if more episodes are ordered, but it premieres in March as a shortened season. I digress. I’ll have more time off in February, but just a few weeks.” Agatha explained, steeling herself. They were starting to get to the difficult part of their relationship, Agatha’s job.

Rio nodded. “We have Winter and Spring breaks together, though, right?” she asked hopefully.

Agatha smiled, “Of course. Now, before you leave me and I don’t see you until the weekend, come take a shower with me.”

Once Rio had gotten home that evening, she had quite a bit to do to readjust to being home. She still had to unpack, clean out Raven’s feeder, and give the feline much-needed attention, chores, and all the things that had been neglected since Saturday. Alice had come over to oversee everything, and Rio had put her to work folding clothes.

“Are you and Jen going to go through with it? Build a house on the compound?” Rio asked from the kitchen as she prepped their lunches for the rest of the week.

“We talked about it and decided it’s the best option. Her townhouse isn’t big enough for both of us. Hell, her bathroom is barely big enough for her skin routine.” Alice laughed at her girlfriend’s preferences. “Wanda and Agatha are her only clients, so that makes sense. Then she’ll still have her office for meetings with studio heads or if she needs a quieter space than the compound.”

Rio was disappointed that her best friend and sister would be moving out, though it would hopefully be months before it happened. The question she wanted to ask nagged at the back of her mind, and she knew she would find out eventually, so she might as well ask.

“Did Nat?” She looked at Alice, not even finishing the rest.

Alice nodded.

“Yes. Bitch put in bids and drove prices up, but nothing had been finalized. Nothing else from there.” She kept her expressions neutral and her voice even. She didn’t want Rio to worry.

Rio put the excess food containers in the refrigerator before turning off the light and joining Alice in the living room to take clothes to her bedroom to put away, Alice following her.

“Thanks for your help, Alice.” She started, “As for the problem, how did she know? How is she doing all this?” SF was still in some facility. It had been in news articles and tabloids that she had lost the kids and been ‘sent to work on herself after a mental breakdown due to stress.’ Different rumors were leaking, and the current plan was to ensure no one connected Rio to the supermodel.

Alice shrugged, “That’s what Jen and Nat are trying to find out.” Alice stretched out across Rio’s bed after helping her with all the chores. “Rio?” Her voice had turned serious.

Rio hung up the last piece of clothing and shut the closet door, lying down next to Alice. “Yeah?” The tone of Rio’s voice matched Alice’s.

“Do you still have all of Gran’s jewelry?” Alice turned her head to face Rio.

Rio was already turned toward Alice, a question on her face.

“Yes, why?”

When Gran died, Alice and Rio donated or sold almost everything, keeping only that which was sentimental or a family heirloom. Rio, being the more responsible of the two, stalker issue aside, had held on to the jewelry and had it in a safe with important documents.

Alice was trying to choose words carefully, as she wasn’t completely sure herself. “Just making sure is all,” she replied.

“Wait! Do you need the rings? Are you going to propose to Jen?” Rio asked excitedly.

Alice huffed a laugh. “I don’t know. I just wanted to make sure we had them.” She chewed on the inside of her cheek. “I know I want to spend the rest of my life with her, whether that’s marriage or not. I often wonder what would have happened if I hadn’t been an idiot for a decade, where we would be by now. I never wanted kids. I have plenty of those at work, but we could have four dogs by now.”

Rio raised her eyebrows as her forehead crinkled, “Jennifer Kale does not seem like a dog person to me.” She thought for a moment. “Well, maybe one of those little yappy purse dogs.”

Alice gave a small smile, “You’d be surprised, dogs, cats, she likes to hike, not the primadonna everyone thinks she is. We all have layers, Ri.”

“I know that. Everyone’s just full of surprises around here. Anyway, if you need Gran’s rings, I have them. Also, like I said before, let me know if you want me to wear a suit or a dress and if Agatha is the Best Man or not.” Rio grinned at the woman she considered her sister.

“Good thing I love you, you ass. Speaking of love…”

Rio cut her off with a groan. “Don’t start. I care about her, okay? Can we leave it at that? It’s been four and a half months. It’s too soon.”

“It’s not, really. When you know, you know. You’re basing too many things off of she-who-must-not-be-named. Love doesn’t have an incubation period.” Alice had been looking at Rio seriously, trying to get the words to seep through her thick skull.

Rio stared up at her ceiling fan and blew out a breath as she ran her hand through her thick hair, deep in thought.

“What if I’m not ready?” she turned her head back to meet Alice’s eyes.

“That is different than saying it’s too soon or denying it. Everyone around you two sees it. Not being ready to go there is one thing, but at least let yourself feel and accept it. Let her in. Let Agatha Fucking Harkness love you and help heal you. YOU’RE not broken, Rio. You never have been. People chose to do terrible things to you, ripped your heart out, and left you to pick up the pieces. You have someone who will gladly help put it back together and make you realize how worthy and deserving of love you are,” Alice put her hand on Rio’s arm. “Let her. She would do anything for you. Let her.”

Rio’s eyes were glassy when Alice finished. “I’m still not ready to admit anything, but I’m ready to be more forthcoming and stop keeping secrets. I can start there, at least.” She covered Alice’s hand with hers. “I’m so glad our preteen angst matched each other.”

Alice let out a small laugh. “You’d be lost without me.”

“Worse.”

Rio lay in bed, Alice’s words looped in her head. She would not admit any feelings to Agatha, but she would be more honest. That was a good first step. She petted the cat that slept and purred on her chest, speaking out loud.

“Raven, you know I love you, but there’s a woman I’d like in my bed, and it’s not you.”

The immortal cat opened her eyes and emitted a low growl before returning to sleep.

Rio picked up her phone, careful not to disturb the queen.

Me:

I have to chaperone the

winter formal on Saturday

I’ll miss movie night.

Agatha 💜

Looks like I’ll be chaperoning

the dance, too, then.

Agatha 💜

Will you go to the dance

with me?

Me:

Did you just ask me

to a High School dance?

Agatha 💜

I did. I’ve never been to one.

It could be fun.

Me:

Just a warning, I do dance

with the goal to embarrass

my students. I also hunt down

the ones that sneak off.

Me:

Which will include our son

and nephew.

Rio had sent the text and didn’t even catch what she had called Nicky before it was too late. She could edit it, but it was already marked as read. It was too late, and sending an “I mean your” text wouldn’t change it. She didn’t realize she was holding her breath as the three dots popped up and disappeared repeatedly until her lungs started to burn from the lack of oxygen, and Raven’s head popped up with alarm. Rio returned to breathing as Agatha finally landed on a reply.

Agatha 💜

Poor kid out here

collecting parents like

they’re participation trophies.

Meanwhile, he’s just trying to get

Tony to let him TAKE Morgan to

The dance.

Sarcasm, classic Agatha. Rio could handle that and found she preferred it at this moment. Raven had settled back down, knowing now that her human was not dying.

Me:

What do you mean? Why?

Agatha 💜

Nicky wants to drive now that

he has a license. Pepper is fine

with it.

Me:

Don’t worry about it.

Agatha 💜

He even said “He’s a Harkness,

what if he’s like his mother?”

To which I said, “Well thankfully,

Morgan IS like her mother.”

To which Pepper laughed.

Me:

Aggie. You can’t taunt him.

Agatha 💜

Anyway, Nicky’s crushed.

Me:

I said I’d take care of it.

Agatha 💜

You really don’t have to.

Me:

Don’t worry about it.

Goodnight Aggie.

Agatha 💜

We’ll see what happens.

Good night, Ri.

Rio looked at Raven and smirked. She knew she would fit the crazy cat lady stereotype if anyone ever overheard exactly how much she spoke to Raven.

“She doesn’t understand how much these parents listen to me.”

She leaned forward and kissed Raven on the head before rolling onto her side, holding the cat in place so she shifted instead of falling.

⭐⭐⭐

Seeing Rio call Nicky ‘our son’ had made Agatha’s heart leap. Yes, it had happened before, and yes, it was scary, but it also made her feel like maybe she could do this again. She sighed, putting her phone on the nightstand and looking to her left, where Rio had been the previous night. Agatha never imagined she would ever want to consistently share a bed or night with someone again, let alone monogamously. She lay there, staring at the shadows on the ceiling as she was flooded with memories of her ex, her hand unconsciously touching the tattoo of Swords on her hip.

“Stay the night? Please?” Agatha had asked. She was twenty-three, and after years of Agatha chasing her, Swords had finally given Agatha a chance.

Swords looked her up and down. She was so much older than Agatha and made Agatha painfully aware of it.

“I don’t stay the night, kid. I also don’t do monogamous. So don’t think I’m going to start now.”

Agatha was crushed, but she wasn’t going to cry. Not in front of Swords. She couldn’t look like a baby in front of her. She just nodded.

“Right. Don’t stay the night. Don’t give the paparazzi anything to see.” Swords was a mentor, after all.

“Precisely, good job.” Swords kissed her in a way that left Agatha feeling more owned than loved.

Another memory, 10 years later.

“Agatha, I’m too old to have or raise a child, and with your schedule? I would be a primary parent. People would absolutely know we were together then.” Swords scoffed after Agatha brought it up yet again.

“You’re right about me. I can’t be a good parent with this life.” Agatha conceded.

“You can’t risk being your mother.” Swords reminded her.

Agatha winced at the blow. Another point Swords had used to talk Agatha out of having kids.

Agatha didn’t cry at these memories anymore. The ache remained, but the sting was gone. She had turned them off to keep going on with her life. Instead, she thought about how exactly Rio would pull off getting Tony to agree to Nicky taking Morgan to the dance. Rio. This woman had come into Agatha’s life and turned it upside down in the best of ways. Yes, she had unknowingly brought a stalker with her, but that they could handle. Agatha’s heart was healing after eighteen years. She was learning to care deeply about someone who wasn’t her family again. If she could just share the trauma of her past, she would be ready to admit her actual feelings. First, she needed Rio to stay over more. She pulled out her phone again and ordered what she needed to make her home Raven-friendly.

⭐⭐⭐

Rio met Tony Stark Wednesday afternoon when he picked Morgan up from basketball practice. He was standing in front of the gym a few minutes before practice was scheduled to let out, and she walked up to him wearing a bright smile.

“Hey, Tony! Morgan did great on her semester final today. I just finished grading it.” Rio said, stopping in front of him. It was true Morgan had crushed the paper portion, and Rio expected she would do just as well on the lab portion the following day.

Tony flashed a smile at Rio.

“Hey Rio, She’s actually been studying with Nicky,” he sounded surprised, “he’s Agatha’s kid, so of course he’s smart. I just thought I’d have to separate them more.”

“Because he’s Agatha’s son?” Rio raised an eyebrow questioningly.

Tony suddenly remembered that Rio was dating Agatha but didn’t let his demeanor change.

“She’s your girlfriend. Tell me I’m wrong to think that.”

Rio had hoped he would reply that way.

“Did Pepper worry that Morgan would run through teenage boys like you ran through women?”

Shock crossed Tony’s face before he was able to control it. He had not been expecting that from her. “Okay, maybe I deserved that”

“Nicky is a great kid, Tony. I’m sorry for whatever past transpired with you and Agatha but, it’s been years. More importantly, it has nothing to do with Nicky. Leave the kid out of it, please..” She was still smiling, and her tone was still very friendly.

Tony felt zero animosity towards Rio, even though he knew he should.

“They’re still teenagers. What about him wanting to drive?”

“Didn’t you at that age? Or did you want your dad’s drivers watching your every move? He just got his license, Tony. It’s one of the few times he can drive other minors without an adult. Yes, they’re teenagers. Smart ones that were raised well. Pepper did a good job with Morgan, and Wanda had a fabulous influence on Nicky.” Rio knew that while Tony found Agatha difficult to work with, he adored Wanda.

Tony nodded as Morgan burst out of the gym doors, waving to the other girls on the team and walking to where her father and teacher stood, eyeing them both. “Does this mean I failed?” Her eyes darted between both of them nervously.

“Yes, you’re grounded.” Tony said, and Rio answered, “You crushed it, Morgan!”

Rio looked at Tony and rolled her eyes.

“No, you did a great job, Maguna. I’m proud of you.” Tony smiled at his daughter. Tony, an arrogant Hollywood heir and proud dad. He turned to Rio and let out a defeated sigh.

“I’m only doing this because you made a compelling argument.” he turned back to the sixteen-year-old, who looked at him questioningly. “Call your boyfriend and tell him to look decent for the dance on Saturday.”

Morgan’s face lit up as she hugged her dad and then Rio before leading her dad away, telling him about the dance’s theme before calling Nicky.

Rio smiled as she walked back to her classroom before meeting up with Alice. They both were staying late. She entered her classroom as she received a text and grinned.

Agatha 💜

You’re Nicky’s favorite person.

Morgan told him it was you.

Rio shouldn’t have been surprised. Teenagers were fast, and of course, he had already messaged Agatha about it.

Me:

I told you I’d handle Tony.

Agatha 💜

Thank you for that.

He’s extremely excited about driving.

Have to get back to filming.

Agatha sent the text and had been so close to typing something else but didn’t. Not before she told Rio everything, especially not in a text, that was immature and stupid. No, she pushed that feeling down and back into its box where it needed to stay. Instead, she typed something much safer.

Agatha 💜

You’re my favorite person, by the way.

There. That was safe, but she didn’t want to see the reply right now. Instead, she put her phone away and walked back onto the set.

Rio saw the text, and a giant smile crept onto her face.

Me:

I need a plaque that says

“Favorite Woman of the Harkness

Family”

Achievement of a lifetime.

Me:

You’re my favorite too, Agatha.

She was still smiling as she put her phone in her pocket. She gathered her backpack and lunchbox when she heard footsteps.

“Almost ready, Alice,” She said, turning as her eyes landed on Lilia. “Oh, Ms. Calderu! I thought you’d have left by now.” It was five, and while it was finals week, Rio expected teachers and coaches to be at school, not her boss.

Lilia gave her a polite smile that didn’t quite reach her eyes as she took her usual spot on top of a desk and gestured for Rio to sit. Rio was automatically filled with panic as Lilia never stayed for very long as she perched on a stool.

“I saw you speaking with Tony Stark.” It was a statement, not a question.

“Yes, ma’am, Nicky wanted to take…” Rio was interrupted by Lilia before she could explain.

“I know the situation.” Lilia crossed her legs at the knee, clasping her hands over where they connected. This woman had to be psychic, how could she know already too?

“Rio, I’m happy for you both and the way you handled Tony. Only a few can do so, myself included. Just remember that you have to put your own oxygen mask on first. Has Agatha put hers on?” She stood up and glided towards the open door before turning back to Rio, “I’m sorry if I worried you, but you already had that heavy backpack on, and I didn’t want you to hurt your back. You’re rubbing the left side of your chest. It’s your tell when you're nervous.” She turned back to the door, “See you at the dance on Saturday. Tell Agatha to wear a shirt under her suit. Four school days to go!” She called as she left.

Rio sat on the stool for a few minutes. Oxygen masks? Has Agatha put hers on? The fuck? She blew a breath out between her lips as Alice came in.

“What was that about? I saw Calderu come out of here.” She gestured with her head for them both to leave.

Rio rose from the stool and followed Alice out of the building. She recapped what Lilia had said, even about her nervous stim.

“Oh, you always do that as a self-soothing technique. Since we were kids.” Alice noted.

“What do you think she means?” Rio asked as she put her seatbelt on and started the car.

Alice just shrugged. “Who knows? She’s known Agatha forever and knows everything in general anyway. The vague comments don’t help.”

Rio thought as she drove. Lilia’s sporadic appearances and cryptic ‘advice’ were slightly unnerving to her. She shrugged it off. The next Tuesday would be the last day of school for the semester, and they would have a break. She could work through whatever this was then. “You’re right, Nicky’s gone to SHIELD since Pre-K.” She acknowledged.

“Oh, Lilia and Agatha were friends before then.” Alice supplied as she texted Jen.

Rio raised an eyebrow. She vaguely remembered Alice mentioning that once but had forgotten the context.

“Before SHIELD was as big as it is, not that it’s huge, some teachers would privately tutor child stars. Lilia was one of Agatha’s teachers. That’s how Lilia hooked Agatha up with the right lawyers during the trial when Agatha was sixteen.”

Alice reminded Rio.

Rio had forgotten that Lilia had been the one to help Agatha out all those lifetimes ago.

“Oh, right. She does know a lot about Agatha then. No wonder she’s been dropping random things this semester and winning so many of the dating pool bets.” Rio laughed.

“Bitch won the whole damn thing because she knows your story too!” Alice groaned.

“Sorry, there, sister-friend. No one beats Lilia Calderu, apparently.”

⭐⭐⭐

Finals week had ended and things had gone well. Rio and Agatha hadn’t seen each other since Tuesday and Rio felt very teenage girl about it. She had just gotten home and set her school bag down when she heard the text alert.

Agatha 💜

Come over, bring Raven.

You’re staying all weekend.

Bring what you need for Monday.

Please.

Rio looked around at her home, she didn’t have any chores to do, and laundry was caught up. Taking Raven was such a hassle though, it was like packing for an infant. She also needed to start keeping clothes at Agatha’s.

Me:

Raven has more paraphernalia

than an infant.

Agatha 💜

I said Raven, not her

accessories. I may have

done some shopping.

Agatha then sent pictures of a litterbox, and the pet cameras that were already set up in various rooms so Rio could still check on the cat, auto feeder, and Raven’s food. Rio was impressed as she felt her heart melt. This woman knew exactly how to get to her.

Me:

I see how it is. I

win over your son,

you win over my cat?

Agatha 💜

I think we’re past that part.

I just know she’s an integral

part of you staying here for

more than one night at a

time.

Hadn’t Agatha figured out by now that she had won Rio over? She was right about Raven, though. Even if Rio checked in on the ancient cat while she was at Agatha’s. She smiled as she went to pack her bag.

Raven wasn’t happy about the car ride, but when she found Nicky’s room, she forgave Rio. Rio dropped her bag and dress off in Agatha’s room and returned to the kitchen, where Agatha had dinner ready.

“Is Nicky at the clubhouse?” Rio gave Agatha a chaste kiss as she took their plates to the table.

Agatha grabbed their drinks and followed, “No, he took Morgan out on a date.”

Rio had a look of shock on her face as she placed their plates on the table and lowered herself to her seat. “A date tonight AND a dance tomorrow? I AM good!”

Agatha let out a breathy laugh, “According to Pepper, Morgan told Tony she REALLY likes Nicky, so they are going on a double date. Chaperoned by Tony and Pepper.”

Rio placed her hand over her mouth as she tried to suppress her laughter, and failed. “Oh no, poor Nicky.” Rio let out in between breaths.

Rio’s laughter was contagious, and Agatha began to laugh as well. “He made Nicky drive so he could judge his skills,” Agatha added.

Rio’s laughter died down, “The poor boy. That will cause anxiety.”

Agatha had taken a bite of the steak taco she had made and was still chewing as she nodded and swallowed.

“He was still muttering ‘hands at ten and two’ when he left.” She looked at Rio’s plate, “How are you finished? You were talking and laughing!” She exclaimed, laughing herself. She was adjusting to Rio’s quirks and found them adorable.

Rio shrugged, “It just happens! Raven helped, too.”

Agatha looked down to see the cat gazing up at her, licking her lips. “You’re worse than a dog.” She remarked.

“Those are fighting words,” Rio retorted.

Agatha found that being with Rio was easy. Their conversations, banter, choosing movies, and even just how they fit together when they sat and cuddled. It had been this way since they met, and Agatha knew how rare that was. Even when they made…uh had sex, everything came easily. It was like they already knew each other’s bodies and preferences. Agatha had never truly believed in the concept of soulmates. That theory had been shot down when she was young.

They had been lying in bed at Sword’s ‘secret’ apartment where they would meet. Agatha was 25 and traced the older woman’s collarbone with her fingers as their eyes met. Swords was actually looking at her lovingly, and Agatha blushed.

“What are you thinking, my pet?” Swords had asked.

Agatha was afraid to tell her but did it anyway.

“Do you believe in soulmates?” The younger woman whispered timidly.

Swords’ eyes filled with sadness for Agatha as she suddenly sat up and looked at her, gathering the blankets in front of her naked chest.

“Agatha, we are not soulmates.” She said firmly.

Agatha looked away. She had sat up as well, pulling her knees close to her chest. “I didn’t say that we were. I was just asking what your thoughts were on the subject.” Agatha had fixed her expression and no longer felt like she would cry as she turned back to look at the woman she had loved for years.

“I don’t believe in soulmates, Agatha.” Swords said as if she were slamming a door closed on a conversation. Her face softened a bit.

“As famous as you are and will continue to be, Agatha? If you remain in this career? You shouldn’t either.”

Rio shifted in Agatha’s arms as she slept, bringing Agatha out of her memories. The slants of moonlight highlighted the scars on Rio’s side. Agatha lightly ran her fingers along Rio’s ribcage, causing her to nuzzle further into Agatha. Agatha was having a difficult time finding sleep as she was plagued with guilt. She pulled Rio closer and whispered.

“Please don’t become another scar.”

⭐⭐⭐

The coven had arrived to chaperone the winter formal before the boys did and had taken the opportunity to speak with other parents. When the boys and their dates arrived, the coven had taken pictures, much to the teens’ dismay. Morgan’s dark brown hair was down in tight curls, and she wore a strapless floor-length red dress with black heels. Tommy’s date, Cassie Lang, had her light brown hair pulled up, her green halter dress stopped just below the knee, and with black ballet flats. The boys wore black suits and bowties that matched their date’s dresses. Billy and Eddie complemented each other in shades of blue.

The dance went well. Rio did her best to sing loudly and embarrass her students. For a few minutes, no one could find four of the six teens, but they returned, saying they had gone to the restroom.

Rio watched as the students got too close to each other or tried to sneak off to dark corners, and she went to haul them back to a more populated area.

Agatha laughed. “Is this what teaching is like?”

“You know, it’s not much different. With this age group, it really is 65% keeping them from touching each other.” Rio laughed as she shot a look at a few students lingering by the refreshments.

“Thankfully, these guys aren’t huge into fighting,” Alice added.

“I fought a lot,” Nat remembered.

“Shocker,” Wanda smirked.

“You’re all standing too close. Leave room for Jesus.” Jen barked, causing Wanda and Nat to step apart, Alice to roll her eyes, and Agatha to pull Rio into her.

The last song, a slow song popular with the kids, played when Agatha noticed that Nicky had pulled something out of his pocket. He saw that his mom was watching him, so he grinned and rolled his eyes toward the ceiling, clicking the button. Pounds of white glitter, confetti, streamers, and feathers shot out of cannons above them. While the sounds were startling, once it was discovered what was happening, people laughed, throwing handfuls of confetti and glitter at each other. It was a harmless prank, though a few people slipped, but nothing too bad, Agatha thought.

Agatha leaned toward Wanda, “These are the pranks I can handle.”

Wanda sighed, “I was worried about them stealing chemicals from Rio’s lab.”

Nicky and Tommy had overheard them as they walked up, both looking hurt at the admission.

“We would never do that Mom! Well, not to Rio, specifically.” Nicky said as Tommy nodded. Agatha hugged them all as everything ended, and the gym lights came back on.

“You’re decent kids,” Agatha told them.

“Just decent?” Nicky grinned.

“Don’t push it,” She said sarcastically.

“Can I drive everyone home, Mom? I promise to drive the girls and Eddie straight home and then the three of us, please? I have Mr. Stark’s permission. If it’s okay with the other parents?” Nicky asked.

Agatha looked over at Wanda. She knew that Nicky was a rather safe driver and had become pretty responsible, minor pranks aside. “I’m okay with it, Wanda?”

“I trust him with the twins. Just make sure the other adults agree.” Wanda nodded, her voice slightly nervous as she looked at Nat, who squeezed her hand and nodded in agreement.

Agatha inhaled deeply before turning to Rio. She knew this was an important step, and she could easily twist it to be less than what it actually was. “Thoughts? These are your kids, after all, and I know you care about them. You also know Nicky’s reaction times.”

Rio was slightly shocked, this wasn’t her decision to make, and she saw through Agatha’s facade. Agatha was sharing a parenting decision with Rio and testing the waters.

“Nicky can handle five other people in the car and tune out distractions if you ask my professional opinion. As your girlfriend, I say let him have this.” She smiled and kissed Agatha.

Eddie and Cassie’s parents had agreed, and the six teens left. They were in a rather large, safe SUV, and no one was too worried as the roads were dry, and even though it was dark, the sky was clear.

The women had stayed to help clean up as Lilia pulled Agatha aside.

“She’s been hurt, and if you’re not completely honest with her, it will only cause more damage.” The older woman mused as she swept up glitter.

Agatha scoffed. “What would you know about honesty?”

“I’ve grown a lot as I’ve aged. Now, if you’ll excuse me, your son’s prank caused a few slight injuries. Glitter and eyeballs don’t mix, and I need to find Jen and do damage control.” Lilia handed the broom and dustpan to Agatha and hurried away.

Agatha looked after her and shook her head as Wanda drew up beside her. “What was that about?”

“Our sons’ prank caused minor eye damage. A few kids got glitter in their eyes.” Agatha said flatly.

Wanda shrugged, “We’ve had glitter in our eyes plenty of times. I’m more worried about the kids that slipped on feathers.” Wanda looked around. Everyone was fine and had a good time, and no one was seriously injured. “Lilia’s just bent out of shape because she didn’t catch them first.”

Agatha half-smirked. “She does hate it when they get one over on her.”



⭐⭐⭐

They were back at Agatha’s, comfortable clothes on, as Jen ranted about having to talk a few parents down from their precious babies having glitter in their eyes or slipping on something.

“It’s glitter! They’re always around glitter! These kids are idiots! They’re more likely to do something a hundred times dumber at school. Most of them were laughing about it!” Her hands were flying through the air as Alice sat next to her, drinking some fancy craft beer, trying not to laugh.

“Babe, she’s just trying to make sure there are no slip-ups or cracks. She’s been pretty tightly wound since Agatha filled her in on everything.” Alice put a comforting hand on her girlfriend's thigh as Jen relaxed slightly.

Nat’s phone rang, and she left the room, speaking quietly in Russian. Wanda and Agatha were straining to hear.

“I think it was cute, and the pictures came out great. Agatha, Rogers sent me this one of us,” Rio leaned over to show Agatha a picture of glitter swirling around them as they looked at each other, laughing. “Peggy took it, obviously.”

Nat came rushing back in. Agatha and Wanda hadn’t been able to hear her side of the conversation.

“SF was released this morning. No one knows where she went. She’s not in San Francisco.” She was trying to keep a calm face, but her eyes were full of panic.

Jen looked at her phone to check the time and went back to the others.

“Why aren’t the boys back? It’s not like them to not check-in.”

Agatha felt dread seep into her bones as her phone rang at the same time Wanda’s did. They locked eyes as they both answered them with shaky hands. They had read the names on their caller IDs.

Cedars-Sinai Medical Center.

Agatha answered as a nurse explained that Nicky had been in an accident with three other people, two boys and a girl, Morgan. She would be the last one to take home , Agatha thought. They would get more details at the hospital, but they needed to get there as soon as possible.

They had already been on the move once their phones had rung. Nat called Vision to meet them at the hospital as he was out with friends. Jen and Alice were getting keys to the Tahoe so they could all go in one vehicle.

Nat drove, and no one commented on their speed or the fact that zero traffic laws were being obeyed. Wanda sat in front, white-knuckling Nat’s thigh, face pale. Agatha looked like a ghost, and Rio wrapped her arms around her, trying to provide some sort of comfort. Jen and Alice had remained quiet, Jen looking for news stories and searching police scanners.

They ran into the emergency room, where a nurse opened a door and pointed down a hallway to a set of chairs where Pepper sat with Morgan and Tommy. Morgan was wearing a pair of sweats that her parents had most likely brought her and had a small bandage on her forehead. Tommy had taken his suit jacket off, and his shirt had some dried blood on it. He had a few cuts on his face, and his wrist was in an ace bandage. Tony leaned against a wall.

“It’s about fucking time!” Tony Spat. “None of your phones were working!”

Morgan nodded. “The boys' phones auto-dialed when we were in the crash, and nothing happened.”

Tommy jumped from his seat to run to his mom when he heard Tony speak.

“Mom! It happened so fast, Billy.” He started sobbing into his Mom’s hair.

Agatha looked around for the other two boys, grabbing Rio’s hand and clearing the lump in her throat before asking.

“Where’s Nicky and Billy?”

“Dad!” Tommy shouted as Vision joined them and hugged his father, still crying.

Wanda held on to Nat, not giving a single fuck what anyone saw or thought.

Pepper had started to speak when a doctor came through the double doors and looked at the large group that had gathered.

“Ms. Harkness, Mr. and Mrs. Maximoff, may I speak to the three of you?” The surgeon asked asked them.

Agatha kept hold of Rio’s hand as she walked a few steps off with him. He introduced himself as Dr. Walker, and Wanda held on to Nat, though Vision told Tommy to remain with the others. Walker lifted an eyebrow at the addition of the other two women but said nothing.

“Ms. Harkness, Nicky received the most impact. When he swerved to miss the other car and was essentially run off the road, the front of the vehicle hit a tree, causing the airbags to inflate. He crossed his arms in front of his face, which, upon impact with the airbags, broke his left arm. He also has scratches from the glass, which were treated. We’re watching his head for any brain bleeding, and he’s in surgery. His appendix ruptured, and he has three broken ribs and a punctured lung. But everything is going well.”

Agatha wasn’t sure how she was still breathing or standing. Tears were running down her cheeks as she sobbed and turned into Rio.

Rio held her tightly, tears running down her face as well, but trying to hold it together for Agatha.

Dr. Walker looked at the Maximoffs. Wanda was crying for Nicky, her hand on Agatha’s back. “Tommy has a wrist sprain, and he protected the girl, Morgan.” He turned back toward Agatha, “Morgan has a cut above her eye, but that’s it. Make sure to ask her why. You’ll be proud of your son, Ms. Harkness.” He turned back to the three adults before him. “Billy hit his head hard against the glass. He has a pretty serious head injury and is unconscious right now, but he’ll be okay. He’s had stitches, and we’re watching for brain swelling. He’ll need to be here for a day or so.”

He nodded to both families and went back to the operating room. Wanda was relieved and felt terrible for feeling that way. They rejoined the others, and Nat relayed the information to everyone. Rio looked at Morgan, her face a giant question mark.

“Nicky wouldn’t let me sit in the front seat. We dropped Cassie and Eddie off, and then he made me get in the back. He said the drive to my place had a potential for deer, and the roads were darker, so no one was allowed to sit up front. Then, when the car came into our lane, and Nicky veered off the road so we wouldn’t hit it, he yelled, ‘Tommy, get Morgan,’ and Tommy grabbed me, which is why his head didn’t go into the glass like Billy’s did. However, I did take some glass to the forehead.” Morgan touched the bandage on her head gingerly.

Agatha’s hand was over her mouth as Pepper stood and hugged her.

“He’s a good kid, Agatha. He’s going to be okay.”



Rio was upset. She was also livid. This wasn’t an accident. It had Lou written all over it. How far gone was she to go after CHILDREN?! Rio looked over at Jen.

“Are you going to have to issue a statement?” Rio’s voice barely over a whisper.

Jen shrugged as she glanced at the multiple parents.

“It was a weekend accident with major injuries, so it was on the news. It will depend on what those six want.” Jen always included Nat when she mentioned the parents.

Nat walked over to Rio and put an arm around her shoulders.

“I need you for a minute,” she said as she led her off to the side. “I need to go check out the scene, look at all of our phones, but you know…”

Rio interrupted her. “It had to be...” Rio’s phone went off, and she looked at Nat. They both knew who it was.

Blocked

Oh, my beautiful Rio. Not their kids.

They’d be safer without you around.

My children, our children, were always

safe with you around.

A picture of the kids with Rio was attached. Rio knew that the oldest had known, but the other two didn’t. Rio was just a good friend of their mom’s. They had been watching a movie and were all four in a pool, smiling.

Rio’s eyes filled with tears as she looked at Nat. “I have to tell Agatha myself.”

Nat nodded, “I’m going to track this. I had a feeling.”

Much to Morgan’s repeated protests, the Starks departed, though Rio promised to update them. As they left, Tony put his hand on Agatha’s shoulder and leaned close to her ear.

“I’m sorry for all the hell I’ve given you, Harkness. He’s a good kid. His decision probably saved my daughter. Keep me updated.”

Alice and Jen had returned to the compound and Nat’s house, conveniently very close to the compound, to get clothes for those staying at the hospital, which would be Wanda, Nat, Agatha, and Rio.

Rio and Agatha sat in the cushioned chairs of the waiting room in the early hours of Sunday morning, waiting for Dr. Walker to give them an update. The Maximoffs had been able to see Billy, and while he was groggy, he was okay. He had a few bruises and a head injury, but he would make a full recovery. Once he had seen that his brother was okay, Vision was able to talk Tommy into going home and resting. Vision assured him that the best he could do for everyone was to go to Agatha’s and take care of Raven.

Nicky had taken the majority of the damage.

Rio was nervous but knew she had to tell Agatha that this was essentially her fault. She sighed as she squeezed Agatha’s hand. “Aggie?”

“It’s not your fault,” Agatha said automatically, voice full of care as she turned to look at Rio.

Rio was taken aback, “You don’t even know what I was going to say.”

“Nat told us Satan was released this morning, and then we got a call. It wasn’t that hard to do the math, babe. Then you and Nat went off to talk, and you’ve been nervous since. What did the text say?”

Rio showed her the text, and Agatha rolled her lips, keeping them in a thin line.

“It’s not your fault,” Agatha repeated as she squeezed Rio’s hand. At that time, Dr. Walker came out, and Agatha stood and met him.

“Ms. Harkness, Nicky is going to be fine and make a full recovery.”

⭐⭐⭐

Rio had called Lilia early Sunday morning and taken the two days of that week off, starting her winter break early. Lilia had understood.

“Even the strongest of towers need support, especially during storms,” Lilia had said. That message, Rio had understood.

Nicky was in a private ICU room, though considering who his mom was, the limited number of guests had been wavered. He was being kept in a medically induced coma for a day while his lung was being monitored, and Agatha sat at his side. Nat walked in and flopped on the couch next to Wanda with a defeated sigh. It was just the four women and Nicky in the room, and they could speak freely.

“She’s bouncing her signal all over the place, so I can’t locate her. A cell tower was mysteriously down which caused our phones not to take calls for a bit last night. All I know is she’s not in the country.” Nat explained angrily.

All eyes turned to Rio, who shook her head, “She has multiple places to hide. She was always good with investments. Shane too. So even with the divorce, that’s not an issue.” She explained.

“How’d she get released?” Wanda asked.

“Good lawyers and technicalities,” Nat answered. “No evidence of child endangerment apparent. She signed all of her rights over to Shane. There’s no hard evidence that it was her last night. It’s all circumstantial. I’ve reached out to some former contacts that are more global. They all have very specific orders.” She finished, looking at Agatha seriously.

Agatha nodded. “Good. Right now, I’ll make it through the winter if it kills me.”

Rio was starting to worry that it might, if Nat didn’t find Lou soon.

⭐⭐⭐

Billy was released on Monday, and Nicky was released the day after. The news had leaked, and Jen had released a statement on behalf of the family. An unknown driver had run Nicky off the road, and Nicky used his best judgment. All passengers were safe, and all parents were very grateful that it wasn’t worse. The families asked that everyone respect their privacy at the time. The Starks made a similar statement adding that Nicky had made sure Morgan was sitting in the back to begin with and had made sure everyone was as safe as possible.

“Harkness, Maximoff, and Stark Teens in Car Accident”

“Cedars-Sinai Reportedly Treats Children of Stars”

“Accident or Intentional?”

The headlines continued, some truly harmless, some speculating.

“So Nicky is a knight in shining armor? Refusing to let his girlfriend sit in the front seat because it’s dangerous at night? Someone find me a Harkness!”

“Don’t Tony and Agatha famously hate each other? How are their kids dating?”

“Romeo and Juliet?”

“Reportedly, Pepper and Agatha get along.”

“Rio’s a teacher, right? She can negotiate with anyone. Even directors.”

“I hope the twins are okay! Agatha breaks up with Wanda and then her sons are in a car accident with Agatha’s son.”

“Wanda and Agatha were not dating, get a grip.”

“Some people that were at the hospital took pictures of them! Rio was there to comfort Agatha!”

“Lorna Wu’s fucking daughter is Rio’s best friend!”

“Wanda was leaning on the bodyguard for support, not Vision.”

“Women are just more supportive, Wanda is straight.”

“People. This is about the KIDS.”

“The hug Pepper is giving Agatha looks a little too personal.”

“It’s Agatha Harkness.”

“I’m just glad the kids are okay and I hope they find the asshole that ran them off the road.”

It was too much for Rio. Comments were all over the place, and she needed to stop reading them. Her own socials were flooded, asking if everyone was okay. Why were people so parasocial all of a sudden? She shook her head and looked over at Agatha, who was asleep and curled in a ball on the couch. It was Tuesday afternoon, and they had just gotten Nicky settled in a guest room on the first floor. He wasn’t thrilled, but he needed to wait until his ribs weren’t as tender before climbing the stairs several times a day. Raven had been annoyed that they had left her in a house she wasn’t used to, but upon realizing Nicky was hurt, she curled up next to him and stayed there.

Rio knew Agatha needed a shower and actual sleep in her bed. They had eaten a late lunch, so that was one need out of the way. Rio stood and placed a hand on Agatha’s shoulder. “Aggie, honey.” She said as she shook her slightly.

“What?” Agatha grumbled, eyes still closed.

“Nicky’s going to sleep for a while. Let’s get you cleaned up and in an actual bed, up you go.” Rio helped her up as Agatha stretched and lazily draped an arm over Rio’s shoulders.

Rio guided them up the stairs, through Agatha’s bedroom, and into the bathroom. They had taken quick showers at some point at the hospital, but Rio didn’t think that counted as really getting clean. That was removing a basic layer of germs. She opened the door to the large walk-in shower and turned the water on to get the temperature just the right side of molten lava they both liked while they both undressed. When the water began to steam, Rio took Agatha's hand and led her into the shower, turning Agatha to face her. Agatha groaned as the hot water hit the muscles in her neck and shoulders, where she had built up tension from the past few days.

“I didn’t realize how tight everything was,” Agatha said softly as Rio ran her hands through Agatha’s hair to make sure it was wet.

“You've been holding a lot in. Keeping a mask on for everyone else.” Rio said honestly as she turned Agatha around and began to wash her hair.

Agatha was taken aback. No one was ever this candid with her. Here Rio was, caring for her in such an intimate way, calling her out in a way no one else would dare to do.

“Pardon?” Agatha said with a French accent as Rio massaged her scalp, and Agatha let a moan slip.

“You heard me. I watch you, Agatha. As much as you watch everybody else. You were terrified, and rightfully so.” She rinsed the shampoo from Agatha’s hair and began to repeat the cycle with conditioner. “That’s your son, baby. You had every right to scream, cry, yell, throw things. You remained calm and strong for everyone around you.” Rio let the conditioner sit as she put body wash on a loofah and began to clean Agatha’s body thoroughly. “We are here for you, baby. I am here for you. Let me be the one to comfort you. Let me be the one you cry on.” Rio rinsed the conditioner from Agatha’s hair before moving to rinse the soap from Agatha’s body. “Let me take care of you in the good times and the bad.”

Agatha felt like a dam within her would burst, and every feeling, guarded thought, and memory would come flooding out toward Rio. She couldn’t let that happen. Instead, she turned around and brought Rio in for a searing kiss underneath the still-hot water of the shower. She needed Rio at that moment, and she showed it with her lips and her tongue while her hands ran over Rio’s slickened body. She was not expecting Rio to pull back and drive the tip of a finger into her chest. Agatha looked at Rio, shocked, lips parted, water still streaming down both of them.

“No, you do not get to run and hide from your feelings with sex.” Rio was staring into Agatha as if reading her mind. “We will finish this shower, dry off, get dressed, and then I will hold you while you talk about your feelings and process like a healthy adult,” Rio spoke sternly, and it both annoyed Agatha and turned her on.

“Fine,” Agatha said curtly.

“And you call me a brat?” Rio teased.

They were sitting in the bed in lounge clothes. Nicky had been checked on and was still asleep with his feline protector. Rio was sitting with her back against the headboard, Agatha’s head on her shoulder as Rio stroked her hair.

“Agatha, talk.”

Agatha sighed.

“It’s just a lot. I don’t blame you at all. You cannot control what that psycho does.”

Agatha really did not blame Rio. Would this have happened if she and Rio weren’t dating? No. Did she want a life where she wasn’t dating Rio? No. What she wanted was a world where Lou Falco wasn’t a problem, and she had people working on that. She had learned a long time ago that she couldn’t control everything or what other people did. She could, however, arrange for those people to be taken care of.

“The important part is that I raised my son to be mindful of his surroundings and to think. It could have been so much worse. Vision taught him well, too. I’m not discounting that man's driving lessons. I sent him out in a tank of a vehicle, and that was absolutely the correct choice. He didn’t want to take any other car.” She chuckled, “He said Tony would want her in the safest vehicle we own, Mom.” She mimicked her son.

“That’s good that you can acknowledge all of that, babe. Thank you for not blaming me. I blame myself. I know I brought this all down on everyone.”

“No. She made that decision. You did nothing.” Agatha yawned. “My biggest fear is losing Nicky and then the people I love and care for. This is the closest I’ve ever gotten to that, and it was terrifying.”

Agatha wasn’t sure when she started to cry, but she knew there were tears on Rio’s shirt now.

“I was afraid. Afraid for everyone else, but also because I love those kids, especially Nicky. He truly means a lot to me, Agatha.” Rio finished with a whisper.

The gravity of the admittance hit Agatha right in the heart. Agatha knew how she felt for Rio, but she had to tell her everything, and this just wasn’t quite the right time for that.

“Thank you for being there with me and not leaving my side. Only Wanda and Nat have done that for me. Thank you, that means so much.” Agatha sat up and looked at Rio. “I can do anything with YOU by MY side, Rio.” Agatha kissed her softly before settling into the bed and pulling Rio down with her, laying back on Rio’s shoulder.

“Now, what do you want for Christmas?”

⭐⭐⭐

The following week leading up to Christmas was full of adjustments and healing. Until Nicky’s ribs felt better, he had to stay downstairs, and the twins spent more time at Agatha’s instead of everyone being at the Clubhouse. Tommy’s wrist had healed quickly and Billy’s injury was starting to fade. Morgan hung out too, not because she felt obligated, but because she genuinely wanted to.
