Throw in Eddie and Cassie, and Agatha felt like her home was turning into a teenage hangout.

“This is literally why we have a separate house for them,” Agatha bemoaned while sitting in Wanda’s living room two days before Christmas. “I don’t like it here as much.”

Wanda rolled her eyes.

“You’re just mad because you and Rio haven’t had much alone time.”

“We’ve spent most of our time at Rio’s but come back at night. Still, I’d like for there not to be six children eating me out of house and home.” Agatha said as she wrapped a present for Nicky.

“Hey! I foot half that bill, thank you!” Wanda laughed as she threw a gift bow at her sister.

Nat walked into the room at the separate piles of gifts and the mounds of gift-wrapping paraphernalia.

“I still don’t see why you two don’t just wrap a few things yourselves and hire someone else to do the rest. You know, like all your other famous friends do?” Nat mused before sitting down and grabbing a present to help. “Where’s everyone else?” She asked.

“Jen and Alice are packing and showing Jen’s house, I think is what they said?” Wanda answered as she finished another gift and placed it in the Billy stack.

“Rio said she had things to do at her place.” Agatha shrugged.

“Vision is playing racquetball with Herb but should be home soon,” Wanda added.

“Never beating the dating allegations,” Nat smirked as she wrote on a gift tag.

“I wonder what Rio got you for Christmas, Aggie?”

Wanda grinned and looked at Agatha. She was looking forward to seeing someone who was actually dating Agatha give her a gift. Wanda had never witnessed it before. From what she could remember, Agatha’s secret girlfriend never gave her anything, and if she had, Agatha had never talked about it.

“She doesn’t have to give me a present.” Agatha kept wrapping gifts, partially ignoring the looks of the other two.

“It’s so hard getting gifts for women that have or can buy anything they want or need,” Nat spoke from experience.

“You are not to get me anything for Christmas. We file the divorce papers, and then it’s our personal Christmas.” Wanda said, looking at Nat as if she were prey.

“I am well aware.” Nat returned the same look.

“I am literally right here. Not waiting until it’s finalized there, kids?” Agatha raised an eyebrow.

“We’ll wait for it to be finalized before we get engaged. However, it’s been 20 years, so, no.” Wanda said, voice serious.

“Well, we’ll make sure to be nowhere near here.” Agatha grinned. “You better still get this one an actual present though. You know she’ll pout if you don’t,” Agatha added, looking at Nat.

Nat just looked at the task at hand and smiled as Wanda shot Agatha a look.



Rio stood at her kitchen table with her project spread in front of her. All of her presents were wrapped, and this was the last thing she had left to complete. Agatha’s present was ninety-nine percent completed, and she was putting the final touches on it. She wrote one final word on the last page before putting it together and wrapping it. She was nervous but couldn’t wait to give it to Agatha. It would either go incredibly well or burst into flames. She cleaned up the table, threw trash away, and put the scissors, glue, tape, and other supplies back where they belonged. They would be spending the next few days at Agatha’s and she had replenished the clothes in her duffel and gathered all the gifts in a bag. Raven was still at Agatha’s as the feline refused to move from Nicky’s side unless necessary. She was often curled up next to his ribs as if she were trying to heal them faster. Nicky told Raven she was helping.

Rio loaded everything in the wagon she used to take things to the car so she didn’t have to make more than one trip. She was getting ready to leave when someone knocked. She froze as she wasn’t expecting anyone. She really needed to get a video doorbell or start tracking everyone. “Who is it?” Rio called as she walked cautiously toward the door, picking up a potted plant in case she needed to smash it over someone’s head.

“It’s Jen, and I’ll use my key if I have to,” Jen called back.

Rio released a breath she didn’t realize she was holding as she unlocked and opened the door. Jen’s eyes immediately went down to the plant.

“Were you going to fend someone off with a love fern?”

“It’s not a love fern, and if needed, yes.” Rio sat the plant back down as she let Jen in, closing and locking the door behind her. “Why didn’t you text or call?” Rio asked as Jen stood in front of the couch. She looked nervous, and Jennifer Kale was never nervous. “Jen? Sit.”

Jen sat, her leg bouncing. This was not the calm, cool, and collected Jen that Rio was used to. “Is it Alice? Is she okay?” Rio sat down next to Jen, turning to look at her, concern etched on her face.

“Yes. Yes, it’s Alice, and yes, she’s okay, it’s just…” Jen’s voice shook, and she looked like she was going to cry. Rio put a hand on Jen’s for comfort.

“Breathe, Jen. Do you need water? A beer? Anything.” Rio offered.

Jen took three deep breaths, regaining her composure. “No, I’m okay. I brought Alice back to her apartment, and I was going to leave but I made a last-minute decision. You’re the closest thing Alice has to family, and it’s old-fashioned and stupid, but I do love her, and I have for years.”

Jen’s face was soft and tears were forming. Rio realized that she had tears of her own forming. She knew what was coming, and at the same time, she was shocked that Jen was in front of her, giving her this speech.

Jen locked eyes with Rio and continued.

“I promise that I’ll take care of her, and if I don’t, you may send Nat after me. Can I have your blessing to marry Alice?”

While it was a serious moment, Rio couldn’t help but burst into laughter. “I am so sorry, Jen, it’s just, you are the last person I imagined would ask for someone’s hand in marriage, just because it’s such a patriarchal thing and you are so, not that,” Rio said, hand clasped over her mouth, trying to reign it in.

Jen scoffed, “You’re not wrong. If it weren’t you, I wouldn’t. However, given your relationship and that you’re all the family she has, it felt right.”

“It does feel right. Yes, you have my blessing and/or curse to marry my sister. However, you have to do it with the right ring.”

Rio stood up and turned toward her room, motioning for Jen to follow her.

“I was going to ask you to look at what I had narrowed it down to.”

“No. I have the rings. Gran left us her wedding set. Her husband died when Alice was young. I never met him. When Gran got sick, we had strict instructions to keep the rings; whoever married first was to get them, and the other was supposed to give them to whoever proposed. So,” Rio had entered her closet, found where the safe was hidden, entered the code, grabbed the ring boxes, closed, locked, and concealed the safe and reappeared. She handed Grandpa Wu’s ring to Jen.

“She wants to be proposed to with this one.” Rio handed Jen a white gold band that was inlaid with fire opal. The inside of the band was engraved with ‘full of fire’.

Jen turned the ring over in her hand before looking back at Rio suspiciously, “I don’t think Gramps had fingers this small.”

Rio smiled slyly, “I had a feeling this was coming and already had it sized.”

Jen gently shoved Rio’s shoulder, “Look at you, you big softy.” Her eyes fell on Gran’s set of rings. “What about those?”

“These, I will be keeping in case Alice wants to use them,” Rio said as she went to put them back in the hidden safe. When she returned, she let Jen know that Alice did not want a proposal made in front of the family or on a holiday. “She thinks it’s cliche,” Rio explained. “She wants private. Intimate.” She offered.

Jen took the advice before hugging Rio. Jen wasn’t much of a hugger, but this deserved a hug. “Thanks, Rio. I’m not sure when it will happen, but I know it will be soon.”

Jen departed as Rio pulled the wagon full of presents and clothes down to the car and drove back to Agatha’s.

Christmas morning had been a fun kind of chaotic. Everyone would gather at Agatha’s that afternoon to eat and exchange gifts, though the two families currently living at the compound did their Christmas mornings separately. Nicky had gotten everything he wanted, including a car, as long as he kept his grades up and the troublemaking to a harmless minimum. Nicky had gotten Rio a coffee cup as a gag gift that said “#1 Stepdad”. His serious gift to her was a signed Phoenix Mercury jersey and a framed picture of all of them from the concert, and he had gotten Raven, a pillow for his bed.

Nicky left the room to attempt to play video games with one arm, so Agatha and Rio could exchange presents alone.

They looked at each other, cheeks turning red. Rio had always been embarrassed when she bought or created things for SF.

“Open yours first.” The women said at the same time.

They both smiled, blushing slightly. Agatha had given Swords things over the years but had never received them with genuine gratitude. Agatha always felt like she was being humored, and her gifts were not those of a lover.

“Here,” Agatha said, practically shoving two boxes into Rio’s hands.”You first,”

They were sitting on the floor next to the tree, and Rio turned so she was sitting cross-legged, facing Agatha. Agatha mirrored her. Rio peeled back a layer of gift wrap and revealed a black jewelry box. Opening the box, Rio pulled out a silver chain with a shooting star charm on it. “For our first date,” Rio smiled, looking up at Agatha as Rio turned it over in her hand.

“Yes, but also, it’s part of my promise to keep you safe. You can click it like a button, and no matter where you are, Nat can track you, just in case,” Agatha added solemnly. They had been on edge, and this provided them a sense of security.

“I love it, and thank you for the added features. That means a lot.” Rio tilted her chin up as she leaned forward and gave Agatha a chaste kiss on the lips.

Rio opened the other box and pulled out two framed charts. One was a star map that had November 28, 2024, written across the top, and at the bottom, it read ‘The Night A Wish Came True’. Rio looked up at Agatha, “Thanksgiving? That was a terrible day.”

Agatha gave her an emotional smile. “Yes. However, my wish for you to be mine came true that day. It’s when you said you wanted actually to do this with me.”

A tear rolled down Rio’s cheek, “Only you could take a bad memory and make it happy, Agatha.”

Rio looked at the other frame. It was another star map with a specific star labeled. At the top, it read ‘The Vidal Star’ and gave the coordinates of a star near the Cancer constellation. Rio began to laugh, a genuine one that turned into a giggle. “It’s the anti-death star!” She finally said.

Agatha smirked, “I knew you’d like it.”

Rio’s giggles faded as she cupped Agatha’s cheek. “Baby, I love it all,” and kissed her. Agatha pulled away before Rio could go further.

“No, I have to tell you one more thing, and then I will open the present from you.”

Rio nodded, motioning for Agatha to continue.

“I cleaned out some space in my dresser and closet. Will you keep some of your clothes here? Please? For impromptu sleepovers?” Agatha’s eyes were questioning, searching Rio’s glassy ones. Rio nodded.

“Who knew playboy Agatha Harkness was a romantic?” Rio joked, wiping another tear away as her heart expanded.

“I sure as hell didn’t,” Agatha responded in a whisper, pressing their foreheads together. They stayed like that for a moment before Rio broke the silence.

“I have nothing as amazing as any of that but here.” Rio handed Agatha a large box.

Agatha unwrapped it carefully and opened the box, taking out a scrapbook. On the front was a picture of Agatha and Rio from the premiere, and letters spelled out ‘The Story of Us’. Agatha looked up, “This all about us?”

Rio gave a slightly embarrassed smile. “I didn’t know what to get the woman who has everything or can buy anything she needs or wants. So, I made this. Open it.” Rio gestured towards it.

Agatha could already feel the tears forming as she opened the front cover with a picture of Agatha cheering at a football game. Rio had written various lyrics, what she loved about Agatha, and different things that had been said or that happened around the picture. There were several solo pictures, pictures of both of them that had things she loved about them, pictures with Nicky, and pictures of them with their friends. Each page had lyrics, quotes, and things that she loved about Agatha or them that pertained to the picture.

‘I love the way you make me giggle.’

‘I love how protective you are of those you care about.’

‘I love the way we always wake up holding each other.’

‘I love how you look like a chipmunk when you chew.”

‘I love how you kiss me.’

‘I love how your eyes sparkle when you look at me.’

‘I love how down-to-earth you are.’

‘I love how involved you are with Nicky.’

‘I love the way you and Jen bicker and how it’s never really mean.’

The song lyrics were a mix of everything, and Agatha enjoyed them, too. The last page was a picture of just Agatha, one that she hadn’t even been aware Rio had taken. It was at the Halloween party. Agatha was dressed as Moiraine, and she looked over at Rio standing next to her. Agatha’s face and eyes lit up. The emotion was plain as day, though they wouldn’t call it what it was. This page held promises from Rio.

‘I will always stand beside you.’

‘I will always care about you.’

‘I will never become a stranger whose laugh you recognize anywhere.’

‘I will never leave the toilet seat up.’

‘I will always walk the red carpet with you.’

There were a few blank spaces. Agatha wondered why but didn’t ask. She closed the book and realized she was crying as she looked at Rio.

Rio reached out and wiped the tears away.

“Rio,” Agatha whispered, “No one has ever done anything like that for me. It’s beautiful.”

“I’m glad you like it,” Rio whispered back. “Merry Christmas, Aggie.” Rio cupped Agatha’s jaw and pressed her lips to Agatha’s.

“Merry Christmas!” Billy said as he walked into the house carrying presents.

Agatha groaned. “Why? Why do we do everything at my house?”

Presents had been exchanged, dinner had been eaten and cleaned up, and everything was going well. Vision, Tommy, Nicky, Alice, and Nat were deep into a football game in the living room, Billy and Jen were discussing awards season on the end of one couch, and Agatha, Rio, and Wanda were sitting at the kitchen table talking.

“Jen is going to propose to Alice,” Rio said conspiratorily.

“It’s about damn time,” Wanda replied, sipping wine.

Agatha smiled.

“I’m not surprised it’s Kale doing it first. Control freak.”

The other two women chuckled.

Rio noticed Wanda was wearing a new necklace and gestured towards it.

“Your gift from Nat?”

Wanda nodded, “Yes, it’s also a tracker if I push it.” Wanda turned to Agatha. “Like Rio’s, I assume?”

Agatha shrugged. “We’re protective of our women around here.”

Rio smiled. She liked being Agatha’s and being protected by her. She thought it was sweet.

Rio stiffened when she received a text.

Blocked

You’re mine baby.

Only mine.

A shiver went down Rio’s spine as if she had been drenched with ice water. She looked around her as Nat walked in, and Agatha took Rio’s hand.

“I’m tracking, but the signal is bouncing everywhere. I don’t think she’s in the country.”

Blocked

I want this Christmas to be

better than last year baby.

I’m so sorry about what happened.

I love you.

Rio looked at Agatha with wide eyes. “What does that mean?” She wondered out loud. The other adults had come into the kitchen as the boys had been instructed to stay in the other room.

“Tell her it is better,” Alice scoffed.

“You’re not wrong,” Rio confirmed.

Blocked

Oh, baby, you need to learn

that you’ve been brainwashed.

I hate when I have to teach you

these lessons. I especially hate

when I have to use your friends

to do it.

Nat looked around and put her finger to her lips, and found a notepad and pen. She wrote down, ‘she can hear us somehow, no one say anything’. Everyone nodded.

Blocked

Oh baby, this will hurt me, and

them, much more than it will hurt you.

I’m doing this for your own good.

I love you.

The texts stopped and Nat looked around the house for a potential bug. Wanda wrote out on the notepad. ‘FUCKING HOLIDAY CRASHER’.

Thirty minutes later, everyone had moved to the living room when every phone went off with notifications, texts, and phone calls. News articles, requests for interviews, requests for statements from Jen texts from friends.

Pictures from the past twenty-five years were everywhere: Wanda and Natasha holding hands, kissing, looking at each other. If it was slightly compromising, it was there.

Wanda was scrolling furiously through article after article, “How did she get these pictures?”

“Aren’t they only on our phones and computers?” Agatha asked brows furrowed.

“Something is not right,” Nat muttered as she swore in several different languages.

Wanda Maximoff’s Secret Double Life

Wanda Maximoff and Bodyguard’s Relationship Exposed

WandaNat Actually Real

Wanda Maximoff a Lesbian!?!?

Wanda was shocked and looked at the notifications in horror. She silenced call after call and ignored the texts and messages. Vision’s lips were a thin line, and his face was paler than normal. Nat was speaking fluent Russian, and it was more threatening than it normally sounded.

Rio was suffocating. She knew this was her fault. It was Christmas. CHRISTMAS. They had been CELEBRATING.

Blocked

Oh baby, it could have been

a much better Christmas.

Look at all the pain and

suffering you’re causing.

Nat looked up from where the message had been mirrored on her phone as she crossed over to Rio and kneeled in front of her.

“Nyet. It is not your fault. We knew we had flown too close to the sun with this, and there was a giant possibility it would come out before it was announced. Does this suck? Yes. Do NOT let this psychopath get in your head, okay? These were her decisions, not yours.”

Nat spoke with conviction, and Rio didn’t dare go against her or doubt a single thing she said.

Agatha loved how her friends had embraced Rio and didn’t blame her for anything. Agatha pulled Rio against her side. “I promise to always be beside you.” Agatha whispered in Rio’s hair.

Nat had gone back to Wanda’s side. Wanda looked up and at every single face, one by one.

“What do we do now?” Wanda asked, her green eyes questioning.

Agatha looked at the redhead, “It’s up to you, superstar.”

“I was afraid you were going to say that,” Wanda grumbled. “It’s just so much easier when you take charge.”

“I know angel, but you all have to decide this. Do you want to post a statement right now? Do you want Jen to reply to something? File divorce papers first thing in the morning? Go on a talk show after the first of the year? There are a lot of options.” Agatha was formulating a plan as well.

Wanda thought as she looked over at Nat, Vision, and the boys, and then at the women who hadn’t been in hiding.

“I don’t want to hide anymore. I’m done hiding. Vision, are you okay if Jen makes a statement to People? I’ll post to my socials tonight, and we’ll file in the morning?” It was a lot all at once, but she was ready to get the ball rolling.

Vision grinned. “You’ve learned quite a bit from your sister. Yes, darling, that all sounds great. Boys, we’re going to the cabin in Lake Tahoe tomorrow. Don’t ask questions. Nicky, you’re coming with us. No, you can’t do any winter sports.”

The boys took that as a cue to leave, mumbling about adults being gross and the twins stopping to say they agreed with the plan and they were proud of everyone.

Nat looked at Wanda, “Are you sure?”

Wanda nodded. “I’m not waiting any longer. We’re posting, we’ll handle the world, we’ll file the papers that have been ready for over a month, and we’ll kick Agatha and Rio to Rio’s apartment so no one interrupts us. Entire compound to ourselves.”

“Do you need the whole…” Agatha started.

“Fuck off, Harkness,” Wanda replied.

Jen cleared her throat. “What do you want me to say?”

Wanda looked at her, contemplating, “Wanda and Vision have always done what was in the best interest of the boys. However, the time has come for her to finally live her truth, and they have decided to divorce amicably. Please respect their privacy at this time. Or something like that. I’m going to call Drew. I’ll do an exclusive interview with her. She’s the only one I trust. So, I’ll call Drew, I’ll post, and then you return the call or e-mail from People?” Wanda looked at Jen, hopefully.

Jen smiled and nodded. “You have learned from Agatha. Good job.”

Wanda blushed. When it came to dealing with the major issues, Agatha always handled them. She turned to Agatha, “So?”

Agatha looked at her proudly, “That was good, kid. Now get on it. I’m proud of you.”

While phone calls, texts, and posts were being made, Nat sat next to Rio and took her hand.

“It’s not your fault. I think Wanda is relieved it happened this way.” Nat glanced over at Wanda as she updated her socials, having already set a date with Drew, who had been ecstatic for them.

“As for Looney Toons herself, I called in the big guns, and they were able to narrow a small radius in Morocco and have an…acquaintance in the area. I’ll keep you updated.” She looked at Rio and saw the worry and fear in her eyes. “Rio, if I have to neutralize the threat myself, I will. That’s a promise.”

“Posts are made!” Wanda said triumphantly.

Nat walked back over to Wanda and read one of the posts over her shoulder. Agatha sat back next to Rio so they could read together.

“Dear World,

It seems that I was outed today, on Christmas of all days. People come out every day, and so many famous people belong to the LGBTQIA+ community these days that it shouldn’t be a big deal. However, because it’s me, and because my public life was as the girl next door with the cute boyfriend, it’s shocking. I would even venture to guess, some of you feel betrayed or lied to. I also have a husband and two amazing boys, which adds to it. All of that was true, and my love for Vision and my family was genuine. While there have always been rumors that I was queer, even some of me with Agatha, you were only partially right. Those of you who started WandaNat, you guessed it. You have to understand that while America embraced Agatha for being a lesbian, my then-agent and PR team did not, and it was too late. I fell in love with Natasha twenty-five years ago and have been madly in love with her ever since.  Still, with Nat’s support, I chose my career. Vision and Nat have both knowingly been integral parts of my life. I love my husband, but we’re not in love. He’s my best friend and an amazing co-parent. There are no hard feelings there, and he’s encouraged me to do this. There was no infidelity, no going behind backs, none of the things I’ve seen insinuated since everything leaked. If I lose roles or fans because of this, that’s fine. I am finally living my truth and being the mother and role model that my sons, and you, deserve. Never sacrifice who you are for your career.

Love,

The Wanda Maximoff.

With it, Wanda had posted various pictures of her and the family, her and Nat, her and the boys, Nat and the boys, and all of them together.

“I love it, Darling.” Vision said, kissing Wanda on the top of her head.

Nat looked at Wanda, arms wrapped around her waist. “Does this mean we’re officially together again?”

Wanda laughed, “I think it does.”



⭐⭐⭐

The week between Christmas and New Year’s was marked by social media posts with WandaNat trending most of the week, more articles, divorce papers being filed, and Agatha and Rio taking Raven and spending time at Rio’s apartment for a few days so Wanda and Nat had uninterrupted time. On New Year’s Eve, they were back at Agatha’s, getting ready for a party hosted by Tony.

“He does throw great parties.” Agatha was saying as she put her earrings in. “Done, how do I look?” She turned toward Rio, who had been admiring her from where she sat on the bed for the past fifteen minutes.

“You’d look better naked, horizontal, on your back,” Rio said, tongue running along the inside of her cheek.

Agatha gave her a wicked smile.

“If I wasn’t wearing fresh lipstick, I’d kiss that smirk off your face.” She stood and walked toward Rio. She came to a stop and did a full turn, modeling for her.

It was a simple floor-length halter dress cut down to her navel, champagne-colored silk, with a thigh-high slit on the left side and black stilettos accentuating her calf and thigh muscles. Agatha wore her hair down, parted in the middle, in loose waves with diamond drop earrings.

“You’re gorgeous, as always, baby.” Rio kissed Agatha alongside her jaw, her own lipstick having already set. “If we don’t leave, though, we won’t make it to the party.”

“I don’t think that’s a bad thing,” Agatha replied, gripping Rio’s hips.

Rio stepped back, “C’mon Harkness, we have a new year to usher in.”

Rio wore a tight, strapless, black, sparkly dress that hit her mid-thigh. She had paired it with black pumps, pulled her hair up, and worn the necklace Agatha gave her. She hadn’t taken it off since Christmas and, luckily, had earrings that matched it.

When they arrived at the party, they met up with the other coven members. Alice excitedly glided towards them in a gold dress.

“LOOK!” She said excitedly as she held up her left hand with the engagement ring firmly planted on her ring finger.

Alice had called Rio right after, so she and Agatha knew, but she hadn’t been able to hug her yet. She threw her arms around her and squeezed.

“I am so extremely happy for you. I love you so much.” Rio said in her ear, trying not to cry. She released her so the other woman could hug her as Jen approached them with Agatha beside her.

Rio looked at Jen, ever gorgeous in her black suit trimmed with gold.

“I’m so happy for both of you. Do anything to hurt her, and I will end you.” She said as she hugged her.

Jen looked at her seriously, “I will never do so intentionally. I promise you that, Rio.”

“So we have our first engaged couple for the new year,” Agatha said, holding up a flute of champagne that she had picked up off a nearby table as she handed one to Rio. Everyone else picked up one of their own as they toasted to Jen and Alice.

“It’s still…” Wanda started as Agatha held up her hand to stop her.

“No, it counts. The two of you will be next, I call maid of honor. Vision will probably be Nat’s best man because of the very special bond you two have.”Agatha thought aloud as Wanda blushed slightly. The announcement had been surprisingly well received with little backlash. If SF had been trying to ruin Wanda’s career, she failed.

Nat raised an eyebrow and looked at Agatha and Rio, “What about you two?”

“We’re not there yet,” Rio said quickly.

“Not the marrying type.” Agatha lied.

Alice saw the hurt in Rio’s eyes as Wanda saw the lie in Agatha’s.

“I say we all go dance,” Jen spoke up, shooing everyone towards where the dancing was happening.

The boys each had their dates, Nicky still with his broken arm, though his ribs were feeling better, which he attributed to a certain magical feline. He and Morgan were dancing to the best of his abilities with his arm in a sling a week and a half after the accident.

“Are you going to be able to play our senior year?” She asked as they danced.

Nicky nodded, “Doc says it’s looking good, so I should be able to.”

She smiled, “I don’t care either way, I just want to know if I need to keep my Friday nights open.”

Nicky’s ears turned pink. “So you plan to keep me around?”

“As long as my dad doesn’t murder you first.”

A slow song came on, and he led Morgan outside on the balcony.

“Hey, I know this is kind of soon, but I live with a bunch of women, and I’m in tune with my emotions. Um, Morgan? I love you.”

Morgan’s smile stretched across her face. “Well, my parents are surprisingly hopeless romantics, and I love you too.” She kissed him before hearing her dad clear his throat behind them, causing Nicky to jump.

“Sir, I am so sorry.” He apologized.

“Daddy, he loves me, and I love him,” Morgan said.

Nicky looked at Mr. Stark with a look of fear.

“Unless you’d rather me not, sir. Then I will push it back down, sir.” He swallowed. Even though he was taller than Tony, the man terrified him.

Tony looked him up and down. “Do you love Morgan?”

“Yes, sir,” Nicky replied.

“I believe you think you know what love is having grown up at Compound de Harkimoff. But you’re still just a kid. If you hurt her, not even Nat can save you, got it?” Tony said, eyes dark.

“Yes, sir,” Nicky answered.

Tony nodded and turned around, looking for Agatha.

Rio looked around. It was a few minutes until midnight. Nicky was in love, Alice and Jen were engaged, Wanda was glowing now that she was fully out, Nat finally had her girl, Vision seemed happier, and Agatha was actually getting along with Tony as they both talked to some other actors. Still, Agatha kept glancing over at Rio and smiling, Billy and Eddie were out, and even Tommy had a girlfriend. For all the drama SF was trying to cause, good was coming from it. Rio knew the other shoe would drop, but for right now, things were good. Agatha came over to her and kissed Rio’s cheek.

“It’s almost midnight, and I believe I am supposed to kiss my girlfriend.” Agatha handed Rio a fresh flute of Champagne.

“Good thing that’s me. I would be very upset if it weren’t.” Rio took the glass and pulled Agatha close to her.

The countdown began as she stared into Agatha’s ocean-blue eyes. This woman Rio cared so deeply for.

The countdown hit midnight, and they kissed before toasting and then kissed again. She tasted the champagne on Agatha’s mouth and made a silent wish for an amazing year with this woman.

Rio’s phone was on a nearby table and went off. Her eyes flew open. That text’s timing could only be from one person.

She turned and looked at the phone as if it had started speaking, then looked back at Agatha.

“It’s okay, baby, it’s just scare tactics.” Agatha ran her hand down Rio’s back.

Rio picked up the phone and almost dropped it.

There was a picture of LAX.

Blocked

I can’t wait to see you

again my beautiful Rio!

Happy New Year!



⭐⭐⭐

It was January 1st, 2025, and the restraining order officially expired.

Chapter End Notes

Thank you so much for all the love you've shown! Kudos, comments, love on socials, etc.

Realistically, unless I start writing smaller chapters again, there are probably only a few chapters left. However, I want to make this a series and do a follow-up with one-shots of different moments of life. What would y'all like to see? There are some things I want to go back and do different POV's of, further into the future on some things, etc.

If that's something anyone would be interested in, of course.

Glitter On The Floor After The Party

Chapter Summary

The order has expired and New Year's Day is full of confrontations and confessions, leading into Golden Globes weekend.

Will Lou meet her end?
Will Agatha come clean? About everything?
Will our favorite idiots confess their feelings?
Will Death's Beloved win all the awards?

Chapter Notes

Trigger Warnings: Light violence, dangerous driving, Lou, heartbreak, mention of pregnancy loss (from personal experience, don't come at me), so much angst, hurt, and anger.

But then, not.
I'm here if anyone needs comfort or to talk. We're at the turning point, really.
Yes, it's another long chapter. You guys know me by now.

Thank you ALL so much for the comments, kudos, WE SURPASSED 10K views and I cried. I came back to writing after an extremely long time of not (as evidenced by the first few chapters) and this journey has been amazing. The love on the socials, just THANK YOU ALL SO MUCH!

Thanks to my amazing Beta/Editor/AuDHD thought detangler.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

It was New Year’s Day, barely after midnight, as Rio clung to Agatha and stared at her phone. “Do you think she’s actually here? In LA?” Rio wasn’t asking anyone specifically.

“I was able to mask the tracking and finally pinpoint her. According to that, she’s here. As in about to crash the party.” Nat said, head whipping around.

“We will not be staying for that,” Agatha said firmly. “Nat, divide up security. Stay with the Maximoffs. Jen and Alice are with them. Ri, carry your shoes and come with me.” Agatha took Rio’s hand after they both slipped their shoes off and led her in the opposite direction from the main exit.

This was a plan Agatha and Nat had already devised in case SF reared her gorgeous blonde head. Nat would separate the others into two cars and send them home, while Agatha and Rio would slip out the back. While it would look like they got into one of the standard large black SUVs, they would get into an unassuming black Subaru Outback capable of off-roading with Nat as the driver.

“Agatha, you know I trust you, but where are we going?” Rio asked as she was led away from the door.

“We planned for her,” Agatha said over her shoulder as the power went out. They both stopped. “We did not plan for that.” Thankfully, backup generators roared to life, and the power flickered back on. Agatha quickened their pace to where they were practically running as they burst through the back door. “Okay, go around the SUV and get in the Subaru,” Agatha instructed.

Rio flung open the passenger door to see Wanda.

“Shotgun.” Wanda waved.

“Nat, Wanda is supposed to be at the compound with everyone else!” Agatha admonished as she and Rio climbed in the back, buckling their seatbelts.

“You try telling her no.” Nat shot back as she pulled away from the building.

“I have. Plenty of times.”

“To be fair, you’re not in love or sleeping with me.” Wanda turned around in her seat, smiling.

Agatha rolled her eyes.


Blocked

Oh Cinderella, we didn’t

even get to dance.


Rio let out a feral scream, causing Nat to run off the road momentarily before correcting.

“I’m tired of this! I hate this! Why can’t she just leave me alone?” Rio had tears in her eyes as Agatha held her hand. “Agatha, being with you has made me so happy, yet I have cried so much. I have caused you so much pain. We had to leave a party early, in a fucking getaway car, because of my ex. Thanksgiving and Christmas were partly ruined. The boys have been put in danger. Who knows what else she’ll do? I can’t lose you, Agatha. I can’t lose any of you.”

She had been looking at Agatha before looking at Wanda, who had turned around in her seat. She inhaled deeply. She had made a decision, and it hurt, as all she wanted to do was go back to Agatha’s and sleep in her arms. “When we return to the compound, I’m going home.” She immediately winked at Agatha and Wanda. She didn’t know if SF could hear them, but she wasn’t taking any chances.

Darcy had upgraded the networks at the compound and all houses, as they hadn’t found a single bug anywhere. Lou was dangerously intelligent for a supermodel, although that had been more of a cover for her cat burglary gig. Modeling was also how she caught Shane's eye all those years ago. She hadn’t necessarily confessed to Rio as much as Rio had found out about it accidentally.

Rio turned to Agatha. “When we get to the house, I’ll explain everything.” She whispered in Agatha’s ear.

“Well, Cinderella, looks like your Princess Charming from Hell is trying to collect on that dance,” Nat said as she looked in the rearview mirror. “Everyone, hold on. This is our exit.”

“This isn’t an…” Wanda started as Nat made a hard left onto a hidden dirt path, thankful there was no oncoming traffic on this particular backroad.

Nat didn’t slow the vehicle down as they bounced over the uneven terrain and careened around trees, following what seemed like an invisible path. Rio kept her eyes trained on the headlights behind them before they finally fell out of view. She turned around, slumping in her seat, “Where are we?”

Agatha leaned close as a precaution before whispering, “Secret back entrance.”

They went through a gated entrance at the back of the compound, passing by the foundation of Alice and Jen’s house, the skeleton of the guest house, and around to the front of Agatha’s. As they exited the vehicle, Rio motioned for them to leave their phones in the car and stay outside for the moment.

“I don’t know if she can hear us through the phones, but I’m now highly paranoid, pumped full of adrenaline, and running on champagne fumes at one forty-five in the morning on New Year's Day. Here’s the plan, in case she can still hear us in the house. We’re going to go in there, and you two,” Rio looked at Wanda and Agatha. “Are going to use those Oscar-winning skills of yours. I will go off about how I need to go home and think about things, and we’ll improvise from there. I will go to my apartment, and she’ll most likely meet me there. Hopefully, I can convince her to leave me alone, or Nat, you do you.”

“I’m not a fan, what if…” Agatha started before Rio put her fingers to Agatha’s lips.

“Please. Let me do this. We have to try and end this. If it doesn’t work, it doesn’t work. I still believe she won’t hurt me.” She removed her hand and kissed Agatha before she could respond.

Wanda and Nat exchanged looks. They weren’t as certain as Rio seemed to be.

Agatha swallowed the tears and confessions in her throat. “Alright,” she turned to Wanda, “Let’s go superstar.”

“Oh! Wait!” Rio held up a hand before they entered the house. “Raven is staying here. They hate each other, and I do not trust Lou to be nice. Now, everyone, get your phones first.”

Rio stormed into the house first, where everyone was gathered. “I can’t do this to everyone anymore, Aggie! I want this so much, but it’s just causing pain!”

Agatha was on her heels, “Rio! We can work this out. I…” Agatha realized this suddenly felt very real to her, and she almost let it slip. Again. She had to confess, and soon. “Care about you so much. We’ve met her head-on since this started. We can handle this together. We can end the entire thing.” Agatha had given an overly theatrical wink to everyone in the room when they had come in, and Wanda had done a double thumbs up. Agatha hoped everyone knew this was fake. Wasn’t it?

Rio had winced when Agatha had, once again, avoided saying ‘love’. “At least Lou is honest with me about her feelings. Psychotic or not.” She said in a whisper.

It was a blow to the chest, and Agatha deserved it. Looking at Rio, hurt in her eyes, she said, “Baby, I…”

“I’m going home, I need to think some things over,” Rio stated louder as she turned and stormed out the door.

Agatha dropped onto a couch, Alice scooting over just in time.

“Was that acting?” Agatha asked, looking at Alice.

“I’m not sure,” Wanda answered.


Nat had followed Rio outside. “Hey, stop for a second!” She called out as Rio stopped right in front of her car, not turning. Nat stopped behind her, whispering. “She does, you know. She just doesn’t know how to say it. Don’t give up on her.”

Rio turned around slowly, her face blank. “Nat, I just,” she sighed. “I want to tell her, and there’s a woman who IS telling me, and I just…” she trailed off.

Nat put a firm hand on her shoulder. “Look, you have to go in there with a clear head, okay? Also, do you want this?” She offered a small handgun, not wanting to say the words aloud for fear of Lou’s eavesdropping.

Rio shook her head. She could shoot, but she didn’t want to. “No, I’m covered.” She said, brandishing a small knife. A rather creepy smile pulled at her lips.

“Scary bitch. Alright, go. We’ll see you soon.” Nat let her go and stepped back as Rio left.


Rio was numb on the drive to her building, and facts swirled around her head.

Lou was insane and dangerous.

Lou loved Rio but felt like she owned her.

Lou viewed Rio as an object.

Agatha was amazing and wanted a life with Rio.

Agatha refused to find and/or admit her feelings.

Rio was not in love with Lou.

Rio had caused her friends a lot of problems.


Rio was madly in love with Agatha.

Rio felt her stomach churn.


She was conflicted. Rio knew she had these feelings for Agatha, but she hadn’t fully admitted them to anyone else. She should be able to share them, shouldn’t she? She had told Alice she wasn’t ready, but when would she be? What was holding her back? Was she waiting for Lou to disappear? Was she waiting on Agatha? When would she know?

She had to keep her head clear and protect the people she loved.

Rio was jolted out of her thoughts when her phone dinged, and her GPS showed a new location. Lou must be controlling her phone. Rio would have to tell Nat but couldn’t risk using the compromised cellphone.


Blocked

I have a surprise planned for us.

I can’t wait to see you.


Rio felt sick, but she also knew she needed to follow the GPS. She drove as it directed, trusting that Nat would be able to find her, and followed the automated voice to a fancy apartment building in an isolated nice area of town.



Nat saw the message mirrored on the clone of Rio’s phone as Rio turned in the opposite direction. She would have to follow discreetly.

“What’s she doing?” Agatha asked from the passenger seat.

They had been able to convince everyone else to stay at the compound.

“SF hacked Rio’s phone and sent her to a different location,” Nat responded, careful to follow from a distance. The route started to look familiar. “Agatha, are we headed to…”

“Yes.” Agatha interrupted. “My condo.” She finished.

“You still own it?”

“Yep,” Agatha said with a pop.

“Have you listed it?” Nat looked at her briefly before turning back to the road.

“No,” she said with a sigh. “This is going to be really bad.”

Nat nodded.

Agatha had learned from Swords that it was best to keep a separate place for flings and one-night stands. Her condo was out of the way, discreet, and everything she needed it to be. She had meant to list it and hadn’t yet, knowing she had to confess to Rio first. Then, she would sell it as a final symbol of shedding her past life.

Nat slammed her fist into the steering wheel, startling Agatha.

“Blyat, Agatha! I threatened Rio so she would open up to you when YOU’RE the idiot here! I have been keeping your extremely dark secrets for decades and making sure none of your poor decisions come back to bite you in the ass. Rio comes into our lives and, in a few months, does things that even you couldn’t do, Agatha. Yet YOU won’t tell her everything. I know there are secrets that Lorna took to the grave with her, but it’s time to confess all of those to Rio and finally tell her that you love her. She’s not going to wait for you forever, Agatha.”

Nat’s face matched her hair, and Agatha understood where her anger and frustration were coming from.

“You’re right,” Agatha whispered, staring straight ahead as they reached the building.

“I know I am. Now, before we bust in there, we’ll listen to the conversation to see when we need to save the day.” Nat had produced a laptop, and her fingers were flying across the keyboard.

“How?”

“There’s a mic in the necklace that I can tap into,” Nat said as she brought up the audio. “Don’t worry, it’s ONLY for emergencies,” Nat added, seeing the look of concern on Agatha’s face.


Rio took a deep breath as she reached the building she had been directed to, trusting that Nat and Agatha weren’t far behind.


Blocked

Look at the row of

mailboxes. You’ll be

able to find it from there.


Rio walked into the building. The doorman looked at her slightly confused before he nodded and greeted her by name. She walked the row of mailboxes and saw A.H. on the last one.

A.H.? Agatha Harkness? Why did Agatha have a condo here?

She took the elevator to the penthouse and stepped out before walking to the unit. 821. The numbers seemed familiar to Rio before it clicked, Lorna’s birthday, backward. January twenty-eighth. Rio approached the door and looked at the keypad on the door. If this really was Agatha’s place, then she knew the code would be the day the order to be free of her mother was granted. She prayed it wouldn’t work as she typed in the numbers. The light flashed green, and the lock clicked as she turned the knob and pushed the door open.

Rio was still wearing the dress from the party, and she suddenly became painfully aware of how revealing it was as the door closed and locked behind her. Lou was standing in front of her, wearing jeans and a black cable knit sweater. Lou looked flawless as her eyes wandered possessively over Rio’s body.

“I’ve missed you, my pet,” Lou said.

“I hate you,” Rio walked into the room, past Lou, and perched on the arm of a couch. The apartment didn’t have many personal effects, but it oozed Agatha’s personality.

“What is this place, Lou?” Rio asked with a sigh.

A smile crept across Lou’s face as she stalked towards Rio.

“Agatha’s fuck pad, pet. She didn’t take women home with her. She brought them here. Funny, she still has it.”

Rio looked away, not wanting to give Lou the satisfaction of seeing the hurt on her face. It felt like someone had stabbed her in the heart and twisted. No wonder Agatha hadn’t told her she loved her. She couldn’t let go of the past. “Okay, so it hasn’t sold yet.” She said quietly.

“It’s not even on the market.”

Another blow. Rio stood, moving further from Lou.

“Like you were always truthful, Lou? I almost died because you weren’t.” Rio was trying to keep her voice flat and void of all emotion, but the emotions were seeping in.

“I realize my actions caused you harm, and I would never hurt you, Rio. I am so sorry, and as for all of this,” She gestured around, “I had to get your attention, baby.”

Lou was right behind Rio as she wrapped her arms around Rio’s waist.

Rio stiffened. “You don’t get to touch me like this anymore, Lou.” She said hotly, flinging Lou’s arms away, stepping out of the embrace, and turning around. “Is this your master plan, Lou? Come in here, show me Agatha has a secret apartment, and hope I break up with her? This is a conversation, Lou. Not a reason to break up with her.”

Rio was more annoyed at this point.

“Honestly, I thought she’d be here with you.”

Lou had closed in on her again, and Rio felt like she was being chased around the apartment.

“So you’re stalling until she shows up? God, Lou. You have lost it. We are not getting back together. You hurt our kids, Lou.”

Rio spat, looking right at Lou, who was only inches away.

Lou looked hurt at that.

“Baby, I would never hurt our kids. They’re safe with Shane.”

Rio was shocked. Did Lou really think she was talking about Lou’s kids?

“No, Lou. Nicky. Billy. Tommy. Hell, even Morgan. You…”

Rio was cut off as Lou slapped her, open-handed across the face, before shoving her against the wall, hand wrapping around Rio’s throat with the slightest amount of pressure. Rio brought her hands up and wrapped them around Lou’s wrist, trying to push it away.

Lou raised an eyebrow. “You used to love it when I choked you. Begged me to choke you harder.” She purred.

Rio scoffed, “That was before you completely ignored the safe word and blew past all my boundaries. You know who doesn’t do that? Agatha. You know who takes care of me in bed? Agatha. In bed and everywhere else. It will always be Agatha. Not you.”

Lou’s grip tightened despite Rio’s best efforts to loosen it.


The door flew open as Nat and Agatha ran in. Lou quickly removed her hand and looked at the newly arrived women as Rio fell to her knees, gulping in lungfuls of air.

“Well, it’s about fucking time!”

Lou watched as Agatha ran to Rio and knelt beside her. Agatha had tears building from seeing and hearing where Rio had been hurt and from the anger toward Lou rising in her.

“Rio, are you okay?” Agatha asked as she lightly touched Rio’s reddened cheek where Lou had slapped her.

Rio looked at Agatha, hurt and pain in her eyes.

“No, I’m not. We have to talk.” Rio’s voice was raspy as Agatha helped her stand.


Nat hadn’t drawn her weapon, but she had positioned herself where Lou would have to go through her first to get back to Rio or Agatha.

“Leave her alone, Lou, she doesn’t want you,” Nat said harshly.

“This isn’t your fight, you feisty chihuahua.” Lou looked back at Agatha. “Ah, and the bitch that stole what’s mine.”

“She doesn’t belong to anyone.” Agatha took a step forward, her arm protectively in front of Rio.

“I gave her everything!” Lou screamed.

Lou’s face was screwed up in fury as she stepped towards Agatha only stopping when she saw Nat take a step forward.

Rio lowered Agatha’s arm aside, moving towards Lou.

“You gave me NOTHING. You took and took everything from me. You stripped away my sense of safety, my sense of security, my love, my freedom, my self-respect, my dignity, my sanity, my ability to TRUST.” Her voice went from acidic to soft as she turned to Agatha. “Agatha gave that BACK to me, Lou.”

A sardonic smile cut across Lou’s face.

“Please. She can’t even tell you she loves you. Do you think THIS was the only secret she was keeping from you? Ha. She’s the queen of deception, pet.” Lou looked at Agatha. “Are you going to tell her, or should I?”

Agatha narrowed her eyes at her. “You don’t know anything.”

“Do you think Rio’s phone was the only one I hacked? I heard an interesting conversation between the unholy trinity here, my sweet. Looks like Superstar and Jane Bond only know half-truths about your global sweetheart here, too.”

Lou’s arms were crossed in front of her chest, and her smile was a Cheshire cat-like grin.

“Nat, end her,” Agatha instructed.

Rio shook her head, moving forward.

“Agatha, Nat, is this true?” Rio asked, her eyes filled with uncertainty and pain.

Agatha responded, flustered, “There was never a right time to bring it up.”

“I’m so sorry, Rio,” Nat said.

“Nat, it wasn’t yours or Wanda’s place to tell me. Agatha? How many times do we have to go through this? YOU were the one begging me to open up, and you only fed me enough, so I wouldn’t ask more questions? Is there more to why you don’t do relationships? Tell me now. No more excuses..” Rio’s voice betrayed her hurt, rage, and unfinished ultimatum.

Sensing the opening, Lou jumped in. “She doesn’t love you the way I do, baby. Just look at her phone. Go to November first.” Lou instructed.

Agatha handed her phone to Rio, scouring her memory of that night.

“That’s the night I went out to the club, Wanda brought my drunk ass to your house. I have nothing to hide, Rio.”

Rio took the phone and tapped in the code.

“At least she has been forthright. I trust the blonde sociopath over there more than you right now.”

She pulled up the photo app and went to November 1st. There were pictures from the football game and pictures from the club, and then, Rio’s hand flew to her mouth as she let out a gasp. Nat rushed to look over Rio’s shoulder, shock on her face.

“What? What is it?” Agatha asked.

“Oh? You don’t remember?” Rio spat each word dripping acid. Lou began to laugh maniacally.

“I was extremely drunk,” Agatha tried to explain as Rio thrust the phone into Agatha’s hands. Agatha looked at the picture. There she was, passionately kissing Lou, one hand in the blonde’s hair, the other gripping her shoulder tightly.

Lou interrupted in the most rational voice she could muster.

“She doesn’t tell you she loves you. She gets drunk and makes out with someone less than twenty-four hours after kissing you, and then a few hours later, what does she tell you? Oh, right, that you’re the only one she’s wanted since she first saw you. Now, I admit I’ve fucked up, but baby, not only has therapy helped me. But if I could turn back time to take back all the things that hurt you, I would, baby. I love you so much. I wouldn’t hide a ten-year-long secret relationship from you. Oh, shit. Guess the cat’s out of the bag now.” Lou put her hand over her mouth like she had accidentally let it slip.

Agatha just flinched, and Nat looked at Agatha with her eyes wide.

Rio turned to Agatha.

“WHAT! Is THAT why you don’t date?! Because of some brutal relationship you had? That’s an important thing to tell me!” She exploded.

Nat turned back to Lou. She was going to make this bitch disappear. “Where did she go?” She asked out loud.

In all the commotion, Lou had escaped. The door was slightly open, so Nat ran out to see if she could chase her down, closing it behind her.

Rio looked at Agatha, flames in her eyes, “You begged me to be honest and upfront and tell you everything, and I did. But, you kept lying to me!”

“I didn’t lie. I just didn’t tell you the complete truth,” Agatha lied.

“That’s even worse!” Rio yelled. “You were in a relationship for TEN years, Agatha. TEN YEARS. You didn’t think that was something I needed to know? You told me you wanted me, that I was IT, and yet you KEPT YOUR SECRET APARTMENT. The place you only used to fuck other women. But, most importantly, you didn’t tell me about it. You didn’t think THAT would piss me off?!” Rio was pacing in front of Agatha, hands flying.

“You seemed pretty calm about that a moment…” Agatha started.

“Because I wasn’t going to say anything in front of my sociopathic stalker of an ex, dumbass! So  for the record, I am NOT CALM ABOUT IT.”

Rio stopped and looked straight at Agatha. Rio ran her hands through her hair, she couldn’t stay here right now, she didn’t want to hear Agatha’s excuses or explanations, but she also knew they weren’t over.

“God, Agatha, to think, I was ready to tell you…it doesn’t matter. I can’t look at you right now. I’m going to my place, I’ll get Raven later. Just know that we are NOT broken up, and  this conversation is nowhere near over We have the Globes this weekend, so this is not a pass to go fuck someone or make out with my ex, since I have to be clear about that.”

Rio saw Agatha wince as the verbal blow hit, and she didn’t care how vicious her words were.

“I have thinking to do, and you have confessions to make. Get your shit together, Harkness.” With that, Rio stormed out, refusing to look back at Agatha. Rio hadn’t even let her talk.

Agatha sank onto a couch, buried her face into her hands, and cried.


⭐⭐⭐

Rio had gotten home around five in the morning. She needed Nat to remove the malware on her phone but didn’t care right now. Let Lou listen in on things, what did it matter now? She had stripped off her dress, sobbed in the shower, put on pajamas, and then sobbed in her bed. She had spent most of the break in Agatha’s arms every single night. She didn’t even have her cat. She sighed as she grabbed her phone.


Me:

Where are you?

Sister Alice:

On my way with Raven.

Give me 10.

Me:

Wear PJ’s. You’re the

big spoon.

Sister Alice:

Nat told us everything.

I even have ice cream.


Rio had sad, heartbreak songs playing when she heard the door open and the distinct sound of the bell on Raven’s collar.

“I’m going to feed her first and put the ice cream in the freezer,” Alice called out.

Rio didn’t respond, just lay there, waiting for her found sister, when the bed moved, and Alice curled up behind her. They would cuddle and talk for hours growing up and then during breakups or any disappointment they experienced through the years. They had found comfort in each other for the past twenty-three years of their lives, and Rio was thankful they were in the same city again. Rio rolled over and buried her head in Alice’s shoulder, sobbing again.

“I know, Ri. I’m proud of you for telling Agatha you had to talk first and not break up. That’s great!” Alice praised her friend. It was a big step. Usually, Rio ran instead of facing things head-on. She wasn’t proud of everything Rio had said, but she was focusing on the good right now.

“Why didn’t she tell me?” Rio sniffed.

“I think it caused some serious damage and trauma for her, according to Nat and Wanda. And they don’t even know the whole story or who the woman is. It happened eighteen years ago, and she hasn’t had a relationship since.” Alice provided.

“She still needs to tell me everything,” Rio stated.

“She does. Wanda says there’s probably a lot more.”

Rio rolled onto her back, “I don’t know what to do. Lou apologized, you know?”

“Right before trying to kill you.” Alice reminded her.

“Foreplay?”

“You cannot go back to her, Rio.” Alice gripped Rio’s hand, trying to convey the seriousness of the situation.

“I wonder if that’s the only way everyone will be safe,” Rio responded, sounding hopeless.

“Nat finally decided to use all means necessary to end it,” Alice whispered.

Rio’s eyes went wide. She knew what that meant and wasn’t exactly sure how she felt about it. “Oh,” was all she could utter.

“It’s the only real way to keep us safe, and you know it,” Alice assured her.

“I know it is.”

What surprised Rio was how empty she felt on the inside. She had loved that psychotic woman, and now they were talking about her demise, and she felt nothing but relief.

⭐⭐⭐


Agatha felt absolutely awful. She hadn’t been honest and now everything had blown up in her face. Nat was working on Agatha’s phone and double-checking that all of Lou’s malware had been removed while Wanda sat on the opposite end of the couch from Agatha, glaring at her.

“I can’t believe you,” Wanda said for what had to be the hundredth time.

“You’ve already established that.” Agatha’s voice was hoarse from crying since she left the penthouse. Jen was already working on getting it listed.

“I just, I can’t believe you hadn’t listed that place! God, Agatha! What were you thinking? We know that bitch has been listening in to conversations and will do anything to break the two of you up. You’re going to lose her, Agatha, and it will be your fault.” Wanda said harshly.

Agatha knew Wanda was serious, and she had started to cry again.

“I know that.”

“She’s the best thing to happen to you since Nicky.”

“I’m well aware.”

“Are you, Agatha? Or are you taking her for granted? This is your true second chance, and you might not get a third. Not everyone wants to take a chance on your playboy ass, or if they did, they would be after your money or other ulterior motives.”

Wanda’s words were like a punch to the gut. Had she been aware of how lucky she had been? Wanda was right. It was hard dating when you were Agatha Fucking Harkness. That’s what Swords had taught her, wasn’t it? Rio never cared about Agatha’s fame or her money. Half the time, Rio insisted on paying and got annoyed when Agatha beat her to it. The job was just now starting to bother Rio, and only because Agatha was about to be away for longer. Rio wasn’t interested in fame for herself or getting her foot in the door. Agatha looked at Wanda.

“I should have listened to you.”

“You fucking think?”

Nat handed Agatha her phone back.

“It’s fixed and secure. Don’t text Rio until I tell you it’s okay. I’m going over there now. Don’t make me keep secrets from her again.”

She looked at Agatha as if it were more of a threat than a warning before leaving.


⭐⭐⭐


Rio was eating her Half Baked ice cream straight from the container at ten on the morning of New Year’s, and she didn’t care who judged her for it. Her relationship was rocky, and her ex was still out there somewhere, most likely listening to everything she said. She and Alice sat on Rio’s bed, Raven trying to steal bites of ice cream from both of them. Alice was the friend she needed to be, but she was tired and had barely slept since she woke up the day before.

“Maybe I should just make a playlist and send it to her…” Rio was saying when Alice received a text from Nat.

“Hey, Nat’s coming over to clear your phone. Then, you need to try to go to sleep.” She looked at Rio and saw how exhausted she truly was.

“I don’t know if I can.” Rio sounded two inches tall.

“You can’t talk to Agatha like this. I’ll make Gran’s tea. That always knocked you out.” Alice said decisively as she stood, “Speaking of, do you…”

“In the safe,” Rio finished for her.

Alice smiled, glad Rio could read her mind.

“Thanks, I knew you’d keep Gran’s set.”

She walked into the closet before looking down to where the safe was hidden, seeing where the clothes had been tossed aside and the door to the safe hanging open.

“Rio, the safe…” Alice’s voice trailing off.

Rio placed the empty ice cream container on the end table, Raven practically pounced on it.  Rio scrambled off the bed to join Alice in the closet. She stood next to Alice, phone in the pocket of her pajama pants.

“She usually doesn’t leave a safe ajar,” Rio said sarcastically as she knelt to open it and looked inside. She knew all of Lou’s tricks and knew Lou would never figure out the code. “Everything’s here,” she said, handing Alice the rings and taking out the manilla folder that held Lorna’s papers.

Rio fished her phone from her pocket and muttered, “3, 2, 1…” and pressed the text as soon as it popped up.


Blocked

Just needed pictures of

documents baby.


Rio sighed. She might as well just speak aloud until Nat arrived. “Why leave the door open?”


Blocked

Call it a ‘heads up’.


“Why Lorna’s papers? What’s so important?” She turned her head to look at Alice questioningly, only to receive the same look back.


Blocked

Call it a hunch. I was

right, by the way.

Now I have important

information.


Rio rolled her eyes. “Lou, whatever it is you think you have, I promise you it’s nothing.”


Blocked

It’s about your sweet

Aggie.


Rio’s jaw clenched as she stood back up and looked at Alice. “What did your mom know?” She mouthed.

Alice shrugged, looking just as confused as Rio felt.

“You don’t get to call her Aggie, and whatever you have, she’s going to tell me.”


Blocked

You don’t really believe that.

Or else she would have told you

she loved you.


Rio opened her mouth to reply but couldn’t find the words. She and Alice turned as they heard Nat come in the front door. They took the contents from the safe and emerged from the closet.

“We’re in here,” Alice raised her voice loud enough for the other woman to hear.

Nat entered the room, and her eyes immediately fell on the envelope Rio was clenching. Nat took Rio’s phone and gestured with her head so they would follow her to the living room.

Alice grabbed the ice cream trash, which was licked clean by a certain tortoiseshell feline and spoons, and they followed.

Nat started to work her magic, and once the diagnostics were working and she was positive Lou could no longer hear them, she began talking.

“What is that?” She gestured towards the envelope.

“Lou was here,” Rio said blankly. “She made copies of whatever is in here, apparently information Lorna had on Agatha.” She looked up at Nat, eyes questioning.

“I don’t know the answers. If Agatha trusted anyone with absolutely everything, it was Lorna,” Nat answered.

Alice’s eyes were wide. “Lorna Wu? My mother? More than Wanda?”

“Wanda was still young, finding her way. She had a lot going on, meaning me. Agatha wasn’t going to burden her with anything extremely heavy. Lorna had her demons, but she loved and protected fiercely. Just like you do, Alice.”

Nat had a metal water bottle and took a drink from it. “I wish you two had gotten to know her like we did,” Nat gave them a half smile.

“Just getting to hear people talk about her the way you guys do is enough,” Alice said, her eyes glistening.

Nat handed Rio her phone back once it had finished. “Get some sleep and then text her, please. Wanda’s trying to get her to sleep as well. Everyone needs some damn sleep.” Nat yawned. “It won’t help to change the locks, will it?” Nat asked, knowing the answer as Rio shook her head. Lou would get past whatever they attempted. “Then I’m going bitch hunting,” Nat added with a dark smile.

They watched as she left before Alice turned back to Rio. “Right, so, Gran’s tea.” Alice looked at the thick envelope. “What are we going to do with that?”

Rio held it up and examined it. “Nothing yet. I’ll give Agatha a chance, and if I feel like I need to read it, okay. I may just give it to Agatha, honestly.” After all, it seemed like the information was all Agatha’s.


Rio and Alice still didn’t know what secrets Lorna would have had about Agatha that Lou would find interesting.


⭐⭐⭐


Agatha had napped on the couch, waiting for Nat to text her, telling her she could text or call Rio. She checked the time on her watch, eight p.m. “Ugh,” she groaned, knowing her sleep schedule would be off. There was also a text from Nat saying she could text Rio now. She felt slightly less tired but still like the terrible girlfriend she had been. All she wanted to do was hold Rio, run her fingers through her hair, apologize, and tell her everything. Well, almost everything. Some things may still be better off unsaid. She rolled onto her back and stared at the high ceiling of the living room.


“Why don’t you ever tell the whole truth?” A 21-year-old Agatha asked Swords with naive curiosity. Swords was still rejecting her, but they had run into each other at the New York club that Agatha secretly owned and were speaking as friends.

“Why don’t you want anyone to know you own this place?” Swords countered.

“I don’t want people to try and get in my good graces just for free drinks or to expect me always to be here. Or to think I’m trying to curate my own pool of women, as it were.” Agatha explained.

“Right. You want to uphold a certain image, and so do I.” Answered Swords. “ There are certain things I want to keep quiet about myself. I don’t mind that people know I have a life outside of my career or hold secrets by NDA, but being with some of the biggest names in Hollywood? I can’t have that. I can’t be accused of the things I would be accused of, kid.” Swords replied, flipping her hair back.

“Do you ever want kids…?” The question was out of her mouth before Agatha even thought about it.

Swords chuckled. “With my job? Probably not. I’m already getting past that age, and you know how hard it is to have a successful career and kids. Look at Lorna.”

Agatha nodded and looked down, trying to school her expression. Alice was five, and Lorna barely saw her. She said she was better off with Norma and away from this life where she would spend all of her time with nannies.

“It’s better to create the image you want people to see, Aggie. Keep a few people close and most distant. The world loves you and accepts your bad girl ways. Play into it. It makes you unique. Keep the new kid, the redhead, close. She’s interesting and your opposite. You want that balance. Be her Lorna. Keep Lorna close to you, too. Now, we’ve been seen together for far too long, and there is a very cute girl over there with pink hair that has been trying to get your eye.” Swords said as she left.


Swords had taught Agatha a lot over the years—the influence upon her life beyond quantifiable. Even if Swords had destroyed her, she had done some good things. However, the wound she intentionally or unintentionally inflicted left a scar so sensitive, that Agatha was still so hesitant to let herself love again.


Agatha was 27, and she and Swords were having a pleasant night in. They had had an actual date. Pizza, movies, and Swords was trying to develop Agatha’s taste for wine. Agatha preferred harder liquor and fruity things. However, Swords was trying to make her more sophisticated.

“You’re on Broadway, kid. You spend time with people like Meryl Streep and Cher. You need to drink more than Malibu, Grey Goose, and…are these Smirnoffs?” Swords said, looking through Agatha’s fridge.  It was summer, and they were in New York again.

“So? I’ve been on Broadway before, I won a Tony as a teenager, in case you’ve forgotten and Cher is more your friend. Lorna, Wanda, Drew, Madonna, none of them have problems with what I drink.” Agatha reached for a green Smirnoff, only for Swords to swat at her hand.

“None of them have an EGOT with another Broadway show forthcoming.” Swords was pouring a glass of wine. “A lady knows her wines.”

“I have never been accused of being a lady,” Agatha responded wryly.

“You certainly don’t fuck like one,” Swords laughed.

Agatha leaned against the counter, crossing her legs at the ankles. “You don’t have to get me drunk for that if that’s what you’re trying to do.”

Swords handed the wine glasses to Agatha as she grabbed the bottle and led Agatha to the living room of Agatha’s Manhattan apartment.

“Oh, I know I don’t.”

They sat, Agatha, tucking her legs under her. “...do you love me?”

Swords stilled as she turned towards Agatha.

“Of course I do,” she replied, eyebrows knitting together.

“No, I mean, are you in love with me?” Agatha took a deep breath. It’s just, that I am so deeply in love with you. You won’t let me tell anyone we’re seeing each other, while I want to scream it from the rooftops. I want to spend the rest of my life with you, and you laugh it off, insisting we see other people.” Agatha’s eyes were about to betray her as she tried to hold her tears back.

“Lorna knows,” was the only reply she was awarded: serious, curt, angry.

Agatha gave a small cry of frustration.

“Of course she does! I can’t help that she figured it out! Stop ignoring the question!”

Swords was surprised by Agatha’s tone.

“Yes, I love you, Agatha, but those things are impossible. I don’t want any of that, and you know it. I will not explain it to you again. Now, don’t go ruining a good night before it’s even begun.” Swords reached for Agatha’s cheek as Agatha retreated backward.

“Leave,” Agatha said abruptly.

“What?” Swords was shocked. Agatha had never kicked her out before.

“You heard me, leave.” She said, standing up and walking to her bedroom.

She heard Swords leave and sobbed, calling Lorna, who was so proud of her for standing up for herself.

Agatha begged Swords to forgive her four days later.


⭐⭐⭐


“No wonder I’m like this,” Agatha said out loud to no one as she picked up her phone. Swords had told her repeatedly never to grovel or beg, and finally, she had stopped. Stopped going back to her. She raised her phone again and typed out multiple messages before finally landing on one.


Me:

Jen listed the condo.

Rio, I am so sorry.

Can we please talk?



Okay, so Agatha probably should have told her in person, but she needed to get that off her chest. She stared at her phone, waiting for the response.



Rio read Agatha’s text. She knew Jen had listed the condo, and Alice had told her as much. She didn’t care about that, not really. She was mad that it had been a secret. Of course, she wanted to talk to Agatha, but where? If they talked at Rio’s, then she could kick Agatha out if she needed to. If they went to Agatha’s, Rio could very dramatically walk out. Either way, she wanted to see Agatha.

“You’re a sap, Vidal.” She said to herself.

Rio 💚

Alice told me.

Yes.

When?


There, the ball was in Agatha’s court. She could decide where, when, and everything.


Agatha was panicking. Did she want Rio to come over? Should they eat? Should she order something? Nicky was up at the clubhouse with the twins. She should order dinner and have Rio over, is what she should do, she finally decided.


Me:

Have you eaten?

I’ll order something?

Meet here in an hour?


Rio was biting her thumbnail, thinking. She was hungry, but part of her wanted to make Agatha wait. The problem with that was she would have to wait, too. She sighed as she typed back and got out of the bed she had been sleeping in to change into sweats without holes in them.


Rio 💚

Yes.

You better be ready.


Me:

I am.

Pizza?


Rio 💚

Yes, and good.


⭐⭐⭐


Rio was facing Agatha on their usual ends of the couch, pizza devoured, water cups on the coffee table, and Rio had music playing as she stared at Agatha.

“Time to come clean. I’m only going to ask you this one more time. Why don’t you date, Agatha?”

Agatha took a deep breath in through her nose and let it out through her mouth before beginning. “It’s not pretty,” She started.

“It doesn’t have to be,” Rio could handle it.

“A long time ago, I loved someone, and she destroyed me. I was young, twenty-three, and I had chased her for years. I call her Swords.” Agatha started.

Something clicked in Rio’s brain, “Your tattoo…it’s for her. She’s your heartbreak. You’ve lied to me since the first time we had sex.” Rio’s voice was hollow.


Agatha took Rio’s hand and tipped her chin to look into Rio’s big brown eyes.

“Rio, look at me. That was not the time to bring that up, okay? Was I supposed to say, ‘yeah, that’s for the ex I never told you about’ right after the best sex I’ve ever had?”

Agatha’s voice was sincere and Rio could tell that she was, in fact, being truthful.

“I’m sorry, best? Agatha, that’s hyperbolic.” Rio’s voice was skeptical.

Agatha shook her head.

“It’s not just sex with you, Rio. That’s why. It’s more than that, and it is for you, too, so don’t play it off. Now, may I continue, or are you going to keep giving me that disbelieving look?”

“You’re right. It is more than that.” Rio pushed her hair behind her ears and tried to put a more neutral expression on her face. “Okay, continue. You were twenty-three and in love with Swords.”


“Yes. She was much older, and no, I’m not telling you who she was.”

Rio had raised an eyebrow in question, and Agatha reached over and poked it, eliciting a small smile from the younger woman.

“We dated for roughly a decade on and off before I finally decided I had had enough, and I couldn’t take the pain anymore. I believe she loved the idea of me, but she wasn’t a marriage and family type of woman. I wanted it all, and she didn’t want anyone to know we were together. We weren’t monogamous; she never wanted to be, which was fine at first. She told me to keep up my reputation, and I did. She was the kind of woman that dealt in secrets and information, hidden identities, falsehoods, and half-truths,” Agatha let out an empty laugh. “She taught me everything I know. She’s the one who told me to keep a hidden condo and curate my image and what I let people see. She tried to talk me out of children for the longest time when I finally decided I wanted a child more than I wanted her.”

Agatha’s voice was full of sadness, but she didn’t cry. It felt good to get it off her chest, to confide in Rio.

“She worked with a lot of celebrities in different capacities and didn’t want being associated with me to screw it up. Imagine being associated with me being viewed as a bad thing.

“Wanda and Nat didn’t even know I was dating at the time. Not until it was over, and they still don’t know who she is to this day. Lorna was the only one who knew, and only because she knew Swords, too. I told Lorna everything back then.

“Anyway, I was madly in love with her, but she didn’t love me the same way, and every single time she broke my heart, I would just crawl back to her, and every single time she would act as if she were teaching me some valuable lesson.”

Agatha looked off to the side as though she saw the memories pass by.

“After I finally walked away, I told myself I would never let myself experience that heartbreak again. I threw all my love into my family, career, and other projects.

“Then you came along, and for the first time in eighteen years, I felt something more than mere lust or desire.”

Agatha cradled Rio’s cheek and wiped away a tear that had escaped one of her beautiful brown eyes.

“It terrifies me to feel this again, Rio. But, I know you wouldn’t hurt me.”


Rio was processing everything Agatha had just told her and felt tears on her cheeks for the young Agatha. Rio’s story wasn’t great, but ten years of a dead-end relationship with someone who was never completely yours because they didn’t want to be? What was it with the women around here having tragic love lives?

“Agatha, why didn’t you just tell me all of this in the first place?”

Rio searched Agatha’s eyes for something that told her she was being dishonest, and all she saw was pain and regret.


“Our original conversation had come to a natural end, and I didn’t know how to restart it. I was scared. I was so afraid it would be too much. I haven’t allowed myself to have any romantic feelings until you, and look at how much I’ve fucked it up.”

Agatha let out a sob as she dropped her forehead against Rio’s.

“I am so sorry, baby. Then, the apartment, I should have told you. Part of me was waiting to make this big declaration and listing it as this big symbol of shedding the last part of my old life when I should have done it from the start.”

Rio wiped Agatha’s tears as she lightly kissed her cheeks where the tears had been.

“Oh Agatha, I’m not surprised you had an apartment. It was the fact you hadn’t listed it yet, or even told me about it. We’ve barely been together for a month. I’m not stupid. You’ve just sounded so sure of everything, so set on this, on us. Then you throw out things like you’re not the marrying type.

“You refuse to call this what it is. It’s confusing. I know we’re still at the beginning of this, though it feels like I’ve known you my whole life, and everything comes so easy with you.”

Rio sat back, immediately missing the contact. Agatha blew out a breath and looked down at her hands.

“Again, this terrifies me. You have seen me in some of my most emotional states, Rio. Yes, I begged you to let me in and bare your soul to me, and you did. This is me laying it all out to you,” most of it, Agatha thought, a small pang of guilt slicing through her heart. She still felt like she had to protect Swords. There were even darker secrets to be told, and she was still trying to control the narrative of her image. Some secrets were between her, Swords, and Lorna. “I just don’t know if I can say those words yet, and I am so furious at myself for that because I want to, and yet, I can’t.” Agatha started crying again. Rio put her hands on Agatha’s knees.

“Agatha? Do you believe in soulmates?” She asked, echoes of young Agatha’s voice echoing in her ears.

“I thought I did once,” Agatha said timidly.

“I’m beginning to think I do, and Agatha, with how quickly we’ve come together, how quickly I’ve fallen for you, and how natural everything seems? I don’t think there’s any other explanation, but it shouldn’t be this hard to admit it.” Rio was going back and forth. Should she tell Agatha, and just not expect anything back? Should she leave? There was no script for this.

“You were so worried that you were broken, and you are not, Rio. People did things to you, yes, but that’s not you. You were not broken. Being with you is as easy as breathing, Rio. I am damaged. I am the one that has to learn how to do all these things normally. You had every right to be afraid, and you went for it. I completely understand if you need to walk away, if you need us to take a break for a while…” Agatha’s tears flowed freely now, and there was no use trying to get them to stop. She knew her eyes were red and puffy, and she couldn’t imagine what would happen if the deepest, darkest secrets ever came out. Swords was a completely different person now, and she didn’t want to cause her pain or potential pain to Alice if she knew that her own mother had known.

Rio had been looking down as she played with the hem of her sweats, and her head snapped up. “A break? Excuse me? You’re about to go to Canada, and you want to take a break?” Her voice was bordering on hysterical.

Agatha’s hands had been in her lap, and she quickly took Rio’s hands. “Baby, no, I was saying if you wanted a break because I can’t tell you how much…” Her voice trailed off, not able to say the words. Something deep within her told her she couldn’t without telling her everything, and how was she supposed to do that?
