Rio gave her a defeated nod as she stood. “I don’t want a break, and I’m not walking away. However, I am exhausted, and we both need sleep.” She stood there for several minutes, looking between the stairs, in the direction of the front door, and at Agatha.

Agatha watched as Rio struggled to make her decision before Rio finally sighed and grabbed Agatha’s hand, hauling her to her feet.

“I’ve developed a bad habit; we’re going to bed,” Rio said, her voice shaking.


Minutes later, Rio was drifting off to sleep in Agatha’s arms. She wasn’t as peaceful as usual, but she was where she wanted to be. She knew Agatha well enough by now to know that Agatha hadn’t been completely honest with her and was still holding back. Agatha’s breathing had evened out, and upon checking, Rio deemed that she was asleep.

“I love you, Agatha,” she whispered before falling into a fitful sleep.

Agatha hadn’t been completely asleep when Rio’s whispered words filled the dark bedroom. Her heart stuttered, and she wished she could return the sentiment and show Rio how much she loved her. Instead, coward that she was, Agatha continued to feign sleep.


⭐⭐⭐


Lou had heard the whole conversation. She couldn’t listen into their phones anymore, but she was still tapped into the houses through the pet cameras and mic. She looked down at the copies of the documents she had taken pictures of. The marriage certificate that had never been filed or signed, with the other name blacked out. The ultrasound pictures dated 6 weeks, 8 weeks, 10 weeks, and 11 weeks, with Harkness, Agatha in the top corner. Lou had guessed what had happened after that, seeing as how they were from two years before Nicky was born. She had also taken pictures of pages from Lorna’s journal, although even she had used the nickname for Agatha’s ex. It was all spelled out there in print. She just had to convince Rio to look at the envelope of evidence and prove to Rio that Agatha was still refusing to tell her the whole truth.


⭐⭐⭐


Agatha was still awake with Rio asleep in her arms when Nat’s text came through around one, letting Agatha know that Lou had whatever secrets Lorna had and may have kept. Agatha turned her head toward Rio with a tortured look. Rio mumbled in her sleep, and Agatha only caught every few words. Lyrics to Taylor Swift songs, something about ‘just tell me’ and ‘fucking Lou’. Agatha ran her fingers through Rio’s hair and thought about the past few months. The day she met Rio, or well, met grown-up adult Rio. She had been stopped in her tracks by Rio’s warm mahogany eyes while surrounded by the smell of Axe. It would absolutely be a story one day. Rio was right in how easy and natural everything was between them. Agatha would never have crawled into bed with someone just to cuddle. The great Agatha Harkness had been brought to her knees by this woman in less than a month and had tried to ignore it until she couldn’t any longer.

Then, the scrapbook Rio had made her, the pictures, the things Rio had remembered to write, the lyrics. Rio often spoke in lyrics, movie and TV quotes, and pop culture references. She had put all of that into the scrapbook and enlisted Wanda’s help with pictures, pulling them from every available source. The woman was as cunning as Agatha was, and Rio had left blank spaces around the picture of Agatha; she assumed she would fill in the future. However, there were blank spaces around a few other pictures.

It was then that the light bulb went off. Rio had seen every single one of Agatha’s movies, including the most recent one. Death’s Beloved and a few of the mystery and action ones had included black light ink. Agatha carefully removed herself from beneath Rio, kissing her lightly on the forehead as she left the bed, grabbed the scrapbook off her nightstand, and went to Nicky’s room. She knew he had a blacklight pen left over from a spy-themed birthday party from a few years ago. She found it and took the two items downstairs. She settled on the couch cross-legged, opened the scrapbook in her lap, and turned on the blacklight.

The picture Wanda had taken of them at the back-to-school party. They were sitting on the beach, talking animatedly with the sunset in front of them. She scanned the page as hidden lyrics popped out, separated on the page. ‘Met you at the right time’; ‘Livin’ in a movie I’ve watched,’; ‘Funny, ‘cause you couldn’t have called it’.

She flipped through pages, a movie night where Wanda had taken a picture of them cuddled on the couch, Rio’s head in Agatha’s lap, Agatha playing with Rio’s hair and talking to Jen or Alice off camera. In blacklight ink, lyrics from ‘Enchanted’, a recurring song throughout so far, ‘two a.m. who do you love?’, ‘please don’t be in love with someone else’. That picture had been taken in early October, it seemed.

Pictures from New York, a paparazzi shot of all things, but a damn good one. Above this one, ‘We fell in love in October, that’s why I love fall.’ Agatha waited for her heart to stop, for some wild reaction, raised anxiety, and nothing. She was calm as a warmth washed over her, and she smiled. Agatha had started falling for Rio before October, but that was a definite turning point.

A picture from their first date, ‘All my life, I’ve been looking for this’. Another date, ‘For me, it’s always you’. Thanksgiving and the social media posts, ‘Is it alright to feel this way so early?’, ‘You’re the risk I’m gonna take it’. A picture from the winter dance, ‘Too soon to tell you I love you’. Rio and Agatha on the couch in Nicky’s hospital room. Wanda had taken it because she thought it was sweet. Agatha had succumbed to emotional exhaustion, and Rio was holding her as if she was trying to protect her from everything going on in the world, “I want you for worse or for better.”


The hidden messages combined with what had already been written only amplified the meaning of the gift that Agatha held. She did her best not to get any tears on the scrapbook and was thankful for the page protectors. She knew this had been made with care and love, but it was so much more. It was the words Rio had said to her just a few hours before. Then the picture of Agatha. She took a deep breath as the blacklight landed on the page, and it lit up like Coachella in the middle of the desert.

‘I wanna teach you how forever feels,’; ‘You could absolutely break my heart, that’s how I know we’re in love,’; ‘I’m gonna give you my heart’; ‘I want the secrets your secrets haven’t found,’;

‘What if I let it slip? (Tell you that oh, my God, I fucking love you)’.

At the bottom, there was an arrow telling her to turn the page, and on the back was a letter from Rio.

Agatha,

As I am writing this, it has been roughly five months since we met at the fieldhouse at SHIELD. We have been through so much in that time. Our friends will say we put them through a slow-burn romance, though compared to those four, we sped through this. I know it’s a stereotype that sapphics move rather quickly, but I think we just go with what feels right, and this does.

I grew up watching you and idolizing you. Never in my wildest dreams did I think I would ever be friends with you, let alone one of your inner circle. Then, we fell for each other; you kissed me, you took me on dates, Agatha Fucking Harkness swept me off my feet, and fuck it, I fell in love with you and am finally admitting it to myself.

I hope when you find this, we’ve either finally admitted it or you’ve at least figured out how you feel. I feel like we’ve lived a movie of our own, full of drama, and we deserve a happy ending.

Please be my happily ever after.


Love, Rio.



Agatha’s hands were shaking as she closed the scrapbook, turned off the pen, and placed them both on the coffee table before her. She wasn’t shaking from Rio’s confession, but because she knew without a shadow of a doubt that she was ready to tell Rio what she hadn’t told her earlier. Fuck Swords, she could protect herself, and Rio would never find out who she was anyway.

“I see you figured out the secret?” Rio’s voice came from behind the couch, starting Agatha. She hadn’t even heard her.

Agatha hummed as Rio sat next to Agatha, curling her legs underneath her arm on the back of the couch. Agatha turned to face her, mirroring their positions from earlier in the night.

“I did, and I love everything in it. Everything.”

She made sure to hold Rio’s gaze as she said it. Agatha cupped the back of Rio’s neck with one hand and her cheek with the other as she pressed her lips to Rio’s. Agatha’s thumb caressed gentle circles along Rio’s jaw as she kissed Rio tenderly. As Rio attempted to deepen the kiss, Agatha reluctantly pulled back. Rio made a whining sound at the sudden loss as she watched Agatha sit back.

“Rio, there are some things I need to tell you before we go any further,” Agatha was finally ready to share some of her deepest and darkest secrets that only two other people knew, and one of them was no longer alive.

Rio nodded and sighed. “I suspected there was more,” Rio said bitterly, looking away.

“I’m going to explain that too, okay?” Agatha looked at Rio as if seeking her permission and approval to continue.

Rio looked back at Agatha, “Okay, Agatha.”

She could see the resignation written on Agatha’s face and could tell that Agatha truly was ready to get everything out in the open. Agatha took Rio’s hands in hers.

“First, you need to understand that outside of professionals, the only other people who knew about these events were myself, Lorna, and Swords. The reason I didn’t tell you earlier was that I wanted to control my own narrative and protect Swords. However, I believe your feelings about me won’t change, and Swords can protect herself. I’m still not telling you who she is, just in case your paths ever cross.” Agatha looked at Rio for confirmation.

Rio nodded, “I knew there was still something, which is why I wasn’t sleeping well and then came down here when you weren’t in bed.”

“You were right. However, there’s something I need to tell you first. I wasn’t asleep earlier, and then with everything written in there,” Agatha gestured toward what was now one of her most prized possessions, sitting on the table. “I’ve been running from my feelings, and I have to stop. Rio Vidal, I am absolutely in love with you.”

These were not words Rio was expecting to hear yet, and they were words she was willing to wait for, but hearing them made her heart almost burst with joy. She knew Agatha had things to tell her, but right now, she was just happy that Agatha Fucking Harkness was in love with her and finally admitted it. Rio surged forward, grabbing Agatha by the shoulders to kiss her hard and fast. There would be time for more later, but she had to kiss her right now. As Rio leaned back, she looked at Agatha and grinned.

“I love you too, Agatha Harkness. Now, tell me everything.”

Agatha smiled at her, feeling at peace even though she was about to share a part of her that she hadn’t shared with anyone. “I am going to, but I’m old, and I have spent too much time on this couch and not enough time holding you.” Agatha stood, holding out her hand for Rio. As they went back upstairs, Agatha began. “Remember how I told you that Swords didn’t want kids?”

Rio nodded as they walked back into Agatha’s room.

“Well, she continuously talked me out of it, and finally, at 33, I decided I would with or without her.”

They had assumed their normal position, and Agatha sighed contentedly. Something about holding Rio gave her the confidence she needed to get through this. Agatha continued.

“We had had a discussion, and I had asked her one last time about kids, and she shut it down and told me I couldn’t risk becoming like my mother. She didn’t know that I was already going through fertility treatments and had chosen a donor, the whole nine yards. It was my roundabout way of seeing where she was at for the final time. She shot it down, so I knew what would happen if I succeeded.”

Agatha was looking down at Rio, who was, in turn, looking up at her. They had perfected this way of lying and talking, Agatha running her hand up and down Rio’s side, Rio’s hand in Agatha’s hair.

Rio was doing the math. Nicky was born in 2008 when Agatha was 35. It had been a thing, the world’s favorite lesbian becoming a single mom thanks to science. It put women’s healthcare and fertility treatments in the spotlight. Yet another trailblazing moment for Agatha Harkness.

“Agatha, please tell me that it was near the end of the year, and it took a bit to get pregnant,” Rio looked at Agatha, concerned. Nicky’s birthday was in June, and Agatha’s was in March. She had been barely 35 when she had Nicky, and the math wasn’t mathing the way Rio wanted it to.

Agatha gave Rio a sad smile.

“No, baby. We’re getting there. I got pregnant pretty quickly. It was September 2006 when I told her about it. Anyway, Wanda and Nat were out of the country as Wanda was filming. Lorna knew, though. I wasn’t trying to baby-trap Swords. I made sure she knew that. I gave her the option to break up, and we did. Then, something changed her mind in October, and she decided to marry me and have a family. I had asked her so many times if she was sure, and she said yes.” Agatha sighed. “I lost the baby a few days before the wedding and told Swords. There I was, in November, on the beach with Lorna, a white dress and all…” Agatha’s voice trailed off, and Rio desperately tried to fill in the blanks and hurt this obviously caused the woman she loved.

“Did she use the loss as her way out? To leave you at the altar? I’ll kill the bitch!” While she was so thankful she had Agatha, her protective side wanted to throw hands.

Agatha shook her head.

“Not the main reason, no. She initially put her career first, but it was a reason. She said the pregnancy clouded her judgment, and to play devil’s advocate, she never did want marriage and kids. I was still shattered, though. I was so desperately in love with her, and that’s the day I decided I was done with her.”

Rio’s heart hurt for Agatha. This woman, who seemingly had everything, hadn’t been allowed to have the one thing she wanted most: a relationship.

“Does she work in Hollywood?” Rio asked softly.

Agatha nodded.

“Yes, and before you ask, yes. We still come in contact with each other, and she has changed since then.”

She saw the question in Rio’s eyes.

“No, I wouldn’t. She could beg me to come back, and I would tell her no. She missed her chance and absolutely destroyed me. Comparing this to that, well, there’s no comparison. This? What you and I are building? This is pure. It’s not built on youthful infatuation and puppy love. We went in with eyes wide open and knew the risks. We have mutual respect, and we have our autonomy. This?” She gestured between them, “is real, Rio.”

Agatha had bared everything except for Swords’ true identity and how they met. She finally felt free of the secrets she had been holding.

Rio felt the intensity of the moment as she stroked along Agatha’s jaw and down the length of her neck. Her thoughts were overwhelmingly crowded, and she couldn’t separate them. What she did know was that she loved Agatha more than she had ever loved before and she couldn’t imagine why someone would throw away the opportunity to spend their life with Agatha and throw it away for some dumb career in Hollywood. She hoped Swords’ money and, Rio assumed, career made Swords happy because Rio was the true winner in this. Rio kissed Agatha before she looked at her again.

“I have questions,” Rio stated.

“I’m sure you do.”

“What does she say about Nicky?”

Agatha took a moment to consider her response.
“Honestly, I think she’s surprised I’m this involved.”

“Good. That bitch,” Rio smirked. If she ever found out who Swords was, she couldn’t promise niceties.

Agatha huffed a laugh, “What if it’s someone you like? It could be Cher.”

Rio looked scandalized. “She is twenty-seven years older than you.”

“The fact that you automatically knew that isn’t surprising at all. Would you believe that Cher’s “Believe” is about me? But no, it’s not her.” Agatha hated that damn song. “I have a question for you, though, about something you mentioned previously.” Agatha bit the inside of her lip, not sure how she was going to respond to the answer.

Rio knew that Agatha had picked up on what she had said and knew this was coming as she motioned for Agatha to go on.

“When you said that you and the supermodel from hell had been talking about marriage and planning a future…” Agatha trailed off, letting Rio take it from there.

“Yes,” Rio answered the unasked question. “We were talking about kids, even hypothetically planning it, very ‘talking rings and talking cradles’, though I know now that she is not the love nor the loss of my life. I told you Labor Day weekend that it was dependent upon who I was with, and you do come with a kid. Three, technically.”

This was not a conversation Rio wanted to have right now. They both needed sleep, and she wanted to engage in other activities once she knew what day it was.

Agatha picked up on Rio’s hesitation and left it for now. She knew they needed to sleep, though that was not the activity she wanted to engage in. Being responsible was not for the faint of heart. However, neither of them had slept much in the past forty-eight hours, and that would not do for what she had planned.

“We can revisit everything later. Our sleep schedules are now nonexistent. It might be Thursday, and we have a big weekend ahead of us,” Agatha said as she kissed Rio’s forehead and settled in more. “Besides, I need energy to show you how much I love you. I hope you don’t have plans later today.”


⭐⭐⭐

By Sunday morning, sleep schedules had returned to somewhat normal, and award season was kicking off. Agatha’s house was full of makeup artists, hairstylists, and stylists, and the boys had been sent on yet another caffeine run.

Wanda and Agatha were having their hair done in Agatha’s bathroom, attempting to talk while having makeup applied at the same time.

“I am so excited for you actually to have Nat with you as your date, Superstar,” Agatha said, careful not to move her eyes.

They would have other security with them. Wanda deserved this. Vision was glad he didn’t have to do the whole dog and pony show and could just sit in the family sections with the boys and Alice. Jen would be working and checking in with everyone as she could. She had already briefed red-carpet correspondents and was currently texting other PR reps. Several of them wanted to make sure their celebrities were able to get pictures with Agatha and Wanda. Several young actresses were especially interested in being in Agatha’s orbit, and Rio was not amused.

Wanda was trying very hard to keep down the emotions. She had been waiting for this for so long, and it was finally here. She would get to be seen, very publicly, with the true love of her life. They were skipping the whole girlfriend title, Jen putting them out there as partners. If she could, they would already be married, but even no-contest divorces in California took six months to finalize. No amount of fame could get around that. She and Agatha both tried.

“It’s still so surreal. I finally get to be public with her, and it’s absurd to me the way it happened,” she was trying to talk without moving her mouth, and after all these years, she and Agatha could understand each other perfectly.

Agatha smiled and was promptly scolded, “When are you proposing?”

“As soon as I fucking can. We know we’re getting married. We didn’t even have to have that discussion. I still want to give her the proposal she deserves. Enough about me, I’ll get in trouble if I cry and ruin my makeup. When are you proposing?” Wanda grinned with her eyes as she was instructed to hold her mouth a certain way for lipstick.

Agatha choked on her spit. “I just told her I love her, Wanda,” A chorus of ‘awws’ went up from everyone within earshot. “I am not ready to think about marriage,” It was mostly true. Agatha had absolutely thought about it, and even though she didn’t believe you had to get married, she wanted to. Just not anytime soon.

“Oh, please. You would move her in here yesterday if you could. The two of you have played house all winter break. Billy called her Aunt Rio yesterday, and no one blinked an eye. Raven practically lives here now.”

“That’s moving in, Wanda. Not marriage.”

Agatha had thought about that one, though, and would suggest it when Rio’s lease came up for renewal this summer. Wanda rolled her eyes as she started getting dressed.

“At least you’re moving faster than anyone else did around here.”

⭐⭐⭐


The red carpet for the Golden Globes was crowded with interview stations set up everywhere and cameras constantly flashing. The audience area was full of screaming fans, and many celebrities were taking pictures or stopping near the area so their pictures could be taken. Agatha and Wanda were stopped continuously, which meant Nat and Rio were as well. Wagatha were two of the most sought-after celebrities on the carpet, as were their current headlines. Wanda had an upcoming interview with Drew and everyone had questions about what she was going to say, though her only answer was, “You’ll just have to wait.” The two megastars were more than happy to talk about the film, talk up their fellow nominees, and gush about the women with them.

With the hype surrounding Death’s Beloved, Wanda and Agatha had used their red carpet looks to pay homage to their characters, as well as incorporate their signature colors. Wanda had gone with a one-shoulder floor-length black dress that was gathered at the right side with a ruby-encrusted silver skull, an ode to her role as a serial killer. The dress was cut up to her thigh on the same side and she wore red heels. Her hair was down in a side part, ruby red earrings that matched the heart necklace Nat gave her for Christmas.

Wanda had convinced Nat to wear something that didn’t make her blend in or scream bodyguard, and Wanda was kicking herself for it because all Wanda wanted to do was skip the ceremony and go right back to the compound to their newly redesigned and refurnished bedroom and take the strapless dark red dress right back off Nat. Nat’s hair was up, and Wanda kept staring at Nat’s neck, thankful for makeup that covered the bruises and bitemarks Wanda may or may not have put there. Nat’s dress also had a high slit, and Wanda was making a mental note that Nat was to wear suits at awards shows for the rest of their lives, as she wondered if anyone would notice if they went missing for a while.

“You’re drooling,” Agatha whispered in Wanda’s ear. “I get you’re making up for lost time, but you’re not ready for quickies at awards shows, angel. I’m the punchline for that joke in every single opening monologue.”

Agatha knew there would be at least one joke at her expense, most likely about her reputation. One year, it was a joke about looking under your seat for your designated time to meet up with Agatha in the bathroom. The joke was on them, though, that year. She had scoped out a room in the back and a beauty with whom to share it.

Agatha had leaned into the FBI agent she played with a perfectly tailored suit. However, she opted for an eggplant purple corset underneath, her signature brooch perfectly in place, hair up in a twist, and the same purple stilettos she had worn on her first date with Rio.

Agatha turned to Rio and squeezed her hand.

“Are you ready for all of this?”

Agatha gave her girlfriend a comforting smile and a quick kiss on the cheek, grazing the corner of Rio’s mouth. Rio nodded.

“It’s time to get used to it, and I’ve had media training from Jen.”

Rio’s smile was electric and purely for Agatha. She hadn’t been in Agatha’s life when she made the movie, but she was still extremely proud of her and her accomplishments. Rio wore a forest green halter dress that came down to mid-thigh. It was draped on her left thigh in a way that showed off a silver thigh chain that had amethyst stones and an amethyst A. She wore black strappy heels, and her shooting star necklace, and her hair was in loose waves around her shoulders.

They chatted with friends, and the starlets were pulled and directed several different ways. Rio received several hugs from students' parents, some of whom were nominated in the same categories as Agatha, Wanda, or the movie itself.

Once the chaos of the red carpet was over, they entered the main hall and found their seats. The host’s monologue started, and of course, several jokes were aimed at Agatha and Wanda, who at this point of their career, took it in stride.

“It’s a sad day for the women in attendance. If you haven’t had your gay awakening yet, Hollywood’s sapphic tour guide, Agatha Harkness, is in a relationship and is no longer offering her services. In a surprising turn of events, the straightest woman ever, Wanda Maximmoff, is a  lesbian. So if any of you had ‘hell freezes over’ on your Bingo cards for this year, cross that one out.”

“Death’s Beloved is set to win everything at every awards show this year. So, if you are nominated in a category against them, you may go ahead and go to your after-parties. Sorry, everyone.”

There were jokes at Tony’s expense and a few of their other friends before getting started on the awards. The first category was Film Supporting Actress, Wanda’s category. Why was Wanda shocked when she won? The coven would never understand.

Wanda immediately turned and kissed Nat. For the first time accepting an award, she was fucking kissing Nat. She had been waiting for the moment she could thank Nat from a podium and it was finally happening. She was shaking and had frozen. Nat mouthed, ‘Go, this isn’t your first time, go!’

Agatha grabbed Wanda’s hand and pulled her towards the stage. Wanda looked back at Nat, words formulating in her head. By the time they got to the mic and Wanda the award, Wanda nodded at Agatha, indicating she could handle it. Agatha stepped back next to the presenters.

Wanda took a deep breath and focused on Nat.

“I haven’t frozen when winning an award since I was a kid, and Agatha,” she looked at her best friend. “Was the one that dragged me to the stage then too. I have been waiting to say this on stage since I was in my twenties. Natasha, thank you so much for supporting me and always being there for me, you’ve always been there for me. I love you so much.”

Just the responses to that were deafening, and she laughed.

“Rarely do thank you speeches get that kind of response. I’d also like to thank Vis, Billy, Tommy, Agatha, Alice, Rio, Nicky, and Jen for supporting me and being there for me for all of these years and being the best family I could have ever hoped for…”

She thanked the usual people associated with the film and gave a heartfelt thank you to the fans before adding. “Always live your truth. You, you can’t get back lost time.”

Wanda looked at Agatha, who grabbed her hand, and they walked off together. Nat met them backstage to go with Wanda to do a follow-up press interview, and Agatha went to get ready for her musical performance.

Rio checked in on social media, most of which was positive, and she put it back in her clutch. Agatha would be performing the song from the movie soon, and Rio was excited to see Agatha perform live, something Rio had never been able to do. Rio was very thankful for her position at SHIELD as Rio knew quite a few people in attendance, and even though her three table companions were currently missing, Rio still had people to talk to and didn’t feel out of place. They had come back from a commercial break, and the hostess commented on Wanda being the only person ever to thank a soon-to-be ex in an acceptance speech.

Tony announced Agatha, as the board always thought it was funny when the two of them presented or announced each other, as they infamously did not get along. This time, he added, “the woman who happened to partially raise my daughter’s boyfriend, though I attribute all of his good qualities to Wanda, Agatha Harkness.” They always tried to throw each other off their game.

Agatha had been prepared and, thanks to her in-ears, was completely unphased. The hauntingly beautiful melody began, and the spotlight fell on Agatha, who was wearing a black ball gown as she began to sing. Agatha’s eyes were on Rio the entire time, and the song was quickly becoming a favorite. The experience was over incredibly too soon, and she was covered in goosebumps.

Everyone had returned to the table, and Agatha was back in her original outfit, arm draped over Rio’s shoulders as they watched friends and acquaintances get flowers for their hard work over the past year. Death’s Beloved lost Best Screenplay but won for Director and Score.

Best Song was up next, and while Agatha was used to this and calm, Rio was on edge. She had always been nervous watching from home, but being here, dating and in love with Agatha, was different and much more intense.

As Agatha’s name was announced, she leaned over and whispered to Rio, “I save my big speeches, don’t get mad.”

Agatha kissed Rio and made her way to the stage, where she met with the songwriters, making sure they went first. Then, she gave her very practiced Agatha Harkness smile and thirty-second speech. She thanked her family and friends, everyone who worked on the song, and that was it.

Wanda leaned over to Rio.

“She’s taking a calculated risk,” Wanda explained.

“I know, I’ve seen this show before,” and Rio had. She knew that the older Agatha got, the less joint awards became about her. That wasn’t the case when Agatha was younger, but now it was.

Agatha and everyone else were right back on the stage after the next commercial break for Film Cinematic and Box Office Achievement.

It had been a long evening, and it was only eight when the last four categories arrived. Two of which were theirs, Film Drama Actress and Film Drama. First up was the Actress category, and Rio squeezed Agatha’s hands as they went through the nominees. She knew Agatha was the favorite, and that was her calculated risk. Rio wasn’t surprised Agatha won, but she was still elated.

Agatha loved it when she won for her sapphic performances. This film hadn’t pushed an agenda, it was just the story, and that story happened to be queer. Agatha smiled as she kissed Rio, reaching back to smack Wanda on the shoulder as Wanda made snide comments behind her. When Agatha reached the stage, she blinked against the blinding lights and looked around the room.

“Wow, thank you for this,” Agatha’s eyes fell on Rio. “First, I’d like to thank Rio, my amazing girlfriend, for supporting me through the end of this past year and being there for all of us. I love you so much, and you are one of the best parts of my life. Thank you to my amazing family and friends. Wanda, thank you for convincingly playing my wife. That’s absolutely why we won tonight, and I am so proud of you, kid. Thank you to everyone who worked on this film and, most importantly, supported this film. Knowing that ALL stories are normalized now is truly a great thing to see, and I am so proud to be a part of it. To our boys, thank you for supporting us, and I am so proud of the young men you are becoming. Again, a huge thank you to everyone and all of you beautiful humans in this room. You’re all amazing.”

Agatha waved as she walked off to applause. She would do the media and press later. The movie was up for Film Drama in roughly ten minutes. She made her way back to the table, hugging people as she walked past them. She kissed Rio again as she sat down,

“I love you, Agatha.” Were the first words out of Rio’s mouth once they broke the kiss.

Agatha gave her a genuine smile. “I love you, too. I am exhausted, though.”

Nat leaned over Wanda to Rio.

“Almost done,  and put your shoes back on,” she instructed.

They had changed from heels to something more comfortable for most of the ceremony, Wanda and Agatha only changing when they were up for something.

Rio gave her a questioning look, and Agatha answered, “More press after, babe.”

Rio sighed. “The fact that I am wearing heels for you is a testament to my love.”


Death’s Beloved won, and the awards season was off to a great start. As they headed to an after-party, Rio was reminded that they all returned to the real world in a few days. Everyone would resume filming, work, or school on Wednesday, and she dreaded it. For now, she reveled in the fact that Agatha Harkness loved her and everything was right in their world.

Chapter End Notes

The next chapter or two will be rainbows, butterflies, smut, social media reactions, all the smooth sailing things before we have some final drama.

The main fic will conclude at the end of SHIELD's school year. Then we'll have one-shots, lore, the Lorna story, the Swords story, etc.

I do post sneak peeks during the week, and pics of Raven as everything must meet her standards. Of course.

Y'all are amazing.

Safe As Kittens

Chapter Summary

Everything is calm and right in the world of our beloved coven. Agatha is filming and coming home on weekends, Rio has a new role at SHIELD, and Wanda has her interview with Drew.

A nice, calm month-ish with Oscar nominations, the Grammy's and a realization for Nicky.

Chapter Notes

Triggers: SMUT. Two scenes. SPICE. Bit of self-inflicted blood, the woman was feral, okay?

The Grammy's will be a one-shot later, because this year's Grammy's were a hoot and a half and inserting Agatha and Rio sounds like a blast to me.

Note: I kept Sokovia as a real country, so Wanda's native language is Romanian (sticking to the show). Nat occasionally speaks to her in Romanian and Wanda speaks to Nat in Russian, because they're effing adorable.

Thank you all for the love and for reading the words! More fluffiness and potentially getting back to some drama next chapter...

Oh! I've been obsessing over curating the perfect 'soundtrack' (Spotify playlist) for the past week). I need your recommendations for songs for each chapter. Thanks!

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Harkness and Maximoff Stun on Red Carpet

Death’s Beloved Sweeps Golden Globes

Harkness and Maximoff Thank Girlfriends

“Did you guys see how happy Wanda looked walking that carpet with Natasha?”

“I saw how horny Wanda looked…”

“Nat sure did have a lot of makeup on…”

“Leave them alone, they’re adorable.”

“Who will play them in the movie they’ll make about it?”

“Do we know if Nat acts?”

“Does Chappell act?”

“Agatha and Rio, I LOVE THEM!!”

“That thigh chain Rio has?!”

“It’s giving lesbian Guilty As Sin.”

“How do I get Vis’s number though? Surely he needs support during these trying times.”

“He has got to be one of the best men out there. Seriously, as if a woman wrote him.”

“He’s going to have women throwing themselves at him.”

“While I’m sad Wagatha isn’t endgame, they are adorable together.”

“Can Agatha Harkness be my personal emotional support lesbian too?”

“I want her to be the reason I need a personal emotional support lesbian.”

“Judging by the looks Rio gave some of the women that approached Agatha, you would need a coroner if you even got close.”

“Are we actually in the timeline where Agatha settles down?”

“Pour one out y’all.”

“The Wagatha boys are so proud of their parents!”

“Did you guys catch the ring on Alice Wu’s RING FINGER?!”

“Wait, LORNA’S daughter?!”

“Where’s the screencap? Someone find Jennifer Kale’s left hand!”

“RING SPOTTED ON KALE’S LEFT HAND!”

“How has Rio not posted anything about her best friend being engaged to her girlfriend’s fixer?”

“Do you guys ever think we’re too parasocial?”

“Don’t overthink it.”

“Agatha is definitely going to win the Grammy and an Oscar! Triple EGOT!”

“I swear this woman is a witch.”

Rio just shook her head as she closed out of her socials. While some comments she could do without, some just made her laugh. Class started soon, and it would officially be the Spring semester at SHIELD. Agatha had left that morning, though she would be back on Friday. Rio was trying to readjust to being back in a routine, and her pack of teenagers calling her Ms. Vidal again. With all of the developments over the break, she was not surprised when Lilia strolled in.

Rio put her phone down. She looked up and saw a flustered Sicilian in front of her.

Rio’s eyes filled with concern as she checked the time. It was another fifteen minutes before students would be in class, and Lilia was usually in her office preparing for the morning announcements.

“Rio, I need you to fill in for Coach Carter for the girl's basketball team for the rest of the season.”

Rio loved basketball. She had played for years, kept up with the WNBA, and used to coach. However, she didn’t think Lilia knew any of that.

“Why me?”

“You’re one of the tallest actual lesbians on staff. I assume you play. Not to mention, you wear Phoenix Mercury shirts often. You played in college and coached at your last school. I did read your resume, Vidal.” Lilia kept looking at her phone as if waiting for a call or message at any moment.

“Did Peggy go into labor this morning?”

Rio knew Peggy was due soon. She was far enough along that the baby would be healthy, and that was the only reason she could think of for her to miss the rest of the season, though Rio could have sworn she saw Peggy in the lounge earlier.

“Not yet. Anyway, Daisy Johnson will fill you in on everything,” Lilia’s phone rang, and she answered automatically.

“Yes, Steve, I’m on my way there right now. Go ahead and take her to the hospital, well, I told you both she would go early…” Lilia’s voice faded as she left Rio’s room and rushed down the hallway.

Rio shook her head as Alice popped her head in the doorway, “Peggy’s having a baby!” Alice said excitedly.

“I know. Lilia just assigned me to the girls’ basketball staff. She’s psychic, I swear. So unless you want to be stuck here after school, separate cars it is.” Rio explained.

“Bonding with your future daughter-in-law!” Alice grinned as she ducked back into the hallway when the rock-shaped stress ball sailed past Alice’s head.

Rio had decided to take lunch in her classroom so she could go over the folder Morgan had brought her from Coach Johnson. The roster, remaining schedule, and potential for the playoffs were all there. She wasn’t sure what athletic voodoo or super serums Rogers and Carter were potentially giving their athletes, but the girl’s basketball team was potentially better than the football team and had a better chance of going to state. Morgan, and a few of the other girls, were already being scouted by universities as juniors. SHIELD held many surprises and the sheer amount of budding athletes running around this place was one of them. According to Alice, this was the first year in at least a decade that the SHIELD Superstars had done well in any sports outside of track and archery. Rio hadn’t wanted to tell Lilia, but she was excited about the coaching opportunity. The season was cut short for her last year when her previous school pulled her from the coaching staff the millisecond she filed the restraining order against Lou.

She knew this was just for the rest of the year, and she was only the assistant coach, but it felt good to get back into it. She already had ideas for a staff vs. student game and hoped she could get on the court with the team. As she began reliving memories of her days playing in high school and college, she received a text from Agatha.

Agatha 💜

Is it Friday yet? I truly love

my job, I wouldn’t want to do

anything else in the world, but I’d

much rather be with you.

Me:

Agatha Harkness. Are you high?

Because damn that was

sappy.

Agatha 💜

High on you.

Rio rolled her eyes. This woman. Ever since they had shared their feelings and Agatha had come clean with Rio, Agatha had gone full cheesy lesbian Hallmark movie. Rio was loving it.

Agatha 💜

I heard that eye roll.

Me:

Do your co-stars know you’re

like this?

Agatha 💜

Oh, fuck no.

I would never live it down.

I can already HEAR Danvers.

Hard pass.

Rio couldn’t help but wonder if Swords was working on this movie, but she quickly pushed it out of her mind. Agatha hadn’t mentioned anything about basketball and Rio assumed Nicky hadn’t said anything.

Me:

I now have plans for Friday.

Peggy went into labor this morning.

Still no baby.

Anyway, Calderu assigned me

as the new assistant bball

coach for the rest of the season.

Varsity game on Friday.

Agatha 💜

Well, that interrupts time.

Varsity, or JV and Fresh too?

Rio wasn’t surprised Agatha knew the breakdowns of teams, or that she was disappointed about getting less time together. Rio was also disappointed about having less time with Agatha when she would be home.

Me:

No, I’m just doing varsity.

Games won’t always be Friday nights.

Might cut into FaceTiming though.

Practices every evening.

We can talk about it later, my next

class is about to start.

Agatha 💜

We’ll make it work.

We’re both quite flexible.

Have fun wrangling the monsters.

I love you.

Me:

Just remember to stretch

and hydrate.

Me:

Just another test, we can

handle it.

I love you too.

Rio’s cheeks had turned a light shade of pink as the bell rang. They had been rather inseparable since the events of the previous week, and even though they had just parted this morning and would see each other Friday night, Rio felt like her heart had left the country.

Because a piece of it had, and that scared her.

I am so fucking gay. She thought before starting class.

⭐⭐⭐

Rio felt Agatha’s eyes watching her as soon as Agatha entered the gym Friday night. It was still early in the game, and they were tied. So far this season, the Superstars were undefeated, and the two coaches didn’t plan on letting them lose now. Rio had donned a black suit with a white button-up shirt, the top two buttons were undone, gold heels to stick to school colors, and her hair was clipped up. She held her clipboard as she shouted directions from the sidelines. She looked more like a college coach, which was what Rio preferred. She idolized women like Kim Mulkey and Pat Summit and always emulated that when she coached. Rio loved knowing that Agatha was now seeing Rio in her element: in control, wearing something she knew Agatha loved seeing on her.

Coach Johnson now deferred to Rio and let her call the shots, and the team performed better following her lead.

Wanda leaned into Agatha, “Did Rio just dom Daisy for control of the team?” Wanda whispered into Agatha’s ear.

“I think she did. Damn.” Agatha gave a low whistle.

She preferred to be in control, but watching Rio coach was something she had never seen, and she had zero right to feel like this at a high school sporting event. Alice leaned forward from the row directly behind them.

“At her previous school, parents with zero ties to the basketball team often showed up to support the team.”

Their section was there to cheer on Morgan and support Rio. However, the merry band of Sapphics understood exactly why a parent would tell their kids they were going to support the basketball team just for fun.

Nicky still had his arm in a cast, but Rio had allowed him to be on the sidelines and play water boy so he could be supportive. Rio had pulled Morgan out of the game for a few minutes because her temper was getting to her, and when Nicky tried to offer Morgan words of advice, she nearly bit his head off. Nicky sidled up to Rio and looked at her, confused. Rio didn’t take her eyes off the game.

“NEVER tell a woman how to play their game, Nicky. You’re smarter than that. If you’ve built up a - PLANT YOUR FEET KHAN, trusting and respectful relationship where you can give each other constructive criticism, then you can plan times for it. Never in the heat of the moment. LOOK AT CHAVEZ!”

The game went to halftime, and Rio looked up at the tall teenager.

“She never told you how to fix your aim when you threw an interception, remember that.”

Rio patted him on the shoulder and gave him a loving, motherly smile as the team went into the locker room for ten minutes.

Agatha saw the whole exchange, and while she couldn’t hear it, judging by the look on Rio’s face, it had been something sweet. Agatha had never planned on sharing her son with someone she was involved with, and she melted at the thought. That mixed with Rio’s confidence and domineering persona while coaching and Agatha wasn’t sure how she would make it through the rest of this game. Agatha turned to Wanda.

“We might not make it back to the compound tonight.”

“No, really?” Wanda deadpanned.

⭐⭐⭐

Rio had forgotten to load the auto-feeder for Raven. She lamented needing to stop by her apartment first when all she wanted to do was go home with Agatha. Agatha wasn’t mad at the lost time, as Rio saw it. Instead, she rode with Rio to the apartment to maximize what little time they had together before she had to be back on the set.

They had barely stepped into the darkened apartment, Rio sitting her backpack down, when Agatha was on her, pushing Rio’s back against the door, pressing the fronts of their bodies flush together. Her mouth was on Rio’s in a needy kiss; one hand caressed Rio’s cheek softly, and the other gripped her hip tightly, Agatha’s nails leaving half-moons in her tanned skin. Rio carded her hand through Agatha’s hair at the base of her skull and placed the other hand on Agatha’s backside, massaging her buttcheek through the denim of Agatha’s jeans. Rio kissed Agatha back just as needily, pausing for a moment to look at Agatha.

“I missed you,” she rasped, staring into the ocean-blue eyes that felt like home.

Agatha smiled back. Rio’s eyes were already dark brown pools of desire.

“I love you,” Agatha said, resuming the kiss before Rio could respond. Rio would never tire of Agatha Harkness saying those three powerful words.

Agatha used her thigh to nudge Rio’s legs apart as she slid her denim-clad thigh right up against Rio.

Rio whined at the slight friction, moving her hips to feel something more.

Agatha moved her hands to meet at Rio’s sternum and slid them up and under the suit jacket as she mirrored her movements across Rio’s collarbones, pushing the jacket off Rio’s shoulders. Agatha gave Rio just enough room to shimmy out of the jacket as it fell to the ground. She kissed along Rio’s jaw up to her ear, sucking her earlobe between her teeth. Rio let out a small hiss as she slid the hand that she had on Agatha’s ass to the hem of Agatha’s hoodie and tugged, still trying to feel something from Agatha’s thigh.

Agatha sighed, reluctantly pulling back a few inches from Rio, causing Rio to whimper as she moved her leg. Rio quickly removed Agatha’s hoodie and shirt together, throwing them on the ground as if their mere existence had personally offended Rio before attempting to tangle her hand in Agatha’s hair and resuming their kiss.

Agatha pushed her back against the door and leaned in, right next to Rio’s ear, “You’re still overdressed, coach.” Agatha made a long lick down Rio’s collarbone while expertly unbuttoning and discarding it.

“Does someone have the hots for the coach?” Rio asked as her bra was removed.

Agatha hummed an affirmation while she circled one nipple with her thumb while sucking a bruise on the side of Rio’s tit.

Rio moaned at the sensations. Her clit was already pulsing, and heat in her lower abdomen continued to build. Agatha had admitted Coach Rio turned Agatha on. It didn’t matter how many times they had made love. Knowing Agatha was attracted to her would never get old.

“I like it when you take charge.”

Agatha breathed across one of Rio’s hardened nipples. Rio grinned wickedly as she took a fistful of Agatha’s hair and used it to turn Agatha’s face upwards to look at her.

“Drop to your knees, Harkness,” Rio growled, immediately releasing her hold on Agatha’s hair.

Agatha dropped to her knees as she was told, raking her nails down Rio’s sides, kissing Rio’s stomach and along the waist of the black pants. Agatha loved the sounds Rio made and knew exactly where to touch and kiss to elicit them. Agatha’s hands continued down Rio’s thighs and she kissed her over the fabric, where Agatha knew Rio wanted her attention. Rio might be giving orders, but Agatha was still very much in control.

“Undress me, Harkness,” Rio ordered, and Agatha was more than pleased to oblige.

“Yes, Coach.”

She grinned up at Rio and undid the two buttons, making a show of pulling the zipper down with her teeth, slowly sliding Rio’s pants off her legs, and leaving the gold heels. Agatha felt her stomach tighten as she rocked back on her heels and leaned back to take Rio in. Agatha raked her eyes over the woman she had against the door, wearing only gold heels and light green boy shorts that betrayed how aroused Rio was. Rio was beautiful, and Agatha planned to ruin her in the best of ways.

Rio glanced down before taking another fistful of Agatha’s hair and tugging her forward towards Rio’s cunt.

“We can do sweet later. Right now, you have a job to do.”

“Yes, Coach.”

Agatha grabbed the top of Rio’s panties with both hands and forcefully pulled them down, helping Rio out of them. The heels were staying on. Agatha lifted Rio’s right leg and hooked it over her shoulder, kissing Rio’s thigh in the process. She kissed and licked along where Rio’s inner thigh met her body, feeling the muscles already trembling against her cheek. Rio used her free hand to palm at one of her breasts, twisting her nipple. The hand still in Agatha’s hair attempted to pull her closer to where Rio needed Agatha’s mouth to be.

“Impatient?” Agatha crooned, her mouth right above Rio.

Rio’s body was on fire from just Agatha’s teasing, and she rocked her hips forward, searching. Agatha kissed across Rio, placing a very light kiss right above Rio’s clit, continuing to move to the other thigh. Rio used the grasp she had on Agatha’s hair to move her head back to where she needed it.

“Yes,” she murmured, watching as Agatha flicked her eyes up to meet Rio’s and ran the flat of her tongue up Rio’s slit.

Rio threw her head back against the door as Agatha held her steady, lapping at Rio’s entrance. Rio moved her hips in a steady rhythm against Agatha, desperate for more.

Agatha tightened her grip on Rio’s thigh, using her other hand to spread Rio enough for Agatha’s tongue to thrust into her, keeping a steady pace. She knew Rio wanted hard and fast and would give her that. First, she wanted to taste her and build her up. Agatha had a long night planned. They had plenty of time for anything Rio wanted.

“Harkness,” Rio tried her best to go back to her coach's voice and failed. “Enough teasing. Make me…”

⭐⭐⭐

Wanda took a deep breath as she looked over herself in the mirror. Her makeup was perfect for the HD cameras, still with America’s sweetheart vibes. Her hair was partially pulled back, and she wore a black jumpsuit with a red blazer. She looked very Wanda Maximoff™ Wife, Mother, Girl-Next-Door. Wanda sighed as she put the necklace from Nat back on. The stylist that told her not to wear it could fuck off. She took off the wedding ring that some producer insisted she wear, though Wanda nor Drew liked that idea. Wanda and Vision had naturally stopped wearing their rings on Christmas when they announced their divorce to the world. She wasn’t putting it back on for an interview. Wanda loosened a few strands of hair, so they framed her face and unbuttoned the blazer.

“Asta pare mai natural,” now that looks more natural, Nat walked into their bedroom, where Wanda had been getting ready. The interview was being held at home as a special interest piece, not a part of Drew’s regular show.

“The only person I want to be,” Wanda turned away from the mirror and kissed the woman next to her.

“Are you ready to do this?” Nat’s eyes searched Wanda’s for any hint of doubt. Nat and Jen would shut this down in an instant if Wanda weren’t ready.

“I am, finally. Whatever happens, we can face it,” Wanda smiled brightly and went in for another kiss. “Ya tebya lyublyu.” I love you.

Nat took Wanda’s hand to lead her downstairs. There were no threats here, but Nat would always do what she could to keep Wanda protected from anything, even if it were just stairs.

“Te iubesc.” I love you.



Wanda and Drew were now sitting in the living room for the question part of the interview. They had walked around outside, and moved around different parts of the house, showing Wanda Maximoff the person, not the megastar. It would be edited together to show a normal afternoon between Drew and Wanda.

Of course, the background story left out how they actually met and went with the ‘met while out partying with Agatha’, which was partially true.

They had made the story a bit more suitable for public consumption, though they left the important points about how the industry could curate how someone was perceived, such as the need to be closeted if you were packaged and branded in a certain way, and the need to hide who she was for so long.

“Wanda, you and I are close friends, and we’ve known each other since we were teenagers,” Drew smiled genuinely. “We’ve discussed all of these questions, so none of these are surprises,” Drew added the disclaimer so neither of their fanbases would be upset when the update aired.

“Right, and that’s why you’re the only person I would do this interview with. We know too many things about each other to not be honest.” Wanda smiled, and they both laughed.

Drew put a comforting hand on Wanda’s knee.

“Ready?”

“Let’s do it.” Wanda took a breath, knowing that she was about to get a mix of general and fan questions.

“Did Vis know?”

“That question right off the bat? Well, as I said, when I was going through the history, I was always upfront with Vision. When he and I first started dating, he knew about Nat. When we got engaged as a PR response to the leaked pictures, he suggested we call off the engagement. Vis has always known, and there was never any dishonesty there.

“I did have beards, though he was not one, and they are all out now and extremely encouraging of this. I have always had Agatha’s support. She truly is my family, and I always had Natasha and Vision’s support. I never meant to betray or lie to anyone. It just grew into something bigger than myself.”

Drew blinked away a tear. “What about the boys?”

They all had workshopped and practiced this answer to make sure she wouldn’t be skewered for anything she said.

“The boys have always known, even though we never said anything. We thought not telling them was the right thing to do. That way, we didn’t have to worry about anything leaking, the boys saying anything, etc. We just raised them all together. Agatha had Nicky, and he automatically had four parents. Then, when Vis and I had the twins, we did the same thing. We did tell them before this came out on Christmas since we had decided to separate and announce everything after the first of the year. The timing was just a few weeks earlier than planned.”

“So the twins are okay with everything?”

“Absolutely. Nothing is technically changing for them, just where the adults live. No custody battles, no smear campaigns, we will just be a larger and slightly more dispersed family.”

They went through a few more questions that fans had asked to keep her image squeaky clean. No, no one was cheating. No, again, it wasn’t a bearding situation. Yes, their love had been real once. Yes, the boys were biologically Wanda and Vision’s, red and blonde did make brown hair.

Drew looked at Wanda, the loving and adoring friend.

“As we wrap up, what would you tell others who may find themselves in this situation? Or anyone faced with a similar situation?”

Wanda returned a watery smile. “Always be yourself, and don’t hide who you are. I have received so much overwhelming support, and even if I hadn’t back then, I would have come back from it. We can’t get back that time, and while I don’t regret anything, Vis and I both know that we should have separated years ago but stayed together for the sake of my career, afraid of what could happen. However, I have truly been the happiest I’ve ever been since coming out and making this life-altering decision, and he has been too.” Wanda looked past the cameras to where Vision and Nat stood, smiling proudly at her. Vision nodded in agreement, and Wanda focused back on Drew, “so again, be yourself and do what’s best for you and your family and this is what is best for ours.”

Wanda closed the laptop and set it aside. She and Vision had just watched the edited interview, and they were both pleased with how it turned out. He gave her a squeeze around the shoulders and a kiss on the temple. She laid her head on his shoulder, and they sat like that for a few quiet moments. They would always be an integral part of each other’s lives, with a closeness that would always be there. Wanda was thankful that Nat understood the dynamic and was okay with the pet name, head kisses, and otherwise intimate gestures. Wanda worried that any future partner of Vision’s would not feel the same way.

“Vis, do you think you’ll date?”

“Actually, darling, I was going to talk to you about that.”

Wanda sat up and turned to face him. “I was just asking as a hypothetical, what?”

He chuckled. “Jealous?”

“Surprised, actually.” Her eyes were wide. He had never so much as glanced at another woman in all the time she had known him. “You’ve never mentioned another woman to me.”

Vision grinned at her, waiting for it to hit.

“Holy shit. It’s not a woman.” She stated.

He shook his head, “No, there is a man however,”

“IT’S HERB!” Wanda blurted. Herb was another professor at UCLA and had been Vision’s racquetball partner for years. “Racquetball indeed, sir!”

“We are just racquetball partners and colleagues, though there is an interest there. When the story broke, he did text to offer his condolences, though the emojis he used suggested that he was not upset about it,” Vision recalled with a smile.

Wanda did not need the details of that.

“I’m so happy for you, Vision,” she hugged him and kissed him on the cheek. She loved the life they had curated on the compound, even if Vision would be moving off of it in a few weeks.

“Still moving into the house a few blocks away or waiting to see how things go with Herb?” She teased.

“Not a lesbian.” He reminded.

“Oh shit, I owe people money,” She remembered.

“Did you have a betting pool going?” He deadpanned.

“I have betting pools on everything.”

Alice clicked through the different tabs she had open for wedding venues, trying to narrow it down as she yelled in frustration. This startled Jen, who sat in their bed next to her on what was otherwise a nice, quiet Wednesday evening. Jen’s townhouse had sold, and she had moved in with Alice while their house on the compound was being built.

“Everything okay?”

Jen looked over at Alice with amusement as she closed the book she was reading. She knew what Alice had been working on and had found it entertaining how seriously Alice was taking the entire process. Alice slammed the computer closed and placed it on the nightstand.

“No. Everything’s either booked out for over a year, or there’s a minimum guest requirement. I don’t need a huge wedding. I need you, our friends, and an officiant. And I want to get married around Spring Break,” Alice slumped against the headboard and looked over at her fiancée. “Is that too much to ask?”

Jen smiled softly at Alice and placed a hand on her shoulder.

“Why don’t we elope? Or, well, elope but take the core people because Rio would murder me if she weren’t there. We can have a big reception after.”

A slow smile started to form as Alice thought it over.

“We get married at the beginning of break, have a short honeymoon, you will take me on a longer one later, and have a reception the following Saturday before we go back to school?” Alice thought out loud. Jen’s own smile widened.

“It’s a date. Want to do the cliche Vegas wedding?”

“My mother would personally haunt me if I didn’t.” Alice huffed a small laugh. “We still need a venue, though.” Alice started to worry again.

“Good thing we know a guy that loves a good party.” Jen returned her book to her nightstand and turned off the light. “Now kiss me, and let’s go to sleep. One of us has teenagers to wrangle tomorrow, and thankfully, it’s not me.”

⭐⭐⭐




“Dude, it’s time to talk with her.”

Tommy Maximoff said Wednesday night. The three boys were sprawled around the game room at the Maximoff house, where they usually camped out when Nicky stayed with them if Aunt Aggie was out of town. The room was dark, and they were supposed to be asleep. Instead, they talked through Nicky’s current dilemma.

Billy had created a nest of cushions and blankets and looked toward Nicky.

“You love her and need to see how compatible this will be,” he added to his brother.

Nicky sighed. He was on a couch, curled under a blanket.

“I don’t know. How do I even approach the conversation with my mom?”

Tommy scrunched his nose from the TaunTaun sleeping bag he insisted on sleeping in. “Why do you have to ask Aunt Aggie?”

“Because it’s her house? And her girlfriend!” Billy shot towards his brother.

Nicky sighed. He loved his younger cousins.

“I have to tell Mom where I’m at with Rio. Mom is obviously serious about her. We’ve all seen it.”

“Total simp,” Billy agreed.

“Right,” Nicky confirmed. “While I love staying here, Aunt Wanda and Aunt Nat need to be able to be together with you two as an actual family unit.”

Aunt Nat had finally moved in with Aunt Wanda, and Nicky was so happy for them. Uncle Vis decided to stay at the clubhouse until his house closed, and the place never looked so clean or organized.

Tommy snickered, “Nothing has changed. Nat’s always been a mom figure. Now they just kiss in front of us.”

“They play tonsil hockey in front of us,” Billy clarified.

“I’m just glad our moms had the foresight to soundproof bedrooms.” Nicky mused.

“YOUR mom did that. Our mom did not. We are scarred for life.” Tommy informed him.

“Anyway,” Nicky continued, “I need to ask Mom if Rio can stay the rest of the filming schedule at House of Harkness so we can bond more. I should just be honest with her, right?”

It was more rhetorical than anything.

“Yes, she needs to know that you want to learn more about Rio and how her living in the house would go. Also, cat bonus.”

Billy grinned. All three boys loved Raven and always hoped she’d be at Aunt Aggie’s when Rio was there.

“Okay, I’m gonna text her.” Nicky nodded decidedly.

#1 💙🏈

Mom, I need to talk to you.

Just you. Please.

It’s nothing bad, I promise.

Agatha read the text over and over. It was 10:30 on a school night. He should be asleep.

Me:

Do you want me to call?

#1 💙🏈

No. It can wait until Friday. I want to talk to you in person.

Agatha chewed at the skin on the side of her ring finger. Was the other shoe about to drop? Was this about Morgan? Or even worse, Rio? Was Tony about to murder him for something Nicky did? The possibilities were endless when it came to her son as she stared at the phone. She wondered if there was a possibility she could get home early, but knew there wasn’t. She had to film until late afternoon and wouldn’t be home until around noon on Friday. She was thankful they followed an American schedule and had a four-day weekend with SHIELD.

She huffed, and where she would normally pick Wanda’s brain, it was Rio who she texted, even though they had just hung up from their very spirited FaceTime session.

Me:

The child is acting weird.

He wants to talk with just me on Friday.

Which is highly unusual.

Rio 💚

His grades are great, I’ve kept tabs.

His arm is doing well and he’s on

track to have his cast removed,

in 2.5 weeks. He’s a huge help

to the team, and things are going

well with Morgan. I’m sure everyone

is fine, Aggie.

Agatha blew out a breath. Rio always knew what to say to put Agatha at ease.

Me:

What if it’s about you?

Rio 💚

Then we’ll all sit and talk about it.

I love you and everything will be fine.

Get your beauty sleep, my queen.

Me:

Will do, Ms. Cheesy.

Thank you.

I love you too.



Nicky was waiting at home when Agatha arrived on Friday. He was on the phone with Morgan and hung up when he saw his mother come in.

“Hey, mom! Can we talk once you get settled?” He asked, still somewhat anxious about the whole thing.

Agatha picked up on his nerves and immediately started thinking of worst-case scenarios. Had he cheated on Morgan? Committed felonies? She had always been very upfront with him to stick to basic misdemeanors. Was this about Rio? Agatha dropped her things on the end of one of the couches before settling in her usual spot and patting the spot next to her.

“Come sit, kid. Tell me everything.”

Nicky took a huge breath as he trudged toward her, the death march playing in his head. He plopped onto the couch and lowered his eyes with a sigh.

“It’s about Rio, mom.”

Agatha deflated. She would always put her son first, even if it meant losing the woman she loved.

“Okay,” She nodded. “I’ll talk to her, and we’ll put an…”

Nicky’s head popped up, “What?! Wait! Mom, what do you think I’m about to say?”

Agatha tilted her head and gave him a look of pure confusion.

“That you don’t like Rio, that you want me to break up with her?”

“Oh! God! Mom! NO!” He was bewildered. For someone so smart, how could she be so bad at reading her own son? “No, I want her to come stay with me while you finish filming!”

He rushed the words, only to see more confusion on his mother’s face, and he continued to rush forward.

“The twins need time to get settled into new routines and having Nat as even more of a mom, and Vis needs time to adjust to being somewhat single. Rio and I need to see how well we would do without you here as a buffer since you won’t be here every day. I know I have to ask her, but I have to ask you first since she is your girlfriend, and it’s your house, Mom. I just know you two are serious about one another and if the next logical step, no matter when it is, is her moving in with us, then we have to know how well we cohabitate.”

Nicky was panting after the word vomiting he had just done when he looked up at his mother, who was, crying?

“Mom, I’m sorry, I didn’t mean to…”

Agatha pulled him into a tight hug. “I love you so much, Nicholas.” She pulled back and looked at her son, truly her number one. “Yes, we can talk to Rio and see what she thinks. It’s up to her, and we don’t want to scare her off, got it?”

Nicky nodded and grinned. “Mom, she’s seen you first thing in the morning, no makeup, giant glasses, full-on morning breath. You couldn’t scare her away if you tried.”

Agatha attempted to give him the mom look, “You, sir, are an asshole.”

He returned one of her signature smirks, “I learned from the best.”

Agatha couldn’t help but smile. She had taught him well.

Rio had come over shortly after Agatha and Nicky’s conversation, with Raven in tow. They ate dinner at the kitchen table, and Rio was somewhat suspicious of Nicky’s presence since he spent all of his time with the twins or Morgan. After Nicky cleared the dishes, he sat back at the table and looked at Rio very seriously.

“Rio, Mom and I were talking, and since changes are happening at Aunt Wanda’s house, and I want to stay in this house more, will you stay here with me while Mom is filming?” His face was hopeful, and he looked much younger than sixteen at that moment.

Rio turned toward her girlfriend, “Are you sure? I’d be parenting him a few days a week. Are you sure you’re okay with that?”

“Are you going to let him do anything stupid?” Agatha countered.

“Not intentionally, no,” Rio answered honestly.

“Then yes. I would love to know that you are caring for o…my son. Waking up in ou…my bed every morning. I just wish I was next to you.”

Agatha was stroking her hand up and down Rio’s thigh and looking at her softly. Nicky cleared his throat.

“Well, you guys are doing that thing where you make it weird and gross, so I’m leaving now.”

He waved as he left to meet the twins at the clubhouse.

Rio looked back at Agatha, “You almost said ‘our’ twice.”

Agatha put a finger to her lips in thought, “No, don’t recall that.”

“Sure you don’t.” Rio smiled before turning serious. “You’re sure you trust me with him?”

Agatha held Rio’s hand and ran the pad of her thumb across her knuckles.

“Of course I do, why wouldn’t I? If you can keep a roomful of teenagers alive around open flames and chemicals, you can keep one of them alive for a few days.”

Raven gave a meow of doubt from a few feet away.

“I’ve kept you alive, you traitor.” Rio glared.

⭐⭐⭐

Once Agatha had left, it was easy for Nicky and Rio to establish a routine. When they got home from basketball practice, Nicky did his homework while Rio made dinner, and they cleaned up the kitchen together. With Coach Carter’s blessing, she appointed him a coaching assistant, and they would go over different plays and drills. Morgan would sometimes come over after practice, and Rio would have to play the mean mom by not letting them go anywhere in the house unoccupied.

Even if it was just a few days, she thought she was doing a pretty good job, and they were bonding over video games, music, and other common interests. They FaceTimed with Agatha for the Oscar announcements, but they were only slightly surprised when both Wanda and Agatha were nominated for Best Actress. Rio had Agatha on Facetime as they got ready for school and watched the broadcast.

Agatha was thrilled that Wanda had been nominated for lead and really hoped that her sister would win instead of her. Agatha and Rio chatted a bit more before they hung up. Rio looked at Nicky, who had also been watching them as they sat in her classroom early.

“What do you think?”

He sighed. “Is it bad that I do hope Aunt Wanda wins more than Mom?”

Rio thought about it.

“No, not at all. Your mom wins a lot in everything. Wanda is amazing, and so was her performance. However, the people who vote for The Oscars, well, a lot of them are going to feel betrayed and lied to.”

Rio knew that as stupid as it was, there was a possibility that Wanda would lose votes solely because of what Lou did.

“That’s really stupid,” he grumbled.

“I agree with you. People live vicariously through celebrities and put too much pressure on them to live a certain way. Just because Wanda should get the Oscar doesn’t mean she will, and it’s not right or fair.”

Nicky nodded and looked at Rio.

“You’re really insightful, considering you’re not that old.”

Rio smirked. “Thanks, it’s the trauma.”

It was another Friday night basketball game, and Agatha wasn’t going to get in until late due to filming. Nicky’s cast was off as he gestured wildly on the sidelines, and both lead coaches shouted at the team.

“Nicky! You’re not a coach. Sit down!” Daisy yelled at Nicky, face red, and steam practically radiated from her ears.

“You just called a play that we didn’t have the right people for, and it was disastrous because you didn’t want to let Rio call it!” he spat back.

“Both of you! Nicky, you know better than disrespecting an adult and undermining the coach in public. And Daisy, he is a child. Both of you, cool it!” Rio barked before turning back to the team on the court.

Daisy turned to Nicky and did not see him as a student at that moment but as yet another member of the Rio Vidal Fan Club.

“She doesn’t care about you. She’s just trying to get to your mother.”

Her words were meant to sting, and they did.

Nicky was a Harkness, through and through, and did not back down. His mother had taught him not to fight women, but he was about to.

“You’re just jealous because she’s a better coach and, apparently, better for my Mom.”

He was fuming, and the Harkness temper was about to come out when Rio called a time-out and stood in front of him.

“Daisy,” Rio said calmly. “It sounds like this is between you and me, not something to take out on my son. I will finish this game without you. You’re dismissed.”

Daisy opened her mouth to protest, and Rio gave her The Look™. Daisy closed her mouth and turned on her heel, only to see Lilia Calderu giving her a similar look as Daisy followed Lilia out.

Rio quickly turned to Nicky to see him looking pale and staring at Rio. She put a comforting hand on his arm.

“Go sit with Wanda and calm down, okay? I’ll kick her ass in the parking lot. Go.” She jerked her head in the direction of the bleachers. He gave her a small smile and went to find the rest of his family.

Rio realized the team had gone relatively silent, as had much of the crowd. She shrugged and turned back to the team.

“Stop gawking. Time-out is over, and we’re about to lose. Let’s go!” She shouted as everyone snapped back into action.

Nicky recounted the scene with Daisy to his aunts, uncle, and cousins. He looked at his Aunt Wanda,

“She called me her son. I don’t think she even caught it. I know we’ve joked about it, but” he shook his head and took a deep breath. “I need to talk to my Mom.” He finished.

Wanda nodded. “She’ll be home later tonight.” She assured. She couldn’t imagine how Nicky was feeling. She rubbed his back as he kept his eyes on Rio. Morgan glanced up at him questioningly, and he gave her a thumbs up. The other teenager didn’t believe he was okay either. Smart Girl. Wanda thought before texting the events to Agatha.

Rio got her head back into the game, and SHIELD barely won. She would need to talk to Coach Carter in their next catch-up about what happened. The team debriefed, and Rio walked back through the gym, knowing everyone else had already left, when she saw Lilia waiting for her.

“You did great with Daisy.”

Rio scoffed.

“What was that about, anyway?” She asked as Lilia fell into step with her.

“Daisy is still young. She had her chance to emerge from Peggy’s shadow, and you immediately eclipsed her in the first game. You have done what no other woman before could do and stole Agatha Harkness’s heart, something most teachers here have tried. You have endeared even the most stubborn of parents to you, several students are highlighting teenage bullying and inequality in schools across America, you nudged Wanda Maximoff out of the most see-through glass closet, and you finally forced Alice and Jen to admit their feelings. You didn’t expect jealousy?” Lilia looked at the younger woman expectantly.

“You’re not speaking riddles,” Rio parroted Agatha from months ago.

“I don’t know why everyone points that out. For some reason, new life makes me even more observant,” Lilia mused, referencing Steve and Peggy’s newborn son, James.

“Ah,” Rio studied Lilia for a moment.

“Why me, Lilia? Why offer me the position and bring me out here?”

Lilia hadn’t known her. She just came to San Francisco at Alice’s request last Spring and offered her the position.

“I trust Alice, and she wanted to keep you safe. I knew you would be a good fit, and you have been, in many ways. Now, I have a few security things to take care of. Start prepping for playoffs, coach!” With that, Lilia walked off.

“We haven’t made it that far!” Rio called after her, but the woman didn’t reply.

Nicky made it home to the compound before Rio did. He had given Morgan a quick hug and kiss before promising to call before bed. He was relieved to see his Mom as he sat next to her and buried his head on her shoulder like a little kid.

“Did Aunt Wanda tell you what happened?” He asked in a whisper.

She ran soothing fingers through his hair like she often did when he was a little boy.

“Yes, and I’ve talked to Lilia.” She had also verbally eviscerated Daisy, but he didn’t need to know that.

“Rio called me her son.”

“I heard. How do you feel about that?” Agatha still wasn’t sure how she felt about it. She loved it and was scared at the time.

“Honestly?” He pulled back and looked her in the eye.

She nodded.

“Honestly. No cap, or whatever the kids are saying,” She gave him a sarcastic smile as he groaned. Parents should not use teen slang.

Nicky thought about it, letting the words marinate. “I know we’ve joked, and I’ve called her dad, which is still funny because it’s Rio, and she gives those vibes,”

“Fair.”

“But it’s always been you and me. Aunt Wanda, Uncle Vis, and Aunt Nat too, but you know what I mean,” Nicky looked up through long, thick eyelashes.

Agatha knew exactly what Nicky meant. He would always have this family, but Agatha was his anchor.

“I love her mom, and she’s a good addition to our family. I don’t want to say a missing piece because we aren’t incomplete, though we were sorely lacking in the ancient cat god department,” he said as Raven jumped into his lap and purred.

“I think those choose the human, not the other way around,” Agatha mused. “What part about it scares you?” Her stomach dropped at the look on his face. “Right. Still me.”

“Do you blame me, Mom? I got into an argument with a grown adult because she’s jealous of your girlfriend as she’s one of I don’t even want to think of how many women you’ve slept with. I’ve defended you since I was a kid. I should not know this much about my mom’s extra-curricular activities. I’m sorry if I worry.”

Agatha had always tried to keep her hobbies away from Nicky but knew he would be exposed to them, no matter how hard she tried.

“I have changed, Nicholas.”

“I know, Mom. Just know that she really is becoming like a mom to me, even if it hasn’t been that long, okay? Or at least until I get in major trouble and she has to put her foot down. I might change my mind then.”

Headlights washed over them as Rio drove up the long driveway.

“That’s my cue since you two tend to get gross. I love you, Mom,” he hugged her before heading up to his room, Raven in his arms.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha woke up the Monday morning after the Grammy’s, thankful there was no school and that she could hold Rio in her arms. The ceremony had been a celebration of women, queerness, POC, and just fun. Rio had been a pure chaos demon with Taylor, and Agatha had taken it all in. Agatha had won her third Grammy, and while that technically gave her her triple EGOT, she wasn’t counting it until she won her third Oscar for Best Lead Actress. She was an overachiever after all. She had thanked Rio first last night, causing several of her friends in the industry had lost their minds, including the younger sapphics in the crowd. It truly was a great night, and she looked forward to always having Rio next to her as her forever date for these events and all of the memories they would create.

Her phone brought her out of the lavender haze as Nat texted the group chat, instructing everyone to put their clothes on and meet in Agatha’s living room in forty-five minutes.

“Why MY house?” Agatha grumbled as she kissed Rio awake.

Forty minutes later, everyone was in Agatha’s living room with the TV on.

“Care to fill us in, Nat?” Agatha asked, nursing her second large thermos of coffee and still grumpy about being awake at eight in the morning.

“I received some information last week, and this morning, one of my…shadow associates, if you will, told me to watch the news at this time. So here we are.” Nat was focused on the TV.

The boys were groggy but had been dragged from their beds for this. Jen and Vis were the only two that looked put together, the others all looked as if they put on the nearest, cleanest, most acceptable articles of clothing and game over or downstairs, which is what they had done.

“Hush, it’s starting!” Wanda quieted everyone.
