“Breaking news! Out of San Francisco,” The reporter started. “We go now to recently disgraced supermodel Lou Franco’s house where the FBI has identified her as the person behind many high profile jewelry thefts…”

The color completely drained from Rio’s face as she clapped a hand over her mouth, and the other rubbed those too-familiar circles on the left side of her chest. She wasn't sure why she was anxious or crying.

There was Lou, glaring into the camera, in handcuffs.

All eyes in the room landed on Rio. Agatha put her arms around her.

“It’s over,”  she whispered into Rio’s ear.” in an attempt to soothe her.

Jen turned to Nat, “How’d you finally do it?”

Nat stood there, watching the oversized screen, taking it all in. Yes, she had gotten documents and fingerprints, but they weren’t necessarily the ones that would tie all the heists together.

“I didn’t do this.” Nat started. “ I provided some evidence to an associate, but this wasn’t me,” her voice was low and hollow.

Alice shrugged.

“Whoever it was, they’re on our side, and we can breathe more easily,” she said assuredly.

Rio looked at the screen. Things were going well at school. She was coaching again, Wanda and Nat only had a few months until they could get married, Jen and Alice were beginning to plan their wedding, all the boys were doing well, Vis was settling into his life, the boys were doing great, and she and Agatha were doing well. The Oscars and Spring Break were coming up, and best of all, Lou was gone.

Rio looked back at the screen just in time to see Lou look into the camera as her lips curled into an evil smile, “Te veo, pet,” she mouthed before passing out dramatically as the camera cut off.

I see you.

Rio just shook her head. “Nope, bitch is gone, and all is well.” She decided.




Chapter End Notes

It's about time, AWAY LADY.

Thank you so much for all the love! I ADORE YOUR COMMENTS! Here, social media, everywhere, and I try to respond to all of them! Also, thank you for sharing! I can't believe my words are being read by so many people!

Oh, and umm, Happy Valentine's Day, you're welcome.

She Means Everything To Me

Chapter Summary

Another month for our coven!
Valentine's Day, a surprise getaway, Rio's a disaster, communication, The Oscars, a wedding, Spring Break...
You know, just normal coven things.

Chapter Notes

I am so sorry this is later than usual. The next chapter won't be as eventful, so should be shorter.
It was a week.

Thank you, as always, for all of the comments, kudos, and general love!

See the end of the chapter for more notes

It was Valentine’s Day, and as cliche as it was, Wanda was going to propose. She was tired of waiting, and with everything finally being calm, now was a good time. She looked down at the dark wooden box in her hand, opening it to reveal the black titanium engagement ring inlaid with red diamonds and a red ruby in the center. The ring had never been worn despite being purchased over twenty years ago.

“Aggie, I’m going to propose to Nat,” Twenty-four-year-old Wanda Maximoff said decisively to thirty-year-old Agatha Harkness.

Agatha was sitting in the living room of the house in LA, which they shared with Nat, who was out on a run. She set down the script she was reading and looked at the young girl in front of her.

“Oh honey, do you think you’re ready for that? What about Vision?”

Wanda had thought of that.

“Yes, and I love him so much Aggie, but Nat is the one. I don’t care what the fans say,” Wanda sat down next to the woman who had become her older sister over the past eight years.

Agatha smiled and extended her hand, “Let me see the ring.”

“How do you know I have a ring?” Wanda asked indignantly.

“Because you always do this when you see rings that remind you of her. I assume you saw the perfect ring, no one was there to tell you no, and you bought it.”

Wanda’s entire face flushed. Agatha could read anyone, especially Wanda. She sheepishly handed Agatha the box, pouting that the older woman had been partially right.

“Close, I saw one I liked and had this, and the matching band custom made.”

Agatha opened it with a smirk, having been proven mostly correct, and let out a sad sigh when she saw the ring.

“Oh, angel, it’s perfect.”

She looked at Wanda with a sad expression, gripping her hand.

“You WILL get to put it on her hand one day, and I will be by your side either as your Maid of Honor or the one giving you away.”

Wanda nodded, a tear sliding down her cheek. She knew Agatha was right. Wanda wouldn’t have the courage to do it, no matter how much she wanted to spend her life with Natasha.

Wanda closed the box and tucked it in the pocket of her maxi dress. Agatha had been right. The concert had happened a few days later, and everything after that was a blur. Here they were, over two decades later, and Wanda would finally get to propose. She had her speech, decorated the gazebo out back, and planned what to make for dinner. Everything was ready down to the music; she had practically grown up in the movie industry, so the cinematics were inevitable.

Nat could be covered in mud and wearing a potato sack, and Wanda would still think she was the most gorgeous being in the universe. Wanda knew she would remember the jeans and Lilith Fair hoodie Nat was wearing for the rest of their lives as they sat outside watching the sunset on the cold February evening around the firepit in the gazebo, drinking hot chocolate and snuggling together after dinner. It had been a quiet Valentine’s evening at home, which was precisely what they had wanted.

“Do you remember our first Valentine’s together?” Wanda asked, moving closer to Nat.

Nat smiled at the memory.

“We were living at the clubhouse, and Agatha was still teaching us how to cook. You were much better at it than I was, though that’s not saying much. You tried to make Beef Wellington and almost burned the house down. We had to order pizza, and then we watched But I’m A Cheerleader for the hundredth time. Agatha was gone that night, and when she got home, she laughed for a good ten minutes.”

Wanda laughed at the story. “I was trying so hard to impress you!”

“You didn’t need to! We had been together for a few months, and I was already completely in love with you. You could’ve made me a Hot Pocket, and it would’ve been the best day ever,” Nat took Wanda’s hand and looked at her with pure love.

“What about the first concert we went to?” Wanda prodded, eyes dropping down to Nat’s hoodie.

“1998 Lilith Fair. I spent most of the festival trying to keep track of Agatha. You and I, however, danced very inappropriately to Missy Elliot, considering we were just friends and I was your bodyguard...”

“You were definitely guarding my body,” Wanda interjected.

Nat smirked as she continued, “and gave new meaning to the song ‘Something to Talk About’.”

Wanda just grinned. “We do cause a commotion at concerts, don’t we? Spice Girls, Warped Tour, NSYNC, Madonna, Lollapalooza,” they had been to so many over the years, she couldn’t begin to name them all. “Good thing we can be out now.”

“Music is important to us, and we tend to get carried away. Now we can.” Nat leaned forward, kissing Wanda. “My turn to ask a question: do you remember the first time I officially acted as your bodyguard?”

“YES! It was for the Emmys! Agatha and I were up for Sisters! I couldn’t stop staring at you in that suit! I should’ve known I wasn’t straight, but I kept telling myself I was just envious that you pulled it off so well.” Wanda laughed.

“Solnyitza, you should have known. Every guy you were set up with was PR. Nothing happened with any of them. Vis was the first and only man you ever did anything with, including unscripted kissing,” Nat reminded.

Wanda thought back on all of her so-called relationships.

“Good point. However, the same can be said about you, zvezdochka, star. You’ve been the only woman for me,” She scoffed. “Forty-five, and I’m still the girl next door.”

“Forty-six in two days,” Nat reminded.

“Remember my thirtieth birthday?”

“Agatha threw you that eighties-themed birthday party and made everyone dress as their favorite eighties character! It was so cute! I still don’t know why you got so mad at her.”

Wanda huffed, “Because Nicky was almost one, and what no one else saw was Agatha teasing me all night about how I wasn’t getting any younger and I needed to choose my baby daddy,” She rolled her eyes. “She also brought Nicky over earlier that day in a shirt that said, ‘I love my aunts and uncle’. Lorna kept telling Agatha to lay off.” Wanda shook her head.

“If it weren’t for Agatha, I never would have met the love of my life,” Nat caressed Wanda’s cheek. “Besides, you have two baby daddies. Those are my boys, too.” Nat pulled Wanda in for another sweet kiss.

When they parted, Wanda looked up at Nat, glad that her plan was going the way she wanted. “Do you remember when we found out I was pregnant?”

Nat’s face softened, a smile still on her lips as the clear visual came into her mind.

“I’ll never forget it. You came out of the bathroom, test in hand, and we stared at it for what seemed like forever. We were in Budapest on a set, and you had gotten sick. You thought you were just sick when the doctor on hand sent you to your trailer with a pregnancy test. We watched in agony as it turned positive, and even though we weren’t technically together, I was so happy. You weren’t mine, no, but Wanda, I was always yours, and I am so grateful that I was allowed to be there every step of the way. You called Vis, and he told you he knew you were in good hands for the rest of the shoot.”

Wanda was trying not to cry, and while she had more memories to bring up, she knew it was time.

“Moya zvezdochka, my star, I’m getting cold. Will you grab a blanket from the chest?” Wanda asked, giving Nat her best puppy dog eyes. Nat rolled her eyes.

“You have two perfectly good legs, but yes, solnyitza, sunshine, I will.” Nat got up and walked across the gazebo to fetch the blanket in question.

“Nat, do you remember the time I proposed?”

“Don’t be silly, you’ve never…” the words died in her throat as she turned around to see Wanda down on one knee, holding an open box with the most beautiful ring Nat had ever seen. The blanket fell from her hands as a hand flew to cover her mouth, and tears filled her eyes. It was finally happening. Her feet propelled themselves toward Wanda.

As she cleared her throat, Wanda had tears of her own, watching Natasha walk toward her. “Natasha, I have loved you for twenty-five years, and I so badly want to be yours. You have been by my side through every up and down in my life since I was eighteen. You were the first woman I ever kissed, the only woman I’ve ever loved, and one of the few people I’ve shared everything with. You are a parent to my sons, the person I want to fall asleep next to every night, and the person I wake up to every morning. So many of my best memories are with you, and I want to create more of them for however many years we have left. I want to grow old with you, make out at concerts, and actually impress you with my cooking skills now that I can,” Wanda laughed through her tears, remembering the multiple cooking failures they had both had. “You are the one I want by my side at every event, in every picture, in every way imaginable.

“I have wanted to put this ring on your finger for so long and never thought this day would come. I know this is the most cliche day to ask you this. Natasha, you are the love of my life, the person I have been madly in love with since we were kids, who patiently waited for me, who supported all of my decisions, maddening as they were. Will you finally marry me?”

Tears streamed down Wanda’s face as she looked into Nat’s eyes, awaiting her response.

Nat had knelt in front of Wanda, tears of her own mirroring Wanda’s as she nodded. She held out her shaking left hand as Wanda slid the ring on her finger.

“Yes, Wanda. I will absolutely marry you. No matter what day you asked me.” She smirked, swiping at her tears. “You have always been it for me, and I would have been content just being here, a part of your world. Being with you again is something I had only dreamed of.”

Nat leaned forward, hands clasped in between them. She slid her lips against Wanda’s, bringing a hand to Wanda’s cheek, and smiled into the kiss. It was sweet and slow, tips of tongues swiping across each other, not quite going deeper. Wanda whimpered as Nat pulled back.

“One moment, îngeraș, angel,” Nat stood up and brought Wanda with her. “You’re not the only one that has been holding on to something for a ridiculously long time. I was trying to wait until your birthday.” Nat said, fishing a box out from the pocket of her hoodie.

“Because that’s not just as cheesy.” Wanda sniffed, new tears creeping from her eyes.

“You still win on that.”

Nat laughed as she opened the box, revealing a gold ring with a ruby heart. The band was braided with a row of diamonds on either side.

“Wanda, when you sent that text a few months back, my heart stopped. I watched you play house for decades, and I would do it again and again if it meant being even a fraction of your life. You asking if you could be mine were words I never thought I would see or hear. They were wishes and dreams. I couldn’t get back here, back to you, fast enough. We have loved each other in secret and lived for the stolen moments for so long, and to finally be ourselves has been freeing and amazing. But know that I would do it again and again if it meant that I would still get to be with you. I love you so much, Wanda, the Sunshine in my day. Will you marry me?”

Wanda was ugly crying, and she knew it.

“You already said yes,” Wanda sobbed a laugh.

“Just give me this.”

“Yes, my North Star, I will marry you.” Wanda grinned.

Nat placed the ring on Wanda’s finger, wrapping her arms around Wanda’s waist and kissing her passionately before turning off the firepit and leading her to their bedroom.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha had never planned an actual Valentine’s Day. She had absolutely ‘celebrated,’ but a proper date? It had never been her thing in the past. She had tried with Swords but had been shot down. Maybe it was time to start tackling the Swords' trauma with her therapist. She shoved the thoughts aside. Tonight was not the night for those memories. Rio had offered to plan the night, but this was something Agatha wanted to do.

Except her plans were shot when Rio turned into Coach Vidal, and there was a basketball game on fucking Valentine’s Day. The rest of the coven didn’t even come since her kid was the only one technically involved, and her girlfriend was the coach. She could salvage it, right? But then, it was a doubleheader, and Rio was coaching both JV and varsity due to the JV coaches having some mystery flu. Calderu had suspended Daisy from coaching until March, and no matter how much Agatha had tried, Lilia hadn’t let up.

That’s how Agatha found herself in the gym at SHIELD, cheering alongside the Starks and the Langs, wearing a SHIELD jersey with VIDAL Number 13 across the back.

Tony smirked, leaning across Pepper to talk to Agatha.

“I never thought I’d see the day Agatha Harkness had another woman’s name on her,”

“I’d never thought I’d see the day Pepper would give you your balls back, and what do you know? I still haven’t,” Agatha shot back to Pepper’s laughter.

“She has you there, honey,” Pepper added.

Tony turned bright red.

“Good thing Nicky learned from his other parents,” he grumbled.

“For the most part,” Agatha smirked before turning to Pepper as Tony returned to his original place. “Morgan is a really great kid. Are you sure it’s okay for Nicky to take her out after this without an adult chaperone?”

Nicky and Morgan had planned to go out to a diner and a late movie with a pass, stating it was a school event. If they did get pulled over and caught, Agatha and Tony planned to play the megastar and extremely famous director card to get around the California state minor driver rules. Misdemeanors, not felonies and all that.

Pepper smiled.

“I trust them both to make good choices, and I trust you two to get them out of any trouble they could potentially get into. By that, I mean I trust our collective legal teams,”

“Smart girl,” Agatha smiled.

They continued to cheer the team on. Junior varsity had already played, though they still sat on the bench out of support and respect. Tommy had come along to support Cassie Lang. They were ‘hanging out’ and not necessarily dating. Neither of them wanted labels, stating that they were too young for that and only freshmen. Yet if you asked Billy, he and Eddie were going to get married, Eddie was going to be a doctor, Billie would follow in his mother’s footsteps, and they would have two point five children, two cats, matching Subarus, and work closely with LGBTQIA+ organizations. The twins could finish each other’s sentences but were as different as night and day.

Agatha turned to watch Rio and Nicky as they coached in sync. They made a great team, and it made her heart flutter. Rio was back at her apartment during the week now that filming had wrapped up, and Nicky missed her. Agatha hadn’t addressed the ‘My Son’ comment with Rio, and Rio hadn’t brought it up, but Agatha found Rio catching herself slipping and purposely not saying it.

Agatha was lost in her thoughts when she felt eyes on her and looked over to see Lilia staring at her, the old bat. Agatha just glared at her and flipped her off. She was still mad about the basketball game situation. Lilia rolled her eyes.

SHIELD won yet another game and Agatha said her goodbyes to the other parents while waiting for Rio when Lilia pulled her aside.

“What do you want?” Agatha said coldly.

“You can’t remain angry at me for a schedule created before I made Rio the coach,” Lilia said with an exasperated sigh.

“I can be rather stubborn.”

“I’m painfully aware Agatha. You can still have a good night and watch your movies. She has tests to grade this weekend, no matter where she is. Also, she had a rough day. Just be there for her, not whatever extravagant thing you tried to plan to sweep her off her feet with tonight,” Lilia suggested.

“You are the one to be giving me romantic advice? Why the sudden interest?” Agatha asked suspiciously.

“Even Hollywood playboys deserve to settle down eventually, no matter with whom, Agatha. Besides, happy coaches win games. Winning games leads to state. Winning state propels the school,” Lilia smiled. “It may also be time to address when she called him “her son” - everyone heard it, and I am sure you haven’t approached it.” She added before turning away and briskly walking away with a wave over her shoulder. Agatha had known the woman forever, and she was still often confused by her. She shook her head as she turned to see Rio headed toward her, looking exhausted.

Rio took Agatha’s hand, kissing its back. “I love the jersey, m’lady,” she looked around as the gym cleared out, eyes landing on Morgan and Nicky as the teens walked up to them.

“We’re headed out, and I’ll be home by curfew,” both teens hugged the women.

Agatha looked up at her son. “Be very careful. Watch for deer and insane blondes, got it?”

“Yes, Mom,” he hugged her again and looked at Rio, “Another great night.” He waved as he took Morgan’s hand. “Good night, Happy Valentine’s Day. Love you, Moms!” Nicky flashed another smile, and they left.

Rio turned to Agatha, “Did he call me mom in a non-joking manner?”

“He did.”

Rio remembered the last time she became attached to someone else’s children. Three blonde kids she would still do anything for. The oldest was in college now and all three crossed her mind occasionally. She couldn’t take losing another kid, set really, as she considered the twins partially hers as well.

“We should probably discuss this.” Rio’s face was solemn as she watched Nicky and Morgan leave the gym.

“My house?” Agatha watched Rio carefully. She knew Rio had referred to it as home before but didn’t want to push it right now.

“Your house. Where’s Tommy?” Rio scanned the gym for the Maximoff they were supposed to be in charge of.

“He went with Cassie and family for pizza and a game night. Vision will pick him up later.”

“That’s what the kids call ‘hanging out’ these days?”

They started walking to Rio’s car as they continued their conversation.  Agatha shrugged.

“It’s so different from when I was a kid. Then again, I snuck around on movie sets and in trailers. My teenage dating life wasn’t really typical.” Agatha recalled.

Agatha opened the car door for Rio like she always did, and Rio rolled her eyes.

“No wonder you make all the women swoon,”

“I’m oozing with charm.” Agatha leaned in, kissing Rio. “I’m sorry our first manufactured day of love isn’t more romantic.”

“Spoken like Agatha Harkness. Let’s go back to the compound and talk about the whole son thing. We’ve been pushing it off long enough. Then we’ll go from there, okay?”

They sat on the couch, a movie playing in the background.

“Are you okay that I called Nicky my son? It really did just slip,” Rio asked, drinking a cider and watching Agatha’s expressions.

“I’ve thought about it, and I am. It surprised me at first, but more because I’ve never had a serious romantic partner that I saw as his other parent before. Swords came around when he was born, and I think she considered it for a minute but ultimately decided against it.” Something dark slid over Agatha’s face.

“I mean, she bailed after the first pregnancy ended. I shouldn’t have been surprised when there was an actual baby.”

Rio hated this Swords bitch. “But you have concerns,” Rio surmised.

“Nicky is still concerned about the me factor,” Agatha confirmed with a sigh.

“He loves you. He trusts you. You are becoming another mom to him, and it scares him how fast it’s happening. However, there’s still my past and reputation. I’m not exactly the poster child for steady relationships.”

“I’m proud of you for communicating and being open and honest.” Rio wanted to validate Agatha before she shut down. Sometimes, she had to treat her with teacher gloves. “I love him very much, as if he were my own, but it did happen very quickly, and I can back off some. I’m still going to be protective of him, but if I overstep, just tell me. I love you and will respect all of your boundaries.”

“Damn Rio. I am a powerful woman, and you just come in here, break down my walls, and make me completely vulnerable in every way except physically.”

“I have a gift,” Rio smirked.

“Now, I heard you had a bad day. Come here and tell me all about it,” Agatha adjusted so Rio could lay against her and vent all she needed to.

“I teach high schoolers, and it was Valentine’s Day. I also had the freshman biology class dissect sheep hearts, so that was fun. Do you know how many students I had to pull apart from each other today? Khan and Chavez were like sapphic bunnies. They were EVERYWHERE too! I do have a very decent supply of candy and chocolate in my desk drawers, though, and I ran a carload of flowers and stuffed animals home after school before the game.”

Agatha almost interrupted her to tell her she didn’t see any of those things around until she remembered Rio meant her apartment and not Agatha’s house. Rio was still recounting her day.

“The gifts do not matter to me, but it’s so sweet that they bring me things, especially at this level. So that wasn’t bad. I could’ve done without some of the teasing from the jealous teachers though.” Rio rolled her eyes. “Lilia keeps shutting it down, and then I feel like a teacher’s pet. I still love my job and wouldn’t trade it for anything, but I’m about to take Nat to work with me one day. My yearly evaluation is coming up soon, too. I’m worried the coworker dynamic will affect it.”

Agatha ran her nails along Rio’s scalp.

“I’m sure it won’t, baby. There’s nothing to worry about.”

“Says Agatha Fucking Harkness. Oh! That reminds me, I got you something. It’s in my school bag,” Rio hopped up from the couch and retrieved a card from her bag that she had sat at the table. When she returned, Agatha was holding a gift box of her own.

Rio looked around, trying to determine the origin of the box.

“I hid it under the couch,” Agatha said cooly.

“Of course you did. It’s not much, but here, you go first because I went first at Christmas,” Rio instructed as she sat back on the couch.

“Yes, Coach,” Agatha handed Rio the box, opening the envelope. The card was sweet and held tickets to the Beach Life Festival for the first weekend of May.

“It’s mainly for Alanis, Train, Sublime, Lenny...okay, there’s a lot of people we like. I thought we could make a weekend out of it since it’s a four-day weekend for us anyway.” Rio pushed her hair behind her ear, looking down sheepishly. Agatha leaned forward and kissed her.

“I love it and look forward to making memories with you. However, I think we’re beginning to think alike. Open yours.”

Rio tilted her head questioningly as she opened the box revealing plane tickets that had ‘Harkness Airlines’ on them with Lima, Peru as the destination, tickets to see Shakira, and a pack of gum.

“Is this because of how badly my ears popped the last flight?” Rio laughed as Agatha nodded. Rio looked closely at the dates. “Wait, Aggie, this says we leave tomorrow!”

“Concert is Monday, got you out of your teacher workday on Tuesday, and Lilia took care of Wednesday for you. I couldn’t get you out of the game tonight, but I did get you out of Wednesday. Alice, Wanda, and I packed for you. Alice and Jen will Raven sit. Lesson plans are also taken care of, courtesy of one of your backups that you already had in your Google Drive.” Agatha had planned everything.

“Agatha Fucking Harkness, if I wasn’t exhausted…”

“There’s always time for that later. It’s a 9-hour flight. Now, let’s get those sore muscles of yours into a hot bath, and I will take care of you, my love.”

Rio’s breath caught at the new pet name, my love. That was the one, the one that fit them the best.

⭐⭐⭐

“So why did you choose Shakira?” Rio asked Agatha as they cuddled on the private jet.

“I appreciate your Latin roots. Also, it’s Shakira,” Agatha trailed her fingers up and down Rio’s spine.

“I liked her better as a brunette, and I prefer her older stuff, Ms. Know-it-all,” Rio began to sing “Perteneciste a una raza antigua, De pies descalzos y de sueños blancos.” then kissed Agatha’s neck, eliciting a sound from her girlfriend that made her thankful the few crew members were back in the enclosed area of the jet.

“You do have a type, and I may have made a few requests for the set list,” Agatha put an arm around Rio, pulling Rio onto her lap. “I love when you sing to me.”

Rio took the hint and straddled Agatha, wrapping her arms around Agatha’s shoulders.

“How did I get so lucky?”

Rio gazed into the sapphire blue eyes of Agatha Harkness. In them, she saw the hope and promise of their future and at the same time, she still wasn’t sure what their future was. They hadn’t been together that long, but she still wondered.

“I believe I’m the lucky one here, my love,” Agatha kissed the hollow of Rio’s neck. “You came to LA and all.”

Rio pushed other thoughts aside as she leaned down and kissed Agatha. The kiss quickly turned heated. Rio sucked on Agatha’s tongue before breaking away, gasping for air. “You know, I am not a Mile High Club member,” she smirked.

Agatha grinned as she reached for a remote and turned off the lights in the cabin. “I believe we can remedy that.”

As they entered their suite in Peru hours later, Rio entered the bedroom and collapsed on the bed. Jetlagged and exhausted from their in-flight activities. With customs, the flight, a three-hour time difference, and getting checked in, it was nearly midnight, and Rio was ready to sleep for the next twenty-four hours.

“Are you going to change first?” Agatha lay next to her and slung an arm across Rio.

“I took my shoes off, and I’m tired,” Rio muttered back as Agatha’s phone went off, the ringtone indicating it was Jen. “I love her, but it’s midnight, and she knows where we are.”

Agatha groaned as she answered, and Jen’s face appeared on the screen. “We could have been naked,” Agatha said in place of a greeting.

“Nothing I haven’t seen before. Anyway, is your other half around?”

“You can see her next to me.” Agatha moved the phone more to show Rio.

“No, the redheaded one.” Jen rolled her eyes as if it were obvious who she meant.

“No, it’s Valentine’s and her birthday weekend. Nicky’s staying with the twins and Vision. Wanda and Nat won’t surface until we get back, most likely. Why?”

“Wait, you’re in Peru without Nat?” Jen looked alarmed.

“I’m not stupid, Kale. We do have security with us. I know I can’t go anywhere without an armed bodyguard,” Agatha huffed. It was not something she liked about her fame. “Get on with it. Why are you calling me this late at night and looking for Wanda, too?”

“I have had a flurry of phone calls and e-mails, and you’re both about to be very busy women…”

“I’m already a busy woman,” Agatha turned her head and grinned at Rio as Jen rolled her eyes again.

“Stop acting like a teenage boy and go into global megastar mode, please?”

Agatha disgruntedly blew a strand of hair out of her face as Rio suppressed a giggle.

Jen carried on, “You’ve had requests to present at a few awards shows and accepted them, the Spirit awards, you’re presenting at the Oscars for Supporting Actress, then the SAG, QUEERTIES, GLAAD, and you’re nominated at all of them. That’s for now, though we know there will be the Emmys, possibly the Tonys, and who knows what else.

Then there’s press for the TV show, all the late-night shows, the morning shows, the talk shows. Everyone wants you, Agatha, and we’re giving you to them. Marvel and Disney are starting table reads, alternating, in May. However, they need you in for some things in April. The show needs some additional filming next month, and it looks like your summer will be packed. Wanda’s too, probably.” Jen finished and looked at Agatha expectantly.

Both Agatha and Rio were sitting up now. Rio was trying to take everything in.

“Wait, what about the festival in May? Coachella? Spring Break? Your wedding and reception? Prom? There are some important things we had planned,” Rio looked between the phone and Agatha.

Agatha put her hand on Rio’s shoulder.

“Welcome to the reality of being me. The past few months were a break period. Now everything is kicking into high gear. I’m sure Jen worked out a few non-negotiables, but some sacrifices will have to be made. Weekends are generally free, most shoots are Monday through Friday, and we can only work five consecutive days a row. Thank you, SAG-Aftra. Spring Break is probably one of those sacrifices, though,” Agatha turned back to the phone to confirm her suspicions.

Jen was nodding. “I was able to negotiate your music festivals. Coachella was easy. Most celebrities go. Nicky’s junior prom is safe, and our Vegas wedding and the reception are safe. Your Spring Break plans are not. Agatha, you’ll be making the promotion rounds that week.” Jen gave Rio a sad smile, “I’m so sorry. I hate to be the bearer of bad news this weekend, but I had to sync calendars and deliver the news as soon as I could.”

“I understand, mood killer,” Agatha smirked. “Send me the itinerary for Spring Break, please, once you plan it. Maybe we can salvage something.”

They hung up, and Agatha turned to Rio, who was pouting.

“I wasn’t lying when I said this is one of the reasons I didn’t date before. It’s a lot to ask of someone.” Agatha said softly, trying to gauge where Rio was at with her feelings.

“I understand it’s your job. I just hate how everything can change so quickly.”

“I know, baby. It’s a lot. Let’s enjoy this weekend, and then come with me or stay with Nicky when you can. Please?”

Agatha was not used to asking someone these things, but it felt right. She liked knowing that Nicky could stay at the house with Rio and loved having Rio by her side.

“I understand if you can’t come on school nights. Lilia might start saying no to you being out so much,” Agatha did not want to hear what Lilia had to say about that.

“I hope you can make it to the playoff games if the team makes it, though even if we lose the rest of the regular season, we should still make it.”

Saturday night faded into Sunday morning and they were up extremely early, despite the time change and jet lag. Rio had wanted to do some sightseeing while in Peru and dragged Agatha out to Machu Picchu. Agatha had been before but enjoyed exploring the ruins with Rio and was glad that Alice had been part of the packing team. Alice knew Rio would want to go and had packed the appropriate gear for the hike.

Agatha had to guide Rio away from a tarantula they saw amongst some rocks that Rio had wanted to ‘rehome’ back to LA.

“She’s cute, and I want to name her Victoria,” Rio said.”

“I will buy you a tarantula, my love. Wait, what would Raven do?” Agatha tilted her head, picturing the cat-shaped Eldritch creature training an overgrown furry spider to do her bidding.

“Probably snuggle with it,” Rio shrugged, waving at the spider and walking back to the main group with Agatha and their security detail.

“I was imagining her training it to do things for her,”

“Also a possibility,” Rio agreed.

After breakfast on Monday morning, they lay in bed discussing the upcoming busy schedule.

“All of the awards shows are in or around LA, which makes it easy. Talk shows are mainly in LA and New York and mostly over Spring Break. It looks like filming for the show is back in LA, so that makes that easy. Marvel and Disney are between LA and New Jersey, and then this summer, I’ll be in England, Italy, and Australia. It may extend longer. You can come with me over Spring Break if you want to.” Agatha was trying not to sound desperate to spend every moment with Rio and failing.

“It’s a possibility, depending on the schedule. I was hoping we could have some fun this summer, not you working the whole time.”

“It doesn’t make sense to bring you along either, especially Marvel. It’s a closed set. If they could cut you off from the entire world until the film was done, they would.” Agatha spoke seriously.

“So I’ve heard. I can come to visit, though, right?”

“We’ll figure out something. I cannot go for three months without seeing you and Nicky,” she kissed Rio before turning the discussion to upcoming awards shows.

The concert was amazing, Rio honing into her Spanish-speaking childhood. They sat in a box, which was safer for them, even with security. Rio’s hips absolutely did not lie as she danced the entire night and sang to Agatha. Rio rolling her R’s made Agatha feel very indecent things that she planned on capitalizing on when they returned to the hotel.

Keeping to her word, Agatha had indeed made a few requests for the setlist as several older songs were played, and Shakira mentioned singing them for ‘a friend’, winking in the direction of their box.

They flew back early the next day so they could recover on Wednesday before Rio had to go back to teacher mode. They mostly slept on the plane and looked through the pictures they took and a few that popped up online.

“I’m still getting used to people posting pictures of me online. It’s so weird. People will post ‘Rio Vidal sightings’ now.” Rio mused.

“This is your life now,” Agatha looked at her. “As long as you want it.”

Rio’s breath caught for a millisecond. What did Agatha mean when she said those things? Did she just want to date forever? Get married? Live together? Would she be okay if Rio ended things? Was she waiting for Rio to end things? Agatha was not the most forthcoming when it came to commitment, although Rio was the one they had waited on in that department. She would drop it for now and focus on other things.

They had left Rio’s car in the lot near the private hangar at the airport. Security escorted them until they sat in the car, Agatha climbing in the passenger seat after opening Rio’s door when she pointed at something.

“Rio, what’s that?” Agatha pointed at a letter on the dashboard that was addressed to Rio in Lou’s handwriting.

Rio froze for a few beats before reaching out and calmly taking the letter. “It’s from Lou,” She said, her voice cold. “I thought she was still in prison?”

Agatha sighed, her head falling against the headrest. “I swear this bitch has nine lives. Her lawyers must have gotten her out on house arrest or something. We will need to tell Nat. Do you want to open it or wait until you’re alone?” Agatha wanted Rio to share with her, but it was for Rio, and she wouldn’t force her.

Rio swallowed hard as she carefully opened the letter.

“My Dearest Rio,

The night Agatha confessed to you, she knew I had documents proving the near marriage and her first pregnancy. Did she tell you because she wanted to or because she wanted to beat me to it? Can you trust her to tell you the truth without being forced?

What else is she still keeping from you? Other than her ex’s identity?

You will always be mine,

I love you. No more secrets.

Lou."

Rio didn’t know how to feel. She just slouched in her seat and leaned her head back, clutching the letter. She let out an exasperated sigh, not even turning to look at Agatha. “Speak.” She commanded in a tired tone.

“I had felt guilty for not telling you ever since that initial discussion. When Nat told me Lou had Lorna’s documents, I knew which ones they were. I knew I had to tell you, and then I discovered the secret about the photo album. I knew after then that it was no longer that I felt forced to tell you, but I wanted to. I wanted, I want, to be honest about everything. I haven’t told you who Swords is because I don’t want our sordid past to cloud your judgment of her if your paths cross, just like I said. She’s a different person now, and I do interact with her occasionally,” Agatha reached out to take Rio’s hand, only for Rio to pull away in the small confines of the car.

Agatha winced. “Baby, please. Look at me.”

Rio turned her head to look at Agatha, hurt in her eyes.

“What do you want me to say, Agatha? My psychotic ex tells me in a letter that you bared your soul after you found out she knew your dark secrets. You wouldn’t tell me you loved me until then. I somewhat understand why your ex is still a mystery.” Rio sighed. She did that too much lately. “Let’s just go back to the compound.

They drove in silence. It was early evening with the time difference, they noticed Alice’s car parked outside Agatha’s house.

“Did Alice or Jen mention coming over?” Rio’s voice was hoarse from silently crying.

“No,” Agatha grabbed for the luggage.

“Leave mine, I’m going home tonight,” Rio said softly before walking toward the door.

Agatha took a deep breath and put on a smile for her friends as she walked through the door, noticing that Rio had done the same.

“Hey, what are you two doing here?” Agatha asked as she took her suitcase straight to the laundry room to deal with later.

“Wanda told us to meet here. She had us pick up dinner,” Alice answered, pointing at food containers in the kitchen.

“Here!” Nicky called as he and the twins walked through the door. “Practice went fine, Rio, I think we have a great shot at state,” He grinned.

“We have to get there first.” Rio hugged him.

“We’re here!” Wanda shouted as she and Nat came in the door and through to the living room, both glowing. They had laid low since Friday, waiting to gather everyone together.

“Great, would you like to tell me why everyone is gathered at my house?” Agatha joked. She had a pretty good idea of what they were about to say.

Wanda and Nat looked at each other, excitement and pure joy radiating from them.

“We’re getting married!” The redheads exclaimed at the same time, holding up their left hands.

There were hugs, congratulations, and cheers. Rio was excited and happy for them. Of course, she was. They had waited for this for so long, and they deserved it. She and Agatha hadn’t been together long enough to discuss it, so why did she feel hollow? Because it’s all vague, and you don’t know if she’s still holding anything back.

“Are you okay?” Alice whispered in her ear.

“What? Yeah! I’m fine.”

“Liar. I know you better than anyone. Something’s wrong,” Alice kept her voice low, eyes darting to Agatha. “What did she do?”

“She knew Lou had your mom’s stuff. It’s why she told me everything. Lou left a letter in my car,” Rio’s voice was low as she tried not to look suspicious. “What else is she hiding, Alice?”

“It is Agatha. She plays her cards close to her chest. It’s how she’s always had to do things, Ri.”

“Why are you taking her side?” Rio was quickly starting to lose trust in everyone.

“I’m not. What did she say?”

“That she didn’t tell me because she knew psycho had the information, but because she wants to be honest with me.” Rio echoed what Agatha had said earlier.

“Then there’s your answer. She loves you, Rio. Don’t do this thing where you get scared and push her away because you overthink it because I know that’s what you’re doing.” Alice glared. It was a habit Rio had.

“I am not.” Rio looked away, knowing that was what she was doing. “I do think I’m going to sneak out and recover from the weekend, though.”

“You’re as subtle as a heart attack, you idiot.” Alice muttered, “Go, I’ll cover you.” Alice made her way back to the main group, gushing over the rings and trying to draw attention away from Rio as she left.

Agatha’s eyes followed Rio as she left. “What’s going on with her?”

Alice sighed, “She’s trying to run because she’s an insecure idiot who thinks she needs to have a five-year plan three months into a relationship.”

“So, a lesbian then?” Nicky quipped from the table, mouthful of chicken alfredo.

“Oh, like you’re one to talk. I saw you and Morgan discussing which colleges to go to,” Tommy shoved his shoulder as Nicky choked on a noodle.

“No choking!” Wanda called, making sure no one was actually choking before looking back at Agatha, “I know the two of you have moved quickly, but have you vocalized what direction you’re going in? Other than a vague forever?”

“Forever is vague?” Agatha quirked an eyebrow.

“That woman is going to break up with you.” Jen used her fork to gesture at Agatha. “And it will be your fault.”

“No, I thought when you told someone you wanted to be with them for ‘as long as they wanted you’ and ‘forever’ and all that, it meant, well, forever!” Agatha said, frustrated, and turned to Wanda and Nat. “It worked for you two!”

“Only because I was married,” Wanda said dryly.

“If Rio didn’t believe in or want a marriage, that would be one thing. But she does. Have you talked about moving in together? Or a timeline? A timeline for getting married? Anything?” Alice questioned.

“No, why would we? We just started dating.” Agatha did not understand women.

There was a collective groan.

“Agatha honey,” Wanda started again, “let me put it this way, do you love Rio?”

“Madly.”

“Do you want to live with Rio?” Wanda continued.

“Absolutely.”

“When do you see that happening?” Nat jumped in, trying to help draw more out of Agatha.

“Within a few months, I planned on asking her when her lease came up,” Agatha answered honestly.

“Okay, great! Do you want to marry her?” Alice took over the next question.

Agatha chewed on her bottom lip, “I’m not the marrying type,” She went back to her safe answer.”

Wanda waved her hand dismissively. “That’s a bullshit protective answer, and we all know it. Try again.”

“IF I were to get married, it would be her. I do want her to remain in my life romantically.” Agatha affirmed after thinking about it.

Jen reached out and put a hand on Jen’s knee. “Then go tell her that. Come on, we’ll give you a ride, and we have a cheesy idea for how to get her to let you in.”

After they finished eating and Agatha packed food for Rio and Raven, she knew better than to leave out the cat, she was headed back to Rio’s with Alice and Jen.

“You’re sure this will work?” Agatha asked skeptically.

Alice grinned, “Oh, absolutely. If nothing else, Jen will get some great pictures.”

“I hate you both.”

Agatha had decided against using her key to get into Rio’s. She knew Rio was mad at her. She knocked instead, not surprised when she heard Rio tell her to go away.

“Please, baby? I want to talk.”

“No,” Rio called back.

Rio listened for any reply and didn’t hear one. She turned from the door and stopped when she heard music, followed by the undeniable voice of Agatha Fucking Harkness.

“I have learned the lesson of all that’s foul and fair. Our love was forged in fire, water, earth, and air…”

Rio wasn’t sure if she should laugh or cry. She was getting her very own Say Anything moment, except instead of a teenage boy, it was a fifty-one-year-old mega star in the hall of her apartment building.

She turned around and walked to the door, Agatha still singing.

“...and so I dare, to risk this heart of mine.”

Rio opened the door to see Agatha holding her phone over her head in the classic John Cusack pose, and Rio couldn’t help but laugh.

“That smile makes this worth it,” Agatha surged forward, pocketed her phone, kissed Rio heatedly, and closed the door behind her.

“Don’t run from me, from us, from this, okay?” Agatha gasped, peppering Rio’s face with kisses, hands running through her hair. “I’m sorry that I’m not the best at this. I do want you. I don’t know what our future holds, okay? I don’t know what the five-year plan is. I know I want you to move in with me when your lease is up. I know I want you by my side. I know that we’ve moved really fucking fast, and it terrifies me. I know there are things I still need to tell you.”

Rio put her fingers to Agatha’s lips and looked at her. “Aggie, breathe.” Rio led her to sit down. “We don’t have to plan all of this out right now. I don’t want to force you to do anything you’re not ready for, just, I was led on for so long with a potential psycho killer, and I can’t do that again.” Rio looked down, eyes filled with sad and painful memories.

Agatha put her hand on Rio’s cheek and turned Rio’s face to look at her.

“You’re not forcing me. I’m just bad at communicating without a script. I love you, and the moving-in thing was something I had already planned. I will start telling you about these things when I think of them, okay? I really don’t know where I am on the marriage thing. If I were to get married, it would be to you. If I were to have more children, it would be with you. If I were to have a dog, or buy another house, or own another business, it would all be with you, Rio. I fucking love you. Please don’t run or overthink it.”

Agatha leaned forward and kissed her tenderly, trying to convey her point. Rio leaned into the kiss, placing her hand on Agatha’s chest. She pulled back slightly, looking at Agatha.

“That helped. I will try to keep the overthinking to a minimum and stop comparing this to my extremely unhealthy last relationship. Now, what do you still need to tell me? No more secrets. That’s what got us in this damn mess.”

“It is, but it’s late. Can we at least change and get in bed?”

“Who said you were staying over?” Rio feined offense.

“Fine, I’ll just go home.” Agatha stood.

“Whatever. You have clothes in my room, and Raven is asleep on your side of the bed.” Rio stood and walked towards the bedroom, turning off the lights. “You can put the food you packed for me in the fridge, please!”

Agatha stared after Rio, trying to decide if that was an invitation or a dare. Possibly a threat. She had brought a bag in with food after all, though it seemed like Rio had either eaten or wasn’t hungry. She turned toward the kitchen and put the food away, going over the day’s events. She was so incredibly excited for Wanda, and still a little confused over Rio. Where had all of that really come from? Had the letter from Lou caused that? As soon as she thought it, her eyes fell on the letter as she passed back through the living room. The lights were off, but she could tell it was the same letter. She picked it up and used her phone as a flashlight to read over it. Rio had read it in the car, but Agatha hadn’t seen it. She opened it now to see that there was more writing than what Rio had read.

“I have done some thinking. I want to marry you, now.

You’re not getting any younger. Let’s have a baby.

I’m 55 and I still want kids.”

That fucking psychotic bitch. There was even a phone number. She took a screenshot of the letter and sent it to Nat before folding it back up the way she found it and going into the bedroom. Rio was already in bed and waiting for Agatha.

“Made your decision?” Rio looked at her expectantly.

Agatha handed Rio the letter and put her bag on the dresser, looking for her pajamas. “I did bring a bag and all.”

She took out her pajamas and began to change.

“What I need to tell you is that when I decided to marry Swords, I was going to give it all up: my career, the fame, everything. I was only 33 then, so you can imagine what I would have missed. So I really dodged a bullet when she didn’t show,” Agatha lifted her hair from her pajama shirt and walked toward the bathroom to brush her teeth while Rio watched her, shocked. “Offering to give it all up was the only way I could get her to agree to marry me, pregnant or not. I think the seriousness of it all scared her. She claimed she couldn’t let me throw away everything I built,” Agatha began brushing her teeth while Rio processed it.

“I can see why you left that part out since it didn’t happen, but damn Agatha. That’s big. I’m glad you didn’t get married. I mean, obviously, but also because of everything you wouldn’t have done. I’m not even mad at that admission. Oh, early, what businesses do you own?” Rio had thought about it, but she didn’t remember Agatha owning a business.

“So, the club in New York? That’s mine. I own a few other side things here and there.”

“Wait. The secret lesbian club? You own that? Not Andi?” Rio wasn’t sure how to feel.

“Since I was eighteen. I didn’t want people to think I was curating my own selection of women or trying to get free drinks. A place like that was needed, so I created it.” Agatha scooted in next to Rio and kissed her with minty, fresh breath. “Mad?”

Rio considered everything before looking over at Agatha. “No, baby girl, I’m not.”

Agatha snorted. “Did you call me baby girl? I am…”

Rio cut her off, “I don’t care how old you are. You are wearing bunny rabbit pajama bottoms, your hair is in a braid, and you stood at my door reliving a scene from a brat pack movie after chasing after me. You are very much baby girl right now,” Rio laughed and kissed her on the forehead.

“I’m head over heels in love with you is what I am,” Agatha grinned before remembering the letter. “Why didn’t you tell me everything that was in that letter?”

Rio made a disgusted sound, “Because she was trying to get in my head, and I knew you would want to charge off and commit murder.”

“Well, it worked. She did get in your head, and what’s wrong with a little murder between mortal enemies?” Agatha shrugged.

“Oh, you’re mortal enemies now?”

“Obviously. She’s trying to use your insecurity against you and trick you into marrying her and then babytrap you!”

“Oh, that is what she was doing!” Rio had been so caught up in her feelings she hadn’t realized the obvious. “Okay, no, you can end her now.”

“It’s about time I had your blessing.”

“You already sent Nat that phone number, didn’t you?”

Agatha didn’t answer. Instead, she grinned, straddled Rio, and kissed her.

⭐⭐⭐

The rest of February went by with a flurry of awards shows and basketball games. Playoffs began, and SHIELD was the team to beat, and Death’s Beloved was sweeping everything. Yet again, the coven was on top. Plans for the reception after Spring Break were being solidified, the plans for the wedding itself were solidified, and Spring Break plans had been made. Wanda and Nat had announced their engagement by showing up on a red carpet with rings on their hands, and social media had lost it. Wanda confirmed it later with a well-received post.

Now they were at the Oscars. It had already been a wonderful night. Agatha had performed her song and won the Oscar for it. They were waiting for the last two Oscars of the night; Best Actress, and Best Film. They were honestly all hoping for Wanda to win best actress, as Agatha and Wanda held hands while they went through nominees, Agatha turned to Wanda. “You earned this one angel. If you don’t win this one, I’m going to lose my shit,”

“The Oscar goes to…AGATHA HARKNESS!”

“Fuck that,” Agatha said, as she hugged and kissed Rio and whispered, “Please still love me in a few minutes.”

She turned and grabbed Wanda’s wrist. “Let’s go, superstar,” and pulled her to the stage with her. Agatha wasn’t at all surprised that their friends were cheering and clapping. Never underestimate Agatha Harkness.

They reached the stage, Agatha in a dark purple gown, Wanda in a scarlet one, trying to keep on a smile and not a smirk. Agatha graciously took the golden statue and went to the microphone.

“Hey everyone, as you can see, I brought the person that actually deserves this award with me.” Several people cheered, “Well, tell me what you really think. Anyway, this movie would not be as amazing or powerful as it is without this amazing woman standing next to me. The only reason I won and not her is because too many of you took her personal life into account when you voted, and that’s not fucking right.” The music began to play, and Agatha looked straight at the sound booth, “Cut the music now Ralph, before I call your wife.” It stopped automatically, and she continued. “Anyway, you’re voting based on our on-screen performances, not our off-screen performances. Wanda decided to live her truth this year, and you cannot punish her for that by robbing her of a much-deserved award, you cowards. So, Wanda, this is yours.” She stepped back and handed Wanda the award with a hug.

Wanda stepped to the mic and looked around. “With as long as I’ve been doing this, I’m not sure this is allowed…”

“She’s Agatha Fucking Harkness, she’s allowed!” Tony yelled from the audience to several people agreeing.

Wanda looked over to Conan, who was hosting the show, as he just shrugged.

“Okay, so, thank you to my amazing and wonderful fiancèe, Nat, my family and friends, Agatha for being…Agatha, everyone who worked on the film, and Agatha, I swear if the Academy strips both of us of this and whatever else, I’m never having fake sex with you in a movie again.” Wanda smiled as Agatha smirked, and the crowd laughed. It didn’t matter where they were. They, they made everyone around them feel like it was a small gathering of friends. Wanda waved as they walked off stage, and Conan sent it to a commercial break before the final award since Wanda and Agatha had to get back to their seats as they were the favorites for the final category.

One of the members of the board for the Academy came rushing over to them, “Ms. Harkness, you can’t just…”

Agatha held her hand up, “Who had the second most votes?”

“Well, Wanda…”

“Okay then, I refuse my Oscar. Give it to second place.” Agatha said resolutely and walked back to her seat, the board member stuttering and stammering behind her, Wanda cackling.

Death’s Beloved won Best Picture, the press wanting to speak with Agatha as she ignored them, taking Rio’s hand.

“Come dance with me at a few after-parties, please, my love?” She asked.

“Of course! I do love a Hollywood rebel.”

⭐⭐⭐

It was the state championship game, and Rio was nervous. SHIELD was hosting as the home team, and the stands were packed. She looked at her players. They were truly her team now, even though Coach Carter was in the stands watching and had come and talked with them. Peggy told her she was thinking about staying home with the baby next year and that Rio should take over the team if she did. The possibility was an exciting one, but right now, Rio was focused on the game ahead of them.

“Alright, minions, listen up, you guys are amazing, and I love all of you nerds. The girls over there? Yeah, their coach doesn’t love them, and they aren’t as cool as you guys are.”

The team laughed and rolled their eyes. Rio always did this at the beginning of a game, saying random things to calm their nerves and make them laugh.

“Okay, seriously though, I am so forking proud of all of you. You have done an amazing job this season, and I am so thankful that you guys let me just come in and take over. Now, when we win, no dumping Gatorade on my head. This isn’t football. We pop champ…sparkling cider bottles over at Morgan’s house like the ladies we are, got it?” Several of the girls giggled. The plan was that if they won, there would be a party at the Stark’s house. Even if they lost, Tony would still host a party. “Just play your best, okay? That’s all I ask.”

The game started and SHIELD was losing quickly. Rio grimaced. At least they made it this far. She, Nicky, and now Coach Johnson, now that she was back, gave their all from the sidelines. They finally started picking up speed, and by halftime, they were only down by six.

“Great job, my minions, I’m so proud of you! Keep it up, draw those fouls, and get some of those girls ejected. Throw elbows if you have to. Play dirty!” Rio was fired up.

“Go for the knees!” Agatha said behind her.

“Mom, wrong sport,” Nicky reminded her.

“Not if you make it look accidental,” Agatha shrugged.

“No one listen to her,” Rio instructed as several girls snickered. They were used to Agatha from growing up with Nicky.

Rio turned to look at Agatha with her jersey on and gave her a quick peck on the cheek. “You can’t be down here. Go before we get in trouble.” Rio poked her in the side

“I came to give you a good luck kiss and wish the team luck, good luck youngins!” She winked and gave Rio a chaste kiss. “Besides, I’m Agatha Harkness, and I do what I want. You look really sexy in a suit, and I couldn’t stay away any longer, but the ref keeps giving me side eye, so I’m going now.” Agatha sauntered back to her seat with the rest of the coven, and Rio rolled her eyes, turning back to the team with very red cheeks.

Kamala Khan had asked Morgan something that Rio didn’t hear, only Morgan’s response of “Oh, you can ask Nicky too. They are always like this.”

The game resumed, and SHIELD was back. They tied it up and got ahead quickly, then the other team was ahead, then they were ahead, it was close, and it came down to the last few seconds. Morgan made the shot from the three-point line, a shot she usually wasn’t good at for her height. Rio watched through her fingers. Agatha watched through her fingers. It went around the rim and through the net just as the buzzer went off. 72-71 SHIELD.

Rio couldn’t believe it. If Morgan tried that shot again, she probably wouldn’t have made it, but right now, she had. Everyone was cheering, running, jumping, and doing the most. Agatha spun her around and threw her arms around her.

“Earth to Rio! Your girls won! You did it!” Agatha exclaimed, buzzing with energy. “I am so proud of you, baby!”

“I can’t believe it! We won! We did it! Oh shit! We’ve gotta do the lineup and handshake thing.” Rio was scrambling and nervous about interacting with the other coach. The other team had matched them the entire time. Rio knew they would.

It was Rio’s old team.

Rio’s previous assistant coach.

Rio’s plays.

Rio blew her whistle and had the girls meet at halfcourt for the customary high fives and good games. Rio saw the questioning looks on the faces of the other girls. None of them knew what had happened, just that one day, Rio was gone.

Parents were gathered on the court and had stepped aside as the girls went through the line, yet none of them would high-five or shake hands with Rio.

Rio was finally face-to-face with the other coach. “Mindy, I see you erased the sportsmanship I taught the girls.”

“You left, and they lost in the first round last year because of it,” Mindy replied coldly.

“I left because I had been stalked and attacked, and therefore was placed on admin leave.” Rio attempted to explain. “You would know that if you had been a real friend.”

“Whatever, you knew our plays. Your team cheated.”

“They’re MY plays, Mindy. It’s not cheating when I was the one who wrote the entire playbook.”

“Why don’t we ask the official?” Mindy was right in her face as Nat appeared at Rio’s side.

“Ri, is there a problem?” Nat asked, sizing up the other woman.

Rio gritted her teeth, “Grab the official, will you Nat? I have a question.”

Nat raised an eyebrow but went to get the official. All eyes were on the two coaches at this point, wondering who would throw the first punch. Lilia and Wanda each had Agatha by an arm, holding her back.

“Agatha Harkness having her bodyguard keeping an eye on you now? Is she worried someone’s going to harm her plaything?” Mindy teased.

“Why are you such a bitch, Mindy? I never did anything to you.”

“YOU didn’t, but you had what I wanted, and you threw her away.”

Rio wanted to scream. She turned as Nat came back with the official. Mindy opened her mouth, and Rio put up her hand and explained, in a nutshell, the issue.

“So, ma’am, did we cheat?”

The official shook her head. “No, we knew you were the former coach. The win is legitimate.” She looked at Mindy. “Your team needs to vacate now. We will send the second-place trophy to the school.”

Agatha had wrangled her way out of Wanda and Lilia’s grip, finally and joined them, glaring at Mindy as she slinked away.

“Can I add her to Nat’s list?” Agatha asked.

“Yes.” Rio turned to Agatha, “We can celebrate now.”

The celebrations resumed, and Nicky lifted Morgan so she could cut down the net. When he lowered her back to the ground, she looked up at him questioningly.

“You’re not mad or jealous?” Morgan asked.

Nicky laughed, “No, why would I be?”

“Well, the football team lost in playoffs, and we just won state…”

Nicky put his hand up, “Wait. You were at the Oscars last Sunday, right?”

“Yes, we sat next to each other.”

“Just making sure, and you saw Agatha Harkness give the metaphorical finger to the Academy, refuse her Oscar, and give it to my Aunt because she deserves it more, right?”

Morgan wasn’t following, “Of course I did, Nicky, but what does that have to do…”

“I’m getting there, but you know that’s my mom and my aunt, and they raised me, right?”

“Nicky, yes, but…oh.” It hit her. Nicky didn’t have a jealous bone in his body. If someone worked hard for something and deserved it, they deserved it, and he would support them.

“Nicholas Harkness, you are the most well-rounded and well-adjusted 16-year-old boy to exist. I love you.” She stood on her tiptoes and kissed him.

Nicky laughed.

“My mom had me do a lot of therapy early on to make sure I’d be like this.”

“She’s a smart woman. Now stop kissing my daughter,” Tony said, walking up to them as Pepper rolled her eyes. “We have a party at the house to get to.

Tommy jogged up to Cassie, “Hey, are you going to the party? I know you didn’t play or even dress out, but uh, good job?” He offered as she laughed.

“You’re such an idiot, Tommy. Yes, I’m going, are you.”

“I mean, if you are…” Tommy ran a hand through his hair.

Jen groaned. “This kid is painful,” She said to Wanda, who nodded.

Wanda looked at them, “We’ll give you a ride, Cassie, come on.”

Rio watched as everyone dispersed, most people headed towards Tony’s. She looked at Agatha, “I should make an appearance, huh?”

“You did coach them to victory,” Agatha offered.

“Okay, fine, but just a few minutes, then take me home.”

⭐⭐⭐

It was finally time, Vegas weekend. Wedding weekend.

Alice wore an ecru tux with an orange vest and heels. Her black and red hair was in big, loose curls. They were getting married in a chapel her mother had frequented. It was cliche and a bit cheesy, but very ‘Wu’.

“You’re beautiful,” Rio gushed, eyes full of tears.

“Don’t cry, you’ll ruin your makeup,” Alice scolded, knowing that if Rio cried, she would too.

Rio was wearing a simple orange spaghetti-strap dress with heels and had a bouquet of pink roses. “No promises once the ceremony starts, okay? I just wish…” She trailed off.

“I know. I wish they were here too.” Alice finished. She knew Rio was thinking of Lorna and Gran but was trying to spare them tears.

They turned when they heard Wanda come into the small room.

“Oh, you’re both beautiful.” Wanda fanned her eyes so she wouldn’t cry. “Alice, let’s get you married.”

Alice stood at the altar. Vision was officiating; they were just using the chapel. Nat, Wanda, and the boys sat in chairs in the audience.

Agatha waited just outside of the chapel with Jen. Agatha wore a black suit with a pink bowtie and matching vest. Her chestnut hair was down in long waves, a pink rose in her chest pocket, and pink stilettos completing the outfit.

Jen wore a strapless, tea-length ivory dress that flared out at the waist with a petticoat underneath. It had a pink sash around the middle, and she wore a pink knitted bolero, pink heels, and carried pink roses that matched Rio’s. Her hair had grown out since the fall, and she wore her natural curls close to her scalp with a simple strand of pearls.

She wore Gran’s engagement ring, a white gold band with three pink gemstones in the middle.

Agatha’s heart stopped when Rio approached. She was beautiful. The orange perfectly complimented her skin, and Agatha couldn’t help but picture what Rio would wear to Wanda’s wedding or potentially their own one day.

“Wow,” Was all Agatha could say as she kissed Rio, careful not to smudge her makeup.

“Wow, yourself,” Rio parroted before turning to Jen. “You look absolutely radiant. I am so happy for you. But if you hurt her, whatever Nat is capable of will seem like child’s play.”

Jen gulped, taking Rio seriously, “I don’t know if I’m afraid or if I want your number…”

Agatha narrowed her eyes at Jen as she heard the music start from inside.

“That’s our cue. My love?” She held out her arm for Rio as they went inside and headed down the aisle to their places.

Jen followed, and Alice’s lips parted in a delighted sigh. Jen did the same when she saw Alice. Rio thought they looked perfect for each other because they were perfect for each other.

The ceremony began, and the time to exchange vows came. They had each prepared their own, and Alice shifted nervously as she started.

“I’m going to try and get through this without crying, and I usually don’t cry,” she laughed shakily, seeing only Jen in front of her. “Jennifer, you were one of my first friends in LA when Agatha and Wanda pulled me in, and you saw me for more than Lorna Wu’s daughter and Agatha’s goddaughter.”

Rio had been listening intently and was shocked at that small revelation as she caught Agatha’s eye and exchanged glances with her. They would discuss that small tidbit later, but that explained why she talked to Alice at Lorna’s funeral. Rio had kept listening while in her own thoughts.

“We quickly became friends and walked that very thin line for far too long. The classic tale of falling in love with one of your best friends and not wanting to lose that. When we finally went on that first date last fall, it was like someone threw gas on a smoldering fire. We couldn’t go fast enough, though some people will say we took too long, myself being one of them. We’re finally here, and that’s what matters. While part of me feels like we’re making up for lost time, I know we spent most of that time together in some capacity. We fell in love over a decade ago, and it took us that long to figure it out. Hanging out at the compound, parties, texting during awards shows, so many little things brought us here to this moment, and I am grateful for them all.”

Alice swiped a tear away from her cheek as Rio handed her Kleenex, and Agatha did the same for Jen. Alice took Jen’s hand in hers and continued.

“I vow to love you fiercely, to always ask your expertise when creating lesson plans, to be your hiking partner, and to listen to you rant about certain people in this room.”

They both grinned and looked at Agatha after the last one, who just shrugged before turning back to the moment at hand.

“Most importantly, I vow to be your rock, the person you come home to, the one you can lean on for the rest of our lives and whatever comes after that.” Alice wiped a tear from Jen’s cheek with her thumb.

“How am I supposed to follow that?” Jen took a breath, steadying herself. “Alice. You came into my life at a personal turning point in so many ways. I was proving myself in my career and my identity. I was thirty and having a crisis. The first major interaction we had, I was ranting about a major scandal I was having to fix because of your fairy gaymother back there, and you made me dinner, helped me work through it, and then picked my brain for historical accuracy for something. You’ve always been able to calm my inner rage in a way no one else has. I was so afraid of losing that that I denied my feelings for so long. When you called hours after I told you I just wanted to be friends, I was so thankful that you saw through it, like you always do. It does seem like we ran with things when, in reality, it was painfully slow. I can’t imagine doing life without you. I vow to drive you insane with my skincare routine, to love you intensely, to always be by your side, and to never intentionally cause you pain.”

Rings were exchanged, a pronouncement was made, and Alice and Jen were officially married. There was not a dry eye in the place.

They had a small dinner afterward to celebrate before everyone went their separate ways and began their Spring Break plans. The newlyweds were off to somewhere tropical for a few days before being back in LA for the reception. Vision and the twins were going to hike in the Grand Canyon for a few days. Nat and Wanda had plans to hustle a few poker tables for sport, and Billy went back to his room to talk to Morgan. He was going skiing with the Starks for a few days, and they were making plans for the trip. The boy was exactly like his mother when it came to love.

Rio and Agatha sat in a private VIP area at The Wynn, overlooking the activity and watching two redheads win hand after hand.

“Wanda Maximoff, gambler and bookie. Who knew?” Rio observed as Agatha sipped champagne. “Speaking of surprising things, godmother?” Rio turned to look at the woman next to her.

“Alice didn’t want to make a big deal of it, and it was her secret to tell, not mine,” Agatha waved it off.

“That’s fair. Is that what you told her at Lorna’s funeral?”

“Part of it, again, this is a discussion you need to have with Alice. What I can tell you is that when Lorna had Alice, she asked me to be the godmother more as a formality. However, she did want Alice to have a choice between Norma and myself. So when Lorna died, I gave her that envelope with Lorna’s journal and documents. I knew some of what was in there, not what’s written in the journal, other than it’s for Alice if she ever reads it.” Agatha turned to Rio. “For everything else, you really do need to talk to Alice.”

Rio accepted that. She knew Lorna and Agatha had been close and that there were things about that particular history that she would never know.

“Will do. Now, we have a busy week ahead of us and an early flight to New York tomorrow. I say we leave the red-headed demons to their hustle and, um, turn in for the night, my love.” Rio placed her empty champagne flute on a nearby table and took Agatha’s hand, leading her to the door.

“Oh? Tired already?” Agatha placed her glass down and followed Rio.

“Something like that.”

⭐⭐⭐

Spring Break felt like a movie montage. The first few days were spent in New York, and the second half was spent in LA, leading up to Alice and Jen’s reception. Wanda and Nat had come with them so Nat could do bodyguard duty, and Wanda was on some of the press as well, with the awards' success. Rio loved watching Agatha do interviews and talk about her projects; how animated she was speaking about her work, facial expressions, and reactions. Agatha was so dedicated to what she did, and it showed in interviews. Everyone she spoke with would ask Agatha about Rio and Nicky, and she would gush about Nicky and try to be close-lipped about Rio but blush and say things were good and she was happy.

They went to Buzzfeed, where Wanda and Agatha both filmed a segment for ‘Thirst Tweets,’ which was full of laughter and was highly inappropriate. Rio and Nat were both mentioned in several of them and could be heard from behind the camera.

They flew back to LA for more press as everyone else started to return and prepare for the reception on Saturday. Lou’s trial was also that week, and everyone watched it closely. They expected her to plead insanity and get it. The sentencing is what they were the most concerned with, and how to keep her wherever she was sent to. That was the hard part.

Rio sat in front of the TV at Agatha’s, waiting for the final word when everyone else came back from whatever show they had been filming for.

“Any word yet?” Agatha asked.

Rio just shook her head, eyes glued to the TV as Jen’s phone rang and the special report cut off.

All eyes turned to Jen as she hung up the phone.

“Before sentencing occurs, Lou is requesting to speak to you privately at the courthouse. She says she has information you’ll want, and some things that belong to you, specifically those videos. Lawyers will meet us there.” Jen turned on her heel and walked back out.

Rio sat in the judge’s chambers across a table from Lou. Everyone else was outside and pissed about it—no recording devices, just the two of them, as Rio glared at her.

Lou inhaled sharply, “Where do you want me to go, Rio?” She asked sincerely.

Rio looked at her cautiously. “What are you playing at?”

Lou let out a shrill laugh. “I want you more than anything, and I love you more than anything. Yet here I am. The past finally caught up to me. Oh, we’re not over, not in the least. I’ll get out, and I’ll get you back. I still have eyes and ears on you and Agatha. However, it’s not looking great at this moment. Lawyers say if I can get a good character witness, I might not be sent to the Island.”

A sarcastic smile slid across Rio’s face before she started laughing.

“Are you fucking kidding me? You’re asking me to put in a good word for you? You’re unbelievable! No one gets out of The Island. It’s the most secure psych institution in the world; people disappear from there, never to be seen again, Lou, and I sincerely hope you’re one of them. How the fuck do you still have eyes and ears on us?” That tidbit just hit Rio as she backtracked.

“You are obsessed with knowing what your precious cat is doing all the fucking time,” Lou let out an exasperated sigh. “I just laid it all out for you, and you’re still going to let them put me away, possibly never to see me again?”

“I hate you, and I don’t hate anyone! You made me despise you! You made me genuinely afraid for my life! You tried to hurt my son, Lou! I can’t trust you. You want to know where I want you? In a fucking grave! It’s the only way to know I and everyone I love is safe! Alice and Jen got married, and I was nervous you would find a way to ruin their day! You tried to win me back with empty promises of a baby, that’s a whole new level of fucked up, even for you! No, I will not be a character witness. Wait, is that the information you had? How you were still listening in? Through the pet cameras?” Rio was livid.

“It got you down here, didn’t it?”

Rio slammed her hands on the table, causing Lou to jump. “Where are these belongings? Videos? Whatever the fuck else you have?”

“House, in the safe. You need me…”

“No, I don’t. You’re predictable, and I know all your codes.” Rio stood and walked toward the door. “I don’t want to see your face again, Lou Falco. I feel so bad for Shane’s kids and pray they have a wonderful therapist. I hope you rot and are never heard from again. I was in love with you once, but I’m not now. When I do get married and potentially have a baby, it will be with Agatha.” With that, Rio left the room, Lou screaming behind her.

Rio looked at Nat as she walked past her, “I assume you got all that somehow, and we’re disabling Raven’s cameras and sending a team to Lou’s?”

Nat nodded.

“Good.” Rio took Agatha’s hand as they walked out a side door where they had come in unseen. “I have a feeling she’ll disappear in a few days and won’t be heard from again.”

⭐⭐⭐

Alice and Jen couldn’t have asked for a more beautiful reception. Their friends had outdone themselves, and everything was going amazingly well. They had repeated their vows for everyone and were mingling, staying together, and accepting congratulations from co-workers, friends, and acquaintances.

Rio watched as everyone enjoyed themselves. The past few days had happened in a whirlwind, and she had to work in less than forty-eight hours. She watched as Wanda and Nat whispered to each other, discussing their future wedding most likely. The boys and their dates were dancing and laughing, and Agatha was talking animatedly with the Starks, among others, when she looked back at Rio and winked.

Rio’s mind wandered, taking in the expanse of celebrities and people now a part of her daily life. Everyone seemed to be here. Suddenly, she couldn’t help but think about how Agatha had mentioned that Swords was still in her life. Was Swords here? At Alice’s reception? It bothered Rio that Swords might be someone who would be at big events like this, and she didn’t know it. She looked around, surveying the room for older Hollywood women.  That’s when Rio heard a familiar voice.

“...you know, I helped Cher write Believe.”

No. The world stopped in the worst of ways, everything happening in slow motion. The voice floated to Rio’s ears again.

“C’mon kid, the song isn’t THAT old.”

Is that where Agatha got the ‘kid’ habit from?

Swords wasn’t a Hollywood powerhouse. No, she was the mysterious figure in the shadows, a keeper of secrets. Not famous, but infamous.

Puzzle pieces started to fall in place. The older woman, the caring mentor.

Rio turned her head, eyes landing on the owner of the voice, knowing full well the owner’s identity but not wanting to admit it to herself as the full weight of it hit her like a punch to the chest, shattering her heart, and sucking all the air from the room.

The Calderu knows best attitude, arrogance, and entitlement.

Lilia was Agatha’s ex.



Chapter End Notes

Oh damn.

I Have Learned The Lesson Of All That's Foul and Fair

Chapter Summary

A chronological flashback from 1987 to 2024.
How did we get here?
How did Lilia become the Swords of Agatha's tortured past?
How did Lorna fit in?
Just where did everything go so very wrong?

Chapter Notes

In this universe - Agatha is Idina Menzel as well, so, original Maureen, Elphaba, Elsa...

Trigger warnings: All of them.
Death, loss, implied abuse, pregnancy loss, addiction, drug use, implied minor doing illegal things, implied illegal activities, Agatha being Agatha, illness, birth, cold cougars.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

1987 Los Angeles

“Mom! Why can’t I just go to school normally like other kids? Instead of being tutored and doing everything from a distance? There’s that school for kids of celebrities and the rich that’s right here. I could socialize and be out of your hair.” Fourteen-year-old Agatha Harkness asked her mother.
