She spent all of her time either under Evanora’s very controlling thumb or on a set. She had her ways of rebelling and doing what she wanted, but more freedom was something she craved. She had gone through tutors and homeschool programs, and if her mother would let her, she could test out of high school and probably a few degree programs. She could speak multiple languages, code those new computers, and run all the movie equipment…what else was a bored child star supposed to do with her spare time? She broke laws, learned, and had a lot of mature fun with her costars.

Her mother wanted her to waste her talents on bible studies and high school work.

“You have been learning things above your station, Agatha. You will also spend more time in your religious studies. I only allow you to continue this devil’s work because it is good money for your future.” Evanora replied in her normal, harsh voice.

“Ha. You mean for you.” Agatha responded and received a slap across the face. It was a wonder she didn’t have a constant bruise on her left cheek.

“You will not smart back to me!”

It was then that there was a knock on the door. Evanora looked at Agatha severely. Agatha couldn’t remember a time when there was love in her mother’s eyes.

“That school you so desperately wanted to attend sends teachers for distance students. No need to physically go to the school. Ms. Calderu will be your teacher for the rest of your high school years. You will be respectful. Now, I have errands to run.”

Evanora opened the door of the trailer, welcomed Ms. Calderu in, and introduced her to Agatha before leaving. Agatha knew her mother was going to some church function.

“Good afternoon, Miss Harkness,” Lilia greeted her, “I’m Ms. Calderu, and I’ll be your teacher for the remainder of your high school experience. I understand that you’re actually quite advanced,” The teacher smiled warmly at her, setting books down on the table and sitting.

Agatha looked Lilia up and down. The older woman had long brown hair that she wore up, hazel eyes, and an Italian-New Yorker accent. Agatha propped her chin in one hand. This was going to be fun.

“What’s your first name?” Agatha asked innocently.

“I don’t see why that’s relevant,” Lilia replied, sorting through a stack of papers.

“You know mine, and if I’m going to be stuck with you for the next several years, it’s only fair I know it,” Agatha reasoned.

“I’ve been warned about you, Miss Harkness. Your tactics won’t work, and you won’t scare me.”

Lilia had indeed heard how Agatha had either outsmarted, outwit, psychologically tortured, or plain seduced any previous person who tried to teach her. Purely for fun. SHIELD was the elder Harkness’s last option. Agatha wasn’t a bad kid. She was just trying to spite her mother.

“Oh? Did Evanora warn you? Tell you I was born evil and should have been killed at birth?”

Agatha’s face hardened. She no longer looked fourteen, but much older. Lilia wanted to soften but had been warned about this, too.

“No, a mutual friend of ours, and I’ve been told you like a mystery. So, for every lesson we complete, I’ll either answer a question of yours or let you guess who our mutual friend is, agreed?”

Lilia had always been good with these Hollywood kids. She had grown up adjacent to Broadway and moved out to LA when her best friend had. The child stars had to be given some semblance of control as they had so little of it.

Agatha looked at her cautiously, “For each assignment I complete, not a lesson, an assignment could be a worksheet, that could be four or five a session,” She countered, her blue eyes sparkling.

Lilia scoffed. “Every two,” she counteroffered.

“Deal.” Agatha agreed.

That day, Agatha learned that Ms. Calderu was 28, her first name was Lilia, she was originally from New York, and her favorite color was yellow.

When Lilia returned to the house she shared with her friend, she sat her bag down and sighed.

“You’re right. She’s a smart kid but is trouble with a capital T and is shadier than a forest. Her mother is also a complete loon and abusive.”

Hollywood superstar Lorna Wu nodded.

“I hope to get her out of there one day, sooner rather than later. She has a bright future ahead of her if we remove the crazy factor. That’s where you come in.”

⭐⭐⭐

1989 Los Angeles

“After being outed as a lesbian, and her mother attempting to murder her, Agatha Harkness, the sixteen-year-old award-winning actress, has won her legal battle against her mother and is now legally independent from her…”

Agatha cut off the TV that showed a picture of her with a stoic expression on the steps of the Los Angeles courthouse, watching as her mother was escorted away. Evanora had been hurling bible verses at her as they led her away. Lawyers surrounded Agatha, the face of America’s brave lesbian sweetheart. Sixteen and on her own.

She sat in her house. She planned on selling it and buying a condo, with Lorna and Lilia in the condo on either side, watching her carefully. She had guessed that Lorna was the ‘mutual friend’ three months into Lilia being her teacher.

“I’m sixteen,” She said softly, lip quivering. “I’m sixteen and will be seventeen in a few months. My closest friends are a twenty-nine-year-old movie star who made me the godmother of her child and my thirty-year-old teacher. I am technically a self-inflicted orphan, the face of the queer community, a child star still on the rise, I have a growing reputation for being a bit of a party girl, and people want to be me,” A tear fell down her cheek as she looked at both of them. “I don’t want to be me right now.”

Lilia pushed a piece of hair behind her ear. “I know Aggie. We’re both here for you. You have a staff of people, and you are the strongest person we know. You can go to school now if you want. You can stop acting, whatever you want.”

“No, I love acting and telling stories. My filming schedule isn’t conducive to going to school, and I’m almost done. I do want to get my college degree, though. I just, it’s a lot. Thank you both for everything you’ve done to get me out of there. Lorna, you’ll be a great mom. I really think if you moved Gran and Alice out here…” Agatha trailed off. She knew it was no use. Lorna was more of a mom to Agatha than Evanora had been, but Lorna was insistent on Alice having a ‘normal’ life.

Lorna pulled out a small box and handed it to Agatha.

“We got you something to commemorate today, and our little coven, so to speak,” Lorna looked to Lilia as Agatha opened the box.

Agatha took in the gold, purple, and ivory locket with the three women on it, looking at the women on either side of her, eyebrows raised in question.

Lilia reached over, pointing to each silhouette as she spoke. “It’s the triple goddess, maiden, mother, crone.”

Lorna nodded, adding. “The three of us. Lilia’s the crone,” She grinned. “It’s a reminder that while you’ve freed yourself from Evanora, you’re never alone. We’re in this together, sweetie.”

Agatha lightly ran her fingers over the three silhouettes, an idea pushing it’s way to the front of her brain. “Wait one minute!” She got up, her bare feet running to the bathroom and she returned with three small rubberbands and scissors, one of the very few times her age actually shone through her more mature facade.

“Cut off a small lock of hair and hand it to me.” The older women did as commanded, and Agatha took the auburn, chestnut, and jet black locks of hair and secured them in the locket.

“There,” she said, voice wavering with the weight of her emotions, “the three of us, together, forever.”

⭐⭐⭐

1991, Los Angeles

It was Agatha’s eighteenth birthday. Although she had been an adult for longer, she was now legally an adult. That day, she bought a large piece of land that she eventually wanted to build on. For now, she would need to work on getting permits, clearing the land, and extending utilities to it.

Agatha was not like any other eighteen-year-old in the world.

She also was finally going to act on recently developed feelings.

Lately, she had begun to see Lilia in a different light.

Lilia had always been one of her two mentors. She had come into her life with a mission to free her from Evanora’s clutches, had been there for her during very dark times, and was one of her closest friends.

Agatha absolutely loved Lilia, just as she loved Lorna. Her best friends happened to be in their thirties, and Agatha happened to be more emotionally mature than her eighteen years on this earth. She wasn’t in love with Lilia, no, but she was attracted to her. She always had been to a degree, but now, it was boiling at the edge, and she needed to act on it.

For her birthday, they had rented out a club in downtown LA. She had been drinking. No one cared how old you were when you were as famous as she was. She had finally mustered up the courage, and “Rescue Me” by Madonna was playing when Agatha found Lilia standing on her own.

Perfect.

“Happy Birthday, Gorgeous!” Lilia smiled as Agatha approached.

Agatha was fueled by vodka and couldn’t stop. She went all in, hooked her fingers through the belt loops of Lilia’s jeans, and kissed Lilia like it was the last thing she was going to do.

The best part was when Lilia kissed her back.

Agatha relaxed into the kiss when Lilia forcefully shoved her back, eyes wide, lips swollen.

“Agatha, I…” She was stepping away from Agatha in shock. “I hate Madonna,” she blurted before she darted away. Agatha went to go after her when she felt a hand close around her bicep. She turned to see Lorna, eyes full of worry, as she pulled her to a quieter area.

“What were you thinking, Agatha?” she asked. She wasn’t mad, just confused.

“You know I’ve always been attracted to her,” Agatha replied. She was leaning against a wall, still fuzzy from the alcohol, arms crossed, eyes trained on the toes of her shoes.

“Yes, but that doesn’t mean kiss her the day you’re no longer jailbait, sweetie,” Lorna put a comforting hand on her shoulder. “Do you really want to turn one of your best friends into another conquest? You just turned eighteen, and everyone knows what you’ve been up to since you were thirteen. Now, you can broadcast it. Do you want Lilia to be another on a growing list?”

Lorna wasn’t judging. At thirty-one, she had a long list herself and several divorces to her name. There was no shame here. Agatha let out a sound of agreement.

“You’re right.”

“I usually am. Now let’s go find Lilia and get some water in you, birthday girl.”

⭐⭐⭐

1993 Los Angeles

Agatha had just returned from several months of filming overseas, and Lorna was still abroad. Things had gone back to normal in the past two years, though Agatha still had feelings for Lilia.

Lilia wasn’t sure how she felt about Agatha, but she knew she didn’t feel right dating her, and she was not going to be another notch in her metaphorical bedpost. She was, however, going to take her out to celebrate her return. Agatha had matured more and looked far less like the fourteen-year-old girl she first met six years ago and more like the woman she was destined to be. Lilia was proud of her. She was racking up awards and rising in fame and popularity. She hadn’t gone the way of some childhood stars, and she was using her fame for good.

Lilia loved and cherished her career. However, she also loved the position it put her in and the proximity to power. You could take the Italian out of New York…

She dealt in secrets and information, her ability to read people gave her the upper hand on these power players, and she could negotiate a Hollywood contract with the best of them. Lilia was behind some major dealings in Hollywood, and no one would ever know. She had recently purchased an old Victorian mansion and was having it renovated. ‘Oh, an uncle back east passed.’ she had said. More like, ‘Howard Stark closed a major deal with my help. ’

Some of this knowledge she would pass to Agatha. How to be discreet in your affairs, curate the you that you wanted the public to see. Agatha was going to be a star like no other. She knew it, and so did Lorna. Or at least, that was their hope.

Agatha had told Lilia all about filming and stories about her co-stars. She had been nervous throughout dinner and then afterward at the bar they had gone to. She’d even turned down every woman who approached her, which was very unlike her. Lilia had an idea of what was happening, and she had to stop it.

“Lilia, I’ve been thinking about what happened when I turned eighteen…” Agatha looked at her nervously.

“Agatha, you can’t even legally drink yet,” Lilia knew what she was about to say.

“I’m drinking right now. In public.”

“Not legally.” Lilia pointed out.

“Lilia, I’m legally an adult. I own property, in fact, several homes. Hell, I run a successful club in New York, have an EGOT, and more awards than some actors three times my age.” Agatha listed off qualifications as if she were in an interview.

“Agatha, you know I don’t care about any of that. I’m not going to be another name on your list.” Lilia didn’t expect Agatha to be offended.

“Lilia, that’s not what I want. I respect you too much to just make you another nameless woman in a pile of bodies. I want to date you.”

Lilia was shocked. “Agatha, we cannot date. People would talk,” she lowered her voice. “People would think I had had a relationship with you when I taught you. I could lose my career.” Lilia had invested too much to get where she was.

“You didn’t though, that’s disgusting. I thought you were pretty, but no.” Agatha wrinkled her nose.

Lilia smirked, “Because you weren’t having age-inappropriate relations at that age?”

“Not with you,” Agatha replied.

“We can’t. Go home, Agatha, you’ve had too much to drink, and you’re not thinking straight.” Lilia took her drink from her.

“I’m not capable of thinking straight,” Agatha muttered.

⭐⭐⭐

April 1996 New York City

It was the opening night of Rent, and Agatha was playing Maureen. She had just put on an amazing performance and was hoping this would be her second Tony. She met Lorna and Lilia backstage, both of them glowing.

“Oh, sweetie, you did such a great job! I am so proud of you! Your second Broadway show, and it’s going to be such a success!” Lorna gushed like the proud surrogate mother she was. Alice was seven, yet here Lorna was, a bigger part of Agatha’s life than Alice’s. Agatha would always carry that guilt with her.

“Do you really think so?” Agatha asked, seeking the approval she so desperately desired.

“Of course, you’re the perfect person to play Maureen. No one can cage you.”

Lilia added, pulling Agatha in for a hug. They both smelled like the lavender incense that engulfed Agatha’s Brooklyn apartment. Agatha’s feelings for Lilia had only increased, and she knew that once she added alcohol to the mix, she would be making moves tonight.

They were celebrating opening night and were out with the cast, crew, friends, and family. Lorna had already left with her current flavor of the moment, and Agatha and Lilia had stolen off to a corner and were considering leaving themselves. Agatha had shows the next day and had been drinking.

“How’s the kid?”

Agatha asked, referencing the sixteen-year-old Sokovian refugee, Wanda Maximoff. She had recently been taken in by her aunt, a producer, in LA after losing her parents and twin brother after the war had basically wiped her small country off the map. She was a sweet kid and was cast alongside Agatha in a TV show, ‘Sisters’. Agatha was doing Rent for a few months while they were off and then would return to LA. Agatha didn’t trust Wanda’s aunt and was keeping a close eye on her. Agatha fully planned on being the Lorna in her life at Lilia’s insistence if needed. She would have done it anyway. She knew what it was like to be in an abusive environment.

“Wanda’s aunt is getting worse, but we have eyes on the kid. She’s asked about you. Give her a call or an email sometime, will you? Wanda misses you. You’re like an older sister to her. She really doesn’t have anyone, and we’re ancient in her eyes. She’s not as mature as you were at that age.”

Lilia had concern in her eyes and was acting as a teacher to Wanda just as she had to Agatha nearly a decade ago.

“I’m glad she has both of you, like I did. How’s Lorna doing?” Agatha asked as they started to head toward the exit.

Lilia just made an exasperated sound. Lorna’s ‘demons,’ as they called it, were increasing. Agatha’s own drinking was increasing, so she couldn’t say much. Lilia was the only one who had any control of anything and had to for her career.

“I’m keeping an eye on her. I wish she would move Norma and Alice to LA. Enroll Alice at SHIELD. Norma would keep her healthy, at least. She’d get you into shape, too. Every time you visit them, she lays into you.” Lilia gave Agatha a knowing look.

“I know. I know she wants Alice to experience some type of normalcy. She can still be involved, though.”

Agatha ended that line of conversation there, switching back to the musical as they talked until they arrived at the apartment in Brooklyn. She had dreams of renovating an entire floor of a building one day, maybe in Manhattan. For now, she liked this one.

Agatha always had an air of confidence bordering on cockiness, and it was partly how she got to where she was. The older she got, the more it grew, and the bolder and more arrogant she became. Her increasing alcohol usage did not help, and her increased tolerance hid her lack of sobriety well.

They settled for the night, and it was now or, well, next time. Agatha looked at Lilia, who was telling her a very animated story about school. NDAs be damned, Lilia told Agatha anyway and just spoke in initials. They were sitting close together on the loveseat, twisted towards each other. Agatha kept subtly moving closer, something that naturally happened between them until she was leaning against Lilia. Agatha turned her head, their faces inches apart. Their eyes locked.

This time, it was mutual.

It was passionate.

It was forbidden.

Lilia was, once again, the one to pull back.

“Agatha, we can’t.”

Instead of panicked, she looked sad.

“We can, and I’ve thought about it. Just listen,” Agatha had both hands on Lilia’s forearm. Lilia had one arm on the back of the couch and a hand on Agatha’s thigh. “You have to go back to LA, and I’m staying here. We both excel at casual. That’s what we do.”

Lilia wasn’t so sure.

“Agatha, you’ve wanted to date for years. I don’t think you can do casual, and I don’t do monogamous. You’ve known me for almost ten years, and you know my history. Lorna is a serial monogamist. I date many people, and you’re, well you…” Lilia trailed off.

“I have a very high libido,” Agatha finished for her. “I can do this, Lilia. Just don’t fall in love with me. I’m very irresistible.”

It was an extremely bad idea.

They made an extremely bad decision that would change their lives.

⭐⭐⭐

April 1996 Los Angeles, a few days later

“YOU HAD SEX WITH AGATHA?! WHAT WERE YOU THINKING?!” Lorna screamed.

Lilia expected this reaction as they stood in Lilia’s kitchen. Lilia was trying to get her to eat something after a few days of Lorna obviously not eating anything.

“That she’s a very attractive woman and can make her own decisions,” Lilia responded, directing Lorna to the table.

“No, Lilia. You cannot do this. We practically raised her. This is not okay.” Lorna sat, accepting the bowl of pasta from her friend.

“YOU practically raised her. I’m more of an older friend and gay auntie. Okay, yeah, that makes it weird.”

“You have taught her how to lie, cheat, curate her image, and now what, hone her skills in other areas? No, do NOT tell me about that.” Lorna added as Lilia began to speak. “She is going to fall in love with you, Lilia. She’s been halfway there for years as it is.”

“She said it would be casual,” Lilia repeated. She had repeated it to herself over the past few days, trying to make herself feel better about what had happened.

“You believe that? Agatha is manipulative. You know that! You, of all people, should be impervious to her tactics, but you let your desires get in your way. I get that she is young, smart, and beautiful. She even makes me question myself. Alas, tragically straight. But Lilia, she’s not one of your Hollywood side items. She’s our Aggie.” Lorna finally took a bite of pasta, trying to let her words sink in.

“What if I want to continue this? What if I like being with her, Lorna?” Lilia asked, slumping into a chair with pasta of her own.

“Do you want commitment? Do you want to be public?” Lorna asked between bites, being careful not to eat too fast.

“No. I can’t risk my job like that, teaching or behind the scenes,” Lilia sighed. “I also want her to have someone there for her. Her drinking is getting out of hand and…” She looked away from Lorna, not able to see it.

“And I started out as a functional alcoholic, and you don’t want her to spiral like I am?” Lorna raised an eyebrow.

Lilia worried at her bottom lip. She couldn’t voice what she was actually thinking. Yes, Lorna had things mostly under control for now, but that was the real reason she didn’t want Alice and Norma to move to LA. Lilia knew Norma would be tougher on Lorna than Lilia and Agatha were. Norma would light into Agatha, too. It’s what both of them needed, really.

“You know I just want what’s best, and I worry. I want you to live to see Alice get married, have kids if she wants, and excel in whatever career she chooses,” Lilia gestured with her hands.

Lorna smiled. She spoke with her mom every day about Alice. “She plays school with her dolls every day. She’s going to teach, like you.” She pointed at Lilia with her fork.

“Hopefully, the teaching system gets better. There’s always a place at SHIELD for her.”

Lorna shook her head, “You’re going to run that place, could you imagine? Her teaching for you?”

Lilia laughed, “Yeah, and Agatha will have kids there. That’ll never happen.”

Lorna finished eating and returned to being serious. “Are you going to keep this thing up with Agatha?”

Lilia shrugged, “I don’t know. I care for her, and we have fun. What’s a little harmless fun between friends?”

“It’s not harmless,” Lorna said darkly.

“We’re both adults, Lorna.”

“I know you’ve been cryptic with her on occasion. When she acts flirty with you. She tells me everything. You have to remain that person. You can’t let her fall in love with you Lils. You can’t commit. You can’t treat it like a real relationship. You cannot hurt her,” Lorna sighed. “It has to strictly be casual.”

“I know.”

“Do you? Because you’re playing with fire.”

Lilia knew she was playing with fire, but she could also be cold,  cryptic, and distant.

What she didn’t know was how much damage her decisions would actually cause.

⭐⭐⭐

August 1996, Lilia’s Side of the ‘stay the night’ Flashback

Lilia was trying so hard to be cold to Agatha, and it hurt like hell. She knew why she had to do it. She couldn’t let Agatha fall in love with her. Lorna would murder Lilia. She could not lose Lorna or Agatha. They were the two most important people in her life. Sleeping with Agatha was not the best decision she had ever made, but that was Lilia’s infatuation.

Lorna had drugs.

Agatha had alcohol.

Lilia had Agatha.

Agatha had asked Lilia to stay the night, but Lilia’s heart dropped. She so badly wanted to say yes. She wanted to wake up with her the next morning, but that really wasn’t her thing, and she couldn’t give the paparazzi anything. Instead, she played the role of the older woman here for casual sex. Lilia played two roles. Lilia, her loving friend and mentor, and Lilia, Agatha’s casual fling..

It was exhausting.

It might destroy her.

⭐⭐⭐

Christmas 1997 San Francisco

Agatha and Wanda had gotten close. Agatha was a Lorna to Wanda. They were on a hit show together, and they had built a house on the land Agatha had bought at eighteen. A gorgeous Russian spy had attempted to kill them, now, said spy lived with them, and her mother was dead.

What a time to be alive.

She was also sleeping with Lilia Calderu, so that was a plus.

What wasn’t a plus was that Agatha was currently in San Francisco.

She loved Norma Wu not only because she was Lorna’s mother but also because she had raised Lorna to be an amazing woman.

However, she could have done without Norma’s lectures, especially when Lilia would take her side every single time.

The five women walked into the large home on Christmas Eve to be welcomed by the small, rambunctious eight-year-old, followed by a joyful but judgy older woman.

“Welcome, girls! Come in, get settled in your rooms! You must be Wanda and Natasha!” Norma addressed the two younger women.

“Alice, show these two to the guest room. I hope you two don’t mind sharing a bed.”

She added, raising an eyebrow at Agatha. Norma just assumed everyone was gay at this point. Agatha shrugged. She had an inkling, but no clear confirmation yet.

“Come with me! Where’s your accent from? Gran says our house is like the United Nations!” She chattered as she skipped down the hallway.

Gran turned towards the other three, a finger in their faces. “No sex, no drugs, no alcohol. Do you three understand me?”

Agatha scoffed. “Always such fun, Norma.”

“Agatha Evanora Harkness,” Norma began, causing Agatha to wince. She hated her middle name and wanted to legally change it to Fucking. Norma was five foot even and was truly the only person on the planet that terrified her. Especially when she used Agatha’s middle name.

“Yes, ma’am,” She agreed.

“Good, Lorna and Agatha, take everyone’s things to their rooms, presents under the tree. Agatha is staying with you, Lorna, in the other guest room. Lilia, I need to see you in the kitchen.”

Norma turned and walked toward the kitchen while the three women exchanged glances. Whatever was about to happen to Lilia wasn’t good. There were sharp objects in there.

Lilia and Norma reached the kitchen, and Norma pointed to the table, instructing her to sit.

“Any progress with either of them?” Norma asked, taking a seat.

“No,” Lilia looked defeated. “They haven’t gotten worse since they’re busy filming, so that’s something? It helps that Agatha has the girls now, and she’s been roping Lorna in with that.” Lilia reported.

“And your…”

“On and off,” Lilia supplied, knowing what Norma meant.

“It’s been over a year. Don’t you think it’s time to end it?” Norma asked.

“I’ve been done this road enough, Norma. I brought them here for Christmas like you wanted. Now, you can try your intervention on them, not me. There are two people involved in my situation, not just me.”

“You’re older, Lilia,” Norma reminded.

“She’s a consenting adult, Norma.”

Agatha thought Christmas had gone well, though that was the first Christmas Lilia had been weird about their gift exchange, and their dynamic started to change.

⭐⭐⭐

1998 Los Angeles Lilia’s Side of the Soulmate Conversation

Agatha had just asked her if she believed in soulmates. Lilia was a student of tarot. Of course, she believed in soulmates.

She sure as hell wasn’t telling Agatha that.

She remained cold, distant, and casual—an older fling.

She believed Agatha had a soulmate somewhere.

Lilia Calderu had to believe she was not that person, though, and it killed her.

Right now, she was not letting her emotions color their moment, lying here.

Right now, she focused on Lorna’s warning and her duty to Agatha, to keep her at an emotional arm’s length.

Instead of romance and sweetness, Lilia reverted to her now well-practiced advice on the challenge of maintaining a long-term, steady relationship with the amount of fame Agatha held. She deliberately lectured and called Agatha ‘my pet’ to reduce the intimacy she felt as they recovered from another beautiful time together.

Her tactics worked. Lilia could feel the discomfort and slight hurt from Agatha as the wedge she hammered in place deepened the gap between them. The longer Lilia had to play cold and distant, the harder it became to separate the two identities.

It’s why, though she tried to stay away, stay strong, and stay cold, the infatuation won out, and they always fell back into bed.

Lilia could barely resist Agatha, and she knew that could be as damaging as any addiction.

⭐⭐⭐

1999 Los Angeles, Harkimoff HQ

Agatha, at an old and wise twenty-six years old, knew that something was going on with the two redheads. It once more brought up the mystery of that night two years ago. There had been no reason for Natasha not to murder Agatha that night, but Natasha hadn’t expected Wanda’s presence— or at least, that’s what the former assassin kept saying. Agatha knew she was lying. No, Natasha hadn’t just been distracted by Wanda; she had been mesmerized. Cupid’s arrow struck the assassin as she confronted her target, abandoning her assignment, and spent the next two years watching Wanda figure out her feelings.

Now, here sat a Wanda, who seemed younger than her twenty years, asking Agatha how to tell if she liked girls. Agatha knew she should be helpful and supportive. Instead, she cackled. Agatha had always known she was gay. She took one look at boys as a kid, said no thanks, and started going after girls and women.

Now, sweet, innocent, girl-next-door Wanda Maximoff, who had been raised by Agatha for the past four years, was asking for help deciphering her confusing feelings for Nat.

“Just go fuck her, Wanda. That will be pretty telling,” Agatha said after she finished laughing.

“You are of no help,” Wanda said, getting up to storm off.

“Angel, no, seriously. Do you have any desire to do anything with any of these PR guys you’ve been set up with?” Agatha asked.

Wanda sighed, hands gathered on one hip. “No.”

“What about Nat?”

Wanda looked at the ceiling and blew out a breath.

“I want to do unspeakable things and grow old with her,” she finally admitted.

“Welcome to club lesbian. Your membership card will be in the mail. Go get your girl,” Agatha grinned.

Agatha watched as Wanda climbed the stairs, knowing she was headed to Nat’s room.

If only her own situation were that easy. She was happy to see that these two dear friends would share a sweet moment and maybe more. But their happiness and Wanda’s excitement stirred a hidden pain in her. She and Lilia had been going back and forth for three years now, and things were only getting more confusing.

⭐⭐⭐

2000 New York City

Lilia’s Side of the ‘Do You Love Me’ Night

They had had a nice night, and Lilia, still vibrant and alluring at forty-one years old, had so wished she could be ‘friend’ Lilia to Agatha right then, but those lines were blurring more frequently. She was in too deep, and she knew she should have listened to Lorna four years ago when this had started. Lilia scolded herself. She should have put a stop to this a long time ago. She still should stop it now.

To no one’s surprise, Agatha had grown more beautiful and was intoxicating at twenty-seven. Lilia couldn’t get enough of her. There was no gum or patch for this. No twelve-step program. No rehab.

Agatha and Lorna had tried rehab and failed for their vices. Lilia didn’t even have that option. Here she was, trying to get Agatha just to drink wine and not the myriad of alcohols and liquors she had in the fridge. The kid referred to Smirnoff as ‘alcoholic kool-aid’.

Lilia couldn’t believe Agatha had brought up Madonna. She knew she had done it just to goad her, too. Lilia couldn’t stand Madonna’s acting or singing. Agatha adored her.

It was a good night with their usual banter, the easiness they built up in the past thirteen years, and then it happened.

Agatha had her legs tucked under her. She was effortlessly beautiful and always had been.

“Lilia, do you love me?”

Lilia had stilled, turning towards Agatha, trying to decide how to answer. They had used that term platonically for years. But no one had ever said so romantically.

“Of course I do,” Lilia replied, as she had done so many times before, silently praying Agatha would drop it. Knowing she wouldn’t. Her eyebrows knitted together, preparing what to say next.

“No, I mean, are you in love with me?” Agatha took a deep breath. “It’s just that I am so deeply in love with you. You won’t let me tell anyone we’re seeing each other, while I want to scream it from the rooftops. I want to spend the rest of my life with you, and you laugh it off, insisting we see other people.” Agatha’s eyes were watery. Lilia hated being the one who made her cry.

Agatha was right. Every time she brought up that line of conversation, Lilia pulled back, and they stopped things for a bit.

Every single time, they found their way back into each other’s beds.

Every single time, Lorna got pissed at Lilia.

“Lorna knows,” Lilia replied, hoping it was some small consolation. She so badly wanted to hold Agatha, console her, and kiss away her sadness. But she didn’t reach out, steeling herself against the emotion and guilt that bubbled within.

Agatha gave a small cry of frustration.

“Of course she does! I can’t help that she figured it out! Stop ignoring the question!”

Lilia was surprised by Agatha’s harsh tone. Lorna had suggested she act like she figured it out and not tell Agatha that Lilia had outright told her. Half-truths abounded in their small family.

“Yes, I love you, Agatha, but those things are impossible. I don’t want any of that, and you know it. I will not explain it to you again. Now, don’t go ruining a good night before it’s even begun.”

Lilia reached for Agatha’s cheek as Agatha retreated backward. It was a knife to Lilia’s heart, and she fought back tears herself. All she really wanted to do was apologize for being this way. Yes, she was in love with Agatha, something that was hard to admit to herself, but she was not a one-woman woman and never would be.It truly wasn’t who she was at heart. Knowing that while she could give in and be happy for a little while, her true nature would eventually emerge and ruin both their relationship and the beautiful woman with whom she was in love.

“Leave,” Agatha said abruptly.

“What?” Lilia was shocked. Agatha had never kicked her out before.

“You heard me, leave.” She said, standing up and walking to her bedroom.

Lilia watched Agatha walk down the hall as she got up to leave herself. She and Lorna had a New York apartment they shared, and she would call Lorna once she got back. That would give Agatha time to call her first.

Agatha came to Lilia’s four days later to apologize. Lilia planned to be Lilia, the friend. Forgive Aggie and put an end to everything for good.

But Agatha was water, and Lilia, the lover, was parched in the middle of a desert.

⭐⭐⭐

2003 Los Angeles, Harkimoff HQ

She was only thirty, but she felt so much older than Wanda. Things had been going well for her younger sister, and when the scandal broke as Wanda planned her proposal to Nat, Agatha knew it wouldn’t happen. Agatha’s own ‘relationship,’ if you could call it that, was doomed, and here, Wanda had two people madly in love with her and willing to dedicate their lives to her in any capacity.

The pictures had broken, tabloids and proper newspapers alike displayed them on front pages, and Agatha wanted to burn the whole world down for Wanda.

Is this how Lorna felt about Agatha at thirteen years older? Fiercely protective? Lorna was slowly getting worse but was still a proud mom. The relationship between Lorna and Lilia who was one year her senior, was starting to fissure, and Agatha couldn’t help but wonder if it was her fault. No, it was Lilia. Lilia who was two different people with her, and it was fucking with Agatha and had for going on seven years now.

But right now? Right now, her priority was the younger sobbing redhead in her arms.

“Shhh, Angel. We’re going to figure it out, okay? What do you want me to do? I will gladly commit murder for you. We’ll invest, you can retire and marry Nat. We WILL make it work, okay?”

Agatha gently lifted Wanda’s head and looked into Wanda’s eyes the way Lorna had the many times when Agatha had cried over Lilia.

“I love you, Angel, and I am going to do everything in my power to make things better, kid, okay?”

Wanda nodded, burying her head back into Agatha’s shoulder, effectively wiping snot on her sweatshirt.

“I love you too, Aggie. You really are my sister, you know that?” She said between sobs.

“I know, honey, I know,” Agatha soothed.

Agatha truly believed that you didn’t need blood to make a family, and she had believed that since she was sixteen. Vision had told Wanda they could call off the engagement, and Nat and Vision both supported Wanda no matter what. Good thing, too, because Agatha happened to know an Italian who could make a few phone calls if needed.

It then dawned on her.

“You three hold it down here,” She addressed the two women and Vision. “I need to go look into something.”

Agatha had called Lorna and Lilia and met them at Lilia’s. Lorna and Agatha were never truly sober anymore. Lorna usually had at least taken a pill of some type, and Agatha was always running on a shot of something. Lilia didn’t know what to do with either of them anymore. Agatha stormed in, dropping the newspaper in Lilia’s lap.

“I know the shady shit you do behind the scenes, did you have a hand in this? I told you about Wanda’s plans, and I swear to Lorna if you messed with my sister, not only are we over, but I will murder you right here,” she said menacingly, eyes devoid of any caring feeling.

Lilia gave her a look. “Agatha, I know I can be cold, but I care about those girls too, and I am rooting for them. I would never do that. I didn’t tell anyone, this wasn’t me. We all know that the studios would take a huge hit if America’s STRAIGHT Sweetheart came out as gay too.”

Agatha looked at Lorna. “Do you know anything?”

Lorna shook her head as she lit her pipe, “No, I’ve been keeping an ear out, though. Howard is passing things off to Tony. If Howard knew, he may have hired someone to get proof that Wanda isn’t squeaky clean to push her back into hiding.”

“I will fucking murder Tony,” Agatha turned on her heel to head out the door. Lilia stood and grabbed her hand.

“Agatha, no. It’s not Tony’s fault, and we don’t even know, okay? It may have just been random paparazzi. You’re not throwing your life away. We wanted to kill Evanora and never did. Now sit down.”

Lilia was in caring friend mode and was happy to be there. Agatha flopped in her favorite armchair and started crying.

“I just want to protect her!”

Lorna blew out a puff of smoke and passed the pipe to Agatha.

“It’s what I’ve been trying to do for you for years. Sadly, I can’t protect you from everything or everyone.”

Her eyes shot to Lilia, who looked away. Lorna took the pipe back, lit the bowl, and took another puff. Neither of them were smoking to get high, merely to calm their nerves and maintain a calm demeanor. She offered the pipe to Lilia, who looked at Lorna like she had grown a second head. Lorna shrugged and passed the pipe and lighter back to Agatha.

“You can’t protect them from everything, and it’s one of the many reasons Alice stays with Mom. Speaking of, Mom sent pictures! She basically has Alice’s friend living with them. Cute kid. Here.” Lorna pulled out a stack of pictures, handing them to Lilia. Agatha put the pipe and lighter on the glass coffee table and moved to sit by Lilia to look at the pictures. She laughed as she saw the pictures of Alice and the other fourteen-year-old sitting on inflatable furniture, sticking their tongues out.

“Norma’s house is the United Nations,” Agatha noted as they flipped through the pictures. It was the distraction they needed. Lorna nodded.

“Puerto Rican kid. Rio. They met two years ago. Similar story as you, shitty parents. She spends most of her time at Mom's unless her parents need her for some scam. Mom said she was skin and bone when Alice dragged her home one day after hearing gunshots at her apartment. They move around a lot. Mom hopes she sticks around or her parents disappear and leave the kid. It makes me feel less guilty for only having one kid.” Lorna got up to rummage in the pantry.

“You didn’t need more kids,” Lilia called after her.

“Damn right,” Lorna called back.

“I want kids.” Agatha looked at Lilia.

Lilia shook her head. “I don’t. Besides, that requires you to get sober.”

Agatha’s face fell. “I could get sober if I was motivated enough.” She took out a flask and took a long drink.

Lorna came back from the kitchen with a bag of 3-D Doritos.

“Why does the Wanda thing bother you so much anyway?” She asked Agatha. Lorna knew why, but she needed Lilia to hear it. Agatha glanced at Lilia before moving back to the armchair.

“I want Wanda to be happy. To be with the woman she loves, openly. Have babies. Build a home. All the things I’ll most likely never get to do.” Agatha’s voice had shrunk as she said it.

Lilia had looked away.

Lorna popped a Dorito in her mouth and turned to Lilia. “Maybe we should listen to our friends, hmm?” she whispered.

⭐⭐⭐

April 2006 Los Angeles

Lilia’s Side of the ‘Baby’ Convo

Lilia hated it when Agatha brought up having a baby. It meant she had to don her ice queen persona again. This back-and-forth had been going on for ten years. It happened so frequently that Lilia could barely keep track of which version of herself she was anymore.

“Agatha, I’m too old to have or raise a child, and with your schedule? I would be a primary parent. People would know we were together then.” Agatha was almost thirty-three, and Lilia herself was nearly forty-seven. She was scheduled to have a hysterectomy soo,n and while she regretted never having kids, she was trying to push it out of her mind. She still didn’t want to risk her career. There was talk of the principal retiring, and she was on the shortlist for it.

“You’re right about me. I can’t be a good parent with this life.” Agatha conceded.

“You can’t risk being your mother.”

Lilia wanted to take the words back as soon as she said them and saw the hurt on Agatha’s face. She had gotten so good at being cold when she was this person that it came naturally, and she hated it.

Lilia wondered if she had lost who she once was and their relationship had changed so much. Even when they were being friends, it didn’t come as easily. Agatha’s alcohol use had gotten so bad that Lilia’s main concern was the withdrawals in Agatha’s preparations to have a child. Could her body even handle it? She’d been drinking for so long that she wasn’t sure how she would handle it. Lilia knew that Agatha would be an amazing mother.

Just not with Lilia.

Lilia hadn’t known that Agatha had taken that as Lilia’s final decision. She had gotten good at continuing to act like she was drinking. But, she had stopped last Halloween and had been preparing to start treatments and trying for a baby.

Agatha didn’t need Lilia in order to have or raise a baby.

⭐⭐⭐

September 2006 San Franciso

Agatha had gone to Norma’s for a couple of reasons. She needed to bring Lorna back to LA to continue filming their show, and she needed to tell Lorna that she was pregnant. The fertility treatments and artificial insemination had worked. Agatha was going to be a mom.

She hadn’t told Lilia yet and had no idea how she would react.

Norma opened the door and let her in.

“She’s trying to get sober, Agatha,” She said in place of a greeting.

“I know, and that’s part of why I’m here. I need her to come back and finish filming. I’ll keep an eye on her. I’ll also get her into rehab as soon as we’re done. I’m sober, Norma. If I can do it, so can she.” Agatha explained.

Norma squinted at her. “Are you serious?” Norma didn’t trust her.

“A hundred percent.”

“I’m proud of you. She’s in her room. I hope you can do it.”

Norma stepped aside to let her in. Agatha walked down the familiar hallway lined with pictures of Alice and the other teenage girl. It was late, and she didn’t even knock. Norma was awake, and she looked up from her reading. Agatha set down her overnight bag on the floor before she made Norma scoot over.

“How’s the detox going?” She asked.

“Terribly.” Lorna laid her book down and put her head on Agatha’s shoulder. “I don’t see how you did it.”

“They say alcohol is easier. But, I think I had a great motivator.”

“I have a whole kid and still…” Lorna sighed. “How is operation reproduction going?”

“It worked,” Agatha smiled.

“What? I’m going to be a grandma?” Lorna said excitedly.

“Yeah, so get clean so you can be around for this one and Alice,” Agatha said with tears in her eyes.

Lorna hugged her tightly before loosening her grip and laughing.

“I guess I won’t squeeze baby Harkness too hard.” She looked back at Agatha, concerned. “Have you told her?”

“She made it very clear she didn’t want a baby, Lorna.”

“You should still tell her, you never know what she’ll decide. She might surprise you.”

“I don’t need her criticism,” Agatha chewed on the side of her thumb.

“I know, but at least give her the chance,” Lorna implored.

“I’ll think about it. I’m going to get ready for bed. Do you want me to stay with you tonight?” Agatha knew Lorna would have nightmares at this point in the process.

Lorna looked at her lap and nodded. She was forty-six, but at that moment looked like a vulnerable little girl.

Agatha would do anything she needed.

She grabbed her pajamas and toiletry bag as she left the room and collided with a teenager who was not Alice.

“Who the fuck…” The dark-haired girl started and then noticed who she ran into and her eyes went wide. “Holy fuck, you’re…”

“Agatha Harkness, hi. You must be Rio. Nice to meet you,” she shifted everything to one hand and held out the other. Rio shook her hand, still shell-shocked.

“How?” Rio started again.

“Norma talks to Lorna, and Lorna’s my best friend. I’ve got to get ready for bed though. It was nice to meet you. See you in the morning.” Agatha turned on her heel and walked to the bathroom. She had to pee and could not hold it, she also felt the need to puke.

⭐⭐⭐

Later September 2006 Los Angeles

Agatha was nervous. Wanda and Nat were overseas and they didn’t even know she was pregnant. hey certainly didn’t know she had been in a quasi-relationship with someone for ten years. Lilia was coming over and she was going to tell her about the pregnancy.

“You’re not drinking?” Lilia asked, surprise written all over her face.

“Lilia, I haven’t had a drop of alcohol since last Halloween. I’ve been faking it.” Agatha replied casually.

Lilia was suspicious and confused. “Why?”

“I didn’t want anyone to know I was doing fertility treatments.”

They were sitting in the living room at Agatha’s, she was eating blueberries and drinking water, gauging Lilia’s reactions closely.

Lilia had just taken a sip of tea and nearly choked.

“WHAT?” She yelled. “Isn’t that a discussion?”

“No. I’m my own person, we aren’t anything,  which you’ve made very clear, and I don’t need you to have or raise a child, Lilia.” Agatha popped another blueberry in her mouth.

Lilia looked at Agatha cautiously, “So are you…”

“Pregnant? Yes. Due in May,” Agatha answered before Lilia finished the question. “I don’t expect you to continue this with me, to marry me, to do anything. I know this isn’t what you wanted and I respect that. If that side of this stops, then I do want our friendship back, Lilia,” Agatha laid it out. She wanted Lilia to remain in her life. They didn’t have to keep up whatever they had, but she needed Lilia in her life, in this child’s life.

Lilia stood abruptly. She looked like a fish gasping for air.

“I have to go, I can’t, Agatha, I’m sorry,” And she was gone.

Agatha thought she could handle motherhood solo, and she could handle Lilia saying no. But she couldn’t handle her walking out of her life, and the sobs spilled out of her.



Lilia went to Lorna’s. She was back in LA and was trying her best to be sober, weed they could handle. Lilia sat on the couch, face splotchy from crying.

“God, Lorna. What have I done?” Lilia asked looking at her best friend.

“Been the world’s worst surrogate gay aunt for ten years,” Lorna said, munching on a bag of Chips Ahoy.

“Please stop referring to me as that, it makes it sound so creepy,” Lilia already felt terrible enough.

“What do you want me to say, Lilia? You acted like a bipolar bitch for a decade. I know I’m not a saint, none of us are, but you’ve ruined the kid when it comes to relationships, do you not see that? She is in love with you and tries so hard to hide it. You love her too, in your own infatuation way,, but you’re not the settling-down type and that’s okay. She doesn’t hold it against you as long as she gets to be with you. This whole thing never should have started. AS I SAID. She’s not even asking for you to marry her or help her raise the kid, she just wants you to stay in her life and be her friend again Lilia, yet you’re in too far and you can’t separate the two Lilias anymore.” Lorna looked at her, face hard. “I told you this would happen, and Lilia, all you had to do was listen, but just like always, you think you know best.”

“So sorry that I didn’t take advice from you!” Lilia spat.

Lorna sat straight up in shock. “What’s that supposed to mean?”

“You know what it means,” Lilia’s eyes had grown cold as she walked out of the condo.

⭐⭐⭐

October 2006, Los Angeles

Lilia left SHIELD in September, she hadn’t told anyone, and it was part of the reason she had been such an ass lately. That and her hysterectomy. The loss of children she never had and never would grated on her soul, but she wasn’t saying that out loud to anyone. She regretted what she had said to Lorna, and she didn’t mean it. Not really. She loved Lorna and she would never hold her addiction against her, just like she didn’t hold Agatha’s against her. Lilia was so proud of Agatha for being in recovery, no matter what it had taken.

The pregnancy news shocked her, but Agatha would be a fantastic mother if she could balance it with her career.

Lilia had a tarot reading done weeks earlier. New beginnings, it said. New life.

Maybe she was supposed to take a chance with Agatha.

That’s how she found herself at Agatha’s now, ready to take that leap if Agatha would have her.

Agatha was shocked to see her at the door.

“Lilia? What are you doing here?” Agatha’s voice was full of skepticism.

“May I come in?” Lilia was nervous and lacking the confidence she always had.

Agatha stepped aside as Lilia walked past her. “I’ve been thinking, Agatha, I do love you, and I have, for years. I’m sorry for before, but I am here now.”

Lilia’s voice was sincere and shaking with the weight of the words. She looked at Agatha, Lilia’s eyes were full of years of regret and poor judgment.

Agatha was cautious.

“Why now? I don’t want you to feel obligated, that’s not what I was trying to do.”

“I know it’s not. I just think it’s time I stopped being two different people and chose you. Let’s get married. I want to raise a family with you.”

Lilia kissed her in a way that actually conveyed love and not ownership. Agatha let her carefully built guard down.

A mistake if there ever was one.

“You’re absolutely positive?” Excitement rang in Agatha’s voice.

“Yes baby, yes.” Lilia smiled sincerely. She could do this, she absolutely could.

⭐⭐⭐

November 2006

“I’m so sorry Ms. Harkness, there’s no heartbeat, you have…”

A high-pitched tone replaced the doctor’s voice as she droned on and Agatha felt her chest tighten. She hadn’t even told Lilia she was going to the doctor, afraid of what was happening. Lorna was with her instead. Lorna wrapped her arms around Agatha’s shoulders.

Agatha couldn’t even cry. She just felt hollow. Her precious baby. Her child.

“Now, after the fetus passes and there’s an exam, it may take a few months…

Agatha knew Lorna was listening for her, thankfully. Something about how long it would be before cycles returned and she could try again. Fertility and success percentages.

It wasn’t something she was concerned about right now. Right now she was concerned about her body failing her. It had one basic job and it had failed. Or had she failed it?

“This is rather common, 1 in 4…”

Her doctor was trying to comfort her with statistics and Agatha just wanted to scream.

Would Lilia still marry her? Why was Agatha even having those thoughts? Surely she would. Lilia loved her, right?

The doctor finally handed a card to Lorna before putting her hand on Agatha’s shoulder.

“If the fetus doesn’t pass in about a week, give us a call, I am so sorry Ms. Harkness,” with that, she left the room.

Agatha looked at Lorna, she knew she had been crying silently, as Lorna handed her tissues.

“Oh sweetie, I am so sorry. I love you so much. What do you need?” Lorna took it upon herself to wipe Agatha’s face.

“Just take me home?” She asked meekly. She didn’t want to be here in this gown anymore, she just wanted to be home, in her bed.

“Of course, Aggie, whatever you want.”

When Agatha told Lilia, she didn’t react, just nodded. They were to be married in three days on the beach. She craved Lilia’s love and warmth. Agatha had lost a baby but all Lilia could do was nod.

It took three hours before Lilia finally wrapped her arms around Agatha and cried.

Wedding Day

Lilia wasn’t going to show up. It was apparent now. Lorna’s phone rang and she answered it. It was Lilia. She said, “Tell her I’m sorry,” before the line went dead.

Lorna turned to Agatha, sorrow in her eyes.

Agatha fell to her knees, sobbing.

She lost her entire future in three days.

Lorna walked her up to the beach house and helped her get changed. That day, Agatha drank and cried herself to sleep. It was the first drink that crossed her lips in over a year. Lorna would stay and make certain Agatha remained safe. Her maternal instincts, repressed for so long, were on heightened alert. When she went off to get Agatha some water to help flush her system, she returned to a scene that broke her heart for her pseudo daughter for the second time that day. Agatha was finally asleep, her tri-goddess locket in her hand. On the floor lay a small lock of hair. Lorna didn’t have to look closely to understand what had happened. Agatha had removed Lilia from her life.

Once Lorna was certain Agatha was safely asleep, she drove over to Lilia’s and stormed through the door.

She slapped Lilia the minute she saw her.

“HOW COULD YOU!?” She screamed. Lorna saw red.

“I can’t be tied down, I just, I can’t Lorna.” Lilia rubbed her cheek. It stung, but she deserved it.

“You have to talk to her, she deserves that much. She thinks you left her because she miscarried.” Lorna was so mad she could spit nails.

“I think the baby clouded my judgment…” She told Lorna about the reading.

“YOU AGREED TO GET MARRIED BASED ON TAROT?!” Murder was the only thing on Lorna’s mind.

“I knew you wouldn’t understand. I think I misunderstood the new beginnings card. I was offered the principal position at SHIELD, and I think that’s where I need to be. I also can’t do that while with Agatha. Neither of us would truly be happy. She needs someone closer to her age, or younger. I would eventually be unfaithful, we both know that. She needs someone who can compliment her lifestyle and career, someone who hasn’t fucked with her for ten years. Someone who doesn’t tell half-truths, who loves her fiercely. None of that is me, Lorna.” Lilia tried to defend herself and knew she was failing.

“You basically fucked with my daughter, Lilia,” Lorna spat.

“I don’t get you, Lorna! You treat her like this daughter you never had and let her come between us. But you HAVE a daughter that you NEVER see. Agatha is only thirteen years younger than you, and you’re closer to her than your own flesh and blood. Why is that? Why do you choose her over Alice? Over everyone?” Lilia was furious.

“Because we saw what growing up in this world did to Agatha. You know what having famous parents has done to those brats you teach. How entitled and spoiled they are. Alice is none of those things. Alice is grounded. Alice saw a kid in trouble and acted like we did, Lilia. Your brats wouldn’t do that. Agatha was an alcoholic before she was ten, just like other kids in this town. Do you think I want Alice around me? No. She doesn’t need to see me like this! Alice has a better chance in life because of my choices.

“If I can shape Agatha at all, I’m going to fucking do it, and God knows I’m trying. You were trying too until you decided to fuck her and ended up breaking her, and she is broken, Lilia. She is one of the strongest women I know and I don’t know if I can pick up the pieces this time after what you’ve done.”

Lorna had never spoken to Lilia this way. They had never spoken to each other this harshly and they had never fought like this. Not over Lorna’s demons, not over other disagreements, nothing.

Only over Agatha.

Lilia’s voice shrunk. She knew she was in the wrong. She had become a monster and she had destroyed thiswoman with so much life ahead of her.

“I never meant for any of this to happen. I had to separate myself into two different people. I want to console her and tell her everything is okay, yet I’m the one that did this. I lost one of the people closest to me.” Lilia sobbed.

“Two people,” Lorna’s voice was cold and hard. “I can’t do this with you anymore, Lilia.” With that, she left.

It was one of the last times Lilia saw Lorna alive.

⭐⭐⭐

June 12, 2008 Cedars-Sinai Hospital Los Angeles

Despite the labor pains, Agatha’s excitement still showed through for those initial centimeters. The baby boy Harkness would be here soon.

But, now. Right now she was very much not amused at this process. Oh, god, it hurt.

Lorna was on one side of her, Wanda and Nat on the other.

“I hate all of you!” She screamed as they gave her instructions on how to breathe.

“Sweetie, you did this to yourself,” Lorna reminded her.

“Well, right now, I blame you three.” She said through gritted teeth. Her doctor chuckled.

“I take it you're ready for the epidural, Ms. Harkness? Last chance.” Dr. Munsie offered.

“Nope. I got it doc.” Agatha was about to break Wanda’s hand, but she was going to prove the ghost of her mother wrong. She was not weak.

“Having an epidural doesn’t make you weak, sweetie. It helps you relax and means Wanda leaves here with her hand intact, which Nat will appreciate…one day.” Lorna affirmed and winked at Wanda and Nat.

Agatha chose the epidural, which also did wonders for her anxiety.

Nicholas Scratch Harkness was born at 10:14 PM PST. He came into the world crying and perfect.

Agatha chose Scratch because that’s how he was made—from scratch, with love. Also, Nicholas Scratch was a nickname for the devil, and the kid was of her. She wasn’t exactly a saint.

Lorna was the one who sent Lilia the text. It had been very to the point.

Cedars-Sinai.

RM 220

She had the baby.

Get your ass up here.

Lilia visited twelve hours after Nicky was born. Wanda and Nat were delighted to see her. hey had missed her coming around. She blamed work. They were trying to put more effort into the athletics department and it was taking a lot of time to find quality coaches, and she was starting to scout college players.

Lorna suggested to the two youngest women that they go in search of food, letting Lilia and Agatha have time alone with Nicky.

“Why are you here, Lilia? I accepted your apology. You are off the hook.” Agatha said dryly, holding a sleeping Nicky. He had been fed, burped, and changed, and was now sleeping peacefully. Lorna had been the first one to call him Nicky minutes after he was named.

Lilia had told Agatha the same thing she had told Lorna, when Agatha finally agreed to talk to her again. Lilia was honest, even including that the pregnancy had clouded her judgment and that she still loved her, but not in the way that justified marriage.

It crushed Agatha for the hundredth time, breaking what was left of her heart.

Agatha swore off relationships for the rest of her life. She would never allow herself to be hurt like that again, and now she had to think of Nicky.

She looked at Lilia, wanting to stab her with a scalpel.

“Lorna texted me. Told me to get my ass up here. I wanted to come, Agatha. I still care about you. You’re still you and I’m still me,” Lilia tried.

Agatha shook her head.

“You killed that relationship long ago, Lilia,” She was trying not to cry and she didn’t want to wake Nicky. “You should go.”

“He’s perfect, just like you.” Lilia turned and left, stopping at the door. “Cute name by the way. You always were a little devil,” she left, running into the other three on her way out.

Lorna grabbed Lilia’s arm. “Wait.”

She looked at the other two women. “You two take the food up to Agatha, I’ll be right there.” She watched them go and turned back to Lilia.

“I’m sober. I thought you should know.” Lorna let go and walked away.

⭐⭐⭐

January 2009 San Francisco

Agatha had left Nicky with Wanda, Vision, and Nat. She was back in San Francisco to bring Lorna back for filming. The story was that she had gone to Norma’s to get clean.

It was a lie.

“How’s she doing?” Agatha asked Norma, sitting in the kitchen before going to Lorna’s room to talk with her.

“The girls are visiting, so I’m not sure if that’s helping or hurting, but she’s not doing great, as expected,” Norma looked stressed and Agatha didn’t blame her. She was worried as well.

“I’ll go talk to her then.”

She stood and went to Lorna like she had so many times before. She didn’t bother knocking. She just walked in like she owned the place and sat at the foot of the bed.

“We’re going to beat this.” Agatha wasn’t sure they would, but it was her turn to be strong for Lorna.

It didn’t matter what Lorna had been diagnosed with. What mattered was that for all of her years battling addiction, it was something unrelated that had her health declining.

Lorna’s eyes were puffy, “Aggie, I’m about to be forty-nine and I’m dying.”

“You’re not dying,” Agatha said firmly. “We’re fighting this.”

“Agatha, you heard what they said.”

“So did you, and there’s a lot that can be done. We’re fighting this thing together. Alice and I can’t lose you,” Agatha was not going to cry.

Lorna looked at Agatha sadly, “I think this movie will be my last.”

“Only because you’re going to retire,” Agatha was trying to remain hopeful.

Agatha emerged from Lorna’s room, eyes puffy and red. She was headed to the kitchen when once again, she ran into Rio. She had slightly matured but still looked like the teenage girl that she was.

“We must stop meeting like this,” Agatha said, her throat was raw and her voice was hoarse.

“Hi Ms. Harkness,” Was all Rio could manage as she blushed.

Agatha smiled. This kid lived with Alice Wu-Gulliver, yet she was starstruck around Agatha. It was cute. “Hey, Rio.”

Rio was stunned, “You remember my name?”

“Sure do.” Agatha noticed Rio get flustered. “Have a good night kid, I need water,” Agatha winked at the young girl and walked past her. She was exhausted but never got tired of people fawning over her. She didn’t know that the next time she saw Rio would be at Lorna’s funeral.

⭐⭐⭐

October 2010 Los Angeles

Lorna stood in front of Lilia’s house. She had to do this. She was dying and she was going to soon. But do it on her terms. But first, she had to talk to Lilia. They wouldn’t be able to go back to how they were, but she at least had to talk to her. Lorna took a deep breath and knocked on the door. Lilia looked like a ghost was standing before her.

“You look like shit. I thought you were sober?” She said it as a question, stepping aside and helping Lorna in.

“Different types of drugs, these are prescribed,” Lorna half laughed, as she took a seat. “I’ll cut to the chase. I’m dying and I want to say I’m sorry for the way I said what I said,” Lorna chewed on her lip, not meeting Lilia’s eyes.

“You’re WHAT?” That was all Lilia could ask. Lilia was shocked.

“Yeah, this is a wig, the press and media, no one knows.” Lorna looked away. “I don’t want to die with things still shitty between us. I don’t want Aggie to think it’s her fault.”

Lilia’s face fell. Right, of course, this was for Agatha.

“So, this is more for Agatha, not so I don’t live with guilt for the rest of my life,” Lilia said slowly.

Lorna sighed.

“She will never be the same. Ever. She throws everything she has into that beautiful boy of hers, into Wanda’s babies. Wanda had twin boys, by the way. The three of them are going to be terrors,” Lorna had a wistful smile, how badly she wanted to watch the three of them grow up. “She’s promised to watch over Alice. To try and get Alice here, to you. To SHIELD.” Lorna looked at Lilia hopefully.

“What? You want Alice at SHIELD? Lilia repeated.

“I may still blame you for destroying Agatha, but I know Alice will be in good hands there. I need eyes on Alice, Mom won’t be around forever and I need people around her, to tell of the person I really was. She didn’t get to know me and that’s my fault. I just need people who will love her in the way I would. You also have to watch over those boys Lilia,” Lorna was staring into Lilia’s soul.

“Agatha isn’t going to let me do that, Lorna,”

“Yes, she will. If you tell her that I wanted you too, she will.” They both knew that Lorna was right.

“So keep manipulating her? Didn’t we do that enough when she was younger?”

Lilia had wondered over the past years just how much damage they had both done to Agatha.

“We guided her Lilia. There’s a difference,” Lorna looked like she was trying to convince herself more than Lilia.

“How long?” Lilia whispered.

Lorna knew what she was referencing. “A few months. I’m hoping to live long enough to win the Oscar.”

“Why did you wait so long to come to apologize?” Lilia asked casually.

Lorna’s head shot up. Lilia had struck a nerve. “Because I’m not the one who was in the wrong, Lilia. I’m not the one who broke Agatha. I’m not the one who caused the damage. I came to clear my conscience, yes, but you should have come to me long ago,” Lorna sighed as she got up. “I can die in peace now. This is yours to live with now. Goodbye, Lilia.”

Lorna walked out the door.

It was the last time Lilia saw Lorna alive.

⭐⭐⭐

February 27, 2011, Los Angeles

Oscars Night

After winning an Oscar, Lorna had insisted on going to an after-party. Agatha had wanted to take Lorna home. She wasn’t doing well at all and Agatha knew the end was near, something in her gut told her it was. Lorna hadn’t told anyone about her diagnosis. Only Agatha, Lilia, and Norma knew. She allowed everyone to think she had relapsed. The way she looked certainly supported that narrative.

She didn’t want anyone’s sympathy, didn’t want anyone to think of her as a sob story that had beaten her addiction only to be taken by some cruel twist of fate. So she controlled the narrative. The Witches’ Road was a dark movie. She said it had consumed her, taken a toll on her psychologically that she couldn’t deal with alone.

Agatha hated it.

Lilia was nowhere to be found.

Wanda, and everyone else, believed it.

When Lorna collapsed at the party and 911 was called, Agatha knew it was the end.

She knew Lorna wasn’t going to make it to the hospital.

Lorna made her promise to watch after Alice.

Agatha rode in the ambulance.

Lorna was pronounced dead before they even reached the hospital.

Lorna had always said she would go out on her own terms, and she had.

It didn’t matter what she took. But she had.

Agatha wasn’t angry with her.

Agatha knew Lorna didn’t want to suffer in the final days.

They knew it was stage 4.

They knew it was incurable.

Inoperable.

Spread throughout her entire body.

Afterward, she found that Lorna had left letters. A journal. An envelope of documents.

Agatha kept the letters addressed to her and would give everything else to Alice.

⭐⭐⭐

March 4th, 2011 San Francisco

The Funeral

Norma had closed the funeral to celebrities. The media said it was because she was angry and blamed Hollywood for Lorna’s death.

In reality, it was because she didn’t want it to turn into a media circus.

She knew Agatha and Wanda would be there in disguise. She had asked them to do so. Alice and Rio didn’t know that. A few other celebrities had done it that way.

Lilia had disguised herself as well.

Agatha, Wanda, and Nat hugged Norma out of sight in a back hallway. Wanda and Nat offered their condolences before slipping into the main area, leaving Agatha and Norma alone.

“Norma, I…” Agatha’s voice betrayed her as she started to cry.

“Agatha, it’s not your fault. You can’t heal the sick,” Norma looked at Agatha the way Lorna did, causing Agatha to cry harder.

“You shouldn’t have to bury your child,” Agatha admonished in between her crying.

Norma was crying herself as she looked at her tenderly. “Once Lorna fell into this cursed lifestyle, I knew I would outlive her. I started having some hope when she was pregnant and sober, but then I found myself raising my grandchild. When Lorna finally achieved sobriety, I thought maybe she would be a parent to Alice and outlive me. Fate is a cruel mistress. I hold nothing against you, Agatha, and neither does Alice. You’ll be able to make it up to her in due time, and I trust you will. Right now, you need to grieve. I grieved my daughter long ago, and while I am grieving, I always knew that this was the most likely outcome. I used to think so about you, too. You proved me wrong. Now, go talk with Alice,” Norma hugged her. Though Norma was crying, but had much more composure than Agatha did. Norma turned to head back into the main room before pausing at the door and turning her head slightly.

“Lilia is here.”

With that, Norma pushed through the door and disappeared.

Agatha and Wanda waited until after the funeral to pull Alice aside.

“This is from your mother.” Agatha handed her the large manilla envelope. “There’s a lot in here she wanted you to have. You also need to read this,” Agatha handed her a letter. “You’re going to have a lot of questions kid. You need to call me. I know you graduate this year and there’s a job at a school in LA if you want it…”

Alice cut her off, “Why would I go there, Agatha? I saw what Hollywood did to my mom.” Alice’s eyes were dark, haunted. “It took her away from me, Agatha. I only saw her on holidays and when she tried to get clean.”

Alice’s words stabbed at Agatha, releasing guilt that had built up for years. Hollywood hadn’t taken Lorna away from Alice, Agatha had. Agatha was sixteen when Alice was born, and Lorna had been putting everything she had into making sure Agatha was safe, that Agatha was okay. That Agatha had a mother figure.

When Lorna had found out she was pregnant, Agatha and Lilia insisted she keep the baby.

Lorna took Alice to Norma, “Mom, I can’t possibly care for an infant in this environment. She needs a Norma, not a Lorna.”

Lilia and Agatha had tried to convince Lorna to raise Alice with their help, but Lorna refused. She was adamant that Alice grow up far away from the life they led.

Agatha knew Alice was better for it, but it didn’t mean she had any less guilt.

“Just read the letter, please. I don’t know what it says, but it was important to your mom,” Agatha hugged her and left. Wanda and Nat followed behind.

As they left, Agatha saw Lilia out of the corner of her eye. “You two go ahead, I’ll be right there.”

Wanda shrugged and she and Nat went to the car as Agatha turned and walked to Lilia.

“I didn’t know if you’d come,” Agatha said softly. She expected to be angry, but couldn’t find the energy.

“Of course I did,” Lilia stared straight ahead.

“I’m glad you came, Lorna told me…” Agatha couldn’t finish the sentence.

Lilia looked down. “How did it happen?”

“Her own terms. In my arms. We made it to the back of the ambulance,” Agatha swallowed hard. It would forever be a core memory for her.

“Dramatic as always.”

Agatha chuckled at that, “Lifetime Achievement Award just for that.”

“Agatha,” Lilia turned to look at her.

“No.” Agatha said firmly as she turned and walked out of the door.

⭐⭐⭐

March 6th, 2011 Los Angeles

Agatha was on the phone with Alice, who was very confused.

“This whole time and you’ve been my godmother?” Alice was shocked.

“Yes. I was sixteen and your mother thought it was a good idea to potentially leave you with me. I was stunned.” Agatha laughed. It was good to talk to Alice.

“And she really had been sober…” Alice was proud of Lorna and finally understood her decision at the end.

“Yes, I was so proud of her,” Agatha echoed Alice’s earlier sentiment.

“I don’t hate you, Agatha. Mom explained why she made the decisions she did. Gran has raised me in a way that I don’t think Mom was capable of. She also talked about some of the men she was with and no thank you. She basically gave me this little alternative family.”

“I appreciate that, though anytime you need anything, I’ll be there. I’ll always feel like I took your mom away from you. The men were terrible, though, and I’m glad you found your chosen family, too.”

“I don’t think I’m ready to move out there yet. Gran needs me.” Alice wasn’t ready to leave Gran, and it was too soon to enter her mother’s old world.

“Whenever you're ready, we’re all here for you.”

⭐⭐⭐

July 2012 SHIELD

Nicky would be in Pre-K that year, and SHIELD was the only institution Agatha trusted, whether she wanted to or not. Lilia of all people was droning on and on as she showed her around. They were doing a pretty decent job at pretending like they didn’t know each other. Agatha couldn’t take it anymore, and they were in a deserted hallway in the elementary building.

“Lilia, cut the bullshit,” Agatha stopped walking and leaned against a wall. Her heart still ached from the loss of Lorna, and Lilia reminded her of what she had lost. “You’re the only person I trust to oversee Nicky in a sea of children.”

Lilia's eyebrows shot up. “You trust me?”

“With my son, Lilia. Not with me. There’s a difference.”

Agatha knew Lilia was great with kids and she knew that Lilia would protect Nicky with everything she had. She would treat Wanda’s boys with a side of favoritism as well. Agatha herself was a different story.  Lilia looked away.

“Got it. You know I’ll take care of him, Agatha. As if he were mine.”

“He could have been,” Agatha turned and started back to the office to finish enrollment.

“Aggie, wait.”

Agatha froze but didn’t turn around. “You don’t get to call me that anymore.”

“I’m sorry,” Lilia said sincerely. “Did you actually change your middle name, legally?”

“Yes,” Agatha was tense and refused to turn back.

“Is it actually ‘Fucking’?”

“That’s what the rags and some of my documents say,” Agatha smirked to herself.

“What did you really change it to?”

“I’ll give you three guesses and the first two don’t count,” Agatha started walking again.

“She’d be honored,” Lilia called after her, holding back tears.

⭐⭐⭐

April 2014, San Francisco

Norma’s Funeral

Agatha had disguised herself at Norma’s funeral, Alice didn’t need to deal with anyone knowing that Agatha was there. Agatha wanted to be there for Alice, she needed someone. Agatha and Alice had kept in touch over the past three years, unbeknownst to almost everyone.

“Psst, kid.” Agatha stepped up next to Alice. She was wearing a blonde wig and oversized sunglasses.

“You look ridiculous Agatha,” Alice whispered.

“I look amazing, how are you holding up?” Agatha looked her over. She looked tired, but otherwise, not awful.

“I’ve been better. Rio’s been amazing. She helped so much in the last several months. Best sister I could ask for.”

“I’m glad you two have each other. Have you thought about my suggestion?” Agatha had yet again suggested she call Lilia.

Alice nodded. “I don’t want to leave Rio, but I think it’s time.”

“Good. Let me know what you need. I’m hiring a new right-hand girl. She can help you get set up in LA.” Agatha squeezed Alice by the shoulders. “Welcome to the family, kid. We’re fun.”

⭐⭐⭐

The rest of 2014 - Early 2024

Let’s speed this up, shall we?

Lilia had only professional interactions with Agatha. That is to say, only in her professional capacity, although she took every one of them personally. Agatha was always a fierce competitor, and as a mother, she was unbeatable. Every time Nicky found himself in trouble, there was Agatha, convincing Lilia to let him off the hook. She knew Agatha thought it was because of her powerful personality, but  Lilia had never overcome the guilt and sadness of the losses of the most important women in her life. She let it color her interactions with Agatha and allowed the starlet to get away with anything. Even when against school policy or when it dealt with Wanda's twin boys.

Agatha always treated Lilia with a side of cynicism, but her anger lost its edge the day that Lilia showed up to the house with a bag of sick-day material.

Lilia was at Agatha’s house, Nicky was a freshman, fourteen, and had mono. She came to make him soup like she had done for Agatha when she had caught mono from another actress when she was sixteen.

Agatha opened the door and her eyes went wide seeing Lilia with the oldest pot on the planet.

“Is the tetanus free?” She asked once she had recovered.

“Oh shut up and move over Harkness.” She pushed past Agatha and easily found her way into the kitchen.

“Lilia, what the hell are you doing?” Agatha followed her.

“Do you remember when you had mono?” Lilia asked, placing the pot of soup on the stove and turning on the burner.

Agatha smiled at the memory. “Unfortunately. You made me some Italian soup from scratch in that same pot, which I swear made me feel so much better. You also had some type of tea, and homemade bread, made me watch The Wizard of Oz, and sleep,” Agatha recalled.

“All of that, except the sleep, is in that bag,” Lilia gestured to a bag on the counter.

Agatha pulled everything out of the bag and smiled, yet she was confused. They didn’t have this type of relationship and hadn’t in a long time.

“Why are you doing this Lilia?” Agatha leaned against the kitchen counter and crossed her arms over her chest.

Lilia stirred the soup as it heated, turning her head to look at Agatha.

“You were always terrible at taking care of the sick, and Agatha, I do care about him, and you too, I know things may never be the same between us, but if I can occasionally do something for him, and maybe even you, I’m going to. So excuse me while I finish this magical soup for the kid,” Lilia fixed Nicky's soup, bread, and tea, and pushed Agatha to take it up to Nicky. He was surprised to see real soup, not something out of the can, and ate eagerly.

Agatha sighed. If she was being honest with herself, she missed Lilia, the old Lilia. Her friend and the woman that would take care of her when she was sick, or teach her how to cook. The woman who was caring for her son, that was the Lilia she missed. Not the woman whose memory was tattooed on her hip. No, that was a different woman entirely, wasn’t it?



By early 2024, Agatha and Lilia were on even ground with each other. Nicky was calming down. He had earned the starting quarterback position the previous fall after the previous quarterback was injured. He knew that in order to remain the quarterback for the varsity squad, he had to keep any and all pranks to a harmless minimum. He had also started seeing Morgan Stark, someone he had known since he was four, in a much different light. He didn’t want to lose his chance in any of these respects, so he adjusted his behavior accordingly. Now, Lilia had fewer reasons to call Agatha to deal with her son, and Agatha had fewer reasons to show her sharp tongue. It was a new equilibrium. It wasn’t much. But, it was a start.

⭐⭐⭐

March 2024. Los Angeles
