Alice abruptly hung up the phone as she got up from the couch and headed towards the door, grabbing her bag. “I have to go to San Francisco, I’ll be back in a few days. It’s an emergency,” she said over her shoulder at the other four members of the coven.

Agatha looked alarmed. “Is it a Nat emergency? I’ll send her with you.”

“No. It’s Rio, I’ll be back.”

She was out the door and on the phone with Lilia.

Alice made it to San Francisco in record time. Rio looked like shit. Alice was just thankful she was alive.

“I’ll kill the fucking bitch.” Alice saw red and she was looking around for any sharp object she could find.

“Alice, no. Lou didn’t do this, Shane did. She would never hurt me, she loves me.” Rio slurred from the pain medication.

“Therapy. You need therapy.” Alice was pacing. “You’re moving to LA, my boss is coming out here tomorrow. Just do a small interview, please?” Alice pleaded.

She had tried to get Rio to SHIELD for years, and now it was the only way to make sure she was completely safe.

Rio let out a defeated sigh. “I’ll talk to her okay?”



Rio Vidal would be the perfect addition to SHIELD. But she was very much a broken woman, physically and emotionally. It didn’t take a psychic to see that. It reminded Lilia of a memory she had buried from long ago. No one should be hurt like that without a chance to heal. Somehow, she had to give Rio just that chance. Alice would be key to that. So would the little family that Agatha had pulled Alice into. Maybe Rio could also fill part of that gaping wound she had opened in Agatha? But that was one stubborn fifty-year-old woman. Maybe Rio would be up to that challenge or distraction, too? Perhaps with enough work, there could be room again for Lilia in their lives. It wouldn’t be what it once was, but to have Aggie back in her life would be enough.





Chapter End Notes

Reaction chapter on Saturday (hopefully)!

This was a hard one to write. I always knew that Lilia had turned herself into the villain in Agatha's story, though she never intended for that to happen, but damn.

Also, LORNA?! God.

Damnit Lorna.
For those of you that don't remember Agatha and Rio meeting before, Chapter 3 and 5 reference it, though it's vague.
Now we know why Agatha made sure the boys went to therapy as kids. Well-adjusted humans with healthy coping mechanisms from the start.

Does she still drink? Sure. Does she sometimes drink too much? Maybe.

Is she in control now?
Absolutely.

Somethin' Happens When Everybody Finds Out

Chapter Summary

Rio finds out.
Wanda finds out.
Agatha and Lilia face each other.

Chapter Notes

I'm sure there are trigger warnings, but honestly, you know what you're walking into now.

For those of you that follow me on socials, you know that this past weekend was rough and I had to put this chapter on hold. Thank you so much for being patient and understanding!

Raven, my cat who is Rio's cat in the story, crossed the Rainbow Bridge on the last day of February. She was at least 18 and I had her for 16 years. We went through A LOT together (I was a wee baby at 23 - I'll be 39 at the end of this month). I'm so sorry this chapter is late!

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Present Day

Lilia was Agatha’s ex.

Rio felt as though the walls of the familiar venue were closing in on her. She looked around frantically. She couldn’t fall apart here, not on her sister’s big day. She would never do this to Alice. She had to get out and get out now. Rio slipped into a back hall, thankful that she had driven. Agatha could call her driver. Rio didn’t care. Seriously, fuck Agatha. No wonder Agatha hadn’t wanted to tell her. Lilia?

Rio needed to talk to Alice, but she couldn’t.

Rio had so many questions for Agatha.

Rio had so many questions for Lilia.

Rio needed an Ouija board.

Rio didn’t know what to do.

She stumbled through the back hallways towards the parking garage, stopping to empty the contents of her stomach. She was reeling from the revelation.

She knew that Agatha and Lilia had known each other for a long time.

Since Agatha had been fourteen.

She thought back to The Harkness Brag Room. Why weren’t there more pictures with Lilia in that room?

She retched yet again on the concrete, leaning against an unfamiliar vehicle. Unfortunately, it wasn’t Lilia’s.

Snippets of previous conversations sprung to her mind as she climbed into her car.

“I was young, twenty-three, and I had chased her for years…”

Rio let out a feral scream. Rio remembered when she stood in Lilia’s office to ask if she could experiment with her chemistry class using open flames and different chemicals. Lilia had, of course, agreed. But, as she watched Lilia consider her request, Rio had noticed several pictures around the office, including one of Lilia, Lorna Wu, and Agatha on the opening night of Rent.

Yet, in every picture Agatha had from that night, Lilia was missing.

She drove away from the venue toward Agatha’s house to collect Raven. They couldn’t stay there right now. The urge to snoop and find evidence of Lilia being Swords crept in. Rio quickly dismissed it. She had all the evidence she needed.

“I call her Swords.”

Rio recalled seeing the deck of tarot cards in Lilia’s office. Lilia also had a few signed Playbills and posters hanging around her office. She had several of Agatha’s, mainly from 2006 and earlier, which fit with the timeline that formed in Rio’s mind.

She grew more conflicted by the second.

Rio needed her emotional support Raven.

Then she needed to return to her apartment and plan which of these Hollywood cougars to confront first.

Rio was angry.

Rio was disgusted.

Rio was hurt.

⭐⭐⭐

It had been thirty minutes since Agatha had last seen Rio. One minute, Rio was standing and observing everything from the side, taking it all in. Agatha looked over and winked at her, eliciting a gorgeous smile. Rio, in that orange dress that Alice had picked out with her. Rio’s hair had gotten longer in the past few months, and she kept tossing it over her shoulders. Rio, with her brown eyes that expressed everything and saw into the depths of Agatha’s soul. Rio, the woman who had absolutely shown Agatha what love was truly supposed to be, even if Rio’s own history caused doubts. They had been able to work through them all - together and in such a short time. Weddings had never made Agatha wistful or weepy, but now? With Rio? She was starting to think of a future that she had previously thought impossible.

And it no longer terrified her.

But at this moment, Agatha could not locate Rio. Agatha’s eyes scanned the area for any sign of her and saw none. She took a mental headcount of the coven and children, four sapphics, two adult queer men, six teens, herself, no Rio. She pulled out her phone and called, straight to voicemail. Agatha began to worry but didn’t want to alarm Alice. The psychotic blonde had been sent to The Island, never to be heard from again, so she didn’t need to worry about that. She locked eyes with Wanda as she headed toward the redheads.

Wanda saw the alarm in Agatha’s eyes, no matter how hard she was trying to hide it. “Aggie, what’s wrong?” She asked, meeting her in the middle.

“Have you seen Rio? Her phone is going straight to voicemail.” Agatha was trying to appear calm, though Wanda saw right through it. Wanda looked over at Nat, who had her phone out.

“I’m tracking her now. It's a good thing she never takes that necklace off.” Nat looked up at both of them. “Oh, I can track the necklaces whether the buttons are pushed or not. Emergency feature. Seems Rio already left. It shows she’s headed back to the compound.” She looked at Agatha, clearly confused.

Wanda gave Agatha a look. “What did you do this time??”

Agatha threw her hands up. “I didn’t do anything, I promise.”

“Maybe the festivities led her to have doubts again?” Wanda wondered out loud.

Agatha shook her head. “No, I think we’re okay there. The only thing I’ve kept back is ‘the ex’s’ identity. But, I haven’t told any of you that.”

Agatha threw her hands up in the air in frustration.

“Maybe something just happened to her. How did this end up as my fault? Stop giving me that look. Your face is going to freeze like that.”

Agatha pulled out her phone when it dinged with a text from Rio.

Rio💚

Hey, started feeling sick and

didn’t want to worry Alice.

Grabbing Raven from your

house and heading home.

Don’t want to get you or

Nicky sick

Agatha waved her phone at Wanda and Nat. “See, what did I tell you?”

Wanda raised an eyebrow. “Do you believe her?”

Agatha bit her lip and shook her head. “No, but I’m about to be the best girlfriend ever. Tell Alice and Jen I love them, and take my kid home, please. I need to go take care of a sick Rio.”

⭐⭐⭐

Rio had gotten back to the apartment and found a spiral notebook. She knew she couldn’t just march over to Lilia’s without being entirely sure that it was Lilia. The Swords that Agatha talked about was cold and distant. A heartless bitch that didn’t love Agatha and had left her at the altar.

The Lilia Calderu she knew was manipulative and cryptic, sure, but she had also let Rio off on late notice to go gallivanting around the world with Agatha. She had given her insight into Agatha and noticed when she was anxious.

Frantically, she wrote down everything she had thought of while in the car. Starting with when she met Lilia a year ago. She knew she had to sort out her thoughts before confronting either of the older women.

March 2024 - San Francisco

AWG [Alice] visits RV [Rio] in the hospital and tells RV to come to LA to SHIELD.

LC [Lilia] visits RV and offers RV position on AWG’s request. LC does conduct a quick interview. Explains that she understands needing to start over and the importance of being near family, blood or otherwise.

Signs LC is Swords

LC is 65 - 14 years older than Agatha (AH)

  • Knows a significant amount about AH
  • Favoritism towards coven
  • Very protective of the coven
  • Friends with Lorna?
  • At SHIELD forever
  • Won all major bets - insider knowledge?
  • Helped Cher write Believe
  • Says ‘kid’
  • Broadway obsession
  • Loved Wicked (AH the original Elphaba and a cameo in the movie)
  • Rent (AH the original Maureen)
  • Extra protective of Nicky and Wanda’s boys
  • Warned me about guarding certain secrets - Wanda came to me shortly after
  • Tarot

Rio stopped writing and thought back on different interactions and what she knew. Lilia being Swords would explain why she had such a soft spot towards Nicky, but so would Lilia and Agatha being close.

Lilia and Agatha didn’t act like they had been close for thirty-seven years. They didn’t act like bitter exes, either.

It was some weird middle-ground.

That also explained why no one else knew. Leave it to Agatha to not want her past to cloud other people's judgment of someone.

So WHY had Lilia pushed Rio towards Agatha? She needed to talk to Lilia. Lilia had emotionally scarred Agatha and ruined her.

This same woman had helped Rio find a home and allowed her to coach again, knowing it would bring her joy.

Had she done that out of loyalty to Alice, because of her last name?

What in the holy hell was happening? It was eight on Saturday night, and the reception had been wrapping up, so hopefully, Lilia would be home by now.

Rio grabbed her keys and headed to Lilia’s.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha had soup, crackers, Gatorade, and everything she needed in case Rio had actually fallen sick. If she had had time, she would make soup herself. It was something Lilia had taught her how to do that week Nicky had mono.

The week that an unspoken truce had been made between them.

Agatha even had a giant stock pot now that would one day also be ancient and possibly come with a side of tetanus.

If you’re lucky, Lilia had said, shaking her head at Agatha’s sarcasm.

Agatha had even picked up treats for Raven and flowers for Rio. Agatha commended herself for getting better at this girlfriend thing. She was hoping she would still have a girlfriend in a few hours. Agatha was still in her suit from the reception as she knocked on Rio’s door and waited.

Nothing. Just as she feared.

“Maybe she’s asleep.” Agatha thought aloud. She used her key and let herself inside. “Baby? It’s just me. I brought you soup! From the place you like down on the corner!”

She put everything in the kitchen and checked around the apartment, looking in the bedroom. Agatha flopped on the bed. Raven lifted her head and meowed.

“My thoughts exactly, Raven.”

Agatha called Rio, surprised when she answered.

“Hey baby, I’m sorry I didn’t tell you I was leaving.” Rio’s voice was hollow.

“It’s okay. I was just going to stop by and bring you some soup and other things. Maybe we can watch a movie?” Agatha asked, hoping Rio would tell her the truth.

“You know, I just got out of the shower and am going to bed. I’ll call you tomorrow.”

“Sure, my love. I hope you feel better. I love you, Rio.” Agatha was holding back a sob.

“Goodnight, Agatha.”

The line went dead.

Agatha hung up the phone and cried. Raven came over and rubbed against her.

“What have I done now?” Agatha asked Raven, searching for answers in those wise, feline eyes. Then the horror of the possibility hit her. Could Rio have figured it out?

“I should have just told her it was Lilia, and then I could have explained everything, how I was young and stupid. What young lesbian doesn’t fall for an older woman? God, Raven. Why am I like this? She went to Lilia’s, didn’t she?”

Deep down, Agatha knew she had.

Raven hopped down from the bed and started walking toward the living room. She stopped and looked back at Agatha.

“What? You want me to follow you? Did Timmy fall down a well? You know, following an immortal cat isn’t the strangest thing I’ve done.”

She stood and followed Raven into the living room. Raven jumped onto the coffee table and pawed at the open notebook Rio had been writing in.

“She does know.”

Agatha looked at everything Rio had written down.

“Of course, she has pictures in her office. Idiot.” Agatha scoffed. “Okay, Raven, here’s the play. I leave everything I brought for her, including your treats, I write her a note, go home and cry, and then it’s up to your mom and whatever fucking Lilia says.”

Raven meowed.

“Yeah, I know. I’m fucked, and not in any of the fun ways.”

⭐⭐⭐

Rio had been to Lilia’s a handful of times but had the address to the Victorian house in Angelino Heights saved in her phone. She wasn’t sure if she was glad Lilia was home or disappointed. She had just hung up with Agatha and was upset with herself. Rio had lied to Agatha and hadn’t told her she loved her because she felt conflicted. The plethora of feelings roared back to life as she walked up to the door and knocked.

Lilia was not surprised to see her.

“I was wondering when you would figure it out.”

Lilia turned, walking back into the house, not waiting for Rio.

Rio was right on her heels.

“So you just expected me to figure it out on my own? KNEW I would come over?”

“I read people, Rio. It’s how I’m so good at my job. Tea?” Lilia offered, walking to the kitchen like she had invited Rio over, not like it was possibly her last few minutes on earth.

“What? No! I’m pissed and homicidal, Lilia!” Rio was still following her, hands flailing. She couldn’t stay still. Lilia walked back to the living room with a cup of tea and sat down on a grey, overstuffed couch while Rio began her habit of covering every inch of floor space.

“I know you’re going to walk around while you rant, but feel free to sit when you need to. You know how Agatha reads people? I taught her that,” Lilia said, sipping the hot liquid, and the subtle reminder of Lilia’s influence over Agatha enraged Rio more.

“Right, because you taught her everything! Which brings us to my first thing. Lilia, you were her teacher. How could you?” Rio’s words were laced with venom as she aimed them at Lilia.

“I will stop you right there, Rio Vidal.” Lilia’s words came out harsh for the first time. “NOTHING ever happened when Agatha was underage. EVER. I didn’t ever think of her like that, and I never would.” Lilia sighed deeply as the pain of the past overtook her features. “Do you want the whole story, or do you just want to pace around and yell?”

“What I WANT is to cause some damage,” Rio said seriously, wishing she had the dagger she used to carry on her for SF-like emergencies.

“Lorna wanted to murder me, too. I’m honestly shocked she never did.” She looked at Rio, watching the gears turn in the woman’s mind.

“Talk,” Rio said with all the restraint she could muster, “ but then I get to ask questions, and you will answer honestly. Without being cryptic.”

Lilia sat her tea down and looked past Rio, seeing the women of the past parade past her eyes.

“I met Agatha when she was fourteen and I was twenty-eight. Her mother had been abusing her for years, and Agatha would do whatever she could to ruffle Evanora’s feathers.”

Lilia told her about young Agatha, the mischievous child star that terrorized anyone and everyone she could if she didn’t try to seduce them first. A menace first and Hollywood starlet second. Lilia had an encyclopedia of stories from the mundane to the absolutely absurd, and they were all very Agatha, from the hilarious to the horrifying. Lilia also explained Lorna’s plan to get her away from Evanora and where she came into it.

“Evanora did it for us, though. Lorna and I, that is. When she caught Agatha with another girl, she lost it with her righteous bullshit. I’ll never get over the irony of holier-than-thou Evanora Harkness turning Agatha into Lesbian Jesus. Regardless, she shot at Agatha in public, thankfully, she missed. The woman couldn’t hit the broad side of a barn if it were lit up like a Christmas tree. I happened to know a powerful attorney, and you know the rest, Agatha gained her freedom. Lorna was like a mother to her, and I was, eh,” Lilia hesitated, “an older figure.”

Lilia still felt guilty all these decades later. She had helped shape this woman, took all of Agatha’s love and trust, and thrown it away and destroyed it. Destroyed Agatha. Lorna had once called her Lilia ‘The destroyer of all that is well and good in this world’ Calderu during an argument. Lilia turned around and called her Lorna ‘Mother to all but her own child’ Wu. It was one of their nastier fights near the end of their friendship.

“Lorna was a mother to Agatha, but not Alice,” Rio said slowly, trying to make it make sense. Alice had yearned for more of a relationship with her mother, but understood, at least when older, that her time with Norma had been better for her. Alice was so well-adjusted and had a happy, loving childhood,  raised by Gran. Alice and Agatha were pretty close these days, and Alice was so happy for Rio. Alice had even encouraged Rio’s relationship with Agatha. Did she know the dynamics of Agatha and Lorna’s relationship? How much of any of this did Alice know? Had ALICE, her chosen sister, been lying to her? Rio pushed that thought aside, one potential traitor at a time.

“Lorna had chosen a different life for Alice after seeing how growing up here had affected Agatha. She knew that her hopes for Alice’s life and future were incompatible with the celebrity lives they led; sex, drugs, and alcohol.  As she must have told you, Agatha was an alcoholic before she was in double digits, as were most Hollywood kids back then. And, Lorna, well, you know her story.” Lilia waved her hand.

“Agatha was an alcoholic? I knew she partied. I didn’t know she was an alcoholic. She still drinks now, should she not?” Rio was mildly irritated that her concern for Agatha was eroding at the anger that was fueling her conversation. Lilia nodded.

“From the time she was a kid until she was thirty-two. She quit when she started her fertility journey. I didn’t know immediately, having pushed her away as much as I could, showing her a colder vision of myself. Let’s go back…”

The truth hurts, Lilia reminded herself, even to the person saying it. Yet, this was a necessary hurt today. Rio’s main concern was evident. She needed honesty after months of being lied to. Anything less would remove any chance Lilia had of staying in Rio’s good graces. And, if Lilia had any chance of returning to Agatha’s life, she had to be honest with Rio. Lilia had to overcome the survival instinct to battle what she imagined was Agatha’s narrative of the story, that of Lilia the cold-hearted villain. If Lilia wanted to manipulate this to her advantage at all, this would be the way, by not omitting anything or attempting to portray herself in a positive light.

Lilia let the story escape, a torrent held by an age-old dam. Nothing was held back anymore. She told Rio everything, how Agatha had kissed her on her eighteenth birthday, the conversation about Agatha’s feelings, and how they decided to be casual. She told Rio about how she separated herself into two personas to keep Agatha at arm’s length. Lilia added Lorna’s take on it too, her insistence that Lilia was in the wrong and the fights it caused between the best friends.

Unable to stop the truth, Lilia talked about how the dynamic shifted after Nicky was born. Although it had been clear that there were no second chances, she continued the cruel demeanor to focus Agatha away from her. After all, she was, wasn’t she?

“And that’s where we are today,” Lilia finished, eyes puffy from crying.

Rio ran a hand over her face. Thoughts fluttered around her brain, making it difficult to pin one down for long. But her emotions stayed strong. Rio was livid and conflicted. Her heart ached for Agatha and Lilia in completely different ways. It made her want to stay and comfort or run far away.  These situations were just so fucking convoluted. One thing was clear to her. She couldn’t imagine losing Alice because one of them had made such a stupid life choice. To endure this crazy life and do so alone and without forgiveness and closure from your best friend. It was heartbreaking.

This whole thing was messy. It was complicated. It was a psychological drama Rio wasn’t sure she wanted to play a part in anymore. Still, Rio was never one to stand down when justice needed to be done.

“What the fuck, Lilia? There is so much to unpack here. I don’t know where to start.”

“Give it a shot,” Lilia said with tired cynicism, “I’ve heard or thought it all.”

“Agatha trusted you! You were supposed to be a safe person for her! You were one of two people that she had in this world and you didn’t just fuck her over, you actually fucked her. You KNEW better, Lilia! So WHAT if she was a consenting adult, or mature? She was still a kid compared to you, Lilia. One of your two best friends! Lorna was a much better woman than I am.”

Rio paced the room, fists clenched. Yes, it took two people to get into the situation, but Agatha had been young, and it had been Lilia’s job, no, her duty, to shut the whole thing down.

“Lorna was a better woman than all of us,” Lilia corrected. “I regret what I did to Agatha and how. I took a moment of weakness and stretched it for a decade. I destroyed myself, Agatha, and the family we built in the process. Each one of us had a vice, and somehow, mine turned out to be the most damaging. I suspected from the beginning I wasn’t the person for Agatha, but I selfishly thought maybe it could work out. I deluded myself that I was balancing it well, jumping between two personalities with her, warmly loving and coldly distant.  It didn’t work as well as I had thought, and Agatha had cried, god, I don’t know how many times to Lorna, who affectionately called me a ‘bipolar bitch.’ It wasn’t just a bad situation, it was a disaster, and I was too blind to see it or what it did to me. I completely lost sight of who I was, and every word said under that other version of me made me hate myself, too. I’ve never been able to forgive myself.”

Lilia looked down at the floor, her eyes following patterns in the wood.

“What about Nicky? You didn’t want him, so why bother treating him with such favoritism?”

Rio had stopped pacing for a moment.

“He’s a part of Agatha, and that makes him special. Just as Agatha will always be special to me, I will always love her, Rio. Not the way you do, no, but the way I have always loved her,”

Rio jumped in, “The way you always loved her? Like you still want to sleep with her?” Rio’s face was one of utter disgust.

Lilia’s face did not change. “No, Rio, I do not want to sleep with Agatha, that is not a part of it. As I was saying, I also care about all our students. But I will go further to protect Nicky and the twins out of loyalty to Agatha, Wanda, and, most importantly, Lorna.”

“WANDA!” Rio remembered the good-hearted redhead with an inability to keep secrets, realization dawning.

“She must not know any of this! Does she even know what truly happened to Lorna?”

“I doubt it. One great thing Agatha picked up from Lorna is a protective streak. With that, and Agatha’s maturity beyond her years, she sheltered Wanda from all that she could.  Agatha swooped in and has always tried to take away Wanda’s pain from the atrocities of genocidal war and then an exploitative family member. Agatha immediately got Wanda into therapy once she got Wanda away from her aunt. If Wanda will ever tell you about those first few weeks at Agatha’s, let her. It will give you more insight into Wanda and why she has let Agatha lead and guide her for so long. But I digress.”

Lilia finished the now cold tea as she tried to remember where she was going with that last story and looked at Rio. “You’re conflicted.” She stated.

“No shit,” Rio replied. “On the one hand, the ex Agatha told me about is this awful, terrible, cold, and unfeeling woman…”

“She was,” Lilia confirmed.

Rio’s face twisted as she looked at Lilia.

“But, you’re not. You’re Lilia. You care about these kids, especially the boys. You hate injustice, you get nervous over a teacher going into labor, and you worry about students getting glitter in their eyes. You want what’s best for former friends, you give women second chances, and you watch over your former best friend's daughter. Alice told me about the advice you gave her about Jen. You warned me about keeping Wanda’s secrets. The Lilia Calderu I know, cryptic and shady though she may be, is not the woman of Agatha’s past.” Rio didn’t know what to do or really what to think, about anyone.

“There’s a lot of shady characters around here, my dear.”

“You and Agatha, primarily,” Rio said, looking at the floor as she had a thought. “Lilia, why did you bring me here? Don’t give me the bullshit about teaching or because of Alice, why did you really bring me here?”

Rio knew the answer but needed to hear it straight from the source.

“Do not discount my support for Alice and her moment of need. But, yes, I also saw two broken women who could support each other. With what I knew of that little wild coven, it would be inevitable if you came back with us.”

Lilia waited for Rio’s reaction, knowing it could go a myriad of ways, really hoping anger wasn’t the one Rio chose, but knowing too well that her cards were upside down at this point.

Rio chose anger.

“WHAT?! You tried to set us up?”

“No. Words have meaning, my dear. I said you could support each other. I didn’t expect all of this to happen, though part of me always hopes for the best for Agatha, in love as well. I only want her to be happy and for Nicky to be surrounded by love. Everything else? All of that was more than I could hope for, but I would do it over and over if I knew the results would be the same.”

All Rio heard was how Lilia had set them up.

“You KNEW I had a stalker, and you put them in harm's way to make up for the pain you caused her? That’s selfish, Lilia!”

Rio was moving faster now, careful not to trip over her own two feet as she wore a path in the wooden flooring. Lilia’s eyes followed Rio.

“Yes, a stalker. Not a crazy, psychopath who would follow you across the state! I didn’t know Lou would completely lose it, and neither did you. I certainly didn’t know she’d go after Nicky. I would have never let it get that far.”

“You weren’t at the hospital when he got injured,” Rio spat out the accusation.

“Not that you were aware of, sure. I told Wanda not to wake either of you or tell you I was there. Anytime Nicky or the twins hurt themselves, I am in the shadows. Don’t underestimate me, Vidal. You’re losing focus.” Lilia reminded her with a slight smile.

“This is too much,” Rio shook her head. “You were a monster and shattered her. Now,  you’re what, trying to redeem yourself? You bring me here, effectively putting her in danger, Agatha and I fall in love, what do you get? Absolution of your sins?”

Rio’s mind was reeling.

“My sins are my own, and I have to live with those, with what I did and what my actions caused. I lost my family because of what I did, Rio. All the money and notoriety in the world can’t replace that, and it was a hard lesson to learn. I went from being a planet in someone’s solar system to a monster lurking in the shadows.” Lilia was willing Rio to understand, without a side of anger.

Rio had stopped making her laps in Lilia’s front room. It was late, and her energy was fading. “So this was your idea to what, to return to dwarf planet status, Pluto?” Rio had her hands on her hips, her hard expression returning.

“Something like that.”

Lilia finally stood, walking back to the kitchen to put away the now empty teacup—more to keep herself busy than anything else. Rio was shaking, though she wasn’t sure whether it was from convoluted emotions, adrenaline, or shock.

“Is this your entire redemption arc, Lilia? Restore love to Agatha’s life and maybe, just maybe, find your way back in, because I may not allow that to happen, purely out of protection. What if I don’t trust you anymore?”

Lilia scoffed, rinsing the cup and placing it on the drainboard, turning to face Rio.

“You don’t have a malicious bone in your body. Norma raised you better. It’s why Lou is still breathing and why I’m still standing. If Agatha didn’t want me here, I wouldn’t be, and you won’t go against Agatha.

“You also won’t hurt Alice, and she still sees the good in me. Alice is the one who called me to you, in case you’ve forgotten. She is the one that gave me the idea to bring you here, and it’s already crossed your mind how much she’s withheld.”

Rio quickly looked away. Lilia was, after all, the master at reading people.

“Did my career amount to anything in your principal Calderu eyes? Or was it merely my trauma and relationship with Alice?”

Rio was floundering.

“Of course it did. I wasn’t letting an unqualified teacher around my kids. I may be kooky, Rio, but I’m not certifiable. I do have some standards.”

For the first time during their conversation, Lilia felt slightly offended. Rio shook her head, walking back through the living room.

“This conversation isn’t over, Lilia. I don’t know what to think. I don’t know who you are anymore. I’m confused about my feelings. I have no idea what’s real and what was manipulated. I need the week, Lilia, I won’t be in.”

Rio headed for the door as Lilia caught her arm.

“Rio, wait, just hang on for a minute.” Lilia dropped her hand and went to where keys were hanging by the door, taking one on a triple moon keyring and writing down an address in the Sierra Nevada region.

“Go home, get some sleep, drive up in the morning. It’s a good place to do some thinking. Lorna and Agatha did a lot of…retreats there. I know things aren’t okay, and I know they aren’t okay with us. I’ll take care of work, and you go clear your head. As for Agatha, a couple of things. Her drinking is under control, and she has a small Army of friends to pull her away from most bad situations. But, lastly, and most importantly, I can bring people together Rio, but I can’t make them fall in love. You have every right to be mad at me, and if you burn down the cabin, I deserve it. But don’t take it out on Agatha, don’t shut her out, and don’t cause her pain like I did.”

For the first time, Lilia actually looked her age, and older.

“No one is even slightly capable of doing what you did, Swords,” Rio retorted as she took the key and address and closed the door firmly behind her.

She had gone in, guns a-blazin’, and came out surrounded by ghosts. Calling Lilia Swords was what Rio had needed to remind her that this woman had hurt Agatha all those years ago.

Lilia could only smirk at the nickname as she watched Rio through the glass in the door. Confirming the rumor of the tattoo and recalling something Lorna had once said before Witches’ Road had even been written.

“You’re not taking a knife to her heart, Lilia, you’re taking a sword to it.”

⭐⭐⭐

It was after midnight when Rio entered her apartment and turned on the light. Her eyes automatically landed on the flowers on the coffee table and the opened spiral. She picked it up and sat on the couch, reading the note from Agatha.

My Love,

I came by to bring you soup and other supplies. Everything is in the fridge. Raven led me to this.

Yes, Lilia is my ex. Please give me a chance to explain after you hear her side of the story. I love you so much, and I do not want to lose you. I know you have your reasons for not telling me you were going to see her, and I respect that. I am here to answer any and all questions you have when you’re ready.

I love you.

Your Love,

Agatha

Agatha had even left her boutonniere from the reception lying next to the letter. Rio put her head in her hands and sobbed. When she finally stopped, she dragged herself to the shower, sitting on the floor as the hot water washed away her tears and thinking about her next move. She had a lot to process, and thanks to Lilia, the root cause of most of this, she had a fortress of solitude awaiting her escape. Still, her thoughts scattered like the water around her.

She needed Alice, but she couldn’t ruin this time for her and Jen.

Alice might also have been keeping secrets all these years.

Rio hadn’t felt this alone since before she had crossed foot into Norma Wu’s house. Even on her own in San Francisco, she could call Alice and talk to her. Meet up on the weekends.

Oh, Wanda. Poor Wanda, too. Someone else who would just be crushed. She would have to wait for Wanda to reach out to her.

Rio lay in her bed, having transitioned from her shower to the comfort of her blankets, and emotional support Raven curled on her stomach as she let out a harsh laugh. This damn situation was so convoluted because Agatha was at the center of this little lesbian universe.

No, she would do as Lilia had instructed, wouldn’t she? Get some sleep, because deep down, Rio knew it was the best thing to do. Not because Lilia had suggested it but because it was the smart thing to do.

She would pack, leave a note for Alice to take Raven to Nicky, and flee to the cabin in the mountains.

Maybe she would burn it down.

After all, she did feel like causing some damage.

⭐⭐⭐

“Lilia. It was Lilia?”

Wanda’s face drained of color, a feat for the already fair-skinned redhead. Then, it contorted to hurt as the realization of the past nearly thirty years crept in. Lilia and Lorna had been such a force, their friendship stood solid through everything, and they had been there through everything. Lilia had always been there for them, and then suddenly, she hadn’t been there as much. Then, she had more to do with Wanda, and by extension, Nat. Not as much with Agatha. Wanda hadn’t known what had happened. All she remembered from those years was the inherent sadness that never left Lorna’s eyes and how her emotions flared and tears flowed if anyone even mentioned Lilia.  Her heart ached for their friendship, derailed by horrid decisions and poor life choices. Wanda couldn’t imagine losing Agatha’s presence like that, and the mere thought made her eyes well up with tears once more. Lilia had brought Wanda so much hope when she came to visit the hospital on the day of Nicky’s birth, thinking things would be all right again, but nothing came of it. Which again sent a wave of sadness over her now as it had years ago. Wanda knew Lilia would have been over the moon for Lorna’s sobriety had she been made aware.

They had all been so proud of Lorna.

Then she and Agatha did that movie.

Lorna relapsed.

Lorna died.

The memory of the days surrounding her death made her eyes sting again.

They all moved on, honoring Lorna with the best lives they could lead and raising their sons in a happy and stable home. Then, it came time to send the boys to school, and Agatha insisted on SHIELD. Wanda was elated to have Lilia back, if even on the periphery of their small family. That’s when she could see Agatha and Lilia's relationship begin to thaw slowly.  It was one of the reasons Wanda was so insistent on becoming the ultimate school moms. The parties, the functions, everything. She so badly wanted to rebuild their group’s old dynamic.

It wasn’t just for Agatha, who would never admit to needing Lilia around. Wanda had needed Lilia back in almost the same way that Lilia needed them.

Wanda turned to Nat, not quite looking at her.

“You’ve kept secrets for Agatha, did you know?”

Nat shook her head. “No, this is one she didn’t let me in on either.”

Nat looked up at Agatha, eyes searching.

“They were our friends too. Missed holidays, birthdays, and milestones. It was our family. Agatha, you had no right to decide for us.”

Wanda nodded, something darker rolling across her face and into her tremulous voice.

“I get wanting to have secrets, but why didn’t you tell me? Me, Aggie. I told you everything, every time. I thought it was mutual. You’re my sister, for god's sake! You know what I mean. And it isn’t just one secret now like you always said. Not just the identity of your ex. But, also the two biggest losses and moments in your life before Nicky and I never knew a thing. I didn’t even suspect something was wrong. Why didn’t you trust me with these and let me be there for you? Pregnancy, miscarriage, stood up at the altar… what the hell, Aggie!” The words tumbled out of her. Wanda felt as though she had been stabbed in the back repeatedly.

By Agatha.

Agatha looked at Wanda with the same sisterly look as always, “You were filming overseas, I wasn’t going to burden you, I -”

“NO!” Wanda shouted and jumped up, startling both of the other women. “NO! You don’t get to do that. I wasn’t a little girl, Agatha! I wasn’t then, and I’m not now.

“But, Agatha, I so badly wanted and still want to be there for you, for everything. I was honored to be in that delivery room the day Nicky was born. I gladly would have let you break my hand that day. Every scandal when I got to be there by your side and handle something the way you taught me to was when I finally got to be the person that Agatha Harkness was for me. Now, I find out that you kept two of your darkest moments from me? So I was not able to be there as I should have been and so wished to be. Aggie, I am just devastated.”

Tears were dripping from Wanda’s chin and jaw. Nat sat beside her on the couch, also crying, but holding Wanda’s hand. She looked up at Wanda, nodding in agreement and resisting the urge to put her arms around Wanda.

Agatha wiped her face with the sleeve of her shirt, which was covered in tears and snot, despite having changed just a little while earlier.

“I didn’t want to cloud your judgment now by telling you who, and back then, I didn’t want to bother you with everything you had -”

This time, it was Nat who interrupted.

“We aren’t an excuse to use, Agatha. We all knew what we were doing, and Wanda did a damn good job at balancing us with everything else.”

Wanda nodded.

“I would’ve been back here immediately. Lilia being your ex doesn’t cloud my judgment now, and it wouldn’t have then. This was something you did for you, not us. I am capable of talking to someone and separating their past indiscretions. You wanted to maintain control and the facade of your life. We can call it a lesson you picked up in your youth. But, that control would have cracked had you tried to reconcile the truth with the facade you presented. Yes, it was complicated, but Lilia was a big part of all our lives. And whether you want to admit it or not, you needed her back in your life when Lorna died. You chose to go through so much on your own when you had me, us, and probably her too.” Wanda gestured. “All you had to do was tell us.”

Wanda took a moment to breathe. Her words marinated in Agatha’s head.

“Rio is good for you, the best really. But, it’s not all about having a perfect partner.” Wanda looked at her hand in Nat’s before reaching out with the other to Agatha. “You also need the old Lilia to fill a different role. Stop thinking of her as your ex. That woman is long gone, and you know deep down that she is.”

Agatha did not reach for Wanda’s outstretched hand, unconsciously taking a step back instead and causing another wave of sorrow to hit Wanda.

“Agatha, God! I’m so over the manipulations and the betrayal! Nearly THIRTY years of it. How can I trust you, that this is all of it, that you’re not holding back anything else important?” Wanda looked away from Agatha, her bottom lip quivering.

“I was never supposed to be the one being betrayed, Agatha.”



Agatha leaned forward, her head dropping. She knew that the hardest challenge still lay ahead, and she dreaded having to add salt to Wanda’s emotional wounds tonight. She hated the feeling of causing pain to the person she swore to protect.

Yet, Agatha still had to tell her how Lorna died. Not the fabricated story that she had reiterated so many times over the years it almost felt like a truth of its own.

There was also the letter Lorna had written for Wanda, too. Agatha had never given it to her, not because she didn’t want to, but because it had specific instructions:

When Wanda and Nat get married.

Or 15 years from now (2/27/26),

whichever comes first.


She assumed that Lorna had written it in that letter. Lorna had a flair for the dramatic, but no, she needed to tell Wanda now, before she did any more damage.

“You’re right, Wanda. There’s more I need to tell you. But this one was not exactly my secret.”

She raised her eyes to meet Wanda’s scrutinizing gaze, knowing this was going to deal a harsh blow.

“You might want to sit down.”

Wanda didn’t sit down. Instead, she remained standing, knowing she might end up bolting out of the door and sprinting across the compound. Nat stood, taking up a protective stance next to her.

Agatha blinked back tears and drew a shaky breath.

“Please know that Lorna didn’t want anyone to know, but she never relapsed. She remained sober.”

The confusion instantly hit both redheads.

“Then what happened? Agatha, we know what substance abuse looks like. Lorna looked so bad at the end. She looked…”

It clicked for both of them at that moment as she looked at Nat. Where Nat accepted it with a silent nod, Wanda let out an ear-splitting scream.

“NO! She was clean, she was sober, she was vibrant! WHY?! Why did this happen to her? Why didn’t you tell us?”

Wanda wasn’t really standing as much as Nat was holding her up as she relived that night.

“Lorna didn’t want anyone to pity her, didn’t want to become some sob story or martyr. She was down to the last few weeks, days really. And, went out on her own terms at that party we were all at, and you know the rest.”

Agatha had relived the memory so many times over the years. It was a piece of her.

Wanda clung to Nat as she let the initial shock subside and stood straight. There was time for rehashed grief later.

“In hiding all of this, you did more damage than good. Once more your secrets and manipulations hurt us all. We deserved to be there for Lorna and for you. We deserved to mourn and honor her.  Lilia isn’t the only one that’s been playing two different people, Agatha. She may have started the demolition, but you kept it going.”

She motioned to Nat for them to go as she looked back at Agatha.

“I have to think some things over. You know we’ll be okay in a few days because that’s what family does, Agatha. They forgive each other, no matter the transgressions or duration. Ironically, it was you and Lorna who taught me that. Just give me some space to process this.” She paused a moment. “I just hope Rio can forgive you. If Alice knew ANY of this and didn’t tell Rio, then may God have mercy on her soul.”

Hand-in-hand, Nat and Wanda walked out of Agatha’s house. Wanda would absolutely forgive Agatha in a few days. She wouldn’t let this come between them. There would be scars, but she saw how haunted Lilia still was to this day, and she would not make those same mistakes.

As the hurt redheads returned to their house, Wanda turned to Nat as they buried themselves in each other’s arms.

“How do you manage to remain so stoic and not completely lose it?” Wanda sniffed.

“Some training never leaves you, but also, I had a hand in Lorna’s death,” Nat whispered.

Wanda pulled back and looked up at her.

“What? But she chose that herself, you had nothing to do with that unless you gave her the…” Wanda looked at her in horror and stepped back. Nat ran a hand through her hair.

“I didn’t give her anything! She came to me asking about certain pills and medications. I assumed it was for a role, and I answered her. Just like I would you. I gave her the names of some people from whom she could get more information. She asked me if I used to carry things on me, and I told her I still did for…trickier situations I occasionally find myself in involving you two. Stalkers, if anyone is ever kidnapped, any possible revenge; you know, the shadier side.”

Agatha Harkness was not your normal, run-of-the-mill celebrity.

Wanda nodded, not liking where this was going. Nat crossed her arms, tears gathering at the rims of her eyes.

“I did not tell her where I kept anything; she was clever,” Nat smiled. “ It was Lorna, she had known me for fourteen years and had a hand in forming me. She knew how to get into my house. She knew all my secret hiding places, the secret panels. A few days after she died, I had a suspicion, and I checked. It was all missing, with a note that said, ‘I’m so sorry, you’ll learn why one day. I love you, Nat. Take good care of them, especially Wanda.’”

The tears had spilled over as she looked at Wanda.

“I am so sorry, Wanda.”

The sleeves of Wanda’s shirt were permanently tear-stained, she was sure. “Why didn’t you tell me?”

Nat couldn’t meet her eyes. “To protect you,” she whispered.

Wanda was done.

“I am NOT a child that needs protecting! I wasn’t then, and I’m not now! You’re sleeping on the couch.”

She turned on her heel and stomped up the stairs.



Agatha had just stood and stared after Wanda and Nat. Her hands had dropped to her sides, and she truly did not know what to do with herself.

She was losing people quickly.

Her phone dinged, Nat had installed the tracking app for the necklace. Rio had just left Lilia’s. Agatha huffed.

“Alright, you old bat, time for us to do this,” she said aloud.

She swore she heard Lorna’s laughter.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha sailed into Lilia’s house as though she belonged there, locking the door behind her.

“I see you were expecting me.”

Agatha noticed a mug of black coffee on the coffee table as she settled in the old armchair beside it, the one that had always been her favorite. It was still just as comfortable.

“You should keep the door locked. You never know what shady characters will come in, present company excluded, of course.”

She looked down at the wooden floor and noticed a burn mark, a memory of a time she had missed the ashtray. Lilia walked in from the kitchen with a new cup of tea.

“It’s two in the morning, and you’re the second dark-haired lesbian in my house tonight. I think I’ll take my chances.”

“I take it things didn’t go well,” Agatha concluded, pushing away the memories of her time spent here. In the past, the two women had at least kept their activities relegated to different spaces. This house held the better memories - the memories of family. There were arguments, especially near the end, sure. However, this house mostly reminded Agatha of who and what she lost and she realized she was losing her anger quickly, even though she was trying to hold on to it.

“I’m alive, so it could have been much worse. Have you heard from her?” Lilia quirked a brow.

“No, and I don’t think I will for at least a day or so. She lied to me about coming here. She told me she was at her apartment while I was physically standing there, so that’s where we are with that. Did you orchestrate this? Don’t tell me you didn’t. You know better than to meddle in the affairs of a Harkness.”

Agatha could have sworn she saw ghosts of their younger selves everywhere. Agatha had crossed her legs at the knees and held her mug at her lips. Lilia sighed.

“You and I both know that I am not the woman I was all those years before.”

Lilia’s eyes met Agatha’s.

“Maybe not completely, but there’s still that Calderu manipulation there. That was always a staple. Half-truths, cryptic advice, the ultimate mastermind.”

Agatha took a sip of her coffee, her gaze steady.

“Yes, and you are my protege, don’t forget that,” Lilia reminded.

Agatha looked away at that.

“The question still stands,” Agatha replied.

“I brought her here because she would be a great fit at SHIELD, Alice needed her, and she would also fill gaping wounds.” Lilia studied Agatha’s reaction carefully.

“Fifty-one, and you’re still trying to control my life,” Agatha barked, though they both knew there was no real bite to it.

“I just want what’s best for you.”

The anger in Agatha attempted to shove forward with that admission. “What’s best for me? WHAT’S BEST FOR ME?! Was staring at me for three hours after I told you I miscarried, what was best for me? Was leaving me at the altar what was best for me? Was using me for ten years? Don’t tell me what’s best for me, Lilia. Actually, it’s about time we have this heart-to-heart, isn’t it?”

Lilia settled into the sofa, her face soft after the wave of verbal blows from Agatha. Agatha paused, chest heaving after her release.

“I couldn’t process it, Agatha.” Lilia began. “There was so much going on with me and in my head. I had lost my ability to have children and had barely worked through that. I was so concerned and worried for you and your future. I knew you needed comfort, and at the same time, not from me. Yet, I mourned for you inwardly. As cold as I was to you, I felt it all rage within me. I had compartmentalized completely until I disassociated from everything and everyone. The two people I had become warred with one another, as was so frequent for me then.

“My first instinct was to hold you, Agatha. It always was. The night you told me you were in love with me? I wanted to console you, kiss you, hold you. All of the times that widened the gap between us? I never wanted to cause the divide to begin with, but I knew I wasn’t that person for you either, Agatha. I could have tried, but it would have ended with even less chance of any reconciliation.

“I couldn’t let you end your career, Agatha. Not when you sacrificed so much and worked so hard, look at all the good you’ve done! We both know that neither of us would have been happy, and I never meant to use you.”

Agatha had expected a verbal fistfight. She didn’t expect Lilia to roll over this easily, and she certainly didn’t expect the venom to be drained from her own proverbial fangs.

She looked at Lilia, skeptically. This had to be a ruse of some type. A power play or manipulation. This was not the ruthless Don Calderu she had known. Yet, the realization of the truth also stung.

“Your first instinctual reaction to me confessing my love for you was to console me?” Agatha shook her head, scoffing aloud. “I guess I never really understood our dynamic and you are one fucked up lady.”

The shock on Lilia’s face and the reversal of the conversation energized Agatha.

“So, what then? Why are you confessing everything now? Not even arguing or defending yourself? This is not like you.” Agatha’s arms were crossed tightly against her chest as she scrutinized Lilia’s every movement.

Lilia placed her tea on the table next to a scratch caused by a glass pipe long ago.

“Lorna was my friend too,” She said softly.

Agatha felt as though Lilia had screamed it. Again, she felt that age-old hurt of coming second to Lorna in Lilia’s eyes.

“Lorna told me for ten years to end it, that I was breaking you, little by little, yet I kept on. Kept convincing myself that you were an adult, kept trying to prove to her that I wasn’t wrong. I was technically correct, but I still should have ended it or never let it begin in the first place. You were my vice, and I became the worst of the three of us. You and Lorna stayed true to who you were. In the end, I left you, unrecognizable, and I lost my best friends and my family, in the process.”

Lilia’s face showed the worry of someone who had experienced great loss. Not the carefully constructed mask she had worn for so long.

“I should have been there with you, Aggi...Agatha.” She stopped herself from using the nickname Lorna had given Agatha all those lifetimes ago. “I should have been there the whole time, the treatments, the appointments, at the end. Supporting you. I know you didn’t tell Wanda, she and Nat were one of the worst-kept secrets in Hollywood, and she wouldn’t have been able to keep Lorna’s secret. You did it all alone, and you shouldn’t have had to.”

Lilia had moved to be closer to Agatha and tentatively put a hand on Agatha’s knee. Agatha stared at Lilia’s hand, which she had known so well, that had brought her comfort and pleasure and was now offering comfort again. It was also offering a bridge back to something more familiar.

Agatha covered Lilia’s hand with hers.

“I told Wanda everything tonight.”

She spoke around the lump in her throat. She had wanted to say other things, lash out at Lilia for not being there, and continue to shift the blame. She couldn’t do it anymore. Lilia’s honesty had disarmed her.

Manipulative bitch.

“Oh, Agatha, how did she take it?” Lilia asked. Her voice was that of the caring, loving Lilia from long ago. The Lilia that Agatha had so desperately needed since Lorna died.

“Not well,” Agatha sniffed. “She said that I also turned into two people, and she’s right. I betrayed the person I swore to protect,” Realization dawned on Agatha.

“Oh my gods, Lilia. I didn't screw her, but I sure as hell screwed her over…fuck,” Agatha was going over everything in her mind. She was two different people, and in some of the same ways, she had done to Wanda what Lilia had done to her. “I kept telling myself that it wasn’t my secret to tell. Lorna left a letter for her to open when she and Nat either got married or fifteen years later. I told myself I was protecting her.” Agatha looked at Lilia with tired, watery eyes.

“Ah yes, Lorna’s letters. I received an envelope of them in the mail the day after she died. I told myself I was trying to protect you and keep our friendship intact. As you can see, it did not work. As for Wanda, she isn’t that scared little girl anymore.”

“That’s exactly what she said, and then I covered a secret for Nat, so that’s going to make things worse, and there’s still Rio. She hates me, doesn’t she? I’ve lost my sister, two of them really, and the love of my life because I don’t know how to tell the truth, and I wish I could blame you, but I can’t.” Agatha saw Lilia in a different light.

If Agatha keeping secrets from Wanda did major damage, wouldn’t she do whatever it took to stay in Wanda’s life? To repair things between Wanda and Nat? The things Nat had were for one specific purpose and didn’t count as drugs in her eyes. Nat had zero ill intentions and never suspected that Lorna would do what she did. She had come to Agatha as soon as she had discovered them missing, and Agatha had been the one to tell her not to tell Wanda. She would shoulder all the blame for it if she needed to. They had finally gotten engaged. They were finally getting married. Agatha wouldn’t let anything come in between that.

Isn’t that what Lorna and Lilia had always done?

Lilia would always be there for the boys, Agatha, Wanda, and Nat, if they ever needed her. And Agatha had been playing the Lilia card all year to get Rio, and occasionally Alice, out of work, hadn’t she?

Lilia had shed the Swords persona long ago.

Agatha had developed one of her own.

Now, Agatha was paying for it, and she had blamed Lilia for it this whole time.

“I have to talk to Rio.” Agatha stood up abruptly and lost her balance. Lilia pulled her back down to sit before she fell over.

“No. I gave her the key to the cabin so I could keep an eye on her and know where to send you. She’s going to find out that Alice knew, and her heart will absolutely shatter. I also knew you would come clean with Wanda soon, and she would turn to Rio. Wanda will probably go with her if she catches her early enough. You and Nat can head up in a day or so. I’ll give Alice time off when she needs it. You should probably make sure Jen and Vision are ready to be on kid duty.” Lilia laid it all out, the mastermind at work.

“Our family has everything, doesn’t it? Drama, generational trauma, so many lesbians…” Agatha was trying to make jokes like she always did, but her heart wasn’t in it. “Am I going to lose her?”

Lilia thought about it.

“Not if you’re honest, and I do mean completely. If I can learn how to tell the truth, you can too.” Lilia leaned in and patted her shoulder.

Agatha smiled and gave a small laugh.

“I know that’s a manipulation technique, Lils. I am, after all, the Luke to your Yoda.”

Lilia’s heart stopped at the old nickname as she stared at Agatha, who had looked away when she realized what she had said.

“Don’t make a big deal out of it, okay?” She scoffed, looking back at the other woman.

“Says the woman who expressly put nicknames off limits.”

“Do you blame me? I was still hurt, and I still am, but things have been somewhat repaired, and Rio has been integral to that. I cannot lose her, Lilia,” she reiterated. “I know that now. How long do I give it?”

Lilia laughed.

“You’re looking at me now? For relationship advice?”

“I mean, you’ve done pretty well so far…” Agatha smiled at the absurdity of it all.

“You’ll know. Don’t make the same mistakes I did, okay, kid?” Lilia squeezed Agatha’s hand. Both of them had done entirely too much crying tonight.

Before Agatha could overthink it, she moved from the armchair to Lilia’s side, wrapping her arms around her, and cried on her shoulder.

They were sixteen and thirty again, the self-inflicted orphan and the gay aunt.

Agatha had memorized the letters Lorna had written to her. She hadn’t opened them all, as some had explicit instructions. But the one she had opened on her fifty-first birthday nearly a year ago when she turned the same age Lorna had been when she had died was explicit:

Aggie,

I know you’re freaking out this year because it’s the age I died at. If you have yet  to forgive Lilia, it’s time. You know you need her.

Make your family complete again, Agatha.

Even dead, I know you.

I love you, Sweetie,

Lorna

P.S. I really hope you’ve found love by now. You’re not broken, just bent.

⭐⭐⭐

Wanda hadn’t slept well. How could she? She knew Nat had left, either to the Clubhouse, Agatha’s, The Beachhouse, hell, maybe even Tahoe. At that moment, she didn’t care that much. She had never been this upset with Natasha. To sit on a secret for thirteen years, and one this big? How dare she! Nat had never kept something like this from her, and she certainly had never told anything more than a white lie to Wanda. Something like ‘of course it tastes fine burnt, sunshine.’ Wanda couldn’t imagine staying there right now, and she knew one person she could talk to about all of this.

Rio.

Rio had also been hurt, albeit much worse, by Agatha. Wanda still didn’t know how much Alice knew and preemptively hurt for Rio in that regard. She needed to talk to Rio, and going to her apartment sounded like a good idea.

Me:

Agatha lied to me too.

So did Nat.

I can’t stay here.

Can I come over?

Wanda didn’t know why she was nervous while she waited for a response.

Rio:

I talked to Lilia last night.

I assume that’s what you’re

referring to.

Lilia gave me the keys to a cabin

in the mountains. Do you want

to come?

We can be miserable together?



Rio thought it would be nice to have another person with her, someone to talk everything out with. Someone Agatha had also betrayed. She couldn’t imagine how Wanda felt. Probably the same way she was going to feel once she talked to Alice and confirmed her suspicions.

Me:

I haven’t been there in

quite some time.

I’ll take you up on that.

Yes. Meet at your place at ten

this morning?

Wanda glanced at the clock. It was seven, and they had plenty of time.



Rio nodded silently to herself.

Rio:

Sounds great.

See you then.

I’ll bring the wine.

Wanda quickly sent Vision a message asking if he could take the boys for a few days, and he agreed.

Instead of leaving a note for Nicky on Alice’s door, Rio texted him about Raven, and he was excited, although a little confused about why he needed to take care of Raven. He would pick her up later that day, probably after Rio and Wanda left.

Rio hugged Wanda when she arrived before loading her luggage into the car.

“You don’t exactly pack lightly, Maximoff,” she joked as Wanda smiled.

“Have you talked to Alice?”

Rio’s mouth was a thin line as she shook her head.

“I’m afraid to. What if she did know?”

Wanda put a hand on her friend’s shoulder.

“Then we face it together, and I’m here to get you through it. Let’s go cry, drink, and potentially cause trouble in the mountains.”

Rio was grateful for the other woman in the car. Already her heart felt lighter. Even in what felt like her darkest moments of this year, she had never been alone. The hurt was still there, but the love that surrounded her, here, and she knew, in Agatha’s heart too, comforted her nonetheless.




Chapter End Notes

Thank you all so very much! I love your comments and replying to them! I also love seeing y'all on Thread, Tumblr, and Bluesky!

Two more chapters maybe?

In My Defense, I Have None

Chapter Summary

Wanda and Rio head up to the mountains for some much-needed bonding time.

Meanwhile, all of the conversations are had back in LA, and then up in the mountains. A lot happens in a short amount of time.

Chapter Notes

Thank you so much for your patience and for being so amazing! This is an extremely long chapter.
I apologize for nothing. Hydrate. It's broken up for convenience, like always.

Trigger warnings: We know me. A roller coaster of emotions with a side of darkness. Touches on addiction, and extremely illegal activities, we get into childhood backgrounds a bit more and they aren't pretty.

Also, welcome to the musical episode of Buffy - I went on some side quests apparently.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

It was Wanda’s turn to drive as they talked about the revelations of the past eighteen hours and how they were feeling about everything.

“I feel so stupid, believing I was exempt from Agatha’s betrayal,” Wanda began. “I mean, small lies and omissions, sure. Not big things like this, though. Then Nat kept something from me on top of all of it? Nat has NEVER lied to me apart from things like ‘no sunshine, I’m not getting you anything for your birthday’, or ‘no honey, I didn’t eat the last of the ice cream, it was Tommy’. Tommy doesn’t even like pistachio ricotta cheesecake ice cream!”

Wanda was waving wildly with one hand and barely keeping her other on the steering wheel as they headed north.

“Well, welcome to this side of your sister. Sorry, it’s your first time.” Rio deadpanned.

She felt terrible that Wanda was having to experience it, yet in the short time she had known Agatha, and the even shorter time she had been with her, this seemed to be a reoccurring event, and she hated it. Yes, Rio had kept things from Agatha, but she had made a concerted effort not to do that any longer after the Lou fiasco.

Yes, Rio had slept with a sociopathic jewel thief, thought for a millisecond she was still in love with her, and briefly considered going back before she realized the manipulation techniques’ effects. However, she had made it very clear to both Agatha and Lou that she no longer wanted anything to do with Lou Falco. She very much wanted to see her dead, and Lilia was right when she said Norma had raised her better than that.

Then there was Agatha. Agatha had slept with Rio’s boss for a whole decade. Rio didn’t know her back then, but it still felt strange, especially knowing that Lilia had acted as a puppet master to some extent. To her, it was very much like a sapphic Lifetime movie. She still needed to process and maybe even talk to Lilia more. She especially wanted to hear stories of a young Agatha. If they ever got to a comfortable sharing point, that is.

“I’m sorry she’s been doing it to you this whole time,” Wanda was saying as she looked between the road and Rio. “How long are you going to hold it against her?”

Rio looked at Wanda. “How long are you?”

⭐⭐⭐

Nicky had Agatha’s key to Rio’s apartment and was opening the door to get Raven as Alice and Jen were leaving theirs. They had another week until they moved onto the compound and were in full-on packing mode. Alice was confused upon seeing Nicky, as he usually didn’t come over.

“Hey, what are you doing over here?” She asked with a friendly smile.

“Hi Alice, hi Jen.” Nicky smiled back. “I’m keeping Raven this week. Rio’s going out of town, I think, though I’m not sure why.”

Confusion crossed his face as he hoped these two women would give him answers, though they seemed just as confused as he was. The newlyweds looked at each other, an unasked question passing between them.

“I didn’t know she was going anywhere,” Alice answered, unsure why Rio hadn’t asked her to keep the centuries-old feline.

“Agatha and Wanda have to work this week, and you guys return to school. I don’t know what’s going on.” Jen added.

Nicky raised his eyebrows in growing concern. “Billy and Tommy are with Uncle Vision this week. Aunt Wanda went with Rio.”

“What about your mom and Aunt Nat?” Jen asked, trying to decipher just how difficult her job was about to become.

Nicky shook his head. “Aunt Nat left the compound last night. Billy and Tommy think their moms got into an argument, and then mom didn’t come home until this morning. I think Mom did something stupid again, but my aunts never argue, so it must be really bad, and clearly, they have forgotten their communication techniques.”

Nicky bent down to pick up Raven, who had come into the hall to investigate and rubbed her in between the ears.

“Rio does stupid things too, Nicky,” Alice said in defense of Agatha.

Nicky put Raven’s head to his chest and tried to cover both of her ears. “Don’t talk about her mother like that in front of her,” he gasped.

Jen tried to hide her smile. For a kid who wanted nothing to do with acting, he had his mother’s natural talent.

“I’m going to call Rio and find out what’s going on. You take good care of the cat-god there.”

Alice turned on her heel and went back into the apartment, already calling Rio. Jen shook her head.

“I have a feeling my blood pressure is going to be very high this week.”

She waved to Nicky and followed her wife back inside. Nicky looked down at Raven.

“Let’s get your stuff packed up, and then you can tell me everything you goddess, you,” he scratched between her ears as he walked into the house, Raven meowing the whole time.

⭐⭐⭐

Alice knew something was horribly wrong. Rio hadn’t stayed late at the reception yesterday, she hadn’t even given Alice a goodbye hug or an ‘I love you’—something they always did. Yes, Alice and Jen were celebrating their marriage, but Alice would always make time for Rio, no matter what.

She thought Rio felt the same way.

That old familiar pang of jealousy slowly crept up on Alice, and she had to bat it away mentally. It was something she had dealt with after her mother had died and the real reason she couldn’t move out to Los Angeles immediately. She had to work through a lot in therapy before she could be within striking distance of Agatha.

Now, as she waited for Rio to pick up, she was reminded that the feeling was reappearing, and once again, Agatha Harkness was responsible. It went to voicemail, and Alice hung up, not leaving one. She looked up at Jen, worried. “Maybe there’s no service where they’re at right now?” Alice looked to Jen for reassurance.

“Of course, love, I’m sure everything is fine.”

Jen took Alice into her arms and held her against her. She didn’t know what was going on, but her senses told her nothing good. The only person who ever saw this soft side of Jen was Alice, and she would deny she even had one until the day she died.

Wanda looked at Rio as Rio stared at her phone.

“You have to call her back. You know she’s going to worry, and she doesn’t even know what’s going on. Put it on the Bluetooth.” The redhead instructed.

Rio bit the inside of her bottom lip before replying.

“What if I’m right? What if she did know?”

“Then you have to talk through it, Rio. You can’t just let it fester.”

Wanda spoke from recent experience. Rio sighed and called back.



Alice jumped as her phone rang, and she removed herself from her wife’s arms, answering and putting the call on speaker. She heard the undeniable sounds that signaled car Bluetooth. Wanda was listening.

“Hey,” Alice answered, not sure what she should ask first.



Rio looked nervously at Wanda. “Hey, sorry, you popped up as a missed call. I guess we were going through a dead spot.” It was all she could say.



Alice looked at Jen, clearly confused. This was not like Rio. Alice decided to get straight to the point and sat down, Jen taking her place by her side, running her hand through Alice’s hair, calming her.

“We saw Nicky getting Raven. He said you and Wanda are headed up to the mountains. What’s going on, Ri?” Alice asked, her voice shaky.



Rio inhaled deeply through her nose and exhaled through her mouth.

“Wanda and I are heading to a cabin in the mountains so we can do some thinking,” She said slowly, not sure how much she should say.

Alice was even more confused. “What happened?”

“Last night at the reception, I heard a conversation someone was having and started looking for them. I found out who Agatha’s ex is, and it’s Lilia.” Rio waited for Alice’s reaction and reply. Just how much had Alice actually known?

Alice’s and Jen’s eyes went wide as they looked at each other. They had their suspicions, but to have it confirmed was something different entirely.

“Lilia!? Jesus! Well, that at least makes sense now that I think about it. Have you talked to Agatha? Is that why you two are headed to the mountains?”

Alice was starting to get a clearer picture, and she needed to know just how much Rio knew.

“Wanda did. I talked to Lilia. Alice, I need you to answer me honestly. Me. The person that may have withheld some things from you, but never lied. You always told me that Lorna had relapsed and overdosed. What really happened?”

Rio was blinking back tears as Wanda reached over and put a hand on her knee. They would be at the cabin soon and could debrief then.

Jen moved closer to Alice and wrapped an arm around her. She knew that Alice had known quite a bit about Lorna. She also knew that Alice had grown close with Lilia over the past ten years and had flown under the radar. It’s why they had their suspicions that something had happened between Agatha and Lilia. Alice had shown Jen the letter that Lorna had left her. Alice confided in Jen in a way that she couldn’t in Rio because Jen had signed the same NDAs, and Alice, the law-abiding citizen that she was, wouldn’t break that NDA. While Alice could have told Rio about Lorna, she didn’t want Rio to worry, especially with Alice working at SHIELD.

Now, it was coming back to bite her.

Just as Lilia had warned Alice.

Alice’s heart squeezed as a tear slid down her cheek.

“It sounds like you already know.”

Rio choked back a sob. “How long have you been keeping it from me?”



Alice leaned into Jen, needing the support. “Mom left me a letter and that envelope we kept in your safe. Agatha gave it to me at the funeral, along with Lilia’s information. I never did open the larger envelope, but I read the letter and called Agatha a few days after the funeral and talked to her. Mom put everything in that letter.”

“You knew for thirteen, no, fourteen, years, and never told me. Three of those years, you were still in San Francisco, and we taught at the same school Alice. I’m not having the rest of this conversation over the phone. It’s not fair to either of us. I love you, but we are not okay.”

Rio waited before she hung up. They had agreed a long time ago to never hang up on each other, especially when upset.

“I love you too, and that’s fair. I’ll be up there in a few days. Be careful.” With that, they hung up, and Alice slumped into Jen.

“What did I do?” Alice whispered.

Jen smoothed her hair and rubbed her back.

“What you thought was best, and you stuck to the law, no matter how many times Lilia told you to tell Rio everything anyway vaguely.”

Alice sat up to look at her wife.

“What do I do now?”

“What you always do. Seek Lilia’s guidance and then head up to face the music while I face whatever fresh hell is about to break out here.”

Jen knew that nothing good was about to come from whatever part Nat had played and just where Rio’s mind had gone when she found out about Lilia.

“How are you feeling?” Wanda asked Rio after the connection ended.

Rio was looking out of the windshield at the scenery, trying to process it.

“Fourteen years,” it was all she could say.

“I know how you feel,” Wanda said in solidarity.

“We should go snowboarding while we’re up here, or snowmobiling, definitely to a bar.” Rio was compartmentalizing but also trying to find something to distract both of them.

“We have no security detail, recognizable faces, and it’s a peak time of year.” Wanda grinned mischievously.

“Look up stuff for us to do. We’re going to have fun, and for once, I’ll be the one causing Jen’s blood pressure to rise.”

⭐⭐⭐

Rio set her green suitcase on the floor of what Lilia had called a cabin. Rio was expecting a log cabin with a fireplace, maybe two bedrooms, and a bathroom. This was more like a mountain retreat, complete with a hot tub and a game room.

“Wow, you guys call this a cabin?” Rio let out a low whistle.

She’d been a part of this bitch pack for seven months now and she still hadn’t gotten used to the magnitude of it all. Wanda smiled as she searched for something.

“It has wooden features, doesn’t it?”

She took a picture frame off the wall and opened a panel behind it, revealing a switchboard of some kind, and flipped several off. Rio’s eyes followed the woman as she closed the panel, replaced the picture, and continued on her search.

“What are you doing?” Rio finally asked as she watched Wanda find a screwdriver and head upstairs.

“Disabling the inside cameras. Of course, there aren’t any in private areas, and I’ll keep the outside ones enabled, but the inside ones go to an app on Lilia’s phone. We don’t need anyone to know what we’re up to. That’s exactly why she sent you, us, here. So she could keep her eyes and ears on us both. She forgets who raised me. I WIN LILIA!”

Wanda finished before heading up the stairs.

“Oh, go claim a room. Lilia has maintained this place and recently renovated it, but knowing her, the room I usually stayed in is still mine, and Agatha’s is still hers, up here, hall on the right, room on the left.”

Rio picked her suitcase back up and headed up the stairs behind Wanda. She might as well stay in Agatha’s room. She knew what was going to happen. She and Wanda would talk things through with each other, Agatha and Nat, maybe even Alice, would come up, and everyone would talk, and she would fall back into Agatha’s arms. That was the habit they were building, wasn’t it?

Agatha hadn’t done anything other than withholding information, which Rio had admittedly also done. It was just that Agatha kept doing it, and this was important for Rio to know. Especially since it affected her relationship with her best friend. Not to mention there were a lot of holes to fill in with the past now. She remembered every detail of those run-ins with Agatha all those years ago and the following mornings. What was the real story as to why she was there?

They could talk through that later. As for now, she walked into the room that Wanda had labeled as Agatha’s, and Rio agreed. It had been updated, but Lilia had kept the touches of Agatha here. Not as a shrine, but as a reminder of the times spent here, the times Rio assumed Agatha had spent here with Lorna trying to detox. Wanda had talked about their trips here, all those years ago. Of a time Agatha had broken her leg trying to show off for a hot ski instructor, Lorna bringing back a souvenir. The souvenir was a 6’3 blonde man who was visiting from Texas, and they dated for four months.

Rio looked around the room with its purple accents. A stuffed moose that looked old enough to have chronic back pain sat in the middle of the bed on the pillows. There was a picture on the nightstand of an extremely young Agatha - not more than twenty, with Lorna and Lilia on either side of her, leaning against a pool table. Agatha had a devilish smirk on her face as if she knew she had each woman wrapped around her little finger. Lorna had an arm wrapped around Agatha, with Lilia’s hand in hers. The way these women held themselves was as if they knew that their mere presence could cast a spell on someone, and they would immediately be at their service.

Looking around the room, Rio could see how much hope Lilia had that everything would eventually be reconciled, whether Rio was in the picture or not. She ran a hand through her hair and huffed. Rio knew how important found and chosen family was and how much she would put into reconciling with Alice, no matter how betrayed she felt at the moment.

“It’s a lot to take in, and the feelings are all over the place. I get it.” Wanda was leaning against the door frame. “So, snowmobiles? Bars? Tattoos? What reckless decisions should we make?”

“Yes.” Rio turned to her suitcase. “Let me find something more weather-appropriate. Have you told Nat where you are?”

“No. She can track me if she’s so concerned,” Wanda said curtly as she pushed off the doorframe and went back to her room to change.

“You’re not wearing your necklace!” Rio called after her.

“She’s a big girl, she can figure it out,” Wanda called back.

It was late afternoon when they arrived at the snowmobile rental place. Wanda looked over at Rio.

“Have you ever driven one of these?”

“No, but Taylor and Harry did it. How hard can it be?”

“They also got into an accident and almost died.”

Wanda hadn’t done this in years, and the last time she had, she very narrowly missed a tree.

“Well, let’s not do that.” Rio looked at her as if it was a simple task.

“Well, if we die, Jen can’t be mad, right?”

“Exactly,” Rio confirmed with a nod.

While they had attempted to disguise themselves, they had done so poorly as people near them were starting to whisper and take pictures. Wanda looked delighted. “You wanted this to happen, didn’t you?” Rio whispered out of the side of her mouth.
