Agatha was sitting cross-legged on the couch, arms wrapped around her stomach as if she were trying to hold herself together. She was trying to keep herself and her world from falling apart.

Wanda was tired of crying, and she knew everyone else in this house was too. Yet, she felt the all-too-familiar sting at the edges of her eyes creeping up on her. She knew Agatha was trying to protect them, she always was, but she needed confirmation of something.

“Agatha, why didn’t you want to tell me?” Wanda barely spoke above a whisper.

Agatha knew she had to be honest, and she hated it.

“Wanda, everyone knows you can’t keep a secret.” Agatha looked at her as if saying the words caused her pain. Wanda recoiled as if Agatha had slapped her. She closed her eyes as a tear slid out.

“Agatha,” Wanda’s voice wavered. “When have I EVER let one of YOUR secrets slip to anyone that wasn’t Nat?” Wanda’s voice trembled as she blinked her eyes open, trying to fight back the tears.

Agatha furrowed her brows as she thought about it before it hit her.

“Never.”

Wanda was not the best at keeping secrets, but when it came to Agatha? She only told Nat. Wanda had once told Agatha that she could trust her with anything. She had meant it with her entire being.

“Not. Once.” Wanda replied, voice hardening.

She turned her head sharply, looking at Nat. “You knew that! Just like with you! I tell the two of you everything, yes, so I can get it out, but that’s it. All other secrets are fair game. The two of you? Absolutely not.

“Agatha, you are my best friend, my big sister, the person who will and has done anything and everything for me. I share almost everything with you. I would never do anything to hurt you, and then you betrayed me. I know it’s how we grew up and how we were raised, but it’s up to us to change that. To stop it. It’s not going to be easy, Agatha, but how many more of us do you have to hurt to realize what you’re doing?” Wanda was pleading for Agatha to hear her. Agatha had her hand to her mouth, genuinely listening to what Wanda was saying. Wanda had never spilled one of Agatha’s secrets, yet she had been adamant about not sharing with her. She had betrayed and hurt Wanda. Keeping things from her and causing herself to deal with so much pain on her own.

Wanda was so goodhearted. So many bad things had happened to her, yet she still saw and found the good in life and people. Still defended Agatha no matter what she did. Bad ‘boy’ Agatha Harkness and girl-next-door Wanda Maximoff. Sure, Hollywood had played off of their dynamic for decades, and it was a classic buddy comedy trope, but it worked for them. She knew that the past really would have been different if she had taken the kid gloves off earlier and shared more with her earlier.

Agatha looked up and locked eyes with Wanda.

“You’re right.”

Wanda looked shocked. “Someone record this.” She said, genuinely surprised at the omission.

“I mean it, Wanda. I did become two separate people, and I hate it. I have been doing a lot of thinking. I do need Lilia back, we all do, and she and I have done a lot of talking already.”

“She brought the chair back out,” Nat interrupted. She had moved to Wanda’s side and was holding her hand. Wanda had allowed it and smiled. They would be perfectly okay.

Agatha nodded.

“I didn’t know she had put it up, obviously. It was there when I went over late Saturday Night, early Sunday Morning. Anyway, I don’t want to be that person anymore, and I’m working on it. You all deserve better. Our sons deserve better. Alice deserves better, Rio, that beautiful woman who has somehow not run away screaming, my future if she’ll still have me? Deserves the world. I’m lucky enough to have people around me who care and will call me on my shit, I fucking love you, Wanda. I am so sorry for being overprotective, and I will step back. I can’t promise to never be a little too overprotective, but I’ll work on it. I’m also working on the whole secrecy thing. I know sorry isn’t enough, but I am so sorry, kid.”

Agatha had stood at some point to walk and gesture while she talked. She had come to a stop, hands stopped mid-gesture.

“Oh! And please forgive Nat. She wanted to tell you she loves you more than anything, and I saw her full-body sob, and it was absolutely heartbreaking and terrifying, frankly.” Agatha added.

Wanda turned her head to look at Nat, “You full-body sobbed? Why, my love?”

Nat brushed Wanda’s hair behind her ear and locked eyes with her.

“You’re the sunshine in my day, Wanda. Do you know what happens to Russian assassins when they fail a mission and are compromised? They’re permanently retired. I saw you and immediately risked it all. The shady bitch over there pulled some major strings for everything to work out the way it did because she saw something spark between us. I’ve loved you since we were eighteen in a back alley, Wanda, as sappy and awful as that sounds. To lose you a second time after finally being able to have you fully? I couldn’t handle it. So yes, for the second time in my life, I was full-body sobbing on Lilia Calderu’s couch as Lilia held me. Before you ask, the first time was in the same spot, with the same woman, plus Lorna, because I thought I was losing you the first time.”

Nat was caressing Wanda’s cheek. She had never told Wanda that she had struggled with the thought of losing her when Wanda fell for Vision. Agatha stood there listening to Nat, wiping tears away from the corners of her eyes. She would never forget that night. She knew exactly what would happen to Nat after Agatha escaped their encounter unscathed. Thankfully, Agatha knew a guy, or, well, Lilia knew a guy. That was also how the deal was made involving Agatha’s mother, and they had themselves a Nat.

Wanda was fully crying as she leaned forward and kissed her fiancée.

“It took me two years to figure out how I felt about you. I had you for five years before I married Vision, and then I was at arm’s length for twenty. We are getting married in a few months, Natasha. I am not losing you over a secret my stupid sister made you keep, okay?”

Nat nodded, sniffling slightly. “Okay. Te iubesc.” Nat leaned forward and kissed her again.

Wanda grinned. “Ya tebya lyublyu.”

Agatha wiped away more tears. When had she become so sentimental?

“Hi, stupid sister here. Am I forgiven?” she asked, knowing she probably was, but asking anyway.

“Get over here,” Wanda waved her arm.

Agatha sat next to Wanda and hugged her. “I’m so sorry, honey. I didn’t even realize what I’d been doing.”

Wanda tucked Agatha’s head under her chin.

“Aggie, I fucking love you too. Stop lying to your therapist and authentically work on the things you need to work on. That woman in there wants you to be her future, and I want that for both of you. I also want a niece, and you’re getting old.” Wanda smiled as Agatha pulled back to glare. “You have to start being honest with us and stop telling half-truths. I’m not saying she’s perfect. She’s not, but she’s perfect for you.”

They turned toward the kitchen as the sound of giggling floated in.

“Looks like you’re up, slugger,” Wanda said.

“If you children are done, then come in here,” Nat called.



⭐⭐⭐

Rio and Alice walked in, leaning against each other and laughing over something. Rio’s laughter immediately died upon seeing Agatha. She hadn’t stopped because she was still angry, she was fighting her instincts to run to her and tell her all was forgiven.

No, they needed to genuinely talk.

Agatha pulled away from Wanda as Wanda and Nat stood.

“Alice, come with us, we’re going to play pool.” Wanda gave Alice a look that told her she didn’t have a choice, so they left the room.

Rio looked at Agatha, hugging herself, the look of hurt she had worn so many times over the last forty-eight hours returning.

Agatha stood up, walking to Rio. She reached out and lightly placed her hands on her arms, waiting for resistance.

There was none.

Rio stepped into Agatha, letting Agatha wrap her arms around her.

“I understand you’re angry,” Agatha started.

“I’m not angry,” Rio murmured. “I’m hurt, Agatha.” She stepped out of the embrace and took a seat, still hugging herself.

“I accepted that your ex was someone you occasionally worked with, someone I might meet in passing, someone in your orbit. But finding out it’s Lilia? That’s really something I needed to know. I know you didn’t want to change how I saw her, but Agatha. Don’t you think I would have found out eventually? What was going to happen the longer this went on? It would have been even worse. The lies, the omissions, they have to stop.

“Then, I need you to start telling me more about the past. I had no idea just how involved Lorna and Lilia were in your life. I just read ten letters to Alice from Lorna. Lorna telling Alice to give you a chance, to form a relationship with Lilia. Aside from Alice and Norma, you and Lilia were the best parts of her life, and you and Lilia convinced her to keep Alice. Thank you for that, by the way.

“It was just so much. I know I don’t need to know everything, but it also explains a lot about why you and Nicky get away with murder. Plus, she’s my boss, and I trusted her. I was forming a friendship with her. I respected her, Agatha!”

Rio’s thoughts were everywhere, and the more she looked at Agatha, the more she wanted to kiss her and have Agatha tell her everything was okay. Agatha sat beside her.

“You’re right, and yes, I’ll record it for you if you want me to. Wanda and Nat gave me that same look. You have every reason to be hurt and I should have told you who it was, as it does have a major effect on our lives. You can still have a friendship with her and respect her, Rio. I understand the emotions are very conflicting right now, but you’ve read the letters and know how big a part of my life she played, other than that. I’ve spent a lot of time over there the past forty-eight hours, and fences are being mended. If you’re not okay with that, then I’ll stay at arm’s length.

“This isn’t about that right now. This is about us. Rio, I want there to be an us. For a very long time, and for that to work, we have to be open and honest with each other, I agree. Wanda says I have to stop lying to my therapist and start working on some other things.”

“Wanda’s right,” Rio interjected.

“She has an uncanny ability for that.” Agatha smiled. “Rio, I don’t want to fight, and I don’t want to argue. Over the past few days, I realized that I also became two separate people, like Lilia did. I know I need to work on myself, and I plan on doing that. I also know we need to work on our communication. I don’t want to watch Heartbreak Karaoke featuring you and Wanda on a grainy live stream every time we don’t communicate. However, it was highly entertaining, and now I do want to go to karaoke with you.

“I digress. I want you to read the letters I have from Lorna. I want to be more open with you, and I want you to be more open with me. I love you, and I want this to work, whatever it takes.”

Agatha had never been much of a crier off-screen, yet these past few months had been brutal, and she blamed menopause. Rio huffed.

“How do you do that? Completely disarm me to where I can’t be mad at you if I wanted to be? I’m still slightly wounded, but not nearly as much as I should be.”

Agatha smirked. “I came by it honestly.”

She had done the same thing to Rio that Lilia had done to her. She hadn’t meant to, it just happened. Now that she thought about it, Lorna had the same ability.

“I have a feeling you learned it from the women who raised you.” Rio’s eyes narrowed.

“I learned a lot from them. They raised me. Back to us, I know we still have some things to work out and work through, as well as relationship dynamics to navigate. Are we okay for now while we build this to what we want it to be?” Agatha cupped Rio’s cheek and caressed it with her thumb.

“Agatha Harkness, where is this coming from all of a sudden?” Rio was slightly taken aback at the sudden relationship skills her girlfriend had.

“Would you believe me if I said my son?” Agatha winced slightly.

“Yes, actually.” Rio was not surprised in the slightest.

“He recommended a podcast. The advice was not from him,” Agatha clarified.

“I’m slightly disappointed in our son now,” Rio smirked.

Agatha smiled at hearing Rio saying ‘our son’ again.

“In case you hadn’t gathered, we’re okay for now. I love you.”

Rio leaned in and kissed her, running a hand through Agatha’s hair. She could work with this. They could work through these things and navigate whatever came at them. Agatha placed her hand on the back of Rio’s head, only for Rio to hiss in pain and pull back. Her head was still tender, and the Tylenol from earlier was wearing off.

Agatha looked at her, worried. Why was she hurt?

“Rio, what’s wrong?” Her eyes scanned over Rio, noticing a few light bruises.

“I flew off of a snowmobile yesterday,” Rio admitted.

“What? Why didn’t you tell me? Wait, why were you drinking last night? We need to get you checked out, baby. I am so sorry. Does anything feel broken?”

Agatha’s mind was racing. Rio had gotten injured almost a year after the last time she had been seriously injured. Rio started to laugh.

“Aggie, I’m fine. I have a mild concussion, and I’m banged up. We went to the first aid station. Very nice woman.”

Agatha was still looking at her in horror. “I saw you on live streams! You were completely plastered! That’s not good!”

Wanda walked back in upon hearing Agatha panicking.

“We were at Sharon Davis-Hart’s bar, Agatha. Remember her? Anyway, she did a good job of making us think we were drunker than we were. She kept an eye on Rio and made sure she was fine.” Wanda sat on a couch catty-corner to the one Agatha and Rio were on.

“Lilia,” Agatha stated. “Of course, she called Sharon. Well, still, that means it’s time for you to go to bed and rest your head.” Agatha was still concerned. She had read several studies and articles from medical journals about concussions when Nicky started playing football and did not play around with head injuries. Agatha helped Rio up, and they said their goodnights, walking up to Agatha’s old room.

Nat and Alice had taken their bags up to their rooms. Agatha stepped into the room and passed echoes of herself through the ages. Teenage Agatha getting ready to go skiing, teenage Agatha making out with someone, teenage Agatha dancing around with Lorna and Lilia, young adult Agatha with a broken leg, laughing with Wanda, young adult Agatha dancing around and drinking with Wanda, Nat, Lilia, and Lorna.

She had seen these memories playing out like old film reels downstairs, but she hadn’t expected it as much here. They all faded away when she realized Rio was in front of her.

“Agatha, are you okay?” Rio asked, wrapping her arms around Agatha.

“Yes, just remembering the times I spent here is all. And, at least the room’s decor has been updated. I was serious about sleep and no strenuous activities.”

Rio pouted.

Agatha got right next to Rio’s ear, her breath hot. “Once you’re cleared, we can be as strenuous as you want,” she purred before stepping back to change for bed.

⭐⭐⭐

Tuesday morning had begun with Jen calling Agatha to get all of them to the nearest TV in the next fifteen minutes. She woke Rio and pounded on two bedroom doors. Ten minutes later, they were waiting for the news as Jen had instructed. Coffee was brewing in the background and they would eat after they watched whatever it was they were supposed to watch.

Breaking news music played, and the graphic flashed across the screen as the announcer came on the screen.

“Lou Falco, famed supermodel and ex-wife of former NFL quarterback Shane Falco was found deceased at the infamous Island psychiatric prison complex early this morning…”

She didn’t know if someone had muted the TV, but Rio didn’t hear another word, and her body numbed at the words. She wasn’t sure how to feel. She didn’t want to be happy that someone was dead, but she was relieved to know she was finally safe, and that slightly concerned her.

It was how she felt when her parents died. However, back then, she was very young. That she didn’t find it concerning.

The touch of Wanda’s hand brought Rio out of her spiraling thoughts.

“It doesn’t make you an awful person to feel safe,” Wanda said, leaning over the couch and rubbing Rio’s arms. “It makes you human, and that’s okay.”

“I just... three kids lost their mother,” Rio said, voice barely above a whisper.

Alice reached out and put her hand on Rio’s knee.

“Ri, those babies haven’t had a mother in years. You even told me that when the oldest left for college, she went no contact and only talked to you.”

Rio nodded as Agatha sat beside her and wrapped her arms around her, kissing her cheek.

“I love you. You’re one of the strongest women I know, and I am here for whatever you need.”

Rio knew she was surrounded by love, and yet again, it was comforting.

It was definitely time to get back to therapy, though.

Agatha was projecting all the warmth she could onto Rio. Inside, she had chilled to the core. Jen had known, what else had been set into motion? Agatha thought of the Russian assassin. She wasn’t sure how or when Nat had done it, possibly pulling some strings and making some calls. She never questioned Nat’s methods in all of these years, she was just glad everyone was safe. But, the ruthlessness and reach sometimes surprised her, and Agatha worked to keep it all to herself.

Nat continued to read the words that were scrolling across the screen. She had sent a few details here and there, but just like always, she hadn’t done the dirty work. Not enough to get into any serious legal trouble.

She hadn’t gotten her hands bloody in that way since Agatha had made her a part of their ragtag family twenty-seven years ago. Fights, framing, blackmail, and threats to make people disappear. Yet, she was never the one who followed through on that, and the women always dismissed her denials as necessary theater and plausible deniability. But Nat knew that was untrue.

Someone else just always got there first.

Chapter End Notes

Sharon!

I thought things would be more explosive, but I have no control anymore and this is where it went. I am literally the author and I control nothing.

The next chapter might be the last, might not. I don't know anymore.
What I do know is I have ideas for a sequel and so many one-shots, a series of Lorna's letters, the Nat and Wanda story, just so many things. Make sure to subscribe to the series! I've given over 200k words and I have many more to give!

Get Your Shit Together, So I Can Love You

Chapter Summary

Things start to settle down with the coven and Wanda talks to Lilia.

Chapter Notes

It's a shorter chapter.
Warnings:
Underage drinking alluded to, other illegal activities. Like we do.
Smut!

Again, thank you so much for all the love! Make sure to subscribe to the series!
There really is only one more chapter for this part!

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Hours after the news broke, Rio sat on the couch, scrolling through social media. The internet was buzzing with news that Agatha and Wanda were not at their respective sets, videos of drunken, angry karaoke, pictures of someone that looked like Rio coming out of the small clinic on the mountain, and just pictures of WandRio in general.

They were sticking with the nickname to everyone's annoyance.

Alice had been texting back and forth with Jen all Tuesday morning, especially after the news of Lou’s demise had broken. Jen was trying to make sure no connections were made. She had already intercepted a picture of Rio from the previous school, coaching one of Lou’s daughters, hoping nothing else leaked. Combined with speculation from the internet, Jen was trying to put out fires everywhere and was drafting a statement to put out just in case.

The news had said that the supermodel succumbed to natural causes, and an autopsy would be performed. The woman had only been fifty-five, and Rio wondered what natural causes people would believe. She tried to focus on her mindless scrolling and read various things about her and her friends.

I hope Rio is okay after whatever happened while they were snowmobiling!

So are they on a girl’s trip (friend) or a girl’s trip (forget our wives)?

Neither of them has a wife…

Yet

Wanda still has her engagement ring.

I'm pretty sure it’s a friend trip.

They are so bestie coded.



Rio and Wanda singing Father Figure? Your honor, I am gay.

I just want older women to do unspeakable things.

I always pictured Agatha with this song, but this performance has clearly changed my

mind.

Where is Agatha? Why isn’t she there?

Wherever she is and whatever she did, surely she regrets her life choices.

I would just like to thank all of the people who live-streamed this for HOURS and then uploaded the videos for us.

You know that bar did great that night.

Where is the security detail? My friend was there and said it was just the two of them. Something is going on.

While I hope everything is fine, I’m glad it gave us THIS.

It isn’t safe for them to be in public without security. Wanda is too famous.

Wanda looks like she can protect herself.

Rio doesn’t exactly look like a damsel in distress herself.

Some of these songs sound personal. Extremely personal.

Trouble in paradise?

Breakup announcement incoming? In which case, Agatha, I am here for a rebound.

Dude, no.

Guys, these songs are getting dark, and they are singing with their whole chests.

They’re drunk. Wanda is baby Gen-X and Rio is a solid millennial, dating a Gen-Xer.

Of course, these are the songs they’re singing.

Surely everything is fine.

I still find it odd that they aren’t at the place Wanda and Agatha share in Lake

Tahoe.

Do you know where all their houses are?

Doesn’t everyone?

Rio made a mental note to check just how much information was on the internet and out there for the public. Maybe she could talk to Jen about that. She looked at what was currently trending, and her stomach flipped.

RIP Lou Falco, you were so amazing and such a great role model!

All of the charity work she did? She was such a great woman!

I feel so badly for her children!

Are y’all forgetting that she was also a felon?

Because she was such an intelligent woman! Look how long she got away with it!

Computer smarts, creativity, sneaky, seriously, perfect woman!

You people are weird.

I feel so badly for her kids! They already had to see her go through that awful witchhunt of a trial, and then she was murdered!

She wasn’t murdered! She died of natural causes.

Please! At that place? At 55? She was murdered!

Oh please. I doubt it. Don’t be disrespectful with your conspiracy theories!

Rio paused. It’s what she had been worried about. There were always conspiracy theories when these things happened, especially when high-profile people died while incarcerated in any type of way. This was not good, and while their relationship had been buried and those directly around the woman had signed NDAs, the school itself had not required them as SHIELD did.

This did not bode well for anyone.

“Why do people keep saying nice things about this horrid woman?” Nat spat, tossing down her phone.

“Can you wipe it all?” Wanda looked up at her from where she was lying in her lap. The two had been even more tactile than usual since apologizing.

Nat smiled down at her future wife, “No, iubirea mea, that would look highly suspicious. Plus, that would be more of a Darcy-level task.”

“So? I’m sure everyone would blame the ex-husband.” Wanda’s gaze traveled to Rio. “How are you doing?”

“Trying not to vomit. I’m going to stay off social media for a few days. I can’t handle all of this, and my head still hurts. I wonder if Sharon will write a note so I can say this was mandated time off.” Rio wondered out loud, her head still throbbing, though she didn’t like to admit it.

“She already did, though Lilia had no problem with you taking time in the first place.” Agatha walked into the room and took a seat next to Rio. Agatha handed her the next dose of pain meds, and Rio took them gratefully, curling into Agatha after swallowing them with water.

Alice had been on the other side of Rio and sighed. “It’s not for Lilia. It’s so the Fencing Club doesn’t bully her about being the principal’s pet because she’s sleeping with you.”

Agatha, Wanda, and Nat all turned to look at Alice with shocked expressions.

Agatha was the first to speak. “What? How do you know about the fencing club?”

“Because I work there and have ears, Agatha. It’s not all of them, obviously, but it’s led by one or two of the more jealous types. Then the sorry bitches who just tag along to be cool and enjoy the commiseration of being among the many rejected by THE Agatha Harkness. Lilia can’t just fire them, though she wants to. Several teachers, other than myself, have come to her defense, notably Rogers and Carter.” Alice’s frown deepened. “Lilia won’t let me punch the smirks off their faces.”

“Are you in high school or teaching high school?” Nat’s eyebrows formed a question mark.

Rio laughed dryly.

“It’s the same thing. English departments are the absolute worst, though. High School teachers are just as clique-ish as the kids.”

“So all of the women you slept with at SHIELD call themselves the fencing club after your tattoo?” Wanda asked incredulously.

“That’s high school for you,” Agatha confirmed.

“I am so glad that part of your life is over. One less thing for Jen to worry about.”

Wanda had repositioned herself and was now leaning her head against Nat’s shoulder, her legs draped over Nat’s lap, with Nat absentmindedly running her hand up and down Wanda’s back, looking at Wanda like she was the only woman that existed.

Seeing the two of them in this state, Agatha would never know that Nat had been somehow responsible for handing out consequences to some very terrible people over the years. It still blew Agatha’s mind how Nat could compartmentalize so well and easily slip back into that role.

Nat’s insistence that she hadn’t been the cause of all those unfortunate events was starting to wear at Agatha’s assurance that it was Nat. Something had started to poke at her mind and memories, and it was something she would have to explore later. Right now, Agatha was focused on her happiness for Wanda and relief that Wanda had been more upset than angry.

“Speaking of my…our incredible fixer,” Agatha started and winced. The words ‘future sister-in-law’ had threatened to spill out, but she had thankfully been able to stop them. Seeing the smirk on Wanda’s face, however, told her that someone had noticed. Had she really begun to think of Alice as her sister by proxy of Rio?

“Jen has had to handle several things already. It doesn’t look good when you leave a set and don’t come back or don’t show up to one.” Agatha cut her eyes to Wanda. “There’s also the speculation about the weekend shenanigans, my son’s posts about catsitting Raven, as the cat has a cult following, Vision was pictured on a date with Herb, and then she’s having to closely monitor any leaks about any connection to the wicked witch. It’s too late in the afternoon to drive back today, but we have to head back tomorrow and get back to everything. Alice just got married. We can’t make her a widow already because Jen had a heart attack.” Agatha smiled over Rio’s head at Alice.

“Just make sure Jen gets a decent Christmas bonus,” Alice responded, shaking her head as she texted calming affirmations to her wife, who was currently ranting about the disease that was social media.

“Consider your house paid for,” Agatha countered, nonverbally discussing it with Wanda.

“I was joking.” Alice rolled her eyes.

“We’re not,” Wanda added. “We haven’t put her through this much in a while, and if anyone does make the Rio/Super Psycho connection, it’s going to get worse.”

Rio had dozed off, leaning against Agatha. She knew that some of this was her fault. She hadn’t communicated, hadn’t talked rationally about anything, just grabbed Wanda and ran away. She was also the reason for the SF complication. Well, that was partly Lilia, wasn’t it? She had brought her out here after all. Hadn’t she?

Her head was swimming, and she was tired. It was mid-afternoon. They could go back now.

Rio sat up and yawned. “We could go back now,” She said groggily, stretching her arms above her head. It was Nat who spoke first.

“No, the damage has been done, and while it’s not that late, it will look better if we stay a bit longer in case the connection is made. It would be good for us to be seen around town this evening.”

Nat spoke from experience, and while people wouldn’t necessarily suspect Agatha or any of their family if any of the stalking or connections came to light, it would still be better to establish alibis. As it was, Nat wouldn’t be surprised if they were questioned depending on the autopsy reports. Nat hadn’t spoken with Shane and didn’t know who had pulled off the hit. The restraining order and court appearance were enough that if Nat were a detective on the case, she would come sniffing around asking questions of the coven at least. Nat began to wonder if there was something or someone else at play. This wasn’t the first time that Nat had this nagging suspicion. To this day, she hadn’t figured out what befell the stalker who came after Wanda and the boys all those years ago. While Nat had let almost everyone assume it had been her doing, she had been clear with Wanda that that wasn’t a Nat operation. Still, no one asked questions and the event just faded into the background.

“I agree with redheaded demon number two,” Agatha was saying. “How are you feeling?” Agatha looked at Rio, worried.

“I’d like to take an actual nap if that’s okay. Sorry.” Rio yawned. Although her head hurt, this did not feel at all like the concussion from last year. Rio reluctantly agreed with Agatha and conceded to a nap.

“As long as most of us are seen, it should be okay.” Agatha nodded, forehead wrinkled with worry. She looked over at Alice. “Are you coming, Mrs. Wu? Or is it Mrs. Kale?” She asked with a smile. Lorna would have been so happy for all of them, and Agatha had to swallow the lump in her throat.

No one had called her Mrs. yet, let alone asked which last name she was taking. Alice felt her cheeks redden as she sunk into the couch.

“We haven’t settled on last names yet and may just keep ours. We thought of hyphenating, but both Wu-Kale and Kale-Wu sounded off to us, and I really want to keep Wu. I’ll drop Gulliver, but I’m not losing Wu,” Alice and Jen had done this back and forth for almost three months now. Jen didn’t want to lose Kale for business reasons, and Alice had finally started feeling a connection to what her last name truly meant. They had their disagreements, but this had been the longest-running one.

“Please go with Wu-Kale and adopt a few dogs so I can call them the Wu-Kale clan,” Rio looked at her with pleading eyes.

Alice failed to suppress her laugh. “Absolutely not, Method Man, go to bed.” She looked at Agatha. “As for the original question, I think I’ll stay here and keep an eye on this one.” She pointed at Rio, who was standing to go upstairs.

“Good,” Rio grabbed Alice’s hand and tugged. “Come tell me about the decade of your life I wasn’t here for,’’ Rio bent back down to give Agatha a quick kiss.

“Please be very careful. You don’t have a full security detail. Don’t do anything Wanda and I would do. Te amo.”

Rio turned quickly and disappeared upstairs with Alice.

Agatha watched her go and groaned. “That woman is going to be the death of me.”

⭐⭐⭐

Jen swore out loud as she hung up the phone and put her head in her hands, leaning back in her office chair. They were a few days away from the big move and her office provided the calm she needed, whereas the apartment was a mess of boxes and bubble wrap.

She had completed damage control with two separate directors. One for Agatha’s TV show, and the other for Wanda’s movie. They had both continued filming scenes that didn’t include the two stars, and as long as they were at their respective studios on Thursday morning, everything would be fine. Wanda’s director, Maxwell, understood immediately and was very understanding. Wanda didn’t disappear or pull stunts. That was always Agatha’s department. Agatha’s director, Dottie, had been miffed, but once Jen explained that it was a matter of love, she softened.

Jen knew everyone’s weaknesses.

No, it was not the directors that had Jen exasperated and anxious. It was Disney and Marvel. Fittings and table reads were scheduled to start soon, and they were not happy with two of their stars splashed all over the internet. Especially when their very wholesome star was now very suggestively participating in drunken karaoke.

No, this was not good, and Jen had to do some serious explaining. Thankfully, just mentioning a particular lawyer’s name was enough, but Jen certainly did not want to call him. Team Wagatha had a team of lawyers. There were the lawyers for the more mundane things like Wanda’s divorce, contract negotiations, real estate, and NDAs. Then, they had their very special lawyer. Jen was certain the man was ageless. He kept everyone out of prison, dealt with anything that could go awry, such as breach of contracts, and expedited citizenships. He was the kind of lawyer that people were afraid of because he wasn’t crooked. He was just that good and terrifying.

The minute Jen said, “I can always send their contracts over to their legal team. I’m sure the head of their team, Mr. Fury, would be happy to speak to you,” The executive on the other end of the line had gone silent. Breathing was the only thing Jen heard. “Oh? So you have heard of him? I can have,”

The executive cut her off. “No, Mrs. Kale, that won’t be necessary. Just make sure your clients are where they need to be when they need to be there.”

The line had gone dead immediately.

Now, Jen had to let Fury know, and she did not want to contact him. Contacting him about these types of things showed weakness, and she hated that. She reached for her phone and opted to text.

Me:

I had to mention you

to Disney/Marvel. It

shouldn’t become a thing.

Man:

I thought you might have to.

I’m always watching.

Man:

You could have reached out.

Before now.

You looked beautiful.



Jen inhaled sharply. She hadn’t seen him at the reception though that didn’t mean he hadn’t been there. He was friends with Tony, another client. Tony had probably told him as he was one of the few people who knew of her connection with the powerful attorney.

Me:

You weren’t invited.

Man:

I was, just not by you.

Me:

Who?

Man:

Several people that

love and care for you.

Jen made a mental note to put her walls back in place and interrogate Alice. Love had made her too soft. She was finished with this conversation, and she knew the longer she stayed here, the likelier it was for him to track her location and show up. Just because they had set boundaries didn’t mean he wouldn’t cross them. She slipped her laptop into her bag, turned off the lights, and locked up. Once in her car, she rechecked her phone just to see if Fury had gotten the hint that she was finished talking with him.

He had not.

Man:

She’s a great girl, Jennifer.

I knew her mother.

Me:

I know.

It’s part of why I

have this job.

Agatha told her otherwise constantly, though Jen knew that in the beginning, his connection to this particular trio was precisely what landed her the job. Hollywood was filled with nepotism in all industries, and she hated being one of them, even if only a small number of people knew. She knew that Agatha wouldn’t take just anyone to be her sole representation for everything, but she still did everything she could and worked as hard as she could to prove to Agatha that she was more than her genetic makeup.

It was also why she had been so insistent on not dating Alice. She didn’t want Fury to take credit for everything she had in her life. She tossed her phone into the passenger seat and drove back to the apartment.

He’d probably take credit for the house on the compound, too.

Man:

I’m proud of you.

I mean it.

Jen sat on the couch in the apartment, surrounded by boxes. She couldn’t wait to get everything into the new house on Saturday. The last message had irritated her. Where was that the last forty years? No. She didn’t have time for this. She was done with this conversation.

She pulled out her laptop and began working on a statement for People.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha sat in a booth at Sharon’s bar, drinking water, knowing that this wasn’t the time for alcohol, reaffirmed by the all-knowing look from Wanda as the thought crossed Agath’as mind. They had eaten dinner, wandered the area, and been highly visible. The way her notifications were going off told her it was working.

“Have you two set a date?” Agatha asked, trying to be happy for them but completely focused on her relationship.

“Not yet. The divorce isn’t finalized until June twenty-sixth,” Nat answered, drinking some type of stout.

“The wedding is June twenty-eighth,” Wanda said decidedly. “It’s the last Saturday of Pride. We can make sure to be home from filming for a few days. I’ve already checked the filming schedules and with the director. We’ll have a small wedding. I was thinking of either the beach house or the compound.”

For once, Nat had nothing to say. She just sat there slack-jawed and stunned. This was a side of Wanda she hadn’t seen. Her first wedding to Vis had been a production, and Wanda had been indecisive about every little detail, down to her shoes. Nat looked at Agatha, who seemed more attentive now, before turning to her equal half and putting her hand on Wanda’s knee.

“Why the rush?”

Wanda looked down at her hands where she was picking at her cuticles, an old habit she occasionally reverted to.

“With the recent revelations and my self-reflections, I don’t want to spend any more time as anything but your wife, Nat. Girlfriend, partner, fiancée, that’s not what I want to be. I’m more sure about this than I was about having kids. There are still days I’m not so sure about them.” She turned and smirked at Nat. “Besides, what better way to end Pride month than with a lesbian wedding?” Wanda leaned over and quickly pressed her lips against Nat’s in a chaste kiss. “Please?”

Nat pressed her forehead against Wanda’s. “I will never tire of hearing you refer to yourself as my wife, and we’re not even married yet. Yes, June twenty-eighth, whenever and wherever you want.”

Nat kissed Wanda again, sliding her hand into Wanda’s hair to deepen the kiss. Agatha loudly cleared her throat.

“Hey kids, this is a very public bar, and your pictures and videos are already being posted on the internet. I already have two texts from Jen that say, “Oh God, make it stop,’ and ‘kill them now’”

Wanda finally pulled back, laughing. “Tell Jen it’s my turn to be a menace.”

Agatha pressed stop and sent the video. “You told her yourself.”

Agatha stuck her tongue out and did a little dance on her side of the booth as if bragging. Wanda just shook her head. She was glad that they were acting like them again. She took a drink of the fruity concoction the bartender had made for her before looking back at her sister.

“So when are you proposing?”

Agatha choked on air.

“Excuse me? It hasn’t even been a year!” Agatha exclaimed after she recovered.

Wanda shrugged. “Okay, and? Get it together, Harkness.”

“Not happening. I am, however, officially asking her to move in. We have discussed that at least, so I’m going to make it official.”

Agatha was proud of herself. It was Wanda’s turn to choke on something., “What? When?” She spluttered as Nat just smirked.

“Are you really surprised? They’re always over at each other’s places anyway, and with Alice moving to the compound this weekend, they can still carpool.” Nat commented.

Agatha rolled her eyes at Nat before answering Wanda. “It would be in June, so, you know, all the gayness during Pride. I want to ask her on my birthday.”

“That’s on Friday. That’s THIS Friday!” If Wanda hadn’t been in a booth, she would have fallen off her chair.

“I know when my birthday is.”

“Nicky’s going to be so excited. He loves that cat,” Nat smiled, leaving the unspoken “and Rio” in the air.

“I’ve never lived with a partner before,” Agatha realized out loud.

Lilia had always shot that request down, no matter how many times Agatha brought it up. Agatha had lived with Wanda and Nat, and Lorna had lived with her a few times in between men before she stopped marrying them and finally bought her own place and just dated. She had shared places with other actresses while filming, but this would be a first for her. Agatha started laughing.

“I am nearly fifty-two and have done things in the strangest order my whole life. I divorced my mother and struck out on my own at 16. I technically adopted a teenager at twenty-two,”

“Excuse you, a sibling,” Wanda interjected.

“She did raise you,” Nat pointed out.

“Shut up,” Wanda retorted.

“I was in the world’s most messed up relationship for ten years, had a kid on my own, my real mother was only thirteen years older than me and had a child that I took in later, I adopted the person hired to kill me, and now she’s marrying the other woman I raised, I fell in love with the best friend of another child I took in…” Agatha stopped as she snorted. “Oh my God, it’s this entire universe. I should chart it. I’m like the sun.”

Wanda just shook her head. “It’s like you’re drunk on your own absurdity. We’re taking you home.”

Agatha raised her head and looked around. “Wait, where’s Sharon?”

As if summoned, the woman appeared beside their table.

“I’m just dropping by for a second. I was told you were here and wanted to check-in. Is everything okay?” Sharon was eyeing Agatha.

Wanda nodded. “We were just about to leave and get back to Rio and Alice.” Wanda and Nat slipped from the booth and hugged the older woman, sensing that Agatha and Sharon needed to talk for a few minutes.

“Everything okay with you and Rio?” Sharon asked as Agatha stood, and the two whispered together conspiratorially.

“It will be,” she stopped next to the door and turned to face Sharon, looking down at the shorter woman. “Thank you for watching over them and taking care of her.”

“You’ll always be that sixteen-year-old kid causing trouble, Aggie. Global award-winning star or not, and Lilia asked me to keep an eye out.”

Agatha’s smile softened. “How are things going between you two?” she gave Sharon a knowing look.

Sharon gave a sly smile. “How did you know?”

“A hunch, and I know Lilia,” Well, I did. Agatha shrugged.

“It’s casual, has been for a few years. We spend time together when she’s here, or I’m in LA. We have a good time together, and that’s it. See other people, that kind of thing.” Sharon’s face said it was moving past casual for her, and Agatha knew she was going to be poking and prodding at Lilia soon.

“Maybe she’ll retire soon and move up here,” Agatha tried to be hopeful.

The older woman just scoffed. “She’ll never leave that haunted house of hers.”

“At least the ghosts are friendly.”

Sharon locked eyes with Agatha, her voice tinged with sadness, “The ghosts of the living are never friendly, Agatha.”



⭐⭐⭐

Rio had fallen asleep while talking to Alice, during which time she had learned more about Alice’s ten years in the city before Rio’s arrival. When Agatha introduced Jen and Alice, they quickly became friends. Then, when Alice found she had no one to talk to about everyday life at SHIELD, she had Jen, who often had dealings with the school and had signed the very same NDAs. They grew closer, and Alice woke up one day and realized she was in love with Jen. By then, she valued her ever-growing relationship but didn’t want to ruin anything. Alice hadn’t dared to do anything until Rio came back into her daily life and gave her that push.

Alice recounted how she decided she needed to build a relationship with Lilia to learn more about Lorna and that she had built a closeness with Agatha but still kept her at a slight distance. Rio had fallen asleep as Alice’s little spoon while clutching the geriatric moose while Alice was telling her about different pranks the boys had played. Rio usually did not sleep like the dead. That was Agatha’s job. She hadn’t noticed when Alice left, tucking a blanket around her before she went.

Agatha and the redheaded wonders returned to see Alice watching crime documentaries.

“Is Rio still asleep?” Agatha asked, already on her way up the stairs.

“Yes, do not wake her up, Agatha Fucking Harkness!” Alice yelled as Agatha vanished.

Wanda and Nat joined Alice as the expert on the television was explaining how the murder was committed.

Nat scoffed. “He’s giving the wrong directions.”

“Babe, they’re not directions,” Wanda looked at her in amusement.

“They’re still wrong.”

Agatha quietly slipped into the bedroom and stood at the side of the bed, watching Rio as she slept, curled up with the ancient moose, before she joined Rio on the bed and pressed herself against Rio’s back. She carefully draped her arm over Rio’s torso and covered the younger woman’s hand with her own, placing a kiss on the back of Rio’s neck.

Agatha thought about her little family and how she had watched Wanda and Nat while they were out, as well as the look on Alice’s face when she texted Jen. The way she oversaw and, at times, facilitated those journeys over the years, the people she loved and cared for the most had all found love.

There was no reason why this couldn’t finally work out for her, and she was fully committed to it.

“I love you,” she whispered into Rio’s ear.

“Love, too,” Rio mumbled, rolling over and nuzzling into Agatha’s chest, moose in between them, like an anchor to reality.

“How’s your head, my love?” Agatha ran her hand up and down Rio’s back and kissed her forehead.

“Better, hurts less.” Rio forced her eyes open as she brought herself out of her slumber to look at her girlfriend. Agatha’s beauty disarmed her momentarily. She was so in love with this woman. Then thoughts and questions from the past several days flooded her mind and out of her mouth as though she hadn’t just woken up.

“The two times we ran into each other at Norma’s, why were you really there? I remember running into you and then pretending like we hadn’t bumped into each other the night before when Norma introduced us or reintroduced us. What was that about?”

Agatha ran a hand through Rio’s hair, not showing sadness at a ruined moment but committed to telling the truth.

“Well, the first time was to bring Lorna back to LA but also to tell her I was pregnant. She was so excited. I just panicked the next morning, not wanting anyone to think I was creeping around the night before. The second time, she had run after finding out she was sick, and I came to bring her back and tell her we were fighting it together. It was another panic moment. You were also of age then, and I did not need an interrogation from Norma. She knew about the relationship with Lilia and kept telling me I was going to rob a cradle one day. She’s laughing from beyond the grave, I’m sure of it.” Agatha smiled.

“I remember every detail of both times. I couldn’t share either time with Alice. I knew she’d make fun of me forever. As it was, she teased me over how much I blushed the times we officially met. There are only a handful of things I’ve kept from Alice, those being two of them.” Rio fought the urge not to bury her face in her hands. “Did I ever tell you that you were my gay awakening?”

Agatha snorted. “I have heard that millions of times, but to hear it from you is a completely different feeling,”

Rio could feel her cheeks reddening. “I was young, maybe seven or eight? It was a high school slasher flick you were in, and you had on this purple bikini, coming out of the water, and it was that moment when I felt something and thought, ‘I like girls.’ As I got older, it never went away, and I never felt the same way about boys. So, I can honestly say I fell in love with the woman that made me realize I was gay.”

Agatha smiled. Something about knowing she was the person who first made Rio realize she liked girls made her heart stutter.

“Full circle, huh?” While Agatha’s words were teasing, her eyes were not. “That was a terrible movie. You were way too young to watch it, and I was in an awful headspace. I am glad you watched it, though, and that seeing me on a screen helped you realize your attraction to the fairer sex.”

Rio looked down at the comforter in thought for a moment before looking back at Agatha.

“It wasn’t just your looks, Agatha. It was the way your personality shone through. I went to the library after that and looked up every interview you had done, anything I could find on you—newspaper articles about your court case, any newsreel. Even though I was young, I had this thirst for knowledge and a slightly obsessive nature. I had been forced to grow up already, and I connected with something in you. I needed to know more, and I found all of that out. You immediately became my favorite actress. While other little girls in 1997 were obsessed with boy bands, I was obsessed with learning about how you had advanced queer rights, changed laws, and continued to be yourself and not shy away from it.

“Meeting Alice and being taken in by her and Gran was one thing. I was starstruck the first time I met Lorna, but Norma tried to make sure we weren’t there when Lorna was detoxing. Your presence nearly killed my baby gay self.” Rio laughed and then changed the subject quickly to cover up her awkwardness. “Who gave you this senior citizen?” Rio motioned with the moose she held, assuming there was a story.

Agatha let Rio get away with the topic switch and looked down at the moose, a hint of sadness in her eyes.

“I was sixteen, Evanora was in prison, and it was technically my first Christmas on my own, even though I wasn’t. Lorna and Lilia brought me up here and we spent Christmas up here. I saw a moose in the wild for the first time. It was huge and scared the life out of me. I was skiing and almost went into a snowbank. So they presented me with the moose here. They thought they were hilarious.” She smiled at the memory.

“Does he have a name?”

“I was sixteen…” Was the only answer Agatha gave, her face flushing.

Rio couldn’t help but laugh. “You named him horny, didn’t you? Those are antlers, Agatha.”

“I was sixteen!”

Rio’s laughter died out as she cupped Agatha’s chin and made Agatha look at her.

“I want to hear about them and the past, Agatha. I want to hear more stories about Nicky as a kid and what playing Elphaba was like. Reliving the past can hurt, I know, but that’s how we keep those memories alive,” Rio placed her thumb in the middle of Agatha’s cleft chin. “I love you, Agatha, and if I have to tell you that every hour of every day for you to get it through your thick skull just how much I mean it, I will. The good, the bad, the ugly, I’m here for it all, Agatha Fucking Harkness.”

Rio’s hand slid from Agatha’s chin to her chin and around to the back of her head as she moved to press herself against Agatha, who moved the Moose.

“Lorna,” Agatha replied. “I actually changed my middle name to Lorna.”

She clarified as she pulled Rio closer and drew her in for a kiss.

⭐⭐⭐

The five women had returned to their homes in the city on Wednesday to prepare for their return to their various places of employment on Thursday. Rio, however, was not allowed to return until Monday due to her recovery. Sharon and Lilia’s orders.

Jen had put out a statement in People that had gone over well, and the celebrity-obsessed internet was making video edits of either one deceased supermodel or two women singing drunk karaoke. With everyone returning to their normal lives, Wanda had one issue that needed resolution.

Wanda hadn’t technically asked Lilia if they could meet. Instead, she texted the older woman and declared when she would be at her house and that Lilia needed to be there. Wanda, being more polite than her counterparts, though, at least announced her arrival as she crossed the threshold into the house.

“Lilia, just what the hell were you thinking!” Wanda yelled as she stormed in, volume and anger surprising even herself. Her plan to walk in calmly and talk this out flying right out the window. This was Lilia, Wanda reminded herself. They needed her, and she was family. And family wrongs must be addressed.

Agatha was more than just her best friend. She was her sister.

Agatha had been there through everything. Agatha had taken her in and taught her almost everything she knew. Agatha held her through nightmares through heartbreak and had been there when the boys were born. Agatha was one of the most important people in her life, and to know she had been hurt so deeply by Lilia, of all people, and under Wanda’s nose activated every protective instinct in Wanda she possessed.

Wanda was furious.

Lilia sat at the kitchen table, legs crossed at the knees, drinking her tea, a stack of papers set to the side. A cup of tea sat across from her next to a plate of homemade pizzicati.

“I was not surprised to get your text, Wanda. I just didn’t know when I would get it, ceai?” Lilia gestured toward the seat.

Wanda stood and glared at Lilia, awaiting an answer. Lilia exhaled sharply through her nose.

“I’ve been asked that question a few times the past few days, and I honestly don’t think I was. Not clearly, anyway.”

“Well, that’s fucking obvious!” Wanda spat, full accent on display.

Wanda’s accent came out on three occasions. When she used it with Nat on purpose, when she was drunk, and when she was furious.

Her accent had not come out with Agatha or Nat the other night. She had been upset with them, but not to this level.

Lilia had only seen Wanda like this once before, and she remembered that day with clarity.

When Wanda was stalked, the stalker went further than any of them thought he would.

He threatened the boys. Agatha had been out of town when the threat to the boys had been made, and Wanda immediately appeared at school. She walked a path across Lilia’s office, speaking three different languages, like a caged tiger.

The body washed up on the beach a week later.

Right now, Wanda stood, eyes attempting to set Lilia on fire.

“In my country, you would be food for pigs. I hear it’s the same in most of the American South.” Wanda continued. Her arms were crossed, and she was tapping her foot. She looked every bit the disappointed mother but much more frightening, especially with the Romanian accent.

“How could you do that to her? You left her when she needed you! You helped raise her! You had a relationship with her?! I’m still both shocked and disturbed by that. If the two of you had just communicated at all, none of this would have happened!” Wanda threw her hands in the air with a cry of exasperation.

Lilia opened her mouth to speak, but the words died before leaving her mouth when Wanda held up her hand to silence her, and the younger woman continued.

“That’s what everything boils down to, isn’t it? No one knows how to talk to each other?”

“I-” Lilia started.

Wanda’s glare just intensified as she continued talking.

“You can speak when I’m done,” Wanda said sharply. “Look at what you did to yourself, Lilia! Was it worth it? Was playing this game and losing Lorna worth it? I saw how it broke Lorna those last few years. Every time Nicky did something for the first time, she and Agatha both wished you were there to witness it, Lilia. The looks they gave each other and the sadness they communicated. I knew, maybe no one else did, but I understood that you were missing. I never knew what happened until this past weekend, and no one would tell me, but damnit Lilia. I can’t imagine how I would have felt if Agatha hadn’t been there for those moments. Or at least watch videos of them. I missed you being there with the twins’ milestones, too, but I knew I could still bring them over here occasionally until they learned to talk and rat me out.”

Wanda still spoke from a place of anger but softened when she talked about the boys. It hardened again when she jumped to the next topic.

“I was so hopeful when you were at the hospital when Nicky was born! Lorna was sober, and I know you would have been happy for her.”

Lilia spoke up then.

“Lorna told me when she sent the two of you back up with Agatha. I was so proud of her. I texted her later, telling her as much. She never responded. I knew she was sick at the end. She came to my house a few months before it happened. She wanted to apologize for the things she did. I said something stupid, and instead of being us again for the last few months of her life, that was the last time I saw her. I’m sorry Agatha didn’t tell you what happened.” Lilia blinked the tears back.

Wanda’s face hardened. “I’ve forgiven her because that’s what family does, Lilia. You and Lorna let your pride get in the way. For the two women who taught Agatha and me how to be a family, you two didn’t listen to yourselves.” She moved to drink her ceai. Her voice was hoarse from talking so much.

“I also haven’t told you it’s your turn yet.” She replaced the cup on the table. “How could you destroy her like that? I have watched her for eighteen years continue to give her body to near strangers night after night because she wouldn’t form a connection with anyone. Only sex. She would constantly tell Nat and me that sex was all she could do. I watched her scream along to angst-filled songs that ripped at her heart and cry over old pictures when she didn’t think anyone was watching. I watched her live with that pain, Lilia.”

How Wanda was still in control of herself, she wasn’t sure.

“Wanda, I,” Lilia tried again.

“Nope. When Lorna died, she needed YOU, Lilia. Not me. YOU. I’m a big enough person that I can admit that. Hell, we all needed you, but Agatha especially. She still wears that locket to everything, and even when you can’t see it, she has it on her. Yes, she told me what it means. I asked her once when I was younger. She may have removed your hair from it, but she still has it.” Wanda had walked the length of the kitchen twice and was boring a hole into Lilia’s soul.

Lilia had known that Agatha had removed her lock of hair. Lorna had told her. Lilia did not, however, know she had kept it.

Wanda’s anger was beginning to wane somewhat, but only because she was so tired of all of this. “You didn’t just hurt Agatha. Your actions hurt all of us. She trusted you, and yes, she is beautiful and alluring, but damnit Lilia. You two couldn’t have kept it in your pants?” She spat, her face had lost the scarlet it had held while she was angry, and she had lost much of her steam. “You may speak now.”

Lilia looked up at her with pride. The one they had always protected had become a protector.

“You have grown into an incredibly strong woman, Wanda. I am so proud of you. You’re one of the few people who Agatha will let fight for her because she fully trusts you. I know you are outraged with me, and I would be too. I am, still. You watched Agatha and Lorna go through it, not understanding what happened, and now that you know that I caused it. The one person who was never supposed to cause any of them, or you, harm, you’re angry. You’re also sad.”

“Don’t think you can Calderu your way out of this, Lilia.”

Lilia scoffed. “Oh, I know I can’t. You’re going to keep a very watchful eye on me.”

“Absolutely.” Wanda’s accent was dissipating, but she was still weary of Lilia. She loved the woman, but Lilia was right. Wanda would be watching her. Wanda slid into the kitchen chair.

“I never meant to hurt anyone, and as they say, hindsight is 20/20. If I could undo it all, I would. However, I did, and I have to own up to that. I did hurt everyone I love and I can never fix things with Lorna, which is why I want to at least try and repair things now. I am so sorry for what I did to all of you. I needed all of you in my life, and I’m glad I had you in the small capacity I did. I’m sorry she kept it from you, but that’s my fault. Lorna and I taught her to keep things close to the vest, and she did.” Lilia left out the hair tidbit and watched as Wanda thought everything over.

“And Rio?”

Lilia smiled warmly. “I did not plan on them falling in love. That’s a little too manipulative, even for me. For example, when I scheduled Morgan and Nicky to be in the same classes since pre-k, I was trying to mend the relationship between Tony and Agatha. Certainly not matchmake children. They’re children.” Lilia hated that someone had even suggested it.

Wanda took a bite of one of the jam-filled cookies before her. “What is the story between Tony and Agatha? She’s never told me.”

A memory flashed across Lilia’s face as she shrugged.

“Ask again. I’m sure she’ll tell you now. Back to the topic at hand, I’m glad you and Agatha can move past things and that you and Rio get along so well. You also didn’t find all of my hiding places in the cabin.” She smirked, referencing the mics, “Hopefully, you and Nat can set a better example of healthy relationship dynamics. Wanda, it’s my fault she never told you about us. I insisted that we not tell anyone. I’m the one who told Lorna, and even then, Lorna acted like she figured it out. When in reality, I told Lorna because while I told myself she’d be happy for us, I knew she’d be upset, and God was she. I destroyed our relationship over it.

What I learned, Wanda, is that if the person you have been closest to for the majority of your life tells you that something is a terrible idea, you should listen. Chances are, they know you better than you know yourself. Lorna was right, and she kept telling me I was ripping Agatha and myself apart. Unfortunately, we’re all stubborn and hard-headed here.

“I’m truly happy for Agatha and Rio if that’s what you're truly worried about. I have no romantic feelings for Agatha anymore. Like I told Rio, and she, I’m sure, told you, I love Agatha very much, but it’s the way I always loved her. Not the infatuated love I had for her once. I care for her, for all of you. I love all of you. Being in Agatha’s life in a larger capacity doesn’t just depend on Rio, and I know that” Lilia eyed Wanda, measuring just where she was now.

Wanda looked at the now empty plate. Damn Lilia and her culinary skills. “Lilia, I love you, and we need you in our lives and our family,” Wanda’s eyes turned dark, “But if you cause any more harm to my sister or anyone in this family, it won’t be Nat that you’ll have to worry about.”

Lilia had never been afraid of Wanda. The woman was kind and gentle. She once squashed a spider and cried.

At this very moment, the sweet redhead terrified her.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha had decided long ago to only have large celebrations for her birthday every five years. She had told Rio no gifts, though Agatha had a feeling she wouldn’t listen. No one ever did.

Agatha had arrived home late from filming that Friday evening and had told her family that if they insisted on celebrating, then cake only. Maybe dinner. Rio was still restricted from too much activity until Sunday, which, while disappointing, just meant they’d have a bit of a busy week.

Agatha was slightly shocked that only Rio’s car sat in front of her house, though she knew better.

She walked into her house, the distinct smell of Lilia Calderu’s cooking in the air.

Lilia was in her house, on her birthday.

Most likely making Pasta Alla Norma.

It’s what she made Agatha every year for her birthday. Starting when she turned fifteen, Evanora had always spent Agatha’s birthday at church. Praying it would be Agatha’s last. Lorna and Lilia snuck Agatha out of her house and threw her a birthday party.

Her very first one.

Agatha walked into the kitchen to see her family, original members, and new additions.

“I said no celebrations unless it was cake.” Agatha tried to act annoyed.

“You said dinner was a maybe, so we took that as a yes,” Rio kissed her on the cheek. “Happy birthday.”

In all actuality, Agatha loved it. She had missed celebrating with Lilia. It just felt right. There was still work to be done, but things were improving. The kids didn’t even ask about her presence. They were smart. They could figure it out.

Herb had fit right in, and the boys seemed to like him. Vision was taking things slowly with him, and Jen had handled their date expertly in the People article.

“Vision has always remained faithful to Wanda, though now that they are separated, he is free to date whoever he likes and has his family’s full support.”

The three additional teens were a stable fixture. No one knew where Cassie and Tommy would end up, but they made great friends either way. Wanda had a betting pool going.

The house was filled with noise, celebration, and love. There was cake and Lilia’s cannoli, another birthday staple. Rio somehow managed to get icing on her nose, and Agatha kissed it off. Morgan told them to get a room. Nicky shouted that those were his mothers she was talking about.

After dinner, entirely too much sugar and the presents which slightly annoyed Agatha, the teens made off to the clubhouse, and the men departed. The kitchen had been cleaned when Agatha wasn’t paying attention and suspected that was Wanda and Lilia’s doing.

Lilia took Agatha’s arm. “Walk me to the door,” she instructed.

“Where’s your car?” Agatha asked the question that hadn’t been answered yet.

“We parked at Wanda’s, and the kids started bringing our cars over when they left a few minutes ago,” She winked.

“It’s dark outside. The twins are still fourteen!” Yes, the four freshmen were being taught by Vision, as he was the village driving instructor, and Cassie and Eddie had turned fifteen and had learner's permits, but that didn’t make it safe.

“Agatha, they don’t have to leave the compound. It’s not far, they’ll be fine. How old were you when you learned to drive?”

“Different times,” Agatha conceded.

They stood on the front porch when Cassie and Tommy drove up in Lilia’s black BMW. “I remember a time when you tried to pretend you only made a principal’s salary,”

“I invested very well in the nineties and sold when Apple hit its peak.” Lilia lied.

“Sure you did.” Agatha turned and hugged the shorter woman. “Thank you. For everything. We’re not…”

“I know.”

“But, one day.”

“One day.” Lilia gave her a tight squeeze, waved to the kids, and left.

Nicky walked up to her and looked at her closely. He suspected that he had connected the right dots, and he did not like the picture they made. “Scandalous, Mom. I need brain bleach.”

“Oh, go have the party I’m pretending I don’t know about. No driving, take everyone’s keys, and for the love of God, don’t let anyone post on social media. Turn off all the routers, confiscate and lock up cell phones.”

“Already the plan, love you.” he kissed her on the cheek and turned to where Morgan was waiting for him. The others had already left.

“What’s the rule?” she called after them.

“Misdemeanors, not felonies!” They called back.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha walked back into the house as Alice and Jen were leaving.

“Happy birthday, you old hag,” Jen said as she hugged her. “Don’t forget, you promised to help tomorrow.”

“I know I did. You overgrown prune,” Agatha released her and hugged Alice, “Thank you both. I love you, mainly you, Alice. We’ll see you tomorrow.”

She walked into the living room, where Rio and Nat looked at Agatha as if she were in trouble, and Wanda looked like a disappointed mom.

“Did you tell our boys that they could have a party?” Wanda asked, her voice strained.

Agatha looked shocked. “Why, absolutely not!”

“Then why did Kamala Khan’s mom just email me a signed NDA and consent form?” Wanda held up her phone. Agatha did not have an answer to that.

“What? I don’t know anything about that.”

“It appears that because the children are under eighteen and therefore cannot sign legal documents themselves, the people attending the party were given NDAs and consent forms to have signed by their parents. So that there is legal documentation that they were allowed to be there no matter what occurred and that they would not talk about it or post about it on social media.” Wanda explained, still trying to process.

“At least they’re smart,” Agatha offered.

“Agatha!” Wanda reprimanded.

“Oh, come on, Wanda, let them be kids. It seems like they only invited their close friends, friends with parents we know. They’re all smart kids.” Agatha reasoned.

“I’ve already tapped into the mics and cameras in the house,” Nat was looking at her phone. “So far, so good.”

Wanda dropped her shoulders in defeat. “I’m still worried.”

Nat stood, taking Wanda’s hand. “Then let’s go home and de-stress,” She grinned, whispering into Wanda’s ear and leading her towards the door.

“Goodnight, you two. Love you both. Happy birthday, old lady.” Nat waved.



Agatha and Rio both smiled as Agatha held out her hand to Rio and led her up the stairs.

“Why so many cameras in all the houses?” Rio wondered out loud.

“Liability, protection, several reasons other than basic security and just keeping an eye on things. People love to make things up, especially when they think money or fifteen minutes of fame may be involved. So, we get ahead of it and make sure they can’t.” Agatha shrugged. “I’m so used to being in front of cameras and having my whole life scrutinized that I forget they’re there. It’s an encrypted closed-circuit system, so only certain people have access. With the clubhouse, it’s like baby monitors for teenagers.”

She had changed into sleep shorts and a tank top and slipped under the covers into bed. Rio joined her, clad in a Stevie Nicks shirt and Snoopy boyshorts. She looked at Agatha, startled.

“Are there cameras in this house?”

“No, we don’t invite people we don’t trust into our private residences, and if it’s questionable, we have them sign an NDA.”

Agatha wanted at least one place where she wasn’t in front of a camera, and home was it. The Clubhouse hadn’t been outfitted with cameras until the boys started hanging out there in middle school.

Rio thought back to the very first night she had spent with Agatha. She had never signed anything. “Agatha, you never made me sign an NDA,” Rio watched her expression carefully.

Agatha drew Rio into her lap and regarded her lovingly. “I must have had a good feeling about you.”

Agatha leaned in and pressed a kiss to Rio’s lips.

Rio kissed her back, parting her lips as the kiss intensified. Agatha’s tongue licked the roof of Rio’s mouth, and the backs of her teeth, and rolled against her tongue. Rio moaned at the sensations and readjusted to where she straddled Agatha’s thighs, slightly breaking their kiss so they could breathe.

Agatha took the opportunity to pull back, her hand firmly planted on the small of Rio’s back, holding her steady.

“Not cleared until Sunday.” She reminded them both, her voice husky.

“Agatha, I haven’t had to take anything in twenty-four hours. I’m fine.” Rio whined, fingertips gliding across the expanse of Agatha’s stomach under her tank top. “Besides, there are ways we could do this that aren’t too strenuous…” Rio let her unspoken plea linger in the air.

Agatha shuddered at the light touch, running her free hand through Rio’s hair and lightly gripping the back of her neck. If they were doing this, it would be on Agatha’s terms. She brought their lips together again in a slow kiss, savoring the feeling of Rio’s lips against hers. She moved her hands to the hem of Rio’s shirt, removing it and tossing it into the darkened room. Agatha looked up at Rio, running her fingers from Rio’s temple to her jaw.

“You will tell me if it’s too much or if you start to get sore, a headache, anything, understand?” Agatha leaned forward, planting open-mouthed kisses across Rio’s chest.

Rio moaned and rolled her hips against Agatha’s thighs. “Yes, baby,” she gasped when Agatha bit down on a hardened nipple.

“Already so needy, I can feel you against my thigh.”

Agatha flicked her tongue across Rio’s nipple. In response, Rio wrapped her hands in Agatha’s hair, holding her closer, focusing on the sensations from Agatha’s mouth and hands.

“I missed you.”

Agatha nipped right above Rio’s right breast, causing her to gasp. Agatha felt the familiar sensation of want and need growing low in her belly. Agatha knew she would tease Rio for a little longer before she gave them what they both so desperately needed.

Rio was trying to find any possible friction she could and not getting what she needed. Rio removed Agatha’s top and pinched and rolled Agatha’s nipples, eliciting sounds of her own.

“Agatha.” Rio grabbed a fistful of hair and pulled her head back to make eye contact. “Please,” she begged.

Agatha cupped the back of Rio’s head and kissed her slowly and passionately. Agatha tugged at the boyshorts. Rio broke the kiss and quickly shifted to remove them and took a moment to free Agatha of the rest of her clothing. Rio returned to her rightful place atop Agatha and resumed the kiss, grinding against her.

Agatha cupped Rio’s right buttcheek and ran her other hand up her inner thigh and along the crease of where it met her torso.

Rio’s breath caught, and she whimpered. “Agatha,” she pleaded again.

“What is it, baby?” Agatha asked innocently, her own need hidden by her clenched thighs, though she felt it throughout her entire body.

Rio leaned in next to her ear. “Make love to me,” she purred before kissing Agatha under her ear.

Agatha moaned and slid a finger through Rio’s folds. Rio made a keening sound and pushed her back against the headboard, kissing her as though her lips were made of nectar.

Agatha circled Rio’s clit and applied just enough pressure that Rio bucked and moaned into her mouth, continuing the cycle until Rio was panting into her.

Rio kept clenching around nothing and dropped her mouth to Agatha’s shoulder, biting hard enough to leave a mark. “I need you, now.”

“Impatient,” Agatha smirked, not changing positions, causing Rio to pout.

Agatha brought her other hand up to cup the back of Rio’s head and twisted her hand, inserting two fingers into Rio and feeling the muscles constrict immediately.

“That was fast,” she said before dropping a line of kisses down Rio’s neck.

Rio’s reply was lost in a chain of satisfied sounds as she rocked her hips to meet Agatha’s rhythm as her fingers pumped into Rio. Agatha increased her speed and moved to kiss Rio, who slowed her bucking so that she could move to where she only straddled one of Agatha’s thighs. Before Agatha could ask why, Rio’s thumb was on her clit, rubbing slightly.

Agatha’s hips rolled at the touch. “Not too strenuous,” Agatha warned against Rio’s mouth.

“Never.”

Rio flipped her wrist around and slid two fingers into Agatha’s entrance. They brought their bodies together, legs entangled, and matched the other’s pace.

It was sweet, it was slow, it was gentle. Third fingers were added, pressures were increased, and climaxes were reached. They held each other that way, chins resting on each other’s shoulders before finally detangling from each other.



Rio had her head on Agatha’s shoulder as they lay in bed, tracing shapes on the other’s skin and enjoying their post-coital bliss. Rio propped her head to look at the beautiful woman who loved her.

“How’s it feel to be fifty-two? Any different?”

Agatha turned to look at the last woman she would ever love, the woman that would be her future. “Yes, actually. I was so scared when I turned fifty-one. It’s how old Lorna was when she lost the fight. She left me a letter, of course. You’ll have to read them one day.”

“I do want to, but I think I’m still processing the ones I’ve read from Alice, so give me some time.” Rio interrupted.

“Fair enough.” Agatha resumed her musings. “Like always, she knew just what to say. Told me to fix things with Lilia if I hadn’t yet, hoped I had found love, not to freak out over turning fifty-one, and that I had nothing to worry about. After her diagnosis, I made sure to take control of my health. Every screening and annual appointment, I do them all, and she knew it. She reminded me that every day is a step closer to death, whether I like it or not, and I have to choose what to do with that.

“She knew that I love my family very much, but she also told me to remember to prioritize my happiness, too. Not just theirs. It’s like she knew I would still be single. All-knowing, even in death.” Agatha ran her finger in random lines and squiggles on Rio’s upper arm.

“Are you happy?” Rio focused on a freckle on Agatha’s chest, afraid of the answer.

Agatha placed the tip of her finger under Rio’s chin and tipped it up so their eyes met.

“Yes, my love. I am happier than I have been in a very long time. You play a large part in that.” Upon seeing the grin appear on Rio’s lips, Agatha decided now was the time to ask her question. “I have two questions for you. First, are you happy?”

Rio nodded. “Very.”

“The next question is a bit bigger. I know we’ve talked about it, but didn’t make it official. Don’t look so panicked, will you move in with me? In June? After school is out?” Agatha was trying to sound confident, but her voice was shaking and this time, she was the one rubbing her chest.

Rio leaned in and kissed her, swiping her tongue over Agatha’s bottom lip, not quite deepening the kiss, but a promise of things to come. She pulled back, her eyes a promise of the future.

“Yes, Agatha. I would love to move in with you, and Nicky has basically catnapped Raven, so she’s good with it, too.” Rio laughed as she kissed Agatha again.

Agatha sighed contentedly. “Good, I can’t wait.” She pulled her close and wrapped her arms around her. In a few more months, this would no longer just be her and Nicky’s home.

It would be Rio’s, too.

Agatha was pretty sure anywhere Raven was, was her kingdom.

⭐⭐⭐

The rest of the weekend went by quickly. They gathered at Alice and Jen’s house Saturday morning, and their six teens appeared, along with Vision, Herb, and Lilia. Furniture was moved, boxes unpacked, pictures and art hung. Alice and Jen’s house was officially theirs, and they were officially moved onto the compound. Everyone had turned to Rio, who informed them she’d be moving in in June.

Tuesday was April Fool’s Day. Traditionally, Nicky’s favorite day, although he knew he couldn’t do anything too risky. This year, he asked Lilia’s permission for this particular prank, and after a sigh of defeat, she agreed.

So on April first, all of the fountains at SHIELD turned into bubble fountains dyed in different colors of the rainbow flag, courtesy of Nicky and crew. Rio took pictures and sent them to Agatha.

As for Rio, Alice had informed her that the fencing club was planning on wearing shirts to try and get under Rio’s skin. Another jab at who they saw as Agatha’s latest fling. Their shirts, according to Alice, were black with purple lettering that held the various “class years” to which each woman belonged.

This time, there was no anger or emotional outbreak from Rio. Instead, she had Wanda help her make one of her own.

Her shirt was a light green with purple letters that read:

SHIELD Fencing Club

Final Class

Est. 2024

On the back, Vidal was written across the top.



She could not wait to see the looks on those petty bitches’s faces later.

Rio took a picture and sent it to Agatha. She was finally taking ownership of her relationship with Agatha, and they were working on their communication and making it what they needed it to be.

It was something she had never had before and something she truly looked forward to doing.
