Chapter End Notes

Jen! What? Girl. What is going on there.
What is the Tony and Agatha story?
Lilia and Sharon? IS EVERYONE GAY?
What's that story?

At least we're not sobbing, right?

One more chapter!
And then more things!

So I Dare to Risk This Heart of Mine

Chapter Summary

The end of the school year and a few events.

Chapter Notes

We are here at the end.
Thank you so much everyone! I never thought my little story would get this much love!!
Also, there's uh, the smuttiest scene I've done so far. More smut to come in the next stories.

Keep an eye out on the socials for when I update the first few chapters!
Also, for announcements for the next installment and other stories!

Thank you so much to DaretoDream82 who stuck with me through most of this thing and helped edit, beta read, translate my thoughts, pull thoughts from my head, worked so many late nights with me, even when she was overseas.
I'd also like to thank our wives for putting up with us.

See the end of the chapter for more notes

Part of Rio had been worried that Alice was playing her own April Fool’s Day prank. However, walking into the teacher’s lounge and seeing the looks on a few faces of women in their black Fencing Club shirts reassured Rio that her ride and/or die had indeed been telling her the truth. Alice had gone with her and made sure to immortalize the looks of absolute horror and disgust and sent them to the rest of the coven.

It was a good day with zero harmful pranks, and the students just thought their teachers were referencing an after-school Happy Hour. These kids were not phased by many things and at this crucial time of the year, they had bigger things to worry about than what manner of questionable things their teachers were doing.

The shirt became one of Agatha’s favorites, judging by the way she greeted Rio at the door and ravaged her on the couch, unable to make it to the bedroom.

The school year always moved faster towards the end of the year with testing, ceremonies, performances, and all of the major projects due. With Agatha and Wanda so busy during the week, Rio felt like minutes flew by, but days slowed down. Football was in spring training, track was in full swing, and she found herself looking forward to the weekends more than usual or doing test prep with upperclassmen for college entrance exams. There were also AP tests and Dual Credit tests for those trying to get college credits.

They had Coachella, the Beach Music Festival, the Prom, and the SHIELD end-of-the-year showcase to look forward to.

While not all students at SHIELD were into fine arts, several still wanted to show off their talents and they prepared all year for this week. The last week of school was filled with showcase scrimmages, one-act plays, art displays, and recitals, with everyone showing off their skills. Their lives were packed for most of April and May, leading up to Memorial Day weekend at the beach house to kick off the summer.

The powers that be for SHIELD had deemed the next two Fridays and Mondays ‘virtual instruction’ days since a large portion of students would be either attending one of the two weekends of Coachella or going with their parents to the area but not to the festival itself, much like they did for days after award ceremonies. Most students had already completed the work as teachers had released it early, and Rio had already graded everything that had been turned in.

Rio closed the door to her classroom at the end of the day Thursday and turned to leave, running into Lilia instead.

“Where did you even come from?” Rio put a hand to her chest in surprise.

“Depends on who you ask, my office, New York,” she leaned her head to the side, “hell.”

The side of Rio’s mouth ticked up, and she had a feeling of who had said the last one.

“Lorna or Agatha?”

“Neither.” Something dark flickered across Lilia’s face before her normal cheerful expression returned. “I wanted to ask if you planned on returning next year and if you would want to accept the head basketball coaching position officially. While Peggy is staying, she is resigning from coaching. It comes with a small pay bump.”

“Why wouldn’t I stay?” Rio asked, taken aback by the question.

“To travel with Agatha and be able to spend more time with her,” Lilia answered as if it were the most obvious thing in the world.

Rio mulled it over. It was something Agatha had been worried about, the traveling and distance taking a toll. They had handled it well so far, and this summer would be a much further and longer time apart. However, they were working on their communication skills and building a very strong and solid relationship. Even when apart, there were ways to remain connected in all aspects, and they trusted each other. Why would she give up her career? Agatha would support her if Rio wanted to, but Rio wasn’t that person.

“I’m staying, and I’d love to take the position officially.” Rio finally said firmly. “Walk with me.” She instructed Lilia. The rest of the coven and the confidence they exuded was wearing off on her, and Lilia followed. “I know it’s going to be difficult, Lilia, but what relationship isn’t? I’m done bolting every time things get hard. I can visit if and when I can when she’s gone for months at a time. I love her.”

They exited the building and walked toward Rio’s car. She and Alice hadn’t carpooled, as Rio had been at her apartment and not at the compound. She put her backpack in the passenger seat and turned to look questioningly at Lilia.

“Why? What concerns do you have? I’m still trying to separate the evil ex and the caring aunt, and while part of me screams ‘choke a bitch,’ I see you here, and you genuinely care for her. You want her to be happy, and I truly believe you want us to work. I see how she looks at you now, and she adores you. It’s so confusing.”

Lilia looked down and crossed her arms, letting the unprofessional admission slide. It was after hours.

“I know it is, and your feelings are completely valid. I always thought she needed someone who could travel with her. However, with technology the way it is, she just needs that connection. I have no hard feelings or ill will, no jealousy. Agatha and I  agree that we’re better off with our original dynamic, and somewhere, things went off course for too long. We learned from our mistakes, and we grew from them. It made us who we are today, for better or worse. I do want what’s best for everyone, Rio.” Lilia looked up, locking eyes with Rio. “That includes you, and you can come to me anytime, about anything, when you feel comfortable.”

“I’ll keep that in mind, Lilia. Thank you. I mean that. I know you’ve been protecting me all year, for Agatha’s sake, and I appreciate it.” Rio did mean it, but she was no longer shying away from certain petty women. “I think I have control now, though. I’ll let you know If I need backup.”

Lilia gave her a genuine smile, “Oh, I know you will. You did well last week. It wasn’t all for Agatha, though.”

Rio’s eyebrows formed a question mark. Lilia shrugged.

“Did I ever tell you that Lorna and I grew up together in New York? The City, specifically? Our parents were friends, and I, like you, spent a lot of time in Norma Wu’s house. My parents were Italian. Very stereotypical Bronx Italian, the way movies depict us. Very connected, I kept some of those connections. Norma was more concerned about you than you’ll ever know. Called me a few times about you and your situation with your parents.” Lilia glanced at her smartwatch. “Well, look at the time. I need to get to Palm Springs to pick up Sharon. I’m sure I’ll see you at the festival.” She gave Rio a quick hug and hurried off.

Rio watched her go. What the hell had she meant about Norma?

⭐⭐⭐

They had made it to the extremely exclusive club resort near the Coachella grounds late that night. Agatha tried not to fly short distances, especially if they were in-state, so the whole crew drove southeast. They had an entire wing for their clan and security detail. Rio lay in their bed in her Bulbasaur boxers and a green tank top, thinking about Lilia’s childhood.

She turned her head as she caught sight of Agatha walking in from the bathroom in one of Rio’s Phoenix Mercury shirts and purple hipsters that she knew had Naughty written across the back.

“Hi, gorgeous.”

Rio reached toward Agatha and motioned for her to join her in the bed.

Agatha pulled back the covers and joined Rio, lying on her side to face her, kissing her slowly, and drinking her in. Agatha slipped one hand between Rio’s pillow and her cheek, tracing her jawline with her thumb. The other arm she draped over Rio and pulled her closer to Agatha, sliding her knee in between Rio’s. Rio’s arms mirrored hers, and they lay there, entangled and kissing, until one of them decided they needed more air.

Agatha tilted her head back slightly and sighed contently, her eyes half closed.

“Why do long drives make us so tired?” She asked rhetorically.

The scientist in Rio could not resist. “Our bodies are technically at rest. However, they are being hurtled at unnatural speeds, which can raise our adrenaline levels, and then they drop quickly. There’s also poor air quality and overstimulation from constant input, which leads to mental exhaustion and possible dehydration since you’re generally not drinking as much. That depends on how long the drive is, though. Also, the vibrations from the car can just give you that rocking-a-baby-to-sleep reaction, and then sitting too long has its effects on the body.” Rio was deep in teacher mode, and it was very cute.

“Thank you, Ms. Vidal, that was very informative. It was a rhetorical question, but you’re so cute when you get all educational.” Agatha leaned forward and kissed her again quickly.

Rio smiled into it. She had told Agatha about some of the discussion she had had with Lilia that afternoon and had questions, that, like always, gnawed at her.

“Agatha,” she started. “Is Lilia in the mafia?”

Agatha clicked her tongue. Well, this wasn’t going to go in the direction she somewhat hoped it would go, though she was absolutely exhausted.

“We do not break the spaghetti, and we do not talk about the mafia.” Was the answer Agatha gave Rio. However, Agatha was confused by the random question. “Why?”

Rio chewed on the inside of her lip.

“Lilia told me today that she grew up in the type of New York Italian family that movies portrayed. And all I can see now is the Godfather in my head. That Lilia’s family was connected and she kept some of those connections. Then, she said that Norma talked to her a few times about my home situation with my biological mother and her husband. I did a little bit of research. It’s not Little Italy in Manhattan, like where we visited. She’s from the original and old-school families from up on Arthur Avenue ” Rio locked eyes with Agatha again before she could reply. “Remember, no more lies, half-truths, or omissions.”

Agatha sighed. Shit. She would just spin it differently.

“She came from that type of family, yes, but Lilia distanced herself, with permission, that is. She still has the connections if she needs them, and yes, Norma called her a few times before our blow-up. I don’t know about after.”

Rio nodded—some secrets Rio would have to accept.

⭐⭐⭐

Coachella weekend was an absolute blast for everyone and packed with new memories. Wanda and Nat were excited to be completely open at a concert and acted like teenagers. They recreated their Lilith Fair days to the Missy Elliot set, and even though they were in the VIP section, pictures were taken and put on the internet, some of them by their own family and friends.

Jen Kale-Wu, as she and Alice had officially decided to hyphenate their names, was working overtime. There was not an image clause in either star’s contract, but she knew the studios would frown on this. Specifically Wanda. It didn’t matter what year it was or that the president of the United States was a black married lesbian woman with two kids. Some people were still awful.

Alice Wu-Kale took her wife’s phone away from her for the rest of the weekend and probably saved her life by preventing her from having a heart attack.

Agatha loved seeing her other friends and those who were performing, but as always, she enjoyed taking in the scene around her and living in the moment. She and Tony were both equipped with spray bottles for when Nicky and Morgan made out a little too long, though they were quickly confiscated by Pepper and Rio.

Nicky complained that Billy and Eddie didn’t get sprayed.

“They can’t get pregnant,” Tony and Agatha deadpanned at the same time.

Morgan rolled her eyes and said they were definitely not doing that.

Nicky muttered something about how, if they were, they would be smart about it and how he had gotten the talk way earlier than most.

Jen took the squirt bottle from Rio and shot Wanda in the face.

“Hey! I can’t get pregnant!” Wanda wiped the water off her face.

“No, but you’re going to be the death of me if you don’t calm down,” Jen warned.

Agatha loved her growing group of found family.

Her eyes scanned the area to see Lilia with Sharon. They were only a few years apart in age, and she made a mental note to find out the official story there. Sharon was singing along, and Lilia had a look on her face that Agatha had never seen before. It wasn’t the one Lilia wore when she told Agatha she loved her or even at the end when they had agreed to get married.

No, Lilia looked at Sharon like she was the only person that existed.

Agatha flinched, expecting that age-old hurt to reappear, to feel something in her carefully sewn-together heart rip back open, but it didn’t. If anything, it strengthened the seam.

She was happy for Lilia, and she suddenly understood why Lilia had meddled. To see Agatha look like that. Happy and in love, to truly bring out the best version of herself. Not that she needed to be in love, but Agatha knew she hadn’t been completely happy, and Lilia hadn’t been either, without her family. Agatha had gone soft, and she didn’t completely hate it.

“Pocket-lint for your thoughts.” Wanda had come up beside her and was speaking right into her ear.

“The expression is ‘penny’. You know that.” Agatha looked at her out of the side of her eye.

“A penny is too pricy for your gutter thoughts, Harkness.” Wanda quipped with a grin, bumping their shoulders together. Agatha bumped her back and nodded in Lilia and Sharon’s direction. They were now leaning into each other for a slower song.

“Look at them, look at Lilia.”

Wanda watched the two women for a moment, her face lighting up. “Aww, so cute.” She pulled out her phone and opened an app, typing something.

Agatha sighed. “You have an app, don’t you?”

“I have an app.”

“What do you do with all your romantic betting pool winnings?” Agatha knew Wanda did these things just for fun. They all did.

“Donate it usually.” Wanda shrugged. “Although, I think we should look into starting our own foundation, in Lorna’s name.”

Agatha looked at her, searching for what to say.

“Oh, you know one of us would have thought of it eventually,” Wanda added upon seeing Agatha’s shock.

“I’m more shocked that you want to use illegally gotten gains for charity.”

Wanda’s smile widened. “You know she’d be proud.”

Agatha laughed. “You know, you’re not wrong.”

Somewhere, Lorna was very proud of the girls she raised.

The rest of the music festival weekend continued in much the same way. Agatha sang several songs to Rio, including “Beautiful Things,” Tony made faces, and Pepper smacked him on the shoulder, asking why he didn’t do sweet things like that.

Sunday night, after Coachella came to an end, Agatha, Rio, Wanda, Nat, Tony, and Pepper sat around a fire pit outside of the resort. The resort was hosting only celebrities and their entourages that weekend, and Agatha had been able to see several of her friends throughout the three days.

Rio decided it was the best time to ask exactly what their beef was with each other.

“What’s the deal between you two?” Rio asked, looking between Tony and Agatha.

“He’s difficult.”

“She’s difficult.”

They answered at the same time.

The other women just looked at each other. No one truly knew what started it. Agatha had been a child star, and Tony had grown up in the world of film, following his father around, learning all there was to know. At first, Howard tried to put Tony in front of the camera, but that wasn’t Tony’s calling. He did better at directing. Pepper had been an assistant, and that’s how they met. Agatha had done several Howard Stark films, and she and Tony had strong personalities that matched each other. That had been the beginning.

“He’s bossy,” Agatha offered.

“She’s very hard-headed and stubborn,” he shot back.

“He’s a nepo baby, and throws tantrums,” she countered.

“They’re both children,” Pepper interjected.

Wanda shook her head.

“They used to just bicker and fight like that, but then something else happened when Howard turned everything over to Tony.”

Agatha’s eyes went dark. “He knows what he did.”

Tony’s eyes went wide. “I don’t! You got incredibly pissed at me and went from your particular brand of slightly toxic bantering to a complete bitch to work with Agatha. We’ve gotten to where we can playfully banter, and our kids can date, but I still don’t know what I did wrong.” Tony looked at Pepper, hoping she knew, but the look on her face said she didn’t.

“Let me refresh your mind. You were about to take over all of Howard’s projects and he was trying to make it easy for you. Wanda was in a few of those projects.”

Agatha saw Wanda tense, and Nat took her hand. They knew what was coming.

“When all of a sudden pictures of her and Nat making out at a concert were everywhere, and they were outed, studios started getting backlash, and no one liked their straight golden goose having a preference toward girls, which I still do not understand since I haven’t had a huge problem. Word in the back alleys was that it was your doing. You were the one to take and leak the pictures so that you didn’t have a problem taking the keys to Daddy’s kingdom.” Her voice was caustic as the accusation left her lips. Rio stepped back, shocked that her innocent question had opened up such a volatile confession.

“You what?” Wanda yelled.

“They will never find your body. TWENTY YEARS!” Nat said darkly.

“We’re getting a divorce. How could you!” Pepper spat.

“We’re keeping Morgan in the divorce.” Rio put her hand to her chest, appalled.

Tony looked at all five women.

“I would deserve all of that if I had done it. But I didn’t. Dad wanted me to, but I refused. Dad wouldn’t ‘allow me’ to marry Pepper because she wasn’t famous, and it caused us a lot of lost time and pain. Do you think I was going to do that to Wanda? Someone I considered a friend? No. Was it my dad? Yes. I had nothing to do with it. That’s when I tried to set out on my own, but his heart got him a few weeks later.” Everyone lowered their hackles, considering Tony’s confession. “The damage had unfortunately been done by then, and I couldn’t undo it. I apologize for that, but it wasn't me. Not even my photos.”

Wanda was still for a moment before turning to Tony. “Thank you for being honest and not being that person.”

“You’re welcome.” Tony raised a glass of water in salute.

“Well, you’re still annoying,” Agatha attempted to double down.

“So are you.” Tony agreed.

“I guess I’m glad I didn’t kill you. Your kid does make mine happy.” Agatha admitted reluctantly.

“Your kid isn’t so bad. Thanks for not murdering me. You would have gotten away with it and taken my wife.” Tony mused.

“She would have.” Pepper winked at Agatha.

Nat was laughing. “Growing up in this industry absolutely fucked you guys up. And coming from me, that says a lot.”

“It did.” Tony, Agatha, and Wanda said simultaneously.

“In all seriousness though, no. I wanted things to work out for you two, and I’m glad it finally has. Vision’s a great guy, but come on, if I really wanted to out Wanda, I would have taken pictures at one of the many house parties we were at. Besides, you forget. I am a SHIELD alumnus, too, and Lilia would have murdered me. I knew how close she was to Lorna and you two. I’m not that stupid.” He finished his water and stood. “With that, I am going to bed. Are you coming, honey?” He looked at Pepper.

“I’ll be right there.” She watched him leave, and she turned back to the others.

“I am so happy for all of you. Howard was an elitist asshole and would do whatever it took to make sure nothing affected the bottom line, even if it meant hurting others. He absolutely hated me, we even had to wait until he died to get married. Anyway, Agatha, speaking of losing time, prom is the second weekend of May, and Nicky hasn’t asked Morgan yet. She’s expecting a Promposal.”

Wanda’s eyes lit up. The prom was only for juniors and seniors, but she had been looking forward to it. “I need to help. This is what I was made for.” Her grip was tight on Nat’s forearm.

“What the fuck is a promposal?” Nat asked, loosening Wanda’s grip.

“It’s like a proposal, but for prom. It’s an elaborate way to ask someone to prom, cheesy but cute.” Rio explained. She was also excited.

Agatha was not as excited. This meant acknowledging that her baby boy was getting older. She knew it was happening, but moments like this reinforced it, and she didn’t like it. Agatha blew out a breath.

“What does Morgan absolutely hate, and what might she like?”

⭐⭐⭐

Returning to school that week included preparing for testing. Most upperclassmen would be testing the following week and tensions were rising. Rio wasn’t worried about the students that she taught. She knew they’d be fine. Her personal juniors, Nicky and Morgan, as she considered Morgan hers by extension now, had several exams they were taking, and she knew they would ace them all. They both studied hard, and with their parents, they learned quickly and were pre-disposed to do well academically.

All grades still had testing in May, even in private schools, although the tests were different from state testing.

Before all of that started, Nicky had a question to ask Morgan.

He thought about doing it at school but knew she preferred a more private moment this year. She had said she wanted everything their senior year to be big, so this year, he was going small. Though their moms had insisted it be captured on camera. Which was why they were on the basketball court at her house with Pepper filming from a window and Rio taking pictures from behind one of the garages. Morgan was in the zone, though, and didn’t even notice. While Nicky was the taller of the two, Morgan was still the more skillful and was beating him in their one-on-one game. Height did not mean better. He had fouled her, and she moved to the free-throw line. He switched the basketballs out and passed the new one to her.

“Let’s make this one count.” Nicky dropped his eyes to the ball.

Morgan followed his eyes down to the ball where ‘Prom?’ was written in gold paint pen. She looked up at him, just knowing that Nicholas Harkness, son of Agatha, had something to say.

Nicky was wearing a SHIELD football shirt and, with all the confidence that only a high school football star could possess, looked at her and asked, as smoothly as he could, “Will you make me the luckiest quarterback and be my MVP at prom?”

Morgan couldn’t control her laughter. She clutched the ball and doubled over from laughing so hard.

Nicky put his hands on his hips and looked very unamused, except her laugh was contagious, and he started laughing, too. She sat down on the court, trying to catch her breath, her legs bent at the knees, elbows propped on them, with the basketball in between her feet. Nicky walked over and sat next to her, mirroring her position and facing her, his laughter dying down.

“Was it that bad?”

She nodded, wiping tears from her eyes. “Nicky, we’ve known each other since we were four. The ball was more than enough, this was our moms, all of them, and I know it.” She waved to Rio, who had come out of hiding. “To be clear, I didn’t expect anything more than ‘hey, prom?’ My mother wanted something. You’ve gotta go really big next year, though, Harkness.”

Nicky looked at her with the wide eyes of teenage love. “You’re not going to break up with me before then?”

“Do you plan on doing something stupid?”

“Plan on it? No. It might be in my DNA, though.” He was, after all, a Harkness.

“Mine too. Dad has a history, just like Agatha does. I love you, Nicky. We always said, even as kids, that we would go to senior prom together.” She looked at him with questioning eyes, knowing there was more there.

“I just know that high school sweethearts don’t always stay together. We may go to different colleges, you might find someone way better, you never forget your first love, but that doesn’t mean we’re each other’s last love.”

Nicky and Morgan had discussed the possibilities of their futures, especially when the first few recruitment letters had come in. They were realistic kids, and while they did not plan on breaking up, they knew the possibility existed.

Morgan put her hand on her cheek and saw the mixture of hope and sadness in his eyes. An emotion that Nicholas Harkness, son of Agatha, rarely ever had. Her own eyes were glassy, and tears threatened at the edges.

“So how about this, even if we break up, which would shatter me.” She cleared the lump from her throat and continued her thought. “And as long as we aren’t dating anyone else, we will still go to prom together.” She stuck her pinky out like they had so many times growing up.

“Absolutely, but we’ll still be together, getting ready to go to college, whatever that looks like.” He hooked his pinky with hers, and they sealed their pact with a kiss.

“So, is that a yes?” He tilted his head to the side like a puppy.

Morgan wiped a tear aside with a finger. “Yes, you goofball.”

He hopped to his feet and offered her his hand. “Great, I got you something.” He rushed over to his gym bag and pulled out two stuffed penguins. One wore a football jersey with Harkness on the back and the number 12, Nicky’s number. He handed it to Morgan and kept the other, who wore a basketball jersey with Stark on the back and Morgan’s number, 13, on it.

Morgan smiled, huffing a laugh, and looked up at him.

“You grew up surrounded by women.”

Pepper had come outside and now stood by Rio.

“What happened? I saw them get a little sad there.” Pepper was concerned.

Rio had heard some of the conversation and repeated it to Pepper.

Pepper swore. “They’re growing up and realizing that life isn’t perfect.”

“One of the hardest lessons to learn is that life doesn’t go the way we hope, no matter how much we want it to.” Rio agreed.

⭐⭐⭐

It had been a long two weeks of testing, and Agatha had been busy with wrapping filming for the TV show, as well as table reads and fittings for her other projects. Rio finally had her back for the weekend and the house to themselves. They hadn’t had much time over the past few weeks to connect, and they needed that time very much.

Rio had always loved to be the dominant one in bed and preferred to top until Agatha came along. Rio would occasionally take control, and there were times when Agatha needed her to, but now was not one of those times.

They slowly removed the toy from both of them and set it aside to clean. Rio had rolled onto her side, and Agatha handed Rio her water cup and helped her drink. After she set it back down, she noticed that Rio was staring at her.

“What?”

“You’re going to make me get up and pee, and then you’re going to help me clean up and be all sweet, aren’t you?” Rio asked.

“I hear that’s what you do when you care about someone, yes,” Agatha replied.

“I hope you know you’re stuck with me.” Rio smiled, moving to get out of the bed.

“I’m counting on it. Wanda started a betting pool on when I’ll propose. She won’t let me play. Something about it being unfair.” Agatha walked around to Rio’s side of the bed in case she was unsteady on her feet.

“We haven’t even talked about marriage,” Rio said shakily.

“Which is why it goes out five years.”

“What does she not bet on?” Rio scoffed, trying not to think of herself marrying Agatha, though she very much wanted to.

“Herself, politics, the weather, and the NFL, everything else, including her children, are fair game.” Agatha walked next to Rio in the bathroom and found the wipes. Rio went to the restroom first.

Rio laughed. “I love this family.”

“Good, now help me change the sheets. There’s a puddle in the middle of the bed.”

⭐⭐⭐

Rio was happy with how their life was settling. She had begun to pack up the apartment, and Raven could sense a change on the horizon. They had moved enough times that as long as she was with her human, she was fine. This time, they were just moving to her other house. The time-weathered cat had molded herself to fit inside a box and watched as Rio packed a bag for the music festival on the beach that weekend. So much had happened since she bought those tickets for Agatha in February.

“You’re staying with Nicky this weekend. We’re trusting him at the house by himself. Did I just say we? It’s okay, I said we. We’ve been a family for a while, though. Agatha has said she’s fine with this. It’s absolutely fine. It will be Nicky and the boys on the compound. Jen and Alice will be there, too, but they won’t be around much. They’re going to meet a few potential doggy cousins for you.”

Rio was walking between her closet and the suitcase and over to the dresser. Raven’s eyes followed her, inserting meows where appropriate. She hissed when dogs were mentioned.

“Oh, you’ll be just fine.”

Rio put clothes in the suitcase that sat on the bed. It was Thursday evening, and they planned to drive up to the beach house in the morning. The festival wasn’t far from the house, and they preferred to stay in a familiar space. Rio smiled as she thought of the large house overlooking the water. It was there that she and Agatha had decided to be friends, despite every fiber of her being wanting to push Agatha down in the sand and kiss the smirk off her face that very first night at the party.

Even if someone had told Rio what they would go through between then and now, she would do it all again to end up here, surrounded by boxes, madly in love with Agatha Harkness, who loved her back.

“Life is weird.” She said to Raven.

“Talking to the wise one again?” Agatha asked, having come in undetected.

Rio gave her a warm smile as Agatha walked over and gave her a quick hug and kiss on the cheek.

“One of these days, I’m going to think you’re an intruder.” Rio turned her head and kissed Agatha.

“I’d let you. It could be fun.” Agatha winked.  She looked down at Rio’s full suitcase. “Love, you do not need this much clothing. It’s just us. You could walk around in just a swimsuit, underwear, or naked. I like the last option the best.”

“Are you ever not horny? Don’t answer that. I’ve met your stuffed moose.” Rio rolled her eyes as she headed to the bathroom to gather more things.

“It’s the menopause and my extremely sexy girlfriend.” Agatha sat on the edge of the bed. “Wanda and Nat are safely in Tahoe. Vision and Herb are on call for the boys, and Jen is grumbling about being on a compound with teenagers for the weekend.” She reported.

Rio walked back into the room laughing. She tossed her toiletries into the bag and zipped up the suitcase.

“She’ll live. They’re good kids. The one time they threw a party, it was the most responsible teen party in the history of house parties. If they do anything, it will be staying up all night to watch movies and play video games.” She took the handle of the suitcase and gestured toward Raven.

“Get your stepcat, please, and let's go.”

It didn’t take long for Rio to decide that all festivals should be held near one of Agatha’s homes. Or that they should go house shopping near other festivals. It would also give them solid vacation options. Rio lightly scolded herself. They were moving in together in a month. It was too soon to purchase real estate together.

Wasn’t it?

It was late Friday night, and they were lying in bed as Rio’s thoughts wandered. The first night of the festival was also playing in her head. Singing along to Train and Lenny Kravitz were the highlights.

Agatha rolled over and pulled Rio closer to her. “Do you remember Labor Day weekend?” She asked sleepily.

“You mean the weekend you climbed into the bed with me, and I almost died? No, not at all.”

“Smartass.”Agatha placed her head on Rio’s shoulder. “I knew I wanted you as more than just a friend, and I couldn’t admit it.”

“Really? Could have convinced me.” Rio said sarcastically.

Agatha scoffed, “The only people we slightly convinced were ourselves.”

“Aggie, we didn’t convince ourselves. We gaslit ourselves.”

Agatha looked up at her, her bright eyes cloudy with sleep. “I’m glad we’re here now. I love you forever.”

Rio tucked a piece of Agatha’s unruly hair behind her ear. “I love you, too. Forever. Even though forever isn’t technically classifiable.”

“Nerd.”

The rest of the weekend went by smoothly, with Agatha and Rio enjoying each other and listening to great music. They watched the sunsets paint the sky in the background and saw many of Agatha’s friends who were becoming more than just acquaintances to Rio.   This was becoming Rio’s world, too, and she was becoming recognizable even without being on Agatha’s arm.

The boys back home were doing well and had not thrown any parties, according to the couple that had stayed on the compound.

On Sunday night, it was finally time for Alanis to perform. It was Agatha’s favorite set of the entire weekend, and she sang each song as though she personally had written the lyrics. In some cases, she had helped. Agatha and Alanis had connected before the set, and they exchanged many knowing looks during the performance.

Rio wasn’t jealous at all, she kept telling herself, though she did make sure she was glued to Agatha’s side.

⭐⭐⭐

Prom was a few days away, and while Nicky was looking forward to it, Morgan’s birthday was the following Tuesday, and he had no idea what to get her. He’d been getting Morgan something every year since they had turned five, but it was always the latest toy, a gift card, a trending item or gadget, something you would get a friend. This year, he wanted to do something meaningful but was unsure of what. He didn’t want to do jewelry. No, they weren’t there yet. He had already given her the promposal stuffed toy penguins. He knew it needed to be something personal, and he knew just the woman to talk to.

It was sixth period and Rio’s planning time. Nicky easily obtained permission to speak with Ms. Vidal during sixth period, and he took all of his things and left, Morgan watching him suspiciously as he walked out of Mrs. Wu-Kale’s AP History class.

Nicky stuck his head in the open door, looking for the woman whose advice he was seeking, spotting her at her desk.

“Hey, Ms. Vidal, can I talk to you for a moment?”

Rio looked up and smiled at the teenage boy with his mop of chestnut curls that reminded her of his mother’s.

“Of course, honey,” the endearment slipping out. “Are you hungry? I have snacks.” Rio always kept snacks for herself and used them as random incentives or when a kid needed a boost during the day.

“No, I’m good, thanks.” He placed a stool on the other side of her desk and sat down. “I need some help with something, and I think you’d be a good person to ask.”

Rio stopped what she was doing and looked up at him. He looked nervous and was fidgeting in his seat. Nicky was usually confident, like Agatha, and took command in most situations. Also, like Agatha, he didn’t often ask for help.

“Nicholas, what’s wrong?” Rio’s worry emerged and she questioned if she needed to text for reinforcements.

“Nothing’s wrong, no. Morgan’s birthday is next Tuesday, and I’m clueless. I want to get something that says I love you, but teenage appropriate.” He tapped his foot on the rung of the stool nervously. Rio breathed a sigh of relief.

“Oh! Well, I do know Morgan well, being her coach, but Nicky, you’ve known her most of your life. This isn’t the first birthday or Christmas you’ve exchanged gifts. What have you gotten her so far in your relationship?”

Rio wasn’t sure why Nicky had chosen her, of all people to ask for advice on this. She had difficulty coming up with gifts for Agatha, and it took her some serious contemplation to finally land on the right one. She had never done well in relationships as a teen and young adult, and kids didn’t make mix tapes and CDs anymore. If anything, Wanda or Nat would be great people to ask.

Nicky blew out a breath.

“Umm, Christmas, I got her a gift card for a sports store, and for Valentine’s Day, it was flowers, chocolate, and a giant bear.” He grimaced.

Rio suppressed a smile, even though her eyes brightened. “Oh, Nicholas. I can see now why you’re asking for advice,” She gave him a playful smile and saw him relax. “You don’t always have to give her something tangible. Your mother and I have exchanged material items, yes, but we have also given each other experiences and memories, like concerts and trips.” She clarified upon seeing the scandalized look on his face.

Nicky seemed to think it over for a moment before looking back at her, still thinking, “Okay, well, we’re still teenagers, so there’s only so much we can do, and I have to get Pepper and Mr. Stark’s permission to take her anywhere, and your’s and Mom’s of course.” He added.

Rio felt as though her heart did somersaults when Nicky said he needed both of their permission to do something. While they acknowledged that she was somewhat of a parental figure, she did not have that authority. This would need to be discussed.

“It depends on what and where it is,” Rio said cautiously. “What does she like?”

“Sports, music, surfing, we like a lot of the same things.” Nicky shrugged, trying to think of something they didn’t do a lot or hadn’t done before.

Rio knew this needed to be his idea, and didn’t want to just supply the answer for him, but she had an idea.

“The WNBA season is starting very soon, maybe look that up.” Rio knew it was starting the Friday after Morgan’s birthday, but she wasn’t telling him that.

“It does? Does LA have a team?” Nicky asked innocently. He followed college basketball and was getting more into the professional leagues. Morgan had mentioned to Rio that she wanted Nicky to watch more of the women’s league with her this year.

Rio looked at Nicky and shrugged, silently telling him to do this part himself.

Nicky understood her nonverbal cues and pulled his phone out of his pocket, tapping the screen quickly.

“I found it! I wonder if I can get courtside seats and take her.” Nicky’s eyes lit up.

“You should give Pepper a call. She’ll talk to Tony, and then we can all talk about it tonight.” Rio thought for a moment. She still wasn’t sure why he had asked her. She looked at the clock, noting they still had a few minutes and everything was ready for her next class. “Hey, why ask me? Surely your mom is way better versed in this type of thing.”

Nicky gave her a soft smile as he stood. “Several reasons. You know Morgan and the two of you have some things in common. I know you’re going to be direct with me. Mom would tell me to ask my aunts or you, Aunt Wanda would go with something sappy, and Aunt Nat would go with either a weapon or defer to Aunt Wanda. One year, I did give Morgan a personalized pocket knife. She still has it. Mr. Stark makes her carry it on our dates,” he laughed. “The main reason, though, is you’re my other mom, Rio. Why wouldn’t I ask you?” With that, the bell rang, and he waved and disappeared, leaving her to process that and teach simultaneously.

Kids.

Pepper agreed to the game, and Tony knew the team's owner, making it easier for Nicky to get tickets. All he needed to do was ask Agatha that night. Rio was also there as they discussed it.

“It’s the first game of the season. We’ll be there too.” Rio was saying to Agatha. She knew Agatha didn’t have a problem with it and was just making Nicky sweat.

“We will?” Agatha threw a look at her girlfriend, who was sitting on the couch.

“Well, I’ll be there. I’ll take Alice if you don’t want to go.” Rio continued to grade papers, unbothered by the other woman’s question.

“No, I’ll go.” She turned back to Nicky, who sat, alert, on another couch. “Okay, you can go. Just be very alert sitting courtside, and be a gentleman. It’s a late game, so you’ll have to go with us or a driver.”

Nicky frowned. It was the first time Rio had ever seen Nicky upset with a condition that Agatha had set. He looked over at Rio with a hopeful look on his face.

“I agree. I know your mom has let you drive at night by yourself before, but this is different. It’s late, in a busy area, after a large event, in traffic. Not only is it against the terms of your license, it’s just not safe yet, Nicky. You’ll both be tired, and this way, you can talk or anything else.”

Agatha had wanted to interject before Rio had responded but was impressed with Rio’s response. Nicky huffed, knowing if he didn’t accept the terms, it wouldn’t happen.

“Okay,” he grumbled before adjusting his attitude when he saw the warning looks from each of them. “I’m going upstairs to get all that sorted out like the responsible and respectful young man I am. Thank you for the idea, Ri.” He turned on his heel and retreated upstairs before either mom could change their mind.

Agatha turned to Rio and rolled her lips. She wasn’t exactly upset, but Nicky had always come to her with things, and knowing he had gone to someone else had tied her stomach into a small knot. “He asked you for advice?” There was a shadow of hurt in her tone.

Rio took Agatha’s hand. “Yes, I should have told you. He came to me today and asked me what he should get for Morgan. I asked him why he had asked me when there were three other women to ask. Agatha, he said I’m his other mom.” Rio’s eyes filled with tears at the words she hadn’t quite processed.

Agatha smiled genuinely, though she was just as conflicted as she had been a few months ago. “I know he’s made references to it a few times, I’m just still getting used to sharing him with someone I’m romantically involved with. Decisions, what little discipline he requires, advice, and whatever else is needed. I know I’ve shared these things with the others, but this is different.” Agatha’s thumb drew circles on the back of Rio’s hand. “I’m going to be out of the country for over half of the summer and away from both of you. I know he has several invitations to schools for visits and a few football camps.” Agatha paused, gathering the words. She knew she would be asking a lot of Rio. “You can say no, but since you’ll be living here, and he considers you a parent, would you go on his visits with him and share everything with me? It’s ultimately his decision, but I’d still like to know information about the programs, how the trips were, and both of your thoughts. I want to know that he is with someone I trust to guide him. I know you have the best intentions in mind for him, like I do.” Agatha looked hopeful as she looked into Rio’s eyes.

Rio wasn’t sure how to respond. Agatha wanted her to go through this crucial milestone with Nicky? “Of course. Your summer is busy enough. I do have his best intentions at heart, and yours.” She leaned forward and kissed Agatha slowly, placing her palm over Agatha’s heart. “I love you both, very much,” Rio whispered when she drew back and dropped her forehead to Agatha’s shoulder.

“We love you too.” The knot in Agatha’s stomach had dissipated, and she knew that this was right. Rio was the person with whom she wanted to co-parent Nicky or any future children.

Prom night, like all high school milestones, was a ritual Morgan had been looking forward to. Her dress was a one-shoulder, gold-sequined, floor-length gown with three straps across the back for extra support so she wasn’t tugging it up all night. Her hair was in an up-do and her dad wanted her to wear a scarf because of it.

Nicky wore a tux with a gold vest and bowtie. Both sequined, he was committed to matching. His moms had insisted on tagging along to the Starks so they could take pictures. It meant as much to Nicky that Rio was there for this, too, like a second primary mother. She loved him, and even if neither woman wanted to admit it, he knew Agatha and Rio would get married eventually. He saw how worried she had been when he got in that fight, and when he had gotten in that car accident. That wasn’t the look of a concerned teacher or family friend. That was the look of a worried parent. Right now both of them were beaming at him as they took pictures of the young couple dressed in their sparkly prom attire.

The parents finally let them go on their way.

Rio looked over at Agatha and Tony. “Did either of you tell them Chappell was performing?”

“Now, why would we ruin that surprise?” Agatha took Rio’s hand to lead her to the car.

“I promised her I wouldn’t let anyone know she was here.” Tony and Agatha had both pulled the strings for their special guest, Tony had kept her under the radar.

Pepper pat him on the back.

“You did a great job, honey.” She looked at the two women as she guided Tony toward the house. “I had to take his phone away so he didn’t tell everyone.”

⭐⭐⭐

The last week of school was showcase week and to Rio, each day brought with it a fresh new hell. Thankfully, the last day of school was Thursday, and Friday they were all going to the beach house for Memorial Day weekend.

Rio and Alice had already taken care of their end-of-the-year duties and would be able to leave as soon as the bell rang on Thursday. First, they had to get through four days of student exhibits and showcases.

Nicky, Tommy, and Eddie were playing in a football scrimmage -Tommy and Eddie would be playing on varsity the next year. For the football events, Agatha and Rio wore matching “Football Mom” shirts that both read Harkness on the back with Nicky’s number. Nat’s shirt had Maximoff on the back with Tommy’s number, and Herb wore a “Football Dad” shirt, but whose back matched Nat’s.

Billy wore his Football Boyfriend shirt with pride.

Academic and fine arts projects were on display, and both Rio and Alice were very proud of their student's hard work throughout the year, specifically the students of the coven. Tommy’s grades had improved immensely and his projects demonstrated it. Cassie had a lot to do with that, she encouraged Tommy to study and she found ways to make it fun for him. Wanda didn’t ask questions.

The basketball team also had a scrimmage and a teachers vs. students game. Agatha wore her Vidal jersey and cheered extra loudly when she watched Rio play. Rio was not nice to the girls at all, and the teachers won easily. Morgan grumbled the whole time.

Lilia laughed as she watched, she knew Rio had won a National Championship in college. The girls didn't.

Billy showed off his talent by doing a one-act play and a choir concert where he had a lead part and a solo. Wanda and Agatha were so proud. Jen already had a few people in mind to call about the kid.

It was finally Thursday and as Rio locked her classroom door, ending her first year at SHIELD, she looked forward to the future. This weekend was going to be all about beach time. Then she would move in with Agatha the following week.

Rio walked to her car, confidently optimistic for the future.

⭐⭐⭐

Agatha watched her family from the balcony of the beach house during the first weekend of summer. It was the calm before the whirlwind of summer filming, and they had all gathered to kick it off.

The teens were in the ocean and the boys were attempting to show off their surfing skills and doing terribly. She couldn’t make out expressions from where she stood, but she could tell that Morgan and Cassie were laughing as Tommy wiped out. Billy had snuck up behind Eddie and dunked him. The kids were being kids, and it warmed her heart.

Directly below her by the pool; Vision, Alice, Nat, and Herb were drinking, grilling, and laughing while Wanda, Jen, and Rio were planning the Maximoff-Romanov wedding that was only a few weeks away now. Agatha was ecstatic that the wedding, which had been twenty-five years in the making, would finally be happening. Wanda looked on the edge of joyful tears as she continuously looked at Nat and Vision. The two people who had come together and supported her throughout everything.

And Herb. Herb who had been Vision’s friend for as long as any of them had known Vision. Wanda’s worries about someone coming into Vision’s life and not understanding the relationship between them completely dissipated once Herb entered the picture. This teddy bear of a man didn’t mind the pecks on the cheek and forehead the exes gave each other or the pet names. They were close friends, and co-parents that still loved each other. Wanda and Vision didn’t divorce because they didn’t love each other, they divorced each other because they did, and Herb understood that.

The man, like Nat, was a national treasure.

Agatha had come into this life, born to a woman who did not want her. She became famous at an extremely young age based entirely on luck and was still convinced that her fame was the only reason Evanora had not killed her when she was a young child. She had met Lorna Wu as a pre-teen, and the woman took pity on her, hatching a plan to save her from her circumstances, and she had.

Lorna Wu had taught Agatha what family was.

It had gone off track a time or two, and she had lost people along the way and gained so many. Nearly forty years later, and here she was, Lorna gone, but her legacy lived on. Agatha and Lilia were restoring their relationship, and Lilia, her rightful place in the family.

Agatha and Alice had grown closer and had a deeper understanding of each other, as well as Lorna. Agatha looked on and caught Jen watching Alice with pure love in her eyes. Agatha knew Jen had her own skeletons and only let certain people in. She had been so hesitant about taking the leap with Alice, even though they could all see the love between them. Now, here they were, married, living on the compound, about to adopt a dog. Alice turned to see Jen watching her and walked over to join the planning group, bending down to kiss her wife.

Agatha was filled with a comfortable warmth watching all of them and the familiar pang of Lorna’s absence. It didn’t hurt like it had in the past, it was just the awareness that she wasn’t physically there. Having Lilia back had eased the pain. Having two-thirds of the original trio back.

Agatha had been in a staggering amount of movies, TV shows, commercials, plays, and musicals. She was no stranger to various genres of entertainment. She knew that life imitated art, and if you asked her which genre was her favorite, she would tell you it was life. After all, her life had everything. Romance, mystery, intrigue, murder, angst, anything you wanted, and a lot of things she hadn’t but was better for. Rio looked up and raised her water bottle toward her in a small toast.

Agatha raised hers back and smiled lovingly.

Agatha turned her head as Lilia walked up behind her as if Lilia had been summoned by her thoughts.

“So, you and Sharon?”

Lilia chuckled. “I was wondering when you were going to ask.”

“That woman is in love with you.” Agatha leaned forward and rested her forearms on the railing in front of her. “And you’re in love with her, or as close as Lilia Calderu has ever come to it. I’ve seen how you look at her.”

Lilia hummed. “I remember someone telling me that once before, about a woman being in love with me. I lost my two best friends because of it.”

Agatha stood to her full height and put a comforting hand on Lilia’s shoulder.

“ That was then, this is now, and you’re not that person anymore. You’re older and wiser. I know you’re only dating women now, and I know she’s the only one, you’re also the only person she’s seeing.”

Lilia smirked and raised an eyebrow. “Well, look who took a page from Wanda’s playbook.”

“I am not meddling. Call it a welfare check if you must.” Agatha dropped her hand from Lilia’s shoulder, taking Lilia’s hand instead. “Lils, I’m not saying shack up and get married, I’m saying give her a chance and let her in. She’s a great woman. She put up with a very angry me when I broke my leg, remember? She also put up with me multiple times trying to detox with Lorna. She can handle your not-so-law-abiding self.”

Lilia but the inside of her lip. “She’s a tough woman, I know she can. But monogamy? Neither of us has been monogamous since the seventies, Agatha.” Lilia looked as though Agatha had asked her to impale herself with swords.

Agatha laughed at the thought of either woman in a completely traditional relationship. “No, I would never suggest either of you do that. I’m saying make each other a primary relationship, the majority of your love lives. Whatever that looks like for you two. More than just a few weekends a year, Lilia.”

“I’ll think about it. Neither one of us has said the L word, and we’ve been, having our fun, for years now.”

Agatha squeezed Lilia’s hand before letting it go. “Good, I just want what’s best for you,” she parroted Lilia’s words back to her.

“I know, kid. I know.”

Agatha leaned back on the railing, something about the tone around the familiar phrase Lilia loved to use nagging at the back of her mind. She had to ask Lilia, who also leaned on the railing beside her. Agatha looked at her, considering everything she knew about her old mentor.

“It was you, wasn’t it? Not Nat. Every single time something needed to be handled?”

Lilia gave a wry smile. “Never send a Russian to do an Italian job.”

Agatha shook her head as she left Lilia at the railing, smiling the whole time, knowing that Lilia had still always been there throughout the years.

She joined everyone by the pool and seated herself behind Rio, letting the gorgeous woman lean back into her chest as she would a chair. Wrapping her arms around Rio, Agatha gave her a chaste kiss and looked at her family around her. She knew that this was exactly where Lorna had wanted them to be, and that warmed her heart all the more.

⭐⭐⭐

Until Next Time.

Chapter End Notes

This part is over.

What's to come in the future?
Will Alice ever open that envelope?
What is Jen's background story?
Will Rio be connected to SF?
What's in store for our coven?
What did Lorna write in all of those letters?
What did she say to the redheads?
Will Lilia and Sharon make a move?

THERE ARE SO MANY QUESTIONS!

Next: Editing the first few chapters, and then...converting this to a book and looking into publishing options.
Working on the next installment, some one-shots, and preparing for Agatha All Along week.

This universe will continue.

An Update

Chapter Summary

Updated!

Hello lovely readers!
While posting, I kept saying I was going to update the first few chapters.
Well, I did that. Today (May 6th, 2025), I updated chapters one through five. They are amazing and added quite a few words...
I have updates for other chapters too, especially six, but it's not ready to be posted yet. I added the scene of Alice asking Jen out, and it's cute y'all.

But Ellen (my pen name), what did you change/add/edit?
Well, I know the first several chapters were without a beta/editor (DaretoDream82). Because of that, they were rushed and I was still very rusty in my return to writing. We have more inner dialogue, more character development, just more . The first part feels more like the rest of the fic. I also just working on continuity issues as a whole.
ALSO, for those that did not follow the series, it IS a series now! I'm working on the next chapters for those right now. Good Luck Babe, the prequel of sorts, and I am Your Summer, the short mini-fic before the full-blown sequel.
I was hoping to post for Agatha All Along week, but I may just keep on with what I'm doing, we'll see what happens.
I will post updates as I update more chapters!
PLEASE ENJOY THE NEWLY IMPROVED 1-5!

Afterword
